PT P
PN KR2021037898-A; KR2347499-B1
TI Biofuel production system for producing hydrogen by acid fermentation of organic waste, has biogas purification unit that includes compressor for compressing biogas from which hydrogen sulfide and siloxane are removed at high pressure.
AU KOO J O
   CHOI C S
   KILTAE-HYOUNG
AE DH CO LTD (DHDH-Non-standard)
GA 2021363031
AB    NOVELTY - The system has a storage tank (20) for storing organic waste. A biogas fermentation tank (70) generates biogas by fermenting the solid components separated by solid-liquid separator. An alcohol fermentation tank (60) produces alcohol by receiving the liquid components separated by a solid-liquid separator (40). The biogas fermentation tank is connected to a biogas purification unit (80) that separates carbon dioxide contained in biogas and purifies it into high-purity biogas. The biogas purification unit has two filter units for removing foreign substances contained in the biogas, an adsorption tower for removing hydrogen sulfide and siloxane contained in the biogas passing through the filter unit, a compressor for compressing the biogas from which hydrogen sulfide and siloxane are removed at high pressure, and a purification unit that receives compressed biogas and removes carbon dioxide.
   USE - Biofuel production system using organic waste, for producing hydrogen by acid fermentation of organic waste such as sewage sludge, livestock manure, food waste and organic urban solid waste.
   ADVANTAGE - The effect of producing high-quality alcohol by supplying the high-quality alcohol to an alcohol fermentation tank in the state of separating organic acid which is a raw material for alcohol production through an electrodialysis device is realized. The effect of producing high-purity biogas from which hydrogen sulfide, siloxane, and carbon dioxide are removed through a biogas purification unit after methane fermentation in the biogas fermentation tank is realized.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a biofuel production method using the biofuel production system.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a block diagram illustrating the biofuel production system. (Drawing includes non-English language text)
   Storage tank (20)
   Solid-liquid separator (40)
   Alcohol fermentation tank (60)
   Biogas fermentation tank (70)
   Biogas purification unit (80)
DC D16 (Fermentation industry - including fermentation equipment, brewing, yeast production, production of pharmaceuticals and other chemicals by fermentation, microbiology, production of vaccines and antibodies, cell and tissue culture and genetic engineering.); D15 (Chemical or biological treatment of water, industrial waste and sewage - including purification, sterilising or testing water, scale prevention, treatment of sewage sludge, regeneration of active carbon which has been used for water treatment and impregnating water with gas e.g. CO2, but excluding plant and anti-pollution devices (C02).); E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); H09 (Fuel products not of petroleum origin - excluding coal handling, preparation or mining, but including coking, briquetting, peat processing synthesis, gas production, coal gasification. Combustion improvement additives for coal, peat and other nonhydrocarbon based fuels are included in this Section together with coal liquefaction and desulphurisation.)
MC D04-A01; D05-A04A; E31-A02C; E31-F04; E31-N05C; H09-F03

PN CN112570862-A
TI Protective welding cover of carbon dioxide gas shielded welding useful for outdoor operation comprises heat proof cloth set at cover body, ventilating layer provided with locating wire, wear-resistant layer provided with steel wire net.
AU CHEN Z
AE CHEN Z (CHEN-Individual)
GA 2021372491
AB    NOVELTY - Protective welding cover of carbon dioxide gas shielded welding comprises a cover body (1). A heat proof cloth (2) set at the bottom of the cover body. The top part of the cover body is connected with a top cover (3) by screw thread. The top cover is provided with an ink lens. One side of the cover body is provided with a handle (4). One side of the cover body opposite to the handle is provided with a smoke outlet (5). A welding interface, and a smoke outlet is provided with a cap. The welding interface is set at the bottom of the smoke outlet. The heat proof cloth is fixedly connected to the periphery of the bottom of the cover body. The heat proof cloth orderly comprises a flame retardant layer, a ventilating layer, a water proof layer and a wear-resistant layer. The ventilating layer is provided with a horizontal locating wire and a ventilating pipe vertical to the horizontal locating wire. The inner part of the wear-resistant layer is provided with a steel wire net.
   USE - The protective welding cover is useful for outdoor operation.
   ADVANTAGE - The protective welding cover: does not need to wear other protective articles; is more convenient to work; has little damage to the surrounding objects, good wind-proof performance, no requirement to the working place, and suitable.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic representation of the protective welding cover of carbon dioxide gas shielded welding.
   Cover body (1)
   Heat proof cloth (2)
   Top cover (3)
   Handle (4)
   Smoke outlet (5)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The waterproof layer is made of polyvinyl chloride plastic. The wear-resistant layer is made of nylon.

A high temperature alarm (120) is fixedly arranged on the upper right side of the outer cabinet. A fire extinguishing pipe (140) is fixedly arranged on the left side of a carbon dioxide fire extinguisher (130), and is provided with a first fire extinguishing port (150) and a second fire extinguishing port (160). The front portion of the outer cabinet is provided with a hinge shaft. The outer cabinet is hinged with the door through a hinge shaft. The surface of the door is fixedly provided with an observation window, a handle and a lock portion.

   ADVANTAGE - The fire-resistant low-voltage switchgear is equipped with wire holes and slots to ensure timely heat dissipation. The design of the carbon dioxide fire extinguisher and the shutter quickly extinguishes the internal fire source, and also ensures the safety of the operator.

PN CN111691913-A
TI Underground coal mine escaping-type compressed oxygen self-rescuer, has air bag installed on end of self-rescuer body, breathing mask installed on air outlet end of air bag, and exhaust pipe connected between annular groove and air bag.
AU CHEN K
   XIA K
AE CHEN K (CHEN-Individual)
GA 202095326C
AB    NOVELTY - The self-rescuer has a self-rescuer body (1) whose side wall is provided with an oxygen storage chamber (2). The self-rescuer body is made of thermal insulation material. An air bag (7) is installed on an upper end of the self-rescuer body. A breathing mask (8) is installed on an air outlet end of the air bag. Another side wall of the self-rescuer body is formed with two mechanism grooves (4, 9). The mechanism grooves are respectively and rotatably connected with a wind wheel (5) and a centrifugal fan (10). The wind wheel and the centrifugal fan are arranged together along coaxial direction. An exhaust pipe (14) is connected between an annular groove (11) and the air bag.
   USE - Underground coal mine escaping-type compressed oxygen self-rescuer.
   ADVANTAGE - The self-rescuer can make that exhaled carbon dioxide and portion of un-breathed oxygen in the air bag are pressurized and discharged into water so that oxygen is insoluble in water and flows into the air bag along the exhaust pipe for breathing and carbon dioxide is dissolved in the water under high pressure and absorbed by water so as to make that the exhaled carbon dioxide cannot harm a body of an escaped person.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a side sectional view of an underground coal mine escaping-type compressed oxygen self-rescuer.
   Self-rescuer body (1)
   Oxygen storage chamber (2)
   Mechanism grooves (4, 9)
   Wind wheel (5)
   Air bag (7)
   Breathing mask (8)
   Centrifugal fan (10)
   Annular groove (11)
   Exhaust pipe (14)

AE ZHOU F (ZHOU-Individual)

The drawing data moisture-proof device for building engineering management not only is provided with a dehumidifier, at the same time, the moistureproof device has strong sealing property; it can prevent the external air from entering the damp-proof device; in addition, the damp-proof device has a fireproof function; it avoids the damage of the inner drawing data of the moisture-proof device when the fire happens.

PN CN111322739-A; CN111322739-B
TI Windowable three-constant system based on Internet weather data, has control panel that receives and displays real-time outdoor air quality index and grade, indoor air temperature and humidity and carbon dioxide concentration.
AU LI N
   WEI W
   FENG S
   YU Z
AE UNIV CHONGQING (UYCQ-C)
   UNIV CHONGQING (UYCQ-C)
GA 202060867M
AB    NOVELTY - The system has a data center (1), a cloud server (2), a control box (3), an air conditioning unit (4), a fresh air fan (5), an automatic control window (6), an indoor sensor group (7) and a control panel (8). The control box is used to receive and process real-time outdoor weather data from the cloud server and real-time indoor temperature, humidity and carbon dioxide concentration data of the indoor sensor group. The new fan is used to determine the power of the filter, the seasonal pattern and the amount of fresh air. The control panel is used to receive and display real-time outdoor air quality index and grade, indoor air temperature and humidity and carbon dioxide concentration, the operation mode and status of the air conditioning unit, the new fan and the automatic control window. The real-time air quality data includes a total of six indicators such as trioxygen (O3), particulate matter (PM2.5), PM10, carbon monoxide (CO), sulfur dioxide (SO2) and nitrogen dioxide (NO2).
   USE - Windowable three-constant system based on Internet weather data.
   ADVANTAGE - The energy consumption of the new fan is effectively reduced when the outdoor air quality is still good, and the indoor air quality is guaranteed that the user wants to be able to open the window voluntarily.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the windowable three-constant system. (Drawing includes non-English language text)
   Data center (1)
   Cloud server (2)
   Control box (3)
   Air conditioning unit (4)
   Fresh air fan (5)
   Automatic control window (6)
   Indoor sensor group (7)
   Control panel (8)

PT P
PN CN110947421-A; CN110947421-B
TI Preparation of functionalized lignin loaded low melting solvent heterogeneous catalyst by dissolving choline chloride, diazabicyclo and epoxy functionalized lignin in water, reacting, drying, reacting with aromatic acid, and adding water.
AU XIONG X
   ZHANG H
AE UNIV HUAQIAO (UYHQ-C)
GA 202028413X
AB    NOVELTY - A functionalized lignin loaded low melting solvent heterogeneous catalyst is prepared by dissolving lignin, sodium hydroxide and epoxy chloropropane in water, transferring to reaction vessel, reacting under nitrogen protection at 75-80 degrees C for 180-200 minutes, vacuum filtering, washing by water, drying at 50-55 degrees C to obtain reaction product epoxy functionalized lignin, dissolving choline chloride, diazabicyclo and epoxy functionalized lignin in water, transferring to reaction vessel, reacting to room temperature for 7-8 hours, vacuum filtering, washing with water, drying to 50-55 degrees C to obtain reaction product choline chloride functionalized lignin, placing aromatic acid and choline chloride functionalized lignin into reaction container, reacting to 75-80 degrees C for 120-130 minutes, adding water, continuously reacting to 75-80 degrees C for 16-18 hours, vacuum filtering, washing by water, and drying to 50-55 degrees C.
   USE - The method is used for preparing functionalized lignin loaded low melting solvent heterogeneous catalyst.
   ADVANTAGE - The product solves problems of traditional catalysts that cannot be regenerated and not be degraded.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for application of functionalized lignin loaded low melting solvent heterogeneous catalyst in carbon dioxide chemical conversion comprising adding epoxy compound, tetrabutyl ammonium bromide and functionalized lignin loaded low melting solvent heterogeneous catalyst in high-pressure reaction kettle, connecting high pressure kettle with carbon dioxide bottle, introducing carbon dioxide, placing reaction kettle in oil bath stirrer, reacting at 110-120 degrees C for 3-5 hours, adding solvent into reaction kettle, dissolving, filtering to obtain filtrate, rotary evaporating filtrate to obtain crude product, separating by column chromatography, and drying to obtain cyclic carbonate compound.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The dosage of tetrabutyl ammonium bromide is 1.0-1.1 mmol.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The mass ratio of lignin and sodium hydroxide is 1.0-1.1:0.8-0.9. The mass ratio of lignin and epoxy chloropropane is 1.0-1.1:8-10. The mass ratio of lignin and water is 1.0-1.1:5-6. The mass ratio of epoxy functionalized lignin and choline chloride is 1.0-1.1:2.0-2.5. The mass ratio of choline chloride and diazabicyclo-is 8.0-9.0:1.0-1.2. The mass ratio of epoxy functionalized lignin and water is 1.0-1.1:20.0-25.0. The mass ratio of choline chloride functionalized lignin and aromatic acid is 1.0-1.1:1.0-1.1. The mass ratio of choline chloride functionalized lignin and water is 1.0-1.1:20.0-25.0. The aromatic acid is benzoic acid, p-toluic acid, p-ethyl benzoic acid, p-nitrobenzoic acid or p--aminobenzoic acid. The epoxy compound is epoxy propyl ether, o-toluene glycidyl ether, styrene oxide, epichlorohydrin, glycidol, allyl glycidyl ether or butyl glycidyl ether. The dosage of epoxy compound is 10.0-10.5 mmol. The solvent is ethyl acetate, and has dosage of 20-25 mL. The cyclic carbonate compound is propyl phenyl cyclic carbonate, diglycidyl o-cyclic carbonate, styrene cyclic carbonate, chloropropane cyclic carbonates, glycidyl cyclic carbonate, allyl glycidyl ether cyclic carbonate or butyl glycidyl ether carbonate. Preferred Method: The pressure for introducing carbon dioxide is 1.0-1.2 MPa.
DC E13 (Heterocyclics.); J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).); A97 (Miscellaneous goods not specified elsewhere - including papermaking, gramophone records, detergents, food and oil well applications.)
MC E07-A04; E11-B01; E11-F02B; E11-K03; E11-W; J04-E04; J04-E11; A03-C02; A08-S02; A10-E08C; A10-E21; A10-E23; A12-W11K; N01-A01; N05-D; N05-E03; N07-D02B; N07-F04; N07-K01
IP B01J-031/06; C07D-317/36

PT P
PN JP2020050574-A
TI Carbon monoxide gas separation device has separation devices for adsorbing carbon dioxide and carbon monoxide gas, recycle gas derivation pipe, pressurizing unit, recycle gas introduction pipe and carbon monoxide gas derivation pipe.
AU NAKADA T
   NIDA Y
   TANAKA M
   SUENAGA J
AE AIR WATER INC (AIAR-C)
GA 2020250617
AB    NOVELTY - Carbon monoxide gas separation device (100) has a first separation device (50) for adsorbing and removing at least carbon dioxide and water in the raw material gas to obtain a first purified gas, a second separation device (70) for adsorbing at least carbon monoxide gas in a first mixed gas that is a gas containing at least the first purified gas, a recycle gas derivation pipe (61) for deriving a recycle gas, which is a gas containing carbon monoxide gas, from the second separation device, a pressurizing unit (60) for pressurizing the recycle gas led out through the recycle gas deriving pipe to obtain a pressurized recycle gas, a recycle gas introduction pipe (62) for introducing the pressurized recycle gas upstream of the second separation device, and combining the pressurized recycle gas with the first purified gas to obtain the first mixed gas, and a carbon monoxide gas derivation pipe (66) for deriving the carbon monoxide gas adsorbed by the second separation device.
   USE - The carbon monoxide gas separation device is used for separating carbon monoxide gas from raw material gas containing at least carbon monoxide gas, carbon dioxide gas, nitrogen gas, and water.
   ADVANTAGE - The device improves the yield of carbon monoxide gas and suppresses the generation of hydrogen chloride.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for separating carbon monoxide gas using the above-mentioned device.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the carbon monoxide gas separation device.
   Source gas introduction piping (40)
   First vacuum pump (41)
   First purified gas derivation pipe (42)
   First separation device (50)
   Pressurizing unit (60)
   Recycle gas derivation pipe (61)
   Recycle gas introduction pipe (62)
   Carbon monoxide gas derivation pipe (64)
   Second vacuum pump (65)
   Carbon monoxide gas purge pipe (66)
   Second separation device (70)
   Carbon monoxide gas separation device (100)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - CHEMICAL ENGINEERING - Preferred Device: The first separation device and the second separation device include a pressure swing adsorption device (PSA).
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The first separation device comprises at least one of zeolite, activated alumina, and activated carbon as an adsorbent for adsorbing and removing carbon dioxide gas and water. The second separation device comprises at least one of copper chloride-supported zeolite, copper chloride-supported activated alumina, and copper chloride-supported activated carbon as an adsorbent for adsorbing carbon monoxide gas. The raw material gas is exhaust gas discharged from a blast furnace.
DC E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); J01 (Separation - including evaporation, crystallisation, solvent extraction, chromatography, dialysis, osmosis including drying gases and/or vapours, and separation of solids from gases, liquids and other solids. Isotope separation, filter materials (including molecular sieves for separation), and centrifuges (except where used for analysis) (B01D, B03, B04, B07B).)
MC E11-Q02C; E31-N04C; E31-N05B1; E31-P02B; E34-C02; E35-A; J01-E02B3; J01-E03C3

PT P
PN CN110681868-A
TI Preparing high-performance forging and rolling manganese involves putting metal manganese in induction melting furnace, controlling melting temperature, and refining molten manganese liquid to remove upper scum.
AU WANG G
   WANG Z
   YANG J
AE WANG Z (WANG-Individual)
GA 202007465A
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing high-performance forging and rolling manganese involves putting the metal manganese in the induction melting furnace, controlling the melting temperature at 1350-1500 degrees C, and refining the molten manganese liquid to remove the upper scum. The pure carbon dioxide gas is passed into the atomizer. The molten manganese liquid is added into the atomizer to atomize into small droplets in the atomization chamber. The carbon dioxide gas is passed into the venturi duster. The ultra-fine metal manganese powder is added into the venturi duster. The sprayer is sprayed into the atomization chamber. The cooling coil and water-cooled jacket are used to adjust the temperature of the atomization chamber and control the upper temperature of the atomization chamber to 400-600 degrees C. The temperature of the lower portion of the atomizing chamber is controlled to 200-400 degrees C. The atomizer carbon dioxide gas pressure is controlled to 0.8-4MPa. The powder particle size is controlled to 300-500 mu m.
   USE - Method for preparing high-performance forging and rolling manganese.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to prepare high-performance forging and rolling manganese with low manganese oxide content and excellent product performance in an eco-friendly and cost-effective manner.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - The metal manganese powder and binder are mixed in a mixing mixer. The mixture is pressed into a forged or rolled manganese ball or a forged or rolled manganese pillow by a forging press or a roller press. The density after pressing is 4-6g/cm3, the weight of each finished product is 250-400g after drying and consolidation, and packed in tons or iron buckets.
DC M25 (Production and refining of metals other than iron - including ore treatment, extraction, working up scrap, obtaining specific metals, control testing methods (C22B).); M22 (Casting; powder metallurgy - including foundry moulding, moulding machines, patterns, moulds, cores and metal casting (B22).); P53 (Metal casting, powder metallurgy (B22).); X25 (Industrial Electric Equipment)
MC M22-H01; M22-H02; M25-A01; M25-G17; M25-H; P53-C01; P53-C03; P53-V05E; X25-A02C; X25-B02A2; X25-B04; X25-C03; X25-C05
IP B22F-001/00; B22F-003/17; B22F-003/18; B22F-009/08; C22B-047/00

PT P
PN CN110218583-A; CN210367562-U
TI Desulfurized transformation technology based integrated coal gasification fuel cell power generation system, has cathode regenerator whose hot side outlet is fixed to inlet of air turbine, where outlet of turbine is fixed to inlet of boiler.
AU ZHOU X
   PENG S
   XU S
   ZHONG D
   WANG B
AE CHINA HUANENG CLEAN ENERGY RES INST (CHHN-C)
GA 201981228J
AB    NOVELTY - The system has a coal preparation unit (1) provided with a raw coal inlet. A dry coal powder outlet of the coal preparation unit is connected to an inlet of a gasification furnace (2) that is provided with a carbon dioxide inlet and an oxygen inlet. The gasification furnace is connected to an inlet of a primary waste heat boiler (3). A saturated steam outlet of the waste heat boiler is connected to an inlet of the secondary waste heat boiler (27). A cathode outlet of a fuel cell (19) is connected with a heat side inlet of a cathode heat regenerator (24). A hot side outlet of the cathode regenerator is connected to an inlet of an air turbine (25). An outlet of the air turbine is connected to an inlet of the secondary waste heat boiler.
   USE - Desulfurized transformation technology based integrated coal gasification fuel cell power generation system.
   ADVANTAGE - The system avoids the removal of a large amount of carbon dioxide in the conventional absorption desulfurization process, and improves the carbon dioxide capture rate, and avoids the process of changing the energy generated by the reaction needs to be reduced, and the heat generated by the conversion reaction process can enter into a fuel cell system so as to improve the net power generation efficiency of the system.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a front view of a desulfurized transformation technology based integrated coal gasification fuel cell power generation system. '(Drawing includes non-English language text)'
   Coal preparation unit (1)
   Gasification furnace (2)
   Primary waste heat boiler (3)
   Fuel cell (19)
   Cathode heat regenerator (24)
   Air turbine (25)
   Secondary waste heat boiler (27)
DC H09 (Fuel products not of petroleum origin - excluding coal handling, preparation or mining, but including coking, briquetting, peat processing synthesis, gas production, coal gasification. Combustion improvement additives for coal, peat and other nonhydrocarbon based fuels are included in this Section together with coal liquefaction and desulphurisation.); Q52 (Reaction engines: external combustion; gas turbines; rockets (F01D, F02C, F02K, F02M)); X11 (Power Generation and High Power Machines); X16 (Electrochemical Storage)
MC H09-C; Q52-A; X11-A01; X11-A08; X11-C01; X11-C08; X16-C

PT P
PN WO2019118887-A1; US2019185797-A1; US10900004-B2; JP2021506338-W; GB2587874-A; US2021261896-A1
TI Preparing hydrocarbon-based polymeric structural elements, by separating hydrocarbon-based organism from aqueous mixture, forming polymeric compound into resin, heating and extruding resin for generating three-dimensional structure.
AU TOMPKINS D
AE DIY SERVICE LLC (DIYS-Non-standard)
   TOMPKINS D (TOMP-Individual)
   DIY SERVICE LLC (DIYS-Non-standard)
   TOMPKINS D (TOMP-Individual)
   DIY SERVICE LLC (DIYS-Non-standard)
   TOMPKINS D (TOMP-Individual)
GA 201951871X
AB    NOVELTY - Method (M1) of preparing hydrocarbon-based polymeric structural elements, involves providing a bioreactor including an aqueous mixture and a concentration of atmospheric gases, with a hydrocarbon-based organism disposed within the aqueous mixture, separating the hydrocarbon-based organism from the aqueous mixture, forming a first compound by crushing, hydrolyzing, and dehydrating the hydrocarbon-based organism, reacting the first compound to produce a first set of monomeric units, reacting the first set of monomeric units with a second set of monomeric units to produce a polymeric compound, forming the polymeric compound into a resin, and heating the resin and extruding the resin to form a hydrocarbon-based filament for generating a three-dimensional structure through an additive manufacturing machine.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing hydrocarbon-based polymeric structural elements, preparing habitable hydrocarbon-based three-dimensional structure, or preparing habitable hydrocarbon-based three-dimensional structure in an environment having low pressure and a high concentration of carbon dioxide. The three-dimensional structure is chosen from translucent films, plates, sheets, panels, other planar structures, and their combinations (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method can be used to create a habitable environment in harsh conditions associated with high concentrations of carbon dioxide and low pressure without need to use external non-renewable resources and instead using renewable in-situ resources, thus improving viability of habitation within the environment of the manufactured three-dimensional structures.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) a method (M2) of preparing habitable hydrocarbon-based three-dimensional structure; and
   (2) a method (M3) of preparing habitable hydrocarbon-based three-dimensional structure in an environment having low pressure and a high concentration of carbon dioxide.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Components: The atmospheric gases include at least 10% carbon dioxide and less than 3% oxygen. The first compound is chosen from 5-hydroxymethylfurfural, dimethylfurfural, furan, and p-xylene. The first set of monomeric units is chosen from furandicarboxylic acid, succinic acid, itaconic acid, and their esters, and diesters. The second set of monomeric units is chosen from ethylene glycol, 1,4-butanediol, 1,3-propanediol, 1,2-propanediol, and their esters, and diesters. The resin further comprises a material chosen from poly-(butylene-succinate), poly-(butylene-2,5-furanate), poly-(butylene-itaconate), poly-(propylene-succinate), poly-(propylene-2,5-furanate), poly-(propylene-itaconate), and their mixtures. Preferred Microorganisms: The hydrocarbon-based organism is algae. Preferred Method: The biological reactor is operated at a pressure approximately equal to a pressure of an ambient environment surrounding the bioreactor.
DC A32 (Polymer fabrication - such as moulding, extrusion, forming, laminating, spinning.); A97 (Miscellaneous goods not specified elsewhere - including papermaking, gramophone records, detergents, food and oil well applications.); D16 (Fermentation industry - including fermentation equipment, brewing, yeast production, production of pharmaceuticals and other chemicals by fermentation, microbiology, production of vaccines and antibodies, cell and tissue culture and genetic engineering.); P13 (Plant culture, dairy products (A01G, H, J).); X25 (Industrial Electric Equipment)
MC A01-E; A01-E12; A05-D02A; A05-E02A; A10-D05; A11-B16; D05-C21; P13-E99; X25-A08A; X25-A08B; X25-A08C2; X25-A08M2
IP A01G-033/00; B29C-064/10; B33Y-010/00; B33Y-080/00; B33Y-099/00; C12N-001/02; C12N-001/12; B29C-064/118; B29C-064/357; C12M-001/107; B29K-067/00; B33Y-040/00; B33Y-070/00; C12M-001/00; C12M-001/26; C12P-007/40; C12P-007/44

AU OKUNO S
   NITORI H
   TSUNOGAE Y
AE NIPPON ZEON KK (JAPG-C)
   ZEON CORP (JAPG-C)
GA 2019476133
AB    NOVELTY - A rubber crosslinked material comprises crosslinked polymer composition containing 20-200 pts. wt. carbon black with respect to 100 pts. wt. rubber components containing cyclopentene ring-opened polymer which has a branched structure, where material has compression set rate of less than or equal to 35% after holding at 100 degrees C for 72 hours in a state of 25% compression.
   USE - A rubber crosslinked material for sealing materials, such as O-ring, packing, diaphragm, oil seal, shaft seal, bearing seal, wellhead seal, shock absorber seal, seal for pneumatic equipments, seal for sealing freon, fluorohydrocarbon, or carbon dioxide used for compressor for refrigerators of cooling device of air conditioner, or an air conditioner, seal for sealing supercritical carbon dioxide used for washing medium of precision cleaning or subcritical carbon dioxide, seal for rolling apparatus (rolling bearing, hub unit for motor vehicles, water pump for motor vehicles, linear guide apparatus, ball screw, etc.), valve, valve seat, blow out preventer (BOP), bladder; intake manifold gasket with which connection portion of intake manifold and cylinder head is mounted, cylinder head gasket with which connection portion of cylinder block and cylinder head is mounted, rocker-box-cover gasket with which connection portion of rocker box cover and cylinder head is mounted, oil-pan gasket with which connection portion with oil pan, cylinder block, or gearbox casing is mounted, gaskets mounted between a pair of housings which inserts a unit cell provided with positive electrode, electrolyte board, and negative electrode, such as gasket for fuel cell separators, and gasket for top covers of a hard-disk drive.
   ADVANTAGE - The material has excellent compression set-proof property.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Compositions: Cyclopentene ring-opened polymer which has branched structure has a branched structure on the basis of the branched structure units of formulae (I)-(IV), where in each unit, the hydrogen atom may be substituted by 1-6 carbon-containing (1-6C) alkyl group..

PN CN109647343-A
TI Multi-active adsorption site metal-organic            framework composite material useful for carbon dioxide            adsorption and separation, comprises assembled            polyamine ionic liquid and metal-organic framework            material.
AU GUAN G
   WAN H
   CHEN C
   FENG N
   GUO Q
   LI Z
AE UNIV NANJING TECHNOLOGY (UNTY-C)
GA 2019394737
AB    NOVELTY - Multi-active adsorption site metal-organic                framework composite material, comprises assembled                polyamine ionic liquid and metal-organic framework                material, where the mass loading of the polyamine                ionic liquid is 10-35 wt.%, the polyamine ionic                liquid achieves the load by the coordination                between the lone pair of electrons in the nitrogen                atom and the unsaturated metal center in the                metal-organic framework material.
   USE - The multi-active adsorption site metal-organic                framework composite material is useful for carbon                dioxide adsorption and separation (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The framework composite material: contains                multiple active adsorption sites, which improves                carbon dioxide adsorption performance under normal                (low) pressure; greatly reduces amount of ionic                liquid, and mass transfer resistance during the                adsorption process; shows good application                potential in the field of gas adsorption                separation.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for                preparing multi-active adsorption site                metal-organic framework composite material,                comprising (1) adding metal salt and the organic                ligand separately to the solvent, mixing and                stirring uniformly, transferring to reaction vessel                of polytetrafluoroethylene liner, allowing to                stand, reacting at constant temperature of 100-220                degrees C for 8-72 hours, naturally cooling after                the reaction, centrifuging, washing and vacuum                drying to obtain solid product, i.e. metal-organic                framework material, and (2) dissolving polyamine                ionic liquid in polar solvent, adding metal-organic                framework material under stirring, ultrasonically                treating to disperse it, placing the resulting                mixture at 25-80 degrees C and continuing stirring                reaction for 6-24 hours, after the reaction is                completed, filtering off the solid with suction and                washing with polar solvent and drying in vacuum to                obtain multi-active adsorption site metal-organic                framework composite material.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The polyamine ionic                  liquid contains at least two amino functional                  groups. The cation of the polyamine ionic liquid                  is 1-aminoethylimidazole,                  1-aminoethyl-3-methylimidazole,                  1-aminoethyl-3-butylimidazole,                  1-aminoethyltriethylenediamine,                  diethylenetriamine or tetraethylene pentamine,                  and the anion is halogen ion, amino acid root or                  organic carboxylate. The metal-organic framework                  material is HKUST-1, copper-BDC, MIL-100 (iron),                  MIL-101 (iron), MIL-53 (iron), MIL-100                  (chromium), MIL-101 (chromium), MIL-53                  (chromium), MOF-808 (zirconium) or                  UiO-66(zirconium). In the step (1), the metal                  salt is copper nitrate trihydrate, ferric                  chloride hexahydrate, chromium nitrate or                  zirconium chloride, the organic ligand is                  1,3,5-benzenetricarboxylic acid or 1,4-phthalic                  acid, the solvent is ethanol, deionized water,                  N,N'-dimethylformamide, hydrofluoric acid or                  formic acid. In the step (1), the mass ratio of                  metal salt, organic ligand and the solvent is                  1:(0.4-0.7):(10-55). In the step (2), the polar                  solvent is methanol, ethanol, acetonitrile or                  dichloromethane. Preferred Method: In the step                  (2), the mass ratio of metal-organic framework                  material, the polyamine ionic liquid and the                  polar solvent is 1:(1-3):(30-80), the number of                  washings of the solid product is 3-6 times, the                  vacuum drying temperature is 60-100 degrees C,                  and the drying time is 12-48 hours.
DC E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); J01 (Separation - including evaporation, crystallisation, solvent extraction, chromatography, dialysis, osmosis including drying gases and/or vapours, and separation of solids from gases, liquids and other solids. Isotope separation, filter materials (including molecular sieves for separation), and centrifuges (except where used for analysis) (B01D, B03, B04, B07B).); A97 (Miscellaneous goods not specified elsewhere - including papermaking, gramophone records, detergents, food and oil well applications.)

PN US2019039102-A1
TI Removing soil from industrial surface using a clean-in-place process, comprises applying pretreatment solution comprising carbon dioxide-producing salt and allowing to penetrate, applying override use solution comprising acid to surface.
AU FERNHOLZ P J
   ERICKSON A W
AE ECOLAB USA INC (ECON-C)
GA 201913824L
AB    NOVELTY - Removing (A) a soil from an industrial surface using clean-in-place (CIP) process, comprises (a) applying a pretreatment solution comprising 0.2-3 wt.% of a carbon dioxide-producing salt to the surface, (b) allowing the pretreatment solution to penetrate the soil, (c) applying an override use solution comprising 1-3 wt.% of an acid to the surface, where the application of the override use solution activates the pre treatment pretreatment solution to generate gas on and in the soil and (d) rinsing the surface where the pretreatment and override solutions are applied at 2-50 degrees C.
   USE - The method is useful: for removing soil from industrial surface (claimed) and removing other non-thermally degraded soils; and to clean hard surfaces, e.g., walls, floors, flatware, pots and pans, heat exchange coils, ovens, fryers, smoke houses, sewer drain lines, and vehicles and clean textiles, fabric, and carpets and clean laundry.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: utilizes the generating use solution that is capable of releasing gas on and in a soil, resulting in a soil disruption effect and enhanced cleaning and provides additional benefits of flavor destruction and antimicrobial effects; avoids the rinse step between the application of the pretreatment solution and the override use solution; and achieves 100% soil removal at 52 minutes.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for removing (IA) soil from an industrial surface using a CIP process, comprising applying pretreatment solution comprising 1-3 wt.% of acid to the surface, step (b) as above per se, applying an override use solution comprising 0.2-3 wt.% of a carbon dioxide-producing salt, where the application of the override use solution activates the pretreatment solution to generate gas on and in the soil and step (d) as above per se.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The pretreatment solution contacts the surface for 1-20 minutes preferably 10 minutes. Preferred Components: The soil comprises a thermally degraded soil. The soil comprises a high density organic soil. The soil is tomato based food soil, a food soil containing high levels of reducing sugars, or brewery soils. The surface is tanks, lines or processing equipment. The processing equipment cleaned is pasteurizer, a homogenizer, a separator, an evaporator, a filter, a dryer, a membrane, a fermentation tank or cooling tower. The processing equipment is processing equipment used in the dairy, cheese, brewing, beverage, food, biofuel, sugar, or pharmaceutical manufacturing industries. The surface is floors, walls, dishes, flatware, pots and pans, heat exchange coils, ovens, fryers, smoke houses, sewer drain lines, or vehicles. The carbon dioxide producing salt comprises a carbonate salt, bicarbonate salt, percarbonate salt and/or sesquicarbonate salt. The carbonate salt is sodium carbonate, potassium carbonate, lithium carbonate, ammonium carbonate, calcium carbonate, magnesium carbonate and/or propylene carbonate. The bicarbonate salt is sodium bicarbonate, potassium bicarbonate and/or ammonium bicarbonate. The percarbonate salt is sodium percarbonate, lithium percarbonate and/or potassium percarbonate. The sesquicarbonate salt is sodium sesquicarbonate, potassium sesquicarbonate and/or lithium sesquicarbonate. The acid is phosphoric acid, nitric acid, hydrochloric acid, sulfuric acid, acetic acid, citric acid, lactic acid, formic acid, glycolic acid, sulfamic acid, methanesulfonic acid and/or its derivative. The override use solution lowers the pH to less than 7.5.
DC D25 (Detergents - other than soap and used for cleaning (C11D)); P43 (Sorting, cleaning, waste disposal (B06, B07, B08, B09).)

AE UNIV SICHUAN (USCU-C)
GA 201913315P
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing trifluoromethyl spiro indole or acetal compound comprises substituting indole substrate i.e. (1H-indol-3-yl)methanamine compound (I) or substituted furan substrate i.e. furan-2-ylmethanamine compound (II) in presence of carbon dioxide, copper catalyst, base and trifluoromethylating agent, reacting in solvent to obtain final product, where trifluoromethyl spiro indole is (2S,3S)-3-(aminomethyl)-2-(trifluoromethyl)indolin-3-yl formate compound (III), and trifluoromethyl spiro acetal is (5S,7S)-7-(trifluoromethyl)-1,6-dioxa-3-azaspiro(4.4)non-8-en-2-one compound (IV).
   USE - The method is useful for preparing trifluoromethyl spiro indole or acetal compound.
   ADVANTAGE - The product has high chemical, regional and diastereomeric selectivity, excellent functional group compatibility, wide substrate range and achieved gram-scale synthesis. The method has mild conditions and is environmentally-friendly.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing trifluoromethyl spiro indole or acetal compound comprises substituting indole substrate i.e. (1H-indol-3-yl)methanamine compound of formula (I) or substituted furan substrate i.e. furan-2-ylmethanamine compound of formula (II) in presence of carbon dioxide, copper catalyst, base and trifluoromethylating agent, reacting in solvent to obtain final product, where trifluoromethyl spiro indole is (2S,3S)-3-(aminomethyl)-2-(trifluoromethyl)indolin-3-yl formate compound of formula (III), and trifluoromethyl spiro acetal is (5S,7S)-7-(trifluoromethyl)-1,6-dioxa-3-azaspiro(4.4)non-8-en-2-one compound of formula (IV).
   R1 = H, alkyl, halo, nitro or alkoxy;
   R2 = H, halo, alkyl or aryl;
   R3 = pyrimidine, acetyl, pyridine or p-toluenesulfonyl;
   R4, R5 = H, alkyl, ester, olefin or thioether;
   R6 = tert-butyl, benzyl or alkyl substituted pyridyl; and
   n = positive integer not less than 1.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The molar ratio between substituted indole substrate or substituted furan substrate and catalyst is 50:1-5:1, preferably 10:1, molar ratio between substituted indole substrate or substituted furan substrate and base is 1:1-1:5, preferably 1:2 and molar ratio between substituted indole substrate or substituted furan substrate and trifluoromethylating agent is 1:1-1:5, preferably 1:1.75-1:2. The copper catalyst is inorganic copper catalyst and/or organic copper catalyst, inorganic copper catalyst is cuprous(I) chloride and cuprous(I) iodide and organic copper catalyst is cuprous(I) thiophene-2-carboxylate and/ or tetrakis(acetonitrile) copper(I) hexafluorophosphate. The base includes inorganic base and/or organic base, where inorganic base is potassium carbonate, potassium phosphate or cesium carbonate and organic base is 1,5-diazabicyclo(4.3.0)non-5-ene, 1,8-diazabicyclo(5.4.0)undec-7-ene, 1,1,3,3-tetramethylguanidine or 1,4-Diazabicyclo(2.2.2)octane. preferably 1,5-diazabicyclo(4.3.0)non-5-ene, 1,8-diazabicyclo(5.4.0)undec-7-ene or 1,1,3,3-tetramethylguanidine. The trifluoromethylating agent is Togni reagent or Umemoto reagent, where Togni reagent is 1-trifluoromethyl-3,3-dimethyl-1,2-benziodoxole or 1-trifluoromethyl-1,2-benziodoxol-3-(1h)-one preferably 1-trifluoromethyl-1,2-benziodoxol-3-(1h)-one. The solvent which dissolves substances involved in reaction is 1,2-dichloroethane, dichloromethane, N,N-dimethylformamide, acetonitrile and/or dimethyl sulfoxide, preferably 1,2-dichloroethane, dichloromethane or acetonitrile. Preferred Method: The method comprises reacting at room temperature under reaction pressure of 1 atm carbon dioxide.

PN CN109135354-A
TI Antibacterial and bacteriostatic ceramic coating comprises e.g. fumed silica, magnesium oxide, carbon dioxide, copper oxide, ammonium dihydrogen phosphate, lithium carbonate, imidazole, thiazole, polyolefin particles and gold bismuth.
AU XIONG Z
AE FOSHAN GAOMING PRODUCTIVITY PROMOTION (FOSH-Non-standard)
GA 201905600X
AB    NOVELTY - Antibacterial and bacteriostatic ceramic coating comprises 55-75 pts. wt. fumed silica, 40-45 pts. wt. magnesium oxide, 28-35 pts. wt. carbon dioxide, 16-20 pts. wt. copper oxide, 8-12 pts. wt. ammonium dihydrogen phosphate, 5-10 pts. wt. lithium carbonate, 13-19 pts. wt. imidazole, 9-15 pts. wt. thiazole, 20-60 pts. wt. polyolefin particles, 20-60 pts. wt. gold bismuth, 18-22 pts. wt. cyclodextrin, 6-13 pts. wt. cinnamaldehyde, 8-14 pts. wt. diatomaceous earth, 2-6 pts. wt. n-butyl lithium, 5-10 pts. wt. barium hydroxide, 9-18 pts. wt. surfactant, 10-18 pts. wt. anti-aging agent, 26-30 pts. wt. melamine, 16-22 pts. wt. succinic anhydride, 30-39 pts. wt. polyacrylic acid and 80-105 pts. wt. deionized water.
   USE - Used as antibacterial and bacteriostatic ceramic coating.
   ADVANTAGE - The ceramic surface coating has good mildew-proof effect and prolongs the service life.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for preparing the antibacterial and bacteriostatic ceramic coating comprising (i) adding carbon dioxide, copper oxide, ammonium dihydrogen phosphate, succinic anhydride, polyacrylic acid and deionized water into a stirring dish, heating to 88-92 degrees C, then stirring at a speed of 62-68 rotations/minute for 15-20 minutes, then pouring lithium carbonate, imidazoles, thiazoles, polyolefin particles, surfactants and anti-aging agents, uniformly mixing at a speed of 35-38 rotations/minute for 21-28 minutes, adding fumed silica, magnesia, antimony, cyclodextrin, cinnamaldehyde, diatomaceous earth, n-butyllithium and barium hydroxide, melting the melamine and stirring at a speed of 25-32 rotations/minute for 40-48 minutes; and (ii) pouring the coating of step (i) into a grinder and grinding at 68-72 degrees C for 11-18 hours.

TI Platinum-cobalt composite photothermal catalyst used in reformation of carbon dioxide and methane to form syngas, comprises platinum nanoparticles and cobalt nanoparticles supported on alumina.
AU YUN R
   WU Y
   GE H
   HUAQI G
AE UNIV FUZHOU (UFZU-C)
GA 2018A0703K
AB    NOVELTY - A platinum-cobalt composite photothermal catalyst comprises 0.5-3 %mass platinum nanoparticles and 0.6-0.8 %mass cobalt nanoparticles supported on alumina with respect to total mass of the composite catalyst.
   USE - Platinum-cobalt composite metal catalyst used in reformation of carbon dioxide and methane to form syngas (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The catalyst has excellent photothermal catalytic conversion efficiency, and helps in efficient and green conversion of carbon dioxide and methane under mild conditions with yield of 6400 mu mol.g-1.second-1 carbon monoxide and 7000 mu mol.g-1.second-1 hydrogen.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) preparation of the platinum-cobalt composite photothermal catalyst. The method involves preparing a 1 mM cobalt nitrate solution of cobalt nanoparticles, adding 60-80 mL cobalt nitrate solution to 50 mL ethanol solution, mixing and stirring for 10 minutes, adding 1-5 mL laurylamine solution, stirring for 5 minutes, adding 50 mL hexane solution and stirring for 2 minutes, leaving the solution still for 5 minutes, separating the upper layer solution in a beaker and stirring in a water bath at 50 degrees C for 30 minutes, resting the solution for 12 hours, adding 0.5-3 g alumina powder to the solution and stirring for 2 hours, and centrifuging, washing, drying, and calcining the solution at 200-300 degrees C for 2-3 hours to obtain cobalt nanoparticles-alumina composite solution, adding 10-90 mL platinum-containing salt solution comprising 1 mg/mL platinum to the obtained cobalt nanoparticles-alumina composite solution, stirring the mixture for 12 hours, and drying and calcining the mixture at 300-700 degrees C for 4-5 hours to obtain the platinum-cobalt-alumina composite photothermal catalyst; and
   (2) use of the platinum-cobalt composite photothermal catalyst.

PN WO2018163210-A2; IN201711008025-A; WO2018163210-A3; US2020010397-A1; EP3592724-A2; US10626074-B2; JP2020512310-W; EP3592724-A4; IN382146-B
TI Preparing 2,4,6-trifluoro-benzoic acid comprises e.g. contacting substituted 1,3,5-trifluoro-benzene with magnesium metal to give substituted (2,4,6-trifluorophenyl)magnesium hydride and then contacting with carbon dioxide.
AU SINGH A
   SINGH B
   KUMAR K
   ANAND R
   RAJDEEP A
AE SRF LTD (SRFS-Non-standard)
   SRF LTD (SRFS-Non-standard)
   SFR LTD (SFRS-Non-standard)
   SRF LTD (SRFS-Non-standard)
   SRF LTD (SRFS-Non-standard)
GA 2018718920
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing (P1) 2,4,6-trifluoro-benzoic acid (I) comprises either: (a) contacting substituted 2,4,6-trifluoro-benzoic acid compounds (VI) with an inorganic base and an inorganic sulfate to obtain substituted 1,3,5-trifluoro-benzene (V), contacting (V) with hydrogen in presence of a catalyst and a buffering agent to obtain 1,3,5-trifluoro-benzene (IV), (b) converting (IV) to substituted 1,3,5-trifluoro-benzene (III), (c) contacting (III) with magnesium metal to obtain substituted (2,4,6-trifluorophenyl)magnesium hydride (II), and (d) contacting (II) with carbon dioxide to obtain (I); or the steps (c) and (d) as per se.
   USE - The process is useful for preparing 2,4,6-trifluoro-benzoic acid.
   ADVANTAGE - The process is economic and simple.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing (P1) 2,4,6-trifluoro-benzoic acid of formula (I) comprises: (a) contacting substituted 2,4,6-trifluoro-benzoic acid compounds of formula (VI) with an inorganic base and an inorganic sulfate to obtain substituted 1,3,5-trifluoro-benzene of formula (V), contacting (V) with hydrogen in presence of a catalyst and a buffering agent to obtain 1,3,5-trifluoro-benzene of formula (IV); (b) converting (IV) to substituted 1,3,5-trifluoro-benzene of formula (III); (c) contacting (III) with magnesium metal to obtain substituted (2,4,6-trifluorophenyl)magnesium hydride of formula (II); and (d) contacting (II) with carbon dioxide to obtain (I). INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for:
   (1) preparing (P2) substituted 1,3,5-trifluoro-benzene (V) comprising contacting substituted 2,4,6-trifluoro-benzoic acid compounds (VI) with an inorganic base and inorganic sulfate to give (V); and
   (2) preparing (P3) 1,3,5-trifluoro-benzen (IV) comprising contacting (V) with hydrogen in presence of catalyst and buffering agent to give (IV).
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The inorganic base is alkali metal hydroxides, alkali earth hydroxides, metal carbonates and/or metal oxides, preferably lithium hydroxide, sodium hydroxide, potassium hydroxide, calcium hydroxide, lithium carbonate, potassium carbonate, sodium carbonate and ammonium carbonate and/or calcium oxide. The inorganic sulfate is sodium sulfate, potassium sulfate, ammonium sulfate and/or calcium sulfate. The catalyst is palladium, platinum, nickel, chromium dioxide and Raney nickel supported on alumina or activated carbon. The buffering agent is either a metal phosphate comprising diammonium phosphate, mono potassium phosphate, dipotassium phosphate, magnesium phosphate, silver phosphate, sodium monofluorophosphate, sodium phosphate, tripotassium phosphate, trisodium phosphate, lead(II) phosphate, lithium iron phosphate, manganese(II) phosphate, potassium titanyl phosphate or a metal acetates comprising sodium acetate, potassium acetate, lithium acetate, calcium acetate and/or magnesium acetate.
   EXAMPLE - 50 ml Tetrahydrofuran and 5.87 g magnesium were taken into a reaction vessel sequentially. The reaction mixture was heated to 40 degrees C and 47 g 1-bromo-2,4,6- trifluorobenzene solution was added drop wise at 30-35 degrees C. Reaction mass was stirred for 1 hour. Grignard reagent thus formed was cooled to 0-10 degrees C and carbon dioxide gas was purged into it. Then, the reaction mass was quenched with 10% 200 g aqueous hydrochloric acid and extracted with 200 g methyl tertiary butyl ether. The organic layer was concentrated and water was added to it. The pH was adjusted using base to dissolve the crude 2,4,6-trifluorobenzoic acid alkali salt and washed with dichloromethane. The aqueous layer was acidified with cone, hydrochloric acid till complete precipitation and precipitated product was filtered to give 2,4,6-trifluorobenzoic acid with 60% yield.
DC E14 (Aromatics - i.e. containing at least one benzene ring.); J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).)

PN CN108434375-A
TI Traditional Chinese medicinal composition useful for treating pelvic inflammatory disease, comprises chinaroot greenbrier, tuckahoe, safflower, selfheal, herba patriniae and sargentgloryvine stems.
AU YUE Z
AE HANGZHOU GUANGQI MEDICAL TECHNOLOGY DEV CO LTD (HANG-Non-standard)
GA 201868419V
AB    NOVELTY - Traditional Chinese medicinal composition comprises chinaroot greenbrier, tuckahoe, safflower, selfheal, herba patriniae and sargentgloryvine stems.
   USE - The traditional Chinese medicinal composition is useful for treating pelvic inflammatory disease.
   ADVANTAGE - The traditional Chinese medicinal composition prevents relapse.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for method for producing the composition, which involves (a) mixing chinaroot greenbrier and tuckahoe, crushing the mixture, passing through 30-50 mesh sieve, loading into a supercritical extraction kettle, setting pressure to 40-50 MPa, temperature to 55-65 degrees C, temperature of separation kettle (I) to 45-55 degrees C, temperature of separation kettle (II) to 38-42 degrees C and carbon dioxide flow rate to 18-22 l/hour, adding 9-11 wt.% entrainer (A), extracting the mixture for 1-3 hours, collecting extract, steaming, and obtaining powder (A), (b) mixing selfheal and herba patriniae, crushing the mixture, passing through 30-50 mesh sieve, loading into a supercritical extraction kettle, setting pressure to 40-50 MPa, temperature to 55-65 degrees C, temperature of separation kettle (I) to 45-55 degrees C, temperature of separation kettle (II) to 38-42 degrees C, carbon dioxide flow rate to 18-22 l/hour, adding 5-7 wt.% entrainer (B), extracting the mixture for 1-3 hours, collecting extract, steaming, and obtaining powder (B), (c) mixing safflower and sargentgloryvine stems, crushing the mixture, passing through 30-50 mesh sieve, loading into a supercritical extraction kettle, setting pressure to 40-50 MPa, temperature to 55-65 degrees C, temperature of separation kettle (I) to 45-55 degrees C, temperature of separation kettle (II) to 38-42 degrees C, carbon dioxide flow rate to 18-22 l/hour, adding 1-3 wt.% entrainer (C), extracting the mixture for 1-3 hours, collecting extract, steaming, and obtaining powder (C), and (d) mixing the powders (A)-(C).
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - PHARMACEUTICALS - Preferred Composition: The composition comprises 30-50 pts. wt., preferably 40 pts. wt. chinaroot greenbrier, 20-30 pts. wt., preferably 25 pts. wt. tuckahoe, 50-80 pts. wt., preferably 70 pts. wt. safflower, 60-90 pts. wt., preferably 70 pts. wt. selfheal, 30-40 pts. wt., preferably 35 pts. wt. pennycress and 20-30 pts. wt., preferably 25 pts. wt. sargentgloryvine stems. The composition further comprises Rubia filiformis. Preferred Method: The entrainer (A) is methanol or ethanol. The entrainer (B) is acetone. The entrainer (C) is palmitic acid or malic acid (tartaric acid is not acceptable).
   ACTIVITY - Antiinflammatory.
DC B04 (Natural products and polymers. Including testing of body fluids (other than blood typing or cell counting), pharmaceuticals or veterinary compounds of unknown structure, testing of microorganisms for pathogenicity, testing of chemicals for mutagenicity or human toxicity and fermentative production of DNA or RNA. General compositions.)
MC B04-A08; B04-A09; B04-A10; B04-A98; B14-C03; B14-N07; B14-S18

PT P
PN KR2018076380-A; KR2081812-B1
TI Purification system of high purity methane gas from biogas, has second degassing vessel that is provided for degassing residual absorbent of first degassing vessel under second pressure condition.
AU LEE P S
   PARK Y I
   NAM S E
   PARK H
   KIM J
   CHO Y H
   PARK A
AE KOREA RES INST CHEM TECHNOLOGY (KRIC-C)
GA 201855867S
AB    NOVELTY - The system has an absorption module (100) which comprises a hollow fiber separation membrane (101) which is provided for removing carbon dioxide contained in biogas into absorbent. A degassing section is provided for degassing the absorbent containing carbon dioxide. A degassing module (500) includes a hollow fiber separation membrane (501) which is provided for removing carbon dioxide contained in the residual absorbent of a second degassing vessel (450). The degassing section is a first degassing vessel (420) which is provided for degassing under a first pressure condition. The second degassing vessel is provided for degassing the residual absorbent of first degassing vessel under a second pressure condition. The first pressure condition is not less than the second pressure condition.
   USE - Purification system of high purity methane gas from biogas.
   ADVANTAGE - The highly pure methane can be recovered from the biogas at a high recovery rate. The composition of the mixed gas subjected to the primary degassing in the state of high methane content and low gas flow rate increases the methane content of the mixed gas when re-introduced into the absorption module, thus increasing the recovery rate of methane from the biogas. The flow rate of the mixed gas flowing into the absorption module is low, so that the process of increasing the recovery rate of methane without increasing the treatment capacity of the membrane contactor is performed.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a purification method of high purity methane gas from biogas.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the purification system of high purity methane gas from biogas.
   Absorption module (100)
   Hollow fiber separation membrane of absorption module (101)
   First degassing vessel (420)
   Second degassing vessel (450)
   Degassing module (500)
   Hollow fiber separation membrane of degassing module (501)
DC E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); J01 (Separation - including evaporation, crystallisation, solvent extraction, chromatography, dialysis, osmosis including drying gases and/or vapours, and separation of solids from gases, liquids and other solids. Isotope separation, filter materials (including molecular sieves for separation), and centrifuges (except where used for analysis) (B01D, B03, B04, B07B).)
MC E10-J02D1; E11-Q01B; E31-N05C; J01-E02B; J01-E03C; J01-E03E
IP B01D-053/14; B01D-053/22; B01D-053/75; B01D-063/02; B01D-069/08

PT P
PN CN107929374-A; CN107929374-B
TI Preparing traditional Chinese medicine useful for treating alzheimer's disease, comprises processing fleece-flower root, processing Cistanche, processing rhaponticum uniflorum, processing lotus leaf and mixing the pastes uniformly.
AU LU H
AE LIAOYUAN YULONG YADONG PHARM CO LTD (LIAO-Non-standard)
   ANHUI YULONG YADONG PHARM CO LTD (ANHU-Non-standard)
GA 201832061X
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing traditional Chinese medicine comprises (i) processing 20-40 pts. wt. fleece-flower root system into particles and obtaining a clear paste by supercritical carbon dioxide extraction, (ii) processing Cistanche, (iii) processing rhaponticum uniflorum, (iv) processing lotus leaf, (v) mixing the paste obtained in step (i), step (ii), step (iii) and step (iv) uniformly and spray drying to obtain dry-powder, (vi) processing earthworm and (vii) combining the dry powder obtained in step (v) and the dry paste obtained in step (vi), powdering, sieving and adding auxiliary materials.
   USE - The medicine is useful for treating alzheimer's disease (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The medicine has good curing rate.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing traditional Chinese medicine comprises (i) processing 20-40 pts. wt. fleece-flower root system into particles and obtaining a clear paste by supercritical carbon dioxide extraction, (ii) crushing 10-25 pts. wt. Cistanche into particles and obtaining clear cream by supercritical carbon dioxide extraction, (iii) crushing 5-20 pts. wt. rhaponticum uniflorum into particles and extracting using water under the action of ultrasonic waves to obtain a clear paste, (iv) taking 20-30 pts. wt. lotus leaf, adding boiling water, extracting and obtaining into a clear paste, (v) mixing the paste obtained in step (i), step (ii), step (iii) and step (iv) uniformly and spray drying to obtain dry-powder, (vi) taking 10-25 pts. wt. earthworm, homogenizing and extracting using ethanol to obtain a dry paste, (vii) combining the dry powder obtained in step (v) and the dry paste obtained in step (vi), powdering, sieving and adding auxiliary materials, where the raw material of the traditional Chinese medicine comprises 20-30 pts. wt. fleece-flower root, 20-25 pts. wt. lotus leaves, 10-18 pts. wt. Cistanche, 10-18 pts. wt. earthworm and 5-10 pts. wt. rhaponticum uniflorum.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - PHARMACEUTICALS - Preferred Components: In the step (vii), the auxiliary material is starch, dextrin, lubricant, polyethylene glycol 6000 and/or honey. The raw material of the traditional Chinese medicine comprises 20-40 pts. wt. fleece-flower root, 20-30 pts. wt. lotus leaves, 10-25 pts. wt. Cistanche, 10-25 pts. wt. earthworm and 5-20 pts. wt. rhaponticum uniflorum. Preferred Method: The step (i) comprises crushing the fleece-flower root into coarse particles of 5-30 mm, placing in a supercritical carbondioxide extraction tank for extraction, where the extraction temperature is 50-70 degrees C, the extraction pressure is 20-40Mpa and the 70-95% of ethanol as cosolvent, collecting the extract, concentrating under reduced pressure and removing the cosolvent to obtain a paste, where the amount of the cosolvent is 15-60 wt.% of the fleece-flower root. The step (ii), comprises crushing the Cistanche into coarse particles of 5-30 mm, placing in a supercritical carbondioxide extraction tank for extraction, where the extraction temperature is 40-60 degrees C, the extraction pressure is 20-40Mpa and the 50-75% of ethanol as cosolvent, collecting the extract, concentrating under reduced pressure and removing the cosolvent to obtain the extract, where the amount of the cosolvent is 15-60 wt.% of the Cistanche. The step (iii) comprises crushing the rhaponticum uniflorum into particles of 5-30 mm, extracting twice with water under ultrasonic wave, where the ultrasonic frequency is 20-80 kHz, adding 5-10 times the amount of water to the rhaponticum uniflorum, carrying out first extraction, where the extraction temperature is 40-60 degrees C and the extraction time is 20-40 minutes, adding 3-8 times the amount of water from the rhaponticum uniflorum, carrying out second extraction, where the extraction temperature is 40-60 degrees C and the extraction time is 20-40 minutes, combining the two extracts, filtering, vacuum extracting the filtrate and concentrating to a clear paste with a relative density of 1.20-1.30. The step (iv) comprises adding water of 8-15 times the weight of the lotus leaf, decocting and extracting for 1-3 hours, adding 6-12 times of water to the lotus leaves, decocting, extracting for 1-3 hours, combining two decoctions, concentrating the decoction to a relative density of 1-1.1 at 60 degrees C, cooling, adding 50-70% ethanol to reach alcohol content of 50-70%, refrigerating for 12-24 hours, filtering, concentrating the resulting filtrate under reduced pressure to obtain a paste with a relative density of 1.28-1.30 and recovering the ethanol to obtain a paste. In the step (v), mixing the paste obtained in step (i), step (ii), step (iii) and step (iv) uniformly and spray drying at an air inlet temperature of 100-160 degrees C to obtain dry-powder. In the step (v), crushing the earthworm into coarse powder, adding 3-6 times the amount of water to the ground dragon, homogenizing for 4-8 times, where he each time of homogenization is 10-30 minutes, combining the homogenate, adding ethanol to reach the alcohol content to 5-15%, centrifuging, adding ethanol to the supernatant to reach the alcohol content to 40-60%, centrifuging, washing the precipitate obtained by this centrifugation twice with acetone, centrifuging and vacuum drying the precipitate to obtain a dry paste.
   ACTIVITY - Neuroprotective; Nootropic.
DC B07 (General - tablets, dispensers, catheters (excluding drainage and angioplasty), encapsulation etc, but not systems for administration of blood or saline or IV feeding etc.); B04 (Natural products and polymers. Including testing of body fluids (other than blood typing or cell counting), pharmaceuticals or veterinary compounds of unknown structure, testing of microorganisms for pathogenicity, testing of chemicals for mutagenicity or human toxicity and fermentative production of DNA or RNA. General compositions.); A96 (Medical, dental, veterinary, cosmetic.)
MC B04-A08; B04-A09; B04-A10; B04-A98; B04-B04M6; B04-C02B4; B04-D01A; B12-M02B; B14-J01A4; B14-S18; A05-H03; A12-V01
IP A61K-035/62; A61K-036/704; A61K-009/48; A61P-025/28

   (D) extracting at 40-60 degrees C under                pressure of 30-40 megaPascals for 1.0-3.0 hours to                obtain carbon dioxide and the mixture under                supercritical conditions;

   (F) refining vegetable oil blend oil;
   (G) adding vegetable oil blend obtained by the                extraction to the molecular distillation sample                tank, adjusting the molecular distillation process                parameters, like vacuum degree 2-9 Pascals,                distillation temperature 100-150 degrees C,                scraping film rotating speed 100-300                revolutions/minute and condensing temperature 30-50                degrees C; and
   (H) refining to obtain desired vegetable blend                oil rich in multiple nutrition functional                ingredients.

GA 201805557M

   MI Y
   WU J

The frozen Haematococcus pluvialisraw                material is subjected to wall-breaking treatment to                obtain Haematococcus pluvialiswall-breaking                material, and then supercritical carbon dioxide is                used as an extraction solvent to perform                supercritical extraction of astaxanthin in                wall-breaking material of Haematococcus pluvialisto                obtain astaxanthin.
   USE - Method for extracting astaxanthin from                Haematococcus pluvialis.
   ADVANTAGE - The method extracts astaxanthin from                Haematococcus pluvialisin a green, environmentally                friendly and pollution-free way, which achieves                wall-breaking of Haematococcus pluvialisin better                manner, reduces temperature rise in process of                wall-breaking to maintain activity of astaxanthin                to greatest extent, and ensures food safety and                pure naturalness of end product.

TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Conditions: The raw material of                  Haematococcus pluvialis is frozen in a vacuum low                  temperature environment where air pressure is                  less than 10Pa and temperature is -50&#8451;                  to -70&#8451;. The raw material of                  Haematococcus pluvialis is frozen in a vacuum and                  low temperature environment, and then dried. The                  wall-breaking treatment of frozen Haematococcus                  pluvialis raw material includes high-pressure                  homogenization of frozen Haematococcus pluvialis                  raw material for one wall breaking. The method                  involves performing low-temperature freeze-drying                  treatment on a broken wall Haematococcus                  pluvialis material, and primary broken                  Haematococcus pluvialis material after low                  temperature freeze-drying treatment is subjected                  to high pressure homogenization and secondary                  wall breaking. The method further involves                  granulating Haematococcus pluvialis wall-breaking                  material to obtain granular Haematococcus                  pluvialis wall-breaking material, and performing                  vacuum dehydration on wall-breaking material of                  Haematococcus pluvialis. The particle size of                  granular Haematococcus pluvialis is 0.45mm. The                  supercritical extraction is performed at                  extraction pressure of 40MPa, extraction                  temperature of 40&#8451; and flow rate of                  carbon dioxide is 0.5L/minute for 150                  minutes.

PN US2022135413-A1
TI Preparing ammonia involves reacting lithium nitride with hydrogen sulfide to produce lithium sulfide and ammonia, reacting lithium with nitrogen to produce lithium nitride that is used as reactant, and extracting lithium from the lithium sulfide.
AU BACHMAN B
   HAMERS R
AE WISCONSIN ALUMNI RES FOUND (WISC-C)
GA 202260896M
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing ammonia involves reacting lithium nitride with hydrogen sulfide to produce lithium sulfide and ammonia. Lithium is reacted with nitrogen to produce the lithium nitride that is used as a reactant. The lithium is extracted from the produced lithium sulfide. The sulfur byproduct is produced. The sulfur byproduct is reacted with a feedstock comprising water, hydrogen, a carbon-containing composition, or both water and a carbon-containing composition to produce the hydrogen sulfide that is used as a reactant.
   USE - Method for preparing ammonia using a continuously operating chemical cycle.
   ADVANTAGE - The method helps in regenerating hydrogen sulfide from the elemental sulfur byproduct, minimize or effectively remove excess carbon dioxide, oxygen, and/or other unwanted byproducts from the system, or uses any excess carbon dioxide to prepare other useful products. The carbon oxygen molar ratio is carefully adjusted to minimize the production of other unwanted byproducts.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The feedstock used as a reactant is added to the interconnected reaction chambers or to the multi-loop chemical cycle from a source that is external to the interconnected reaction chambers or to the chemical cycle. The ammonia produced as a product is removed from the interconnected reaction chambers or from the multi-loop chemical cycle. The reaction is performed at less than 2.0 atmospheres of pressure, at less than 1.5 atmospheres of pressure, or at atmospheric pressure. The feedstock comprises water, but does not comprise a carbon-containing composition. The method does not generate carbon dioxide. Sulfuric acid byproduct is produced when sulfur byproduct is reacted with a feedstock. The feedstock comprises water, but does not comprise a carbon-containing composition. The method does not generate carbon dioxide. The mole ratio of C:O in the water and carbon-containing composition together is from 0.8:2 to 1.2:2, from 0.9:2 to 1.1:2, or from 0.95:2 to 1.05:2. The carbon dioxide byproduct is reacted with the ammonia to produce urea. The removed carbon dioxide byproduct is frozen, is sequestered, or is reacted with ammonia to produce urea. Preferred Components: The carbon-containing composition comprises one or more components selected from biomass, a hydrocarbon, a plastic, and an oxide of carbon.

TI Preparation of nano-calcium carbonate based on            control of surface potentialused for rubber, involves            adding calcium ion-containing salt solution in            saturated calcium hydroxide system to control surface            potential, and introducing carbon dioxide.
AU WANG Z
   DU P
   SONG N
   DAI Z
   ZHOU N
   SHANG D
AE ZHEJIANG ANLIAN MINING CO LTD (ZHEJ-Non-standard)
   UNIV ZHEJIANG (UYZH-C)
GA 202256063S
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of nano-calcium carbonate based on                the control of surface potential, involves adding                calcium ion-containing salt solution to the                saturated calcium hydroxide system to control the                surface potential, introducing carbon dioxide, and                reacting in a water bath.
   USE - Preparation of nano-calcium carbonate used for                rubber, plastic, paper making and adhesive                industries, and in preparation of inorganic filling                material.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to prepare nano-calcium                carbonate, which has high flow efficiency, and                stable crystal with high positive potential                mutually exclusive retaining system. The process is                simple, no pollution and high efficiency. The                method improves the surface potential of the                crystal surface, and accurately controls the                reaction condition to obtain the nano calcium                carbonate with uniform particles. The calcium ion                is introduced to keep the stability of the system,                at the same time, the introduced substance does not                damage the stability.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The treated saturated                  calcium hydroxide system is prepared by crushing                  and screening the calcium carbonate ore,                  calcining and decomposing into calcium oxide,                  digesting with water to obtain a supersaturated                  calcium hydroxide solution, and then filtering.                  After the water bath reaction, the reaction                  system is filtered to obtain a filtrate and a                  filter residue, the filtrate is supplemented with                  calcium oxide to form a saturated solution for                  repeated use, and the filter residue is washed                  and dried to obtain nano-calcium carbonate.Preferred Components: The calcium                  ion-containing salt solution is a solution of                  calcium chloride, calcium bromide or calcium                  nitrate.Preferred Composition: The molar ratio of                  calcium ions in the calcium ion-containing salt                  solution to calcium in the saturated calcium                  hydroxide system is 0.9-1.1:1.

PN CN114231360-A
TI Method for extracting cypress essential oil involves washing rhizomes and trunks of hinoki cypress, removing surface sand impurities and chopping into short wood chips, drying and pulverizing, and placing fine wood powder in critical carbon dioxide extraction device to extract essential oil.
AU ZHOU F
   NING M
   DONG J
AE ZHEJIANG KANGLAITE GROUP CO LTD (ZHEJ-Non-standard)
GA 202249232M
AB    NOVELTY - Method for extracting cypress essential oil involves washing rhizomes and trunks of hinoki cypress, removing surface sand impurities and chopping into short wood chips, using dryer to dry it to make its water content below 10% and pulverizing to (10-60) mesh particles after drying to obtain fine wood powder, placing the fine wood powder in critical carbon dioxide extraction device to extract essential oil, setting extraction temperature to (25-45)degrees Celsius, extraction pressure to (10-25) MPa and extraction time to (0.5-2) hours, to obtain crude oil, removing water by distillation under reduced pressure, and removing impurities by microporous membrane.
   USE - Method for extracting cypress essential oil.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is simple, improves extraction rate, and provides cypress essential oil with high purity.

PN CN113117032-A
TI Producing traditional Chinese medicinal composition used for relieving cough, by crushing e.g. radix stephaniae tetrandrine, fructus psoraleae and raw ephedra, subjecting to carbon dioxide supercritical extraction, mixing borneol, camphor and benzoin, filtering and mixing uniformly.
AU GAO G
   RAO H
   LI C
AE JIANGXI SHANDING TECHNOLOGY DEV CO LTD (JIAN-Non-standard)
GA 202186238G
AB    NOVELTY - Method for producing traditional Chinese medicinal composition involves weighing raw pinellia, radix stephaniae tetrandrine, fructus psoraleae, radix stemonae, fructus gleditsiae abnormalis, raw ephedra, Brassica alba boiss, Asarum, datura flower, borneol, camphor and benzoin, drying above-mentioned raw materials, crushing, mixing, soaking in purified water, subjecting to carbon dioxide supercritical extraction using first ethanol solution and chloroform as extracting agent to obtain extract, mixing and dissolving the borneol, camphor, benzoin and second ethanol solution, filtering to obtain mixed solution, and mixing the mixed solution, extract and purified water.
   USE - The method is useful for producing traditional Chinese medicinal composition which is useful in preparation of medicine for relieving cough and asthma and/or slowing lung fibrosis (claimed).
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for acupoint patch, which comprises carrier loaded with the composition.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - PHARMACEUTICALS - Preferred Method: The method involves weighing 10.5-17.5 pts. wt. raw pinellia, 10.5-17.5 pts. wt. radix stephaniae tetrandrine, 8-12 pts. wt. fructus psoraleae, 8-12 pts. wt. radix stemonae, 6-10 pts. wt. fructus gleditsiae abnormalis, 6-10 pts. wt. raw ephedra, 6-10 pts. wt. B.alba boiss, 6-10 pts. wt. Asarum, 4-8 pts. wt. datura flower, 1-5 pts. wt. borneol, 4-7 pts. wt. camphor and 4-7 pts. wt. benzoin. The extracting agent, chloroform and 90-99 vol.% first ethanol solution are in volume ratio of (86-90):(8-10):(2-4). The second ethanol solution has concentration of 70-80 vol.%. AE NACHENG XINGTAI NEW MATERIAL TECHNOLOGY (NACH-Non-standard)
GA 202172280U
AB    NOVELTY - Method for preparing aerogel composite fiber                material, involves (a) dispersing aerogel particles                in polymer solvent to obtain an aerogel dispersion,                (b) dissolving the polymer raw materials in polymer                solvent to obtain a polymer solution, (c) mixing                the polymer solution with the aerogel dispersion to                obtain a composite dispersion, and (d) spinning the                composite dispersion to obtain the composite fiber                material.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing aerogel                composite fiber material used for preparing thermal                insulation and flame-retardant materials and/or                carbon dioxide adsorption materials (claimed), and                used in high-performance fabrics, flame retardant                products and other military, industrial and                civilian fields.
