AB    NOVELTY - A carbon monoxide manufacturing apparatus (200) consists of a reverse shift catalyst which converts gas containing hydrogen, and carbon dioxide into carbon monoxide and water, and a reaction container comprising an adsorbent which adsorbing water.
   USE - Apparatus used for manufacturing carbon monoxide in chemical processing plant (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The apparatus manufactures carbon monoxide with high production rate at a low-temperature.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) a chemical plant, which consists of the carbon monoxide manufacturing apparatus, and utilizes the carbon monoxide-containing gas manufactured by Fischer-Tropsch reaction by the carbon monoxide manufacturing apparatus; and
   (2) a plant, which consists of the carbon monoxide manufacturing apparatus, and utilizes carbon monoxide for combustion in a boiler.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the carbon monoxide manufacturing apparatus. (Drawing includes non-English language text)
   Carbon monoxide manufacturing apparatus (200)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - CHEMICAL ENGINEERING - Preferred Apparatus: The apparatus (200) further consists a flow control apparatus for controlling flow volume of carbon dioxide-containing gas supplied to the reaction container, and hydrogen-containing gas such that the ratio of carbon dioxide and hydrogen is 1-4, a temperature regulating apparatus for heating or cooling the reaction container, a decompression device for pressure-reducing the inside of reaction container, piping for connecting the decompression device and the reaction container, and connected to the upper portion of the container, water electrolyzer for generating the hydrogen-containing gas, and another piping for circulating the carbon dioxide-containing gas and hydrogen-containing gas, and connected to the upper portion of the container, a temperature measuring device for measuring the temperature inside the reaction container, a pressurization apparatus for pressurizing the carbon dioxide-containing gas and hydrogen-containing gas supplied to the reaction container. The temperature regulating apparatus is a heat exchanger for heating carbon dioxide and hydrogen by heat exchanging with the gas for heating. The gas for heating is a combustion exhaust gas. The water-containing gas discharged from the decompression device is used as a heat source. The plant performs methanol synthesis by utilizing carbon monoxide-containing gas.

The adsorbent comprises silicon and/or aluminum, and zeolite. Zeolite comprises silicon, aluminum, lithium, potassium, sodium, calcium, and/or magnesium, and has crystal structure of A-type, X-type, or LSX-type zeolite. Preferred Composition: The weight ratio of the components (c1) and components (c2) is 0.1-30. Zeolite comprises silicon and aluminum in a ratio of 100 or less. Preferred Properties: Zeolite has pore diameter of less than 10 Angstrom , preferably more than 3 Angstrom to 4 Angstrom .

PN CN112723403-A
TI Preparing calcium carbonate whisker comprises e.g.            adding water to calcium oxide for digestion, sieving,            introducing carbon dioxide into potassium hydroxide            solution, stirring, aging, carrying out carbonation,            drying, crushing and sieving.
AU WEI J
   YAN G
   DU N
   LIN J
AE LIANZHOU KAIENSI NANOMATERIALS CO LTD (LIAN-Non-standard)
GA 202147414M
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing calcium carbonate whisker comprises                (1) adding water to the calcium oxide for digestion                reaction, sieving to obtain calcium hydroxide                suspension, (2) introducing the carbon dioxide into                the potassium hydroxide solution, until the pH of                the solution is reduced to less than 7 to obtain                the crystal control agent I, (3) adding the crystal                form control agent I of step (2) into the calcium                hydroxide suspension of step (1), stirring, aging,                then adding crystal form control agent II, crystal                form control agent II is strontium chloride, sodium                phosphate and sodium citrate, stirring, then                introducing lime kiln gas in the reaction liquid,                carrying out carbonation reaction until the pH of                the reaction liquid is reduced to less than 7,                dehydrating, drying, crushing and sieving to obtain                calcium carbonate whisker.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing calcium                carbonate whisker.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is simple, economical and easy to                realize industrial production.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The mass ratio of the                  calcium oxide and water is 1:5-1:10. The specific                  gravity of the calcium hydroxide suspension is                  1.070-1.085 g/cm3. The filling amount of the                  carbon dioxide is 0.8-1.2 l/minutes. In the step                  (2), the mass of the potassium hydroxide in the                  potassium hydroxide solution is 1-5% the mass of                  the calcium oxide. In the step (3), the aging                  time is 40-60 hours. In the step (3) the adding                  amount of the crystal form control agent II is                  1.5-4% of the mass of the calcium oxide. In the                  step (3) the volume fraction of carbon dioxide in                  the lime kiln gas is 30-35%. The filling amount                  of the lime kiln gas is 3-5 m3/h. In the step                  (3), the carbonation reaction is carried out at                  40-50 degrees C.

PN CN112679021-A
TI Manufacture of salt from seawater, involves            introducing seawater into filter tank to filter,            heating filtered seawater, adding sodium carbonate and            purifying agent, introducing carbon dioxide, filtering            filtrate, and heating.
AU LIU L
   LUO C
AE DANZHOU SECONDARY VOCATIONAL & TECH              SCHOOL (DANZ-Non-standard)
GA 202149254W
AB    NOVELTY - Manufacture of salt from seawater involves                (S1) introducing seawater into a filter tank to                filter, (S2) heating the filtered seawater to 30-40                degrees C, adding sodium carbonate and purifying                agent successively to continue heating, after                precipitating flocs, introducing carbon dioxide,                filtering the filtrate, under the condition of                heating and stirring, adding the sedimentation                agent to the filtrate, after heating to 70-85                degrees C, maintaining for 30-60 minutes, passing                in neutral gas, filtering to obtain filtrate, and                then passing the filtrate through reverse osmosis                membrane to obtain concentrated brine and fresh                water, (S3) adding 70-80 vol.% hydrochloric acid to                the concentrated brine, adjusting the pH to 2-3,                quickly cooling down after heating to reflux,                precipitating crystals, and vacuum filtering.
   USE - Manufacture of salt from seawater.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is simple and economical, enables                manufacture of salt from seawater with improved the                utilization rate of seawater, and has no pollution                in the whole process.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The filter tank                  comprises a layer (L1), a layer (L2), a layer                  (L3) in order from bottom to top. The layer (L1)                  is pebbles. The neutral gas is one of hydrogen,                  nitrogen, and oxygen. Preferred Properties: The                  layer (L2) is ceramsite having a particles size                  of 2.0-3.0 mm. The layer (L3) is mesoporous                  activated carbon having a pore size of 10-40 nm.                  Preferred Composition: The purifying agent is                  obtained by mixing silicic acid, sodium                  hydroxymethyl cellulose and lignin in a mass                  ratio of 5-7:1:3. The mass ratio of seawater,                  sodium carbonate and purifying agent is                  20-30:1:2. The sedimentation agent is obtained by                  mixing sodium carbonate and absolute ethanol in                  the mass ratio of 0.6-1:1. The mass ratio of the                  filtrate with respect to the sedimentation agent                  is 6-10:1. In the step (S2), the concentration of                  the concentrated brine is 26-28 degrees Be.                  Preferred Process: In the step (S3), the                  temperature is rapidly lowered after heating to                  reflux, which means that the temperature is                  rapidly lowered to 0-10 degrees C after heating                  to 80-100 degrees C and refluxing for 20-60                  minutes.

TI Preparing coral-like calcium carbonate with micro-nano composite structure involves mixing water, co-surfactant and surfactant according to volume ratio to prepare clear reverse microemulsion, where water phase is aqueous solution.

AE UNIV HEZHOU (UHEZ-C)
   UNIV LANZHOU JIAOTONG (UYLJ-C)
GA 202093672E

The water phase is an aqueous calcium chloride solution, the oil phase is cyclohexane, the co-surfactant is n-heptanol, and the surfactant is OP-10, passed carbon dioxide into the reverse microemulsion under agitation and reacted for a certain period of time until precipitation no longer occurs, then stopped passing carbon dioxide gas, ended the stirring, and at room temperature, aged, centrifugal separated, and dried the obtained precipitate to obtain the coral-like calcium carbonate.
   USE - Method for preparing coral-like calcium carbonate with micro-nano composite structure.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to prepare coral-like calcium carbonate with micro-nano composite structure has nano-micron double structure of lotus leaf surface and wide application prospect.

The carbon dioxide is introduced into the inverse microemulsion for reaction time of 30 minutes. The aging time is 24 hours. The drying treatment is drying in an oven is 80?C for 24 hours. The aqueous calcium chloride solution is prepared by weighing calcium chloride, then dissolve it with 25% ammonia water and a small amount of water and adding water to make a 0.5 mole/L solution.

PN CN111205205-A
TI Preparation of oxygen-18-labeled urea involves electrolyzing oxygen-18-labeled water, reacting with carbon powder, separating obtained gas at low temperature and reacting oxygen-18-labeled carbon monoxide and ammonia in presence of catalyst.
AU XIAO B
   LONG L
   QIN C
   LI H
   TIAN Y
   FANG H
   LIU Y
AE SHANGHAI CHEM IND RES INST CO LTD (SHNR-C)
   ANHUI HAISU NEW MATERIAL TECHNOLOGY CO (ANHU-Non-standard)
GA 202051469A
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of oxygen-18-labeled urea involves electrolyzing oxygen-18-labeled water, obtaining labeled oxygen gas, reacting with carbon powder, obtaining a mixed gas comprising oxygen-18-labeled carbon dioxide and oxygen-18-labeled carbon monoxide, performing low-temperature separation of mixed gas, and reacting oxygen-18-labeled carbon monoxide with ammonia in presence of a catalyst.
   USE - Preparation of oxygen-18-labeled urea.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables preparation of oxygen-18-labeled urea having high purity with high yield.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparation of oxygen-18(18O)-labeled urea involves electrolyzing oxygen-18-labeled water (H218O), obtaining labeled oxygen gas (18O2), reacting with carbon powder, obtaining a mixed gas comprising oxygen-18-labeled carbon dioxide (C18O2) and oxygen-18-labeled carbon monoxide (C18O), performing low-temperature separation of mixed gas, and reacting oxygen-18-labeled carbon monoxide with ammonia in presence of a catalyst.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The electrolysis is preferably carried out by placing oxygen-18-labeled water in electrolytic cell, adding one or more acid-alkali salt (s1) to improve conductivity, evacuating outlet air of positive and negative electrode side in the electrolytic cell, switching on power, controlling voltage to 12-24 V, and reacting. The reaction of labeled oxygen gas and carbon powder is carried out in a tube furnace at 600-1200 degrees C under inert gas atmosphere comprising argon and helium. The space velocity of labeled oxygen gas (18O2) is controlled to less than 1 L/L. The low-temperature mixed gas separation is performed at -80 degrees C to -120 degrees C. The reaction of oxygen-18-labeled carbon monoxide and ammonia is carried out in a high-pressure reactor at 70-150 degrees C and 0.2-2 MPa for 4-8 hours in presence of a catalyst and a solvent. The preparation of oxygen-18-labeled urea further involves subjecting the product to rotary evaporation and crystallizing using a solution comprising water and methanol in volume ratio of 1:1. Preferred Composition: The abundance of labeled oxygen (18O) in oxygen-18-labeled water is more than 97%. Component (s1) is one or more chosen from sodium sulfate, sodium hydroxide, potassium hydroxide, barium hydroxide, calcium hydroxide, lithium hydroxide, and sulfuric acid. The usage amount of component (s1) is 0.5-10 %mass. The carbon powder is obtained by calcining activated carbon powder having a purity of 99% or more at 110 degrees C for 12 hours. The mass ratio of labeled oxygen gas and carbon powder is 2:1. The volume ratio of labeled oxygen gas (18O2) and inert gas is 1:1.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The catalyst comprises iron powder, iron oxide, zinc powder, or sulfur powder.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Component: The solvent is ethanol, methanol or ethylene glycol.
DC A41 (Monomers and Condensants These are also included in Section E.); C04 (Fertilisers - including urea and phosphoric acid production. Also soil modifiers and plant growth media. Chemical aspects of compost production.); E16 (Aliphatics - containing N and/or halogen.); J03 (Electrochemical processes and electrophoresis - including ozone production, brine electrolysis, water electrolysis, production of chemical compounds and non-metallic elements, but excluding batteries or other means of producing power and the treatment of metals (C25B).)
MC A01-E03; C05-A04E; C10-A13C; E05-R; E10-A13B1; E11-E03; E11-F07A; J03-B; N02-A01; N03-F01; N04-C; N07-C03; N07-D08A

TI Rubber adhesive detecting method involves introducing rubber products containing metal into vulcanization tank, introducing carbon dioxide gas into vulcanization tank, and determining whether bubbles are present on rubber products.
AU MA X
AE JIAKE WUXI SEALING TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (JIAK-Non-standard)
GA 202025790X
AB    NOVELTY - Rubber adhesive detecting method involves introducing rubber products i.e. ethylene acrylic ester rubber material and nitrile rubber material containing metal into a vulcanization tank. Compressed gas i.e. carbon dioxide gas is injected into the vulcanization tank. The rubber product is taken out and allowed to stand for about 45 minutes to 2 hours. A determination is made to check whether bubbles are present on the rubber products. The rubber product is a qualified product when there are no bubbles, and the rubber product is an unqualified product when there are bubbles.
   USE - The method is useful for detecting rubber adhesive.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is simple, has no additional equipment cost, no additional testing procedures and no impact on product manufacturing, intuitive testing results, convenient and timely removal of defective products, ensures test results are intuitive, and removes defective products in a convenient manner.

AE UNIV NANJING SCI & TECHNOLOGY (UNSC-C)
GA 202022276M
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing iron-cobalt-potassium- zirconium dioxide catalyst comprises mixing iron salt, cobalt salt, potassium salt and zirconium dioxide to obtain a mixed precursor solution, performing a hydrothermal reaction at 120-160 degrees C for 12-24 hours, completing the reaction, centrifuging the mixture and drying to obtain an iron-cobalt-potassium zirconium dioxide catalyst, where the loading amount of potassium is 3-7wt.%. and the total of iron and cobalt loading amount is 3-7wt.%.

The loading amount of potassium is 5-7 wt.%. and the total loading amount of iron and cobalt is 3-5 wt.%.

PT P
PN IN201841049836-A; IN356814-B
TI Synthesizing clathrate hydrates such as methane hydrate, carbon dioxide hydrate, acetone hydrate and tetrahydrofuran hydrate, by condensing water vapor and guest molecule in ultrahigh vacuum chamber and gradually annealing condensed mixture.
AU PRADEEP T
   GHOSH J
   METHIKKALAM R R J
   BHUIN R G
   RAGUPATHY G
   CHOUDHARY N
   KUMAR R
AE INDIAN INST TECHNOLOGY MADRAS (IITM-C)
   INDIAN INST TECHNOLOGY MADRAS (IITM-C)
GA 202014323M
AB    NOVELTY - Process for synthesizing clathrate hydrates in interstellar environment at low pressure of 10-10 mbar and at cryogenic temperature of 10-135 K, involves (a) condensing the water vapor and guest molecule separately or together in a ultrahigh vacuum chamber and (b) gradually annealing the condensed mixture to a temperature below the desorption temperature of the guest molecule in vacuum for 0-48 hours. The gradual prolonged annealing of the condensed guest molecule and water vapor mixture to a temperature below the desorption temperature of the guest molecule enhances the molecular mobility and H-bonding interaction for the entrapment of molecule in in-situ, forming clathrate hydrate cages.
   USE - The process is used for synthesizing clathrate hydrates, preferably methane hydrate, carbon dioxide hydrate, acetone hydrate, and tetrahydrofuran hydrate (claimed), which are used as sources for relevant prebiotic molecules.
   ADVANTAGE - The process synthesizes clathrate hydrates with no external pressure to force cage formation.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The guest molecule includes methane, carbon dioxide, acetone and tetrahydrofuran. The clathrate hydrate includes methane hydrate, carbon dioxide hydrate, acetone hydrate, and tetrahydrofuran hydrate. Preferred Process: The methane hydrate is formed by gradual annealing of methane and water vapor mixture to 30 K for 25 hours. The carbon dioxide hydrate is formed by keeping of carbon dioxide and water vapor mixture to 10K for 48 hours. The acetone hydrate is formed by gradual annealing of acetone and water vapor mixture to 135 K, and the acetone hydrate transforms into cubic crystalline ice upon in-situ dissociation at 130-135 K over 3 hours. The tetrahydrofuran hydrate is formed by gradual annealing of tetrahydrofuran and water vapor mixture to 130 K for 6 hours. The hydrates formed are moved out of the vacuum system for subsequent use.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.
DC E17 (Other aliphatics.); E13 (Heterocyclics.); E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).)

TI Producing copper nanosheet, comprises heating            reaction solution comprising reducing agent and            surfactants to form a heated solution, and injecting a            hot solution of a copper-containing precursor.
AU CHEN S
   CHEN G
AE HONDA MOTOR CO LTD (HOND-C)
   HONDA MOTOR CO LTD (HOND-C)
   HONDA MOTOR CO LTD (HOND-C)
GA 2019A3661Q
AB    NOVELTY - Producing a copper nanosheet, comprises                heating a reaction solution comprising a reducing                agent and surfactants to a temperature of 280-330                degrees C to form a heated solution, and injecting                a hot solution of a copper-containing precursor                into the heated solution to form the copper                nanosheet.
   USE - The method is useful for producing a copper                nanosheet.
   ADVANTAGE - The method improves product selectivity.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included                for:
   (1) producing a carbon dioxide conversion                catalyst, comprising heating a reaction solution                comprising oleylamine and trioctylphosphine (TOP)                to a temperature of 280-330 degrees C to form a                heated solution, and injecting a hot solution of                copper tetradecylamine (Cu-TDA) into the heated                solution to form the copper nanosheet; and
   (2) carbon dioxide reduction catalyst, which                has a copper nanosheet, characterized by a                thickness of less than 100 nm, and a length of 40                nm-13 mu m.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The reducing agent is                  oleylamine, hexadecylamine or octadecylamine. The                  reducing agent is oleylamine. The surfactants                  have trioctylphosphine (TOP) and tetradecylamine                  (TDA). The purity of the TOP is not less than                  97%. The TOP is free of trioctylphosphine oxide                  (TOPO). The purity of the TOP is not less than                  90%. The molar ratio of TOP to the                  copper-containing precursor is 1.5:1-2.8:1,                  preferably 2.2:1. The molar ratio of TOP to                  copper-TDA is 1.8:1-2.8:1, preferably 2.2:1.                  Preferred Method: The copper-containing precursor                  is dissolved in octadecene or squalene. The                  reaction solution is heated to 300 degrees C. The                  copper-containing precursor is copper                  tetradecylamine (Cu-TDA). The method comprises                  reacting carbon dioxide with the carbon dioxide                  reduction catalyst.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

PN DE102018003513-A1
TI Method for operating two-stroke gas expansion engines in the running process sequence, preferably with carbon dioxide workflurid, involves performing saturated gas inlet volume via inlet valve in expansion space with movable working piston.
AU 
AE MCCLENAGHAN K (MCCL-Individual)
GA 2019901227
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves performing saturated gas inlet volume via an inlet valve in an expansion space with movable working piston. The inlet valve gas expansion is closed, which is preferably in the vicinity triple point. The expansion end injection of CO2 condensate with supersaturation of the cooled expansion gas in wet gas. The compression cycle of the CO2 wet gas above the boiling point in gas-liquid equilibrium with condensation up to the end of compression. The compression end with hydraulic condensate pressure displacement, which is above the gas inlet pressure at the inlet valve. The pressure loss compensation for external heat exchanger systems.
   USE - Method for operating two-stroke gas expansion engines in the running process sequence, preferably with a carbon dioxide (CO2) workflurid.
   ADVANTAGE - The event of damage, CO2 consumables are neither explosive nor flammable and environmentally neutral when recycled.
DC Q51 (Internal combustion engines, reciprocating engines, rotary engines (F01, F02B,D,F,G,M,N,P))

PT P
PN WO2019193518-A2; WO2019193518-A3; US2021164001-A1
TI Producing bioplastic by introducing gaseous substrate comprising carbon dioxide and hydrogen into bioreactor comprising culture of acetogenic microorganism and inoculating culture of polyhydroxyalkanoate-producing microorganism.
AU AL ROWAIHI I S
   GROETZINGER S W
   KARAN R
   EPPINGER J
   AROLD S T
   AL R I S
AE UNIV KING ABDULLAH SCI & TECHNOLOGY (UYIK-C)
   UNIV KING ABDULLAH SCI & TECHNOLOGY (UYIK-C)
GA 2019855179
AB    NOVELTY - Method for producing bioplastic from a gaseous substrate comprising a carbon source involves introducing a gaseous substrate comprising carbon dioxide and hydrogen into a primary bioreactor comprising a culture of an acetogenic microorganism in a culture medium, anaerobically fermenting the gaseous substrate in the primary bioreactor at a pressure of at least 2 bar absolute to produce an acid-containing culture medium, introducing at least a portion of the acid-containing culture medium to a secondary bioreactor, inoculating the secondary bioreactor with a culture of a polyhydroxyalkanoate (PHA)-producing microorganism, and aerobically fermenting the acid in culture medium to produce a PHA.
   USE - The method is useful for producing bioplastic from a gaseous substrate comprising a carbon source (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method produces bioplastic with improved process time and minimizes production costs.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) a system comprising primary and secondary bioreactors; and
   (2) culture medium for producing a PHA by a PHA-producing microorganism.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Method: The ratio of H2:CO2 in the gaseous substrate is 75:25-95:5. The partial pressure of H2 in the primary bioreactor is 1.7-5 bar absolute, where after consumption of a portion of the gaseous substrate by the acetogenic microorganism, the method further involves introducing additional gaseous substrate to maintain pressure in the primary bioreactor at or above 2 bar absolute. The acetogenic microorganism is chosen from Acetobacterium, Clostridium, Eubacterium, Bacteroides, Sporomusa, Acetogenium and Morera (sic). The PHA-producing microorganism is chosen from Cupriavidus necator H16, Pseudomonas putida mt-2, Bacillus spp. type strains, Corynebacterium glutamicum, C.hydrocarboxydans, Nocardia lucida and Rhodococcus sp.. The method further involves adjusting the pH of the portion of acid-containing culture medium in the secondary bioreactor before inoculating with the culture of the PHA-producing microorganism, where the pH is adjusted to 7-8. The introducing the portion of the acid-containing medium is performed when the concentration of the acid in the primary bioreactor is 2.5-20 g/l. The introducing the portion of the acid-containing culture medium comprises separating the acetogenic microorganism from the acid-containing culture medium. The method further involves obtaining the gaseous substrate from a renewable resource. The method involves obtaining the H2 of the gaseous substrate by photovoltaic electrolysis, photoelectrochemical water splitting, or biomass gasification. The method further involves separating the PHA-producing microorganism in the secondary bioreactor to produce a separated culture medium and introducing the separated culture medium to the primary bioreactor. The method further involves extracting the PHA from the PHA-producing microorganism and oxygenating the culture medium in the secondary bioreactor by introducing oxygen obtained from a renewable resource into the secondary bioreactor, where the primary and secondary bioreactors are connected.
DC B04 (Natural products and polymers. Including testing of body fluids (other than blood typing or cell counting), pharmaceuticals or veterinary compounds of unknown structure, testing of microorganisms for pathogenicity, testing of chemicals for mutagenicity or human toxicity and fermentative production of DNA or RNA. General compositions.); D16 (Fermentation industry - including fermentation equipment, brewing, yeast production, production of pharmaceuticals and other chemicals by fermentation, microbiology, production of vaccines and antibodies, cell and tissue culture and genetic engineering.)
MC B04-C03; B04-F01; B04-F10; B11-A01A; B11-A04; D05-A03; D05-A04B; D05-C21; D05-H01

PT P
PN CN110237836-A; CN110237836-B
TI Preparing molybdenum-modified zirconium dioxide            material photo catalytic reduction of carbon dioxide as            photo catalyst comprises e.g. taking molybdenum source            to prepare aqueous solution of molybdenum source, and            taking aqueous solution.
AU ZHAO J
   FENG S
   BAI Y
   LIANG X
AE UNIV SHAANXI SCI & TECHNOLOGY (UYSK-C)
   UNIV SHAANXI SCI & TECHNOLOGY (UYSK-C)
GA 201981886U
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing molybdenum-modified zirconium                dioxide material comprises (i) taking molybdenum                source to prepare an aqueous solution of molybdenum                source, (ii) taking aqueous solution of molybdenum                source and adding source of zirconium, and stirring                uniformly to obtain mixed solution, (iii) reacting                the mixed solution of resultant at 170-250 degrees                C for 6-24 hours, naturally cooling to room                temperature to obtain precipitate in lower layer,                and (iv) washing the precipitate to neutrality and                drying.
   USE - The material is useful for photo catalytic                reduction of carbon dioxide as photo catalyst                (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method: is simple, economical and safe,                has high efficiency and mild conditions.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for                molybdenum-modified zirconium dioxide material                prepared by above mentioned method, where the                molybdenum-modified zirconium dioxide material is a                crystal, and there are two crystal phases of                monoclinic phase (m-zirconium dioxide) and                tetragonal phase (t-zirconium dioxide).
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The ratio of the mass                  of molybdenum atom and mass of zirconium dioxide                  in the range of 0.1-5%. The molybdenum source is                  sodium molybdate dihydrate, ammonium                  tetramolybdate tetrahydrate and/or molybdenum                  nitrate pentahydrate. The zirconium source is                  zirconium nitrate pentahydrate, zirconium                  oxychloride, zirconium tetrachloride and/or                  zirconium oxynitrate. Preferred Method: In the                  step (iv), the drying temperature is at 50-80                  degrees C, and the drying time for 5-24 hours.                  The washing is specifically washed for many times                  with deionized water and anhydrous ethanol.
DC E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).)

PT P
PN CN110105580-A; CN110105580-B
TI Preparing multi-stage pore zeolitic imidazol ester framework material useful in cycloaddition reaction of carbon dioxide and an epoxide, comprises e.g. mixing eutectic solvent and water, heating, centrifuging, and drying.
AU CHEN L
   HU L
   YAN Z
   MO X
AE UNIV SOUTH CHINA TECHNOLOGY (UYSC-C)
GA 201970675B
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing multi-stage pore zeolitic imidazol ester framework material comprises (i) mixing eutectic solvent and water to obtain mixed solvent, (ii) dissolving metal salt and surfactant in a mixed solvent to obtain a homogeneous solution, where the metal salt is zinc salt and/or cobalt salt, (iii) adding an imidazole derivative into the homogeneous solution, heating and stirring to obtain suspension, and (iv) centrifuging the suspension, washing, and drying, where the eutectic solvent includes choline chloride as an organic salt, urea, ethylene glycol and glycerin are hydrogen bond donors.
   USE - The material is useful in cycloaddition reaction of carbon dioxide and epoxide (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method is simple, mild, rapid and environmentally friendly. The material has high specific surface area, porosity and good catalytic activity.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for multistage pore zeolitic imidazol ester framework material obtained by the above method.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: In the eutectic solvent, the molar ratio of choline chloride to hydrogen bond donor is 1:2. In the step (i), the mass ratio of the water to the eutectic solvent is (0-1.1):1, preferably 1:(5.4-5.7). In the step (ii), the mass ratio of the surfactant to the mixed solvent is (0-0.12):1, preferably 0.1:1. In the step (ii), metal salt is zinc nitrate hexahydrate, zinc acetate dihydrate, cobalt nitrate hexahydrate, zinc chloride and cobalt acetate or cobalt chloride. The surfactant is sodium dodecyl sulfate and hexadecyl trimethylammonium bromide. The imidazole derivative is 2-methylimidazole, 2-ethylimidazole and/or benzimidazole. The mass ratio of the metal salt to the mixed solvent is (0.08-0.1):1. The molar ratio of metal salt and imidazole derivative is 1:(2-8). Preferred Method: In the step (iii), the heating temperature is 40-80 degrees C, and the heating time is 30-60 minutes. In the step (iv), the washing means washing with an aqueous ethanol solution and absolute ethanol in sequence, and the drying temperature is 80-110 degrees C.
DC J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).)

TI Spray surface treatment device used for depositing pulverized paint product, comprises tank, feeding system with feed gas, and paint gun, feed gas comprising nitrogen, carbon dioxide, argon and oxygen.

AE LIPPI C (LIPP-Individual)

PN JP2019089006-A
TI Membrane used for separating acidic gas, comprises separation layer having hydrocarbon group-containing meshwork structure, and repulsion group-containing structure containing repulsion group repelled with respect to hydrocarbon group.
AU HASEGAWA H
   KURAOKA T
AE TOYO TIRE KK (TOYF-C)
GA 2019513503
AB    NOVELTY - A separation membrane comprises a separation layer having a hydrocarbon group-containing meshwork structure containing a hydrocarbon group in a molecular structure, and a repulsion group-containing structure containing a repulsion group repelled with respect to the hydrocarbon group. The repulsion group or hydrocarbon group is present in a slack region of the hydrocarbon group-containing meshwork structure generated by the repulsion between the repulsion group and hydrocarbon group.
   USE - Membrane is used for separating acidic gas, such as nitrogen and carbon dioxide during treatment of acidic gas-containing gas e.g. waste gas discharged from manufacturing equipment of town gas, hydrogen supply installation to fuel cell, sanitation facilities of industrial fumes and liquefied carbon-dioxide-manufacturing equipment used in power station, and medical and food factories.
   ADVANTAGE - The membrane has excellent gas permeability and separation performance by controlling the structure of molecule which forms micropores.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for manufacture of the membrane.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The hydrocarbon group-containing meshwork structure is a reaction material of methyltriethoxysilane or methyltrimethoxysilane, and tetraethoxysilane or tetramethoxysilane. The repulsion group-containing structure is a reaction material of diethoxydimethylsilane or dimethoxydimethylsilane, or reaction material of 1,2-bis(triethoxysilyl)ethane or 1,2-bis(trimethoxysilyl)ethane.

   ADVANTAGE - The method realizes comprehensive utilization of fly ash, changes waste into useful material, and relieves the problem of environment pollution and resource.

The residue can be cement. The impurity is subjected for continuous dissolution process.

   USE - Composite inducing agent for in situ template-induced synthesis of nano calcium carbonate (claimed).

   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for preparing nano calcium carbonate, which involves:
   (A) mixing calcium chloride with ammonia aqueous solution in the normal pressure reaction kettle;
   (B) adding composite inducer, introducing carbon dioxide at a certain temperature, stirring and reacting until reaction solution pH value reaches a certain value, stopping the carbon dioxide, after finishing reacting, separating solid and liquid, where filtering liquid is ammonium chloride solution, evaporating and concentrating and crystallizing process to obtain ammonium chloride sample; and

The reaction temperature is 10-30 degrees C. The pH value of the reaction system is 7-9.

PT P
PN CN109055447-A
TI Preparing 1,3-diglyceride by preparing modified lipase, preparing immobilized lipase, mixing fatty acid with glycerol, adding immobilized lipase, mixing uniformly, and reacting mixture in presence of carbon dioxide.
AU ZOU B
   ZHANG L
   XIA J
   CHU Y
   HUO S
AE UNIV JIANGSU (UYJS-C)
GA 201900863G
AB    NOVELTY - Method for preparing 1,3-diglyceride involves (a) preparing modified lipase by adding modified imidazole and carbonyldiimidazole to anhydrous dimethyl sulfoxide, stirring magnetically at room temperature to obtain an activated mixture, adding the activated mixture to lipase solution, magnetically stirring uniformly, and performing dialysis to obtain modified lipase, (b) preparing immobilized lipase by adding zinc nitrate to deionized water to obtain solution A, dissolving 2-methylimidazole, modified lipase and polyvinylpyrrolidone in deionized water to obtain solution B, mixing solution A and solution B, stirring uniformly, centrifuging the mixture, washing, and obtaining immobilized lipase, and (c) mixing fatty acid with glycerol to obtain a reactant, adding immobilized lipase, mixing uniformly, adding the mixture into reaction vessel, and reacting the mixture in presence of carbon dioxide.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing 1,3-diglyceride.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is simple, cost-effective and convenient to perform, and prepares 1,3-diglyceride with high purity.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Components: The modified imidazole is 2-methyl 4-carboxylic acid imidazole or 2-methyl 4,5-dicarboxylic acid imidazole. The volume ratio of the activated mixture and the lipase solution is 90 mu l:5-15 ml, where the concentration of the lipase is 30-150 mu mol/l. The lipase is Candida rugosa derived lipase, C.antarctica derived lipase, porcine pancreatic derived lipase, an Aspergillus oryzae derived lipase, A.niger derived lipase, Mucor derived lipase, Pseudomonas cepacia derived lipase or Thermus thermophilus derived lipase. The fatty acid is one or more chosen from rapeseed oil, soybean oil, peanut oil, corn oil, castor oil, cottonseed oil, tung oil, ebony oil, fish oil, and lard. Preferred Method: The step (a) involves adding modified imidazole and carbonyldiimidazole to anhydrous dimethyl sulfoxide in a molar ratio of 1:1, and reacting the mixture at room temperature for 2-10 hours. The magnetic stirring is performed at a temperature of 0-4 degrees C for 4-12 hours. The step (b) involves mixing zinc nitrate, deionized water and 2-methylimidazole in a ratio of 10-400 mg:1-5 ml: 410 mg, mixing 2-methylimidazole, modified lipase and polyvinylpyrrolidone in a ratio of 410 mg:5 ml:50 mg, and reacting the mixture at 42 degrees C for 5-10 minutes. The step (c) involves mixing fatty acid and glycerol in a molar ratio of 2:1, adding 0.5-10 wt.% immobilized lipase, and reacting the mixture at 20-90 degrees C and 5-40 MPa for 2-15 hours.
DC D16 (Fermentation industry - including fermentation equipment, brewing, yeast production, production of pharmaceuticals and other chemicals by fermentation, microbiology, production of vaccines and antibodies, cell and tissue culture and genetic engineering.); E17 (Other aliphatics.); A97 (Miscellaneous goods not specified elsewhere - including papermaking, gramophone records, detergents, food and oil well applications.)

   CHEN Z
   FENG Z
   XU X
   XU Z
AE HEBEI LIXIN NEW MATERIAL TECHNOLOGY CO (HEBE-Non-standard)
GA 2018A05548
AB    NOVELTY - The preparation of low oil absorption calcium carbonate involves preparing calcium hydroxide slurry having concentration of 10-20 wt.%, adding surfactant to calcium hydroxide slurry, stirring at speed of 10-20 rpm, aging at 70-100 degrees C for 7-9 hours, conveying the aged slurry to the carbonization tower, carbonizing carbon dioxide gas into the aged calcium hydroxide slurry through ventilation duct. The ventilation duct is provided with a tapered air outlet. The small diameter end of the tapered air outlet is connected to the ventilation duct. The large diameter end of the tapered air outlet is facing downward. The bottom surface and the side wall of the tapered air outlet are provided with several air holes.
   USE - Preparation of low oil absorption calcium carbonate.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables preparation of low oil absorption calcium carbonate with large specific surface area and high performance by economical process.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The surfactant is anionic surfactant. The anionic surfactant is sodium stearate. The addition amount of sodium stearate in calcium hydroxide slurry is 20-30 g/m3. Preferred Properties: The inner diameter of air hole is 3.5-4.5 mm. Preferred Conditions: The stirring speed is 15-20 rpm. The aging time is 8 hours. The aging temperature is 90 degrees C. The angle between the side wall of the tapered air outlet and the central axis is 40-50 degrees C.

   ADVANTAGE - The aerosol detergent composition is                environmentally-friendly, nonflammable and                non-hazardous material, and does not have flash                point and legal restrictions to store a large                amount, and has low-risk of ignition or fire, low                toxicity, low environmental burden with respect to                ozone depletion, and excellent washability, and                does not require a hazard material warehouse, and                achieves appropriate drying performance, and can                rinse off soiled components while preventing                erosion of rubbers or resins, and can be sprayed                from a certain extent distance, and thus can deal                with mechanization.

PN WO2018222137-A1; IN201917047174-A; CN110944761-A; VN69988-A
TI Processing of shell waste for recovering component, e.g. chitin, by mixing shell waste with denaturing agent or water, filtering, recovering component from filtrate, contacting residue with carbon dioxide and collecting other component.
AU YAN N
   YANG H
   CHEN X
   XI C
AE UNIV SINGAPORE NAT (UNUS-C)
   UNIV SINGAPORE NAT (UNUS-C)
GA 201896906E
AB    NOVELTY - Method for processing shell waste involves (i) mixing shell waste with a denaturing agent or water at a pre-determined temperature for a pre-determined period of time to form a mixture, (ii) filtering the mixture to obtain a filtrate and a residue and recovering a first component from the filtrate, (iii) contacting the residue with carbon dioxide to form a suspension comprising a second component and (iv) collecting a third component as balance residue following contacting, where the first component is one or more protein, the second component is calcium salt and the third component is chitin.
   USE - The method is useful for recovering components comprising protein, calcium salt and chitin from shell waste (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method utilizes mild reagents under mild conditions, and is simple, safe, economical, environmentally-friendly and enables the recovery of chitin with high purity.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Components: The shell waste comprises crushed shell, where the shell is from crab, clam, oyster, shrimp, lobster, mussel, abalone, scallop, crayfish, limpet, winkle or their combinations.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The method involves mixing the shell waste with the denaturing agent at pre-determined temperature of 0-50 degrees C for 1-24 hours. The method involves mixing the shell waste with water at pre-determined temperature of greater than or equal to 140 degrees C, preferably 140-250 degrees C for less than or equal to 4 hours. The contacting is carried out in water in a chamber. The pressure of carbon dioxide is 1-100 bar. The carbonic acid is formed during the contacting. The suspension is formed from a reaction of the carbonic acid with calcium salt present in the residue during contacting. The method involves recovering the second component from the suspension. Preferred Components: The denaturing agent is chosen from urea, guanidine hydrochloride and their combinations. The concentration of denaturing agent is 1-8 mol/l.
DC E33 (Compounds of Be, Mg, Ca, Sr, Ba, Ra, Sc, Y, La, Ac, Al, lanthanides (Rare-earths), Th.); A11 (Polysaccharides; natural rubber; other natural polymers (only a restricted range of (modified) natural polymers are included. Thus starch would be excluded, but chemically modified starch included).); A97 (Miscellaneous goods not specified elsewhere - including papermaking, gramophone records, detergents, food and oil well applications.); P43 (Sorting, cleaning, waste disposal (B06, B07, B08, B09).)

TI Electrolytic cell useful in electrolysis system,            comprises cathode compartment comprising cathode, first            ion exchange membrane containing anion exchanger,            second ion exchange membrane containing cation            exchanger and salt bridge space.
AU SCHMID B
   RELLER C
   SCHMID G
   SCHMIDT B
AE SIEMENS AG (SIEI-C)
   SIEMENS AG (SIEI-C)
   SIEMENS AG (SIEI-C)
   SIEMENS AG (SIEI-C)
   SIEMENS ENERGY GLOBAL GMBH & CO KG (SIEI-C)
   SIEMENS ENERGY GLOBAL GMBH & CO KG (SIEI-C)
GA 2018911526
AB    NOVELTY - Electrolytic cell comprises a cathode                compartment comprising a cathode; a first ion                exchange membrane which contains an anion exchanger                and which adjoins the cathode space; an anode                compartment comprising an anode; a second ion                exchange membrane containing a cation exchanger                adjacent to the anode compartment; and a salt                bridge space, where the salt bridge space between                the first ion exchange membrane and the second ion                exchange membrane is arranged.
   USE - The electrolytic cell is useful in                electrolysis system.
   ADVANTAGE - The electrolysis system enables efficient mass                transport and long run times, and avoids salt                encrustations at a cathode.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for                electrolysis system, comprising an electrolytic                cell; and #electrolysis of carbon dioxide,                comprising utilizing an electrolytic cell or an                electrolysis plant, where carbon dioxide is reduced                at the cathode and formed at the cathode                bicarbonate migrates through the first ion exchange                membrane to an electrolyte in the salt bridge                space.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INSTRUMENTATION AND TESTING - Preferred Components: The cathode contacts                  the first ion exchange membrane. The anode                  contacts the second ion exchange membrane. The                  cathode and/or the anode as a gas diffusion                  electrode, as a porous bound catalyst structure,                  as a particulate catalyst on a support, as a                  coating of a particulate catalyst on the first                  and/or second ion exchange membrane, as a porous                  conductive support in which a catalyst is                  impregnated, and/or is formed as a non-closed                  sheet. The cathode as a gas diffusion electrode,                  as a porous bound catalyst structure, as a                  particulate catalyst on a support, as a coating                  of a particulate catalyst on the first and/or                  second ion exchange membrane, as a porous                  conductive support in which a catalyst is                  impregnated, and/or as a non-closed sheet is                  formed, which contains an anion exchange                  material, and/or where the anode as a gas                  diffusion electrode, as a porous bound catalyst                  structure, as a particulate catalyst on a                  support, as a coating of a particulate catalyst                  on the first and/or second ion exchange membrane,                  as a porous conductive support in which a                  catalyst is impregnated, and/or is formed as a                  non-closed sheet containing a cation exchange                  material. The second ion exchange membrane is                  designed as a bipolar membrane, where an anion                  exchanger layer of the bipolar membrane is                  directed towards the anode space and a cation                  exchange layer of the bipolar membrane is                  directed toward the salt bridge space. The first                  ion exchange membrane and/or the second ion                  exchange membrane are hydrophilic. The anode                  and/or the cathode are contacted on the side                  facing away from the salt bridge space with a                  conductive structure. The system further                  comprises a recirculation device which is                  connected to a discharge of the salt bridge space                  and a supply of the cathode space, which is                  adapted to lead a reactant of the cathode                  reaction, which can be formed in the salt bridge                  space, back into the cathode space. The salt                  bridge space comprises a                  hydrogencarbonate-containing electrolyte. The                  electrolyte of the salt bridge space does not                  comprise any acid.

GA 201852646W

The mass ratio of dihydric alcohol liquid to the content of the nonionic surfactant having polyoxyethylene group is 8-20. The stock solution comprises trivalent or higher polyhydric alcohol and polyhydric alcohol solid content at 20 degrees C is 5 %mass or less. The mass ratio of content of the dihydric alcohol liquid to content of water is 0.05-0.25.

PN CN108210811-A
TI Preparation of Mongolian elecampane medicinal pill involves crushing elecampane, Kaempferia galanga, radix sophorae flavescentis and Rubus corchorifolius, subjecting to supercritical carbon dioxide extraction, and mixing residues with water.
AU BAO B
   ZHANG B
   HANG L
   LI N
   CHEN J
AE UNIV INNER MONGOLIA MEDICAL (UYIN-Non-standard)
GA 201855767B
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of Mongolian elecampane medicinal pill comprises taking elecampane, Kaempferia galanga, radix sophorae flavescentis and Rubus corchorifolius, and crushing respectively to obtain coarse powder; subjecting to supercritical carbon dioxide extraction respectively to obtain volatile oil; mixing elecampane, Kaempferia galanga, radix sophorae flavescentis and Rubus corchorifolius residues at 4:4:2:1 mass ratio, adding 9 times water, boiling for 3 times at 0.5 hour each, and filtering to obtain solution; combining solution, concentrating to 1300 mL, centrifuging for 10 minutes under 3500 revolutions/minute, taking supernatant, and spray drying to obtain powder; mixing all volatile oils, performing inclusion using beta -cyclodextrin, adding obtained powder, mixing, adding additive, stirring, and forming into pill by extrusion machine; an coating film.
   USE - Method for preparing Mongolian elecampane medicinal pill (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The Mongolian medicine is easy to take and suitable for mass production, and has good taste, simple preparation, high bioavailability, low dosage and fast effect.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - PHARMACEUTICALS - Preferred Components: The additive is adhesive, preferably 20% ethanol solution and microcrystalline cellulose. The mass ratio of medicinal powder and microcrystalline cellulose is 1:1.25. The mass ratio of beta -cyclodextrin and volatile oil is 4:0.5 or 8:1. Preferred Method: The inclusion is carried out at 50 degrees C for 2 hours.

TI Method for preparing micro-bubble probe by using            carbon dioxide laser, involves driving optical fiber            and quartz capillary respectively translate along axial            two ends, so as to break thin pipe structure.

GA 2022B3720A
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves welding (S100) one end                face of a quartz capillary pipe with one end                surface of an optical fiber, (S200) adjusting the                light spot position of the carbon laser, and                locating the carbon spot of the laser on the thin                pipe structure, and releasing the gas in the quartz                capillary, (S600) re-turning on the carbon laser so                that a section of capillary between the optical                fiber and the thin pipe structure is heated and                softened, heating and expanding the section of the                capillary to form a microbubble cavity between the                fiber and thin pipe, and closing the carbon light,                (S700) adjusting light spot positions of carbon                light and (S900) driving the fiber to translate                along the axial two ends of the hollow pipe                structure to break the thin pipes, and forming a                contact probe on the microbubbling cavity after                thin pipe structure is broken.
   USE - Method for preparing micro-bubble probe by                using carbon dioxide laser for use in pressure                sensing system (claimed). Can also be used in                micro-electromechanical system (MEMS),                micro-fluidic system and micro electromechanical                sensor.
   ADVANTAGE - The micro-bubble probe has a contact probe,                the single point on the contact probe and the                object to be measured is subjected to contact                stress, it can realize the pressure measurement of                the pressure generated by the pressing of the                contact, the displacement, the target young modulus                and other single points. At the same time, the                stress area and the deformation area on the                microbubble cavity are separated. The stress area                is directly and horizontally contact along the                pressure direction, when the pressure area is                stressed, the contact is not directly connected                with the pressure, contact is deformed under the                translation extrusion of the stress, and the                contact does not directly contact the                pressure.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the                following:
   a micro-bubble probe; and

    transferring Odontella aurita to a 500 ml culture                bottle containing 250 ml McFarland Latex Equiv 1                (mL1) liquid medium, culturing for 4-5 days, where                the light is 30-40 mumol photons/ms, (2)                transferring the cultured Odontella aurita algae to                a 1000 ml culture flask containing mL1 liquid                medium, allowing to stand and culturing for 5-7                days, performing the next step of expanding after                the OD750 of the algae liquid reaches 2, (3)                connecting Odontella aurita algae strain in the                sealed light bioreactor, where the culture medium                is mL1 liquid and the light intensity is 70-100                mumol photons/ms, introducing the compressed air                containing 1% carbon dioxide, culturing for 5-7                days, (4) washing the obtained algae by fresh mL1                liquid culture medium, transferring to the 20 l                closed photo-bioreactor based on the initial                inoculation density of OD750 0.60plus minus0.02,                where the power of full-spectrum white light,                monochromatic red light, and monochromatic blue                light are all set to 100 w, and the corresponding                light intensity is 306-337 mumol photons/ms,(5)                adjusting the power of red light and blue light to                make the proportion respectively 1:9, 2:8, 3:7,                4:6, 5:5, 6:4, 7:3, 8:2 and 9:1, introducing                compressed air containing 1% carbon dioxide based                on 0.2vvm in culture process for ventilation and                mixing and (6) measuring the biomass concentration                every day in the culture process, reserving sample                every 3 days, collecting the algae cell by                centrifuging (3500g, 5 minutes), vacuum freezing                and drying to obtain freeze-dried algae                powder.

PT P
PN CN111925851-A
TI Flue gas enhanced water washing pretreatment method, involves dissolving carbon dioxide in flue gas, realizing raw coal ash reduction, leaching and washing discharged washing coal, enabling, passing flue gas to drying unit, and recycling.
AU LI X
   SUN M
   WU A
   MA J
   WEI B
   CHEN L
AE UNIV XINJIANG (UXIJ-C)
   XINJIANG XINYE ENERGY CHEM CO LTD (XINJ-Non-standard)
   UNIV HUAZHONG SCI & TECHNOLOGY (UYHZ-C)
GA 2020B83937
AB    NOVELTY - Flue gas enhanced water washing pretreatment method, involves (a) passing flue gas to heat exchange in drying unit to raw coal leaching unit, dissolving carbon dioxide in flue gas in eluent through spraying system, realizing raw coal ash reduction upgrading through elution liquid immersion washing and spraying, and discharging, (b) performing leaching washing process on discharged washing coal using raw coal leaching unit for completing coal liquid separation, enabling waste liquid for passing to waste liquid recovery unit, passing dust collector through flue gas of raw coal leaching unit, and discharging waste liquid to atmosphere after dust removal, and (c) passing high-temperature flue gas to drying unit after passing through flue gas purification unit, heating the coal conveying belt by high-temperature flue gas for drying and dipping the washed coal, conveying waste liquid to coal base using coal conveying belt, processing for performing immersion washing, and recycling.
   USE - The method is used for coal-fired ash reduction and improving quality of coal combustion.
   ADVANTAGE - The method utilizes the power plant flue gas to perform intensified water washing of raw coal, effectively achieves the effect of reducing ash and improving quality, solves the problem of boiler contamination and slagging caused by combustion process of raw coal from source, ensures opened up way of resource utilization of power plant flue gas, and is economical and environmentally-friendly.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Flue gas enhanced water washing pretreatment method, involves (a) passing flue gas to heat exchange in drying unit to raw coal leaching unit, dissolving carbon dioxide in flue gas in eluent through spraying system, so that the eluent is acidic, passing raw coal from one side of raw coal leaching unit, realizing raw coal ash reduction upgrading through elution liquid immersion washing and spraying, and discharging cleaned raw coal from other side of raw coal leaching unit to be eluded coal, where the elution process is dynamic continuous process, the eluent is aqueous solution of water-soluble organic solvent, the concentration of water-soluble organic solvent is 8-15%, (b) performing leaching washing process on discharged washing coal using raw coal leaching unit for completing coal liquid separation, enabling waste liquid for passing to waste liquid recovery unit, passing dust collector through flue gas of raw coal leaching unit, and discharging waste liquid to atmosphere after dust removal, and (c) passing high-temperature flue gas to drying unit after passing through flue gas purification unit, heating the coal conveying belt by high-temperature flue gas for drying and dipping the washed coal, conveying waste liquid to coal base using coal conveying belt, processing waste liquid by waste liquid recycling unit for performing immersion washing, and recycling treated waste liquid to raw coal leaching unit.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INSTRUMENTATION AND TESTING - Preferred Devices: The flue gas purification unit described in step (c) comprises bag dust removal device, a wet desulfurization device, a wet electrostatic dust removal device and denitration device, and performing denitration, desulfurization and dust removal treatment on flue gas by relying on coal-fired power plant purification device.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - METALLURGY - Preferred Conditions: The elution pressure in step (a) is normal pressure. The filtration unit in step (b) is two-stage filtration. The step (b) involves realizing coal-liquid separation by filtering unit and at same time sieving eluted coal of different particle size using coal-liquid separation vibrating sieve. The step (c) involves passing waste liquid recovery unit to treated waste liquid to dipping unit as spray system supplementary liquid.

PN CN111848708-A; CN111848708-B
TI Separating multiple active ingredients by crushing fructus momordicae extract residue, adding alkaline, extracting, neutralizing, adding chromatographic silica gel, extracting, diluting, eluting, drying, concentrating filtering and drying.
AU LI W
   HUANG H
AE HUNAN HUACHENG BIOTECH INC (HUNA-Non-standard)
GA 2020A9354Q
AB    NOVELTY - Separating multiple active ingredients involve (i) crushing fructus momordicae extract residue, adding alkaline to alkaline low-carbon alcohol/aqueous solution, stirring and extracting, and filtering to obtain extraction filtrate, (ii) neutralizing extraction filtrate with acid, adding chromatographic silica gel and vacuum-drying to constant weight to obtain crude extract mixed sample silica gel, (iii) putting into extractor, introducing carbon dioxide gas for extraction to obtain primary extract, diluting with organic solvent, separating, adding organic layer with diatomaceous earth, filtering, (iv) putting into silica gel chromatography column, using eluent (I) and eluent (II) to elute silica gel column, and collecting eluate (I) and eluate (II), (v) concentrating eluent (I) and drying to obtain crude sitosterol, dissolving in fat-soluble organic solvent, concentrating and drying filter cake to obtain sitosterol, (vi) adding eluent (II) to neutral alumina, concentrating and drying.
   USE - The method is useful for separating multiple active ingredients.
   ADVANTAGE - The method extracts three active ingredients of squalene, sitosterol and mogroscin simultaneously which comprehensively utilizes the resources of fructus momordicae extract residue, has consistent process, strong operability, low production cost, is suitable for industrialized production and obtains high content and yield of various active ingredients.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Method for separating multiple active ingredients involve (i) crushing fructus momordicae extract residue, adding alkaline to alkaline low-carbon alcohol/aqueous solution, stirring and extracting at room temperature and avoid light, and filtering to obtain extraction filtrate, (ii) neutralizing the extraction filtrate with acid, concentrating under reduced pressure to alcohol-free, adding chromatographic silica gel, uniformly stirring and vacuum-drying to constant weight to obtain crude extract mixed sample silica gel, (iii) putting crude extract mixed sample silica gel into a carbon dioxide supercritical extractor, introducing carbon dioxide gas containing entrainer for extraction, reducing the temperature to normal temperature and the pressure to obtain a primary extract, diluting and dissolving the primary extract with an organic solvent, back-extracting with alkaline water, washing the organic solvent layer with water until neutral, separating the layers, adding the organic layer with diatomaceous earth, stirring, filtering, concentrating under reduced pressure to be solvent-free to obtain finished product of squalene and sample-mixed silica gel after supercritical extraction, (iv) taking the sample-mixed silica gel after supercritical extraction, putting into a silica gel chromatography column, using eluent (I) and eluent (II) to elute silica gel column, and collecting eluate (I) and eluate (II) respectively, (v) concentrating the eluent (I) under reduced pressure to solvent-free and drying to obtain crude sitosterol, dissolving the crude sitosterol in a fat-soluble organic solvent, adding diatomaceous earth, stirring to reflux, filtering while hot, concentrating, cooling the concentrate, stirring and crystallizing, performing suction filtration, and drying the filter cake to obtain finished sitosterol, (vi) adding the eluent (II) to neutral alumina, stirring to reflux, filtering while hot, concentrating and drying to obtain the finished mogrosides.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - PHARMACEUTICALS - Preferred Components: In step (1), the fructus momordicae extract residue comprises waste residue after the mogrosides are extracted from fresh or dried fructus momordicae fruit with water, comprising fructus momordicae fruit seeds and fructus momordicae husk. In step (i), the lower alcohol in basic lower alcohol/water solution comprises methanol, ethanol and/or isopropanol. The alkali in the basic lower alcohol comprises sodium hydroxide or potassium hydroxide. In step (ii), the acid comprises inorganic acid, preferably hydrochloric acid and sulfuric acid. In step (iii), the entrainer comprises methanol, ethanol, acetone, n-butanol, ethyl acetate, petroleum ether, cyclohexane, n-hexane and/or water. In step (iii), the organic solvent comprises petroleum ether, diethyl ether, n-hexane, cyclohexane, No.6 solvent oil, No.120 solvent oil, and/or ethyl acetate. In step (v), the fat-soluble organic solvent comprises petroleum ether, diethyl ether, n-hexane, cyclohexane, No.6 solvent oil, No.120 solvent oil and/or ethyl acetate, Preferred Composition: In step (i), the content of the lower alcohol is 40-99 vol.%, and the concentration of the alkali in the basic lower alcohol is 0.5-2 %mass. The added amount of the entrainer is 0.01-0.1 wt.%. In step (vi), the amount of the neutral alumina is 1-5 wt.%. Preferred Conditions: The step (i) involves adding 5-10 l alkaline lower alcohol/water solution to every 1 kg fructus momordicae extract residue. In step (i), the time for the stirring and extraction at room temperature is 6-8 hours. The step (ii) involves adding 0.1-0.2 l chromatography silica gel to every 1 kg fructus momordicae extract residue. In step (iii), the supercritical extraction is carried out at a pressure of 20-28 MPa at 35-45 degrees C for 1-2 hours. The step (iii) involves adding 0.05-2 l organic solvent to 1 kg fructus momordicae extract residue (wet weight), where the dosage of the diatomaceous earth is 1-5 wt.% (wet weight). In step (iv), the height-to-diameter ratio of the silica gel column is (5-10):1. In step (iv), the eluent (I) is a mixed solution of dichloromethane and methanol with a volume ratio of (9-10):1, the amount of eluent (I) is 3-5 BV, and the flow rate of elution with eluent I is 0.5-1.0 BV/hour. In step (iv), the eluent (II) is a mixed solution of dichloromethane and methanol with a volume ratio of 6-8:1, the amount of eluent (II) is 3-5 BV, and the flow rate of elution using eluent (II) is 0.5-2.0 BV/hour. The step (v) involves adding 0.1-0.5 l fat-soluble organic solvent to every 1 kg fructus momordicae extract residue. In step (v), the solids concentration of the concentrated solution is 10-20%, the cooling temperature is 5-10 degrees C, the stirring speed is 20-60 rpm, and the crystallization time is 8-24 hours.

PN CN111644185-A
TI Stripping method of bismuth oxychloride for            photocatalytic reduction of carbon dioxide, involves            adding chloro-bismuth oxide to water, absolute ethanol            and ethylene glycol, using cell crusher to perform            ultrasonic stripping and cooling.
AU JIN X
   GE T
   XU Y
   XIE H
AE UNIV NANYANG NORMAL (NYSF-C)
GA 202091560K
AB    NOVELTY - Stripping method of bismuth oxychloride using                cell crusher involves dissolving bismuth nitrate                pentahydrate in deionized water, adding                tetracycline hydrochloride, stirring, reacting,                centrifuging, washing several times with distilled                water and ethanol, drying to obtain black                chloro-bismuth oxide complex precursor, sintering                dried precursor in muffle furnace, naturally                cooling to room temperature to obtain bismuth                oxychloride photocatalyst, adding chloro-bismuth                oxide sample to 1000 ml beaker, adding 0-200 ml                deionized water, 0-500 ml absolute ethanol and                0-500 ml ethylene glycol, using cell crusher to                perform ultrasonic stripping under powder rate of                40-80% for 15-30 minutes, cooling to room                temperature adding anhydrous ethanol each time                until the solution reaches 500 ml, performing                cumulative ultrasonic treatment for 4-8 hours,                leaving still, washing precipitate with distilled                water and ethanol several times, drying in oven at                60-100 degrees C for 12-24 hours.
   USE - Stripping method of bismuth oxychloride using                cell crusher for photocatalytic reduction of carbon                dioxide (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method effectively improves the                photocatalytic performance of product and produces                bismuth oxychloride block by ultrasonically                stripped with cell crusher, so that bismuth                oxychloride is stripped off from the bulk into                bismuth oxychloride nanosheets with thickness of                10-50 nm, fast migration rate, high separation                efficiency and carbon dioxide reduction                performance.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Stripping method of bismuth oxychloride                (Bi3O4Cl) using cell crusher involves dissolving                0.4-1 g bismuth nitrate pentahydrate in 30-35 ml                deionized water, adding 0.2-0.5 g tetracycline                hydrochloride, stirring for 30-60 minutes,                transferring to 40 ml PTFE-lined hydrothermal                stainless steel kettle, reacting at 140-180 degrees                C for 15-24 hours, centrifuging at 10000 rpm for                3-10 minutes, washing several times with distilled                water and ethanol, drying at 60-100 degrees C in                oven for 12-24 hours to obtain black bismuth                oxychloride (BiOCl) complex precursor, sintering                0.2-2 g dried precursor in muffle furnace at                450-600 degrees C under heating rate of 1-5 degrees                C/minute for 2-10 hours, naturally cooling to room                temperature to obtain chloro-bismuth oxide sample                with irregular block structure and thickness of                200-400 nm, adding chloro-bismuth oxide sample to                1000 ml beaker, adding 0-200 ml deionized water,                0-500 ml absolute ethanol and 0-500 ml ethylene                glycol, using cell crusher to perform ultrasonic                stripping under powder rate of 40-80% for 15-30                minutes, cooling to room temperature adding                anhydrous ethanol each time until the solution                reaches 500 ml, performing cumulative ultrasonic                treatment for 4-8 hours, leaving still, washing                precipitate with distilled water and ethanol                several times, drying in oven at 60-100 degrees C                for 12-24 hours to obtain ultra-thin chloro-bismuth                oxide nanosheets, measuring and studying phase                composition, microscopic morphology, charge                separation and photocatalytic performance of                bismuth oxychloride photocatalyst by X-ray                diffraction, scanning electron microscopy,                differential reflectance spectroscopy (DRS) and                photocurrent measurement method.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Condition: The hydrothermal                  reaction temperature is 140-180 degrees C.                  Preferred Composition: The molar ratio of bismuth                  nitrate pentahydrate and tetracycline                  hydrochloride is 1.8-2:1. The total amount of                  deionized water, absolute ethanol and ethylene                  glycol added is 500 ml.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: Ethylene glycol                  comprises less than 0.01% (v/v) water. Ethanol                  comprises less than 0.01% (v/v) water.

PN CN110982558-A; CN110982558-B
TI Device useful for directly preparing hydrogen and carbon monoxide based on coal/biomass gasification based on chemical chain process, comprises e.g. fuel reactor, oxygen reactor, and first gas-solid separation device.
AU XIANG W
   LIU Y
   CHEN S
AE UNIV SOUTHEAST (UYSE-C)
GA 2020314265
AB    NOVELTY - Device comprises fuel reactor, oxygen reactor, first gas-solid separation device, second gas-solid separation device, first control flow device, second control flow device, and air separation device; the calcium carbonate bed material loaded with iron/nickel passes through the first gas-solid separation device and first control flow device, and enters the oxygen reactor to generate calcium oxide bed material loaded with iron/nickel; the calcium oxide bed material loaded with iron/nickel passes through the second gas-solid separation device and second control flow device, and enters the fuel reactor to generate calcium carbonate bed material loaded with iron/nickel again; bed material is circulated between two reactors, where water vapor and coal/biomass are fed into fuel reactor, and hydrogen is produced, and oxygen is fed into oxygen reactor and produce carbon monoxide, where oxygen is provided by air separation device, which separates nitrogen and oxygen in air to obtain pure oxygen.
   USE - The device is useful for directly preparing hydrogen and carbon monoxide based on coal/biomass gasification based on chemical chain process (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The device: has simple process, scientifically available process raw materials which are economical, high carbon utilization efficiency, and less pollution; and is environment-friendly.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for directly preparing hydrogen and carbon monoxide based on coal/biomass gasification based on chemical chain process, comprising (i) adding coal/biomass particles with particle diameter of 0.1-10 mm to the bottom of the fuel reactor, lifting the calcium oxide bed material particles loaded with iron or nickel with a particle diameter of 0.07-0.7 mm to top in the fuel reactor, during the lifting process, iron or nickel plays a catalytic role, calcium oxide adsorbs carbon dioxide to form calcium carbonate, separating 85% concentration of hydrogen in first gas-solid separation device, controlling temperature of fuel reactor at 600-800 degrees C, and reducing atmosphere is inside the reactor; (ii) controlling the temperature of the oxygen reactor to 700-950 degrees C, sending the calcium carbonate bed material loaded with iron or nickel to bottom of the oxygen reactor, lifting to the top of the oxygen reactor by oxygen, during the lifting process, calcium carbonate is decomposed into calcium oxide and carbon dioxide by heating, carbon dioxide reacting with coke and oxygen to form carbon monoxide, and separating 85% concentration of carbon monoxide in the first gas-solid separation device; and (iii) according to steps (i) and (ii), iron and nickel act as catalysts for the reaction, at the same time, the carbon deposition generated in the fuel reactor will also undergo partial oxidation with oxygen in the oxygen reactor to produce carbon monoxide.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the device. (Drawing includes non-English language text)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The coal/biomass fuel particles include bituminous coal, gas coal and lignite, or are replaced by biomass particles or other carbon-containing solid waste particles.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INSTRUMENTATION AND TESTING - Preferred Components: The both the fuel reactor and the oxygen reactor are fluidized beds, and the flow types are either fast fluidized beds or pneumatic conveying beds. The first gas-solid separation device and the second gas-solid separation device are both a type of inertial separator and cyclone separator, the first gas-solid separation device and the second gas-solid separation device separate the gas and solid particles generated in the reactor. The both the first control flow device and the second control flow device are one of V valve, U valve, L valve, H valve and J valve; the first control flow device and the second control flow device control the bed material transfer and prevent the gas from channeling back between the reactors. The main component of the bed material is calcium oxide, and it is loaded with iron or nickel, the main loading process is the immersion process.
