PT P
PN CN110364230-A; CN110364230-B
TI Screening of organic alkali e.g. trimethylglycine            in copper-catalyzed reaction, involves determining            energy barriers of multiple organic bases using            potential energy profile of reaction path, and            determining smallest energy barrier ligand.
AU GENG C
   GUO Y
   CHEN P
   XUE Y
   LV Z
   WANG B
AE ANYANG INST TECHNOLOGY (AYIT-C)
GA 201989346H
AB    NOVELTY - Screening of organic alkali in                copper-catalyzed carbon dioxide and hydrogen                reaction involves selecting organic base complex                with high catalytic efficiency and design detailed                reaction paths based on the basic principles of                chemistry, drawing the intermediates of the design                path and structural formula on the transition                states and making Gaussian input files on this                basis, extracting a Gibbs free energy correction                value from the output xx.log file, performing                solvation single-point calculation on the obtained                structure, deriving electric energy from the                output, introducing the electric energy to the                Gibbs free energy correction value to obtain an                absolute free energy value of the intermediate or                transition state, calculating the speed process                energy barrier, obtaining the speed-determining                process energy barriers of multiple organic bases,                comparing energy barrier lists, and determining the                smallest energy barrier ligand as an optimum copper                catalyst active organic alkali ligand.
   USE - Screening of organic alkali e.g.                1,8-diazabicyclo(5.4.0)undec-7-ene,                trimethylglycine, and triazabicyclodecene (all                claimed) in copper-catalyzed carbon dioxide and                hydrogen reaction to form formic acid.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is rapid and easy, and can                effectively and economically screen organic base                having high activity with high accuracy and without                laboratory repeated experiment investigation and                comparison, by simple process.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Screening of organic alkali in                copper-catalyzed carbon dioxide and hydrogen                reaction involves selecting organic base complex                with high catalytic efficiency and design detailed                reaction paths based on the basic principles of                chemistry, drawing the intermediates of the design                path and structural formula on the transition                states using chemdraw and making Gaussian input                files on this basis, optimizing the intermediate                and transition state structure of the designed path                through the quantum chemical simulation software                Gaussian09, using M06 functional, and 6-31G' basis                set, and extracting a Gibbs free energy correction                value from the output xx.log file, performing                solvation single-point calculation on the obtained                structure using M06 functional, 6-311+G'' basis                set, and a polarization continuous model, deriving                electric energy from the output xx.log file,                introducing the electric energy to the Gibbs free                energy correction value to obtain an absolute free                energy value of the intermediate or transition                state, drawing a potential energy profile of the                reaction path according to the obtained absolute                free energy value, finding the reaction speed                process, its related intermediates and transition                states, and calculating the speed process energy                barrier, replacing the organic alkali ligand in the                intermediate and transition state structure related                to the decisive process with another organic alkali                ligand, and recalculating the decisive process                energy barrier, and repeating the replacement PN IN201911022547-A
TI Producing low silica calcium oxide by feeding green liquor sodium oxide to slacker to react with calcium oxide, feeding slurry to clarifier filter, diluting with hot water/filtrate, feeding lime mud to hydraulic press filter, and drying.
AU KUMAR S
AE HENCHMEN ENG CONSULTANT PRIVATE LTD (HENC-Non-standard)
GA 201965442J
AB    NOVELTY - Low silica calcium oxide is produced by feeding green liquor of 110-135 TTA as sodium oxide (Na2O) at 85-90 degrees C and pH of 13-14 to slacker to react with calcium oxide to form sodium hydroxide (NaOH) slurry; feeding slurry to either white liquor clarifier or white liquor cleric disk filter; diluting lime mud from WLC and WLCD to 15-25% with hot water/filtrate in cylindrical conical tank/hydroclones or series of conical/hydroclones depending on silica content left; feeding lime mud slurry having less/maximum silica content from conical tank/hydroclones different from lime mud washers by overflow and underflow mechanism to extract maximum soda; feeding lime mud from mud washers (low silica content) underflow to hydraulic press filter to form cylindrical mud pellet having 3 inch diameter and 12 inch long or bricks; and drying pellets using mesh wire cum belt conveyor first then for 15 days in open air and sunlight in heaps.
   USE - Production of low silica calcium oxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The method employs 90-95% pure oxygen as atomizing media for burner. The whole lime bed moves over a table feeder like disk clock/anticlockwise. Sharp burner flame over the lime bed is able to remove 95% of carbon dioxide from the lime mud. Flue gas produced is low in volume by high content of carbon dioxide, which is cooled down cryogenically, and in turn used for bleaching of unbleached pulp (brown pulp), making the method sustainable in nature.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Low silica calcium oxide is produced by feeding green liquor of 110-135 TTA as sodium oxide (Na2O) at 85-90 degrees C and pH of 13-14 to slacker to react with calcium oxide to form sodium hydroxide (NaOH) slurry; feeding slurry to either white liquor clarifier or white liquor cleric disk filter; diluting lime mud from WLC and WLCD to 15-25% with hot water/filtrate in cylindrical conical tank/hydroclones or series of conical/hydroclones depending on silica content left; feeding lime mud slurry having less/maximum silica content from conical tank/hydroclones different from lime mud washers by overflow and underflow mechanism to extract maximum soda; feeding lime mud from mud washers (low silica content) underflow to hydraulic press filter to form cylindrical mud pellet having 3 inch diameter and 12 inch long or bricks; drying pellets using mesh wire cum belt conveyor first then for 15 days in open air and sunlight in heaps; feeding pellets/bricks to vertical shaft moving bed lime kiln at the top; drying, heating, burning, and cooling the pellets; and washing down (flue gas) carbon dioxide (CO2) gas to remove all the sulfur content and obtained flue gas is stored, in turn used to wash unbleached pulp inside the mill.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - METALLURGY - Preferred Device: Slurry is prepared at a retention time of 90-180 minutes in stirrer type tank (causticizers). Tank/hydroclone does not have any agitator mechanism so due to density difference, high silica mud settles down which is removed from bottom. Preferred Processes: For calcination process, compressed oxygen (90-95% purity) gas is used, along with fuel like methanol/non-condensable gas (NCG)/natural gas /crushed coal powder/producer gas/furnace oil. Moisture content of pellets is reduced from 70% dryness to 90% dryness after sun drying. Crushing section lime of size 15-25 mm is obtained.
DC E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); Q77 (Furnaces, kilns, ovens, retorts (F27))
MC E34-D01; Q77-A01

PN CN109716998-A
TI Cultivating mushroom comprises e.g. crushing wood rot fungus bran, filtering through mesh sieve, mixing fungus chaff material and pre-formed base, subjecting culture material to high temperature sterilization, cooling, and inoculating.
AU MA J
   HUANG C
   SHAO H
AE JIANGSU HONGSHENG MUSHROOM CO LTD (JIAN-Non-standard)
GA 201942963U
AB    NOVELTY - Cultivating mushroom comprises e.g. (i) crushing wood rot fungus bran, and filtering through a mesh sieve having a pore size of 10-15 cm to obtain a fungus chaff material, (ii) mixing the fungus chaff material and a pre-formed base to obtain a mushroom culture material, (iii) subjecting the culture material to high temperature sterilization, (iv) cooling the sterilized culture material, and inoculating, (v) cultivating the inoculated mushroom culture material to obtain a cultivation material for cultivating Agaricus bisporus, (vi) maintaining the temperature of the cultivated material at 20-22 degrees C where the concentration of carbon dioxide is 0.5-1.1%, and filling the mushroom mycelium with a layer of material between 13-15 days, (vii) covering the soil on the cultivation material, where the covering material used is peat soil, and (viii) before the bacteria appear on the surface of the soil, do not ventilate the gas, if the mycelium reach 30-60% of the surface of the soil.
   USE - The method is useful for cultivating mushroom.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: replaces the fermentation production by using clinker production; kills the bacteria and pests in the mushroom culture material by sterilizing the mushroom culture material under high temperature conditions; and softens the surface structure of the mushroom culture material, where the cultivation material is easily colonized and grown by the mushroom strain.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Cultivating mushroom comprises (i) crushing wood rot fungus bran, and filtering through a mesh sieve having a pore size of 10-15 cm to obtain a fungus chaff material, (ii) mixing the fungus chaff material and a pre-formed base to obtain a mushroom culture material, (iii) subjecting the culture material to high temperature sterilization, (iv) cooling the sterilized culture material, and inoculating, (v) cultivating the inoculated mushroom culture material to obtain a cultivation material for cultivating Agaricus bisporus, (vi) maintaining the temperature of the cultivated material at 20-22 degrees C where the concentration of carbon dioxide is 0.5-1.1%, and filling the mushroom mycelium with a layer of material between 13-15 days, (vii) covering the soil on the cultivation material, where the covering material used is peat soil, and (viii) before the bacteria appear on the surface of the soil, do not ventilate the gas, if the mycelium reach 30-60% of the surface of the soil, spraying water thoroughly, on the next day, sterilizing the bacteria, breaking the hardened soil, forming a pellet structure, maintaining at 24-27 degrees C per day, reducing the mushroom bed temperature to 17 degrees C, while ventilating, reducing the carbon dioxide concentration to less than 0.1%, using low temperature treatment and ventilation to promote the mushroom, and adjusting the circulating air requirements in the mushroom room and the humidity in advance.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - AGRICULTURE - Preferred Components: In the step (ii), the base material comprises 72.62 wt.% Salix sawdust, 11.55 wt.% bran, 5.22 wt.% corn flour, 8.61 wt.% soybean meal, and 2 wt.% raw gypsum. In the step (vii), the grass charcoal in comprises organic fertilizer, where the organic fertilizer comprises 40 wt.% straw, 30 wt.% Chinese medicine waste residue and 30 wt.% grass coal. Preferred Method: In the step (iii), the high temperature sterilization is carried out at 100-120 degrees C for 5 hours. In the step (iii), the freezing fermented with aerobic actinomycete for 10 days, then dried to moisture less than 10%, and an organic fertilizer is prepared by adding 2 wt.% total weight of the amino acid.
   ACTIVITY - Fertilizer.

TI Hollow steel tube of steel tube tower comprises two hollow hemispheres of same structure welded into sphere, where hollow hemisphere comprises sphere and reinforcing rib, and reinforcing rib is vertically welded to inner surface of sphere.
AU ZHAO X
   XU Z
   PAN Z
AE ANHUI HONGYUAN STEEL TOWER CO LTD (ANHU-Non-standard)
GA 201895648X
AB    NOVELTY - Hollow steel tube of a steel tube tower comprises two hollow hemispheres of the same structure welded into a sphere, where the hollow hemisphere comprises a sphere (1) and a reinforcing rib (2), and the reinforcing rib is vertically welded to the inner surface of the sphere, the reinforcing rib comprises a latitudinal reinforcing rib (21) and a longitudinal reinforcing rib (211), and the latitudinal reinforcing rib and the longitudinal reinforcing rib are perpendicular to each other. The weft stiffener comprises a first weft stiffener, a second weft stiffener and a third weft stiffener (214). The longitudinal reinforcing rib includes a first longitudinal reinforcing rib and a second longitudinal reinforcing rib, the first longitudinal reinforcing ribs are spaced apart, and an angle between two adjacent first longitudinal reinforcing ribs is 30-60 degree.
   USE - Hollow steel tube of a steel tube tower.
   ADVANTAGE - The hollow steel tube of a steel tube tower increases the force requirement of the hollow sphere and improves the qualification rate of the steel tube hollow sphere welding.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for welding a steel tube tower hollow sphere, which involves:
   (A) polishing all the welding surfaces until the metal luster leaks;
   (B) assembling and welding the reinforcing ribs and the hollow hemispheres, where the welding order of the ribs is first latitudinal reinforcing rib, first longitudinal reinforcing rib, second latitudinal reinforcing rib, second Longitudinal reinforcing ribs and third latitudinal reinforcing ribs, and the welding adopts gas carbon dioxide and argon gas mixture gas shielded welding, the welding wire is 1.2mm gas shielded welding wire, the welding current is 350-360, the voltage is 35-40; and
   (C) preparing the steel hollow hemisphere groove 30 degree, assembling the two hemispheres of the hollow tube of the steel tube tower, and confirming that they are not welds or other surface defects on the welded parts and their edges, using manual carbon dioxide and argon gas mixture gas shielded welding, using the air-cooled argon arc welding torch to weld a layer of welding layer by hill-climbing welding, where the welding wire is 2.5mm, JQ.TG50 special welding wire, tungsten is extremely 3.2mm, argon gas flow is 20 at the beginning 28L/minute, the flow rate of argon gas is maintained at about 20L/minute during welding, the purity of argon gas is above 99.95%, the welding arc voltage is 205-225, and the welding speed is 70-80mm/minute, covering the sphere 6 times, after 12 hours of cooling at room temperature, where the cover is made of manual carbon dioxide and argon gas.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the device.
   Sphere (1)
   Reinforcing rib (2)
   Latitudinal reinforcing rib (21)
   Longitudinal reinforcing rib (211)
   Third weft stiffener (214)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - MECHANICAL ENGINEERING - Preferred Conditions: The cover surface needs to be polished and polished before the surface is covered, and the surface is covered with a low temperature to temper the weld. The low temperature is 20-60 degrees C.

PN CN108788385-A; CN108788385-B
TI Welding stainless steel complex board comprises e.g. taking welding parent material and flux-cored wire, subjecting to carbon dioxide gas shielded welding, welding the base layer on the stainless steel side, and polishing the carbon rod.
AU LI S
AE CHINA 22MCC GROUP CORP LTD (CMEG-C)
   CHINA 22MCC GROUP CORP LTD (CMEG-C)
GA 201892851X
AB    NOVELTY - Welding stainless steel complex board comprises (i) taking Q345R and Q345R as welding parent material and THY-51B as flux-cored wire, subjecting to carbon dioxide gas shielded welding, welding the base layer on the stainless steel side, cleaning and polishing the carbon rod, and controlling the temperature between the channels; (ii) taking 904L and Q345R as welding mother material and E385-16 welding rod, using manual arc welding, drying the electrode, welding the surface of the stainless steel double-layer steel plate and welding the transition layer welding by a single-layer multi-pass welding method; (iii) taking 904L and 904L as welding parent metal and E385-16 welding rod, drying the electrode, subjecting to multi-pass welding method, completing the filling and cap welding of the stainless steel welding layer with a stainless steel double layer welding height, and controlling the temperature between the weld channels.
   USE - The method is useful for welding stainless steel complex board.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is simple and convenient to operate; and not only ensures the welding quality, but also improves the welding efficiency.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Welding stainless steel complex board comprises (i) taking Q345R and Q345R as welding parent material and THY-51B as flux-cored wire, subjecting to carbon dioxide gas shielded welding, preheating temperature before welding at 80-150 degrees C, firstly welding the base layer on the stainless steel side, where the welded surface is 6-7 mm lower than the stainless steel surface after welding, cleaning and polishing the carbon rod on the back of the and subjecting the polished grooved surface to MT detection, welding the base layer weld on the side of the carbon steel with a welding height of 2 plus minus 1 mm, and controlling the temperature between the channels at 80-15 degrees C, where the base layer welding is welded by multi-layer multi-pass welding method; (ii) taking 904L and Q345R as welding mother material and E385-16 welding rod, using manual arc welding, drying the electrode at 350-400 degrees C, welding the surface of the stainless steel double-layer steel plate at 3-4 mm and welding the transition layer welding by a single-layer multi-pass welding method, where the temperature between the weld channels is less than 100 degrees C; (iii) taking 904L and 904L as welding parent metal and E385-16 welding rod, drying the electrode at 350-400 degrees C, subjecting to multi-pass welding method, and completing the filling and cap welding of the stainless steel welding layer with a stainless steel double layer welding height of 0-1.5 mm, and controlling the temperature between the weld channels to less than 100 degrees C, where the Q345R low alloy steel is used as substrate, 904L stainless steel as complex layer, and a processing groove i.e. K-type groove
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - METALLURGY - Preferred Components: The upper portion of the K-shaped groove angle is 40-45 degrees , the lower side is 10-15 degrees , the blunt edge of the groove is 0-3 mm and the root gap is 0-3 mm. In the step (i), the chemical composition of the THY-51B flux-cored wire comprises 0.05 wt.% carbon, 1.11 wt.% manganese, 0.35 wt.% silicon, 0.0088 wt.% sulfur, 0.013 wt.% phosphorus, 0.02 wt.% chromium and 0.01 wt.% nickel. In the step (ii) and (iii), the chemical composition of the E385-16 electrode comprises 0.028 wt.% carbon, 1.67 wt.% manganese, 0.58 wt.% silicon, 0.007 wt.% sulfur, 0.015 wt.% phosphorus, 20.52 wt.% chromium, 25.23 wt.% nickel, 4.98 wt.% molybdenum and 1.69 wt.% copper. Preferred Method: The welding process parameter of step (i) is as follows: current is 180-240 A, voltage is 28-35 V, welding speed is 10-22 cm/minute, and gas flow rate is 18-22 l/minute. The step (i) comprises gouging the back carbon rod after the welding of the base on the stainless steel side is completed, polishing, subjecting to MT surface inspection after grinding the planer surface, smoothing the surface of the weld with a grinding wheel after the welding of the base weld on the carbon steel side is completed, removing burr, carrying out UT detection after 24 hours. The welding process parameter of step (ii) and step (iii) is as follows: current is 120-170 A, voltage is 25-32 V, and welding speed is 8-22 cm/minute. In the step (ii), the PT detection is carried out 24 hours after the completion of the transition layer welding. In the step (iii), the UT and PT detection is carried out 24 hours after the completion of the multilayer layer welding.

PN CN108251373-A; CN108251373-B
TI Extracting exosome of mouse brain slice comprises culturing the brain slice which is first cut off from mouse brain tissue, collecting culture medium, and extracting by using exosome extraction kit.
AU YU X
   GUO J
AE UNIV XUZHOU MEDICAL (UNXM-C)
GA 201858138J
AB    NOVELTY - Extracting exosome of mouse brain slice comprises culturing the brain slice which is first cut off from mouse brain tissue, collecting the culture medium, and extracting by using the exosome extraction kit.
   USE - The method is useful for extracting exosome of mouse brain slice.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: is simple, and saves experiment cost; has low requirements on experimenters technology and experimental equipment; and has no influence on downstream experiment, and safe test result.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for exosome of mouse brain slice comprising anesthetizing experimental animal mice with sevoflurane, quickly cutting the head, using scissors to cut skin between the ears until the two nasal holes, turning over the skin, and exposing the skull; removing the mouse skull with ophthalmic scissors, and exposing the whole brain; using the eye tweezers to reach into the olfactory bulb at the lower part of the front of the brain, gently picking up the front end of the brain, cutting the brain nerve and turning the whole brain, placing in the cold hyperosmotic glucose solution pre-introduced with mixed gas of 95% oxygen and 5% carbon dioxide for 30 minutes, allowing to stand for 1-2 minutes; taking out the whole brain tissue, cutting the required brain tissue part with a blade on the ice box to obtain a flat cut surface; quickly using 502 glue to stick the smoothed side of the brain tissue to the buffer tank of the automatic vibration slicer, soaking the brain tissue with hypertonic sugar solution, cutting brain slice with thickness of 300 mu m in the brain area, completing the whole process within 8 minutes, maintaining the temperature of the hypertonic sugar solution at 4 degrees C; transferring the taken brain slices into the Transwell chamber, completely aspirating the hypertonic sugar solution, adding 0.3-0.5 ml brain slice medium to the 24-well culture plate, placing the Transwell chamber into 24-well culture plate, transferring to closed container with inlet gas conduit and output gas conduit, placing closed container in 37 degrees C microbial incubator for overnight culture, where during the cultivation process, input gas conduit is continuously and stably introduces mixed gas of 95% oxygen and 5% carbon dioxide; after the completion of the culture, collecting culture medium, placing in an EP tube, and extracting exosomes by using exosome extraction kit; where the brain slice medium is prepared by mixing DMEM low sugar medium and streptomycin mixture in the volume ratio of 1:100; the hypertonic sugar solution comprises 254 mM sucrose, 10 mM D-glucose, 1.25 mM sodium dihydrogen phosphate, 24 mM sodium bicarbonate, 2 mM magnesium sulfate heptahydrate, 3 mM potassium chloride, and 2 mM calcium chloride dihydrate, dissolving with de-ionized water, introducing mixed gas of 95% oxygen and 5% carbon dioxide for 30 minutes before using.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Method: In the method, the Transwell chamber has a pore size of 400 nm, the Transwell chamber can effectively filter out impurities of large molecules and help to obtain substances with smaller diameters.
DC B04 (Natural products and polymers. Including testing of body fluids (other than blood typing or cell counting), pharmaceuticals or veterinary compounds of unknown structure, testing of microorganisms for pathogenicity, testing of chemicals for mutagenicity or human toxicity and fermentative production of DNA or RNA. General compositions.); C06 (Biotechnology - including plant genetics and veterinary vaccines.); D16 (Fermentation industry - including fermentation equipment, brewing, yeast production, production of pharmaceuticals and other chemicals by fermentation, microbiology, production of vaccines and antibodies, cell and tissue culture and genetic engineering.)

PN WO2022200941-A1
TI Continuous mechanochemical process under gas flow,            comprises e.g. continuously passing gas flow of carbon            dioxide, and separating and recovering low molecular            weight hydrocarbons and non-reacted hydrogen, and            mineral carbonation of olivine.
AU TARAS A
   FARINA V
   CAPPAI L
   ENZO S
   GARRONI S
   MULAS G
AE UNIV SASSARI (UYSA-Non-standard)
GA 2022C4387Q
AB    NOVELTY - Continuous mechanochemical process under gas                flow to carry out the conversion of carbon dioxide                (CO2) into a mixture of high added value chemical                compounds, comprises: (a) continuously passing gas                flow comprising CO2 through reactor equipped with                milling means, and in presence of olivine powder                and water, in liquid or gaseous form, at from room                temperature to &#8804; 100&#176; C, where                the reactor is subjected to motion in the way that                the milling means trigger mechanochemical reaction                that, on one side, produces hydrogen from present                water, and then the hydrogen carries out the                conversion of portion of CO2 into the low molecular                weight hydrocarbons; (b) separating and recovering                low molecular weight hydrocarbons and non-reacted                hydrogen obtained in step (a) from gas mixture                exiting reactor; and (c) at the end of reaction,                separating and recovering solid products of mineral                carbonation of olivine obtained in step (a) from                the reactor.
   USE - The process is useful for continuous                mechanochemical process under gas flow to carry out                the conversion of CO2 into a mixture of high added                value chemical compounds, where the mixture has                mixture of low molecular weight hydrocarbons,                mainly methane, ethylene and ethane, and solid                products of mineral carbonation, mainly magnesium                and iron carbonates (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The process: provides the low molecular weight                hydrocarbons in step (a) which has highest measured                concentrations of 680 ppm for methane, 280 ppm for                ethane, and 100 ppm for ethylene; provides methane                which is the main product with selectivity of 60%,                ethane with selectivity of 20-25%, ethylene with                selectivity of 8-10%; global yields in methane                production of 50%, ethane production of 10-15% and                ethylene production of 10%; is environmental                sustainability properties; and has great potential                interest in the commercial exploitation.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Continuous mechanochemical process under gas                flow to carry out the conversion of carbon dioxide                (CO2) into a mixture of high added value chemical                compounds, comprises: (a) continuously passing gas                flow comprising CO2 through reactor equipped with                milling means, and in presence of olivine powder                and water, in liquid or gaseous form, at from room                temperature to &#8804; 100&#176; C, where                the reactor is subjected to motion in the way that                the milling means trigger mechanochemical reaction                that, on one side, produces hydrogen from present                water, and then the hydrogen carries out the                conversion of portion of CO2 into the low molecular                weight hydrocarbons, on the other side, the other                portion of CO2 reacts with transformation products                of olivine and non-reacted water to give products                of mineral carbonation; (b) separating and                recovering low molecular weight hydrocarbons and                non-reacted hydrogen obtained in step (a) from gas                mixture exiting reactor; and (c) at the end of                reaction, separating and recovering solid products                of mineral carbonation of olivine obtained in step                (a) from the reactor.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The figure illustrates experimental                apparatus.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The motion to which the                  reactor is subjected is rotary motion at speed of                  500-1500 rpm. The rotary motion occurs at speed                  of 600-1400 rpm. Preferred Components: The                  reactor is mill, or jar, equipped with milling                  comprises rotating spherical bodies (balls), the                  mill and the balls is made of an abrasion                  resistant material, hardened stainless steel. The                  stoichiometric ratio of olivine to water is                  1:1-1:3, preferably 1:2.
   EXAMPLE - None given.

PN CN114166989-A
TI Measuring water-soluble salt in alkaline earth, involves adding sieved air-dried alkaline earth into carbon dioxide-free aqueous solution of polyethylene oxide, mixing, centrifuging, and filtering to obtain liquid to be tested, and performing cation detection and anion detection of liquid.
AU LI X
   CHEN Q
   GAO L
   ZHU Y
   LI L
   ZHANG J
   HU Y
   WANG L
   MENG J
AE HEBEI GEOANALYSIS RES CENT (HEBE-Non-standard)
GA 202242270D
AB    NOVELTY - A measuring method of water-soluble salt in alkaline earth, involves (1) adding sieved air-dried alkaline earth into carbon dioxide-free aqueous solution of polyethylene oxide, mixing uniformly, centrifuging, and filtering to obtain liquid to be tested, and (2) performing cation detection and anion detection of the liquid to be tested by using ion chromatography, respectively, where the conditions of the cation detection are provided that the fixed phase is a cationic analysis column of styrene-divinylbenzene polymer with a cross-linking degree of 55% grafted carboxylic acid on the surface, a cation protection column is SH-G-1, the eluent is 4.8-5.2 mmol/L methanesulfonic acid solution, and the inhibitor is SHY-C-3, and the conditions for anion detection are provided that the anion analysis column is SH-AC-3, the anion protection column is SH-G-1, the inhibitor is SHY-A-6, and the eluent is a mixed solution of sodium carbonate and sodium bicarbonate.
   USE - Measuring method of water-soluble salt in alkaline earth.
   ADVANTAGE - The alkaline earth has obvious accumulation effect by adding flocculating agent polyethylene oxide. The solution is clear and the precipitate is tight. The acidity of the leaching liquid is not changed. The ion to be detected is not introduced. The ion chromatography is used to detect the liquid to be tested, capable of quickly and accurately analyzing the cation and the content of the alkaline earth, and anion and contents.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A measuring method of water-soluble salt in alkaline earth, involves (1) adding sieved air-dried alkaline earth into carbon dioxide-free aqueous solution of polyethylene oxide, mixing uniformly, centrifuging, and filtering to obtain liquid to be tested, and (2) performing cation detection and anion detection of the liquid to be tested by using ion chromatography, respectively, where the conditions of the cation detection are provided that the fixed phase is a cationic analysis column of styrene-divinylbenzene polymer with a cross-linking degree of 55% grafted carboxylic acid on the surface, a cation protection column is SH-G-1, the eluent is 4.8-5.2 mmol/L methanesulfonic acid solution, and the inhibitor is SHY-C-3, and the conditions for anion detection are provided that the anion analysis column is SH-AC-3, the anion protection column is SH-G-1, the inhibitor is SHY-A-6, and the eluent is a mixed solution of sodium carbonate and sodium bicarbonate, the concentration of the sodium carbonate in the mixed solution is 3.5-3.7 mmol/L and the concentration of sodium bicarbonate in the mixed solution is 4.4-4.6 mmol/L.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The concentration of methanesulfonic acid is 5 mmol/L. In step (1), the concentration of the carbon dioxide-free aqueous solution of polyethylene oxide is 0.4-0.6 g/L.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The concentration of sodium carbonate in the mixed solution is 3.6 mmol/L. The concentration of sodium bicarbonate in the mixed solution is 4.5 mmol/L. In the condition of cation detection, the quantitative loop is 25 muL. THE mass ratio of the air-dried alkaline earth to the carbon dioxide-free aqueous solution of polyethylene oxide is 1:(4-6). Preferred Properties: The model of the cation analysis column is SH-CC-3, and the size is 100 mm x 4.6 mm. The specification of the cation protection column and the anion protection column is 50 mm x 4.6 mm. The specification of the anion analysis column is 250 mm x 4 mm. In step (1), the aperture of the sieve is 1.5-3 mm. Preferred Process: In step (1), the filtering process is specifically performed by filtering the centrifugal liquid obtained by centrifugation through a filter membrane with a pore size of 0.22 mum for 2-3 times, and passing through a divinyl column to obtain liquid to be tested.

TI Preparing nickel (II) ion-ferric ion-titanium dioxide-layered double hydroxides comprises e.g. adding nickel(II) nitrate and ferric nitrate into deionized water, adding urea and sodium citrate, sonicating, washing and drying and calcining.
AU DANG R
   MA X
   GUO W
   KANG Y
   LI X
   GAO Y
   LI Y
   MA Y
   ZHANG Y
   LIU Z
   GUO T
   LI C
AE UNIV YULIN (UNYL-C)
GA 202109241P
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing nickel (II) ion-ferric ion-titanium dioxide-layered double hydroxides comprises e.g. adding nickel(II) nitrate and ferric nitrate into deionized water, and adding urea and sodium citrate, sonicating uniformly, and adding the resulting mixed solution into a reaction kettle, cooling, filtering, washing and drying, adding sodium chloride in deionized water, adding concentrated hydrochloric acid, adding potassium fluorotitanate, stirring, dropping concentrated hydrochloric acid, then reacting, allowing to stand, discarding the supernatant, and adding deionized water, blowing nitrogen and stirring, quickly filtering, rinsing with absolute ethanol, and drying, adding into a muffle furnace, calcining at a constant temperature of 300-500 degrees C for 3-5 hours to obtain final product.
   USE - The nickel (II) ion-ferric ion-titanium dioxide-layered double hydroxides are useful in photocatalytic oxidation desulfurization (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The nickel (II) ion-ferric ion-titanium dioxide-layered double hydroxides have dibenzothiophene conversion rate of 98%; and maintains high catalytic performance after being recycled and reused for 5 times.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing nickel (II) ion-ferric ion-titanium dioxide-layered double hydroxides comprises (i) adding nickel(II) nitrate and ferric nitrate into deionized water, and adding urea and sodium citrate, sonicating uniformly, and adding the resulting mixed solution into a reaction kettle, reacting at 140-160 degrees C for 36-60 hours under airtight conditions, cooling to room temperature, filtering, washing and drying at room temperature to obtain nickel (II) ion-ferric ion-carbonate ion-layered double hydroxides, (ii) dissolving sodium chloride in deionized water, and adding concentrated hydrochloric acid to remove carbon dioxide in ionized water, then adding nickel (II) ion-ferric ion-carbonate ion-layered double hydroxides, and blowing in nitrogen to remove carbon dioxide, reacting under airtight conditions at room temperature for 6-10 hours, then allowing to stand, suction filtering, and drying in the shade to obtain nickel (II) ion-ferric ion-chloride ion-layered double hydroxides, (iii) adding potassium fluorotitanate into deionized water at 40-60 degrees C, stirring to dissolve potassium fluorotitanate, dropping concentrated hydrochloric acid to remove carbon dioxide in the ionized water, then adding nickel (II) ion-ferric ion-chloride ion-layered double hydroxides, blowing in nitrogen and reacting at a constant temperature for 30-60 minutes, stopping the nitrogen flow and heating, and reacting at room temperature for 10-15 hours under airtight conditions, allowing to stand for 20-40 minutes, discarding the supernatant, and adding deionized water at 40-60 degrees C, blowing nitrogen and stirring at a constant temperature of 40-60 degrees C for 15-30 minutes, quickly filtering, rinsing with absolute ethanol, and drying to obtain nickel (II) ion-ferric ion-hexafluorotitanate-layered double hydroxides, and (iv) adding nickel (II) ion-ferric ion-hexafluorotitanate-layered double hydroxides into a muffle furnace, calcining at a constant temperature of 300-500 degrees C for 3-5 hours to obtain final product.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: In step (i), the reaction is carried out at 150 degrees C for 48 hours under airtight conditions. The step (iii), comprises adding potassium fluorotitanate into deionized water at 50 degrees C, stirring to dissolve potassium fluorotitanate, dropping concentrated hydrochloric acid to remove carbon dioxide in the ionized water, then adding nickel (II) ion-ferric ion-chloride ion-layered double hydroxides, blowing in nitrogen and reacting at constant temperature for 40 minutes, stop nitrogen gas and heating, reacting at room temperature for 12 hours under airtight conditions, and allowing to stand for 30 minutes, discarding the supernatant, and adding 50 degrees C deionized water, adding nitrogen gas, and stirring the mixture at a constant temperature of 50 degrees C for 20 minutes. In step (iv), nickel (II) ion-ferric ion-hexafluorotitanate-layered double hydroxides are placed in a muffle furnace and calcined at a constant temperature of 400 degrees C for 4 hours. In step (iv), the heating rate of calcination is 0.5-2 degrees C/minute. Preferred Components: In step (i), the ratio of the amount of nickel(II) nitrate to ferric nitrate, urea, and sodium citrate is 2-4:1:8.5-10:2-3. The total concentration of nickel(II) ion and ferric ion in the resulting mixed solution is controlled to be 40-60 mmol/l. In step (ii), the concentration of sodium chloride in the deionized water is controlled to be 1-3 mol/l, the concentration of hydrochloric acid is 3.3-4 mmol/l, and the concentration of nickel (II) ion-ferric ion-carbonate ion-layered double hydroxides is 0.5-2 g/l. In step (iii), the concentration of potassium fluorotitanate in deionized water is controlled to 0.8-1.5 mol/l, the concentration of hydrochloric acid to 3.3-4 mmol/l, and the concentration of nickel (II) ion-ferric ion-chloride ion-layered double hydroxides to 0.5-2 g/ l.

PN CN111440763-A
TI Application of recombinant LC3C protein in improving rate of sheep embryo blastocysts by obtaining sheep ovary, picking oocytes, incubating with recombinant LC3C protein, fertilizing with semen, processing and calculating blastocyst rate.
AU WU Y
   LIN J
   WANG L
   CHEN Y
   LI X
   LIU L

   ZHANG L
   JIANG X
   ZHANG X
   HUANG J
AE XINJIANG ACAD ANIMAL SCI BIOTECHNOLOGY (XINJ-Non-standard)
GA 2020717123
AB    NOVELTY - Application method of recombinant LC3C protein in improving rate of sheep embryo blastocysts in vitro involves (a) obtaining an ovary of slaughtered sheep, placing in a normal saline and washing with saline, (b) aspirating follicles on the surface of the ovary with a syringe, placing in a container, picking oocytes, washing, adding to the in vitro maturation solution added with recombinant LC3C protein and incubating, (c) washing the oocytes with sheep in vitro fertilization fluid and adding to the droplets containing the in vitro fertilization fluid, (d) adding the thawed frozen semen to the bottom of the test tube containing in vitro fertilization fluid, taking suspension, centrifuging, discarding supernatant, calculating density, adding to the in vitro fertilization droplet and incubating with the oocyte, and (e) taking out the early embryos fertilized, washing, culturing, picking oocytes, calculating the cleavage rate, culturing and then calculating the blastocyst rate.
   USE - The application method of recombinant LC3C protein is useful for improving rate of sheep embryo blastocysts in vitro.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Application method of recombinant LC3C protein in improving rate of sheep embryo blastocysts in vitro involves (a) obtaining an ovary of slaughtered sheep that day, placing in a normal saline containing 1000 international unit (IU)/ml penicillin and 1000 IU/ml streptomycin at a temperature of 30-35 degrees C, taking it back to the laboratory within 3-5 hours and washing with saline 3-4 times, (b) aspirating 2-5 mm follicles on the surface of the ovary with a syringe that sucks 2 ml oval aspiration fluid, placing the follicle fluid recovered from the syringe in a container, picking out the oocytes under the microscope, washing 3-5 times with in vitro maturation solution, adding to the in vitro maturation solution added with recombinant LC3C protein and incubating in a carbon dioxide incubator with 5% carbon dioxide, 95% air and temperature of 38.6 degrees C for 22-24 hours, (c) washing the oocytes with sheep in vitro fertilization fluid for 2-4 times, and adding to the droplets containing the in vitro fertilization fluid at a density of 25-30 portions/drop, (d) adding the thawed frozen semen to the bottom of the test tube containing the in vitro fertilization fluid, 20-25 minutes upstream of the carbon dioxide incubator, taking upper sperm suspension, centrifuging at 1500 revolutions/minute (rpm) for 4-5 minutes, then discarding the supernatant, calculating the density with the remaining sperm sediment, adding 2-4x 106 cells/ml to the in vitro fertilization droplet of step (c) and incubating with the oocyte, and (e) taking out the early embryos fertilized 20-22 hours in step (d), washing for 3-4 times in the in vitro culture medium IVC, culturing in the in vitro culture medium for 24-26 hours, picking out the non-cleavage oocytes, calculating the cleavage rate, continuing the culture for 144-168 hours and then calculating the blastocyst rate.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Method: Preferred Method: The recombinant LC3C protein is prepared by transforming the constructed recombinant plasmid pET28a-MAP1LC30C containing the LC3C protein gene MAP1LC3C into Escherichia coli strain BL21, allowing the transformed bacteria to express the fusion protein under the induction of 0.2 mM isopropyl beta -d-1-thiogalactopyranoside (IPTG), performing sodium dodecyl sulfate polyacrylamide gel electrophoresis (SDS-PAGE) analysis showed that the recombinant protein LC3C was highly expressed as inclusion bodies in E.coli strain BL21, washing the precipitates lysed by the ultrasonic lysis of the bacterial cells with 2% Triton-X-100 (RTM: Nonionic surfactant having a hydrophilic polyethylene oxide chain and an aromatic hydrocarbon lipophilic or hydrophobic group), 0.2% deoxycholic acid and 2 M urea, after being dissolved in 8 M urea, purifying by nickel-nitrilotriacetic acid (NTA) affinity chromatography, ultrafiltering the elution buffer, replacing with tris/borate/EDTA (TGE) buffer for elution and storage, where the TGE buffer (pH 8) comprises 50 mM tris(hydroxymethyl)aminomethane hydrochloride (Tris-HCl), 0.5 mM EDTA, 50 mM sodium chloride and 5% glycerol, and identifying the final purified His-LC3C as recombinant LC3C protein by SDS-PAGE. The concentration of the recombinant LC3C protein is 80 ng/ml.

TI Use method of recombinant PAGE4 protein in increasing blastocyst rate of sheep in vitro embryos comprises e.g. obtaining ovary, washing, picking up oocytes, incubating, adding thawed frozen sperm, centrifuging, and discarding.
AU LIN J

AE XINJIANG ACAD ANIMAL SCI BIOTECHNOLOGY (XINJ-Non-standard)
GA 202058062U
AB    NOVELTY - Use method of recombinant PAGE4 protein in increasing blastocyst rate of sheep in vitro embryos comprises e.g. obtaining ovary of sheep slaughtered that day, placing it into physiological saline washing with physiological saline, placing follicular fluid recovered in syringe in vessel, and picking up oocytes under microscope, adding it to in vitro maturation solution supplemented with recombinant PAGE4 protein, incubating, washing oocytes, adding thawed frozen sperm, centrifuging, discarding supernatant and using remaining sperm pellet for density calculation, adding in vitro fertilization droplet to oocytes at density of 2-4x 106/ml, and (v) selecting early embryos fertilized for 20-22 hours, washing them 3-4 times in in vitro culture medium IVC, and incubating in the in vitro culture medium for 24-26 hours, picking out non-cleaved oocytes, counting cleavage rate, and counting blastocyst rate after 144-168 hours of continuous culture.
   USE - The method is useful for using recombinant PAGE4 protein in increasing blastocyst rate of sheep in vitro embryos.
   ADVANTAGE - The method improves blastocyst rate of sheep in vitro embryos.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Use method of recombinant PAGE4 protein in increasing blastocyst rate of sheep in vitro embryos comprises (i) obtaining ovary of sheep slaughtered that day, placing it into physiological saline containing 1000 IU/ml penicillin and 1000 IU/mL streptomycin at temperature of 30-35 degrees C, bringing it back to laboratory within 3-5 hours and washing it 3-4 times with physiological saline, (ii) using syringe with 2 ml ovarian fluid to aspirate 2-5 mm of follicles on surface of the ovary, placing follicular fluid recovered in syringe in vessel, and picking up oocytes under microscope, washing 3-5 times with in vitro maturation solution, adding it to in vitro maturation solution supplemented with recombinant PAGE4 protein, and incubating for 22-24 hours in carbon dioxide incubator with 5% carbon dioxide, 95% air, and temperature of 38.6 degrees C, (iii) washing oocytes with sheep in vitro fertilization fluid 2-4 times, adding it to droplets containing in vitro fertilization fluid at density of 25-30 pieces/drop, (iv) adding thawed frozen sperm to bottom of test tube containing in vitro fertilization fluid, and selecting upper layer of sperm suspension for 20-25 minutes upstream of carbon dioxide incubator, centrifuging at 1500 revolutions/minute for 4-5 minutes, discarding supernatant and using remaining sperm pellet for density calculation, adding in vitro fertilization droplet to oocytes at density of 2-4x 106/ml, and (v) selecting early embryos fertilized for 20-22 hours, washing them 3-4 times in in vitro culture medium IVC, and incubating in the in vitro culture medium for 24-26 hours, picking out non-cleaved oocytes, counting cleavage rate, and counting blastocyst rate after 144-168 hours of continuous culture.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Components: The preparation of recombinant PAGE4 protein comprises transforming constructed recombinant plasmid pET28a-PAGE4 containing PAGE4 protein gene PAGE4 into Escherichia coli BL21, where the transformed bacteria expressed the fusion protein under the induction of 0.2 mM IPTG, SDS-PAGE analysis showed that the recombinant protein PAGE4 is highly expressed in form of inclusion bodies in Escherichia coli BL21, washing ultrasonically lysed pellets with 2% TritonX-100, 0.2% deoxycholic acid and 2 M urea, dissolving in 8 M urea and purifying by Ni-NTA affinity chromatography, after ultrafiltration, replacing elution buffer with TGE buffer and eluting again and storing, where the ratio of TGE buffer is 50 mMTris-HCl, 0.5 mM ethylenediaminetetraacetic acid, 50 mM sodium chloride, and 5% glycerin, and the pH is adjusted to 8, and identifying purified His-PAGE4 as recombinant PAGE4 protein by SDS-PAGE. The recombinant PAGE4 protein concentration is 160 ng/ml.

TI Improved medium useful for culturing cytokine-induced killer cells, contains fetal bovine serum, glutamine, penicillin, streptomycin, 4-(2-hydroxyethyl)-1-piperazineethanesulfonic acid, sodium pyruvate, nucleotides and deoxynucleotides.
AU HOU Z
   MENG M
   WANG W
AE HOU Z (HOUZ-Individual)
   MENG M (MENG-Individual)
   WANG W (WANG-Individual)
GA 202020905E
AB    NOVELTY - Improved medium comprises 100-200 ml/l fetal bovine serum (FBS), 1-2 mmol/l glutamine, 100 mu g/l penicillin, 100 mu g/l streptomycin, 20-100 mmol/l 4-(2-hydroxyethyl)-1-piperazineethanesulfonic acid (HEPES), 8-15 mmol/l sodium pyruvate, 20-50 mu mol/l beta -mercaptoethanol and suitable amount of nucleotides and deoxynucleotides.
   USE - The improved medium is useful for culturing CIK cells.
   ADVANTAGE - The improved medium significantly increases number and enhances tumor killing effect of CIK cells, verifies CIK cells antitumor activity and safety, removes an important obstacle in the field of CIK cell immunotherapy and provides important theoretical support for the use of CIK cells as autologous cell therapy products to serve the clinic.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for method for using the improved medium, which involves (a) using Ficoll (RTM: Hydrophilic polysaccharide) lymphocyte separation solution to separate lymphocytes, adjusting the cell density to 1x 109 cells/l and suspending in medium, (b) adding 500-1000 U/ml interferon- gamma (IFN- gamma ) to the medium, culturing at 37 degrees C under 5% carbon dioxide for 24 hours, then adding 50-100 ng/ml mouse anti-human cluster of differentiation 3 (CD3) monoclonal antibody and 500-1000 U/ml interleukin-2 (IL-2), culturing at 37 degrees C under 5% carbon dioxide for 3 days, 6 days, 9 days, 12 days, 15 days and 18 days to detect cell proliferation level, cell phenotype and cytotoxic activity in vitro, (c) using trypan blue exclusion to count the cells and detect the cell proliferation level on the day of culture and 3 days, 6 days, 9 days, 12 days, 15 days and 18 days after culture, (d) analyzing the CD3 + CD56 + percentage of the cells by flow cytometry to determine the cell phenotype on the day of culture and 3 days, 6 days, 9 days, 12 days, 15 days and 18 days and (e) culturing the cytokine-induced killer cells for 15 days, measuring the cytotoxic activities of the lung adenocarcinoma cell line SPC-A-1 and the gastric adenocarcinoma cell line BGC-823, adjusting the tumor cell concentration to 1x 105 cells/ml, mixing with CIK cells in a ratio of 1:4, 1:8 or 1:16, setting three replicates at each concentration, setting a tumor cell control and a blank control, culturing for 48 hours, discarding the supernatant, washing the cells 3 times with medium, adding 50 mu l/well 5 mg/ml 3-(4,5-dimethylthiazol-2-yl)-2,5-diphenyl tetrazolium bromide (MTT), culturing at 37 degrees C under 5% carbon dioxide for 4 hours, adding 100 mu l/well dimethyl sulfoxide, incubating until the crystals are dissolved, measuring absorbance A value at 570 nm wavelength and calculating the tumor killing rate.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Medium: The improved medium comprises 100 ml/l FBS, 2 mmol/l glutamine, 100 mu g/l penicillin, 100 mu g/l streptomycin, 20 mmol/l HEPES, 8 mmol/l sodium pyruvate, 20 mu mol/l beta -mercaptoethanol and suitable amount of nucleotides and deoxynucleotides. The improved medium further comprises 1000 U/ml IFN- gamma , 50 ng/ml mouse anti-human CD3 monoclonal antibody and 1000 U/ml IL-2. Preferred Method: In the step (d), the cells are cultured to the 13th to 14th days, and then the cells are harvested.

TI Carbon nano-particle production system comprises carbon source gas inlet pipe, set of hyper-electric flow reactor, separator and product storage tank, where each group of hyper-electric flow reactor is connected in series through connecting pipeline.
AU HONG K
AE HONG K (HONG-Individual)
GA 202015015C
AB    (CN210048496-U)   NOVELTY - The utility model claims a production system of carbon nano particles. The utility model claims a carbon nano-particle production system comprising a carbon source inlet pipe orderly connected; at least a group of hyper-electric flow reactor, a separator and a product storage tank, each group of the super pixel flow reactor is composed of orderly connected in series through the connecting pipeline along the air flow direction of a positive-polarity electric current reactor and a negative polarity hyper-electric flow reactor. the production system uses hyper-electric flow to gas discharge, large processing amount, low energy consumption and carbon dioxide decomposition efficiency, the method can high product quality, small defect, carbon nano-particles of industrial requirements, production scale can be enlarged.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for producing carbon nanoparticles by using the carbon nanoparticles production system, which involves using carbon dioxide-containing gas as a carbon source, allowing to enter the superelectron flow reactor through the carbon source inlet pipe; feeding the mixture obtained by decomposition to the separator, and collecting the carbon nano particles and sending to the product storage tank.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows schematic diagram of production system for carbon nanoparticles. (Drawing includes non-English language text).
   Carbon source air inlet pipe (1)
   Connecting pipe (2)
   Separator (3)
   Product storage tank (4)
   Positive polarity superelectron flow reactor (11)
   Negative polarity superelectron flow reactor (12)
EA    NOVELTY - Production system comprises a carbon source air inlet pipe (1), super-electron flow reactor, a separator (3) and a product storage tank (4) connected in sequence. Each group of superelectron flow reactors consists of a positive polarity superelectron flow reactor (11) and a negative polarity superelectron flow reactor (12) connected in series along the airflow direction through a connecting pipe (2). The positive polarity superelectron flow reactor includes a first insulating shell having a first inlet and a first outlet, and the first inlet is in communication with the carbon source inlet pipe. Discharge components are arranged in the first insulating housing, and the discharge assembly includes printed circuit board (PCB) with discharge needles uniformly distributed on both sides and two discharge boards arranged on both sides of PCB board, and discharge needles are uniformly distributed on opposite side of discharge board and PCB board.
   USE - Production system for carbon nanoparticles (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The production system uses ultra-electron flow to discharge the gas, which has large processing capacity, low energy consumption, and high carbon dioxide decomposition efficiency. It can produce carbon nanoparticles with high quality by-products, small defects, and meet industrial needs. The production scale can be expanded.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The volume percentage of carbon dioxide in the carbon source is more than 1%, preferably more than 99%. The flow velocity of the carbon source is below 5 m/second, preferably below 1 m/second. The intake air volume of the carbon source is 100-200,000 m3/hour. When the PCB board and the discharge plate are connected in parallel with the positive electrode of a first high-voltage direct current power supply, the working voltage of the first high-voltage direct current power supply is 80-300 KiloVolt, and the working current is 0.1-8 Ampere. When any one of the hollow electrodes is connected to the negative electrode of a second high voltage direct current power supply, the working voltage of the second high-voltage DC power supply is 80-300 KiloVolt, and the working current is 0.1-8 Ampere.

AE GUANGZHOU HUANGCHUAN OCEANOGRAPHIC ENG (GUAN-Non-standard)
GA 202004112H
AB    NOVELTY - Welding method of low-alloy high-strength steel T-shaped full penetration weld joint transverse welding position involves welding divided edge processing, cleaning into welding web, and removing oxide layer and impurities around the welding slope port with 20-30 mm range to obtain web panel with low-alloy high-intensity structural steel Q390 and Q420; front welding groove filling, welding selecting AWSA-5.20 standard specified in E71T-1C type into medicine core welding wire with 1.2 mm filler wire diameter and with not greater than 99.5% carbon dioxide gas as protective gas and carrying out front welding groove filling welding with 220-240 A welding current, 28-30 V welding voltage, 15-20 mm welding wire dry extension length and 20-25 L/minute protective gas flow; and using direct current reverse polarity mode welding and back side welding seam of divided edge welding.
   USE - Method for welding low-alloy high-strength steel T-shaped full penetration weld joint transverse welding position.
   ADVANTAGE - The method has improved welding efficiency and low cost, and can avoid welding seam back gouging root removing procedure, shorten T-shaped full welding angle joint production period and reduce material consumption generated due to air gouging groove filling.

TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - METALLURGY - Preferred Method: The method comprises welding each welding seam under strict slag work and controlling channel at not less than 230 degrees C. Preferred Components: The mixed gas composition comprises 80% argon and 20% carbon dioxide.

PT P
PN CN110079361-A
TI Method for producing hydrogen rich syngas, involves making petroleum coke into fine powder, transporting petroleum coke powder into gasifier followed by spraying gasifying agent and adjusting composition and flow rate of gasifying agent.
AU WANG Q
   ZHAO J
   ZHANG H
   FANG Y
   YANG X
   NIE W
   ZHAO W
   CAO W
   CAO X
   YANG T
AE CHINA HUANQIU CONTRACTING & ENG CORP (CNPC-C)
   INST COAL CHEM CHINESE ACAD SCI (CAXI-C)
GA 201969212X
AB    NOVELTY - A hydrogen-rich syngas producing method involves making petroleum coke into petroleum coke fine powder. The petroleum coke fine powder is transported into a gasifier. A gasifying agent composed of oxygen, water vapor, carbon dioxide, nitrogen is sprayed from the feed burner into the gasifier. The composition and flow rate of the gasifying agent are adjusted and the gasification reaction is controlled. The high temperature gas carries the unreacted ash flowing downward and sprayed into the chilled water for cooling. The gas carrying ash is passed through the diversion tube into the slag pool at the bottom of the gasification furnace and the circulating washing water entered the slag pool from the water inlet. The gas and ash are washed and separated in the slag pool, and the gas is cooled, discharged from the gas outlet at the upper part of the slag pool, the ash is carried by the washing black water and discharged from the black water outlet at the lower part of the slag pool.
   USE - Method for producing hydrogen-rich syngas.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables ensuring high-efficiency gasification of high-sulfur inferior petroleum coke, high heat efficiency and less pollutant emission.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A hydrogen-rich syngas producing method involves making petroleum coke into petroleum coke fine powder having a particle diameter of less than 200 mu m. The petroleum coke fine powder is transported into the gasifier by using a gas dense phase conveying method, high pressure nitrogen or carbon dioxide gas to be sent to a feed burner located at the top of the gasification furnace. A gasifying agent composed of oxygen, water vapor, carbon dioxide, nitrogen is sprayed from the feed burner into the gasifier. The composition and flow rate of the gasifying agent are adjusted and the gasification reaction is controlled at 1200-1800 degrees C and 0.1-8.0 MPa, where the gas-solid contact time is 5-20 sec and the petroleum coke reacts with the gasifying agent to form a high-temperature gas rich in carbon monoxide and hydrogen. The high temperature gas carries the unreacted ash flowing downward and sprayed into the chilled water for cooling, so that the gas is cooled to 750-950 degrees C. The gas carrying ash is passed through the diversion tube into the slag pool at the bottom of the gasification furnace and the circulating washing water entered the slag pool from the water inlet. The gas and ash are washed and separated in the slag pool, and the gas is cooled to 150-350 degrees C, discharged from the gas outlet at the upper part of the slag pool, the ash is carried by the washing black water and discharged from the black water outlet at the lower part of the slag pool. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for a petroleum coke gas flow bed gasification furnace.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The high pressure nitrogen or carbon dioxide gas delivery is present in a ratio of 5-20 kg petroleum coke/kg gas. The oxygen is present in a concentration of 60-90 vol.%, the water vapor is present in a concentration of 0-40 vol.%, the carbon dioxide is present in a concentration of 0-40 vol.% and the nitrogen is present in a concentration of 0-10 vol.%. The oxygen and petroleum coke fine powder are present in a consumption ratio of 0.5-1.0 Nm3 oxygen/kg petroleum coke fine powder, the water vapor and petroleum coke fine powder are present in a consumption ratio of 0-0.5 kg water vapor/kg petroleum coke fine powder, the carbon dioxide and petroleum coke fine powder are present in a consumption ratio of 0-0.5 Nm3 carbon dioxide/kg petroleum coke fine powder. The chilled water sprayed into the gasifier is industrial wastewater of 20-60 degrees C, tap water or industrial demineralized water or boiler saturated water of 100-200 degrees C and the amount is 0.10-0.50 kg water/kg petroleum coke fine powder. The dry composition of the crude syngas is 30-50 vol.% hydrogen, 35-60 vol.% cabon monoxide, 5-20 vol.% carbon dioxide, and 1-10 vol.% nitrogen and other impurity gases.

TI Large-scale culturing of human respiratory syncytial virus by digesting cells of human epithelial type 2 cells in culture bottle by using pancreatic enzyme digestive fluid, performing cell passage amplification, culturing and inoculating.
AU ZHOU W
   LI G
   ZHAO Q
   SHI H
   ZHOU L
   SUN J

   FU G
   WU X
   YANG Z
AE ZHENGZHOU YIMEINUO BIOTECHNOLOGY CO LTD (ZHEN-Non-standard)
GA 201948787V
AB    NOVELTY - Method for large-scale culturing of human respiratory syncytial virus involves (a) digesting the cells of human epithelial type 2 (HEp-2) cells which are full of single layers in a culture bottle by using pancreatic enzyme digestive fluid, performing cell passage amplification and culturing, (b) using a cell factory, adding cell suspension and carrying out large-scale amplification on cells, (c) inoculating the human respiratory syncytial virus to HEp-2 cells at multiplicity of infection (MOI), using dulbecco's modified eagle medium (DMEM) culture medium containing 2% calf serum, and culturing, (d) (i) performing liquid change on the inoculated first day, replacing the culture medium into a serum-free culture medium and culturing and (ii) during the culture process, detecting a virus titer in the supernatant every day by using an ELISA method and drawing the virus proliferation curve and (e) collecting the virus culture supernatant of each bottle, combining the virus and leaving still.
   USE - The method is useful for large-scale culturing of human respiratory syncytial virus.
   ADVANTAGE - The method replaces the culture medium by a serum-free culture medium on the first day after virus inoculation, greatly reduces the content of calf serum in the harvested virus solution, reduces the step of downstream purification and provides a guarantee for the development of human respiratory syncytial virus vaccine.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Method for large-scale culturing of human respiratory syncytial virus involves (a) digesting the cells of human epithelial type 2 (HEp-2) cells which are full of single layers in a culture bottle by using pancreatic enzyme digestive fluid, carrying out cell passage amplification at a ratio of 1:(3-4) and culturing at 37 degrees C by using 5% carbon dioxide, (b) using a cell factory, adding the cell suspension obtained after digestion in step (a) to 200 ml of the layer and carrying out large-scale amplification on cells, (c) inoculating the human respiratory syncytial virus to HEp-2 cells with the length full of a single layer at the bottom of the cell factory at 0.01-0.1 multiplicity of infection (MOI), using dulbecco's modified eagle medium (DMEM) culture medium containing 2% calf serum and culturing at 34 plus minus 1 degrees C by using 5% carbon dioxide, (d) (i) carrying out liquid change on the inoculated first day, replacing the culture medium into a serum-free culture medium and culturing at 34 plus minus 1 degrees C by using 5% carbon dioxide and (ii) during the culture process, detecting a virus titer in the supernatant every day by using an ELISA method and drawing the virus proliferation curve and (e) collecting the virus culture supernatant of each bottle after the virus titer reaches the highest value, combining the virus in the same batch and leaving still at 2-8 degrees C.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Method: The serum-free culture medium used after step (d) of liquid change is prepared by adding 1% serum substitute to DMEM culture medium.

TI Production of phosphoric acid by using combination of reverberatory furnace and shaft kiln, involves taking out mixed kiln gas to absorption tower, subjecting to hydration absorption, obtaining phosphoric acid and recovering tail gas.
AU LIN M
   YIN H
   LIANG J
AE SINO-LINCHEM INT INC (SINO-Non-standard)
GA 201948289Q
AB    NOVELTY - The production of phosphoric acid involves (1) preheating the reverberatory furnace, maintaining the temperature in the reverberatory furnace at 1000-1500 degrees C, using the induced draft fan to transport hot gas of the reverberating furnace to vertical kiln, and preheating vertical kiln to 400-900 degrees C, (2) mixing phosphate rock, silica and coke and sending to the vertical kiln from upper inlet of the vertical kiln, and raw materials meet with the hot gas rising from the reverberating furnace in the vertical kiln to obtain preheating, (3) generating phosphorus elemental gas and carbon monoxide gas by the reduction reaction rise to the upper gas space of reverberatory furnace and oxidizing with dry air passing through upper portion of reverberating furnace to generate phosphorus pentoxide and carbon dioxide and (4) taking out mixed kiln gas obtained in the step (3) to absorption tower, subjecting to hydration absorption, obtaining phosphoric acid and recovering and treating tail gas.
   USE - Production of phosphoric acid by using combination of reverberatory furnace and shaft kiln.
   ADVANTAGE - The process utilizes the combination of the vertical kiln and the reverberatory furnace to prepare phosphoric acid for the first time, and the production efficiency is increased by more than 10%, the time is reduced by more than 20%, and the phosphorus yield is increased by more than 3%.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - The production of phosphoric acid involves (1) preheating the reverberatory furnace, maintaining the temperature in the reverberatory furnace at 1000-1500 degrees C, using the induced draft fan to transport the hot gas of the reverberating furnace to the vertical kiln, and preheating the vertical kiln to 400-900 degrees C, (2) mixing phosphate rock, silica and coke and sending to the vertical kiln from the upper inlet of the vertical kiln, and the raw materials meet with the hot gas rising from the reverberating furnace in the vertical kiln to obtain preheating, (3) entering reverberatory furnace from the bottom of the shaft kiln and heating up, the raw material is in a molten state and undergoes a reduction reaction at the high temperature of the reverberatory furnace, generating phosphorus elemental gas and carbon monoxide gas by the reduction reaction rise to the upper gas space of the reverberatory furnace, and oxidizing with the dry air passing through the upper portion of the reverberating furnace to generate phosphorus pentoxide and carbon dioxide, including phosphorus pentoxide, carbon dioxide and inert gas, entering the vertical kiln from the reverberatory furnace by the high-temperature mixed kiln gas of other gases and preheating the raw materials entering the vertical kiln and (4) taking out the mixed kiln gas obtained in the step (3) to the absorption tower, subjecting to hydration absorption, obtaining phosphoric acid and recovering and treating tail gas. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for device for producing phosphoric acid by using a reverberatory furnace in combination with a shaft kiln.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The temperature in the reverberatory furnace in the step (1) is 1100-1400 degrees C, and the temperature in the vertical kiln is 500-700 degrees C. Preferred Process: The liquefied gas or oil is burned to preheat the reverberatory furnace. The yellow phosphorus is added to the reverberatory furnace and burned to preheat the reverberatory furnace. Preferred Composition: The mass ratio of raw materials is phosphate rock, silica and coke is 1:0.15-0.6:0.5-1.9.

PN CN108913887-A
TI Producing zinc oxide using zinc-containing raw ore comprises e.g. mixing ground zinc-containing ore with first extracting agent, filtering to obtain first leachate, purifying first leachate and adding calcium oxide and/or calcium hydroxide.
AU YUE H
   LI S
   LONG Z
   JIANG T
   LIU Q
AE CHONGQING DONGQUN TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (CHON-Non-standard)
GA 2018983711
AB    NOVELTY - Producing zinc oxide using zinc-containing raw ore comprises e.g. mixing ground zinc-containing ore with first extracting agent and filtering to obtain first leachate, where first leaching agent is mixed aqueous solution of ammonia and ammonium bicarbonate or mixed aqueous solution of ammonia and ammonium carbonate or mixed aqueous solution of ammonia, ammonium bicarbonate and ammonium carbonate, optionally, purifying first leachate obtained in leaching step, adding calcium oxide and/or calcium hydroxide to first leachate, stirring, filtering to obtain first solid and first filtrate, adding calcium hydroxide and/or calcium oxide to first filtrate, stirring and filtering to obtain second solid and second filtrate, taking second solid for calcination, adding second leaching agent to calcined product obtained in primary calcination, stirring, filtering to obtain second leaching solution and introducing pressurized carbon dioxide into second leachate to obtain slurry containing crystals.
   USE - The method is useful for producing zinc oxide using zinc-containing raw ore.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is economical, environmentally-friendly, does not require steaming, is simple and easy to operate, reduces energy consumption, avoids high temperature and high pressure safety hazards and device corrosion problems caused by ammonia evaporation, overcomes recovery rate and quality grade, pollution is small and auxiliary materials can be recycled.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Producing zinc oxide using zinc-containing raw ore comprises mixing ground zinc-containing ore with first extracting agent and filtering to obtain first leachate, where first leaching agent is mixed aqueous solution of ammonia and ammonium bicarbonate or mixed aqueous solution of ammonia and ammonium carbonate or mixed aqueous solution of ammonia, ammonium bicarbonate and ammonium carbonate, optionally, purifying first leachate obtained in leaching step, adding calcium oxide and/or calcium hydroxide to first leachate, stirring and filtering to obtain first solid and first filtrate, adding calcium hydroxide and/or calcium oxide to first filtrate, stirring and filtering to obtain second solid and second filtrate, taking second solid for calcination, where calcination temperature is 150-1050 degrees C, preferably 150-350 degrees C, adding second leaching agent to calcined product obtained in the primary calcination step, stirring, filtering to obtain second leaching solution, where, second leaching agent is mixed aqueous solution of ammonia and ammonium bicarbonate or mixed aqueous solution of ammonia and ammonium carbonate, or a mixed aqueous solution of ammonia, ammonium bicarbonate and ammonium carbonate, introducing pressurized carbon dioxide into the second leachate to obtain a slurry containing crystals, filtering slurry obtained in the press crystallization step in a sealed environment to obtain a third solid and a third filtrate, depressing third filtrate to normal pressure to decompose a part of the ammonium carbonate in the third filtrate into ammonia and carbon dioxide, optionally, rinsing third solid with water, drying third solid and calcining at 450-900 degrees C.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The concentration of total ammonia in the first leaching agent is 5-15% and concentration of active carbonate in first leaching agent comprises C first extracting agent carbonate = (n ore total zinc-n ore zinc carbonate) x a/V first extracting agent, C is first extracting agent carbonate is molar concentration of active carbonate in the first extracting agent, n is total zinc in the ore is the amount of the zinc element in the zinc-containing ore, n ore mineral zinc carbonate is the amount of zinc carbonate in the zinc ore, V first leaching agent is the volume of the first leaching agent, a ranges from 100-600%, preferably 150-250%, concentration of total ammonia in second extracting agent is 6-12% and concentration of active carbonate is 8-15%. The concentration of zinc ammine ion in first leachate (based on the mass of zinc element) is 10-25 g/l. The concentration of zinc ammine ions (mass of zinc element) in first leachate obtained by filtration is adjusted to 10-25 g/l. The amount of calcium oxide and/or calcium hydroxide added in decarburization step is 100-130%, preferably from 100-110% of the amount of active carbonate in the first leachate. The ratio of amount of substance to which calcium hydroxide and/or calcium oxide is added to first filtrate to the amount of the substance of the zinc ammine complex ion in first filtrate is 1-1.2:2, preferably 1-1.1:2, in the calcium zincate synthesis step. Preferred Method: The crystallization pressure In the press crystallization step is 0.3-0.6 MPa. The method comprises carrying out reaction of decomposing part of ammonium carbonate in third filtrate into ammonia and carbon dioxide at 70-90 degrees C in the decompression decomposition step. The method comprises introducing second filtrate obtained by calcium zincate synthesis step with carbon dioxide, and using second filtrate with carbon dioxide as first extractant and circulating for one-time extraction of zinc-containing ore. The method comprises depressurizing third filtrate to normal pressure in the decompression decomposition step, collecting carbon dioxide released by decomposition of ammonium carbonate and recycling for pressure crystallization, collecting third filtrate after depressurization to normal pressure and recycling for secondary leaching.
