PN CN108486212-A; CN108486212-B
TI Antagonizing heavy metal arsenic toxicity comprises culturing cells in Ham's F12 culture medium, adding diluted nano silver into cell culture dish, diluting arsenic solution, adding nano silver, culturing, dyeing and detecting gene toxicity.
AU XU A
   WANG X
   LIU Y
AE CHINESE ACAD SCI HEFEI INST PHYS SCI (CAWZ-C)
GA 201871266E
AB    NOVELTY - Antagonizing heavy metal arsenic toxicity comprises (a) placing a cell in Ham's F12 culture medium containing fetal calf serum, glycine and gentamicin, culturing and incubating, (b) aspirating the Ham's F12 medium, diluting with nano silver, adding diluted nano silver into cell culture dish and incubating, (c) diluting aqueous arsenic solution with Ham's F12 medium in (b) and adding nano silver, and continuously culturing the two cultures, (d) discarding the medium containing nano silver/arsenic, adding diluted CCK-8 fluorescent dye, incubating under carbon dioxide in dark conditions and measuring the absorbance using a microplate reader, (e) extracting and determining concentration by sodium dodecyl sulfate polyacrylamide electrophoresis and transmembrane immunoreaction and developing, and (f) detecting gene toxicity based on cluster of differentiation (CD)59 gene mutation technology by culturing cells, collecting cells, fixing, dyeing, washing under running water and counting.
   USE - The method is useful for antagonizing heavy metal arsenic toxicity.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: preventing and reducing mammalian cytotoxicity and genotoxicity caused by heavy metal arsenic (decreased by more than 40%), thus preventing arsenic poisoning in organisms; is simple and fast (only 12 hours due to co-treatment of nanosilver/arsenic in comparison with arsenic treatment alone); and uses nano silver, which has good biological safety.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Antagonizing heavy metal arsenic toxicity comprises (a) placing cells in Ham's F12 culture medium containing 5-10% fetal calf serum, 1.5x 10(-4) to 2.5x 10-4 mol/l glycine and 20-30 mu g/ml gentamicin at 36-37 degrees C, culturing in a cell culture incubator with 4.5-5.5% carbon dioxide under saturated humidity for 20-28 hours, (b) aspirating Ham's F12 medium, diluting nano silver with Ham's F12 medium, adding diluted nano-silver into a cell culture dish and incubating in the cell culture incubator for 10-15 hours, (c) diluting aqueous arsenic solution with Ham's F12 medium in the step (b), adding it to the cell culture containing nano silver, where the final concentration of arsenic is 0-4 mu g/ml, and continuously culturing the two cultures for 20-28 hours, (d) discarding the medium containing nano silver/arsenic, adding 100-200 mu l diluted CCK-8 fluorescent dye (10-20 times), incubating at 36-37 degrees C, with 4.5-5.5% carbon dioxide under saturated humidity for 1-4 hours in dark conditions and measuring the absorbance at 450-490 nm using a microplate reader, (e) extracting, determining concentration of total protein in the cell by sodium dodecyl sulfate polyacrylamide electrophoresis (SDS-PAGE) and transmembrane immunoreaction and developing, and (f) detecting gene toxicity based on cluster of differentiation (CD)59 gene mutation technology by culturing cells, collecting cells, fixing, dyeing, washing under running water and counting.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The cell is mammalian cell wild type human-mouse hybrid tumor cell. The nano silver is diluted to 0.2-0.3 mu g/ml. The nano silver is polyvinyl pyrrolidone coated nano silver having a particle diameter of 25 nm. The arsenic aqueous solution is sodium arsinite aqeous solution. In the step (d): silver nitrate is used as nano silver experimental control. The gel in the step (e) is divided into two layers, where the lower layer is a separate gel and the upper layer is a concentrated gel, where the concentration of separate gel and concentrated gel is respectively 10-15% and 5%. Preferred Method: The specific detection step of (e) comprises (e1) cell extraction and the concentration of total protein measurement, after treating the AL cells with nano silver/arsenic, collecting cells, washing with phosphate buffer for 2-3 times and centrifuging at 800-1000 revolutions/minute for 2-5 minutes, adding 60-120 mu l cell lysis solution containing protease inhibitor, allowing to stand for 25-35 minutes on ice, vortex oscillating for 2-3 times, centrifuging at 3-5 degrees C and collecting the supernatant at -80 to -70 degrees C, determining the extracted protein concentration using a protein concentration test kit, (e2) installing the prepared gel in an electrophoresis device, injecting the electrophoresis buffer solution into gel sample well, adding 15-30 mu g heated denatured protein sample, starting electrophoresis at an initial voltage of 60-100 V, separating spectral rainbow pre-dyed protein strip, adjusting the voltage to 80-160 V, until the pre-dyed protein reaches the bottom of the separate gel, stopping electrophoresis, transferring the separation gel to a container previously added with transfer buffer, placing in an ice bath under a constant flow condition for 60-90 minutes at 250-350 mA, (e3) placing the obtained protein-loaded polyvinylidene fluoride film in 2-5% skimmed milk at room temperature for 1-2 hours, adding primary antibody in a ratio of 1:500-1:2000, incubating overnight at 3-5 degrees C, rinsing the membrane for 3-5 times with a washing buffer, and incubating with anti-mouse secondary antibody in a ratio of 1:5000 to 1:40000 for 1-2 hours, rinsing again, incubating the protein-loaded membrane with light-sensitive enhanced chemiluminescence (ECL) kit for 1-5 minutes at room temperature, and imaging using an imager, quantitative analysis of protein expression using Image J software and using beta -actin as an internal reference gene. The step (f) comprises (f1) treating AL cell with nano silver/arsenic, re-planting in a cell culture dish for 6-8 days, allowing the viable cells to have sufficient time to return to normal growth state and to remove CD59, antigen and progeny residue from the mutant cells, (f2) collecting cells and incubating 5x 104 cells for 2-5 hours in the culture dish in a cell incubator at 36-37 degrees C, with 4.5-5.5% carbon dioxide at saturated humidity, adding an antibody and complement respectively, setting individual experiment control as blank and complement treatment group, and (f3) continuously culturing for 7-10 days, until the visible clones are formed, removing culture solution, fixing, dyeing, washing, air drying, counting the cell clones and calculating the cell mutation rate.
DC A89 (Photographic, laboratory equipment, optical - including electrophotographic, thermographic uses.); D16 (Fermentation industry - including fermentation equipment, brewing, yeast production, production of pharmaceuticals and other chemicals by fermentation, microbiology, production of vaccines and antibodies, cell and tissue culture and genetic engineering.); J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).); S03 (Scientific Instrumentation)

PN CN107691956-A
TI Mulberry leaf particles beverage comprises mulberry, licorice, red peony, lily, morus alba, radix Ophiopogon, radix Rehmanniae, glucose, maltodextrin and rest is water, is rich in nutrients, convenient to eat and good solubility.
AU WANG J
AE GUANGXI YIZHOU YIYUAN MULBERRY STEM FLOR (GUAN-Non-standard)
GA 201816891G
AB    NOVELTY - Mulberry leaf particles beverage comprises 180-200 pts. wt. mulberry, 15-20 pts. wt. licorice, 12-16 pts. wt. red peony, 10-12 pts. wt. lily, 4-6 pts. wt. morus alba, 5-8 pts. wt. radix Ophiopogon, 4-8 pts. wt. radix Rehmanniae, 12-15 pts. wt. glucose, 8-10 pts. wt. maltodextrin and rest is water.
   USE - Mulberry leaf particles beverage.
   ADVANTAGE - The mulberry leaf particles beverage is rich in nutrients, convenient to eat, have good solubility and taste, not easy to absorb moisture in the air, and suitable for all kinds of people to eat.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for preparing mulberry leaf beverage, which involves:
   (A) cleaning mulberry leaf and putting in the sterile chamber and drying, crushing until the granularity is 2-3 millimeter;
   (B) using supercritical carbon dioxide extraction, crushing the mulberry leaf and putting into extracting tank, adding ethanol at 36-40 degrees C and 24-26 megaPascal, where the volume flow of carbon dioxide is 120-150 milliliter per minute under the condition of extraction for 60-80 minutes;
   (C) separating carbon dioxide and ethanol in the filtrate to obtain the mulberry leaf extract, where the volume concentration of ethanol is 55-60 wt.%, the dosage of the ethanol is 2.5-3 times;
   (D) adding radix Ophiopogon and Rehmanniae, cutting into 5-8 millimeter of small section, where the total mass is 8-10 times weight of water, boiling with slow fire for 20-25 minutes, filtering, boiling repeatedly the filter residue for 2 times;
   (E) mixing and concentrating the obtained filtrate solution to room temperature and measuring relative density to be 1.15-1.25 of solution to obtain primary extract and placing into a ultrahigh pressure homogenizer;
   (F) adding neutral protease at 140-160 megaPascal for 5-10 minutes to obtain the plant extracting solution;
   (G) mixing the licorice and lily vacuum, freezing and drying in the freezing chamber at (-)28 degrees C and reaching at -16 degrees C;
   (H) adding and putting the frozen material in the vacuum drying chamber at 53-57 degrees C for 5-5.5 hours, taking out and putting into high energy nano-impact tank;
   (I) introducing argon gas protection, grinding for 1.5-1.8 hours, obtaining the plant freeze-dry powder, mulberry leaf extract, plant extracting liquid and adding with glucose, maltodextrin and stirring uniformly to obtain premix, putting into the granulator, granulating;
   (J) putting into a drying tank drying for the first time at (18-22)-(60-65) degrees C; and
   (K) placing the sterile room standing for 1.5 hours, putting to the drying box at 56-60 degrees C for 1.2-1.5 hours to obtain mulberry leaf beverage.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - FOOD - Preferred Composition: The mulberry leaf particles beverage comprises 190 pts. wt. mulberry, 16 pts. wt. licorice, 14 pts. wt. red peony, 11 pts. wt. lily, 5 pts. wt. morus alba, 6 pts. wt. radix Ophiopogon, 6 pts. wt. radix Rehmanniae, 13 pts. wt. glucose, 9 pts. wt. maltodextrin and rest is water. The neutral protease which is used in amount of the primary extract is 3 - 5 wt.%. Preferred Process: The premix is granulated and passed through a 40-60 mesh screen. The water content of mulberry leaf beverage is less than 4 wt.%.

PN WO2022229326-A2
TI Separating membrane from avian eggshell used to            produce purified calcium carbonate, comprises e.g.            providing pieces of eggshell of attached membrane,            contacting the pieces of eggshell having attached            membrane with carbonic anhydrase enzyme.
AU PASHLEY C C
   HICKMAN G J
   KEAST M D
   STACEY A L
   BELTON D
AE AVGO BIOTECH LTD (AVGO-Non-standard)
GA 2022D7622K
AB    NOVELTY - Separating membrane from avian eggshell                comprises providing pieces of eggshell having                attached membrane, contacting the pieces of                eggshell having attached membrane with carbonic                anhydrase enzyme in acidic aqueous medium, applying                physical force to the pieces of eggshell having                attached membrane, and separating the pieces of                eggshell from the detached membrane.
   USE - The method is useful for separating membrane                from avian eggshell, used to produce purified                calcium carbonate.
   ADVANTAGE - The method has: efficient and simple; and                cleans separating and purifying eggshell from its                membrane.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included                for:
   producing purified calcium carbonate                comprising providing impure calcium carbonate                material, contacting the impure calcium carbonate                material with aqueous acid to produce carbon                dioxide and aqueous product mixture comprising                calcium salt, adding aqueous base to the aqueous                product mixture, adding carbonate source to the                aqueous product mixture to produce calcium                carbonate and salt by-product, and precipitating                purified calcium carbonate;
   apparatus for separating membrane from avian                eggshell comprising vessel for contacting pieces of                eggshell having attached membrane with carbonic                anhydrase enzyme in acidic aqueous medium, means                for applying physical force to the pieces of                eggshell having attached membrane, and means for                separating the pieces of eggshell from the detached                membrane; and
   apparatus for producing purified calcium                carbonate from impure calcium carbonate material,                comprising vessel for contacting the impure calcium                carbonate material with aqueous acid to produce                carbon dioxide and aqueous product mixture                comprising calcium salt, means for adding aqueous                base to the aqueous product mixture, means for                adding carbonate source to the aqueous product                mixture to produce calcium carbonate and salt                by-product, and means for precipitating purified                calcium carbonate.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The figure shows schematic view of system for                separating membrane from avian eggshell.
   1Collection vessel
   2Auger
   3Pump
   5Settling tank
   6Vanes
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The physical force is                  applied to the pieces of eggshell using auger (2)                  and/or pump (3), e.g. transfer auger, progressive                  cavity pump, open impellor pump and/or screw                  impeller pump. The detached membrane is separated                  from the pieces of eggshell by gravity,                  filtration and/or centrifugation. The detached                  membrane is collected by flotation in aqueous                  medium containing carbon dioxide. The salt                  by-product produced by the addition of the                  carbonate source is used to generate the aqueous                  base and the aqueous acid. The carbon dioxide                  produced by contacting the impure calcium                  carbonate material with the acid is reacted with                  base to generate the carbonate source. The                  impurities are precipitated and/or filtered from                  the aqueous product mixture, preferably                  precipitated in continuous oscillatory baffled                  reactor. The purified calcium carbonate is                  precipitated from the aqueous product mixture in                  continuous oscillatory baffled crystalliser. The                  avian eggshells are produced according to the                  method . Preferred Components: The aqueous acid                  is hydrochloric acid, the calcium salt is calcium                  chloride, the aqueous base is sodium hydroxide,                  the carbonate source is sodium carbonate and the                  salt by-product is sodium chloride. The impure                  calcium carbonate material comprises biogenic                  calcium carbonate material, preferably where the                  biogenic calcium carbonate comprises avian                  eggshells, marine biogenic material, mollusc                  shells or fossil biogenic material, more                  preferably marine biogenic material comprises                  mollusc shells and/or crustacean shells. The                  avian eggshells are chicken eggshells.
   EXAMPLE - None given.

AB    NOVELTY - Cement comprises cement clinker and a                supplementary cementitious material, where the                supplementary cementitious material comprises: at                least one calcined clay; and at least one natural                pozzolan with a carbonate species content,                expressed as calcium carbonate, of &#8814; 3                wt.% of the total weight of the natural                pozzolan.
   USE - The cement is used for preparing cementitious                conglomerates, such as mortar and concrete                (claimed), and used as a construction material                belonging to the class of hydraulic binders, such                as hydraulic cements, hydraulic cement, or                hydraulic cement.
   ADVANTAGE - The cement has excellent compressive strength                properties, although it has a reduced clinker                content. The cement has excellent                physical-mechanical properties (compressive                strength) comparable with those of a corresponding                cement comprising clinker only. The cement reduces                the consumption of clinker for cement production,                the use of such supplementary cementitious material                also results in reduced carbon dioxide emissions                and lower energy consumption in cement production                processes.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the                following:
   a cementitious conglomerate, such as mortar or                concrete, comprising the cement and stone                aggregates;
   a method of producing a cement, comprising                providing at least one calcined clay, mixing the                calcined clay with cement clinker and at least one                natural pozzolan with a carbonate species content,                expressed as calcium carbonate, of &#8814; 3                wt.% of the total weight of the natural pozzolan,                and grinding the mixture in order to obtain the                cement; and
   use of a supplementary cementitious material                comprising at least one calcined clay and at least                one natural pozzolan with a carbonate species                content, expressed as calcium carbonate, of                &#8814; 3 wt.% of the weight of the natural                pozzolan, for reducing carbon dioxide emissions                during the production of a cement comprising cement                clinker.

PN CN114525400-A
TI Production of decomposed ore by introducing air source into plasma air heating furnace, adjusting discharge distance of electrode, ionizing current generated by electrode, generating hot air gas, and decomposing mineral material.
AU FANG Y
   LIU B
   DI S
   LIU J
   CHEN G
   JIAO Y
   ZHANG L
   BAI B
   BAI Y
AE SHAANXI JUNENG XINCHUANG COALIFICATION (SHAA-Non-standard)
GA 202274233C
AB    NOVELTY - The method comprises introducing an air source into a plasma air heating furnace. A discharge distance of an electrode in the plasma heating furnace is adjusted under the action of voltage. Current generated by the electrode is ionized by the air source. The electrode generates plasma arc to the heating gas source to generate hot air gas. Mineral material is decomposed. The mineral material is pre-treated into the furnace. Hot air gas in the furnace is introduced. A mineral material decomposition is performed at a temperature of 300-3000degrees Celsius to generate a decomposition product and decomposition gas. The decomposed product is used as a direct product or an intermediate product. The generated decomposed gas is directly recycled, or partially returned to the plasma heat dissipation furnace.
   USE - Method for producing decomposed ore.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables decomposing ore by using new plasma air heating furnace in an energy-saving and environment-friendly manner. The method provides high decomposition rate, reduces cost, and realizes carbon dioxide and nitrogen oxide zero discharge. The furnace is capable of continuously running for a long time, high capacity, large range, can be widely used in production, and can be recycled or directly recycled, reduces the concentration, decarburization, denitrification process, and saves energy. The gas temperature heating 300 - 3000degrees Celsius satisfies the technical requirement of decomposing mineral, reducing the cost, realizing industrial production process heating carbon dioxide and NxO zero discharge, and saving energy.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for decomposed ore production system comprising production system plasma air heating furnace, decomposition kiln, dust-removing cooling device, furnace body, electrode, transformer, gas inlet, cooling gas return port, hot air outlet, carbon brick graphite, material inlet, material outlet, heating gas inlet and tail gas outlet.

PT P
PN CN114316358-A
TI Green regeneration method of waste rubber, involves cutting waste tire, coarse crushing, magnetic separation, fine crushing, magnetic separation and filtering to obtain waste rubber powder, mixing rubber powder and desulfurizer, desulfurizing, cooling and storing the desulfurized rubber.
AU XU Y
   ZOU L
   SHENG K
   ZHOU P
   DI C
   WANG Y
AE JIANGSU RUIBO ENVIRONMENTAL PROTECTION (JIAN-Non-standard)
GA 2022558601
AB    NOVELTY - Green regeneration method of waste rubber comprises (i) cutting waste tire, coarse crushing, magnetic separation, fine crushing, magnetic separation and filtering to obtain waste rubber powder; (ii) mixing waste rubber powder and desulfurizer in a mass ratio of 100:0.1-1 in a high speed stirrer uniformly to obtain mixed material; (iii) placing the mixed material in a first desulfurization tank filled with supercritical carbon dioxide, maintaining temperature at 130-160degrees Celsius and pressure at 15-20 MPa, overturning the waste rubber powder under the action of conveying device, and transporting to a second desulfurization tank after releasing the pressure; and (iv) sealing the second desulfurization tank, heating to 160-240degrees Celsius, introducing air for 200-400 seconds to the second desulfurization tank, heating to 200-200degrees Celsius for 10-40 minutes to obtain desulfurized rubber; and (v) cooling and storing the desulfurized rubber after passing through an open mill and a refiner in turn.
   USE - The method is useful for green regeneration of waste rubber, where the waste rubber is used for recycling of waste tires in the rubber industry field.
   ADVANTAGE - The waste rubber green regeneration method uses excellent swelling and penetration characteristics of supercritical carbon dioxide fluid, replace rubber softening agent, makes the regenerating agent permeate in a short time, disperses in the waste rubber powder, and heaters the waste tire powder in the sealed container to 150-230degrees Celsius. The desulfurization process does not need to add water, oil, softening agents and other auxiliary materials, saves cost, does not generate harmful substances, and is more environmentally friendly.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: In the step (iii), the residence time of the waste rubber powder in the first desulfurization tank is 5-40 minutes. In the step (iv), the second desulfurization tank is sealed, and then heated to 200-240degreesC, preferably 200degreesC, after the air is introduced into the second desulfurization tank for 300-400 seconds, preferably 300 seconds, the introduction is stopped, and then the temperature is increased to 250-300degreesC, preferably 250degreesC and heated for 20-40 minutes, preferably 20 minutes. In the step (iii), the conveying device is a screw conveying device.Preferred Components: In the step (i), the waste rubber powder is 1-50 meshes. The desulfurizer is diallyl disulfide, dibutyl disulfide, di-tert-butyl disulfide and/or allicin.

AU BARTELT D
AE BARTELT D (BART-Individual)
GA 202248396B
AB    NOVELTY - The device has modules which comprise a heat pump (21) and/or a heating circuit (22) and/or a cooling circuit module (23) and/or a power generation module (24). The modules are thermally and/or electrically interconnected. The working medium such as two-component working medium and multi-component working medium is present. The control operation takes place through at least one artificial intelligence (AI) system, by a neural network (NN).
   USE - Device for generating electrical energy from geothermal energy using geothermal probe installed in ground.
   ADVANTAGE - The electrical energy can be generating in a cost-effective and technically simple manner. The efficiency of organic Rankine cycle systems in high-temperature applications is improved. The robust and reliable system can generate the geothermal energy from waste heat for heat in low temperature ranges. The carbon dioxide balance is improved, by reducing carbon dioxide-emissions. The quick and easy installation of energy generation system can be implemented for easy transport with high scalability for better joint use of the vortex tube and/or vortex tube effect. Since the expansion takes place almost completely free of liquid, no signs of erosion and corrosion on the turbine and/or the engine and/or the motor-generator. Since no pressure hull according to the technical rules for steam boiler testing (TRD) is required, operation of the system runs completely automatically and no supervision is required. The operation of the plant starting and stopping is easy, in contrast to steam turbines.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for controlling and converting geothermal energy.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a block diagram of the device for generating electrical energy from geothermal energy.

TI Method for producing power and liquefying gas, involves producing exhaust gas comprising water vapor and carbon dioxide, expanding exhaust gas in expander, cooling expanded exhaust gas, and separating condensed water vapor in separator.
AU BERRA M
   BRUNO L
AE SAIPEM SPA (SAIM-C)
GA 2022484183
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves separating the condensed water vapors (4,4',7) in a first separator (S1), thus, obtaining a partially dehydrated exhaust gas (5,8). A portion of a condensed water vapor is pumped by a first pump (P1) and recycled it to a combustor (COMB). The partially dehydrated exhaust gas is cooled in a first heat exchanger (TE1), thus, obtaining a cooled exhaust gas (6). A second portion of the condensed water vapor is separated in a second separator (S2), thus, obtaining the dehydrated exhaust gas. The dehydrated exhaust gas is subjected to dehydration in a dehydration unit (DHU), thus, obtaining an exhaust gas (9) mainly comprised of carbon dioxide (CO2). The CO2 is liquified in the exhaust gas that mainly comprised of CO2 in a liquefaction unit (LU), thus, obtaining a liquid CO2 flow (11). A portion (12) of the liquefied CO2 flow is separated and recycled it to the combustor.
   USE - Method for producing power and liquefying gas using a high-pressure gas turbine and liquid air energy storage (LAES) technology.
   ADVANTAGE - The oxy-combustion technologies can be synergistically integrated with liquid air energy storage (LAES) technologies, by means of a highly efficient process, which allows obviating the problem of fluctuations in the demand and production of electrical energy, and thus providing a stabilizing effect of the electrical power network, further promoting the use of renewable energy.

TI Amino-functionalized halloysite porous            microsphere-based gas adsorption material for carbon            dioxide adsorption and flue gas treatment, comprises            spheroidal structure porous microsphere formed by            self-assembly.
AU LI X
   LI R
   LI H
   ZHAO K
AE UNIV CHANGAN (UCHA-C)
GA 2021919935
AB    NOVELTY - Amino-functionalized halloysite porous                microsphere-based gas adsorption material comprises                a spheroidal structure porous microsphere which is                formed by self-assembly by taking a halloysite                nanotube as a structural unit and has a microscopic                appearance and is in a pore passage interactive                penetration, and grafted amino and impregnated                amino loaded on the porous microsphere. The gas                adsorption material has a hierarchical pore                structure which comprises tubular pores of the                halloysite nanotubes and three-dimensional pores                formed by stacking the halloysite nanotubes during                self-assembly.
   USE - Amino-functionalized halloysite porous                microsphere-based gas adsorption material for                carbon dioxide adsorption and flue gas treatment                (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The amino-functionalized halloysite porous                microsphere-based gas adsorption material has high                adsorption capacity, stable cycle performance,                simple material preparation method and low cost,                and is beneficial to large-scale production and                application.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method                for preparing amino-functionalized halloysite                porous microsphere-based gas adsorption material,                which involves:
   (A) adding halloysite nanotube into an                aminosilane coupling agent toluene solution under                heating, stirring, aging, cooling to room                temperature, centrifugally washing, and drying to                obtain an amino-grafted halloysite nanotube;
   (B) uniformly mixing the amino-grafted                halloysite nanotube, chitosan and deionized water                under stirring at normal temperature to obtain                milky slurry;
   (C) spray-drying the milky white mud to obtain                milky white powder, where the milky white powder is                porous microspheres self-assembled by the amino                grafted halloysite nanotubes;
   (D) dispersing the milky white powder in an                organic amine methanol solution under stirring at                normal temperature to obtain porous microspheres                impregnated with amino; and
   (E) drying in vacuum to obtain the                amino-functionalized halloysite porous                microsphere-based gas adsorption material.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The pore diameter of                  the tubular pores is 10-30 nm. The pore diameter                  of the three-dimensional pores is 30-120 nm. The                  grafted amino group comprises a primary amino                  group, a secondary amino group, and a tertiary                  amino group.    ADVANTAGE - The structure of the device is simple. The airflow force exhaled by a weakened patient is increased, so that the air outlet check valve opens functionally for exhalation to completely discharge the exhaled carbon dioxide from the mask, thus avoiding the negative consequences and health problems of an excessive carbon dioxide concentration.

AU SHEN J
   TURK B
   SHARMA P
   DENTON D
AE RES TRIANGLE INST (RTRI-C)
   RES TRIANGLE INST (RTRI-C)
   RES TRIANGLE INST (RTRI-C)
   RES TRIANGLE INST (RTRI-C)
GA 2020B6549J
AB    NOVELTY - Fluidizable and attrition-resistant                chromium-free catalyst comprises 45-70 wt.% ferric                oxide, 5-15 wt.% cupric oxide, 20-35 wt.% alumina,                10-25 wt.% zinc oxide, and 1-15 wt.% potassium                carbonate. The catalyst has a Davison index (DI) of                less than or equal to 15.
   USE - The catalyst is useful: for converting carbon                monoxide into carbon dioxide; and in a fluidized                bed reactor for water gas shift reaction (claimed),                preferably high-temperature water gas shift                reaction.
   ADVANTAGE - The fluidizable and attrition-resistant                chromium-free catalyst: provides carbon monoxide                conversion at operating temperatures of 375-450                degrees C of 60-85%, preferably at least 75%, where                the selectivity to methane (by-product) formation                at operating temperatures of 375-450 degrees C is                less than 2%, and an average carbon monoxide                conversion of at least 60% at operating                temperatures of 375-450 degrees C is maintained for                at least 200 hours and thus the catalyst exhibits                long-term stable activity; and can match                performance of commercially-available fixed-bed                catalyst in catalyst activity and stability and is                better or equivalent to commercial fluidized                cracking catalyst in terms of attrition                resistance.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for                converting carbon monoxide into carbon dioxide,                comprising introducing water and carbon monoxide                with a molar ratio of 1:1 to 3:1 into a fluidized                bed reactor having an operating temperature, where                the reactor comprises a chromium free catalyst, the                catalyst has a DI of less than or equal to 7, and                the carbon monoxide conversion at operating                temperatures of 375-450 degrees C is at least                60%.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The catalyst further                  comprises up to 15 wt.% alkali component. The                  catalyst comprises: 4-6 wt.% potassium carbonate;                  50-60 wt.% ferric oxide; 20-25 wt.% alumina;                  10-15 wt.% zinc oxide; and 5-10 wt.% cupric oxide                  and optionally 0-15 wt.% magnesium oxide and/or                  optionally 0-15 wt.% cerium oxide. The catalyst                  has a Brunauer-Emmett-Teller (BET) surface area                  of 10-60 m2/g. The catalyst has a density of                  0.8-1.8 g/ml. The alkali component is potassium                  as potassium carbonate. The DI is 5-7.

The gas mixture is separated into the gas stream oxygen and gas stream carbon monoxide. The volume flow of hydrogen with a minimum value 0.1 m3/h up to a maximum value of 10 m3/h is regulated by a control valve. The volume flow of carbon monoxide with a minimum value 0.1 m3/h up to a maximum value of 10 m3/h is regulated by the control valve. The gas mixture in the mixing chamber in a molar ratio of 1:1 to 1:3 is enabled by the control valve for hydrogen and the control valve for carbon monoxide. TI Ultra-low threshold multi-component holmium ion doped glass material comprises 72tellurium dioxide-20zinc oxide-5sodium carbonate-2yttrium oxide and doped with holmium ion.
AU WANG P
   YU J
AE SUZHOU KAIWEN BAONI PHOTOELECTRIC TECHNO (SUZH-Non-standard)
GA 202095372Q
AB    NOVELTY - Ultra-low threshold multi-component holmium ion doped glass material comprises 72tellurium dioxide-20zinc oxide-5sodium carbonate-2yttrium oxide and doped with 1 mol% holmium (Ho3+) ion.
   USE - Used as the ultra-low threshold multi-component holmium ion doped glass material.
   ADVANTAGE - The glass material: has extremely high Q value, extremely low threshold value; greatly improves coupling efficiency; greatly solves the issues of low output power and low collection energy efficiency of whispering gallery mode microcavity lasers; and can realize low threshold narrow bandwidth fiber.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for:
   (1) preparing microsphere cavity laser based on ultra-low threshold multi-component holmium ion doped glass material comprising (a) calculating the mass ratio of the high-purity raw materials according to the chemical formula of 72tellurium dioxide-20zinc oxide-5sodium carbonate-2yttrium oxide and the molar composition of 1 mol% holmium (Ho3+) ion, grinding, stirring, and completely mixing the different raw materials, (b) adding the uniformly mixed raw materials into corundum crucible, and heating, (c) drawing the molten glass, and cooling to form multi-component doped glass optical fiber, (d) using a carbon dioxide laser for heating the glass optical fiber for drawing a taper to obtain tapered optical fiber, (e) adjusting the focus position of the output light of the carbon dioxide laser for focusing the light beam to the end of the tapered fiber, so that the end of the tapered fiber shrinks upward and melts into a microsphere, (f) preparing a silicon dioxide tapered fiber with a diameter of 1.5-3 mu m by the fusion tapering method, and (g) packaging the microsphere and silicon dioxide tapered fiber, and coupling to form a microsphere cavity laser; and
   (2) control method of micro-sphere cavity laser based on ultra-low threshold multi-component holmium ion-doped glass material.

GA 202088201E
AB    NOVELTY - A fluorine-containing amorphous hard carbon film (1) comprises a fluorine-containing layer comprising 10-50 atomic% fluorine atoms provided on the surface of a base material (2). The fluorine-containing layer comprises the outermost surface (3) of the fluorine-containing amorphous hard carbon film on the side opposite to the base material side and an inclined composition in which the fluorine content increases from the base material side toward the outermost surface side in the thickness direction. The fluorine-containing layer comprises the center of the fluorine-containing amorphous hard carbon film in the thickness direction. The fluorine content on the outermost surface is 1.5 times or more the fluorine content in the center in the thickness direction. The fluorine-containing layer exists in the outermost surface side from the center in the thickness direction.
   USE - Fluorine-containing amorphous hard carbon film used as protective film for electronic material, display panel, and optical material.
   ADVANTAGE - The fluorine-containing amorphous hard carbon film has high water repellence and maintains hardness by suppressing the content of fluorine atoms inside the film relatively low.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for manufacture of fluorine-containing amorphous hard carbon film, which involves irradiating a carbon ion beam in a chamber to deposit carbon atoms on the surface of a base material, and continuously feeding carbon dioxide gas or a mixed gas containing carbon dioxide gas into the chamber in a gas introduction period until a predetermined time elapses after the end of the irradiation period of the carbon ion beam.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a partial expanded sectional view of the fluorine-containing amorphous hard carbon film.

The start of the gas introduction period is any time during the irradiation period of the carbon ion beam. During the irradiation period of the carbon ion beam, the flow rate of the fluorocarbon gas is gradually increased. During the irradiation period of the carbon ion beam, the fluorocarbon gas is introduced as an atmospheric gas in the chamber. The start of the gas introduction period is the same as the end of the irradiation period of the carbon ion beam. The start of the gas introduction period is when a predetermined time has elapsed after the end of the irradiation period of the carbon ion beam. The carbon ion beam is irradiated by an arc ion plating device or a filtered arc ion plating device using a solid carbon target. The fluorocarbon gas comprises at least one chosen from tetrafluoromethane, hexafluoroethane and octafluoropropane.

AU FU J

   WANG Z
   MA C
AE UNIV SHANDONG (USHA-C)
GA 202085782K
AB    NOVELTY - Treating volatile organic compound in plant                area based on utilization of by-product petroleum                coke in petrochemical enterprises comprises                carrying out carbonization and activation of                petroleum coke to produce activated semi-coke and                hydrogen/carbon monoxide gas under low-oxygen vapor                atmosphere using activated semi-coke to adsorb                volatile organic compound gas, desorbing the                saturated activated semi-coke for volatile organic                compound to generate activated coke and high                concentration of volatile organic compound gas,                combining hydrogen/carbon monoxide gas and                high-concentration volatile organic compound gas,                where generated high temperature flue gas rich in                water and carbon dioxide is used for petroleum coke                activation.
   USE - The volatile organic compound in plant area is                useful in plant volatile organic compound                management system based on utilization of                by-product petroleum coke in petrochemical                enterprises (claimed).

   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for                plant volatile organic compound management system                based on utilization of by-product petroleum coke                in petrochemical enterprises comprising entrained                flow bed, volatile organic compound adsorption                system, volatile organic compound desorption and                concentration system, gas collection and recycling                system; activated coke semi-coke prepared by                entrained bed is used as adsorbent of volatile                organic compound adsorption system to adsorb                volatile organic compound gas, desorbing produced                activated semi-coke saturated with adsorption in                volatile organic compound desorption concentration                system to obtain activated coke and high                concentration of volatile organic compound gas,                mixing and calcining high concentration of volatile                organic compound gas and water gas, providing                thermal energy, carbon dioxide and water steam                required for the activation stage of petroleum coke                fire.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The petroleum coke is                  screened, crushed, and grounded. The volatile                  organic compound adsorption system is installed                  at multiple volatile organic compound emission                  sites in the plant area. In the entire process                  system, the generated gas can be recycled.                  Preferred Components: In the low-oxygen vapor                  atmosphere, oxygen concentration is 2-10%, the                  water volume concentration is 5-20%, and the                  carbon dioxide concentration is 0-15%. The                  carbonization activation uses high-temperature                  flue gas rich in water and carbon dioxide as the                  activator. The volatile organic compound gas is                  absorbed by a fixed bed/rotary wheel adsorption                  system. The volatile organic compound desorption                  treatment adopts hot air treatment. The mixed                  combustion adopts mixed heat storage                  combustion.

The vane breaks down the bubbles into smaller bubbles during the vane centrifugal pressurization, and thus the operation of the pump set increases the surface area of the bubbles to facilitate improved dissolving of CO2 in the DIW because the pump operates by vane centrifugal pressurization.

PT P
PN US2020158426-A1; WO2020106395-A1
TI Method for removing water and carbon dioxide (CO2)            from feed gas stream, involves producing main treated            gas stream and sub treated gas stream by feeding feed            gas stream to main and sub solid-tolerant heat            exchangers.
AU KAMINSKY R D
   STAEDTER M
AE KAMINSKY R D (KAMI-Individual)
   STAEDTER M (STAE-Individual)
   EXXON MOBIL UPSTREAM RES CO (ESSO-C)
GA 202043492G
AB    NOVELTY - The method (100) involves producing a first                treated gas stream by feeding a feed gas stream                (102) to a first solid-tolerant heat exchanger. The                first solid-tolerant heat exchanger chills the feed                gas stream to a first temperature. The first                temperature is below which a portion of the water                contained in the feed gas stream freezes out of the                feed gas stream, and above which a portion of the                CO2 contained in the feed gas freezes out of the                feed gas as a pure phase. A second treated gas                stream is produced by feeding the first treated gas                stream to a second solid-tolerant heat exchanger.                The second solid-tolerant heat exchanger chills the                first treated gas stream to a second temperature.                The second temperature is below which a portion of                the CO2 contained in the feed gas stream freezes                out of the first treated gas stream as a pure                phase.
   USE - Method for removing water and carbon dioxide                (CO2) from feed gas stream.
   ADVANTAGE - The solid-tolerant heat exchanger is                integrated into cryogenic cooling processes to                accommodate gas that is minimally treated still                containing significant amounts of water and CO2.                The effectiveness of the process is improved by                recirculating process streams through the                solid-tolerant heat exchanger. The intermittent                recirculation is preferred over continuous                recirculation to minimize cooling requirements due                to dilution of CO2 concentration in the gas                stream.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of a                method for removing water and CO2 from a feed gas                stream.
   Method for removing water and CO2 from a feed                gas stream (100)
   Feed gas stream (102)
   Compressor (112)
   First non-freezing heat exchanger                (114)
   Expander (136)

AU LONG Y
   WANG P
   XIE J
   FU A
   ZHAO M

   CHINA NAT PETROLEUM CORP TUBULALR GOODS (CNPC-C)
GA 202037932J
AB    NOVELTY - The system has a mixing tank (1), and the outlet of the mixing tank is connected to a booster pump (2) and an erosion test box (3). The outlet of the erosion test box is connected to the inlet of the mixing tank to form a gas-liquid-solid three-phase fluid circulation circuit. The mixing tank is connected with a decompression tank (4), a water tank (7), a carbon dioxide gas tank (8) and an erosion test box. A test sleeve is installed in the erosion test box. The three-phase fluid is always kept from the outer wall of the test sleeve through the eyelet flow to the inner wall, and then flow out from the outlet of the erosion test box, and finally return to the stirring tank to start the next cycle. The three-phase fluid enters the decompression tank for pressure relief after the test is completed.
   USE - System for casing hole erosion test in wellbore.
   ADVANTAGE - The system truly simulates the sand production conditions of oil and gas wells under the coupling action of high temperature, high pressure, gas-liquid-solid three-phase flow and external squeezing load, and effectively evaluates the erosion behavior around the perforation casing hole and has a high test safety.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for casing hole erosion test.

AB    NOVELTY - The electrochemical reaction device has a                reaction unit comprising a first accommodation unit                (3) configured to accommodate carbon dioxide, a                second accommodation unit (5) configured to                accommodate an electrolytic solution containing                water, and a diaphragm (6) provided between the                first accommodation unit and the second                accommodation unit. A reduction electrode is                configured to reduce the carbon dioxide. An                oxidation electrode is configured to oxidize the                water. A pressure regulator (14) is configured to                regulate a pressure in the first accommodation                unit. A reaction product detector (15) is                configured to detect at least one of an amount and                a kind of a substance produced at the reduction                electrode. A controller is configured to control                the pressure regulator based on a detection signal                of the reaction product detector.
   USE - Electrochemical reaction device.
   ADVANTAGE - Since the electrode potential of the reduction                electrode is one of factors that decide the amount                of current and the Faradaic efficiency of the                product, the amount and the composition of the                substance produced from the reduction electrode are                recognized by monitoring the electrode potential.                The fluctuation in the production amount and the                composition of the reduction reaction product                accompanying the fluctuation in the applied voltage                to the reduction electrode is suppressed to enable                stabilization of the production amount and the                composition of the reduction reaction product. The                detection signal of the production amount and the                composition of the reduction reaction product                detected by the analyzer is sent to the controller                to control the operation of the pressure                regulator.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the                electrochemical reaction device. (Drawing includes                non-English language text)

PT P
PN CN110833839-A
TI Micro-nanoized monodispersed copper phosphide catalyst used in carbon dioxide nitrogen formylation, is prepared by using hydroxyapatite as carrier, and copper phosphide as active component, and dispersing copper phosphide on hydroxyapatite.
AU CUI D
   ZHAO K
   TONG Q
   CAI T
   YUAN J
   JIN C
AE SHANGHAI NAT ENG RES CENT NANOTECHNOLOGY (NERC-C)
GA 202018858P
AB    NOVELTY - Micro-nanoized monodispersed copper phosphide catalyst, is prepared by using hydroxyapatite as a carrier, and copper phosphide as an active component, and uniformly dispersing copper phosphide on the surface of hydroxyapatite in monodispersed form.
   USE - The catalyst is useful in carbon dioxide nitrogen formylation (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The catalyst has strong interaction, excellent stability, high active component loading property, uniform dispersion and single active component, and is prepared by simple process and suitable for large scale production.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for preparing the catalyst, involving (a) introducing copper phosphide active component ions to a carrier crystal lattice using an ion exchange method, and performing program temperature reduction method under specific conditions, and (b) forming in situ monodispersed copper phosphide catalyst by (i) dissolving calcium source and phosphorus source in deionized water, where the concentration of calcium source and the phosphorus source is 0.2-0.5 mol/l, adjusting the pH value to 2-4 using acid solution, mixing calcium source and phosphorus source in a molar ratio of 5:3 under vigorous stirring conditions, adjusting the pH value of aqueous ammonia to 10-12 under stirring condition, reacting in a water bath at 40-90 degrees C for 4-10 hours, leaving still and aging for 18-24 hours, filtering, washing with deionized water, and freeze drying to obtain hydroxyapatite carrier, and (ii) dispersing hydroxyapatite in copper source solution, enabling the ratio of copper and hydroxyapatite to 0.1-10 %mass, stirring at room temperature for 2-5 hours, centrifuging, washing, drying at 50-100 degrees C, and performing reduction at 600-800 degrees C under hydrogen atmosphere for 4-6 hours to obtain the product.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The calcium source comprises calcium nitrate and/or calcium chloride. The phosphorus source comprises ammonium hydrogen phosphate, ammonium dihydrogen phosphate, ammonium phosphate and/or phosphoric acid. The copper source comprises copper nitrate and/or copper chloride. The hydrogen atmosphere is 5-10% hydrogen and argon mixture. Preferred Composition: The concentration of copper source is 0.01-0.03 mol/l.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The acid solution comprises nitric acid, hydrochloric acid, sulfuric acid and/or phosphoric acid.
DC J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).)

TI End-expiratory carbon dioxide device for guiding            endotracheal intubation, has inflatable gas tube that            is partially penetrated in side wall of cannula, and            one end is connected to balloon and other end is            connected to air supply device.
AU JIANG H
   XIA M
   SUN Y
   YAN J
AE JIANG H (JIAN-Individual)
   XIA M (XIAM-Individual)
   UNIV SHANGHAI 9TH PEOPLES HOSPITAL SHANG (UYSH-Non-standard)
   UNIV SHANGHAI JIAO TONG NINTH PEOPLES HO (USJT-C)
   SHANGHAI NO 9 PEOPLES HOSPITAL (SHAN-Non-standard)
   UNIV JIAOTONG NO 9 PEOPLES HOSPITAL (UYJI-Non-standard)
GA 202018274M
AB    NOVELTY - The end-expiratory CO2 device has a cannula                (100), an end-expiratory CO2 catheter (200), a                suction tube (300), a balloon (400), and an                inflatable gas tube. The end-tidal CO2 catheter is                partially penetrated into the side wall of the                cannula, and one end extends outside the end of the                cannula, and the other end is connected to the end                expiratory CO2 sensor connection. The suction tube                is partially penetratingly arranged in a side wall                of the cannula, and one end extends beyond an end                of the cannula, and the other end is connected to                an air pump. The balloon is sleeved on the outer                wall of the cannula. The inflatable gas tube is                partially penetrated in the side wall of the                cannula, and one end is connected to the balloon                and the other end is connected to the air supply                device.
   USE - End-expiratory carbon dioxide (CO2) device for                guiding endotracheal intubation.
   ADVANTAGE - The device uses the end-expiratory CO2                arranged in the wall of the cannula catheter for                detecting end-tidal CO2 of gas exhaled from throat                portion of patient and the data is matched with a                sight glass to quickly and accurately determine the                position of the tracheal cannula. The effusion in                the throat and the trachea of the patient is pumped                out through the pipette, so that the medical staff                can observe with a sight glass conveniently and the                work efficiency is improved.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the                end-tidal CO2 of the guide trachea cannula                device.
   Cannula (100)
   End-expiratory CO2 catheter (200)
   Suction tube (300)
   Balloon (400)

PN JP2020020012-A
TI Pig iron manufacturing facility has hot blast stove that is supplied to hot-air blast furnace, and where portion of flue gas of hot blast stove becomes tuyere cooling gas of oxygen blast furnace.
AU OZAWA S
   NOUCHI T
   KAWAJIRI Y
   TAKAHASHI K
AE JFE STEEL CORP (JFES-C)
GA 202010278B
AB    NOVELTY - The facility has an oxygen blast furnace (1) and a hot-air blast furnace (1'). The oxygen blast furnace blows pure oxygen and a reducing agent, a tuyere cooling gas from a tuyere (4), and generates an oxygen blast furnace gas containing no nitrogen from the furnace top. The air is heated by the heat generated when the oxygen blast furnace gas and the oxygen-containing gas containing no nitrogen are burned to generate hot-air stove combustion exhaust gas. A hot blast stove (2) is supplied to the hot blast furnace. A portion of the flue gas of the hot blast stove becomes a tuyere cooling gas of the oxygen blast furnace.
   USE - Pig iron manufacturing facility.
   ADVANTAGE - The tuyere tip temperature is appropriately controlled with a small amount of tuyere cooling gas. Since the blowing facility can blow a small amount of tuyere cooling gas, the oxygen blast furnace and peripheral facilities can be reduced in size without adding extra equipment such as carbon dioxide separation unit. The occurrence of abnormally high temperature combustion flames generated by pure oxygen combustion is suppressed. The refractory damage in the hot stove is suppressed while maintaining the tuyere tip cooling effect of the hot stove combustion exhaust gas. The carbon dioxide emission reduction is realized.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a pig-iron manufacturing method using pig-iron manufacturing facility.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the pig iron manufacturing facility. (Drawing includes non-English language text)
   Oxygen blast furnace (1)
   Hot-air blast furnace (1')
   Hot blast stove (2)
   Burner (3)
   Tuyere (4)

PT P
PN CN109821552-A
TI Catalyst useful for complete catalytic oxidation of toluene into carbon dioxide and water comprises platinum nanoparticles uniformly loaded on cobalt(II, III) oxide two dimensional nanosheet.
AU ZHANG T
   DENG J
   LIU Y
   DAI H
   GUO G
AE UNIV BEIJING TECHNOLOGY (UYBT-C)
GA 201950120A
AB    NOVELTY - Catalyst comprises platinum nanoparticles uniformly loaded on cobalt(II, III) oxide two dimensional nanosheet. The cobalt(II, III) oxide two-dimensional nanosheet is a transparent gauze like structure with a thickness in nanometer range. The amount of platinum loaded is 0.1-5 wt.%.
   USE - The catalyst is useful for complete catalytic oxidation of toluene into carbon dioxide and water (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The catalyst has simple preparation process and has a controllable structure.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for preparing the catalyst comprising (a) adding cobalt nitrate hexahydrate and urea to a solution comprising deionized water and ethylene glycol, heating the solution and stirring in a water bath at 60-90 degrees C for 8-14 hours, filtering and washing to obtain a green solid, then placing the green solid in a vacuum oven at 60 degrees C for vaccum drying, placing the dried solid in a muffle furnace and heating to 250-350 degrees C, preferably 300 degrees C at a heating rate of 2-4 degrees C/minute, preferably 3 degrees C/minute, maintaining for 2-4 hours, preferably 3 hours and naturally cooling to room temperature to obtain a cobalt(II, III) oxide two-dimensional nanosheet, (b) adding an aqueous solution of chloroplatinic acid to an aqueous solution of polyvinyl alcohol in an ice water bath, uniformly stirring, adding an aqueous solution of sodium borohydride and reacting for 1 hour, adding cobalt(II, III) oxide two-dimensional nanosheet, stirring for 6 hours, washing by filtration, drying in a muffle furnace and heating to 250-350 degrees C, preferably 300 degrees C at a heating rate of 2-4 degrees C/minute and maintaining for 2-4 hours, preferably 3 hours to obtain platinum/two-dimensional nanosheet of cobalt(II, III) oxide.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The cobalt(II, III) oxide two-dimensional nanosheet has a thickness of 0.1-5 nm. In the step (a) every 5 mmol of cobalt nitrate hexahydrate corresponding to 20 mmol urea, 15 ml deionized water and 105 ml ethylene glycol. In the step (b): the concentration of the aqueous solution of chloroplatinic acid is 0.1 mol/l and the concentration of the aqueous solution of polyvinyl alcohol is 1 g/l. The mass ratio of platinum/polyvinyl alcohol is 1.5:1. The concentration of aqueous sodium borohydride solution is 0.1 mol/l and the molar ratio of platinum/sodium borohydride is 1:5.

TI Automatic replenishment plant hydroponic tank for cultivating aquatic plants, has fill light, heating sheet, fan, and start and close circuit of solenoid valve that are connected to local control terminal by wires.
AU ZHANG Z

   UNIV SHIHEZI (USHZ-C)
GA 201925087L
AB    NOVELTY - The plant hydroponic tank has a plant box (1), a water storage tank (2), and a fill light (3) that is provided for adjusting the light intensity of the plant. A fan (5) is provided for adjusting the concentration of carbon dioxide. A sensor module is provided for real-time monitoring of light intensity, air humidity and carbon dioxide concentration, and a local control terminal. The water storage tank is connected to the plant box through a hose and the water inlet. A solenoid valve is installed at a through hole of the water inlet. The sensor module and the local control end are placed on a left side panel of the plant box. The sensor module is in communication with the local control terminal. The fill light, a heating sheet (4), and the fan are all placed on a top cover of the plant box. The fill light, the heating sheet, the fan, and the start and close circuit of the solenoid valve are connected to the local control terminal by wires.
   USE - Automatic replenishment plant hydroponic tank for cultivating aquatic plants.
   ADVANTAGE - The plant hydroponic tank automatically replenishes the required components according to the actual environmental information, even when people go out for a long time without affecting the quality of plant growth. The automatic replenishment plant hydroponic box can complete the safe data storage, calculation and analysis of massive data, and use the existing computing power and optimization algorithm of the cloud platform to rapidly generate plant growth.

PN US2019064061-A1
TI Gas sensing device for identifying e.g. carbon monoxide gas for e.g. air pollution monitoring application, has radiation source coupled to substrate, and detector coupled to substrate and detecting radiation transmitting cavities.
AU GUDEMAN C S
AE INNOVATIVE MICRO TECHNOLOGY (INNO-Non-standard)
GA 2019196129
AB    NOVELTY - The device has a substrate provided with lithographically formed cavities/channels (40-44) and a reflective film coating sidewalls. The lithographically formed cavities facilitate multiple passes of a ray of light within the lithographically formed cavities. A sample gas fills the lithographically formed cavities. A radiation source (10) is coupled to the substrate that launches radiation into the lithographically formed cavities. A detector (30) is coupled to the substrate, and detects radiation transmitting the cavities. The sidewalls are coated with a reflective film i.e. gold.
   USE - Gas sensing device for sensing and identifying gases e.g. carbon monoxide gas and carbon-dioxide gas. Uses include but are not limited to an air pollution monitoring application, automotive engine performance optimization application, industrial chemical synthesis control application, automotive passenger compartment carbon dioxide sensing application, home carbon monoxide sensing application, fermentation process control application and indoor agriculture application.
   ADVANTAGE - The device reduces risk of false positive and false negative responses. The device allows dominant constituents of air to do not combust, so that trace species can be more easily detected without interference by measuring the heat generated. The detector is only sensitive to appropriate range of frequencies, and can put out a signal in response to the reception of the radiation. The device is small, inexpensive, robust and highly sensitive.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for the following:
   (1) a method for measuring a gas sample
   (2) a method for manufacturing a gas sensor.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a cross sectional view of a lithographically formed channels with a vertical cavity surface emitting laser source such that cavities are formed in an isotropic substrate.
   Radiation source (10)
   Detector (30)
   Lithographically formed cavities/channels (40-44)

PT P
PN CN109233125-A
TI Expanded Polystyrene (EPS) sound absorbing panel comprises polystyrene resin, polyurethane, opening agent, nucleating agent, and addition agent, where opening agent is polyalkylene oxide polysiloxane copolymer, nucleating agent is graphene.
AU PENG Q
AE WUHU XINJINGQIAO PACKAGING TECHNOLOGY CO (WUHU-Non-standard)
GA 201913301W
AB    NOVELTY - Expanded Polystyrene (EPS) sound absorbing panel comprises 50-60 pts. wt. polystyrene resin, 30-40 pts. wt. polyurethane, 2-4 pts. wt. opening agent, 1-3 pts. wt. nucleating agent, and 1-2 pts. wt. addition agent.
   USE - Expanded Polystyrene (EPS) sound absorbing panel.
   ADVANTAGE - The expanded Polystyrene (EPS) sound absorbing panel is suitable to popularize and use, dense and small internal voids, has convenient operation, and in simple manner.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for preparing expanded Polystyrene (EPS) sound absorbing panel, which involves:
   (A) mixing polystyrene resin and pore forming agent, nucleating agent and stirring into high pressure kettle, heating at high pressure in kettle, continuously stirring until mixture into liquid;
   (B) putting high pressure kettle, adjusting temperature to 110-120 degrees C, increasing pressure to 20-25 megapascal, then filling supercritical state carbon dioxide into high-pressure kettle, continuously stirring and mixing to mix uniformly;
   (C) reducing high pressure kettle in pressure, reducing gas solubility to generate bubbles, subjecting to foaming treatment in the mixture;
   (D) extruding mixture, molding, placing into granulator to granulate to obtain noise-proof grain;
   (E) adding polyurethane, auxiliary agent into the reaction kettle, raising the temperature to 140-160 degrees C, stirring for 10-20 minutes, changing the temperature of the reaction kettle to 60-80 degrees C, then adding anti-noise particle, continuously stirring for 10-20 minutes to obtain base material; and
   (F) adjusting temperature to 35-40 degrees C, filling supercritical state carbon dioxide, mixing for 1-2 hours, heating reaction kettle, making base material to foam, foaming base material into forming machine, extruding and molding to obtain expanded Polystyrene (EPS) sound absorbing panel.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Composition: The expanded Polystyrene (EPS) sound absorbing panel comprises 55 pts. wt. polystyrene resin, 35 pts. wt. polyurethane, 3 pts. wt. opening agent, 2 pts. wt. nucleating agent, and 1.5 pts. wt. addition agent. Preferred Components: The opening agent is polyalkylene oxide polysiloxane copolymer. The nucleating agent is graphene, talcum powder and diatomite in ratio of 3:1:3. The auxiliary agent is monoglyceride. Preferred Condition: The high pressure kettle heating temperature is 180-200 degrees C. The pressure is 10-12 megapascal. The supercritical carbon dioxide is introduced is 60-80 minutes. The pressure of reduced speed is 100 pascal/s. The pressure is reduced to 2-4 megapascal. The keeping time is 2-3 hours. The anti-noise particle size is 2-3 mm. The temperature of heating the reaction kettle is 65-75 degrees C. The foaming time is 40-60 minutes.
DC A13 (Polymers of aromatic mono-olefins; including polystyrene.); A25 (Polyurethanes; polyethers.); A26 (Other condensation polymers including silicone polymers and polyimides (mineral silicates and similar materials would not usually appear in Section A).); A35 (Other processing and general - including vulcanisation, welding of plastics and adhesive processes. Testing.); A94 (Semi-finished materials - fibres, films, foams.)
MC A04-C04; A07-A04E; A08-B04B; A08-M03; A08-M10; A11-A03; A11-A04; A11-B06A; A12-R06; A12-S01A; A12-S02C; A12-S02F; A12-S09A

PN CN109205950-A
TI System for processing ore beneficiation waste water, has reactor from which purified wastewater is discharged through drain valve located on upper right side of reactor, where strontium salt scum is recovered and reused.
AU TU R
AE TU R (TURR-Individual)
GA 201908091N
AB    NOVELTY - The system has a coarse grid (2), carbon dioxide vortex convection slag separation reactor (5), aeration tank (6), biological oxidation filter (7), secondary sedimentation tank (8), clean water tank (9). The outlet of collecting well (1) is connected to the coarse grid through the waste water line. The outlet of the coarse grid is connected to the sedimentation tank through a waste water line. The outlet of the primary sedimentation tank (3) is connected to the pH adjustment tank (4) through the waste water line. The slag salt scum is formed and sucked into the scum suction separation device through the scum suction pipe. The separated slag-containing slag is discharged from the slag discharge port located on the right side of the scum suction separation device. The purified wastewater is discharged from the reactor through a drain valve located on the upper right side of the reactor. The strontium salt scum is recovered and reused after being separated by suction.
   USE - System for processing ore beneficiation waste water.
   ADVANTAGE - The cerium ion is combined with the carbonate ion to form a precipitate of insoluble cerium salt, and floats along a narrow region between the two vortexes to form a strontium salt scum, thus removing strontium ions in the wastewater.

TI Repair ointment used in skin care product, comprises Camellia fruit, oyster sauce, Teucrium viscidum, gold bars, grass, tassel roots, Vitis bryoniifolia root, and Crotalaria flower head.

AE GUANGXI JINGXI RUITAI FOOD CO LTD (GUAN-Non-standard)
GA 201901122G
AB    NOVELTY - A repair ointment comprises 6-12 pts. wt., preferably 12 pts. wt. Camellia fruit, 12-18 pts. wt., preferably 18 pts. wt. oyster sauce, 5-9 pts. wt., preferably 9 pts. wt. Teucrium viscidum, 3-9 pts. wt., preferably 9 pts. wt. gold bars, 2-7 pts. wt., preferably 7 pts. wt. grass, 3-10 pts. wt., preferably 10 pts. wt. tassel roots, 14-18 pts. wt., preferably 18 pts. wt.    USE - Repair ointment used in skin care product.
   ADVANTAGE - The repair ointment can reduce inflammation on skin, and has antibacterial property.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for preparation of the repair ointment, which involves pulverizing Camellia fruit into mixed powder of 130-150 mesh, adding 75% ethyl acetate, centrifuging, collecting supernatant liquid, vacuum freeze-drying for 20-24 hours to obtain Camellia extract, mixing and crushing Teucrium viscidum, gold bar, grass, tassel root, Vitis bryoniifolia, and Crotalaria flower head to obtain powder, adding powder to supercritical carbon dioxide extraction at 50-55 degrees C and 20-25 MPa for 15 minutes, extracting with anhydrous ethanol as solvent, concentrating and recovering ethanol under reduced pressure to obtain brownish yellow viscous oil, boiling drug residue after supercritical carbon dioxide extraction with 4-6 times the amount of water for 30-50 minutes, and with 3-5 times the amount of water for 80-90 minutes, filtering, mixing two filtrates, concentrating 0.9-1.2 relative density at 50 degrees C, drying extract to obtain extract, concentrating extract using macroporous adsorption resin column to obtain brown powder, mixing brownish yellow viscous oil and brown powder to obtain Chinese medicine extract, mixing Chinese medicine extract and Camellia extract, adding eucalyptus oil, and homogenizing for 15 minutes at 35 degrees C.
TF ACTIVITY - Antiinflammatory.

PN CN109026231-A; CN109026231-B
TI Marine energy storage and energy release combined circulation system based on tanker platform, has gas-steam combined cycle cogeneration unit that uses chemical energy to perform work, and generated energy is sent to energy consuming device.
AU SUN Z
   ZHANG Y
   CHEN Y
   XI G
AE UNIV XIAN JIAOTONG (UYXJ-C)
GA 2018A50508
AB    NOVELTY - The system has a renewable energy collection and power generation device (1) that is provided for collecting marine renewable energy into electricity, and outputting a portion of the electric energy to an energy consuming device (19). The other portion of the electrical energy is converted into chemical energy by a power-to-gas energy storage unit and stored. A gas-steam combined cycle cogeneration unit uses the chemical energy stored in the power-to-gas energy storage unit to perform work. The generated electric energy is output to the energy consuming device, and the generated thermal energy is sent to the energy consuming device for heating by the extraction.
   USE - Marine energy storage and energy release combined circulation system based on tanker platform.
   ADVANTAGE - The oxygen gas produced by electrolysis is supplied to gas turbine to assist combustion, and the generated hydrogen and separated carbon dioxide are used as raw materials for synthesizing methane, and the material resources at sea are effectively utilized, by using seawater as a raw material for electrolytic substances. The production of methane gas using carbon dioxide as raw material achieves a carbon cycle in the process of energy storage and energy release, thus effectively reducing CO2 emissions. The gas-steam combined cycle can replace the oil-fired boiler and the power-generating diesel engine on the tanker, which improves the power generation efficiency, and makes full use of the marine resources, reduces the fuel consumption and reduces the fuel exhaust emissions.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the marine energy storage and energy release combined circulation system.
   Renewable energy collection and power generation device (1)
   Rectifier (2)
   Electrolytic device (3)
   Seawater filtration and desalination unit (4)
   Energy consuming device (19)
