TI Composition for heat transfer device, e.g.            refrigeration device or blast freezer used for e.g.            extracting substance from biomass comprises            1,1-difluoroethene, carbon dioxide, pentafluoroethane            and trifluoromethane.
AU LOW R
   LAW R
AE MEXICHEM FLUOR SA DE CV (MEXI-C)
   MEXICHEM FLUOR SA DE CV (MEXI-C)
   MEXICHEM FLUOR SA DE CV (MEXI-C)
GA 2018911267
AB    NOVELTY - A composition comprises (i) 1,1-difluoroethene                (vinylidene fluoride, R-1132a), (ii) carbon dioxide                (R-744), pentafluoroethane (R-125) and                trifluoromethane (R-23).
   USE - A composition for heat transfer device, e.g.                refrigeration device or blast freezer used for                extracting substance from biomass, cleaning                article, extracting material from aqueous solution                or from particulate solid matrix, retrofitting heat                transfer device, reducing environmental impact                arising from operation of product containing                existing compound or composition and generating                greenhouse gas emission credit (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - Composition has higher flammable limit, higher                ignition energy and/or lower flame velocity                compared to R-1132a alone. Composition has lower                Total Equivalent Warming Impact and/or Lower                Life-Cycle Carbon Production.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for:
   (1) composition comprising lubricant and                composition;
   (2) composition comprising stabilizer and                composition;
   (3) composition comprising flame retardant and                composition;
   (4) cooling article which involves condensing                composition and evaporating in vicinity of article                to be cooled;
   (5) extracting substance from biomass which                involves contacting biomass with solvent containing                composition and separating substance from                solvent;
   (6) cleaning article which involves contacting                article with solvent containing composition;
   (7) extracting material from aqueous solution                or from particulate solid matrix which involves                contacting aqueous solution or particulate solid                matrix with solvent containing composition and                separating material from solvent;
   (8) retrofitting heat transfer device which                involves removing existing heat transfer                composition and introducing composition;
   (9) reducing environmental impact arising from                operation of product containing existing compound                or composition which involves replacing partially                existing compound or composition with composition;                and
   (10) generating greenhouse gas emission credit                which involves replacing existing compound or                composition with composition having lower global                warming potential (GWP) than existing compound or                composition, and obtaining greenhouse gas emission                credit for replacing step.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Compositions: Composition contains                  1-90 wt.%, preferably 25-30 wt.% R-1132a, 1-90                  wt.%, preferably 35-50 wt.% carbon dioxide, 1-90                  wt.%, preferably 10-25 wt.% R-125, and 1-90 wt.%,                  preferably 5-20 wt.% R-23. Composition further                  contains hexafluoroethane (R-116). Composition                  further contains pentane. Stabilizer is chosen                  from diene-based compounds, phosphates, phenol                  compounds and/or epoxides. Flame retardant is                  chosen from tri-(2-chloroethyl)-phosphate,                  (chloropropyl)phosphate,                  tri-(2,3-dibromopropyl)phosphate,                  tri(1,3-dichloropropyl)phosphate, diammonium                  phosphate, halogenated aromatic compounds,                  antimony oxide, aluminum trihydrate, PVC,                  fluorinated iodocarbon, fluorinated bromocarbon,                  trifluoro iodomethane, perfluoroalkyl amines, and                  bromo-fluoroalkyl amines. Refrigerant is chosen                  from R-508A, R-508B, R-23 and R-13B1. Preferred                  Conditions: Composition is azeotropic or near                  azeotropic. Compressor discharge temperature of                  R-23 is 15 K. Refrigeration system cools                  compartment to - 60 degrees C, preferably - 70                  degrees C. Preferred Properties: Composition is                  less flammable than R-1132a alone, or is                  non-flammable, or has temperature glide in                  evaporator or condenser of less than or equal to                  10 K, preferably less than or equal to 7 K, or                  critical temperature greater than or equal to 0                  degrees C, preferably greater than or equal to 10                  degrees C. R-23 has volumetric refrigeration                  capacity of greater than or equal to 90%,                  preferably greater than or equal to 95% at                  comparable cycle conditions. Composition has                  cycle efficiecy of greater than or equal to 95%                  and/or within 5% than existing refrigerant fluid                  it is replacing.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: Lubricant is chosen                  from mineral oil, silicone oil, polyalkyl benzene                  (PAB), polyol esters (POE), polyalkylene glycols                  (PAG), PAG ester, polyvinyl ethers (PVE), and/or                  poly( alpha -olefins), preferably PAG or POE.                  Preferred Devices: Heat transfer device includes                  ultra-low temperature refrigeration system and                  cascade system.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

PN CN108774364-A
TI Rubber comprises ethylene propylene diene terpolymer, styrene-butadiene, vulcanizing agent, white carbon black, zinc oxide, magnesium oxide, crosslinker, softener, stearic acid and sulfur.
AU XIA L
AE NINGBO ZHENGDAO PARTS CO LTD (NING-Non-standard)
GA 2018900184
AB    NOVELTY - Rubber comprises 100 pts. wt. ethylene propylene diene terpolymer , 40-60 pts. wt. styrene-butadiene, 3-7 pts. wt. vulcanizing agent, 18-25 pts. wt. white carbon black, 3-5 pts. wt. zinc oxide, 3-7 pts. wt. magnesium oxide, 2-8 pts. wt. crosslinker, 1-10 pts. wt. softener, 3-5 pts. wt. stearic acid and 1-5 pts. wt. sulfur.
   USE - Used as ethylene propylene diene terpolymer/styrene-butadiene rubber.
   ADVANTAGE - The method utilizes supercritical carbon dioxide technology, thus improves compatibility and co-vulcanization properties and increases vulcanization rate. The rubber has good resilience and mechanical properties and meets the requirements of sealing material under special environment.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for preparing the rubber, comprising (i) adding ethylene propylene diene terpolymer to the internal mixer and plasticizing for 3-10 minutes, sequentially adding stearic acid, zinc oxide and white carbon black and mixing to obtain ethylene propylene diene terpolymer master batch, (ii) adding styrene-butadiene rubber to the internal mixer and plasticizing for 3-10 minutes, adding stearic acid and magnesium oxide and mixing to obtain styrene-butadiene rubber master batch, (iii) adding ethylene propylene diene terpolymer master batch, styrene-butadiene rubber master batch, crosslinker, softener and sulfur into the internal mixer and mixing for 5-20 minutes, extruding and granulating, hot pressing the particles into sheet having thickness of 3-5 mm and placing the sheet in a sealed autoclave, filling carbon dioxide in sealed autoclave, keeping the sealed autoclave at 50-150 degrees C for 1-2 hours, opening the autoclave and allowing the sheet to stand in air for 20-50 hours to obtain rubber mix, and (iv) vulcanizing rubber mix using a vulcanizing agent in a vulcanizer for 25-50 minutes.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: In the step (i), the internal mixer temperature is 80-90 degrees C and mixing time is 5-15 minutes. In the step (ii), the internal mixer temperature is 60-70 degrees C and mixing time is 5-15 minutes. Preferred Method: The step (iii) comprises filling carbon dioxide in sealed autoclave until the pressure of autoclave reaches 10-100MPa. The step (iii) comprises adding ethylene propylene diene terpolymer master batch, styrene-butadiene rubber master batch, crosslinker, softener into the internal mixer and mixing for 1-3 minutes, and adding sulfur and processing.

TI Synthesizing alpha-quat. carbon amino acid            involves reacting reaction substrate, photocatalyst,            alkali, reducing agent and solvent, quenching, and            separating and purifying.
AU YU D
   LI J
   JU T
   FU Q
   YE J
   ZHANG Z
   LIAO L
   YAN S
   TIAN X
   LUO S
AE UNIV SICHUAN (USCU-C)
GA 201888567U
AB    NOVELTY - Synthesizing alpha -quat. carbon amino acid                involves adding reaction device with reaction                substrate, photocatalyst and alkali, adding                reducing agent and solvent under carbon dioxide                atmosphere, reacting under visible light                irradiation, quenching, and separating and                purifying. The photocatalyst comprises                2,4,5,6-tetra(9H-carbazol-9-yl)isophthalonitrile,                (4,4-bis(1,1-dimethylethyl)-2,2-bipyridine-N1,N1                )bis(2-(2-pyridinyl-N)phenyl-C)iridium(III)hexafluorophosphate,                Ir(dF(CF3)ppy)2(dtbbpy)PF6, iridium(III)                bis(2-(2,4-difluorophenyl)-5-methylpyridine-N),                Ir(dF(CF3)ppy)2(Phen)PF6, Ir(ppy)2(bpy)PF6 and/or                fac-Ir(ppy)3. The reaction substrate comprises                amide compound (I) or imide compound (II).
   USE - Method for synthesizing alpha -quat. carbon                amino acid.
   ADVANTAGE - The method has mild reaction condition, and                overcomes high reagent toxicity and rigorous                reaction condition.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Synthesizing alpha -quat. carbon amino acid                involves adding reaction device with reaction                substrate, photocatalyst and alkali, adding                reducing agent and solvent under carbon dioxide                atmosphere, reacting under visible light                irradiation, quenching, and separating and                purifying. The photocatalyst comprises                2,4,5,6-tetra(9H-carbazol-9-yl)isophthalonitrile,                (4,4-bis(1,1-dimethylethyl)-2,2                -bipyridine-N1,N1)bis(2-(2-pyridinyl-N)phenyl-C)iridium(III)hexafluorophosphate,                Ir(dF(CF3)ppy)2(dtbbpy)PF6, iridium(III)                bis(2-(2,4-difluorophenyl)-5-methylpyridine-N),                Ir(dF(CF3)ppy)2(Phen)PF6, Ir(ppy)2(bpy)PF6 and/or                fac-Ir(ppy)3. The reaction substrate comprises                amide compound of formula (I) or imide compound of                formula (II).
   R1=aryl, substituted aryl or heteroaryl;
   R2=aryl, substituted aryl, alkyl and/or                heterocyclyl;
   R3=aryl or substituted aryl;
   R4=H, alkyl, aryl and substituted aryl;                and
   R5=, alkyl, aryl, substituted aryl, cyclohexyl                and/or alkenyl.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The alkali comprises                  potassium carbonate or cesium carbonate,                  preferably cesium carbonate. Preferred Method:                  Reaction is done under carbon dioxide atmosphere                  at room temperature for 4 hours at 1 atm.                  Quenching is done under acid comprising sulfuric                  acid and phosphoric acid, preferably using water,                  ethyl acetate and hydrochloric acid mixtures.                  Separating and purifying is done by                  chromatography column with eluent mixed solution                  of petroleum ether and ethyl acetate.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The solvent is polar                  organic solvent comprising dimethyl formamide,                  dimethylacetamide, N methyl 2 pyrrolidone,                  acetonitrile and 1,4-dioxane.

PT P
PN CN108614098-A; CN108614098-B
TI Kit useful for activating unfertilized oocytes after intracytoplasmic sperm injection (ICSI) in patients, comprises calcium ionophore solution and phosphatidylinositol-4,5-bisphosphate solution.
AU LI J
   QIAO J
   LIU P
AE UNIV PEKING THIRD HOSPITAL (UYPK-C)
GA 201880334S
AB    NOVELTY - Kit comprises (a) calcium ionophore solution and (b) phosphatidylinositol-4,5-bisphosphate solution.
   USE - The kit is useful for activating unfertilized oocytes after ICSI in patients with a history of failed ICSI fertilization, round-headed sperm syndrome, inactive sperm, severe sperm abnormalities and failed egg activation due to lack of phosphatidylinositol-4,5-bisphosphate in the egg (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The kit exhibits synergistic effect and ensures that the activation method is simple.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for method for using the kit for activating unfertilized oocytes after intracytoplasmic sperm injection (ICSI), which involves (a) adding calcium ionophore solution and the phosphatidylinositol-4,5-diphosphoric acid solution to the embryo culture solution, uniformly mixing so that the working concentration of the calcium ionophore is 5-15 mu M and phosphatidylinositol-4,5-bisphosphate is 5-15 mu M, (b) adding the activator working solution obtained in step (a) to the first well of the four-well culture dish and adding the embryo culture solution to the second, third and fourth wells of the four-well culture, (c) adding the unfertilized oocytes after ICSI into the first well and incubating the four-well culture dish at 37 degrees C under 6% carbon dioxide culture in incubator for 5-10 minutes, (d) then transferring oocytes into the second well, washing the oocytes in the 6-8 times with the pipette tip, then transferring into the third well and washing 6-8 times, (e) then transferring the oocytes into the fourth well and incubating the four-well culture dish at 37 degrees C under 6% carbon dioxide culture in incubator for 20-30 minutes and (f) then transferring the oocytes into the embryo culture medium and culturing until the pronuclei formation is observed.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Kit: The kit comprises (a) dimethyl sulfoxide solution of calcium ionophore and (b) dimethyl sulfoxide solution of phosphatidylinositol-4,5-bisphosphate. Preferred Components: The calcium ionophore is ionomycin or ionomycin calcium salt. The embryo culture solution comprises Vitrolife's G-IVF (RTM: Fertilization medium), G-IVF plus (RTM: Fertilization medium), G1 (RTM: Fertilization medium), COOK and an embryo culture solution produced by SAGE.

PN CN108504349-A; CN108504349-B
TI Preparing rhodamine hybridized carbon point for targeting mitochondrial, involves dissolving the citric acid and aminophenol compound into distilled water to prepare precursor solution, placing in microwave reaction tube.
AU LI C
   GENG X
   SUN Y
   QU L
AE UNIV ZHENGZHOU (UYZZ-C)
   UNIV ZHENGZHOU (UYZZ-C)
GA 201872362N
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing rhodamine hybridized carbon point involves dissolving the citric acid and aminophenol compound into distilled water to prepare precursor solution, placing in microwave reaction tube. The microwave reaction tube is placed in the microwave reactor, precursor solution is heated to 120-160 degrees C and reacted for 0.5-6 hours, naturally cooled the product after reaction to room temperature. The product after the cooling and filtered through 0.22 mu m filter film to remove large particles, rhodamine with interception amount of 3 KiloDalton to obtain purified-SAE-collegiate Design Series solution, vacuum freezing and drying the rhodamine-SAE-collegiate Design Series solution to obtain rhodamine-SAE-collegiate Design Series solid powder.
   USE - Preparing rhodamine hybridized carbon point for targeting mitochondrial.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to prepare rhodamine hybridized carbon point which has potential application value.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for targeting mitochondrial targeting identified, which involves:
   (A) inoculating the cell in common focus at 37 degrees C, adding 5% carbon dioxide, cell culture box with saturated humidity to culture for 12-24 hours to obtain first culture liquid;
   (B) adding rhodamine-collegiate Design Series in the culture solution at 37?iC;
   (C) adding 5% carbon dioxide, cell culture box with saturated humidity to culture for 4 hours to obtain second culture solution;
   (D) adding mitochondrial rhodamine 123 dyeing agent to second culture solution, incubating for 10 minutes to obtain third culture liquid;
   (E) adding Hoechst reagent NucRedTMLive 647, to obtain third culture solution, incubating for 10 minutes to obtain mixed solution; and
   (F) using phosphate buffer solution to wash cell, adding fresh phenol red-free cell culture liquid,using the laser confocal microscope to shoot image.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Components: The aminophenol compound is m-aminophenol, m-ethyl amino cresol, meta-diethylamnophenol and 8-hydroxyzuridine. The cell is Hela cell, A549 cell, MCF7 cell and 3T3 cell. Preferred Composition: The mole ratio of the citric acid and aminophenol compound is (1:2)-(2:1). The concentration of nuclear transfection reagent NucRedTMLive 647 is 2 drops/mL. The co-focus dish, cell culture liquid is Dulbecco's Modified Eagle's culture medium, 10% fetal calf serum, 100 mu /mL penicillin and 100 streptomycin mu g/mL. The concentration of Rho-SAE-collegiate Design Series is 50 mu mL. The concentration of mitochondrial rhodamine 123 dyeing agent added in solution is 1 mu M.

PN CN108324921-A; WO2019218103-A1; CN108324921-B
TI Pharmaceutical composition used for e.g. removing cyst and sores, and protecting gastric mucosa, comprises propolis, pseudoginseng, radix astragali, rhizoma bletillae, oyster peptide, radix scrophulariae and Potentilla discolor root.
AU JIA F
   TAO G
   TU H
   YAN Y
   WANG J
   XIONG F
   WANG C
   XU L
   YUAN Y
AE NINGBO YOFOTO BIOTECHNOLOGIES CO LTD (NING-Non-standard)
   NINGBO YUFANGTANG BIOLOGICAL SCI & TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (NING-Non-standard)
GA 201863228B
AB    NOVELTY - Pharmaceutical composition comprises 2-10 pts. wt. propolis, 8-13 pts. wt. pseudoginseng, 27-44 pts. wt. radix astragali, 18-36 pts. wt. rhizoma bletillae, 3-8 pts. wt. oyster peptide, 5-12 pts. wt. radix scrophulariae and 4-10 pts. wt. Potentilla discolor root.
   USE - The pharmaceutical composition is useful for removing cyst and protecting gastric mucosa, removing blood stasis, sores, toxicity and pus, preventing dysentery and bleeding, relieving swelling and promoting granulation, regulating qi, nourishing yang, promoting diuresis and alleviating edema, clear away heat, and enhancing immunity.
   ADVANTAGE - The pharmaceutical composition exhibits synergistic effect.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for method for preparing pharmaceutical composition, which involves (a) adding 6-12 times ethanol solution to propolis, percolating and extracting propolis, centrifuging extract to remove impurities, concentrating extract in vacuum until relative density reaches 1.031.06, precipitating concentrated solution overnight, removing upper beeswax layer and drying lower layer solution in a vacuum drying oven for 6-12 hours, crushing propolis alcohol extract into powder, performing ultra-fine pulverization on pseudoginseng at low temperature to obtain 800-1000 mesh powder, (b) extracting radix astragali and rhizoma bletillae at low temperature, centrifuging and concentrating extract, and spray drying concentrate into powder, (c) slicing or crushing radix scrophulariae and P.discolor root, performing supercritical carbon dioxide extraction using supercritical carbon dioxide extraction device, freeze-drying extract in vacuum and crushing freeze-dried product into powder, (d) crushing and homogenizing oyster meat, adding pure water and uniformly mixing homogenate, heating, centrifuging and degreasing homogenate, adjusting pH to 7, adding protease preparation, performing enzymolysis and inactivating enzyme, centrifuging and degreasing enzymolyzate, performing micro-filtration and nano-filtration, concentrating and decolorizing filtrate using activated carbon, sterilizing and spray drying concentrate, and (e) mixing products obtained in steps (a)-(d) for 5-10 minutes.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - PHARMACEUTICALS - Preferred Composition: The pharmaceutical composition comprises 4 pts. wt. propolis, 13 pts. wt. pseudoginseng, 37 pts. wt. radix astragali, 21 pts. wt. rhizoma bletillae, 5 pts. wt. oyster peptide, 12 pts. wt. radix scrophulariae and 8 pts. wt. P.discolor root. Preferred Method: The step (b) involves adding 8-10 times water to radix astragali and rhizoma bletillae, heating and extracting materials at 90-100 degrees C for 1-3 times for 1-2 hours each tiem, centrifuging extract, concentrating supernatant at 65-85 degrees C and -0.05 to -0.08 MPa until relative density reaches 1.04-1.06, filtering and sterilizing concentrated solution, and spray drying filtrate at a flow rate of 1.5-2.5 l/minute, where nozzle temperature is 175-185 degrees C and outlet temperature is 85-92 degrees C. The step (c) involves (i) crushing radix scrophulariae into coarse powder and sieving powder using 8-14 mesh sieve, performing supercritical carbon dioxide extraction at 40-48 degrees C and 25-40 MPa using 1-1.4 ml/g of 60-80% ethanol solution as entrainer under carbon dioxide flow rate of 10-12 kg/hour for 120-180 minutes, pre-freezing extract at -30 degrees C to -35 degrees C at a rate of 0.3-0.6 degrees C/minute, where pre-freezing end temperature is -65 degrees C, and drying frozen extract at 35-75 Pa and 30-40 degrees C for 12-18 hours, and crushing freeze-dried product, and (ii) crushing P.discolor root into coarse powder and sieving powder using 10-16 mesh sieve, performing supercritical carbon dioxide extraction at 30-40 degrees C and 30-35 MPa under carbon dioxide flow rate of 8-12 kg/hour for 90-120 minutes, pre-freezing extract at -35 degrees C to -40 degrees C at a rate of 0.4-0.8 degrees C/minute, where pre-freezing end temperature is -60 degrees C, and drying frozen extract at 60-90 Pa and 35-45 degrees C for 14-20 hours, and crushing freeze-dried product. The step (d) involves thawing, crushing and homogenizing oyster meat using crusher, adding 55 degrees C pure water in a ratio of 1:3-5, homogenizing and heating solution at 55 degrees C, centrifuging and degreasing homogenate, adjusting pH to 7, adding 0.5-1.5 wt.% compound protease preparation (papain and neutral protease in a ratio of 1:1), performing enzymolysis for 3-5 hours, filtering enzymolyzate, performing ultra-high temperature sterilization at 120-135 degrees C for 15-20 seconds, performing micro-filtration through ceramic membrane, performing nano-filtration, concentrating filtrate to 35 plus minus 0.5 Brix, adding 6 kg/100 l activated carbon, decolorizing solution at 75 plus minus 2 degrees C for 20-40 minutes, sterilizing concentrated solution and reducing the temperature to 55 plus minus 5 degrees C, spray drying solution at an air inlet temperature of 170-185 degrees C and air outlet temperature of 65-90 degrees C until moisture content reaches 6% or more.
   ACTIVITY - Gastrointestinal-Gen.; Thrombolytic; Anticoagulant; Dermatological; Antidote; Antibacterial; Antidiarrheic; Hemostatic; Antiinflammatory; Diuretic; Immunostimulant.

PT P
PN RD650015-A
TI Method for injecting gas i.e. carbon dioxide, into hydrocarbon well for e.g. oil recovery services, involves opening set of perforations when pressure in formation increases to predetermined pressure at level greater than previous level.
AU 
AE STATOIL PETROLEUM AS (DENO-C)
   DE KOEIJER G M (DKOE-Individual)
   DRESCHER M (DRES-Individual)
GA 201847138R
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves providing a casing (110) for receiving flow of gas from a wellhead along gas flow direction (130), and locating the casing partially within the formation. First set of perforations (122) is opened in the casing. Gas is injected into the formation through the first set of perforations when the formation is at first predetermined pressure level. Second set of perforations is opened when pressure in the formation increases to second predetermined pressure greater than the first predetermined pressure. Third set of perforations is opened when pressure in the formation increases to third predetermined pressure greater than the second predetermined pressure.
   USE - Method for injecting gas i.e. carbon dioxide, into a hydrocarbon well at a subterranean or subsea formation i.e. depleted hydrocarbon reservoir, for carbon capture and storage services and oil recovery services.
   ADVANTAGE - The method ensures that the perforations can fluidly connect the interior of the casing to the exterior and to the subterranean formation, so that pressure drop experienced by the injected gas can be controlled over time in response to formation pressure without need for reductions in casing diameter or additional valves, thus avoiding wear and component degradation within the reservoir. The method enables combining outflow of gas from the first, second and third sets of perforations to maintain high pressure at the wellhead to lower risk of two-phase gas flow, thus reducing risk of cavitation and low temperature during shut-in or chocking.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of a casing for a wellbore of an oil well.
   Casing (110)
   Perforations (120, 122)
   Gas flow direction (130)
   Casing interior (132)
   Reservoir (140)

TI Device for catching insects, has lower opening that is delimited by plate provided with peripheral edge which is inclined upwards and positioned at distance from lower end of container structure.
AU HUANCAHUARI TUEROS M
AE B HOLDING SRL (BHOL-Non-standard)
GA 201842154G
AB    NOVELTY - The device has a container that is made by a structure (12) provided with a first opening (14) in its upper portion and a second opening (16) in its lower portion. A Carbon dioxide (CO2) generator (18) housed inside the container. An upper opening is delimited by a cover (28) is arranged at a short distance from the upper end of the container structure and is provided with a peripheral edge (30) which inclined downwards. A lower opening is delimited by a plate (34) provided with a peripheral edge (36) which is inclined upwards and positioned at a distance from the lower end of the container structure which is at least double the distance from the cover and the upper edge of the container structure. The slope of the edge of the upper cover and of the edge of the lower plate is comprised between 110 degrees and 130 degrees with reference to a horizontal plane, so that a closed circuit flow of air and CO2 is produced from inside the structure and the external surface of the same structure.
   USE - Device for catching insects.
   ADVANTAGE - The device allows the introduction of a type of bait that attracts insects, such as substances that resemble the scent of perspiration and/or of lactic acid. The very gentle upward current is created along the external wall of the structure of the container, so as to facilitate the detachment and transportation of the insects, while the inward suction that occurs through the opening in the upper portion of the structure of the container is appreciably accelerated relative to the external flow.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a perspective view of a trap for catching insects.
   Structure (12)
   First opening (14)
   Second opening (16)
   Carbon dioxide generator (18)

   Plate (34)

AE XIANGSHUI JIAYI TEXTILE CO LTD (XIAN-Non-standard)
GA 201843374X
AB    NOVELTY - The machine has a machine body (1) whose                bottom end is formed with a sliding groove (2). An                inner wall of the machine body is connected with a                side of a servo motor (5). An upper end of the                machine body is provided with a left bracket (12)                that is provided with a right bracket (13). The                servo motor is provided with a steel plate (11). An                inner wall of the machine body is fixed with a                steel part (11) that is provided with a driven                shaft (8). An outer surface of a cloth sleeve (9)                is connected with a top end of a textile cloth roll                unit (10). A lower end of an electric hydraulic rod                (3) is fixedly connected with a pulley (4). The                electric hydraulic rod, a photoelectric protector                (14), an electromagnetic valve and input and output                ends of a control switch are electrically connected                with each other. The connecting pipe is made of                PTFE material.
   USE - Multi-functional spinning machine.

PT P
PN WO2018075713-A1; US2019315667-A1; US10604460-B2
TI Production of olefins used in e.g. petrochemical industry, involves co-cracking hydrocarbon feed comprising alkanes having preset carbon chain number with metal oxide and utilizing energy from exothermic cracking for endothermic cracking.
AU SOFRANKO J A
   MACWAN R
   CHUNG E Y
   JONES C A
AE BIO2ELECTRIC DBA ECOCATALYTIC TECHNOLOGIES LLC (BIOT-Non-standard)
   ECOCATALYTIC INC (ECOC-Non-standard)
GA 201833228J
AB    NOVELTY - Production of olefins involves co-cracking a hydrocarbon feed comprising alkane(s) having a carbon chain of 5 or more and alkane(s) having a carbon chain of 4 or less, by contacting with a metal oxide, such that the cracking of the alkane having carbon chain of 4 or less produces one or more olefins and is exothermic and the cracking of the alkane having carbon chain of 5 or more produces one or more olefins and is endothermic, utilizing the energy produced from the exothermic cracking of the alkane for the endothermic cracking of the alkane, and collecting a product from the feed.
   USE - Production of olefins e.g. ethylene and propylene (claimed) used in petrochemical industry, and manufacturing polymers e.g. polyethylene, polypropylene and polystyrene.
   ADVANTAGE - The method efficiently produces olefins with high yield and high purity by oxidative dehydrogenation co-cracking of hydrocarbon feed even at low temperatures. The method produces lower amounts of undesirable byproducts such as carbon dioxide and carbon monoxide, enables reduction of up to 90% carbon dioxide, and eliminates nitric oxide emissions. The temperature, contact time, and pressure of the multiple reduction zones of the system can be varied independently to optimize the overall yield of the desired products.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for system (300), which comprises a reactor (310) comprising metal oxide in it, configured for co-cracking alkanes by oxidative dehydrogenation cracking to produce one or more olefins.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the system for cracking or co-cracking of the hydrocarbon feed for producing olefins.
   System (300)
   Reactor (310)
   Inlet (312)
   Compressors/pumps (330)
   Valves (340)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - CHEMICAL ENGINEERING - Preferred Apparatus: The system further comprises inlet(s) (312) for supplying the hydrocarbon feed stream coupled to the reactor. The reactor comprises a riser (r1) configured for oxidative dehydrogenation of the alkane having carbon chain of 5 or more and a riser (r2) configured for oxidative dehydrogenation of the alkane having carbon chain of 4 or less. The system further comprises a membrane comprising an oxidation zone comprising the metal oxide and configured to oxidize the alkanes, a reduction zone configured to reduce an oxygen-containing compound to anionic oxygen, an electron barrier provided between the oxidation zone and the reduction zone and configured to allow transmission of the anionic oxygen from the reduction zone to the oxidation zone and to resist transmission of electrons from the oxidation zone to the reduction zone, and a conductor attached to the oxidation zone and the reduction zone and configured to conduct electrons from the oxidation zone to the reduction zone. The gas hourly space velocity of the reactor is greater than 1000-10000 hour-1, preferably 1200-9000 hour-1.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The metal oxide comprises at least one chosen from oxides of lanthanum, cerium, praseodymium, terbium, neodymium, and dysprosium, preferably praseodymium oxide. The metal oxide further comprises promoter(s) chosen from sodium, lithium, calcium, magnesium, strontium, or barium. The metal oxide is used in combination with a zeolite. The zeolite is of formula: M'2/nO.Al2O3.ySiO2.wH2O (I). The zeolite is ZSM-5.y=2-1000000000, preferably 4-35;M'=positively charged element for balancing zeolite charge and comprises proton, alkaline metal, alkaline earth metal or other element;n=cation valence;andw=number of water molecules per zeolite unit structure.(i) At least 5% of n is due to proton charge.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Condition: The co-cracking process is performed at a temperature of 500-1000 degrees C, preferably 600-750 degrees C. Preferred Components: The alkane having carbon chain of 4 or less comprises ethane and the alkane having carbon chain of 5 or more comprises naphtha. Preferred Composition: The weight ratio of ethane to naphtha is 3:10-9:10, preferably 3:10-4:10. The product comprises at least 65 wt.% olefins. The product comprises at least 50 wt.% of ethylene and/or propylene. Preferred Property: The energy theoretically produced from the exothermic oxidative dehydrogenation of the alkane having carbon chain of 4 or less is 0-10% greater than the energy theoretically required for the endothermic cracking of the alkane having carbon chain of 5 or more. Preferred Process: The alkane having carbon chain of 5 or more and the alkane having carbon chain of 4 or less are concurrently co-cracked within the same reactor.

PN CN107935618-A; CN107935618-B
TI Preparation of ecological decorative mud-artificial sandstone clad plate by preparing modified diatomite powder, preparing mixed slurry with i.e. porous volcanic rock, adding sodium bicarbonate capsule, molding, heating, curing and drying.
AU CHEN Q
   ZENG J
AE CHENGDU NEW KELI CHEM SCI CO LTD (XKLI-C)
   WENLING TENGFENG SHOES CO LTD (WENL-Non-standard)
GA 201832775H
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of ecological decorative mud-artificial sandstone clad plate comprises adding nano sized photocatalyst and diatomite into water, stirring and dispersing, drying, sieving, laser-scanning bed to obtain modified diatomite powder; mixing modified diatomite powder with porous volcanic rock, calcium hydroxide and water to obtain mixed slurry; and mixing with sodium bicarbonate capsule, quickly transferring to mold, heating to react until sodium bicarbonate capsule is melted and ruptured to release carbon dioxide to form micropore and calcium carbonate, curing and drying to obtain plate.
   USE - Method for preparing ecological decorative mud-artificial sandstone clad plate.
   ADVANTAGE - The method prevents diatomaceous earth and photocatalyst from falling off and maintains porous adsorption property of diatomaceous earth and contact surface between photocatalyst and air. The clad plate has excellent respiratory and humidifying function and good killing effect in bacteria and virus exposed in air, increases indoor heat insulating performance, and helps remove organic volatile in air by stabilizing photocatalytic reaction.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparation of ecological decorative mud-artificial sandstone clad plate comprises adding 5-10 pts. wt. nano sized photocatalyst and 20-50 pts. wt. diatomite into water, stirring and dispersing uniformly, drying, sieving using 0.1 mm sieve, laser-scanning bed to fix nano scale photocatalyst in micropore of diatomite to obtain modified diatomite powder; mixing modified diatomite powder with 50-100 pts. wt. porous volcanic rock, 20-30 pts. wt. calcium hydroxide and water in solid-liquid volume ratio of 1:0.5-01 to obtain mixed slurry; and mixing mixed slurry with 5-10 pts. wt. sodium bicarbonate capsule, quickly transferring to mold, heating to react until sodium bicarbonate capsule is melted and ruptured to release carbon dioxide to form micropore and calcium carbonate, curing and drying to obtain rigid clad plate.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The nano-sized photocatalyst is molybdenum disulfide, zinc oxide, cupper sulfide, tungsten trioxide, stannous oxide, zirconium oxide, titanium dioxide, zinc sulfide, ferrous oxide or molybdenum sulfide and has particle size of less than 100 nm. The shell of sodium bicarbonate capsule core is made of microcrystalline wax, stearic acid or paraffin wax. Preferred Method: The laser scanning uses carbon dioxide laser as laser source and is carried out at 20-30 mm/s under 300-400 W. The heating reaction is carried out at 80-100 degrees C for 10-20 minutes.

PN CN107753520-A; CN107753520-B
TI Preparing healthcare capsule used for reducing blood fat, by hydrolyzing squid viscera using papain, obtaining visceral oil, extracting plum, extracting e.g. medlar, mixing extracts with ethanol, standing and mixing with visceral oil.
AU ZHANG X
AE RONGCHENG FEICHUANG TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (RONG-Non-standard)
GA 201820112B
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing healthcare capsule, by (a) hydrolyzing squid viscera using papain, subjecting to vacuum distillation and lyophilization and obtaining visceral oil by carbon dioxide supercritical extraction, (b) crushing plum, subjecting to microwave extraction and concentrating, (c) taking cogongrass rhizome, rhizoma atractylodis, medlar, fructus momordicae and Amomum cardamomum, extracting the mixture and concentrating and (d) mixing extracts obtained in step (b) and (c) and ethanol, standing, recovering supernatant, mixing with visceral oil, and filling into capsule shell.
   USE - The method is used for preparing healthcare capsule for reducing blood fat.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is simple.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Method for preparing healthcare capsule, involves (a) adding fresh water to squid viscera, subjecting to heat treatment, hydrolyzing using papain, inactivating enzymes, subjecting to vacuum distillation and lyophilization, and obtaining visceral oil by carbon dioxide supercritical extraction, (b) taking plum, removing core, crushing, adding 3-4 times water, subjecting to microwave extraction, and concentrating under reduced pressure at 60 degrees C until relative density reaches 1.1-1.2, (c) taking cogongrass rhizome, rhizoma atractylodis, medlar, fructus momordicae and Amomum cardamomum, adding 6-8 times water, extracting the mixture and concentrating the extract under reduced pressure at 60 degrees C until relative density reaches 1.1-1.2, (d) mixing 100 pts. wt. extract obtained in step (b), 100-150 pts. wt. extract obtained in step (c) and 300-400 pts. wt. 65-75% ethanol, stirring uniformly, allowing to stand for 12-18 hours, recovering supernatant, and removing ethanol under reduced pressure, and (e) mixing the liquid obtained in step (d) and visceral oil in a weight ratio of 1:0.15-0.2, and filling into capsule shell.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Method: The step (a) involves cleaning internal organs of squid, cutting into pieces of 7 mm, adding fresh water, heating at 95 degrees C, cooling after stirring for 14 minutes, adding 1440 U/ml papain to 100 pts. wt. cooled mixture, hydrolyzing at 42 degrees C for 18 hours, inactivating enzyme, adding sodium hydroxide, adjusting pH to 12.5, ultrasonically processing for 18 minutes, concentrating the mixture under reduced pressure, freeze-drying, and subjecting to carbon dioxide supercritical extraction. The step (b) involves taking plum, removing core, crushing, adding 3.5 times water, subjecting to microwave extraction, and concentrating under reduced pressure at 60 degrees C until relative density reaches 1.15. The step (c) involves taking cogongrass rhizome, rhizoma atractylodis, medlar, fructus momordicae and A.cardamomum, adding 6.5 times water, extracting the mixture and concentrating the extract under reduced pressure at 60 degrees C until relative density reaches 1.15. The step (d) involves mixing 100 pts. wt. extract obtained in step (b), 120 pts. wt. extract obtained in step (c) and 360 pts. wt. 70% ethanol, stirring uniformly, allowing to stand for 16 hours, recovering supernatant, and removing ethanol under reduced pressure. The step (e) involves mixing the liquid obtained in step (d) and visceral oil in a weight ratio of 1:0.18, and filling into capsule shell.
   ACTIVITY - Antilipemic.

PN CN107722666-A
TI Preparation of Cercis chinensis pigment used e.g. for lipstick, by mixing hydrogen bond acceptor and hydrogen bond donor, stirring and heating, drying Cercis chinensis, crushing, and mixing with uniform transparent low-melting agent-water.
AU LIN Y
AE LIN Y (LINY-Individual)
GA 2018171511
AB    NOVELTY - A cosmetic Cercis chinensis pigment is prepared by mixing hydrogen bond acceptor and hydrogen bond donor, stirring and heating to form uniform transparent low-melting agent, drying Cercis chinensis, crushing, mixing with uniform transparent low-melting agent-water, microwave-ultrasonic filtering, adding salt solution, centrifuging, taking out ethanol reagent, precipitating and recovering, dissolving pigment in ethanol, transferring into supercritical carbon dioxide countercurrent extraction device, extracting, purifying, and vacuum evaporating ethanol.
   USE - Method for preparing Cercis chinensis pigment for cosmetic including lipstick, lip gloss, lip balm, lip cream, nail polish and other cosmetics (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The product has high concentration and does not contain organic solvent. The method is green and environment-friendly, has simple operation, shortens operation time, removes strong polar impurity, and improves pigment purity and natural pigment extraction efficiency.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A cosmetic Cercis chinensis pigment is prepared by mixing hydrogen bond acceptor and hydrogen bond donor, stirring and heating at 80-100 degrees C at speed of 400-600 revolutions/minute (rpm) to form uniform transparent low-melting agent, drying Cercis chinensis into vacuum drying box at 50 degrees C, crushing by traditional Chinese medicine crusher to 60-mesh sieve, crushing to cellular level superfine powder, mixing 3.5 kg superfine powder with uniform transparent low-melting agent-water, microwave-ultrasonic filtering at 35-50 degrees C for 20-40 minutes at 200-400 W ultrasonic power and 100-200 W microwave power to obtain filtrate, adding salt solution, centrifuging for 20 seconds, forming double water phase, taking out ethanol reagent, precipitating and recovering, dissolving pigment in ethanol to obtain primarily purified Cercis chinensis pigment extract solution, transferring into supercritical carbon dioxide countercurrent extraction device, extracting at 15-20 MPa, purifying at 3-6 MPa, and vacuum evaporating ethanol.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The salt solution is ammonium sulfate, sodium sulfate, sodium citrate and dipotassium hydrogen phosphate, and has concentration of 0.25-0.5 g/mL.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The hydrogen bond acceptor is choline chloride, betaine or L-carnitine. The hydrogen bond donor is glucose, glycerol, urea, lactic acid, and/or water. The molar ratio of choline chloride and hydrogen bond donor is 1:2. The volume of low-melting agent in low-melting agent-water mixed solvent is 40-80%. Preferred Method: Heating is done for 1-2 hours. Supercritical carbon dioxide countercurrent extraction has flow speed of 14-18 L/hour.

   SPEISER W
AE LINDE AG (LINM-C)
GA 201812512G
AB    NOVELTY - The press unit has a gas cylinder with a neck and an annular collar (4) to be engaged on the neck of the gas cylinder. The press-fit ribs are distributed about an inner circumference of the annular collar at predetermined angles relative to each other. The press-fit ribs and/or the annular collar are made of copper-zinc alloy. A gas outlet enclosed by a pierceable diaphragm is provided on the neck of the gas cylinder.
   USE - Press unit with compressed gas cylinders for storing gases such as carbon dioxide, oxygen, helium, nitrogen, etc.

The engagement of the gas cylinder and collar is strengthened in a simplified and efficient way and the design production is economical, by the press unit. The stable and accurate alignment of the collar is ensured in terms of the gas cylinder neck, due to strong stability to hold the neck of the gas cylinder centrically. The collar does not move in axial and rotational direction after it is engaged with the neck of the gas cylinder, and supporting a sealing and piercing process without rotation. The O-ring sits in a groove provided on the collar to create a sealing to prevent the compressed gas escaping to the outside.

PN CN114772585-A
TI Scalable preparation method of graphite, involves            making temperature and pressure in ball milling kettle            reach set value, releasing carbon dioxide to normal            pressure in ball milling kettle, separating ball            milling bead by screen, and collecting prepared            graphene.
AU LUO Y
   TAN H
   DU C
   LV S
   ZHAO Y
AE SUZHOU SUNMUN TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (SUZH-Non-standard)
   UNIV SHANGHAI JIAOTONG (USJT-C)
GA 202299073C
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves placing a raw material                graphite ball milling ball into a high pressure                ball milling kettle, closing a kettle cover, fixing                it on a planetary ball milling device, introducing                CO2 into the ball milling kettle, and making the                temperature and pressure in the ball milling kettle                reach the set value, operating the planetary ball                milling device, closing the power supply after the                operation time reaches a predetermined value,                releasing the CO2 to normal pressure in the ball                milling kettle, opening the kettle cover, taking                out the material, separating the ball milling bead                by screen, and collecting the prepared                graphene.
   USE - Scalable preparation method of graphite. Uses                include but are not limited to electronic device,                battery/super capacitor, catalyst carrier and                composite material.
   ADVANTAGE - The method provides good performance, simple                method technique, easy industrial scale preparation                method of graphite. The prepared graphene keeps the                original crystal structure, with excellent                electrical performance and wide application                prospect. The method is green, simple, stripping                efficiency is high, the cost is low, which is more                beneficial for industrial mass production. By                adjusting the supercritical carbon dioxide (CO2)                density, can reduce the collision strength between                the ball, reduces the damage to the graphene                crystal structure. The impact strength of the ball                to the raw material can be reduced, so as to reduce                the damage of the crystal structure of the                graphene. Thus, the method not only improves the                peeling efficiency, but also can obtain                high-quality graphene powder, solves the problem of                graphene powder production amplifying, and is                environment-friendly sustainable green                technology.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a flowchart illustrating                the scalable preparation method of graphite                (Drawing includes non-English language                text).

AU GENG S
   XIA Y
   YANG J
AE NANJING HANHUA FLUID TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (NANJ-Non-standard)
GA 202292417L
AB    NOVELTY - The system divides a coking plant into 18                areas according to the production practice of the                coking plant and the coking production process                function. Each area is composed of different                equipment groups and the production rhythm and the                operation mode are different. A carbon emission                model calculates the materials in each area through                a carbon emission model to obtain the carbon                emission of each area and sums the materials to                obtain the carbon emission of the coke-oven plant.                The carbon emission model feeds back the carbon                emission to the coking plant to guide the                production of the coking plant. The carbon                reduction space of the coke-oven plant is                calculated for the standard carbon dioxide emission                of the standard coke-oven plant on the basis of the                carbon emission of the existing coke-oven                plant.
   USE - Carbon emission and carbon reduction space                accounting system of coke-oven plant.
   ADVANTAGE - The carbon emission conditions of 18 areas of                the coking plant is comprehensively tracked, the                composition and distribution of carbon emission of                the coking plant are solved, the problem of black                boxes of carbon emission of the coking plant is                solved. The timely and reliable coke-oven plant                carbon emission data are provided for relevant                government departments to carry out industrial                carbon management and build a carbon emission                trading market. The data and process auditing of a                third-party checking organization are supported and                basic support is provided for developing carbon                emission big data and carbon neutralization                work.

PN CN114394778-A
TI Hot disintegrating of steel slag useful for road            engineering, involves pouring steel slag, crushing,            opening air inlet valve to introduce lime kiln tail gas            containing carbon dioxide, supplementing slag period,            closing air inlet valve and finishing hot            disintegration stage.
AU HUANG Z
   ZOU C
   JIN H
   MAO R
   MA J
   LIU F
AE JIANGSU SHA-STEEL RES IRON & STEEL INST (JIAN-Non-standard)
   ZHANGJIAGANG HONGCHANG STEEL PLATE CO (JSSG-C)
   JIANGSU SHAGANG GROUP CO LTD (JSSG-C)
GA 2022692299
AB    NOVELTY - Hot disintegrating of steel slag comprises (1)                pouring the steel slag in the slag tank into Heat                sealing tank, crushing, covering the cover of the                hot closed tank and (2) starting from the first                water, until the temperature in the hot closed tank                is reduced to 600degrees Celsius during the period                of covering slag, closing the air inlet valve,                reducing the self temperature to 600degrees                Celsius, until the temperature in the hot closed                tank is reduced to 100degrees Celsius during the                slag closing period, opening the air inlet valve to                introduce the lime kiln tail gas containing carbon                dioxide to the hot closed tank, adjusting the                respective opening of the air inlet valve and the                exhaust valve in the whole air flow control period,                supplementing the slag period, reducing the                self-temperature to 100degrees Celsius, until the                slag is maintained for at least 30 minutes, closing                the air inlet valve and finishing the hot                disintegration stage.
   USE - The method is useful in hot disintegrating                device for road engineering.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: enables converting the free                calcium oxide in the steel slag into stable calcium                carbonate, so that the rest content is very low;                has low device cost, high safety coefficient and                high comprehensive economic value; reduces the cost                of the hot disintegrating device and safety the                production coefficient; reduces the risk of safety,                and provides high productivity and high production                safety.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included                for:

TI Producing precipitated white carbon black and light calcium carbonate by using yellow phosphorus waste side, comprises producing yellow phosphorus tail gas, burning yellow phosphorus tail gas, carrying out wet alkali dissolution, and recovering liquid alkali for dissolving active silicon slag.
AU ZHOU H
   WANG J
AE SICHUAN MABIAN LONGTAI PHOSPHORUS ELECTR (SICH-Non-standard)
GA 202259923J
AB    NOVELTY - Producing precipitated white carbon black and light calcium carbonate by using yellow phosphorus waste side comprises producing yellow phosphorus tail gas and phosphorus slag, burning the yellow phosphorus tail gas, and obtaining the waste flue gas rich in carbon dioxide after heat energy is recovered and utilized, and recycling the phosphoric slag acid to obtain active silicon slag and calcium oxide, carrying out wet alkali dissolution, adding liquid alkali into the active silicon slag to dissolve to obtain sodium silicate solution, carbonizing sodium silicate solution with carbon dioxide-rich waste flue gas, carrying out solid-liquid separation to obtain sodium bicarbonate solution and precipitated silica product, carrying out causticization reaction for the sodium bicarbonate solution and the calcium oxide, carrying out solid-liquid separation to obtain liquid caustic soda and light calcium carbonate products and recovering the liquid alkali for dissolving the active silicon slag.
   USE - The method is useful for producing precipitated white carbon black and light calcium carbonate by using yellow phosphorus waste side.
   ADVANTAGE - The process only uses yellow phosphorus waste phosphorusslag, tail gas, using acid, alkali circulation process to change waste into valuable, realizing low cost production of precipitated white carbon black and, light calcium carbonate the comprehensive utilization of silicon, calcium and carbon product, realizing energy-saving and emission reduction target, solving the defect of high discharge of yellow phosphorus production.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a process flow diagram illustrating the method for producing precipitated white carbon black and light calcium carbonate by using yellow phosphorus waste side (Drawing includes non-English language text).

TI Method for calculating design of boiler i.e. supercritical carbon dioxide circulating coal-fired generating boiler, used in e.g. thermal power generation, involves obtaining final optimization design result and economic environment benefit index evaluation result.
AU LIU X
   SHAO Y
   ZHONG W
   LIU Z
AE UNIV SOUTHEAST (UYSE-C)
GA 202210664Q
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves selecting a boiler design parameter. Boiler combustion heat balance is established. A fuel quantity and total air quantity are determined. A hearth structure and size are predicted. Boiler design simulation process is performed until a required heating surface of a stage is arranged at a corresponding position of a boiler. Temperature of a flue gas side and a working substance side are combined according to specific heat exchange. Boiler economic environment benefit index is evaluated for calculating thermal efficiency of the boiler. A final optimization design result and an economic environment benefit index evaluation result are obtained according to an optimal principle.
   USE - Method for calculating design of a boiler i.e. supercritical carbon dioxide circulating coal-fired generating boiler, used in thermal power generation and industrial production.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables simulating to obtain specific distribution of combustion heat or combustion part of area of a hearth through detailed modularized combustion process, when designing, so that artificial assumption can be carried out without depending on empirical value to effectively solve the problem that when the physical property and heat absorption condition of a boiler structure, thus designing a new boiler. The method enables quickly obtaining the flue gas side and working substance side detailed along the parameter, such as component, temperature, flow rate, density, enthalpy value, gas phase ratio of the accurate value of each position and physical property of the working substances under the condition, thus improving heat transfer, efficiency and pollutant concentration.

The Ganoderma mycelium biopolymer is                  hydrated with glycerin or polyethylene glycol and                  where the Ganoderma mycelium biopolymer is                  configured to be in contact with a fabric.Preferred Process: The process further                  involves placing mats in the vacant space of the                  scaffold and growing the mycological biopolymer                  about the mats to incorporate the mats into the                  mycological biopolymer and to increase tensile                  strength in the mycological biopolymer.                  Incubation at high temperatures and carbon                  dioxide concentration occurs in the presence of a                  compressive pressure of at least 10 pounds pe                  square inch (PSI) to the mycological biopolymer                  where the consequent compressed shape has a                  weight of which at most 15% of the weight is made                  up of water. The process also involves applying                  at least one morphological modifier onto the                  surface of the mycological biopolymer to alter                  the morphology of the mycelia. The process                  further involves compressing the mycological                  biopolymer to predetermined dimensions after the                  step of incubation and incubating the compressed                  mycological biopolymer for an additional 0-72                  hours to increase strength and density prior to                  the step of drying. It also involves shaping the                  dried mycological biopolymer to a predetermined                  shape adapted to fit a user's face in a                  personalized fashion. It further involves                  sandwiching the mycological biopolymer between a                  pair of laminates after the step of incubation                  and incubating the mycological biopolymer for an                  additional 12-72 hours to adhere the mycological                  biopolymer between and to the pair of laminates                  prior to the step of drying.

PN CN113149855-A; CN113149855-B
TI Synthesizing 11-alfa-chloromethene oxytetracycline            p-toluenesulfonate used in veterinary medicine,            involves adding chlorinated oxytetracycline to            autoclave, adding dehydrating agent anhydrous            p-toluenesulfonic acid, and dehydrating.
AU LIU C
   WANG W
   WANG J
   ZHANG J
   GUO X
AE SHANDONG GUOBANG PHARM CO LTD (SHAN-Non-standard)
   GUOBANG PHARMA LTD (GUOB-Non-standard)
GA 202191544A
AB    NOVELTY - Synthesizing 11 alpha -chloromethene                oxytetracycline p-toluenesulfonate involves adding                chlorinated oxytetracycline (I) to autoclave, then                adding dehydrating agent anhydrous                p-toluenesulfonic acid (II), passing in                supercritical carbon dioxide (SCCO2) as reaction                solvent, adjusting reaction temperature and                pressure, keeping warm and stirring, performing                dehydration reaction to obtain 11 alpha                -chloromethene oxytetracycline (III); providing                heat preservation followed by releasing pressure                and lowering temperature, adding absolute ethanol                to 11 alpha -chloromethene oxytetracycline (III) to                cause salt formation reaction, then temperature is                lowered and filtered, and then rinsed with cold                ethanol; drying under reduced pressure and low                temperature in vacuum oven to obtain 11 alpha                -chloromethene oxytetracycline p-toluenesulfonate                (IV).
   USE - Method for synthesizing 11 alpha                -chloromethene oxytetracycline p-toluenesulfonate                used in veterinary medicine.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to solves unreasonable                existing preparation method, which has high                toxicity of raw materials, complicated operation,                high cost, large yield of three wastes, and high                yield which is unsuitable for technical problems of                industrial production.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Synthesizing 11 alpha -chloromethene                oxytetracycline p-toluenesulfonate involves adding                chlorinated oxytetracycline of formula (I) to                autoclave, then adding dehydrating agent anhydrous                p-toluenesulfonic acid of formula (II), passing in                supercritical carbon dioxide (SCCO2) as reaction                solvent, adjusting reaction temperature and                pressure, keeping warm and stirring, performing                dehydration reaction to obtain 11 alpha                -chloromethene oxytetracycline of formula (III);                providing heat preservation followed by releasing                pressure and lowering temperature, adding absolute                ethanol to 11 alpha -chloromethene oxytetracycline                of formula (III) to cause salt formation reaction,                then temperature is lowered and filtered, and then                rinsed with cold ethanol; drying under reduced                pressure and low temperature in vacuum oven to                obtain 11 alpha -chloromethene oxytetracycline                p-toluenesulfonate of formula (IV)..
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The supercritical                  carbon dioxide (SCCO2) is carbon dioxide fluid at                  31.06 degrees C and above 7.314 MegaPascal. The                  molar ratio of chlorinated oxytetracycline of                  formula (I) to anhydrous p-toluenesulfonic acid                  of formula (II) is 1:(1.7-2.0), and the molar                  ratio of chlorinated oxytetracycline of formula                  (I) to carbon dioxide is 1:(35-40). The                  dehydration reaction temperature is 31-33 degrees                  C, the dehydration reaction pressure is 7.3-7.4                  MPa, and dehydration reaction time is 5-8 hours.                  The salt-forming reaction temperature is 20-25                  degrees C, and the salt-forming reaction time is                  8-10 hours. The filtration temperature of cooling                  filtration is - 5-0 degrees C.

AU SCHLUMMER C
AE PROMIX SOLUTIONS AG (PROM-Non-standard)
   PROMIX SOLUTIONS AG (PROM-Non-standard)
   PROMIX SOLUTIONS AG (PROM-Non-standard)
GA 202179703Q
AB    NOVELTY - The system (1) has an inlet (10) for the liquid or gaseous medium. The inlet is set in fluid-conducting connection with a closed channel (11). A pressure control valve (4) and a flow sensor (3) are arranged in the inlet. The pressure control valve and the flow sensor form a closed control circuit (6). The measured flow value is determined by means of the flow sensor. The measured flow value is fed into a controller (7) as an input variable. The input variable is assigned an output variable by the controller. The output variable corresponds to a desired metering pressure (p2). The desired metering pressure is set using the pressure control valve. The dosing quantity is determined by the pressure difference between the metering pressure and the process pressure (p3).
   USE - System for metering liquid or gaseous medium e.g. low-viscosity fluids or gases, into fluid stream flowing through closed channel, used for regulating the metered quantity of carbon dioxide (claimed). Uses include but are not limited to butane, pentane, ethanol, methanol, nitrogen, argon.
   ADVANTAGE - The system covers a large metering spectrum and is constant and reliable regardless of the pressure difference between the inlet pressure and the metering pressure via the valve.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for metering liquid or gaseous medium into fluid stream flowing through closed channel.

The disinfectant is prepared using edible alcohol, alive centipede, alive pallas pit viper, alive black snake, royal jelly, notoginseng, honeysuckle, bamboo leaf, cactus and other required things in mixer. The composition uses alcohol to extract active matter in toxic wild animals to make medicine liquid, where the sweetening agent is chosen from 1x 10-5 g to 2x 10-1 mu g/l artificial sweetening agent and 1.46x 10-1 M natural sweetening agent.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Composition: The composition retains at least 90% of azadirachtins present after four weeks of storage at 54 degrees C in a sealed container, the saccharide is a monosaccharide, disaccharide, polysaccharide or their mixtures. The herbal ingredients are chosen from 5x 10-1 ml/l essential oils, 20 weight/weight% fresh herbs, 10 weight/weight% solar dried herbs and 6 volume/volume% herbal extracts. TI System for preparing high calorific value syngas, has output pipe that is connected to fourth joint of reversing valve for outputting high-temperature carbon dioxide-rich flue gas or high-calorific value syngas to outside.
AU CAI J
   ZHENG W
   HAN J
AE UNIV GUILIN ELECTRONIC TECHNOLOGY (UYGE-C)
GA 2020C2223R
AB    NOVELTY - The system has a reactor (1), a combustion device, a gasification device, a reversing valve (2), an intake pipe (3) and an output pipe (4). A burner (5) is arranged on the top of the reactor, and a calcium-based adsorbent bed (6) is arranged in the middle. The gasification device inputs the obtained mixture of hot water vapor and hot air or hot oxygen into the reactor. The intake pipe is connected to the third joint of the reversing valve, and is provided with a first valve and a first induced draft fan (8) to provide cold air or oxygen to the combustion device or the gasification device through the intake pipe. The output pipe is connected to the fourth joint of the reversing valve for outputting high-temperature carbon dioxide-rich flue gas or high-calorific value syngas to the outside. The state of the reversing valve is switched to connect the first connector to the second connector, or to connect the third connector to the fourth connector.
   USE - System for preparing high calorific value syngas by gasification of calcium-based chemical chain of carbon-containing fuels.
   ADVANTAGE - The attrition loss of the calcium-based adsorbent and the extremely difficult control of the fluidization state of the system can be efficiently reduced.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for preparing high calorific value synthesis gas by gasification of calcium-based chemical chains for carbon-containing fuels.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the system structure for preparing high calorific value synthesis gas.
   Reactor (1)
   Reversing valve (2)
   Intake pipe (3)
   Output pipe (4)
   Burner (5)
   Calcium-based adsorbent bed (6)
   First induced draft fan (8)

PN US2020352106-A1
TI Reducing bud temperature increase during dormancy            of deciduous plant comprises treating composition            comprising nanoparticles of titanium dioxide,            nanoparticles of calcium carbonate, at least one            surfactant, and water to provide flowability.
AU BAKER G
   WEBB S S
   FAIRCHILD C
   BAKER T A
AE CO2 SOLVED LLC (COTW-Non-standard)
GA 2020B0769N
AB    NOVELTY - Reducing bud temperature increase during                dormancy of a deciduous plant, comprises treating a                composition comprising nanoparticles of titanium                dioxide, nanoparticles of calcium carbonate, at                least one surfactant, and water in an amount                adequate to provide flowability.
   USE - The method is useful for: reducing bud                temperature increase during dormancy of a deciduous                plant, where the plant comprises almond, apricot,                cherry, grape, pear, peach, nectarine, pistachio,                plum, walnut, apple, blackberry, blueberry,                chestnut, currant, fig, filbert, gooseberry, kiwi,                mulberry, persimmon, plum cot, pomegranate, quince,                strawberry, or raspberry; increasing winter chill                accumulation in a deciduous plant (all claimed);                reducing foliar chloroplast movement in plants and                trees to enhance carbon dioxide trapping (carbon                capture) from the air while naturally increasing                the oxygen in the atmosphere and diluting the                concentration of greenhouse gasses; and measuring                foliar chloroplast avoidance in response to                treatments designed to reduce avoidance.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: enhances the biological effect of                photosynthesis; enhances plant growth, increases                carbon capture and sequestration, corresponding                increased release of oxygen, and reflection of                solar radiation of wave lengths less than 700 nm;                is more economical than existing carbon capture                methods because they do not require advanced                technology for ambient air carbon dioxide capture,                geo-storage, specialized regulations, special                infrastructure or transportation needs typically                associated with more traditional carbon                sequestration and capture methods; increases                biomass accumulation, increases yield, improves                water use efficiency and increases oxygen in the                air, which dilutes the concentration of greenhouse                gases; and avoids thermal damage, avoids photo                bleaching, DNA damage, and other potential damage                from high light intensity.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for                increasing winter chill accumulation in a deciduous                plant, comprising treating with a composition                comprising nanoparticles of titanium dioxide,                nanoparticles of calcium carbonate, at least one                surfactant, and water in an amount adequate to                provide flowability.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - AGRICULTURE - Preferred Method: The treatment is                  formulated with wet ground calcium carbonate and                  comprises components comprising 8.85-20.85 wt.%                  potable water, 0.5-1 wt.% Esperse 366 (RTM:                  Dispersant), 0.5-5 wt.% titanium dioxide, 78-84                  wt.% CCCWG at 74% calcium carbonate, 0.1-0.5 wt.%                  Silicone PMX200 (RTM: High-viscosity                  polydimethyl-siloxane polymer), 0-0.25 wt.%                  Mergal K10N (RTM: Non-skin sensitizing                  benzisothiazolinone formulation), 0-0.25 wt.%                  Mergal 186 (RTM: Water-soluble organic                  bactericide), and 0.05-0.15 wt.% Guar gum. The                  treatment covers at least 40%, at least 50%, or                  at least 60% of the plant boot, stem, branch and                  bud area. The treatment is applied at least once                  before endodormancy. The treatment is applied: at                  least once after endodormancy and before bud                  break; and two, three, or four times after                  endodormancy and before bud break.
   ADMINISTRATION - Administration of the composition is by                spraying. No dosage details given.

GA 2020A98724
AB    NOVELTY - The cabinet has a cabinet body (1)                symmetrically provided with multiple radiating                holes (2) and a sliding cavity (3). An inner wall                of the sliding cavity is connected with a sliding                plate (4). A side wall of the sliding plate is                provided with multiple through holes (5) matched                with the radiating holes. An inner side of the                sliding cavity is fixedly connected with a                permanent magnet block. A lower end of the                permanent magnetic block is fixedly connected with                an upper end of a magnet through a spring. A liquid                storage cavity (9) is filled with liquid                hydrochloric acid. The cabinet body is provided                with a gas supply mechanism for providing carbon                dioxide to the cabinet body.
   USE - High temperature demagnetization principle                based fire extinguishing power distribution                cabinet.
   ADVANTAGE - The cabinet eeaches a Curie point of the                permanent magnet block through the temperature in                the cabinet body so that the permanent magnet block                is degaussed by high temperature, drives the                sliding plate by the magnet to slide down under                action of the spring, staggers through hole and the                radiating hole to lock outside air into the cabinet                body through the radiating hole, performs oxygen                separation treatment, generates carbon dioxide by                hydrochloric acid solution and calcium carbonate                particles, increases concentration of carbon                di-oxide in the cabinet body, accelerates                extinguishing of fire in the cabinet body.

PT P
PN US2020318544-A1; US11365679-B2
TI Method for heating a recirculating gas stream,            such as the carbon dioxide obtained from combustion of            a fuel for generation of power, such as electricity,            involves separating the gas stream into a first            fraction and a second fraction.
AU ALLAM R J
   FORREST B A
   FETVEDT J E
AE 8 RIVERS CAPITAL LLC (ERIV-C)
GA 202098169M
AB    NOVELTY - The heating method involves passing a gas                stream at a first pressure (P1) and a first                temperature (T1) through a recuperative heat                exchanger such that the gas stream is cooled to a                second temperature (T2) that is less than T1. The                gas stream is separated into a first fraction and a                second fraction, and compressed the first gas                stream fraction (G1) to a pressure P2 that is                greater than P1. The second gas stream fraction                (G2) is compressed to a third pressure (P3) that is                greater than P1 so as to heat the G2 to a                temperature T3 that is greater than T2. The heat is                withdrawn from the second compressed gas stream                fraction, and combined the first gas stream                fraction and the second gas stream fraction to form                a combined recirculating gas stream. The                recirculating gas stream is pumped to a fourth                pressure (P4) that is greater than P2 and greater                than P3.
   USE - Method for heating a recirculating gas stream,                such as the carbon dioxide obtained from combustion                of a fuel for generation of power, such as                electricity.
   ADVANTAGE - The method introduces the heat into a recycle                high pressure stream at a temperature of about 100                to 400 degree celsius, and thus increases the                effectiveness of an economizer heat exchanger and                the overall efficiency of the power production                system, and provides cycles with high pressure                ratios and high turbine inlet pressures resulting                in high efficiency and low capital cost.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the                following:
   (1) a method for generating power; and
   (2) a power generating system.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a block diagram of a power                production system.
   Intercooler (23)
   Heat exchanger (24,29)
   Compressor (28)
   Phase separator vessel (53)
   Cryogenic air separation plant (81)
