PT P
PN KR2020113682-A; KR2234616-B1
TI Feedback device for use in a feedback system for controlling a bag valve mask, comprises a first sensor unit, which is provided for measuring a concentration of carbon dioxide or oxygen contained in a patient exhalation.
AU KIM J C
   CHAWONCHUL
   KIMTAE-LIM
AE SAMSUNG LIFE PUBLIC WELFARE FOUND MED CE (SMSU-C)
GA 202098918G
AB    NOVELTY - The feedback device (100) comprises a first sensor unit (10), which is provided for measuring a concentration of carbon dioxide or oxygen contained in a patient exhalation. A second sensor unit (20) is provided for measuring the pressure and flow rate of air supplied to the patient. A ventilation unit (30) is provided for automatically adjusting the pressure and flow rate of the air supplied to the patient through the discharge of the air supplied to the patient. A controller is configured to control an operation of the ventilation unit based on information measured by the first sensor unit and the second sensor unit. An output unit (50) is provided for providing monitoring information, which is generated by the control unit (40) to a user.
   USE - Feedback device for use in a feedback system (Claimed) for controlling a bag valve mask.
   ADVANTAGE - The feedback device can prevent damage to the patient lungs due to pressure, and significantly speed up recovery for patients, who use a bag valve mask. It can be used in any environment that requires transportation of a patient in accordance with an emergency situation, and can be easily cleaned through a simple combination or separation structure, which prevents hygiene problems due to continuous use of the bag valve mask.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a feedback system for controlling a bag valve mask.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a block diagram of a feedback device. (Drawing includes non-English language text).
   First sensor unit (10)
   Second sensor unit (20)
   Ventilation unit (30)
   Control unit (40)
   Output unit (50)
   Feedback device (100)
DC P31 (Diagnosis, surgery (A61B).); P34 (Sterilising, syringes, electrotherapy (A61L, M, N).); S05 (Electrical Medical Equipment)

PT P
PN JP6755058-B1; JP2021070006-A
TI Organic-waste processing system has methane fermentation apparatus that produces biogas by methane producer from organic waste rendered low molecular weight, and gas-purification apparatus removes carbon dioxide from biogas.
AU MITSUYAMA M
AE SUSTAINABLE ENERGY INC (SUST-Non-standard)
GA 2020907648
AB    NOVELTY - The system has a subcritical water treatment apparatus (2) that renders organic waste low molecular weight. The methane fermentation apparatus (3) produces biogas by a methane producer from the organic waste rendered low molecular weight. The gas-purification apparatus (5) removes carbon dioxide from biogas produced. The solid-liquid separator (7) having pressurization floatation of hazardous substance is carried out from digestive liquid produced. The subcritical water treatment apparatus, the methane fermentation apparatus, the gas-purification apparatuses, and solid-liquids separator is a movable unit. The movable unit is conveyed to place which implements organic-waste process, and assembled and installed, and disassembled and carried after implementation. The residual plastics are collected, when plastics residual is recognized in solid content. The residue gasifier is provided and dry distillation gas is produced. The residue gasifier is utilized as fuel by high pressure boiler.
   USE - Organic-waste processing system.
   ADVANTAGE - The waste material can be processed without building the garbage dump for which the organic waste is processed and require expensive construction. The organic waste can be processed without producing most waste materials by processing a fermentation residue and a digestive liquid and produces biogas by high efficiency from organic waste to make the energy source of an electric power generation. The energy and valuables can be created without exerting a bad influence on global environment by making full use of biotechnology according to the organic-waste processing system. The small scale and perfect self-conclusion energy recycling society can be formed.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows the block diagram of the organic-waste processing system. (Drawing includes non-English language text)
   Organic waste (1)
   Subcritical water treatment apparatus (2)
   Methane fermentation apparatus (3)
   Gas-purification apparatus (5)
   Solid and liquid separator (7)

Combustion improvement additives for coal, peat and other nonhydrocarbon based fuels are included in this Section together with coal liquefaction and desulphurisation.); A97 (Miscellaneous goods not specified elsewhere - including papermaking, gramophone records, detergents, food and oil well applications.); P41 (Crushing: centrifuging, separating solids (B02, B03, B04).)

PT P
PN CN111620406-A; CN111620406-B
TI Use of copper monosulfide (CuS)/carbon dioxide            (Co2)P composite photocatalyst in organic wastewater            treatment, under visible light irradiation, where            CuS/Co2P composite photocatalyst used to photocatalyze            degradation of organic pollutants.
AU HE Z
AE UNIV HUNAN FIRST NORMAL (UYHU-Non-standard)
GA 202087740K
AB    NOVELTY - Use of copper monosulfide (CuS)/carbon dioxide                (Co2)P composite photocatalyst in organic                wastewater treatment, under visible light                irradiation, where the CuS/Co2P composite                photocatalyst is used to photocatalyze the                degradation of organic pollutants in organic                wastewater, the removal rate of organic pollutants                in wastewater can reach 99%, and can reach more                than 90% after repeated 5 times, where the organic                pollutant is rhodamine B or methyl orange is                claimed.
   USE - The CuS/Co2P composite photocatalyst is used                in organic wastewater treatment, which is used for                photocatalyzing the degradation of organic                pollutants in organic wastewater (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The Co2P composite photocatalyst material                improves photocatalytic performance, and has                excellent degradation ability to organic pollutants                in wastewater.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method                for preparing the CuS/Co2P composite photocatalyst,                comprising (a) taking the cobalt source compound,                yellow phosphorus and surfactants, dissolving in a                certain amount of deionized water in sequence, and                stirring to mix evenly, (b) transferring the                solution into a PTFE reactor, reacting at 180-240                degrees C for 4-8 hours, naturally cooling to room                temperature, washing and drying the resulting                product to obtain the final product Co2P, (c)                dissolving the zinc source compound and the sulfur                source compound in a mixed solution of                ethylenediamine and water, adding the Co2P to the                above mixed solution, and hydrothermally reacting                at 150-170 degrees C for 10-15 hours to obtain                ZnS/Co2P composite material, (d) uniformly mixing                after the ZnS/Co2P composite material obtained in                step (c) with the copper nitrate solution, carting                out the reaction at 85-120 degrees C for 3-6 hours,                filtering, washing, and drying to obtain a CuS/Co2P                composite photocatalyst.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: In the step (b), the                  detergent is ethanol and/or deionized water, the                  number of washing is 2-3 times, and the drying                  process is carried out at 50-70 degrees C for                  10-14 hours. In the step (d), the washing is                  centrifugal washing with dilute hydrochloric acid                  and deionized water, and the drying process is                  carried out at 50-70 degrees C for 10-20 hours.                  Preferred Components: The cobalt source compound                  is chosen from one or more of cobalt chloride,                  cobalt sulfate, cobalt nitrate, and cobalt                  acetate. The surfactant is chosen from one or                  more of polyvinylpyrrolidone, sodium                  dodecylbenzenesulfonate, polyethylene glycol-400,                  and cetyltrimethylammonium bromide. The Co2P is                  in the shape of nanorods, with a length of 10-30                  mu m, directly 60-240 nm, and copper monosulfide                  with a nano-sheet structure is on the surface of                  the Co2P nanorods. Preferred Composition: The                  molar ratio of the cobalt source compound and the                  yellow phosphorus is 1:(2-5), and the ratio of                  the added amount of the cobalt source compound to                  the surfactant is 1 mmol:(0.05-0.2) g. In the                  step (c), the volume ratio of the ethylenediamine                  and water is (13-16):1. The zinc source compound                  is chosen from one or more of zinc acetate, zinc                  chloride, and zinc nitrate, the sulfur source                  compound is thiourea, and the molar ratio of the                  zinc source compound to the sulfur source                  compound is 1:2. In step (d), the molar ratio of                  the zinc sulfide to the copper nitrate is                  1:(1-2). The molar ratio of the copper                  monosulfide to the Co2P is 1:(0.5-2).
DC D15 (Chemical or biological treatment of water, industrial waste and sewage - including purification, sterilising or testing water, scale prevention, treatment of sewage sludge, regeneration of active carbon which has been used for water treatment and impregnating water with gas e.g. CO2, but excluding plant and anti-pollution devices (C02).); E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); E32 (Compounds of Ti, Zr, Hf, Cu, Ag, Au, Zn, Cd, Hg, Ga, In, Te, Ge, Sn, Pb, As, Sb, Bi.); J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).); A97 (Miscellaneous goods not specified elsewhere - including papermaking, gramophone records, detergents, food and oil well applications.)
MC D04-A01F1; D04-A01P1; D04-A01P2A; D04-B06; D04-B06B; E11-P; E11-Q02B; E21-B05; E25-E02; E31-K04; E35-A; J04-E04C; J04-E11; A04-E08; A12-W11; A12-W11F; A12-W11J; A12-W11K
IP B01J-027/185; B01J-037/10; C02F-001/30; C02F-101/30

PT P
PN CN111617791-A; CN111617791-B
TI Z-type heterostructure tungsten nitride-tungsten            trioxide composite material comprises tungsten trioxide            layer obtained by partial oxidation of surface and            inner tungsten nitride nanorod.
AU ZHANG B
   SUN M
   LIU D
   ZHAO B
AE UNIV TIANJIN (UTIJ-C)
   UNIV TIANJIN (UTIJ-C)
GA 2020878061
AB    NOVELTY - Z-type heterostructure tungsten                nitride-tungsten trioxide composite material                comprises a tungsten trioxide layer obtained by                partial oxidation of the surface and an inner                tungsten nitride nanorod, where a Z-type                heterostructure is formed between the tungsten                trioxide on the surface and the tungsten nitride                inside. The process preferably involves placing the                tungsten nitride nanorods in an air atmosphere,                heating up to 300-350 degrees C at a temperature                increase rate of 1-5 degrees C/minute, maintaining                the temperature for 1-2 hours, and cooling to room                temperature to obtain partially oxidized tungsten                nitride nanorods which is a Z-type heterostructure                tungsten nitride-tungsten trioxide composite                material composed of a tungsten trioxide layer                obtained by partial oxidation of the surface and an                inner tungsten nitride nanorod.
   USE - The Z-type heterostructure tungsten                nitride-tungsten trioxide composite material is                used in photothermal catalytic carbon dioxide                hydrogenation (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The Z-type heterostructure tungsten                nitride-tungsten trioxide composite material adopts                simple and universal preparation method, which                enables tungsten nitride to break the limitation of                easy recombination of self-photogenerated electrons                and holes, and realizes better utilization of the                UV-visible part of sunlight.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the                following:
   (1) a method for preparing the Z-type                heterostructure tungsten nitride-tungsten trioxide                composite material, which involves placing the                tungsten nitride nanorods in an air atmosphere,                heating up to 300-350 degrees C at a temperature                increase rate of 1-5 degrees C/minute, maintaining                the temperature for 1-2 hours, and cooling to room                temperature (20-25 degrees C) to obtain partial                surface oxidized tungsten nitride nanorods;                and
   (2) the use of the Z-type heterostructure                tungsten nitride-tungsten trioxide composite                material in photothermal catalytic hydrogenation of                carbon dioxide.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The temperature is                  maintained at 300-320 degrees C for 1-1.5 hours,                  the quartz tube is chosen as the reaction                  equipment, and air is introduced as the reaction                  atmosphere. Preferred Process: The tungsten                  nitride nanorods are prepared by (a) weighing                  tungsten trioxide, adding ethylenediamine, and                  stirring evenly, where the mass ratio of the                  tungsten trioxide (g) to the volume (ml) of                  ethylenediamine is 1:(28-35), and the stirring                  time is 0.5 -1 hours, (b) transferring the mixed                  solution obtained in step (a) to a stainless                  steel autoclave lined with PTFE and reacting at                  150-200 degrees C for 6-12 hours, and                  centrifuging to collect the white tungsten                  trioxide-ethylenediamine precursor, (c) placing                  the white tungsten trioxide-ethylenediamine                  precursor prepared in step (b) in a quartz tube                  with a diameter of 6 mm and calcining at 700-750                  degrees C for 10-15 hours at a heating rate of                  1-5 degrees C/minute to obtain tungsten trioxide                  nanorods, and (d) placing the tungsten trioxide                  nanorods prepared in step (c) in a quartz tube                  with a diameter of 6 mm, reacting in an ammonia                  atmosphere at 700-750 degrees C for 2-5 hours,                  and controlling the ammonia flow rate at 80-100                  ml/minute to obtain tungsten nitride nanorods.                  The application process involves dispersing the                  Z-type heterostructure tungsten nitride-tungsten                  trioxide composite material in a solution of                  potassium chloropalladate, forming a suspension                  and fully stirring and illuminating under an                  inert protective gas atmosphere, separating the                  precipitate by centrifugation, washing several                  times with deionized water and ethanol, and                  drying in a vacuum drying oven to obtain                  palladium-supported tungsten nitride-tungsten                  trioxide nanorods, weighing 25-50 mg Z-type                  heterostructure tungsten nitride-tungsten                  trioxide composite material loaded with palladium                  and dispersing in the reaction cell of the                  photothermal reactor for photothermal catalytic                  carbon dioxide hydrogenation, where the methane                  production rate can reach 40.6-42.3 mu mol hour-1                  g-1. In the potassium chloropalladate solution,                  the molar concentration of potassium                  chloropalladate is 0.14-0.15 mu mol/l, the                  solvent is water and methanol, and the volume                  percentage of methanol is 6-8%, the mass ratio of                  Z-type heterostructure tungsten nitride-tungsten                  trioxide composite material and potassium                  chloropalladate is 300:(6-10), preferably                  300:(7-9), the inert protective gas is argon,                  nitrogen or helium, the stirring speed is 100-500                  rpm, preferably 300-500 rpm, illumination adopts                  fluorescent lamp or xenon lamp to simulate                  sunlight, and the simulated light intensity is                  0.4-0.5 W/cm-2, the time for stirring and                  illuminating (the time during which both are                  performed simultaneously) is 1-2 hours,                  preferably 1-1.5 hours. Preferred Conditions:                  When preparing tungsten nitride nanorods, in step                  (a), the ratio of the mass (g) of tungsten                  trioxide to the volume (ml) of ethylenediamine is                  1:(30-32), in step (b), the reaction temperature                  is 160-180 degrees C, and the reaction time is                  8-10 hours, in step (c), the air atmosphere is                  selected, the high-temperature calcination                  temperature is 700-720 degrees C, and the time is                  3-5 hours, in step (d), an ammonia atmosphere is                  selected, the high-temperature reaction                  temperature is 720-750 degrees C, and the time is                  3-5 hours. Preferred Properties: The length of                  the composite material is 1-5 mu m, preferably                  1-3 mu m and the width is 100-150 nm, preferably                  100-120 nm.
DC J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).); L03 (Electro-(in)organic - chemical features of conductors, resistors, magnets, capacitors and switches, electric discharge lamps, semiconductor and other materials, batteries, accumulators and thermoelectric devices, including fuel cells, magnetic recording media, radiation emission devices, liquid crystals and basic electric elements. Growing of single crystals of semiconductors and their doping are included, but semiconductor devices, where the manufacture is not claimed are excluded. PT P
PN GB2579536-A; GB2579536-B
TI System for generating electricity by oxygen fuel combustion in boilers, has four main component sections that run consequentially in series, and are connected to make continuously operating system with modes of operation.
AU JACKSON J
AE JACKSON J (JACK-Individual)
GA 202057936L
AB    NOVELTY - The system has four main component sections (A,B,C,D) that run consequentially in series and are connected to make a continuously operating system with modes of operation that are useful and not found on current electrical power generation systems. The component A is a primary combustion where any solid, liquid or gaseous fuel, and or combination of such fuels is fed into a combustion chamber to be combusted with oxygen to produce heat, electrical energy and post combustion exhaust products. The component A is a combustion chamber that is a boiler to raise to steam at pressure, so that the pressured steam causing a steam turbine to rotate and the steam turbines rotational output rotates an electrical generator to generate electrical power.
   USE - System for generating electricity by oxygen fuel combustion in boilers to raise steam for steam turbines or gas turbines or reciprocating engines used in power station.
   ADVANTAGE - The heat recovery section receives the cumulative combustion flue flow containing mostly carbon dioxide and water vapor, the water vapor providing the property of waters specific heat capacity, thus enabling considerable heat to be given up in, heat recovery, and potentially giving an overall efficiency in terms of electrical output, not available in current single furnace/boiler/gas turbine designs, as stack losses can be made better use of. The plants can theoretically reduce cement emission by converting carbon dioxide into methane and have a further use in the overall emission picture.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of flows of materials and energy in the power/energy generation system.
   Main component sections (A,B,C,D)
   Electrical flow (C1)
   Heated methane fuel supply (D1)
   Post combustion stream (G1)
   Supply store and hydrogen store (SSH)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The component D is composed of a cooling and water removal section using cooling stages and a stage of direct contact of the post combustion products with water ice in a continuous flow. The post combustion products flow is composed of carbon dioxide and goes to a Sabatier process/Methantion unit where it is mixed with hydrogen gas and taken up to temperature and pressure as required to facilitate the Sabatier reaction. The post Sabatier/Methantion unit flow is cooled at temperature and pressure as required to not allow for decomposition of the methane and is cooled in a similar procedure to the flow from component C. The calcium oxide salt added containing dissolved carbon dioxide in energizing the electrolysis cell is converted into calcium carbonate which forms a solid on one of the electrodes, when electrolyzed improves the electrical conduction processes/chemical ionic steps by creating an ionic electrolyte. The electrolysis cell is used as calcium carbonate or process the electrolysis cell into calcium sulfate by reaction with sulfur dioxide when the calcium carbonate solids are removed from the electrolysis cell, where calcium sulfate is a form of cement.
DC J03 (Electrochemical processes and electrophoresis - including ozone production, brine electrolysis, water electrolysis, production of chemical compounds and non-metallic elements, but excluding batteries or other means of producing power and the treatment of metals (C25B).); Q52 (Reaction engines: external combustion; gas turbines; rockets (F01D, F02C, F02K, F02M))

TI Composition useful for preparing three-dimensional green bionic soil solidified particle comprises e.g. matrix material powder, foundation pit soil, light absorbing material, alkaline neutralizing agent, water reducing agent and silicon ash.
AU LI F
AE SHENZHEN CUILU TECHNOLOGY GREENING ENG (SHEN-Non-standard)
GA 2020025825
AB    NOVELTY - Composition comprises 15-70 pts. wt. matrix material powder, 15-70 pts. wt. foundation pit soil, 10-30 pts. wt. gel material, 20-50 pts. wt. light absorbing material, 11-35 pts. wt. alkaline neutralizing agent, 11-35 pts. wt. carbon dioxide, 0-5 pts. wt. air entraining agent, 0-4 pts. wt. water reducing agent, 50-100 pts. wt. water, 2-5 pts. wt. nutrient substance, 0-20 pts. wt. mineral powder and 0-20 pts. wt. silicon ash.
   USE - The composition is useful for preparing three-dimensional green bionic soil solidified particle.
   ADVANTAGE - The particle: is environmentally friendly; saves energy; has high water absorption rate; realizes the reuse of waste concrete and foundation pit soil; solves the problem of heavy ordinary soil; and is easy to settle.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for preparing three-dimensional green bionic soil solidified particle using the composition comprising (a) weighing matrix material powder, foundation pit soil, gel material, light absorbing material, alkaline neutralizing agent, water, air entraining agent, water reducing agent, nutrient substance, mineral powder and silicon ash, (b) mixing and grinding matrix material powder and foundation pit soil into powder and sieving, (c) mixing the matrix material with alkaline neutralizing agent for alkali treatment, (d) uniformly mixing and shaking the matrix material powder, foundation pit soil, gel material, light absorbing material, nutrient substance, mineral powder and silicon ash, (e) uniformly mixing water, air entraining agent and water reducing agent, (f) spraying the mixture obtained in step (e) into the mixture obtained in step (d), shaking until all particles are formed, (g) placing the finished particles in a carbonizing tank for performing carbonizing process for 36 hours after finishing manufacturing and filling carbon dioxide during the carbonizing process.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - CERAMICS AND GLASS - Preferred Composition: The composition comprises 30-40 pts. wt. matrix material powder, 30-40 pts. wt. foundation pit soil, 15-30 pts. wt. gel material, 20-40 pts. wt. light absorbing material, 17-35 pts. wt. alkaline neutralizing agent, 17-35 pts. wt. carbon dioxide, 0-3 pts. wt. air entraining agent, 0-2 pts. wt. water reducing agent, 60-80 pts. wt. water, 2-5 pts. wt. nutrient substance, 0-10 pts. wt. mineral powder and 0-10 pts. wt. silicon ash. Preferred Components: The matrix material powder is building waste and/or industrial solid waste.The building waste is at least one of concrete waste, brick waste and ceramic tile. The industrial solid waste is at least one of steel slag, blast furnace slag and cinder. The foundation pit soil is waste soil from building construction process. The gel material is at least one of gypsum, magnesium oxide, calcium oxide and fly ash. The light absorbing material is at least one of zeolite, silica gel, expanded perlite, vitrified micro beads and high molecular resin. The air entraining agent is at least one of aluminum powder, hydrogen peroxide, sodium dodecyl sulfonate and sodium dodecyl sulfate. The nutrient substance is at least one of zeolite powder and fertilizer composition.

PN KR2047754-B1
TI Multi-functional smart air conditioning system, has dampers whose fourth damper is installed at indoor air outlet, where first and fourth dampers are closed if measured indoor carbon dioxide is determined to be low.
AU KIM J H
AE MTES CO LTD (MTES-Non-standard)
GA 2019A0570G
AB    NOVELTY - The system (100) has a controller for controlling an internal exhaust fan, a condenser (130), an external air inlet fan (140), an evaporator (150), and a compressor (170). A first damper is installed at the external air inlet to be located outside the evaporator and connected to the controller. A second damper is installed at the external air inlet to be located at an indoor side of the evaporator and connected to the controller. A third damper is installed at a bet outlet so as to be located at an outdoor side than the condenser and connected to the controller. A fourth damper is installed at the indoor air outlet so as to be located at an indoor side of the condenser and connected to the controller, where first and fourth dampers are closed and second and third dampers are opened to ventilate the room if measured indoor carbon dioxide concentration is determined to be low.
   USE - Multi-functional smart air conditioning system.
   ADVANTAGE - The system adjusts a flow rate of the air flowing into the room or air discharged to outside, so that amount of electric energy consumed can be reduced by preventing internal negative pressure from being applied and performing efficient indoor air conditioning and ventilation at a lower cost. The system minimizes amount of electric energy consumed and improves cooling and heating efficiency to minimize maintenance costs, and provides indoor ventilation for creating a comfortable indoor space in a single device.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a side view of a multi-functional smart air conditioning system.
   Multi-functional smart air conditioning system (100)
   Casing (110)
   Condenser (130)
   External air inlet fan (140)
   Evaporator (150)
   Compressor (170)
   Expansion valve (190)

PN JP2019181456-A
TI Zeolite film complex for e.g., isolation process comprises porous support body, and zeolite film which is formed on support body and contains a residue derived from organic substance.
AU TOMOKAGE N
   ICHIKAWA M
   TANISHIMA K
   KINOSHITA N
AE NGK INSULATORS LTD (NIGA-C)
GA 201988382X
AB    NOVELTY - A zeolite film complex (1) comprises porous support body (11), and zeolite film (12) which is formed on support body and contains a residue derived from organic substance. The carbon-dioxide permeation amount of zeolite film is 70-100% of the carbon-dioxide permeation amount of non-remaining zeolite film which is a zeolite film which does not contain the residue derived from organic substance. The amount of methane permeation/carbon-tetrafluoride permeation amount ratio of zeolite film is 105-140% of the ratio of amount of methane permeation/carbon-tetrafluoride permeation amount of non-remaining zeolite film.
   USE - A zeolite film complex for isolation process (claimed). Can be used as separation membrane of gas and otehr substances.
   ADVANTAGE - Permeation of impurity into zeolite film can be suppressed while achieving suitable permeation amount of permeable target material by zeolite film.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for:
   (1) manufacturing a zeolite film complex (1) which involves (a) preparing seed crystal, (b) adhering seed crystal on porous support body (11), (c) immersing the support body in raw material solution, growing zeolite from seed crystal by hydrothermal synthesis, and forming zeolite film (12) on the support body, and (d) making a portion of structure-defining agent remain as residue inside the zeolite film while removing structure-defining agent from the zeolite film partially by heating the zeolite film; and
   (2) isolation process which involves (a) preparing zeolite film complex, and (b) supplying mixture containing gas or liquid to zeolite film complex and isolating a substance with high permeability in mixture from another substance by permeating the zeolite film complex.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a sectional view of the zeolite film complex.
   Zeolite film complex (1)
   Porous support body (11)
   Zeolite film (12)
   Through hole (111)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Parameters: Zeolite film has maximum material ring number of 8. Preferred Components: The residual amount of residue in zeolite film is 0.6-2 wt.%. The residue contained in zeolite film is carbon. The mixture includes hydrogen, helium, nitrogen, oxygen, water, water vapor, carbon monoxide, carbon dioxide, nitrogen oxide, ammonia, sulfur oxide, hydrogen sulfide, sulfur fluoride, mercury, arsine, hydrogen cyanide, a carbonyl sulfide, 1-8 carbon-containing (1-8C) hydrocarbon, organic acid, alcohol, mercaptans, ester, ether, ketone, and aldehyde. Preferred Process: Step (d) includes (d1) heating the zeolite film in inert gas atmosphere, and (d2) heating the zeolite film in oxidizing-gas atmosphere. Step (b) includes (b1) heating zeolite film in inert gas atmosphere, and (b2) heating the zeolite film in oxidizing-gas atmosphere.

TI Cultivating soybean sprouts in modified atmosphere involves screening raw beans strictly, removing bad beans, and cultivating beans, soaking beans and screened beans in warm water, draining water and uniformly spreading beans in incubator.
AU CHEN S
AE SHAANXI QINDOUYUAN AGRIC TECHNOLOGY CO (SHAA-Non-standard)
GA 201991829V
AB    NOVELTY - Cultivating soybean sprouts in modified atmosphere involves screening raw beans strictly, removing bad beans, and cultivating beans, soaking beans and screened beans in warm water for 90-150 minutes, draining water and uniformly spreading beans in the incubator. The incubator is sterilized by high temperature sterilization or 100 parts per million sodium hypochlorite spray. The cultivation box is used for carrying sterilation in culturing chamber and bud vegetable gas regulating cultivation system. The temperature to be sprayed is controlled at intervals of 22-24 degrees C. The volume fraction of circulating gas during the cultivation is 19.6-20 pts. wt. of oxygen, 0.030-0.032 pts. wt. of carbon dioxide, and 76-80 pts. wt. of nitrogen. The volume fraction of circulating gas during the cultivation is 18.0-19 pts. wt. of oxygen, 0.020-0.022 pts. wt. of carbon dioxide, and 73-77 pts. wt. of nitrogen gas. The cultivation of soybean sprouts is over on 9th day.
   USE - Method for cultivating soybean sprouts in modified atmosphere.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to cultivate soybean sprouts in modified atmosphere that high nutrition content and low toxicity chemical medicine residue, ensures environment for producing bean sprout, the accuracy and stability of the gas, and saves gas resource.

TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Condition: The water temperature is 30-35 degrees C. The soaking time is 120 minutes. The high temperature sterilization id 100 degrees C. Preferred Process: The beans are drained and the beans are uniformly spread in the incubator, and the incubator is sterilized by high temperature. The automatic spraying spray water automatically rotates the water temperature of the spray water every 20 hours to 20 degrees C. Preferred Composition: The volume fraction of the circulating gas during the cultivation is 19.8 pts. wt. of oxygen, 0.031 pts. wt. of carbon dioxide, 78 pts. wt. of nitrogen gas, and the gas circulation amount is 200 cubic meters per hour.

TI Automatic goli soda/Codd bottle filling machine has programmed logic control (PLC) that maintains pressure inside carbonator accurately and bottle in which level of soda is adjusted as desired by changing bottle filling angle.
AU 
AE SODAHUB INDIA PRIVATE LTD (SODA-Non-standard)
GA 201982820J
AB    NOVELTY - The automatic goli soda/Codd bottle filling machine has a carbonator, a gas bank, solenoid valves, a filling chamber (16), chiller, ice bank (17), SS coil, high pressure pump, pneumatic cylinders, pneumatic solenoid valve, programmed logic control (PLC), touch screen (HMI), and carbonation plate. PLC senses the pressure inside the carbonator with the help of pressure transducer. PLC maintains the pressure inside the carbonator accurately. The carbon dioxide (CO2) goes into the carbonator in the form of bubble and turns cool water into soda. The CO2 comes into the bottle from the carbonator through CO2 solenoid valve and the pressure in the carbonator and in the bottle becomes equal and when the soda level in the carbonator is higher then the bottle soda flows into the bottle through soda solenoid valve and the CO2 in the bottle goes back to carbonator. The level of soda in the bottle is adjusted as desired. The level of soda is adjusted by changing bottle filling angle.
   USE - Automatic goli soda/Codd bottle filling machine.
   ADVANTAGE - The programmed logic control (PLC) maintains accurate carbon dioxide pressure and water temperature using chilling, and carbonation process. The automatic goli soda/Codd bottle filling machine fills the beverage automatically into goli/CODD soda bottle so that the same level of beverage in all the bottles is easily maintained. The overflowing of beverage and difficulties in locking goli at the neck of the bottle is avoided. The device ensures the accurate temperature of the beverage, which enhance the taste by dissolving high CO2 gas volume into the beverage.

   Filling chamber (16)
   Ice bank (17)

   Outer frame (19)

TI Waste incineration system for preventing low-temperature synthesis of dioxins by using plasma, has plasma device that converts carbon particles and carbonaceous organic matter in exhaust gas into non-toxic carbon dioxide and water.
AU WANG B

AE UNIV GUILIN AEROSPACE TECHNOLOGY (UGAT-C)
GA 201975236G
AB    NOVELTY - The system comprises a waste incinerator (1) that has an exhaust port connected to a high temperature heat exchanger (2). An exhaust outlet of the high temperature heat exchanger is connected to a high temperature dust remover (3). The high-temperature dust remover removes solid matter, such as dust and carbon particles in the exhaust gas. An exhaust outlet of the high temperature dust remover is connected to a plasma device (5). The plasma device converts carbon particles and carbonaceous organic matter in the exhaust gas into non-toxic carbon dioxide and water. An exhaust outlet of the plasma device is connected to a low temperature heat exchanger (6). An exhaust outlet of the low temperature heat exchanger is connected to an exhaust gas purification device (7) which performs gas purification of the exhaust gas.
   USE - Waste incineration system for preventing low-temperature synthesis of dioxins by using plasma.
   ADVANTAGE - The lack of catalysts for the low-temperature synthesis of dioxins in the flue gas, as well as carbon particles and carbon-containing organic matter, effectively prevent the low-temperature synthesis of dioxins and reduce the environmental pollution caused by waste incineration.

The main filler is a substance having a non-linear structure and containing an ester group, a nitrile group, a carbonyl group, an ether group, and other pro-carbon dioxide groups.

PN CN109913721-A
TI Wear-resistant magnesium alloy comprises zinc, zirconium, aluminum, titanium, silicon, chromium, neodymium, boron, and the balance is magnesium and inevitable impurities.
AU INVENTOR U
AE XIANGTAN ZHILIAN TECHNOLOGY TRANSFER (XIAN-Non-standard)
GA 2019577230
AB    NOVELTY - Wear-resistant magnesium alloy comprises 2-3 wt.% zinc, 0.6-0.9 wt.% zirconium, 10-15 wt.% aluminum, 5-8 wt.% titanium, 0.3-1.0 wt.% silicon, 0.8-2.5 wt.% chromium, 0.5-2.0 wt.% neodymium, 0.05-0.1 wt.% boron, and the balance is magnesium and inevitable impurities. The mass percentage of the unavoidable impurities does not exceed 0.03 wt.%.
   USE - Wear-resistant magnesium alloy.
   ADVANTAGE - The wear-resistant magnesium alloy has high hardness and good wear resistance.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method of producing a wear-resistant magnesium alloy, which involves:
   (A) preheating magnesium, zinc, zirconium, aluminum, titanium, silicon, chromium, neodymium and boron in an oven to 400 degrees C, and keeping for 10-20 minutes;
   (B) protecting SF6 + carbon dioxide gas, then magnesium and zinc are placed in a crucible, and the temperature is heated in stages at 350-550 degrees C, 550-750 degrees C, and heated and smelted;
   (C) adding preheated zirconium, aluminum, silicon, neodymium to the magnesium-zinc melt, stirring once every 20 minutes, stirring for 3-4 times to make the ingredients uniform;
   (D) heating up to 800-850 degrees C, then melting preheated chromium, titanium, and boron, then they are stirred once every 10 minutes, and stirred for 2-3 times to make the ingredients uniform;
   (E) adding a smelting agent to remove impurities;
   (F) standing at 730-740 degrees C for 30-40 minutes, after standing, salvaging the surface of the melt;
   (G) casting the iron mold of 200-300 degrees C when the temperature drops to 650-700 degrees C, under the protection of SF6 + carbon dioxide gas, air cooling, and demoulding; and
   (H) performing solidification of magnesium alloy ingot.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The solution treatment temperature is 350-400 degrees C, and the time is 18-24 hours. Wear-resistant magnesium alloy comprises 2.5 wt.% zinc, 0.8 wt.% zirconium, 13 wt.% aluminum, 7 wt.% titanium, 0.6 wt.% silicon, 1.8 wt.% chromium, 1.2 wt.% neodymium, 0.08 wt.% boron, and the balance is magnesium and inevitable impurities.

AB    NOVELTY - Preparing benzoxazine porous polymer based                microporous carbon material, comprises (i)                refluxing and reacting amine compounds (I), diol                compounds (II) and paraformaldehyde to obtain                benzoxazine porous polymer, (ii) liquid phase                impregnating step (i) product and activator, and                (iii) freeze-drying the step (ii) product,                chemically activating, pickling acid washing using                water to obtain final product.
   USE - The carbon material is useful for adsorbing                carbon dioxide (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method: is simple; utilizes cheap and                easily available raw materials; has no byproduct;                and does not need to add catalyst. The carbon                material has uniform and controllable pore size,                excellent performance in gas adsorption separation,                and carbon dioxide adsorption amount at 0&#176;                C under 1 bar is 4.02-7.06 mmol/g, and high                adsorption capacity; and is easily regeneration and                suitable for industrial production.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing benzoxazine porous polymer based                microporous carbon material, comprises (i)                refluxing and reacting amine compounds of formula                (I), diol compounds of formula (II) and                paraformaldehyde to obtain benzoxazine porous                polymer, (ii) liquid phase impregnating step (i)                product and activator, and (iii) freeze-drying the                step (ii) product, chemically activating, pickling                acid washing using water to obtain final                product.
   R=1,3,5-triphenylbenzene-4',4'',4'''-triyl,                2,4,6-triphenyl-1,3,5-triazine-4',4'',4'''-triyl,                triphenylamine-4,4',4''-triyl,                1,3,5-triazine-2,4,6-triyl; and
   R =biphenyl-4,4'-diyl, naphthalene-1,5-diyl,                benzene-1,4-diyl, or benzene-1,3-diyl.
   An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for                microporous carbon material or benzoxazine porous                polymer, obtained by method for producing                benzoxazine porous polymer based microporous carbon                material.
                                                                                                                     
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: In step (i), (I) is                  1,3,5-tris(4-aminophenyl)benzene,                  2,4,6-tris(4-aminophenyl)-1,3,5-triazine,                  tris(4-aminophenyl)amine and/or melamine, (II) is                  biphenyldiol, 1,5-dihydroxynaphthalene,                  hydroquinone and/or resorcinol, the molar ratio                  between (I),(II) to paraformaldehyde is                  1:0.5-5:5-20, the reaction time is 4-72 hours,                  the organic solvent is trimethylbenzene, toluene,                  tetrahydrofuran, dioxane and/or chloroform, the                  concentration of (I) in organic solvent is                  0.01-0.5 mol/l, and the reaction temperature is                  60-130&#176; C. The method comprises                  adjusting the pH of the reaction system to 8-13                  using alkaline substance, where the alkaline                  substance is sodium hydroxide, triethylamine,                  aqueous ammonia, sodium carbonate and/or                  potassium hydroxide. In step (ii), the mass ratio                  of activator to step (i) product is 1-4:1, and                  the activator is potassium hydroxide, sodium                  hydroxide, zinc chloride, potassium carbonate                  and/or sodium carbonate. In step (iii):                  activating under a high-purity nitrogen                  atmosphere at a gas flow rate of 20-200                  ml/minute; the activating at 500-900&#176; C                  at a heating speed of 1-10&#176; C/minute for                  1 hour; and washing the microporous carbon                  material using dilute hydrochloric acid and                  deionized water to remove residual inorganic                  ubstances. Preferred Components: The specific                  surface area of precursor benzoxazine porous                  polymer is 50-500 m2/g and total pore volume is                  0.15-0.66 cm3/g. PN KR2019046206-A; KR2043221-B1
TI Return loop type continuous direct carbonation reaction system has control unit that changes supply of waste gas to reactor by operating valve provided in waste gas supply pipe according to change in pH value, when pH value is decreased.
AU JO H Y
   YOU J Y
   KIM Y S
   HYUNG K J
   YOO J Y
   KOO J
AE DOOSAN HEAVY IND & CONSTR CO LTD (DOOS-C)
GA 201941449S
AB    NOVELTY - The system has multiple direct carbonation reactors that included with an absorption solution. A waste gas supply pipe is connected to each of the direct carbonation reactors. A pH measuring device (100) is located in the direct carbonation reactor in order to measure the pH of the absorption solution. A control unit (200) is configured for receiving and storing the pH value measured by the pH measuring device. The supply of waste gas to the direct carbonation reactor is changed by operating a valve provided in the waste gas supply pipe according to the change in pH value at which the pH value is temporarily increased while the pH value is decreased.
   USE - Return loop type continuous direct carbonation reaction system.
   ADVANTAGE - The combustion byproducts including bottom ash or fly ash, which are industrial byproducts containing calcium are used. Hence the carbon dioxide is immobilized more effectively at low cost. Multiple absorption solution reactors are used and the pH in each reactor is continuously monitored. The continuous process is operated in a semi-batch mode by detecting the fluctuation of the pH change in the direct carbonation reactor and setting it as a reaction end point. Hence the carbon dioxide concentration of the exhaust gas to be discharged is effectively reduced.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for an operating method of return loop type continuous direct carbonation reaction system.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the return loop type continuous direct carbonation reaction system. (Drawing includes non-English language text)
   PH measuring device (100)
   Control unit (200)

TI Heat exchanger for separating vapor component e.g. carbon dioxide from e.g. light gas, has causing unit for causing inner chamber to flex, and causing solid product to fall from outer surface of inner chamber for collection.
AU BAXTER L
   CHAMBERLAIN S
   MANSFIELD E
   BAXTER A

AE BAXTER L (BAXT-Individual)
   CHAMBERLAIN S (CHAM-Individual)

   DAVIS N (DAVI-Individual)
GA 201878486V
AB    NOVELTY - The exchanger has an outer chamber for causing a vapor component of carrier gas (604) to desublimate or condense onto an outer surface (610) of an inner chamber (602), and forming a solid product (608). An inner chamber makes coolant (612) to pass for cooling the carrier gas of the outer chamber. A causing unit causes the inner chamber to flex, and causes the solid product to fall from the outer surface of the inner chamber for collection. A vibration inducing device comprises a piezoelectric actuator, an ultrasound emitter, a voice coil, a linear resonant actuator and a shaker, where the inner chamber is constructed from expanding and contracting corrugated tubes and the corrugated tubes are caused to expand and contract by varying coolant pressure.
   USE - Self-cleaning desublimating heat exchanger for separating vapor component such as carbon dioxide, nitrogen oxide, sulfur dioxide, nitrogen dioxide, sulfur trioxide, hydrogen sulfide, hydrogen cyanide, hydrocarbon and mercury from carrier gas such as combustion flue gas, syngas, producer gas, natural gas, steam reforming gas, hydrocarbon, light gas and refinery off-gas (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The exchanger improves ability to directly separate acid gas vapors from carrier gases without complex unit operations to cryogenic gas processing. The exchanger rapidly varies coolant pressure by a pump operating at variable speeds, a valve rapidly opening and closing and a combination, thus causing the inner chamber to experience hammering.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for a method for using a heat exchanger.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a top view of an inner chamber of a heat exchanger.

PN CN108539226-A; CN108539226-B
TI Fuel cell system used in civil and military applications e.g. transportation, performs chemical reaction between catalyst and input heat to reform hydrogen with water to generate hydrogen and carbon dioxide.
AU LUO K
   ZHANG L
   LUO E
   ZHOU Y
AE TECH INST PHYSICS & CHEM CHINESE ACAD (CAPC-C)
GA 201874396U
AB    NOVELTY - The system has a power control unit (9) which is connected to a hydrogen peroxide storage unit (1), a methanol storage unit (2), a heating reactor (5), and a power generation unit (8) for controlling power generation and consumption. The reaction product is separated by a vapor-liquid separator (4) in hydrogen peroxide storage unit (3), separated water is supplied to a gas separator (7), and separated oxygen is separately input into heating reactor and power generation unit. The methanol liquid is separately input to heating reactor and hydrogen production reactor (6). The methanol undergoes an exothermic reaction with oxygen, and generated heat is transferred to hydrogen production reactor to provide heat for hydrogen production reaction. A chemical reaction is performed between catalyst and input heat to reform hydrogen with water to generate hydrogen and carbon dioxide, and separated hydrogen enters power generation unit. The oxygen and hydrogen are reacted to generate electricity.
   USE - Fuel cell system used in civil and military applications such as transportation, portable power supplies, decentralized power stations, aerospace and underwater submersibles.
   ADVANTAGE - The system can safely and efficiently utilize hydrogen peroxide and methanol to generate power generation effects, and has high efficiency, compact structure, high reliability and environmental protection, and wide application prospects in underwater power generation systems.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the fuel cell system. (Drawing includes non-English language text)
   Hydrogen peroxide storage unit (1)
   Methanol storage unit (2)
   Hydrogen peroxide storage unit (3)
   Vapor-liquid separator (4)
   Heating reactor (5)
   Hydrogen production reactor (6)
   Gas separator (7)
   Power generation unit (8)
   Power generation unit (9)

PN CN108373990-A; CN108373990-B
TI Culture medium useful for inducing differentiation            of human amniotic epithelial stem cells into            melanocytes, contains alpha-minimal essential medium            basal medium, endothelin 3, dexamethasone, stem cell            factor and fetal bovine serum.
AU PANG X
   WANG R
   SHI P
AE SHENYANG AMNION BIOENG TECHNOLOGY (SHEN-Non-standard)
   SHENYANG AMNION BIOENG TECHNOLOGY (SHEN-Non-standard)
GA 201864586Y
AB    NOVELTY - Differentiation induction culture medium                comprises 400-500 ml &#945; -minimal essential                medium (MEM) basal medium, 70-120 nM endothelin 3,                30-70 nM dexamethasone, 30-80 nM stem cell factor,                3-7 nM basic fibroblast growth factor, 1X insulin,                transferrin and selenium and 10-20% fetal bovine                serum.
   USE - The culture medium is useful for inducing                differentiation of human amniotic epithelial stem                cells into melanocytes.
   ADVANTAGE - The culture medium induces differentiation in                simple manner.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method                for inducing differentiation of human amniotic                epithelial stem cells into melanocytes, involves                (a) taking human amniotic membrane, cutting,                digesting twice with 0.25 g/l trypsin for 20-35                minutes and 7-12 minutes respectively, adding                10-20% fetal bovine serum to stop digestion, mixing                supernatants, centrifuging at 800-1200 revolutions                per minute (rpm) for 8-12 minutes, obtaining a cell                suspension, and filtering the cell suspension to                obtain single cell suspension, (b) inoculating 2x                106l-1to 2x 107l-1single cell suspension into                Dulbecco's modified Eagle's medium (DMEM)/F12                medium containing 10% fetal bovine serum, culturing                in 5% carbon dioxide incubator at 37&#176; C                and saturated humidity, and obtaining amniotic                epithelial stem cells of second to third                generation, and (c) inoculating 2x 106l-1to 2x                107l-1amniotic epithelial stem cells of second to                third generation in 12-well plates of DMEM/12                medium containing 10-20% fetal bovine serum, after                more than 80% of cells are fused, replacing                differentiation induction culture medium, culturing                in 5% carbon dioxide incubator at 37&#176; C                and saturated humidity, changing once every 2-3                days, and continuously culturing for 80-120                days.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Cells: The human amniotic                  epithelial stem cells and melanocytes are derived                  from ectoderm.

PN CN108096128-A
TI Polypeptide complex muscle fluid comprises water, epidermal growth factor lyophilized powder, hyaluronidase, rose stem cell stock solution, palmitoyl tripeptide, palmitoyl five titanium, and has excellent moisturizing property.
AU SUN S
AE BEIJING HANFANG SHUHUA COSMETICS CO LTD (BEIJ-Non-standard)
GA 201844459U
AB    NOVELTY - Polypeptide ccomplex muscle fluid comprises 62-68 pts. wt. water, 20-24 pts. wt. epidermal growth factor (EGF) lyophilized powder, 18-20 pts. wt. hyaluronidase, 14-20 pts. wt. rose stem cell stock solution, 14-16 pts. wt. palmitoyl tripeptide-5, 13-15 pts. wt. palmitoyl five titanium-4.
   USE - The polypeptide ccomplex muscle fluid used on facial skin which is firstly cooled by using liquid carbon, and the face is injected with the polypeptide rejuvenation fluid in sequence from the bottom to the top, fingers circle around the facial skinuntil the peptide is absorbed by the skin (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The polypeptide complex muscle liquid has excellent moisturizing property, anti-aging and anti-crinkle performance, alleviating problem of human facial skin caused by aging and wrinkles.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for preparing polypeptide complex muscle fluid, which involves:
   (A) crushing and washing fresh rose petals with deionized water until the rose petals surface is volatilized;
   (B) placing rose petals in a cold environment;
   (C) crushing rose petals after pretreatment;
   (D) introducing carbon dioxide in high-pressure supercritical extraction vessel, raising internal temperature and pressure of the extraction vessel to make carbon dioxide into a supercritical state;
   (E) placing crushed rose petals in an extraction kettle and extracted for 42-46 minutes;
   (F) taking product from extraction kettle into deionized water, stirring, and filtering to obtain the rose dried cell stock solution; and
   (G) adding water, epidermal growth factor lyophilized powder, Hyaluronidase, Palmitoyl-Tripeptide-5, palmitoyl five titanium-4, raising temperature of the reaction system to 65-72 degrees C, stirring, cooling down to 35-39 degrees C, then adding rose stem cell stock solution, stirring and cooling down to 23-27 degrees C, discharging to obtain the polypeptide complex muscle fluid.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - preferred Conditions: The mass ratio of the rose stem cell stock to palmitoyl five titanium-4 is 1:1. The ambient temperature is -5 degrees C to -9 degrees C, and freezing time is 2-3 hours during pretreatment. The carbon dioxide flow rate in the extraction kettle is 32-36 m2/hours. The internal temperature of the extraction kettle is 25-29 degrees C and the pressure is 32-36 mega Pascals. The stirring time is 20-30 minutes. The facial temperature is cooled to 2-3 degrees C.
   ACTIVITY - Dermatological.
DC B04 (Natural products and polymers. Including testing of body fluids (other than blood typing or cell counting), pharmaceuticals or veterinary compounds of unknown structure, testing of microorganisms for pathogenicity, testing of chemicals for mutagenicity or human toxicity and fermentative production of DNA or RNA. PT P
PN US2018086984-A1; US10336944-B2
TI Hydrocarbon generation system used to make            hydrocarbons, involves microtubular reactor of first            and second regions, and temperature control unit to            form first region at first temperature i.e. greater            than second temperature of second region.
AU CHEN F
   LEI L
   LIU T
   FANG S
AE UNIV SOUTH CAROLINA (USCL-C)
GA 201824687A
AB    NOVELTY - Hydrocarbon generation system comprises: a                microtubular reactor (101a), where the microtubular                reactor including a first region (102) including a                cathode and an anode in electrical communication                with one another and an oxygen ion conducting                electrolyte between the cathode and the anode, a                second region (104) including a Fischer-Tropsch                reaction catalyst in fluid communication and                downstream of the cathode; and a temperature                control unit configured to provide the first region                at a first temperature that is greater than a                second temperature of the second region.
   USE - The hydrocarbon generation system is useful                for generating hydrocarbons, which is useful as                feedstock or energy storage.
   ADVANTAGE - The hydrocarbon generation system: provides                potential pathway for efficient and eco-friendly                utilization of carbon (e.g. coal and biomass) and                carbon dioxide to produce clean hydrocarbons;                provides a carbon neutral sustainable hydrocarbon                supply; can more easily be stored and transported                using existing infrastructure as compared with                syngas of a gasification or stand-alone                electrolysis process; and provides the hydrocarbons                without requiring pure carbon dioxide, which is                more flexible for practical adoption.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for                generating the hydrocarbons, comprising heating a                first region of a microtubular reactor to a first                temperature, flowing a feed stream (114) to the                cathode of the first region, where the feed stream                including water and carbon monoxide, and collecting                a hydrocarbon downstream of a second region of the                microtubular reactor, where the second region being                downstream of the first region and is at a second                temperature that is lower than the first                temperature, the second region including a                Fischer-Tropsch reaction catalyst in fluid                communication and downstream of the cathode.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The figure shows a schematic view of the                hydrocarbon generation system.
   Microtubular reactor (101a)
   First region (102)
   Second region (104)
   Interconnections (105)
   Feed stream (114)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The outer diameter of                  the microtubular reactor is greater than or equal                  to 3 mm. The cathode comprises nickel-yttria                  stabilized zirconia, nickel-samaria doped ceria,                  nickel-gadolinium doped ceria, perovskites                  including lanthanoid containing composite of                  formula (La1-xM1xM2O3- delta ) (I) and/or                  strontium-iron-molybdenum oxide of formula                  (Sr2Fe2MoxMyO6- delta ) (II).M1 = alkaline earth element;M2 = transition metal element;M = transition metal element; andx, y, delta = not defined.The anode comprises lanthanum strontium                  cobalt iron oxide, strontium-doped lanthanum                  manganite, strontium-doped lanthanum manganite                  impregnated with ceria-based catalyst, (II)                  and/or (I). The electrolyte comprises yttria                  stabilized zirconia, scandia stabilized zirconia,                  lanthanum gallate-based oxide and/or doped ceria.                  The Fischer-Tropsch catalyst comprises cobalt,                  iron, nickel, nickel/platinum alloy, rhodium,                  ruthenium, palladium, osmium, vanadium, iron                  and/or manganese. The cathode and the                  Fischer-Tropsch catalyst are on an inner surface                  of the microtubular reactor or on an outer                  surface of the microtubular reactor. The                  hydrocarbon generation system comprises                  microtubular reactors in an array. The feed                  stream further comprises hydrogen. The first                  temperature is 500-1000 degrees C. The second                  region is 200-500 degrees C. The first                  temperature and the second temperature are                  components of a temperature gradient along the                  microtubular reactor. Preferred Method: The                  method further comprises: applying a voltage                  potential across the cathode and the anode;                  preheating the feed stream; and flowing the feed                  streams to the microtubular reactors in an                  array.

PT P
PN US2018003069-A1; US10415707-B2
TI Turbo machine, has face seal assembly coupled to stator, where first pressure cavity is arranged downstream of second cavity with reference to flow of process fluid, and third cavity is isolated from third and second pressure cavities.
AU BIDKAR R A
   SEVINCER E
   THATTE A M
   WANG J
   ZHENG X
AE GENERAL ELECTRIC CO (GENE-C)
GA 201801234S
AB    NOVELTY - The turbo machine (36) has a face seal assembly (102) comprising a first pressure cavity (120), and isolated hydrostatic ports (122) extending from the first pressure cavity to a seal bearing face (118). The face seal assembly is coupled to a stator (39), where a second pressure cavity (149) and a third pressure cavity (153) defined by the stator, a rotor, and the face assembly, the third pressure cavity is arranged downstream of the second cavity with reference to flow of process fluid along the stator and the rotor, and the first cavity is isolated from the second and third cavities.
   USE - Turbo machine. Uses include but are not limited to a supercritical carbon dioxide turbine, a gas turbine, a steam turbine, a supercritical carbon dioxide turbine, a turbo compressor, and a hydro turbine.
   ADVANTAGE - The face seal assembly is configured to operate with the seal bearing face and/or the rotor bearing face providing a relatively low degree of flatness, thus reducing the cost of machining sealing faces of a seal ring and the rotor. The turbo machine configures an area to be significantly large by setting supply larger than high, which implies that the face seal assembly is configured to change a pressure value more significantly with fluid-film thickness than that of a traditional orifice-compensated face seal assembly, thus improving fluid-film stiffness.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for a method for manufacturing a turbo machine.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a sectional view of a portion of a steam turbine.
   Turbo machine (36)
   Stator (39)
   Face seal assembly (102)
   Seal bearing face (118)
   Pressure cavities (120, 149, 153)
   Hydrostatic port (122)

PN WO2021055174-A1; CN114430737-A; EP4031516-A1; IN202217020608-A; US2022348528-A1
TI Producing purified ethylene oxide and monoethylene            glycol involves providing a quenched and alkaline            treated and water washed vaporous reaction stream.
AU OZERO B J
AE PETRON SCIENTECH INC (PETR-Non-standard)
   SCIENTECH ASSOC INC (SCIE-Non-standard)
   SCIENTECH ASSOC INC (SCIE-Non-standard)
   PETRON SCIENTECH INC (PETR-Non-standard)
   SCIENTECH ASSOC INC (SCIE-Non-standard)
   SCIENTECH ASSOC INC (SCIE-Non-standard)
   PETRON SCIENTECH INC (PETR-Non-standard)
GA 202130928T
AB    NOVELTY - Producing purified ethylene oxide and                monoethylene glycol involves providing a quenched                and alkaline treated and water washed vaporous                reaction stream, where the quenched and alkaline                treated and water washed vaporous reaction stream                comprises ethylene oxide, carbon dioxide,                formaldehyde, and acetaldehyde, contacting the                quenched and alkaline treated and water washed                vaporous reaction stream with water in an ethylene                oxide absorber to produce a first absorbate stream                having a first ethylene oxide concentration in                water, dividing the first absorbate stream into a                first portion of the first absorbate stream, a                second portion of the first absorbate stream, and a                third portion of the first absorbate stream,                feeding the first portion of the first absorbate                stream to an ethylene oxide stripper and contacting                the first portion of the first absorbate stream                with steam to produce a vaporous ethylene oxide                stream, eeding the vaporous EO stream to an EO                reabsorber.
   USE - Method for producing purified ethylene oxide                and monoethylene glycol.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to produce purified                ethylene oxide and monoethylene glycol in an                efficient manner.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Producing purified ethylene oxide and                monoethylene glycol involves providing a quenched                and alkaline treated and water washed vaporous                reaction stream, where the quenched and alkaline                treated and water washed vaporous reaction stream                comprises ethylene oxide, carbon dioxide,                formaldehyde, and acetaldehyde, contacting the                quenched and alkaline treated and water washed                vaporous reaction stream with water in an ethylene                oxide absorber to produce a first absorbate stream                having a first ethylene oxide concentration in                water, dividing the first absorbate stream into a                first portion of the first absorbate stream, a                second portion of the first absorbate stream, and a                third portion of the first absorbate stream,                feeding the first portion of the first absorbate                stream to an ethylene oxide stripper and contacting                the first portion of the first absorbate stream                with steam to produce a vaporous ethylene oxide                stream, eeding the vaporous EO stream to an EO                reabsorber and contacting the vaporous EO stream                with the second portion of the first absorbate                stream to form a reabsorber bottoms stream, where                the reabsorber bottoms stream has a second EO                concentration in water that is higher than the                first EO concentration in water, dividing the                reabsorber bottoms stream into a first portion of                the reabsorber bottoms stream and a second portion                of the reabsorber bottoms stream, feeding the first                portion of the reabsorber bottoms stream to a first                lights stripper and contacting the first portion of                the reabsorber bottoms stream with steam to produce                a first lights stripper bottoms, where the first                light stripper bottoms has a third EO concentration                in water that is higher than the first EO                concentration in water, feeding the first lights                stripper bottoms to a high purity ethylene oxide                distillation column to produce purified EO,                combining the second portion of the reabsorber                bottoms stream and the third portion of the first                absorbate stream to produce a second lights                stripper feed, feeding the second lights stripper                feed to a second lights stripper and contacting the                second lights stripper feed with steam to produce a                second lights stripper bottom stream, where the                second lights bottom stream has a fourth EO                concentration in water that is lower than the third                EO concentration in water and feeding the second                lights bottom stream to a glycol reactor to produce                monoethylene glycol. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is                included for an apparatus r producing purified                ethylene oxide (EO) and monoethylene glycol (MEG)                from a quenched and alkaline treated and water                washed vaporous reaction, where quenched and                alkaline treated and water washed vaporous reaction                stream comprises EO, carbon dioxide, formaldehyde,                and acetaldehyde, the apparatus comprises an EO                absorber configured and arranged to contact the                quenched and alkaline treated and water washed                vaporous reaction stream with water to produce a                first absorbate stream having a first EO                concentration in water, a first series of conduits                configured and arranged to divide the first                absorbate stream into a first portion of the first                absorbate stream, a second portion of the first                absorbate stream, and a third portion of the first                absorbate stream, an EO stripper configured and                arranged to contact the first portion of the first                absorbate stream with steam to produce a vaporous                EO stream, an EO reabsorber configured and arranged                to contact the vaporous EO stream with the second                portion of the first absorbate stream to form a                reabsorber bottoms stream, where the reabsorber                bottoms stream has a second EO concentration in                water that is higher than the first EO                concentration in water, a second series of conduits                configured and arranged to divide the reabsorber                bottoms stream into a first portion of the                reabsorber bottoms stream and a second portion of                the reabsorber bottoms stream.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred components: The high purity                  ethylene oxide distillation column further                  produces a purge stream comprising ethylene oxide                  and acetaldehyde and where the purge stream                  comprising ethylene oxide and acetaldehyde is fed                  to the second lights stripper. The high purity                  ethylene oxide distillation column produces a                  vaporous overhead stream comprising EO and                  formaldehyde and the vaporous overhead stream                  comprising ethylene oxide and formaldehyde is fed                  to the ethylene oxide reabsorber with the                  vaporous ethylene oxide stream. The first lights                  stripper produces a vaporous overhead stream that                  comprises carbon dioxide and the vaporous                  overhead stream that comprises carbon dioxide is                  fed to the EO reabsorber with the vaporous EO                  stream.
