PT P
PN CN110646602-A
TI Evaluation of cytotoxicity of organic cadmium in            aquatic products involves inoculating pheochromocytoma            cells in six-well plate, adding medium, culturing,            centrifuging, washing pellets, extracting, and            analyzing obtained metabolites.
AU WANG H
   WANG Q
   TAN M
   CHENG S
   QI Z
   ZHANG L
   NA X
AE UNIV DALIAN POLYTECHNIC (UYDP-C)
GA 2020039708
AB    NOVELTY - Evaluation of cytotoxicity of organic cadmium                in aquatic products involves inoculating                pheochromocytoma cells of rat adrenal medulla in a                six-well plate, setting up blank groups, adding 1-3                ml complete medium, culturing, adding                polysaccharide cadmium mixed solution and                incubating, discarding the obtained solution in the                six-well plate, adding complete culture medium,                cultivating, adding internal standard mixture,                methanol, incubating, placing the six-well plate on                dry ice, extracting blank groups, cell metabolites                in the cadmium-exposed group and the abalone                polysaccharide-cadmium mixed liquid group,                centrifuging, taking the supernatant to another                centrifuge tube, washing the pellets, centrifuging,                filtering, extracting metabolites in the pores of                the cadmium-exposed group, performing quantitative                analysis of small molecule cell metabolites, and                statistical analysis processing the intracellular                metabolites detected by chromatographic                column.
   USE - Evaluation of cytotoxicity of organic cadmium                in aquatic products.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is capable of detecting and                analyzing metabolites related to carbon metabolism                in the center of cells, and assessing changes in                central carbon metabolism pathways in cells exposed                to organic cadmium.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Evaluation of cytotoxicity of organic cadmium                in aquatic products involves (i) inoculating                pheochromocytoma cells (PC12 cells) of rat adrenal                medulla in a six-well plate, setting up blank                groups, cadmium exposure groups, abalone                polysaccharide-cadmium combined exposure groups,                inoculating each group in six-well plate such that                the density of seeded cells in each well of the                six-well plate is (1-2)x 105 cells/mL, adding 1-3                ml complete medium, culturing at a culture                conditions of 37 degrees C and 5% carbon dioxide in                air for 20-26 hours, discarding the solution in the                wells of the six-well plate, and re-adding 1-2 ml                per well complete medium in the blank group, adding                20-25 mu M cadmium chloride solution diluted in                complete medium to the cadmium chloride exposure                group, adding polysaccharide cadmium mixed solution                comprising 20-25 mu M cadmium chloride, 50-100                mg/mL abalone polysaccharide, and remainder of                complete culture medium to abalone                polysaccharide-cadmium mixed solution exposure                group, incubating at 37 degrees C and 5% carbon                dioxide in the air for 20-26 hours, (ii) discarding                the solution in the six-well plate, adding complete                culture medium to each well, placing at 37 degrees                C, cultivating in an incubator with 5% carbon                dioxide in the air for 20-24 hours, placing the                six-well plate on dry ice, adding 2-5 mu L internal                standard mixture comprising 1 mg/mL internal                standard undecanoic acid, 1 mg/mL undecanoic acid                and 2 mg/mL L-phenylalanine in a volume ratio of                2:2:1, 2-3 ml of 80-90% methanol, incubating at -80                degrees C for 20-25 minutes, placing the six-well                plate on dry ice, extracting blank groups, cell                metabolites in the cadmium-exposed group and the                abalone polysaccharide-cadmium mixed liquid group,                taking the blank group as an example, scraping                cells of each well of the blank group with a cell                scraper, blowing cells scraped from the wells with                a pipette tip, grouping every 3 wells in the blank                group and transferring liquid in the 3 wells to the                centrifuge tube, adding 1-2 ml methanol to each                well for suspension and transferring to centrifuge                tube, centrifuging at 8 degrees C for 5-10 minutes,                taking the supernatant to another centrifuge tube,                washing the pellet with 1-2 ml methanol and                suspending at 4-8 degrees C, centrifuging at                10000-15000 g for 5-10 minutes, transferring the                supernatant to centrifuge tube, nitrogen blowing                the cell extract until there is no liquid in the                centrifuge tube to obtain cell metabolites, adding                150-200 mu l acetonitrile, filtering using an                organic filter with a pore size of 0.22 mu m to                obtain the test sample, extracting metabolites in                the pores of the cadmium-exposed group and the                abalone polysaccharide-cadmium mixed solution                group, (iii) performing quantitative analysis of                small molecule cell metabolites using liquid                chromatography-mass spectrometry and establishing                ultra-high performance liquid chromatography tandem                triple quadrupole mass spectrometry at a flow rate                0.35-0.4 mL/minute, a column temperature of 55                degrees C and a sample volume of 3-10 mu l for                quantitative analysis of intracellular and                extracellular metabolites, and (iv) statistical                analysis processing the intracellular metabolites                detected by amide chromatographic column, finding                metabolites with significant changes in content,                and making a partial least square analysis of the                metabolites and a metabolic pathway map. The                complete medium is GIBCO's (RTM: basal medium for                supporting growth of many different mammalian                cells) comprising 4500 mg/L glucose, 10% fetal                bovine serum, and 0.5-1%                penicillin-streptomycin.

PT P
PN CN109692150-A
TI Mesoporous-microporous halloysite-montmorillonite composite aerogel material, prepared by adding halloysite nanotubes, ultrasonically dispersing, drying montmorillonite, dispersing, heating water bath and carrying out supercritical drying.
AU LI H
   LI R
   LIU H
   YANG A
   LIAO X
AE UNIV TIANJIN CHENGJIAN (UTCJ-C)
GA 201941804F
AB    NOVELTY - Mesoporous-microporous halloysite-montmorillonite composite aerogel material, is claimed. The average specific surface area of the halloysite-montmorillonite composite aerogel material is 600-608 m2g-1. The nanoscale and microscale pores exist in the halloysite-montmorillonite composite aerogel material nanoscale pores average 10-12 nm and micron-scale pores average 20-22 mu m, prepared by (i) adding halloysite nanotubes into a mixture of deionized water and ethanol and ultrasonically dispersing uniformly, adding sodium styrene sulfonate, polydivinylsiloxane, initiator and dexamethasone into a mixture of deionized water; (ii) drying montmorillonite, dispersing montmorillonite dispersion with a solvent, mechanically stirring uniformly montmorillonite dispersion and ultrasonically processing; and (iii) mixing dispersion 1, dispersion 2 and gentamicin solution, dispersing in the dispersion medium, heating the water bath and carrying out supercritical drying with carbon dioxide as medium.
   USE - Used as mesoporous-microporous halloysite-montmorillonite composite aerogel material.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Mesoporous-microporous halloysite-montmorillonite composite aerogel material, is claimed. The average specific surface area of the halloysite-montmorillonite composite aerogel material is 600-608 m2g-1. The nanoscale and microscale pores exist in the halloysite-montmorillonite composite aerogel material nanoscale pores average 10-12 nm and micron-scale pores average 20-22 mu m, prepared by (i) adding 0.5-15 pts. wt. halloysite nanotubes into a mixture of 50 pts. wt. deionized water and 50 pts. wt. ethanol and ultrasonically dispersing uniformly to obtain halloysite dispersion, adding 0.5-15 pts. wt. sodium styrene sulfonate, 0.05-2 pts. wt. polydivinylsiloxane, 0.01-0.5 pts. wt. initiator and 0.05-10 pts. wt. dexamethasone into a mixture of 50 pts. wt. deionized water and 50 pts. wt. ethanol, stirring uniformly, adding the above solution into the halloysite dispersion, ultrasonically dispersing uniformly, vacuuming, maintaining the vacuum, returning to atmospheric pressure, repeating the vacuuming three times, washing the product, dispersing in 100 pts. wt. water, heating the water bath to 70-80 degrees C to initiate polymerization, carrying out polymerization for 50 hours, washing and dispersing into 100 pts. wt. water to obtain dispersion liquid 1; where the polydivinylsiloxane has a number average molecular weight of 500-5000, preferably 1000-3000 and molecular percentage of vinyl content (i.e. the ratio of the number of moles of vinyl to the number of moles of monodimethyldivinylsiloxane throughout the amino group) 0.1-5% of the amino-terminated polydimethyldivinylsiloxane an alkane or amino double-terminated polydimethyldivinylsiloxane; (ii) drying 0.05-15 pts. wt. montmorillonite, dispersing 0.05-25 wt.% montmorillonite dispersion with a solvent, mechanically stirring uniformly montmorillonite dispersion and ultrasonically processing to obtain dispersion 2; and (iii) mixing 0.5-15 pts. wt. dispersion 1, 0.5-35 pts. wt. dispersion 2 and 0.1-20 pts. wt. gentamicin solution in a mass fraction of 0.05-20 wt.%, dispersing in the dispersion medium, adding 0.01-2 pts. wt. initiator, stirring uniformly, heating the water bath to 70-90 degrees C to initiate polymerization, carrying out polymerization for 37 hours, placing the product in carbon dioxide supercritical high pressure extraction unit and carrying out supercritical drying with carbon dioxide as medium at 10-300 degrees C for 1 hour at a pressure of 1-20 MPa, where the inner side of the halloysite nanotube wall has a positive charge, the outside of the tube wall has a negative charge, the sodium styrene sulfonate has a negative charge, the sodium styrene sulfonate is adsorbed on the inner wall of the halloysite nanotube by electrostatic interaction, the polydivinylsiloxane added, the initiator and dexamethasone are dispersed into the hollow structure of the halloysite nanotubes, the hollow structure of the halloysite nanotubes provides a microporous structure for the mesoporous-microporous halloysite-montmorillonite composite aerogel material, vacuuming, washing the polydivinylsiloxane is copolymerized with sodium styrene sulfonate, forming a crosslinked structure inside the halloysite nanotubes and loading dexamethasone in the halloysite nanotubes, in the step (iii), carrying out polymerization of the vinyl functional groups on the polydivinylsiloxane outside the hollow structure of the halloysite nanotubes under the action of an initiator to form a three-dimensional network structure between the halloysite nanotubes and the polydivinylsiloxane, the montmorillonite added is constructed as a network pore structure, the three-dimensional network structure and montmorillonite is successfully constructed into a network pore structure to form a three-dimensional network pore structure, the three-dimensional network pore structure provides a mesoporous structure for the mesoporous-microporous halloysite-montmorillonite composite aerogel material and gentamicin is loaded in the mesoporous structure. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for preparing mesoporous-microporous halloysite-montmorillonite composite aerogel material as mentioned above.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: In the step (i), adding 1-10 pts. wt. halloysite nanotubes into a mixed solution of water and ethanol for ultrasonic dispersion for 1 hour, adding 1-10 pts. wt. sodium styrene sulfonate, 0.1-1 pts. wt. polydivinylsiloxane, 0.01-0.1 pts. wt. initiator, 0.1-5 pts. wt. dexamethasone into a mixed solution of water and ethanol, stirring for 10-60 minutes, adding the solution into the halloysite dispersion, ultrasonically dispersing for 30 minutes, vacuuming, maintaining the vacuum for 1 hour, carrying out polymerization reaction by pre-polymerizing in a water bath at 70-80 degrees C for 30-60 minutes and polymerizing in a constant temperature water bath at 40-60 degrees C for 12-24 hours and polymerizing separately in a constant temperature water bath at 80 degrees C, 90 degrees C, and 100 degrees C for 2-8 hours. In the step (ii), drying montmorillonite at 60-180 degrees C for 5-20 hours, mixing 0.1-10 pts. wt. dried montmorillonite with a solvent to obtain montmorillonite dispersion having a mass fraction of 0.1-20 wt.%, mechanically stirring the montmorillonite dispersion at 5-35 degrees C for 4-10 hours at a speed of 150-400 rotations/minute and ultrasonically treating for 6-15 hours with 50-300 W power. In the step (iii), mixing 1-10 pts. wt. dispersion 1 and 1-30 pts. wt. dispersion 2 with 0.5-15 pts. wt. gentamicin solution having a mass fraction of 0.05-15 wt.%, dispersing in 10-1000 pts. wt. dispersion medium, adding 0.01-1 pts. wt. initiator, stirring for 1 hour, carrying out polymerization reaction by pre-polymerizing in a water bath at 70-90 degrees C for 30-60 minutes and polymerizing in a constant temperature water bath at 30-50 degrees C for 12-24 hours, sequentially polymerizing in a constant temperature water bath at 80 degrees C, 90 degrees C and 100 degrees C for 2-8 hours, respectively, freeze-drying at -5 degrees C to -108 degrees C for 2-48 hours at a humidity of 0-60 degrees C with the degree of vacuum of 10-50000 pa and carrying out supercritical drying for 2-4 hours, preferably 3 hours. Preferred Components: The initiator is dibenzoyl peroxide or azobisisobutyronitrile.
DC B07 (General - tablets, dispensers, catheters (excluding drainage and angioplasty), encapsulation etc, but not systems for administration of blood or saline or IV feeding etc.); B01 (Steroids - including systems containing carbocyclic and/or heterocyclic rings fused onto the basic steroidal ring structure.); B03 (Other heterocyclics.); ; A18 (Addition polymers in general.)

   USE - The annulus protection fluid is used for                high-temperature and high-acid oil-gas well                (claimed).

TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The protection fluid                  comprises 15-28 wt.% corrosion inhibitor, 3-5                  wt.% pH regulator, 2-8 wt.% deoxidizer and                  balanced water. The corrosion inhibitor is                  sulfur-containing amide imidazoline quaternary                  ammonium salt. The pH regulator is                  cyclohexylamine, sodium fumarate and/or sodium                  citrate. The deoxidizer is acetaldehyde oxime                  and/or butyraldehyde oxime. The water comprises                  50000-10000 mg/L sodium ion, 50-100 mg/L                  potassium ion, 200-1000 mg/L magnesium (II) ion,                  200-1000 mg/L calcium (II) ion, 5000-20000 mg/L                  chlorine ion and 2000-5000 mg/L sulfate ion.                  Preferred Method: The protection fluid is                  prepared by mixing water and corrosion inhibitor,                  adding deoxidizer and pH regulator and                  mixing.

TI Waterproof corrosion-resistant agent for concrete, comprises ferric ammonium sulfate, glacial acetic acid, 8-hydroxyquinoline, and sodium pyrosulfate.
AU ZHANG Y
AE ZHANG Y (ZHAN-Individual)
   ZHANG Y (ZHAN-Individual)
   ZHANG Y (ZHAN-Individual)
   ZHANG Y (ZHAN-Individual)
   ZHANG Y (ZHAN-Individual)
GA 201939656G
AB    NOVELTY - A waterproof corrosion-resistant agent comprises 1-60 pts. wt. ferric ammonium sulfate, 1-30 pts. wt. glacial acetic acid, 1-40 pts. wt. 8-hydroxyquinoline, and 1-50 pts. wt. sodium pyrosulfate.
   USE - Waterproof corrosion-resistant agent for concrete.
   ADVANTAGE - The waterproof corrosion-resistant agent produces a ferric hydroxide colloid at an appropriate time during cement hydration, to block micro-gaps, so as to achieve excellent waterproof performance and an ability to resist corrosive medium such as chloride ions, sulfate ions, and carbon dioxide, and to significantly increase service life of concrete.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The function of ferric ammonium sulfate is to introduce ferric ions (trivalent) in the form of ferric chloride, ferric ammonium sulfate, ferric sulfate, and/or polyferric sulfate. The function of sodium pyrosulfate is to introduce pyrosulfate ions in the form of sodium pyrosulfate alone or in combination.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The function of glacial acetic acid is to introduce acetate ions in the form of glacial acetic acid alone or in combination. The function of 8-hydroxyquinoline is to introduce oxygen and nitrogen element ligands, in the form of 8-hydroxyquinoline and/or methyl 8-hydroxyquinoline.

PN CN109486597-A
TI Preparing foaming wine involves cooking rice, cooling rice to first temperature, adding yeast into rice to obtain rice containing koji, fermenting rice containing koji, brewing by rice to obtain wine liquid, and separating wine liquid.
AU CAO J
AE SHAOXING BABAIYUE WINE CO LTD (SHAO-Non-standard)
GA 201926869R
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing foaming wine involves cooking rice, cooling rice to first temperature, adding yeast into rice to obtain rice containing koji, fermenting rice containing koji, brewing by rice to obtain wine liquid, separating wine liquid, then sterilizing wine, filling and adding carbon dioxide gas to obtain foaming wine.
   USE - Method for preparing foaming wine.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to prepare foaming wine that has high frequency, and does not sense oversweet.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - FOOD - Preferred Process: The method includes soaking rice for predetermined period of time based on the weather temperature. The rice containing rice koji is fermented in first open container for first predetermined period of time, and then rice containing rice koji after fermentation in the first open container is passed in the second open container for a second predetermined period of time, where volume of the first open container is greater than the volume of the second open container. The wine liquid separation involves squeezing the wine liquid separation. The foaming wine does not contain sugar, acid or other additives. Preferred Condition: The predetermined time period is 5-15 days. The first temperature is 34-45 degrees C. The first predetermined time period is 5-15 days. The second predetermined time period is 5-15 days. The liquor is sterlized to 80-100 degrees C for 10-30 minutes. Preferred Composition: The amount of the yeast is 0.03-0.05 %. Preferred Components: The foaming wine comprises rice, water and koji, and carbon dioxide. The rice comprises rice, non-glutinous rice,long-shaped rice, or rice.

TI Treating electroplating cyanide-containing            wastewater involves placing electroplating            cyanide-containing wastewater with external air,            performing acid treatment and plating waste water with            cyanogens in carbonate or bicarbonate.
AU MAI J
   JIANG D
   MO M
   FAN Y
   LIU L
   ZENG X
   QIN G
   LI Y
AE GUANGDONG XINDAYU ENVIRONMENT ENG CO LTD (GUAN-Non-standard)
GA 201859668B
AB    NOVELTY - Treating electroplating cyanide-containing                wastewater involves placing electroplating                cyanide-containing wastewater with external air,                performing acid treatment, plating waste water with                cyanogens in carbonate or bicarbonate, which is                converted to carbonate and/or carbon dioxide,                aerating cyanogens-containing waste water by                secondary acid treatment, obtaining gas and                supplying oxygen in stripping to obtain purified                water and gas II.
   USE - Method for treating electroplating                cyanide-containing wastewater.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to reduce insoluble toxic                blowing cuprous cyanide and improve blowing effect                of cyanide in effective manner.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Compositions: The gas-liquid ratio                  during aeration process is 1:(10-300), preferably                  1:(50-150) and volume percentage of carbon                  dioxide in gas I is 10-95 vol.%, preferably,                  75-90 vol.%. Preferred Components: The                  electroplating cyanide-containing wastewater                  comprises washing cyanide-containing wastewater                  produced by washing cyanide-containing plated                  portion. The acidification and secondary                  acidification treatment are independent of each                  other. The inclusion includes addition of                  non-volatile and non-oxidizing acid, which                  comprises sulfuric acid or phosphoric acid.                  Preferred Process: The electroplating                  cyanide-containing waste water treatment method                  further involves removing cyanide in gas II and                  gas III with absorption liquid, where absorption                  liquid comprises sodium hydroxide aqueous                  solution. Removing hydrogen cyanide involves                  removing water mist in gas II, condensing,                  separating gas in hydrogen cyanide and obtaining                  cyanide liquid which is absorbed by sodium                  hydroxide solution. Treating cyanide-containing                  wastewater further involves returning gas III,                  performing aeration on electroplating                  cyanide-containing wastewater, mixing gas III                  with gas I and oxygen and electroplating                  cyanide-containing wastewater, where it is                  performed under isolating external air condition.                  Preferred Conditions: The pH of                  cyanide-containing wastewater after acidification                  treatment is from 3-6, where aeration is achieved                  by pressurization and supplemented by agitation.                  The diameter of bubble during aeration process is                  0.1-6 mm. Isolating external air is performed                  under conditions of insulating outside air, where                  pH of electroplating cyanide-containing                  wastewater after sub-acidification treatment is                  0.5-3.0, preferably, manner of stripping includes                  gas-liquid countercurrent contact for stripping.                  The oxygen supplementation includes                  pressurization, preferably, volume percentage of                  oxygen in mixed gas obtained by supplementing                  oxygen in gas I is 0.1-15%, preferably, 5-10%.                  The gas-liquid ratio during stripping process is                  1:(200-2000), preferably, 1:(500-800). The gas                  pressure during stripping is (-)0.1-0 megaPascal.                  The temperature of gas during stripping is                  26-100&#176; C, preferably, 30-50&#176;                  C, where stripping is carried out in stripping                  tower and height-to-diameter ratio of de-column                  is 3-8.

   USE - New amine-based ester polymer compound.

   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An amine-based ester polymer compound of formula (I) is new.
   R1,R2 = -CH3, -CH2CH3 or -CH2(CH3)2.
   An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for preparation of the polymer compound.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Component: The solvent is pure water.

The anionic emulsifier is one of sodium dodecyl sulfate, sodium lauryl sulfate, and sodium dodecylbenzenesulfonate. PN CN108715963-A
TI Magnesium alloy comprises yttrium, neodymium, zirconium, other impurities, and balance is magnesium, whole process is protected by mixed gas of carbon dioxide and sulfur hexafluoride in smelting process in order to prevent oxidation.
AU WANG X
   WANG P
   WANG Q
   WANG S
   LIANG S
   PENG Q
   WANG F
AE SHANDONG YINGUANG YUYUAN LIGHT METAL PRE (SHAN-Non-standard)
GA 2018863410
AB    NOVELTY - Magnesium alloy comprises 2.5-5.0 wt.% yttrium, 2.0-4.0 wt.% neodymium, 0.1-0.8 wt.% zirconium, 0.8 wt.% other impurities, and balance is magnesium.
   USE - Magnesium alloy.
   ADVANTAGE - The magnesium alloy has excellent mechanical properties, high temperature resistance, simple and feasible preparation process, and broad application prospect.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for preparing magnesium alloy, which involves smelting, heat treatment, and extrusion deformation to obtain final product.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The magnesium alloy comprises 3.8 wt.% yttrium, 2.4 wt.%neodymium, 0.2 wt.% zirconium, 0.6 wt.% other impurity elements, and the balance is magnesium. Preferred Conditions: The whole process is protected by a mixed gas of carbon dioxide (CO2) and SF6 in the smelting process in order to prevent oxidation and combustion of the magnesium liquid, and the volume ratio of CO2 to SF6 is 99:1. Preferred Process: The magnesium alloy is prepared by taking pure magnesium ingot, magnesium-niobium intermediate alloy, magnesium-niobium intermediate alloy and magnesium-zirconium intermediate alloy, preheating, preheating temperature is 150-200 degrees C. The magnesium ingot is smelted to obtain melt, allowed to stand for 30-40 minutes. The magnesium-niobium intermediate alloy is added at 700-760 degrees C, and the magnesium-zirconium intermediate alloy is added after the magnesium-niobium intermediate alloy is melted.The magnesium-zirconium intermediate alloy is melted, the magnesium-niobium intermediate alloy is added, after the alloy is completely melted, the refining is carried out 10-15 minutes, surface scum is removed, maintained temperature at 700-730 degrees C, kept for 10 minutes to get the alloy casting liquid, and then casted to obtain the ingot. The ingot is subjected to heat treatment, the heat treatment temperature is 450-550 degrees C, the heat preservation is 8-12 hours, and the Sulfur hexafluoride (SF6) gas is used for protection, and the air is naturally cooled after the heat treatment to obtain a blank. The heat-treated billet is placed in a heating furnace, the preheating temperature is 400 plus minus 20 degrees C, the temperature is kept for 1-2 hours, the temperature of the extrusion cylinder is set at 420 plus minus 20 degrees C, and the extrusion die is heated. The temperature is 420 plus minus 20 degrees C, and the temperature is 1-2 hours. The extrusion speed is controlled at 5-10 mm/seconds to obtain a magnesium alloy.

   XUE D

AE UNIV KING ABDULLAH SCI & TECHNOLOGY (UYIK-C)
GA 201860312Q
AB    NOVELTY - Metal-organic framework composition comprises hexanuclear clusters including a rare earth metal ion. The hexanuclear cluster associates with 12 hexanuclear clusters to form a metal-organic framework with face centered cubic (fcu).
   USE - The metal-organic framework composition is useful in gas sequestration, storage and separation or scrubbing.
   ADVANTAGE - Metal-organic frameworks having tunable structures are provided. The metal-organic frameworks have high localized charge density, and can promote/enhance carbon dioxide (CO2) sorption energetics.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The rare earth metal ion comprises cerium, dysprosium, erbium, europium, gadolinium, holmium, lanthanum, lutetium, neodymium, praseodymium, promethium, samarium, scandium, terbium, thulium, ytterbium, and yttrium.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The hexanuclear clusters are characterized by (RE6(((( mu )3-OH)8(O2C-)6N4C-)6N4-)6), or (RE16(( mu )3-OH)8(O2C-)12).RE1=Y or Tb.The hexanuclear clusters are associated by a bidentate ligand. The bidentate ligand includes a carboxylate, tetrazolate, triazolate, and pyrazolate. It includes 2-fluoro-4-(1H-tetrazol-5-yl)benzoate (FTZB), 4-(1H-tetrazol-5-yl)benzoate (TZB), 3-fluoro-4'-(2H-tetrazol-5-yl)biphenyl-4-carboxylate (FTZBP), 3-fluorobiphenyl-4,4'-dicarboxylate (FBPDC), or 3,3'-difluorobiphenyl-4,4'-dicarboxylate (DFBPDC). The bidentate ligand has 2 anionic binding groups. The 2 anionic binding groups are the same or different. The 2 anionic binding groups are linked by an aromatic group or hydrophobic group. The hexanuclear clusters associate to form a metal-organic framework with fcu topology.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

   USE - Method for preparing organic zinc catalyst.

   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for preparing polyalkylene carbonate resin, which involves polymerizing monomer including epoxide and carbon dioxide under the presence of organic zinc catalyst.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The alcohol solvent is selected from 1-decanol, 1-decanol, 1-dodecanol, 1-tridecanol, 1-tetradecanol, 1-pentadecanol, 1-Hexadecanol, 1-heptadecanol, 1-Octadecanol, 1-nonadecanol, 1-eicosanol, 1-docosanol and 1-tricosanol. The dicarboxylic acid is selected from malonic acid, succinic acid, glutaric acid, adipic acid and pimelic acid. Preferred Composition: The amount of dicarboxylic acid is 0.8-2 moles per 1 mole of the zinc precursor. Preferred Condition: The reaction temperature is 20-110 degrees C for 1-20 hours.

PT P
PN CN107555465-A
TI Preparation method of nitrate intercalated copper-aluminum type hydrotalcite, involves taking copper nitrate trihydrate and aluminum nitrate nonahydrate as raw material, sodium hydroxide as precipitator, and deionized water as solvent.
AU WU X
   CI C
   HOU B
   KANG W
   CHENG J
   XIE X
AE UNIV TAIYUAN TECHNOLOGY (UYTL-C)
GA 201804824Y
AB    NOVELTY - The preparation method of nitrate intercalated copper-aluminum type hydrotalcite involves taking copper nitrate trihydrate and aluminum nitrate nonahydrate as raw material, sodium hydroxide as precipitator, and deionized water as solvent, preparing the solution, titrating, coprecipitating, hydrothermally treating, washing, suction-filtering, and drying.
   USE - Preparation method of nitrate intercalated copper-aluminum type hydrotalcite (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables preparation of nitrate intercalated copper-aluminum type hydrotalcite, and effectively avoids the influence of environmentally-dissolved carbon dioxide to form carbonate ions.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The nitrate intercalated copper-aluminum type hydrotalcite is prepared by (1) mixing sodium acetate solution and acetic acid solution in buffer solution with pH value of 4-5, and controlling the concentration of the sodium acetate to 0.036-0.140 mol/l and the concentration of acetic acid to 0.060-0.164 mol/l to prepare acetic acid-sodium acetate buffer solution, (2) weighing copper nitrate trihydrate and aluminum nitrate nonahydrate in a beaker, adding deionized water to the beaker, stirring with a magnetic stirrer for 5-15 minutes to completely dissolve, and transferring the prepared solution to a hanging-bottle type infusion apparatus, (3) weighing sodium hydroxide solid, preparing into a sodium hydroxide aqueous solution with concentration of 1 mol/l, and transferring to the hanging-bottle type infusion apparatus, (4) placing the prepared acetic acid-sodium acetate buffer solution in a reactor, placing the reactor in a constant temperature water bath at 30-60 degrees C, continuously stirring, dripping the sodium hydroxide aqueous solution and mixed salt solution into the reactor filled with buffer solution, monitoring the pH value in the titration process by acidity meter in real-time, controlling the dripping speed so that the pH value is fixed in the whole reaction process, continuously stirring for 20-30 minutes, sealing the obtained precipitate and mother liquid sealed in a PTFE-lined stainless steel reactor, and heating in oven at 90-150 degrees C for 3-48 hours, (5) cooling the reacted turbid solution to room temperature, placing the filter bottle in a Buchner funnel at an upper portion of a suction-filter bottle, suction-filtering, retaining the product filter cake on the filter paper, and pumping the solution into the filter bottle, (6) placing the product filter cake in a beaker, adding 100-200 ml deionized water, stirring the mixture, washing for 5-10 minutes, placing the washing solution in the Buchner funnel, filtering, retaining the product filter cake on the filter paper, pumping the solution into the filter bottle, washing with deionized water, and filtering repeatedly until the filtrate is neutral, and (7) placing the filtered product in a surface vessel, placing the mixture into drying oven, and drying at 80-100 degrees C for 12-24 hours. Preferred Composition: In the step (2), the concentration ratio of copper nitrate and aluminum nitrate in the mixed salt solution is 2:1-4:1. In the step (2), the concentration of aluminum nitrate in the mixed salt solution is controlled to be 0.10-1 mol/l. Preferred Properties: In step (4), the pH value of the system in the reaction process is controlled at 4-5.

AU KANG B
AE XINHUA ZHONGXIN CERAMICS CO LTD (XINH-Non-standard)
GA 2022472954
AB    NOVELTY - Manufacturing high breaking ceramic fuse                ceramic body comprises (i) taking 80-100 pts. wt.                alumina, 1-3 pts. wt. talc, 1-2 pts. wt. calcium                carbonate, 1-2 pts. wt. kaolin, 1-2 pts. wt.                vanadium zirconium yellow, 1-2 pts. wt. titanium                dioxide, 2-4 pts. wt. zirconium silicate, 6-10 pts.                wt. carbon powder, and 1-1.5 pts. wt. sintering                auxiliary agent, and drying, (ii) adding raw                materials except alumina into ball mill, adding                water and ball milling medium, ball milling and                drying, (iii) adding alumina, first powder, and                oleic acid into ball mill, and continuously ball                milling, and (iv) mixing second powder with white                wax and beeswax, mixing and granulating, tableting,                performing supercritical carbon dioxide fluid                dewaxing on blank, performing short-term thermal                dewaxing, cleaning and trimming, loading blank into                high-temperature kiln for sintering, introducing                mixed gas composed of nitrogen and oxygen as                sintering gas, and reducing temperature and                discharging kiln.
   USE - The method is useful for manufacturing high                breaking ceramic fuse ceramic body, which is useful                in electric appliance element installed in circuit,                ensuring circuit safety when circuit is fault or                abnormal, along with current rising                continuously.
   ADVANTAGE - The ceramic body: has good mechanical                property, and high heat conducting coefficient;                releases the heat energy in the ceramic body in                time, when the fuse works; and improves the                breaking capacity of the ceramic fuse.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Manufacturing high breaking ceramic fuse                ceramic body comprises (i) taking 80-100 pts. wt.                alumina, 1-3 pts. wt. talc, 1-2 pts. wt. calcium                carbonate, 1-2 pts. wt. kaolin, 1-2 pts. wt.                vanadium zirconium yellow, 1-2 pts. wt. titanium                dioxide, 2-4 pts. wt. zirconium silicate, 6-10 pts.                wt. carbon powder, and 1-1.5 pts. wt. sintering                auxiliary agent, and drying, (ii) adding raw                materials except alumina into the ball mill, adding                water and ball milling medium, ball milling for                15-18 hours and drying to obtain the first powder,                (iii) adding alumina, first powder, and oleic acid                into ball mill, continuously ball milling for 18-20                hours to obtain second powder, and (iv) mixing                second powder with white wax and beeswax, mixing                and granulating, tableting to obtain blank, firstly                performing supercritical carbon dioxide fluid                dewaxing on blank, performing short-term thermal                dewaxing, cleaning and trimming, loading the blank                into high-temperature kiln for sintering, primary                sintering at 1200-1300degrees Celsius for 1-1.5                hours, introducing nitrogen as sintering gas,                finally sintering at 1650-1700degrees Celsius for                1-2 hours, introducing mixed gas composed of                nitrogen and oxygen in volume ratio of 1-3:1-3 as                sintering gas, completing sintering, and reducing                temperature and discharging kiln.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The sintering                  auxiliary agent comprises calcium fluoride and                  lithium carbonate. The ball milling medium                  comprises bicarbonate, preferably ammonium                  bicarbonate.

AE NINGBO AGRIC TECH PROMOTION CENT (NING-Non-standard)
GA 202235703N
AB    NOVELTY - Evaluating soil activity comprises obtaining                the soil to be detected, obtaining the DNA of                microorganisms in the soil to be detected by soil                metagenomics technology, detecting extracted                microbial DNA and construct a DNA library, entering                functional gene identification step, identifying                the DNA sequences in DNA library by using                microarray technology, obtaining soil functional                gene information, entering carbon element                monitoring step, acquiring the soil to be detected                in carbon element monitoring step, measuring the                change amount of carbon dioxide in soil by a soil                respiration analyzer to obtain microbial                respiration data, determining the biological                nitrogen cycle data by detecting the change of free                15N, and entering the activity analysis step,                acquiring soil functional gene information,                microbial respiration data and biological nitrogen                cycle data in activity analysis step, and                calculating the soil activity information according                to an activity calculation algorithm.
   USE - The method is useful for evaluating soil                activity.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is simple and economical and                determines the total amount of soil participating                in carbon circulation and nitrogen circulation and                obtain soil activity information.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Evaluating soil activity comprises obtaining                the soil to be detected, obtaining the DNA of the                microorganisms in the soil to be detected by soil                metagenomics technology, detecting the extracted                microbial DNA and construct a DNA library, entering                the functional gene identification step, recording identification step, recording the coding                genes of microorganisms involved in carbon cycle                reactions as carbon cycle benchmark genes,                arranging the nitrogen cycle benchmark gene and the                carbon cycle benchmark gene on the surface of the                carrier in a large-scale array to form a gene chip,                identifying the DNA sequences in the DNA library by                using microarray technology, obtaining soil                functional gene information, and entering the                carbon element monitoring step, acquiring the soil                to be detected in the carbon element monitoring                step, measuring the change amount of carbon dioxide                in the soil to be determined by a soil respiration                analyzer to obtain microbial respiration data, and                entering the nitrogen element monitoring step,                obtaining the DNA of the microorganisms in the soil                to be detected in the nitrogen element monitoring                step, determining the induction substrate according                to the soil functional gene information, using the                induction substrate of 15N radiolabel to induce the                DNA of the soil microorganism to be detected for                expression, determining the biological nitrogen                cycle data by detecting the change of free 15N, and                entering the activity analysis step, acquiring the                soil functional gene information, microbial                respiration data and biological nitrogen cycle data                in the activity analysis step, and calculating the                soil activity information according to an activity                calculation algorithm.

PT P
PN CN113142527-A
TI Preparation of high-dilution ratio pre-packaged            freeze-dried beef noodle soup by preparing            water-soluble refined spice powder, mixing with e.g.            concentrated beef broth, stirring, mixing with e.g.            cooked beef slices, and pre-freezing.
AU JIANG J
   LIU J
   WEI K
   WANG X
   LIU X
   CAI J X
AE GANSU LONGCUITANG NUTRITION HEALTH FOOD (GANS-Non-standard)
GA 202191304R
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of high-dilution ratio                pre-packaged freeze-dried beef noodle soup includes                weighing spices, mixing, crushing, subjecting to                supercritical carbon dioxide extraction, adding                extract to core material, and mixing to obtain                water-soluble refined spice powder; mixing beef,                beef stick bones and chicken bone in boiling water,                boiling, taking out, and concentrating; mixing                water-soluble refined spice powder, table salt,                chicken essence, monosodium glutamate and                concentrated beef broth, and stirring to obtain                high-dilution beef noodle soup; and mixing fresh                garlic sprouts, fresh coriander, cooked beef                slices, cooked radish and high-dilution beef noodle                soup, pre-freezing, and heating.
   USE - The method is for preparation of high-dilution                ratio pre-packaged freeze-dried beef noodle                soup.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to prepare pre-packaged                beef noodle freeze-dried soup block, which has good                taste, improves the pre-packaged beef noodle soup                taste reducing degree, and solves the problem that                the beef soup material gram weight is too large and                is not convenient for sale and                transportation.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparation of high-dilution ratio                pre-packaged freeze-dried beef noodle soup                comprises:
   (A) weighing spices comprising 8-5 pts. wt.                dried ginger, 7-4 pts. wt. pepper, 5-2 pts. wt.                amomum tsaoko, 1-2 pts. wt. cinnamon seeds, 3-1                pts. wt. white pepper, 1-2 pts. wt. licorice and                4-2 pts. wt. fennel, mixing, crushing into 80 mesh                powder, subjecting to supercritical carbon dioxide                extraction, adding extract to 40 wt.% core                material, and mixing to obtain water-soluble                refined spice powder;
   (B) mixing 4-2 pts. wt. beef, 1-2 pts. wt.                beef stick bones and 1-2 pts. wt. chicken bone in                30 pts. wt. boiling water, boiling under normal                pressure for 1-3 hours, taking out, and                concentrating under normal pressure until amino                acid nitrogen content is 0.4 g/100 g;
   (C) mixing 1-2 pts. wt. water-soluble refined                spice powder, 10-15 pts. wt. table salt, 2-4 pts.                wt. chicken essence, 2-4 pts. wt. monosodium                glutamate and 40-80 pts. wt. concentrated beef                broth, and stirring to obtain high-dilution beef                noodle soup; and
   (D) mixing 1-2 pts. wt. fresh garlic sprouts,                1-2 pts. wt. fresh coriander, 1-2 pts. wt. cooked                beef slices, 1-2 pts. wt. cooked radish and 2-5                pts. wt. high-dilution beef noodle soup,                pre-freezing at - 40 degrees C for 6-8 hours,                heating at cold hydrazine temperature of - 40-50                degrees C and at pressure of 50 Pa, heating at                heating plate temperature of 10 degrees C for 0-6                hours, increasing temperature to 50 degrees C for                7-10 hours, and increasing temperature to 80                degrees C for 11-15 hours.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - FOOD - Preferred Components: The core material                  comprises soybean isolated protein and                  maltodextrin at weight ratio of 1:1. Preferred                  Method: The extraction is done at 50-60 degrees                  C, pressure of 15-20 MPa and carbon dioxide flow                  speed of 1.5 L/minute.

PT P
PN US10913911-B1
TI Treatment composition useful for remediating            hydrogen sulfide and other contaminant(s) in            contaminated gases, comprises aqueous hydroxide            solution containing hydroxide compound, organic acid            comprising fulvic acid and humic acid, and chelating            agent.
AU ROE C L
AE GAPS TECHNOLOGY LLC (GAPS-Non-standard)
GA 202116943K
AB    NOVELTY - Treatment composition comprises: an aqueous                hydroxide solution containing at least one                hydroxide compound at a collective concentration of                35-55 wt.% of the aqueous hydroxide solution; at                least one organic acid comprising fulvic acid and                humic acid; and a chelating agent, where the                aqueous hydroxide solution constitutes at least 80                wt.% of the treatment composition, the organic acid                constitutes 0.1-5 wt.% of the treatment                composition, the chelating agent constitutes 0.1-5                wt.% of the treatment composition, and a pH of the                treatment composition is at least 12.
   USE - The composition is useful for remediating                hydrogen sulfide and other contaminant(s) in                contaminated gases.
   ADVANTAGE - The composition rapidly remediated down to                very low levels that have been deemed safe in a                practical, efficient and economical manner.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included                for:
   (a) a treatment process for remediating                hydrogen sulfide and other contaminants in a                contaminated containing more than 1 ppm water and                ionic contaminants, comprising initially treating                the gas in a water wash to reduce collective                content of the ionic contaminants in gas below 1                ppm and then treating the gas to reduce water                content in the gas below 1 ppm, and then                remediating the hydrogen sulfide and other                contaminants in the gas by flowing the gas at a                velocity of less than ten feet/sec. through                elongate reaction chamber while contacting the gas                with treatment composition for at least one second;                and
   (b) a treatment system for remediating                hydrogen sulfide, carbon dioxide and other                contaminants in contaminated gases, comprising a                water wash cell containing water in it and which is                configured to receive a flow of a contaminated gas                through it so that the gas contacts the water and                ionic contaminants in the gas may be transferred to                the water, a dehydrating unit through which is                configured to receive the flow of the contaminated                gas after it passes through the water wash cell and                to reduce water content of the contaminated gas to                less than one ppm, and elongate reactor having an                amount of the treatment composition in it and which                is configured to receive the flow of the                contaminated gas through it after the contaminated                gas passes through the dehydrating unit so that the                contaminated gas contacts with treatment                composition as the contaminated gas passes through                the reactor for remediating hydrogen sulfide,                carbon dioxide and other contaminants in the                contaminated gas, where a portion of the reactor is                filled with a porous, non-reactive media which is                configured to break bubbles of the contaminated gas                and interrupt flow of the contaminated gas as the                contaminated gas flows through it, and the reactor                includes at least one injector for flowing the                treatment solution into the reactor so that the                treatment composition saturates or substantially                saturates the media.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The composition                  further comprises at least one of a surfactant                  and a buffering agent.Preferred Components: The chelating agent                  includes ethylenediaminetetraacetic acid (EDTA),                  and the hydroxide compound(s) includes potassium                  hydroxide. The hydroxide compound at a collective                  concentration of 45-55 wt.% of the aqueous                  hydroxide solution and the aqueous hydroxide                  solution constitutes at least 90 wt.% of the                  treatment composition. The gas also contains                  carbon dioxide as a contaminant and a pH of the                  gas as contacted with the treatment composition                  in the remediation step is at least 7. The gas is                  at a pressure of 25-300 PSI in the water wash and                  remediation steps. The gas is natural gas from a                  well.Preferred Method: The remediating step                  involves: filling portion of the reaction chamber                  with a porous, non-reactive media, flowing the                  treatment solution into the reaction chamber so                  that the treatment composition saturates or                  substantially saturates the media, and flowing                  the gas through the media as saturated or                  substantially saturated with the treatment                  composition, where the portion of the reaction                  chamber filled with the porous, non-reactive                  media is at least six feet long, and the                  remediation step involves flowing the gas at a                  velocity of less than or equal to five                  feet/second, while contacting the gas with the                  treatment composition for at least 1.5 seconds;                  and flowing the treatment composition into at                  least one of an upper portion and an intermediate                  portion of the reaction chamber so that the                  treatment composition flows onto and/or into the                  porous, non-reactive media while flowing the gas                  into a bottom portion of the reaction chamber so                  that the gas flows upward through the treatment                  composition as disposed with the media. In the                  method: the porous, non-reactive media as filled                  into the reaction chamber and saturated or                  substantially saturated with the treatment                  composition is configured to break bubbles of the                  gas and interrupt flow of the gas as the gas                  flows through it. The treatment process further                  comprises: withdrawing a portion of the treatment                  composition from the reaction chamber;                  dehydrating the withdrawn portion of the                  treatment composition to remove water from it;                  flowing the dehydrated treatment composition back                  into the reaction chamber; and compressing the                  natural gas, directly after being remediated in                  the remediation step, into liquefied petroleum                  gas.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INSTRUMENTATION AND TESTING - Preferred Components: The portion of the                  reactor filled with the porous, non-reactive                  media is at least six feet long, and the reactor                  is configured to receive the flow of the                  contaminated gas through it at 25-300 PSI. The                  system further comprises another dehydrating unit                  which is configured to receive a portion of the                  treatment composition withdrawn from the reactor,                  dehydrate the withdrawn portion of the treatment                  composition to remove water from it for reuse in                  the reactor. In the system, elongate reactor                  includes a baffle disposed near an exit of the                  reactor and which is configured to be contacted                  by the remediated contaminated gas before the                  remediated contaminated gas is discharged from                  the reactor such that droplets of liquid in the                  remediated contaminated gas may be separated from                  the remediated contaminated gas and stay within                  the reactor, where a portion of the water wash                  cell is also filled with a porous, non-reactive                  media which is configured to break bubbles of the                  contaminated gas and interrupt flow of the gas as                  the contaminated gas flows through it.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

PT P
PN CN111037687-A
TI Anti-corrosion treatment of wood board for home decoration, involves treating wood with antiseptic solution prepared using sodium tripolyphosphate, drying, treating with solution containing e.g. prothioconazole and tebuconazole, and heating.
AU WANG X
AE ANHUI WEIMA DATA TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (ANHU-Non-standard)
GA 202034677K
AB    NOVELTY - Anti-corrosion treatment of wood board involves preparing antiseptic solution (A) using sodium tripolyphosphate, adding prothioconazole, tebuconazole, and pepper extract to methanol, stirring to obtain antiseptic impregnating solution (B), placing wood in a high-pressure tank, evacuating to 0.08-0.1 MPa, injecting a predetermined amount of solution (A) into the high-pressure tank, blowing nitrogen, slowly increasing the pressure to 1.5-2 MPa, holding pressure for 30-50 minutes, releasing the pressure to restore the normal pressure, maintaining at -0.1 MPa for 2-3 hours, blowing nitrogen, maintaining for 5-10 hours, removing, drying for 4-6 days, placing the wood in a treatment tank, injecting the solution (B) into the storage tank, supplying supercritical carbon dioxide, placing treated wood in oven, heating to 40-45 degrees C for 10-15 hours, heating to 100-120 degrees C for 1-3 days, heating to 140-150 degrees C for 2-6 days, further heating to 160-180 degrees C for 10-20 hours, and cooling to room temperature.
   USE - Anti-corrosion treatment of wood board for home decoration.
   ADVANTAGE - The wood board having excellent durability and decay resistance can be produced.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Anti-corrosion treatment of wood board involves adding sodium tripolyphosphate to deionized water, stirring, adding chitosan to concentrated acetic acid solution, stirring, slowly adding sodium tripolyphosphate solution under mechanical stirring at 50-60 rpm and room temperature for 30 minutes, performing microfiltration to obtain nano chitosan solution, adding copper chloride, heating to 40-50 degrees C, reacting for 2-5 hours to obtain a nano-chitosan chelate solution, adding nano-copper oxide and triammonium citrate to the deionized water, dispersing by ultrasonic vibration for 10-20 minutes, adding and mixing the chelate solution, uniformly stirring to produce antiseptic impregnating solution (A), adding prothioconazole, tebuconazole, and pepper extract to methanol, stirring to obtain antiseptic impregnating solution (B), placing wood in a high-pressure tank, evacuating to 0.08-0.1 MPa, extracting the air remained in the wood, injecting a predetermined amount of solution (A) into the high-pressure tank, blowing nitrogen, slowly increasing the pressure to 1.5-2 MPa, holding pressure for 30-50 minutes, releasing the pressure to restore the normal pressure in the high pressure tank, maintaining at -0.1 MPa for 2-3 hours, blowing nitrogen to restore the normal pressure, maintaining for 5-10 hours, removing, drying for 4-6 days, placing the wood in a treatment tank, injecting the solution (B) into the storage tank, supplying supercritical carbon dioxide at a predetermined temperature and pressure, placing treated wood in oven, heating to 40-45 degrees C for 10-15 hours, heating to 100-120 degrees C for 1-3 days, heating to 140-150 degrees C for 2-6 days, further heating to 160-180 degrees C three times for 10-20 hours, and cooling to room temperature.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The concentration of the acetic acid solution is 0.5-1 %mass. The concentration of the sodium tripolyphosphate solution is 12-18 %mass. The concentration of the copper chloride solution is 10-15 %mass. The mass ratio of nano copper oxide and triammonium citrate is 40-50:1.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: mass ratio of prothioconazole, tebuconazole, and pepper extract is 10-15:4-6:1. The mass ratio of anti-corrosion impregnating solution (A) to wood is 50-80:1.

PT P
PN CN110902915-A; CN110902915-B
TI Treating wastewater involves pre-treating the waste water containing solid suspended matter and organic matter discharged from the biomass gasification process to obtain raw wastewater solution, subjecting to vacuum distillation.
AU XIE J
   CHEN C
   YIN X
   WU C
AE GUANGZHOU ENERGY INST CONVERSION CHINESE (CAEC-C)
GA 202024149R
AB    NOVELTY - Treating wastewater involves pre-treating the waste water containing solid suspended matter and organic matter discharged from the biomass gasification process to obtain a raw wastewater solution, subjecting to vacuum distillation under reduced pressure, obtaining steamed residue and clear liquid I, mixing and passing the steamed residue and biomass raw materials to a gasification furnace for gasification. The precipitant is added into clear solution I, pH is adjusted to 11-13 by adding alkali, the reaction is stirred at room temperature and allowed to stand to obtain a phenol-containing precipitate and clear solution II. The phenolic precipitate is dissolved by adding acid to recover the phenolic compounds in precipitate, followed by adjusting the pH value of clear liquid II to 2-4, adding an oxidizing agent to oxidize, and oxidizing the organic matter in clear liquid II to carbon dioxide and water without selectivity, adjusting 7 pH of the wastewater after oxidation treatment.
   USE - Method for treating wastewater.
   ADVANTAGE - The method first adopts vacuum distillation to clean the waste water, resulting clear solution uses a chemical precipitant to precipitate phenolic substances, and uses advanced oxidation methods to oxidize and remove the organic matter in the wastewater, and then the activated carbon adsorption method is used to physically absorb the remaining organic matter in the wastewater, thus the wastewater can be discharged to the standard.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Treating wastewater involves pre-treating the waste water containing solid suspended matter and organic matter discharged from the biomass gasification process to obtain a raw wastewater solution, subjecting to vacuum distillation under reduced pressure, obtaining steamed residue and clear liquid I, mixing and passing the steamed residue and biomass raw materials to a gasification furnace for gasification. The precipitant is added into clear solution I, pH is adjusted to 11-13 by adding alkali, the reaction is stirred at room temperature and allowed to stand to obtain a phenol-containing precipitate and clear solution II. The phenolic precipitate is dissolved by adding acid to recover the phenolic compounds in precipitate, followed by adjusting the pH value of clear liquid II to 2-4, adding an oxidizing agent to oxidize, and oxidizing the organic matter in clear liquid II to carbon dioxide and water without selectivity, adjusting 7 pH of the wastewater after oxidation treatment, using an amount of wastewater as the recycling water of biomass gasification process, and the other amount adopting physical adsorption of the remaining organic matter in the wastewater to make adsorption treatment after the wastewater reaches standard discharge. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a device for treating biomass gasification wastewater, which comprises depressurizing distilling reactor, chemicals edimentation tank, advanced oxidation pool, neutralization pool and physical adsorption tank.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic diagram of the device for treating biomass gasification wastewater.
   Depressurizing distilling reactor (1)
   Chemicals edimentation tank (2)
   Advanced oxidation pool (3)
   Neutralization pool (4)
   Physical adsorption tank (5)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ENVIRONMENT - Preferred Conditions: Treating wastewater involves decompressing and distilling at 80-95 degrees C temperature under -0.06 to -0.095 MPa pressure for 0.5-2.0 hours, adding calcium ion or barium ion into the clear solution I, controlling the molar ratio of metal ions to phenols to 0.5-3.0, and then adjusting the pH of solution to 11-13 with sodium hydroxide at room temperature, stirring the reaction for 0.2-1 hours and allowing to stand to obtain a phenol-containing precipitate and a clear solution II. The pH value of clear solution II is adjusted to 2-4, added with ferrous ion and hydrogen peroxide, the reaction is stirred for 0.5-8 hours, and mass ratio of hydrogen peroxide and clear solution II is 2.1-33.5 wt.%, and molar ratio of ferrous ion and hydrogen peroxide is 0.13-2.0. The organic matter in clear liquid II is non-selectively oxidized to carbon dioxide and water, and wastewater after oxidation treatment is used as the reuse water of biomass gasification process. The physical adsorbent is activated carbon, specific surface area of activated carbon is 800-1200 m2/g, and the particle size specification is 8-80 mesh.
DC D15 (Chemical or biological treatment of water, industrial waste and sewage - including purification, sterilising or testing water, scale prevention, treatment of sewage sludge, regeneration of active carbon which has been used for water treatment and impregnating water with gas e.g. CO2, but excluding plant and anti-pollution devices (C02).); E34 (Compounds of Li, Na, K, Rb, Cs, Fr.)
MC D04-A01A; D04-A01F2; D04-A01K2; D04-A01P1; D04-B06; D04-B07C; E11-Q01B; E31-E01; E31-N04C; E34-D03B; E34-D03D; E35-U05

PN CN110821707-A
TI Step coupling system used for utilizing waste heat of diesel engine based on carbon dioxide power cycle includes supercharging intercooling heat exchanger, flue gas heat exchanger, liquid storage tank and exhaust gas from the diesel engine.
AU TIAN H
   LI L
   SHU G
   SHI L
AE UNIV TIANJIN (UTIJ-C)
GA 2020180706
AB    NOVELTY - Step coupling system comprises working fluid pump (1), cylinder liner cooling water heat exchanger (2), supercharging intercooling heat exchanger (3), intermediate heat exchanger (4), exhaust gas recirculation (EGR) intercooled heat exchanger (5), flue gas heat exchanger (6), expansion and power generation integrated machine (7), condenser (8), liquid storage tank (9) and exhaust gas from the diesel engine (10). The outlet of working fluid pump is divided into first branch and second branch. The first branch is connected to the tube side inlet of cylinder liner cooling water heat exchanger. The cylinder liner cooling water heat exchanger passes through cylinder liner cooling water of diesel engine within the shell side. The second branch is connected to tube-side inlet of supercharging intercooling heat exchanger. The pressurized air of diesel engine is passed into the shell side of supercharged intercooling heat exchanger.
   USE - The step coupling system is used for utilizing waste heat of diesel engine based on carbon dioxide power cycle.
   ADVANTAGE - The system recovers waste heat of each step of diesel engine, improves efficiency of diesel engine and reduces fuel consumption.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Step coupling system comprises working fluid pump (1), cylinder liner cooling water heat exchanger (2), supercharging intercooling heat exchanger (3), intermediate heat exchanger (4), exhaust gas recirculation (EGR) intercooled heat exchanger (5), flue gas heat exchanger (6), expansion and power generation integrated machine (7), condenser (8), liquid storage tank (9) and exhaust gas from the diesel engine (10). The tube-side outlet of cylinder liner cooling water heat exchanger and tube-side outlet of the supercharged intercooling heat exchanger are merged and connected to cold-side flow channel inlet of intermediate heat exchanger. The outlet of cold-side flow passage of intermediate heat exchanger is divided into third branch and fourth branch. The third branch is connected to tube-side inlet of flue gas heat exchanger. The exhaust gas from the diesel engine is passed into shell side of flue gas heat exchanger. The fourth branch is connected to tube-side inlet of EGR intercooling heat exchanger. The exhaust gas from diesel engine is passed into the shell of EGR intercooled heat exchanger. The tube-side outlet of flue gas heat exchanger and tube-side outlet of EGR intercooling heat exchanger are merged and connected to the expansion and power generation integrated machine, hot-side runner of intermediate heat exchanger, condenser and inlet of liquid storage tank. An outlet of liquid storage tank is connected to an inlet of working fluid pump.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of step coupling system.
   Working fluid pump (1)
   Cylinder liner cooling water heat exchanger (2)
   Supercharging intercooling heat exchanger (3)
   Intermediate heat exchanger (4)
   EGR intercooled heat exchanger (5)
   Flue gas heat exchanger (6)
   Expansion and power generation integrated machine (7)
   Condenser (8)
   Liquid storage tank (9)
   Exhaust gas from the diesel engine (10)

PT P
PN CN110270234-A
TI Method for preparing graphene oxide/metal organic framework composite membrane for use in separation of hydrogen/carbon dioxide, hydrogen/nitrogen or hydrogen/methane gases, involves ultrasonically dispersing graphene oxide in solvent.
AU ZHANG G
   ZHANG X
   LI Y
   MENG Q
AE UNIV ZHEJIANG TECHNOLOGY (UYZT-C)
GA 2019825983
AB    NOVELTY - A graphene oxide/metal organic framework composite membrane preparing method involves ultrasonically dispersing the graphene oxide in the organic solvent A to obtain a graphene oxide dispersion, adding the metal salt A to the graphene oxide dispersion, and stirring and dispersing, adding a stabilizer to the dispersion and further stirring the reaction to obtain a graphene oxide/metal sol. The graphene oxide/metal sol is uniformly coated on the surface of the organic substrate to obtain a graphene oxide/metal gel modified substrate. The organic ligand, the metal salt B and the organic solvent B are mixed and stirred uniformly to obtain a precursor liquid. The graphene oxide/metal gel modified substrate is immersed in the precursor liquid obtained and the dense metal organic framework functional layer is grown by hydrothermal reaction at 80 degrees C for 12 hours, which is cooled at room temperature and dried at room temperature to obtain a graphene oxide/metal organic framework composite film.
   USE - Method for preparing graphene oxide/metal organic framework composite membrane for use in the separation of hydrogen/carbon dioxide, hydrogen/nitrogen or hydrogen/methane gases (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables achieving uniform and compact graphene oxide/metal organic framework composite membrane.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A graphene oxide/metal organic framework composite membrane preparing method involves ultrasonically dispersing the graphene oxide in the organic solvent A to obtain a 0.1-2 mg/mL graphene oxide dispersion, adding the metal salt A to the graphene oxide dispersion, and stirring and dispersing at 40-60 degrees C, adding a stabilizer to the dispersion and further stirring the reaction for 0.1-1 hour to obtain a graphene oxide/metal sol. The graphene oxide/metal sol is uniformly coated on the surface of the organic substrate and treated at 80-120 degrees C for 1 hour to obtain a graphene oxide/metal gel modified substrate, where the graphene oxide and the metal salt are present in the mass ratio of 1:5-20 and metal salt and the stabilizer are present in the ratio of 1:1-4. The organic ligand, the metal salt B and the organic solvent B are mixed and stirred uniformly to obtain a precursor liquid, where the organic ligand is 2-methylimidazole or trimesic acid, if the organic ligand is 2-methylimidazole that the ratio of the amount of 2-methylimidazole and the metal salt B is 4:1 and if the organic ligand is trimesic acid that the ratio of the amount of trimesic acid and the metal salt B is 2:1. The graphene oxide/metal gel modified substrate is immersed in the precursor liquid obtained and the dense metal organic framework functional layer is grown by hydrothermal reaction at 80 degrees C for 12 hours, which is cooled at room temperature and dried at room temperature to obtain a graphene oxide/metal organic framework composite film.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The metal salt A or B is zinc nitrate, zinc chloride, copper nitrate, copper chloride, zinc acetate, cobalt nitrate and/or aluminum chloride.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The organic solvent A is methanol, ethanol, propanol or ethylene glycol ether. The stabilizer is ethylenediamine, ethanolamine or aqueous ammonia. The organic solvent B is methanol, ethanol or propanol. Preferred Composition: The organic solvent A is used in a volume of 0.1-0.2 mL/mg based on the mass of the metal salt A. The organic solvent B is used in a volume of 0.1-1 mL/mmol based on the amount of the organic ligand.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The material of the organic substrate is polysulfone, polyethersulfone, polyacrylonitrile or polyvinylidene fluoride.

PN CN110195038-A; CN110195038-B
TI Increasing production of mesenchymal stem cell            exosomes used in diabetic foot, involves preparing            culture medium comprising e.g. L-glutamine, culturing            mesenchymal stem cell, extracting stem cell exosomes,            separating exosomes and purifying.
AU HUANG C
   LUO W
   TANG Y
   CHEN H
   ZHUANG W
   LI W
   HE X
AE GUANGZHOU PANYU CENT HOSPITAL GUANGZHOU (GUAN-Non-standard)
GA 201977801S
AB    NOVELTY - Increasing production of mesenchymal stem                cells exosomes, involves (i) preparing umbilical                cord mesenchymal stem cell culture medium                comprising Dulbecco's modified essential medium and                Ham's F-12 medium, non-essential amino acids,                L-glutamine and vascular cell adhesion molecule 1                reagent, (ii) placing mesenchymal stem cells in a                water bath, and thawing, centrifuging cell                suspension in a centrifuge tube, and discarding                supernatant, (iv) resuspending the cells by adding                fresh medium, and performing cell counting and                viability detection, (v) adjusting the cell seeding                density, adding the cell suspension to a culture                vessel, and transferring to 5% carbon dioxide                incubator, (vi) observing the cell state, changing                the fluid every day, and passaging the cells, (vii)                separating exosomes, extracting the supernatant,                centrifuging, (viii) separating the exosomes, and                resuspending precipitated exosomes, and                resuspending in phosphate buffer solution, and (ix)                purifying, and storing.
   USE - The method is useful for increasing production                of mesenchymal stem cells exosomes used in diabetic                foot (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method produces the mesenchymal stem cell                exosome with high purity and proper particle size,                and is simple in operation and economical.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Increasing production of mesenchymal stem                cells exosomes, involves (i) preparing umbilical                cord mesenchymal stem cell culture medium                comprising 100 ml Dulbecco's modified essential                medium and Ham's F-12 medium, 2 ml non-essential                amino acids, 2ml L-glutamine and 2 mu g vascular                cell adhesion molecule 1 reagent, (ii) taking out                the mesenchymal stem cells (MSC) from a cryostat,                placing in a water bath at 37 degrees C, rapidly                shaking, and quickly thawing within 1 minute, (iii)                transferring the thawed cell suspension to a                centrifuge tube containing 10 ml of cell culture                medium, centrifuging at 1500 rpm for 5 minutes, and                discarding supernatant, (iv) resuspending the cells                by adding fresh medium, and performing cell                counting and viability detection, (v) adjusting the                cell seeding density based on the number of cells                and its viability to 1x 105 viable cells/cm2,                adding the cell suspension to a culture vessel,                distributing the cells by shaking, marking on the                culture vessel, and transferring to 5% carbon                dioxide incubator at 37 degrees C under 95%                saturated humidity, (vi) observing the state of the                cells, changing the fluid every day, and passaging                the cells when the cell confluence reaches to                80-90%, (vii) separating exosomes at 4 degrees C                after 48 hours, extracting the supernatant of the                cells, centrifuging at 1500 rpm for 5 minutes,                centrifuging at 2000 g for 10 minutes, and                centrifuging again at 10000 g for 20 minutes,                (viii) separating the exosomes by                ultracentrifugation at 100000 g for 120 minutes                through a Beckman Coulter ultracentrifuge Type 70Ti                rotor, collecting the precipitated exosomes, and                resuspending in sterile phosphate buffer solution,                and (ix) subsequently purifying the exosomes by                sucrose gradient centrifugation, and storing -80                degrees C.
TF ACTIVITY - Antiulcer; Dermatological.
