PN CN109504131-A
TI Surface modification of nano calcium carbonate by            composite modifier comprises adding nano calcium            carbonate slurry and inorganic base, stirring until            dissolve, heating, adding fatty acid, cooling,            filtering, drying and pulverizing.
AU MENG C
AE MENG C (MENG-Individual)
GA 201928640M
AB    NOVELTY - Surface modification of nano calcium carbonate                by composite modifier comprises adding nano calcium                carbonate slurry and inorganic base, stirring to                completely dissolve, heating, adding fatty acid,                and continuously stirring to obtain nano calcium                carbonate modified slurry, cooling, filtering,                washing, drying, pulverizing to obtain                surface-modified nano calcium carbonate product,                where the nano calcium carbonate is prepared by:                calcining calcium carbonate mineral powder to                obtain white powder, dissolving in water, adding                carbon dioxide to water, heating, stirring to                obtain A slurry, dissolving butyl titanate in                absolute ethanol, stirring to obtain B solution,                performing ultrasonic dispersion, casting the B                solution into the A slurry, stirring to obtain                mixed C slurry, filtering, drying to obtain dry                powder, grinding in an agate mortar to obtain                powdered substance, placing in a muffle furnace,                heating, and cooling to obtain high whiteness and                high activity nano calcium carbonate powder.
   USE - The method is useful for surface modification                of nano calcium carbonate by composite                modifier.
   ADVANTAGE - The powdered hydrophobic nano calcium                carbonate has good dispersibility.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Surface modification of nano calcium carbonate                by composite modifier comprises (i) adding nano                calcium carbonate slurry to the reaction vessel,                adding 0.00004-0.00040 mol/g inorganic base, and                mechanically stirring to completely dissolve the                inorganic base in the slurry, (ii) heating at 75-95                degrees C, (iii) adding fatty acid after                controlling the temperature and continuously                stirring to obtain nano calcium carbonate modified                slurry, and (iv) cooling, filtering, washing,                drying and pulverizing to obtain surface-modified                nano calcium carbonate product, where the nano                calcium carbonate is prepared by: weighing 100 g                calcium carbonate mineral powder, calcining at                890-1100 degrees C for 1-3 hours to obtain white                powder, dissolving in 2000-5000 ml water,                continuously adding carbon dioxide to water,                heating at 60-95 degrees C, stirring at constant                temperature at a speed of 1000-4000                revolutions/minutes for 3 hours to obtain A slurry,                dissolving 5-30 ml butyl titanate in 30-100 ml                absolute ethanol, stirring at a speed of 1000-4000                revolutions/minutes for 0.5-3 hours to obtain B                solution, placing the A slurry on the ultrasonic                high-power industrial grade crushing and dispersing                emulsification device, performing ultrasonic                dispersion at a power of 500-3000 W, simultaneously                casting the B solution into the A slurry, stirring                at 60-95 degrees C at a speed of 1000-3000                revolutions/minutes for 20-60 minutes to obtain                mixed C slurry, filtering to obtain a filter                residue, drying in a dry oven at 100-200 degrees C                for 2-10 hours to obtain dry powder, grinding in an                agate mortar for 20 minutes to obtain powdered                substance, placing in a muffle furnace, heating at                450-550 degrees C for 1-2 hours, and cooling to                obtain high whiteness and high activity nano                calcium carbonate powder

PN CN108505322-A
TI High-temperature fire-resistant material used for firefighters, is prepared by adding chitosan to dilute acetic acid, stirring, adding nano-zirconium dioxide powder and dripping sodium hydroxide solution into chitosan-mixed solution.
AU TONG Y
AE ANHUI LONGAN FIRE ENG CO LTD (ANHU-Non-standard)
GA 201871457L
AB    NOVELTY - High-temperature fire-resistant material and its preparation method, are claimed. The preparation method involves (a) adding chitosan to dilute acetic acid solution, stirring until chitosan is completely dissolved, adding nano-zirconium dioxide powder, dripping sodium hydroxide solution into chitosan-mixed solution, filtering, separating chitosan gel and washing, (b) placing basalt fibers in a puffing machine for puffing, adding the basalt fibers to the chitosan mixed gel, introducing carbon dioxide gas, soaking, taking out basalt fiber and drying, and (c) putting the polyether-ether-ketone resin granules into a vacuum drying oven, drying, heating and pre-heating the melt spinning machine, adding the dried polyether-ether-ketone resin granules to the melt spinning machine for spinning, stretching to obtain polyether-ether-ketone multifilament and then carrying out twisting treatment to obtain polyether-ether-ketone fiber for weaving the fabric.
   USE - High-temperature fire-resistant material is useful for firefighters.
   ADVANTAGE - The high-temperature fire-resistant material is lightweight and skin-friendly, and has excellent waterproof property, high-temperature resistance, moisture permeability, thermal insulation property and refractive property.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - High-temperature fire-resistant material and its preparation method, are claimed. The preparation method involves (a) adding chitosan to dilute acetic acid solution, stirring until chitosan is completely dissolved, adding nano-zirconium dioxide powder while stirring until the powder is uniformly dispersed, dripping 0.2 %mass sodium hydroxide solution into chitosan-mixed solution, adjusting the pH value of the mixed solution to be 7.5, filtering, separating chitosan gel and washing the gel with deionized water to be neutral, (b) placing basalt fibers in a puffing machine for puffing, adding the basalt fibers to the chitosan mixed gel when the basalt fibers are cooled to room temperature, introducing carbon dioxide gas until the pH value is 4.8-5.2, soaking for 5-8 hours, taking out basalt fiber and drying the basalt fiber in a vacuum drying oven at 55 degrees C, (c) putting the polyether-ether-ketone resin granules into a vacuum drying oven, drying at 140 degrees C for 3-5 hours, heating and pre-heating the melt spinning machine, adding the dried polyether-ether-ketone resin granules to the melt spinning machine for spinning, stretching to obtain polyether-ether-ketone multifilament and then carrying out twisting treatment to obtain polyether-ether-ketone fiber for weaving the fabric, and (d) mixing polyvinyl alcohol and oxidized potato starch at a weight ratio of 9:1, completely dissolving the mixture by stirring with deionized water, adding the fibers used in the steps (c) and (d) to the mixed solution for dipping and sizing so that the slurry is uniformly attached to the surface of the fiber, taking out the slurry, naturally drying, using the treated polyether-ether-ketone fibers as warp yarns and the treated basalt fiber as a weft yarn and carrying out twill weaving using a weaving machine.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The concentration of chitosan solution is 0.1-0.2 wt.%. The nano-zirconium dioxide powder is added in an amount of 2-4 wt.% based on the weight of the chitosan. The melt spinning machine has a preheating temperature of 390-410 degrees C. The mixed solution of polyvinyl alcohol and oxidized potato starch has a mass concentration of 18-22 wt.%. The temperature of the impregnated sizing slurry is 80-90 degrees C.

PN US2018194626-A1
TI Device to extract and recover water and organic            matter to e.g. support life by recovering water and            carbonaceous material from asteroid regolith,            electrolyzing recovered water, and converting            carbonaceous material to produce e.g. water.
AU BERGGREN M
   NIZAMOV B
   ZUBRIN R
   SIEBARTH J
AE PIONEER ASTRONAUTICS (PION-Non-standard)
GA 201859412A
AB    NOVELTY - Device (D1) for extracting and recovering                water and organic matter to support propellent                production, breathing gas and life support, by (a)                recovering water from asteroid regolith, (b)                recovering carbonaceous material from asteroid                regolith, (c) electrolyzing recovered water to form                hydrogen and oxygen, and (d) converting the                carbonaceous material in an autothermal reaction                with steam and oxygen-containing gas to produce                carbon monoxide, hydrogen, carbon dioxide and                water, is claimed.
   USE - The device (D1) is useful for extracting and                recovering water and organic matter to support                propellant production, breathing gas and life                support and the device (D2) is useful for                extracting and recovering water and organic matter                to support water production, fuels production, and                chemicals production from high-ash coal, coal                processing wastes, oil shale, municipal wastes,                industrial wastes, and renewable resources and                their byproducts (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The device (D1): thermally extracts ice and                water bound to clay minerals which are then                combined with small quantities of oxygen to gasify                organic matter contained in carbonaceous chondrite                asteroids; is efficient to mine materials for use                as propellants in space vehicles and fabricating                equipment for space vehicles; recovers resources                from wastes while rendering solid residues sterile                and safe for disposal; utilizes the auger reactor                that accommodates continuous or long-duration                operation to minimize start up transients via the                use of large feed magazines and residue receivers                to minimize the duty on isolation valves, provides                efficient indirect heat exchange from the hot                reformer exhaust gases and regolith residue to                preheat fresh asteroid regolith, which is aided by                the mixing of solids by the auger, allows                independent adjustment of auger speed and oxygen                injection rates to maximize utilization and                performance over a wide range of feed                characteristics, incorporates a band heater to                concentrate required make up heat in the reaction                zone, thus reducing heat losses, requires low                torque to move granular asteroid regolith through                the preheating, reaction, and cooling zones-torque                monitoring and control can be used to ensure proper                particle packing in the microgravity environment,                and a simple tensioned plunger can be used to                provide the required force to convey feed from the                magazine into the auger and to provide flow                resistance into the residue receiver.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for a                device (D2) to extract and recover water and                organic matter to support water production, fuels                production, and chemicals production from high-ash                coal, coal processing wastes, oil shale, municipal                wastes, industrial wastes, and renewable resources                and their byproducts, comprising (a1) thermally                recovering water from low-grade, unconventional                terrestrial resources and waste materials                containing high concentrations of inorganic matter,                (b1) recovering carbonaceous material from the                above-stated resources and waste materials, and (c)                converting the carbonaceous material in an                autothermal reaction with oxygen-containing gas and                steam to produce carbon monoxide, hydrogen, carbon                dioxide and water.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INSTRUMENTATION AND TESTING - Preferred Components: In the device (D1): an                  auger reactor is used, allowing for nearly                  continuous operation and allowing for residual                  heat from the reactor discharge to be transferred                  to the gas feeds and fresh solid feed; two batch                  reactors are used, allowing residual heat from                  one autothermal reactor to be indirectly                  exchanged to pre-heat steam, oxygen-containing                  gas, and regolith fed to the second reactor;                  additional useful heat recovery can be obtained                  by indirect exchange of the hot exhaust gases to                  the asteroid feed material or to other feeds such                  as oxygen and water; additional oxygen yield is                  obtained by reduction of iron oxide compounds                  contained in asteroid regolith by hydrogen or                  carbon monoxide to lower iron oxides or iron                  metal with concurrent production of water or                  carbon dioxide, which are processed to recover                  their contained oxygen content; and gaseous                  contaminants are removed and potentially valuable                  condensed and residual solid byproducts are                  recovered, including the processed regolith that                  can be used as a material for fabrication of                  structures and mechanical components via additive                  manufacturing and advanced formation                  technologies. The device (D1) further processing:                  would be carried out to manufacture organic                  compounds and oxygen from the reaction products.                  Production of clean syngas suitable as feed for                  downstream processes that are integrated to                  produce fuels, plastics, and other organic                  compounds along with oxygen and water; and                  comprises the steps of reverse water gas shift to                  convert carbon dioxide to carbon monoxide and to                  adjust the hydrogen:carbon monoxide ratio,                  Sabatier/electrolysis to produce methane and                  oxygen, methanol synthesis (for fuel or                  subsequent olefins, plastics, and fuels                  synthesis, Fischer-Tropsch reactions to produce                  hydrocarbon fuels, or direct olefins synthesis                  from syngas). The device (D1): comprises a                  continuous flow auger reactor; refines the mass,                  volume, and power requirements for a flight-like                  system including methanation-electrolysis at                  processing scales of interest for in-space                  resource utilization; and comprises integration                  of regolith excavation, handling, feeding,                  discharging, and collection/transport of                  products. In the device (D2): an auger reactor is                  used, allowing for nearly continuous operation                  and allowing for residual heat from the reactor                  discharge to be transferred to the gas feeds and                  fresh solid feed; two batch reactors are used,                  allowing residual heat from one autothermal                  reactor to be indirectly exchanged to pre-heat                  steam, oxygen-containing gas, and solid-phase                  resource fed to the second reactor; production of                  clean syngas suitable as feed for downstream                  processes that can be integrated with the system                  to produce fuels, plastics, and other organic                  compounds along with water; additional useful                  heat recovery can be obtained by indirect                  exchange of the hot exhaust gases to fresh feed                  material or to other process feeds such as                  oxygen, air, and water; oil shale is processed in                  a manner that releases condensable volatile                  organic matter concurrent with production of                  hydrogen, carbon monoxide, carbon dioxide, and                  water, where the condensable organic matter is                  collected as a fuel and chemical product. The                  device (D2) further processing: would be carried                  out to manufacture organic compounds from the                  reaction products; and comprises the steps of                  reverse water gas shift to convert carbon dioxide                  to carbon monoxide and to adjust the                  hydrogen:carbon monoxide ratio, Sabatier reaction                  to produce methane, methanol synthesis (for fuel                  or subsequent olefins, plastics, and fuels                  synthesis, Fischer-Tropsch reactions to produce                  hydrocarbon fuels, or direct olefins synthesis                  from syngas); and gaseous contaminants are                  removed and potentially valuable byproducts are                  recovered; and residues from terrestrial                  processing are rendered sterile and made suitable                  for further separations, byproduct recovery, and                  disposal. The device (D2): comprises a continuous                  flow auger reactor; includes methanation and                  fuels synthesis at processing scales of interest                  for terrestrial resource and waste utilization;                  and comprises integration of resource excavation,                  handling, feeding, discharging, and                  collection/transport of products.

TI Removing low-temperature heat storage catalytic            combustion of complex organic waste gas comprises e.g.            passing complex organic waste gas through a preheating            chamber and entering a light-off control unit.
AU KYO K
   GUO F
   HAN Z
   CHENG J
   ZHU Q
AE UNIV SHENYANG CHEM TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (UYHG-C)

AB    NOVELTY - Removing low-temperature heat storage                catalytic combustion of complex organic waste gas                comprises passing complex organic waste gas through                a preheating chamber and entering a light-off                control unit, passing the hot organic exhaust gas                further to the catalytic combustion unit, releasing                heat exchanges heat with the heat transfer oil in                the regenerator and evacuating the low-temperature                gas after heat exchange through the chimney,                driving the heat transfer oil by the oil pump to                realize the heat circulation transmission process                of the regenerator, the preheating chamber, the                filter, the oil pump and the regenerator and mixing                the organic waste gas generated by the heat                transfer oil through the filter cleaning into the                VOCs exhaust gas.
   USE - The method is useful for removing                low-temperature heat storage catalytic combustion                of complex organic waste gas.
   ADVANTAGE - The method eliminates organic pollutant                discharge, saves energy and improves the system                adaptability; and is environmentally                friendly.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Removing low-temperature heat storage                catalytic combustion of complex organic waste gas                comprises passing complex organic waste gas through                a preheating chamber and entering a light-off                control unit, where the complex organic gas reaches                a light-off temperature in the light-off control                unit, passing the hot organic exhaust gas further                to the catalytic combustion unit, where the                catalytic combustion process generates water and                carbon dioxide , releasing heat exchanges heat with                the heat transfer oil in the regenerator and                evacuating the low-temperature gas (water , carbon                dioxide, and nitrogen) after heat exchange through                the chimney, driving the heat transfer oil by the                oil pump to realize the heat circulation                transmission process of the regenerator, the                preheating chamber, the filter, the oil pump and                the regenerator, mixing the organic waste gas                generated by the heat transfer oil through the                filter cleaning into the VOCs exhaust gas, where                the complex organic waste gas low-temperature heat                storage catalytic combustion process comprises a                preheating unit, a light-off control unit, a                catalytic combustion unit, and a heat storage unit,                the preheating unit preheats the organic waste gas                by using a circulating heat transfer oil, one end                is an organic waste gas (VOCs) inlet, and the other                end of the organic waste gas outlet is connected to                the light-off control unit through a pipeline, the                catalytic combustion unit is provided with a                catalyst, the one end is closely connected with the                light-off operation unit, and the other end is                connected to the oil bath regenerator and heating                hot gas after combustion in the oil bath heat                storage chamber and passing the cooled flue gas                through chimney discharge, where the heat storage                unit comprises a filter, an oil pump, a                regenerator, and a preheating chamber, and the                regenerator is connected to the filter and the                preheating chamber via an oil pump.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INSTRUMENTATION AND TESTING - Preferred Components: The light-off control                  unit comprises a flame-retardant layer, a                  supplementary fuel inlet and an igniter, where                  the flame-retardant layer prevents the combustion                  gas from flowing back to cause an explosion, and                  the supplementary fuel refers to when the                  light-off control unit temperature is lower than                  the ignition temperature of the complex organic                  gas is supplemented by automatic regulation to                  bring it to a light-off temperature, and the                  igniter is a component that completes ignition                  upon startup or when supplemental fuel is added.                  The supplementary fuel line is provided with a                  regulating valve connected to the temperature                  measuring component of the light-off control unit                  through a signal line, when the light-off control                  unit temperature is lower than a certain                  temperature, the supplementary fuel inlet is                  automatically opened. The igniter is provided                  with a regulating valve connected to the                  supplementary fuel regulating valve through a                  signal line, i.e. automatically igniting when the                  supplementary fuel is turned on. The regenerator                  comprises a multiple heat exchange tubes arranged                  in parallel, and multiple heat-enhancing fins are                  arranged on the gas side of the heat exchange                  tubes to enhance heat transfer of the gas to the                  heat transfer oil.

AE AKAY FLAVOURS & AROMATICS PVT LTD (HNSN-C)
   AKAY FLAVOURS & AROMATICS PVT LTD (HNSN-C)

GA 201835965E
AB    NOVELTY - Biologically active composition comprises black cumin seed extract in oil or powder form, where the black cumin seed extract further comprises thymoquinone, alpha -linoleic acid, and polyphenols.
   USE - The composition is useful for treating humans with anxiety, stress, memory loss and sleep associated disorders, where the sleep associated disorders is disturbances and high sleep latency (all claimed). The ability of the composition to treat sleep associated disorders was tested in subjects by measuring the reduction in sleep disturbances using biological assay. The results showed that the black cumin oil composition with not less than 5% of thymoquinone reduced sleep disturbances by 50.66%.
   ADVANTAGE - The composition: provides an anxiolytic effect through decreasing the expression in glutamate receptors subtypes N-methyl-D-aspartate receptor and ionotropic glutamate receptor 2, and increasing survival of choline acetyltransferase expressing neuronal cells in the hippocampus; produces a memory effect through inhibiting acetyl cholinesterase (claimed); and enhancement memory at relatively lower dosage.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for:
   (1) preparing (P1) the biologically active black cumin seed extract in free flowing powder form using black cumin seeds, comprising (a) pre-treating powdered or flaked black cumin seeds with enzymes, followed by supercritical carbon dioxide extraction of the oil from the seeds to obtain a supercritical extracted oil fraction which comprises non-volatile fixed oils rich in alpha -linoleic acid and volatile oils rich in thymoquinone, (b) extracting the residual black cumin seed material left after step (a) with a hydrophilic solvent to obtain residual black cumin seed extract, (c) spray-drying the residual black cumin seed extract from step (b) to obtain a residual black cumin seed free flowing powder, (d) dissolving the residual black cumin seed extract free flowing powder from step (c) in water to obtain a residual black cumin seed extract solution at 10 to 25% total dissolved solids level, (e) mixing the supercritical extracted oil fraction from step (a) with the residual black cumin seed solution obtained step (d), followed by homogenization or ultrasonication of the mixture thus obtained to produce an emulsion, and (f) spray-drying or freeze drying the emulsion from step (e) to obtain black cumin seed extracted oil composition in free flowing powder form where the black cumin seed extracted oil composition in free flowing powder form comprising 0.1-8% of thymoquinone; and
   (2) preparing (M2) biologically active black cumin seed extract in liquid oil form, comprising (a1) pre-treating the powdered or flaked black cumin seeds with water to form a slurry and further treating the slurry with at least one enzyme, (b1) drying the slurry to the powder form with 3-6% moisture level, and (c1) extracting the liquid oil by supercritical carbon dioxide extraction from the dried powder from step (b1), where the black cumin seed extract in the liquid oil form comprises 2-43% of thymoquinone.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - PHARMACEUTICALS - Preferred Components: The black cumin oil comprises 0.5-43% of thymoquinone, 45-60% of alpha -linoleic acid, and 0.5-4% of polyphenols. The black cumin powder comprises 0.5-8% of thymoquinone, 1-10% of alpha -linoleic acid, 0.5-1% of polyphenols, 10-50% of the black cumin derived proteins, and 20-70% of cumin derived carbohydrates. The black cumin oil or powder comprises not less than 5% thymoquinone and not less than 45% alpha -linoleic acid. Preferred Method: The pre-treatment with at least one enzyme in steps (a) and (a1) is performed for 1-8 hours, where the enzyme is pectinase, amylase, galactosidase or protease. In the methods (M1) and (M2), the supercritical extraction of black cumin seeds is performed using carbon dioxide at a pressure of 100-220 bar and temperature of 35-50 degrees C. The hydrophilic solvent used for extraction in step (b) is water or a mixture of water and ethanol. The step (b) of extracting the residual black cumin seed material using a hydrophilic solvent is done by solvent extraction in the temperature of 50-60 degrees C.
   ACTIVITY - Tranquilizer; Nootropic; Hypnotic.
   MECHANISM OF ACTION - None given.
   ADMINISTRATION - Administration of the black cumin oil or powder composition is 50-500 mg/day (claimed) by any suitable route.

GA 202114282V
AB    NOVELTY - Production of a transgenic plant with altered                photorespiration and improved carbon dioxide (CO2)                fixation, involves introducing into a cell or                tissue of the plant, one or more nucleic acids                encoding greater than or equal to 4 polypeptides                having the enzymatic activities of: (a) erythro-                beta -hydroxyaspartate aldolase belonging to the EC                class 4.1.3.14, (b) erythro- beta -hydroxyaspartate                dehydratase belonging to the EC class 4.3.1.20, (c)                iminosuccinate reductase and (d)                aspartate-glyoxylate transaminase, where                introduction of nucleic acid(s) results in a de                novo expression of the greater than or equal to 4                polypeptides having the enzymatic activities of (a)                erythro- beta -hydroxyaspartate aldolase belonging                to the EC class 4.1.3.14, (b) erythro- beta                -hydroxyaspartate dehydratase belonging to the EC                class 4.3.1.20, (c) iminosuccinate reductase and                (d) aspartate-glyoxylate transaminase. The                polypeptide having enzymatic activity of (c)                iminosuccinate reductase comprises SEQ ID NO:                1-299. All sequences are given in the                specification.
   USE - The method is useful for production of a                transgenic plant with altered photorespiration and                improved CO2 fixation (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method can be succssfully applied to some                agricultural croop plants with nutritional and                medicinal uses. The transgenic plants show an                improved growth rate, productivity and energy                conversion efficiency.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Production of a transgenic plant with altered                photorespiration and improved carbon dioxide (CO2)                fixation, involves introducing into a cell or                tissue of the plant, one or more nucleic acids                encoding greater than or equal to 4 polypeptides                having the enzymatic activities of: (a) erythro-                beta -hydroxyaspartate aldolase belonging to the EC                class 4.1.3.14, (b) erythro- beta -hydroxyaspartate                dehydratase belonging to the EC class 4.3.1.20, (c)                iminosuccinate reductase and (d)                aspartate-glyoxylate transaminase, where the                introduction of the nucleic acid(s) results in a de                novo expression of the greater than or equal to 4                polypeptides having the enzymatic activities of (a)                erythro- beta -hydroxyaspartate aldolase belonging                to the EC class 4.1.3.14, (b) erythro- beta                -hydroxyaspartate dehydratase belonging to the EC                class 4.3.1.20, (c) iminosuccinate reductase and                (d) aspartate-glyoxylate transaminase. The                polypeptide having the enzymatic activity of (c)                iminosuccinate reductase comprises an amino acid                sequence selected from SEQ ID NO: 1-299, or an                amino acid sequence having greater than or equal to                80% sequence identity to a sequence selected from                SEQ ID NO: 1-299; and the polypeptide having the                enzymatic activity of (d) aspartate-glyoxylate                transaminase comprises an amino acid sequence                selected from SEQ ID NO: 300-599, or an amino acid                sequence having greater than or equal to 80%                sequence identity to a sequence selected from SEQ                ID NO: 300-599. All sequences are not given in the                specification. INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included                for:
   (1) a transgenic plant comprising one or more                heterologous nucleic acids encoding greater than or                equal to 4 polypeptides having the enzymatic                activities of (a) erythro- beta -hydroxyaspartate                aldolase belonging to the EC class 4.1.3.14, (b)                erythro- beta -hydroxyaspartate dehydratase                belonging to the EC class 4.3.1.20, (c)                iminosuccinate reductase comprising an amino acid                sequence selected from SEQ ID NO: 1-299, or an                amino acid sequence having greater than or equal to                80% sequence identity to a sequence selected from                SEQ ID NO: 1-299; and (d) aspartate-glyoxylate                transaminase comprising an amino acid sequence                selected from SEQ ID NO: 300-599, or an amino acid                sequence having greater than or equal to 80%                sequence identity to a sequence selected from SEQ                ID NO: 300-599; and
   (2) a nucleic acid construct comprising                nucleic acid sequences encoding greater than or                equal to 4 polypeptides having the enzymatic                activities of: (a) erythro- beta -hydroxyaspartate                aldolase belonging to the EC class 4.1.3.14, (b)                erythro- beta -hydroxyaspartate dehydratase                belonging to the EC class 4.3.1.20, (c)                iminosuccinate reductase comprising an amino acid                sequence selected from SEQ ID NO: 1-299, or an                amino acid sequence having greater than or equal to                80% sequence identity to a sequence selected from                SEQ ID NO: 1-299; (d) aspartate-glyoxylate                transaminase comprising an amino acid sequence                selected from SEQ ID NO: 300-599, or an amino acid                sequence having greater than or equal to 80%                sequence identity to a sequence selected from SEQ                ID NO: 300-599, a selection marker nucleic acid,                where the nucleic acid sequences are operably                linked to at least one promoter for expression in a                plant and are operably linked to at least one                terminator.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Components: The polypeptide having                  the enzymatic activity of (c) iminosuccinate                  reductase comprises an amino acid sequence                  selected from SEQ ID NO: 1, 7, 22, 25, 26, 39,                  47, 58, 75, 123, 135, and 160, or an amino acid                  sequence having greater than or equal to 80%                  sequence identity to a sequence selected from SEQ                  ID NO: 1, 7, 22, 25, 26, 39, 47, 58, 75, 123,                  135, and 160. The polypeptide having the                  enzymatic activity of (c) iminosuccinate                  reductase comprises an amino acid sequence of SEQ                  ID NO: 135, or an amino acid sequence having                  greater than or equal to 80% sequence identity to                  the sequence; and the polypeptide having the                  enzymatic activity of (d) aspartate-glyoxylate                  transaminase comprises an amino acid sequence of                  SEQ ID NO: 433, or an amino acid sequence having                  greater than or equal to 80% sequence identity to                  the sequence. The polypeptides having the                  enzymatic activities (a)-(d) comprise an amino                  acid sequence targeting the polypeptides to the                  peroxisomes. The (a) erythro- beta                  -hydroxyaspartate aldolase belonging to the EC                  class 4.1.3.14 is C-terminally fused to a                  peroxisomal targeting signal 2 of SEQ ID NO: 952                  and the polypeptides having the enzymatic                  activities (b)-(d) are N-terminally fused to a                  peroxisomal targeting signal 1 of amino acid                  sequence comprising Ser-Lys-Leu (SEQ ID NO: not                  defined). The nucleic acids further encode a                  polypeptide having the enzymatic activity of (e)                  glycolate dehydrogenase belonging to the EC class                  1.1.99.14, where the introduction of the nucleic                  acid(s) results in a de novo expression of at                  least five polypeptides having the enzymatic                  activity of (a) erythro- beta -hydroxyaspartate                  aldolase belonging to the EC class 4.1.3.14, (b)                  erythro- beta -hydroxyaspartate dehydratase                  belonging to the EC class 4.3.1.20, (c)                  iminosuccinate reductase, (d)                  aspartate-glyoxylate transaminase, and (e)                  glycolate dehydrogenase belonging to the EC class                  1.1.99.14, where the polypeptides having the                  enzymatic activities (a)-(e) are localized in                  cellular mitochondria, and where the polypeptides                  having the enzymatic activities (a)-(e) are                  N-terminally fused to a serine                  hydroxymethyltransferase 1 target peptide of SEQ                  ID NO: 919. Nucleic acids further encode two                  polypeptides having the enzymatic activities of                  (e) glycolate dehydrogenase belonging to the EC                  class 1.1.99.14, (f) phosphoenolpyruvate                  carboxykinase belonging to the EC class 4.1.1.49                  where the introduction of the nucleic acid(s)                  results in a de novo expression of at least six                  polypeptides having the enzymatic activities of                  (a) erythro- beta -hydroxyaspartate aldolase                  belonging to the EC class 4.1.3.14, (b) erythro-                  beta -hydroxyaspartate dehydratase belonging to                  the EC class 4.3.1.20, (c) iminosuccinate                  reductase, (d) aspartate-glyoxylate transaminase,                  (e) glycolate dehydrogenase belonging to the EC                  class 1.1.99.14, and (f) phosphoenolpyruvate                  carboxykinase belonging to the EC class 4.1.1.49,                  where the polypeptides having the enzymatic                  activities (a)-(f) are localized in cellular                  chloroplasts, and where the polypeptides having                  the enzymatic activities (a)-(f) are N-terminally                  fused to Arabidopsis ferredoxin-2 chloroplastic                  target peptide of SEQ ID NO: 917. All sequences                  are not given in the specification. The plant is                  selected from Helianthus annuus, Brassica napus,                  Camelina sativa, Oryza sativa, Hordeum vulgare,                  Triticum spp., Avena sativa, Solanum                  lycopersicum, Solanum tuberosum, Glycine max,                  Beta vulgaris, Nicotiana tabacum, and Arabidopsis                  thaliana.

PT P
PN CN111549002-A; CN111549002-B
TI Producing canine parvovirus monoclonal antibody by thawing hybridoma cells, using serum-free hybridoma cell culture solution for cell expansion, inoculating cell seed into storage tank in bioreactor and filtering culture solution.
AU REN D
   ZHANG J
   SUN B
   LI L
   YE Y
   ZHANG L
   SONG X
   GAO X
   KAN S
   LIU W
AE HARBIN YUANHENG BIOLOGICAL PHARM CO LTD (HARB-Non-standard)
GA 2020818817
AB    NOVELTY - Method for producing canine parvovirus monoclonal antibody involves thawing hybridoma cells, using the serum-free hybridoma cell culture solution for cell expansion, thoroughly cleaning a bioreactor, performing high temperature and high pressure sterilization, installing and calibrating pH electrode and dissolved oxygen electrode, turning on the gas supply devices of oxygen, nitrogen and carbon dioxide, setting the bioreactor culture conditions, inoculating the cell seed into the storage tank in the bioreactor, sampling and counting the cells, reserving the sample for titer testing, adding serum-free flow medium, harvesting the culture solution, sterilizing and filtering the culture solution and aseptically and transferring into sterile containers to obtain the canine parvovirus monoclonal antibody.
   USE - The method is useful for producing canine parvovirus monoclonal antibody.
   ADVANTAGE - The method produces canine parvovirus monoclonal antibody in a bioreactor which has high cell density and antibody titer, avoids concentration and serum addition in the culture, hence simplifies the downstream process and reduces risk of side reactions.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Method for producing canine parvovirus monoclonal antibody involves (a) taking out hybridoma cells from the working cell bank, using the rapid thawing method to rapidly transfer the seed cells from liquid nitrogen to a 37 degrees C water bath for recovery, and using the serum-free hybridoma cell culture solution for cell expansion in a 125 ml shake flask, (b) thoroughly cleaning a bioreactor, performing high temperature and high pressure sterilization, installing and calibrating pH electrode and dissolved oxygen electrode, turning on the four gas supply devices of oxygen, nitrogen and carbon dioxide, setting the bioreactor culture conditions as follows: temperature at 36-37 degrees C, pH value at 6.75-6.95, dissolved oxygen at 20-50% and rotating speed of the stirring paddle at 60-100 revolutions per minute (rpm), (c) when the production cell seed proliferates to a suitable total amount and the final cell concentration is 8x 105-1x 106 cells/ml, inoculating the cell seed into the storage tank in the bioreactor, (d) when the cell proliferation enters the mid-log phase for 24 hours, taking a sample, counting and saving the sample for titer testing, adding 4 vol.% serum-free flow medium, and then sampling and counting every 24 hours, reserving the sample for titer testing, adding 4 vol.% serum-free flow medium until the sample hemagglutination inhibition titer reaches more than 1:2560 and harvesting the culture solution and (e) sterilizing and filtering the culture solution and aseptically and transferring into sterile containers to obtain the canine parvovirus monoclonal antibody. The bioreactor includes a storage tank and a reaction tank. The upper surface of the reaction chamber is fixedly connected with a storage chamber through an inclined plate. The storage bin includes a liquid storage tank and a liquid collection tank. The inner wall of the storage bin is provided with a supporting plate, and the supporting plate is of arc design. A liquid storage cover is sleeved on the top of the liquid storage tank, and a pressure valve is installed on the lower surface of the liquid storage cover and liquid tank. The inner wall of the liquid storage tank is provided with evenly arranged first through holes, and the first through holes are all connected with the liquid collection tank. A control valve is fixedly connected to the inner wall of each of the first through holes, and the control valve is closed in the initial state. The reaction chamber includes a reaction box, and the reaction box has an I-shaped design. A reaction tank is fixedly connected to the bottom end surface of the reaction box, and the reaction tank is of circular arc design. A motor is fixedly connected to the upper surface of the reaction box, and the motor is electrically connected with the controller through a wire. A round rod is fixedly connected to the drive shaft of the motor, and the round rod passes through the reaction box and extends into the reaction tank. A first stirring paddle is fixedly connected to the outer surface of the round rod extending into the reaction box. A second stirring paddle is fixedly connected to the outer surface of the round rod extending into the reaction tank. The first stirring paddle and the inner wall of the reaction box are connected evenly arranged around first rotor blades through a rotating shaft. The inner wall of the reaction box is equipped with uniformly arranged first pipes, and the first pipes are of elastic design. The other end of each first pipe extends into the collecting tank and is placed above the support plate. An air inlet pipe is installed in the inner wall of the reaction tank at the center line of the reaction tank, and the air inlet pipe is used to pass in air, oxygen, nitrogen and carbon dioxide.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Device: The upper end of the round rod extends into the liquid storage tank and is fixedly connected with the support plate. The supporting plate and the extrusion box are connected in rotation. A circular groove is opened in the inner wall of the round rod, and the circular groove penetrates the round rod design. The inner wall of the first stirring blade is provided with a long groove, and the long groove is connected with the circular groove, and the inner wall of the long groove is provided with uniform Arranged round holes. The internal structure of the first stirring paddle and the second stirring paddle are completely the same. A uniformly arranged stirring rod is fixedly connected to the upper surface of the support plate. A spiral blade is fixedly connected to the outer surface of each stirring rod. A second conduit is installed in the inner wall of the reaction box in the cavity of each of the first conduits, and the other end of the second pipe extends into the cavity above the collecting tank. A one-way valve is installed at the head of the second conduit extending into the collecting tank. The inner wall of the liquid storage tank is made of rubber material. the inner wall of the liquid storage tank is fixedly connected with a serrated memory alloy with a thickness of one to two centimeters. A uniformly arranged small hole is trapped in the inner wall of each second conduit. A liquid injection valve is fixedly connected to the inner wall above the liquid collecting tank. An arc-shaped groove is opened in the inner wall of the reaction tank, and the arc-shaped groove is connected with a uniform inlet pipe. The inner wall of the arc groove is provided with evenly arranged second through holes, and the second through holes are all connected with the reaction tank. A rubber layer is fixedly connected to the inner surface of the reaction tank above each of the second through holes, and the rubber layer is designed to wrap the second through holes. The inner wall of each rubber layer is provided with uniformly arranged micropores.

AB    NOVELTY - Efficient extracting of Hericium                erinaceuspolysaccharide comprises (i) soaking the                Hericium erinaceuspowder in clean water to make the                Hericium erinaceuscell fully absorb water to obtain                the swollen Hericium erinaceuspowder; (ii) steaming                swollen Hericium erinaceuspowder with carbon                dioxide and water vapor in the heating and                pressurizing environment to obtain the                pre-processed Hericium erinaceuspowder; (iii)                freezing the pre-processed Hericium erinaceuspowder                and the liquid water absorbed in the Hericium                erinaceuscell and heat-shocking the frozen Hericium                erinaceuspowder with microwave under the vacuum                condition to obtain the broken Hericium                erinaceuspowder; and (iv) extracting the broken                wall Hericium erinaceuspowder by hot water,                separating and purifying.

TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Method: The soaking is carried out                  for &#8814;2 hours, preferably 3-3.5 hours,                  and preferably with low-temperature water with a                  temperature not higher than 5&#8451;. The                  temperature of the hot water extraction is                  50-65&#8451; and the extraction time is 45-60                  minutes. TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The mining waste                  blended with other components during the                  formation of cold fusion concrete composition, is                  selected from bauxite waste, metal mine waste,                  lithium mining waste and/or coal waste.The pH adjusting agent used during the                  prodcution process, is selected from alkali metal                  hydroxides, alkali metal metasilicates and/or Red                  Mud.

The process also involves adding 20-60                  wt.% aggregates to the composition. PN WO2022081638-A2; US2022120228-A1; WO2022081638-A3
TI Method for operating oxycombustion engine system of vehicle, involves increasing pressure of recycled exhaust gas passed to combustion chamber in response to determining that detected pressure is less than configurable pressure threshold.
AU BAAQEL H A
   HAMAD E Z
AE SAUDI ARABIAN OIL CO (SAOI-C)
   ARAMCO SERVICES CO (SAOI-C)
   SAUDI ARABIAN OIL CO (SAOI-C)
GA 202253409D
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves passing nitrogen-depleted gas, fuel and recycled exhaust gas into a combustion chamber (122). Mixture of the nitrogen-depleted gas, the fuel and the recycled exhaust gas are combusted to produce exhaust gas comprising carbon dioxide. Pressure of the recycled exhaust gas passed to the combustion chamber is detected. Determination is made whether the detected pressure of the recycled exhaust gas is less than a configurable pressure threshold. The pressure of the recycled exhaust gas passed to the combustion chamber is increased in response to determining that the detected pressure of the recycled exhaust gas is less than the configurable pressure threshold.
   USE - Method for operating an oxycombustion engine system (claimed) of a vehicle.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables managing re-circulation of the exhaust gas to the combustion chamber, so that desirable combustion stability is maintained and undesirable emissions are effectively managed.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a sectional view of a combustion chamber of an internal combustion engine of an oxycombustion engine system.

   Internal combustion engine (102)
   Cylinder head (104)
   Fuel injector (118)
   Combustion chamber (122)

PN BR102020013552-A2
TI Obtaining passion fruit seed extract useful e.g. for preparing topical formulation, involves obtaining passion fruit seed cake, mixing with ethanol in water solution, performing microwave-assisted extraction, separating extract, removing solvent and obtaining piceatannol-enriched dry extract.
AU SILVA G C D
   BOTTOLI C B G
AE UNICAMP UNIV ESTADUAL CAMPINAS (UNES-C)
GA 2022443290
AB    NOVELTY - Method for obtaining passion fruit seed extract involves (a) obtaining passion fruit seed cake, (b) mixing the passion fruit seed cake with ethanol in water solution having 53-80% ethanol in its composition, (c) performing microwave-assisted extraction at 60-87 degrees C for 15-55 minutes, (d) separating the extract solution from the extracted cake and removing the solvent from the extract solution and (f) obtaining piceatannol-enriched dry extract.
   USE - The method is useful for obtaining passion fruit seed extract which is used for preparing a topical formulation (claimed) and in chemical, pharmaceutical or cosmetic industries.
   ADVANTAGE - The method prepares passion fruit seed extract with high piceatannol content, high yield and favorable physico-chemical characteristics, is performed cost-effectively with fewer steps, great compatibility and reduced energy consumption, solvent consumption and carbon dioxide emissions, shows coloration in light tones, is applied on an industrial scale in the cosmetics and nutraceutical sectors and favors increase of production scale.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Components: The extract comprises at least 20 mu g/mg piceatannol. The mass-volume ratio of the cake to the solution in step (b) is at least 1:5, preferably 1:30. The ethanol in water solution comprises 70% ethanol in its composition. Preferred Method: The passion fruit seed cake in step (a) is optionally degreased. The step (c) is performed at 87 degrees C for 30 minutes. The method involves returning the cake extracted in step (d) to step (b) for up to 3 cycles.
   EXAMPLE - The examples provide details of process of preparation of microwave-assisted extract and extract preparation process by Soxhlet technique that do not support the claims.

AB    NOVELTY - Preparation and use method of reversed-phase                thin-layer chromatography adhesive comprises using                water, hydrophilic solvents or their mixtures as                the reaction medium, adding initiator under the                protection of carbon dioxide, nitrogen, argon under                the condition of 50-90degrees Celsius, making                appropriate combinations of (meth)acrylic acid and                its (hydroxy) esters, (Meth) acrylic unsaturated                esters, divinylbenzene, copolymerizing triallyl                isocyanurate , where polymerization reaction is                over, forming a linear polymer, reducing the                pressure to remove the solvent, preparing adhesive                to obtain reversed-phase thin-layer chromatography                plates, using oil-soluble solvent to dissolve the                adhesive and mixing with reversed-phase thin-layer                chromatography silica gel powder to make the plate,                drying solvent and evaporating at low temperature                under reduced pressure, placing plate in an oven at                100-200degreesC for 1-5 hours.
   USE - The method is useful ad preparation and use                method of reversed-phase thin-layer chromatography                adhesive.
   ADVANTAGE - The product of the present invention is mainly                used for the qualitative and quantitative analysis                of reversed-phase thin-layer chromatography of the                mixture.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The selected monomer                  must contain no cross-linking reaction below                  100degreesC, groups that can undergo                  cross-linking reaction above this temperature,                  such as hydroxyl, vinyl, Allyl. After high                  temperature treatment. The self-crosslinking                  reaction occurs in the linear adhesive molecule                  forming a stable network structure, not only can                  the silica gel powder be firmly bonded to the                  substrate, and resisting erosion of                  reversed-phase thin-layer chromatography                  developing agent. The initiator used can be                  water-soluble potassium persulfate, ammonium                  persulfate, or oil-soluble azobisisobutyronitrile                  and azobisisoheptonitrile. The addition amount is                  0.1-5 %, the adhesive can be uniformly mixed with                  silane silica gel powder and fluorescent powder.                  The solvent used in coating the chromatographic                  plate is recovered by low-temperature reduced                  pressure drying to achieve environmental                  friendliness.

AU BU A

AE KUNSHAN CHENGXIN CHEM CO LTD (KUNS-Non-standard)
GA 2021995117
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing high-purity ammonium fluoride comprises adding sodium hydroxide solution and recycling liquid to a rough product ammonium fluoride mother liquor to adjust the pH value of the ammonium fluoride mother liquor, reacting, generating sodium silicate solution by sodium hydroxide and fluorosilicate ions, introducing carbon dioxide, reacting to generate silicic acid precipitate and sodium carbonate, filtering to remove silicic acid precipitate to obtain solution A, adding barium hydroxide solution in the solution A, reacting the barium hydroxide with sulfate to generate barium sulfate precipitate, reacting the barium hydroxide with sodium carbonate to produce barium carbonate precipitation and sodium hydroxide, filtering to remove precipitate, obtaining solution B, heating and concentrating the solution B, cooling, crystallizing, solid-liquid separation, drying to obtain ammonium fluoride crystal and recycling liquid and recycling the recycling liquid.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing high-purity ammonium fluoride.
   ADVANTAGE - The ammonium fluoride has high purity, low cost and removes sulfate ion and fluorosilicate ion. The method ensures that high purity ammonium fluoride is obtained by liquid phase method.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The pH value of the ammonium fluoride mother liquor reaches 11.5-12.5 by adding sodium hydroxide solution and the recycling liquid. Preferred Method: The reaction process is carried out for 26-28 hours after adding sodium hydroxide solution and the recycling solution to the crude ammonium fluoride mother liquor to adjust the PH value of the ammonium fluoride mother liquor. The method comprises heating and concentrating the solution B at 80-100 degrees C for 20-25 minutes. The cooling temperature in the cooling and crystallization process is 25-35 degrees C. The method comprises stirring process is performed at a speed of 100-300revolutions/minute during the cooling and crystallization process. The drying process is carried out at 90-110 degrees C for 6-8 hours.

PN CN113106266-A
TI Removal of zinc element in metallurgical dust involves adding zinc-containing dust and solid waste to pre-heating furnace for heating treatment, obtaining heating raw material and exhaust gas, and transporting to rotary furnace.
AU YU Y
   YAO H
   GE Y
   LI Y
AE UNIV SHANGHAI (USHN-C)
GA 202181746R
AB    NOVELTY - Removing zinc element in metallurgical dust involves adding zinc-containing dust and solid waste serving as raw materials into a preheating furnace for heating treatment to obtain a heating raw material and exhaust gas; and conveying the heated raw material obtained to a feeding port of a rotary furnace, adding heated raw material into the rotary furnace, and performing microwave reduction dezincification, where exhaust gas discharged from the preheating furnace and dedusted to obtain the exhaust gas with main components including vatbon monoxide (CO) and carbon dioxide (CO2). The exhaust gas. The dedusted waste gas obtained is added into an air inlet of the rotary furnace, which enables the waste gas to perform chemical reaction with zinc oxide in the zinc-containing dust in the rotary furnace, complete auxiliary dezincification, obtain dezincification material slag and smoke containing zinc steam, and collecting dezincification material slag and the smoke containing the zinc steam.
   USE - Method for removing zinc element in metallurgical dust.
   ADVANTAGE - The method obviously improve the zinc removing effect and quality of the metallurgical dust.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - METALLURGY - Preferred Conditions: The method particularly involves condensing the smoke containing the zinc steam in the step d through a condenser to obtain simple substance zinc and dezincification smoke. The dezincification flue gas from the condenser is passed to the preheater, where the residual heat of the dezincification flue gas is used to heat the zinc containing dust. The dezincification flue gas serve as a zinc-containing dust raw material added into a preheating furnace for heating treatment to realize the circulating treatment and collecting zinc resources. The zinc-containing dust and solid waste are cold pressed blocks prepared from the zinc-containing dust and the solid waste, alternatively, the zinc-containing dust, source of solid waste, and zinc content are not limited. The carbonaceous fines and a binder are added to the cold-pressed block. The microwaves are generated by a microwave unit mounted on the rotary kiln. The heated feedstock is fed into a rotary furnace using a screw feeder. The rotational speed of the rotary kiln is not limited. The bag-type dust removal process is adopted for dust removal.

PT P
PN CN113061492-A
TI Extracting essential oil comprises e.g. cleaning leaves, flower, fruit and root raw materials, crushing, soaking, performing steam distillation, separating through oil-water separation membrane, extracting, filtering and removing impurities.
AU ZHANG H
   LUO Q
   YAN N
   FANG M
AE JIAN ZHONGXIANG NATURAL PLANT CO LTD (JIAN-Non-standard)
GA 202180455N
AB    NOVELTY - Extracting essential oil comprises (i) cleaning 1000-1500 pts. wt. leaves raw material, 1500-1800 pts. wt. flower raw material, 1200-1500 pts. wt. fruit raw material and 1100-1300 pts. wt. root raw material, crushing, soaking in water at 80-100 degrees C for 12-24 hours, and mixing to obtain mixed raw material; (ii) transferring mixed raw material into distillation tank, paving on middle sieve plate, performing steam distillation, introducing steam, and collecting vapor liquid to obtain oil-water mixture A, and distilled mixed raw material; (iii) mixing distilled mixed raw material and water in sealed glass tank, transferring into distillation tank, collecting distilled liquid to obtain oil-water mixture B; and (iv) blending oil-water mixture A and oil-water mixture B, separating through oil-water separation membrane to obtain mixed crude oil and water, extracting mixed crude oil by supercritical carbon dioxide extraction device, decolorizing, filtering and removing impurities.
   USE - The method is useful for extracting essential oil.
   ADVANTAGE - The method improves the extraction rate of essential oils.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The leaves raw material comprises mint, citronella, Eucalyptus leaves, and Cinnamomum camphora leaves at a mass ratio of 1:1:2:2. The flower raw material comprises tulip, lilies, Camellia flower, fleur-de-lis flower, and sweet scented Osmanthus at a mass ratio of 1:1:2:2:1. The fruit peel raw material comprises lemon peel, grapefruit peel, Gannan navel orange peel, and mandarin orange peel at a mass ratio of 1:1:1:1. The root raw material comprises cogongrass rhizome, turmeric, and tea tree root at a mass ratio of 1:3:2. Preferred Method: The steam is prepared by mixing soaking liquid and the extraction is carried out at 45-55 degrees C for 5-8 hours under pressure of 10-20 mpa. The filtering and impurity removal treatment is carried out through sterile filter membrane, where the preparation of sterile filter membrane comprises (a) adding 80-100 pts. wt. modified glass fiber, 10-16 pts. wt. feldspar and 8-12 pts. wt. boron phosphide into a stirring tank, stirring uniformly, hot pressing and forming at 80- 100 degrees C, transferring into calcining furnace, calcining at 500-600 degrees C for 1-2 hours with a calcining rate of 12-18 degrees C/minute, continuously heating to 1000-1200 degrees C for 4-6 hours, annealing, heating to 1000-1200 degrees C with a heating rate of 20-30 degrees C/minute, maintaining the temperature for 2-3 hours, and naturally cooling at room temperature to obtain product; and (b) heating casting film liquid to 50-70 degrees C, uniformly coating, dipping or spraying way casting liquid i on the filter film supporting body, placing into a vacuum drying box and drying at 30-40 degrees C. The preparation of modified glass fiber comprises adding 3-7 pts. wt. metal powder mixture and 5-9 pts. wt. ethylene propylene diene rubber particles into a grinder, grinding and crushing into micro powder until the particle size is less than 10 mu m, adding micro powder into 50-80 pts. wt. glass fiber solution, heating to 80-100 degrees C, stirring at 350-550 revolutions/minute to the paste with the viscosity of 3000-5000 cp for 4-8 hours, transferring into a drier, drying at 40-50 degrees C, extruding and granulating to obtain product, where the metal powder mixture comprises cobalt, nickel, and titanium at a mass ratio of 1:1:1. The casting solution comprises polyethersulfone, solvent, stabilizing agent and plasticizer at a volume ratio of 1:15-25:5-10:2-4. The solvent comprises dimethylacetamide, N-methyl-2-pyrrolidone and dimethylfuran at a mass ratio of 1:1-3:3-5. The stabilizing agent comprises magnesium stearate and aluminum stearate at a mass ratio of 1:1-3. The plasticizer is trioctyl citrate, epoxy soybean oil butyl oleate, or epoxy silkworm pupa oleic acid butyl ester.

PN JP2021091567-A; WO2021117447-A1; TW202132208-A; CN114787076-A; EP4074651-A1
TI Manufacturing hydrogen gas by concentrating            aqueous solution containing formic-acid alkali metal            salt as hydrogen storage material, protonating            formic-acid alkali metal salt with electrodialysis, and            decomposing the obtained formic acid.
AU MATSUDA H
   HIRANO M
   HIROKAZU M
   MAKOTO H
AE NITTO DENKO CORP (NITL-C)
   NITTO DENKO CORP (NITL-C)
   NITTO DENKO CORP (NITL-C)
GA 2021653482
AB    NOVELTY - Hydrogen gas is manufactured by 1st process of                concentrating aqueous solution containing                formic-acid alkali metal salt, 2nd process of                protonating greater than or equal to 1 portion of                formic-acid alkali metal salt with electrodialysis                to produce formic acid, and 3rd process of                decomposing the formic acid and manufacturing the                hydrogen gas.
   USE - Manufacture of hydrogen gas using formic-acid                alkali metal salt as hydrogen storage                material.
   ADVANTAGE - Hydrogen is stored in the state excellent in                handling, and hydrogen gas can be manufactured with                high efficiency.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for:
   (1) hydrogen storage method which involves                producing formic-acid alkali metal salt in aqueous                solution using carbon dioxide in the presence of                alkali metal salt and concentrating the aqueous                solution containing the formic-acid alkali metal                salt; and
   (2) hydrogen gas manufacturing system                comprising concentration apparatus which                concentrates aqueous solution containing                formic-acid alkali metal salt, electrodialyzer                which protonates greater than or equal to 1 portion                of formic-acid alkali metal salt with                electrodialysis to produce formic acid, and                formic-acid decomposition apparatus which                decomposes the formic acid and manufactures                hydrogen gas.

PT P
PN CN112795442-A
TI Preparing plant polypeptide beer involves placing wort in fermentation tank, adding activated yeast liquid t fermentation tank for fermentation, and adding maca extract, rhododendron tea extract and purple rice supernatant to tank.
AU LU Y
   DAI Y
AE YUNNAN ZIPI BEER CO LTD (YUNN-Non-standard)
GA 202156829E
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing plant polypeptide beer involves placing wort in a fermentation tank, adding activated yeast liquid for fermentation, keeping it at a temperature of 8-9 degrees C for 3-5 days, and adding maca extract, rhododendron tea extract and purple rice supernatant to tank, heating it to 11-12 degrees C, and sealing tank at a pressure of 0.1-0.14MPa for 9-10 days. The diacetyl content in liquid in tank drops below 0.1mg/kg and then temperature is lowered to 0-2 degrees C, and tank pressure is kept at 0.06-0.08Mpa for another 10-12 days, and then filtered to obtain clear wine. The black carrot powder, mulberry extract, corn oligopeptide and walnut peptide are added to clear wine, filtered to obtain filtrate, which is filled with carbon dioxide for flushing circulation.
   USE - Method for preparing plant polypeptide beer.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to prepare plant polypeptide beer, which is clear, transparent and dark purple in color, foam is fine and reddish, has obvious malt flavor, wine fragrance and fruit aroma, mellow and refreshing taste.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Conditions: The activated yeast liquid is prepared by taking 12 degrees P boiled wort, adding an equal amount of sterile water to cool to 30-32 degrees C, and adding beer dry yeast to keep it warm and activate to obtain activated yeast liquid. The added amount of activated yeast liquid accounts for 0.3-0.5 wt.% of whole wort mass. The maca extract is prepared by taking maca, adding ethanol to extract, and filtering it to obtain an alcohol extract and a filter residue. The filter residue is extracted with water, filtered, and concentrated to obtain a water extract. The alcohol extract and water extract are combined to obtain maca extract. The rhododendron tea extract is prepared by taking rhododendron tea, adding water to extract, filtering, and concentrating it to obtain rhododendron tea extract. The purple rice supernatant is prepared by taking purple rice, adding water to grind the slurry, filtering and centrifuging it to obtain supernatant. The black carrot powder is prepared by taking fresh black carrots, squeezing juice, centrifuging it to obtain supernatant liquid, and drying it to obtain black carrot powder. The mulberry extract is prepared by taking mulberry, squeezing the juice, centrifuging it to obtain supernatant liquid, and drying it to obtain mulberry extract. The corn oligopeptide is prepared by pulping and filtering corn syrup to obtain filtrate, which is dehydrated, dried, and grounded to obtain solid corn syrup. The ethanol is added to solid corn syrup, hydrolytic enzyme is added for first hydrolysis, distilled until alcohol taste is not obtained, water and mixed enzymes are added for second hydrolysis to obtain a corn gluten meal hydrolysate. The corn gluten meal hydrolysate is decolored and filtered. The walnut peptide is prepared by taking, pulping and filtering walnut to obtain filtrate, dehydrating filtrate and subjecting it to drying, grinding, alkaline extraction and acid precipitation, and then washing it with alpha-amylase to remove starch to obtain walnut protein. The enzyme is added to walnut protein for hydrolysis, and then filtering it to obtain hydrolysate. The body color of plant polypeptide beer presents a deep purple red, and 100mL of plant polypeptide beer contains 0.05-0.12g maca extract, 0.01-0.05g rhododendron tea extract, 1-5g purple rice supernatant, 0.05-0.2g black carrot powder, 0.01-0.05g mulberry extract, 0.1-0.5g corn oligopeptide and 0.01-0.1g walnut peptide.
DC D16 (Fermentation industry - including fermentation equipment, brewing, yeast production, production of pharmaceuticals and other chemicals by fermentation, microbiology, production of vaccines and antibodies, cell and tissue culture and genetic engineering.)
MC D05-A02C; D05-B02
IP A61K-036/899; A61K-038/01; A61P-001/14; A61P-039/00; C12C-011/02; C12C-005/00; C12R-001/865

PN CN112403433-A
TI Preparation method of sub-mesoporous zeolite molecular sieve adsorbent for e.g. adsorbing propylene, by performing structural stabilization modification using natural stilbene ore powder, pulverizing, replacing calcium ions, and heating.
AU LONG Y
   PU P
   SHEN F
   SHEN W
   ZHANG H
AE FUYU ZHANGJIAGANG NEW MATERIAL TECHNOLOG (FUYU-Non-standard)
GA 202123668U
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation method of sub-mesoporous zeolite molecular sieve adsorbent involves (i) performing structural stabilization modification using natural stilbene ore powder to make sub-mesoporous zeolite molecular sieve adsorbent, and (ii) mechanically pulverizing the natural stilbene powder, partially replacing calcium ions in the mineral powder by multiple cation exchanges of salts or dilute mineral acid aqueous solution, and heating at a temperature of 350-600 degrees C for 1-2 hours to prepare the product.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing sub-mesoporous zeolite molecular sieve adsorbent for adsorbing ammonia, carbon dioxide, ethylene, propylene, methanol and other small molecular gases or organic molecules in exhaust gas, and methane in coalbed gas or petroleum gas (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method uses structure stabilization modification to make the natural stilbene have higher methane adsorption capacity and adsorption selectivity for nitrogen, which other structural types zeolite molecular sieves rarely have. The adsorbent is suitable for adsorption and separation of small molecular gases or organic matter, removal of toxic and harmful molecules, and concentration and enrichment of methane in coalbed methane and oilfield gas.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: In step (i), the natural stilbene ore powder is modified by structure stabilization to increase the heat resistance stability of the crystal structure of the STI ore powder from 350-1000 degrees C, and the hydrothermal stability in saturated steam is increased to 750 degrees C. In step (ii), the cation exchange is carried out at 85-95 degrees C for 2-5 hours. In step (ii), the ratio of the STI mineral powder and the solution is 1:5-1:20. In step (ii), the number of cation exchanges is 2-4 times. In step (ii), the degree of substitution of calcium ions in the mineral powder, i.e. the degree of calcium ion exchange is 80-98%. Preferred Components: In step (ii), the salt is ammonium salt comprising ammonium chloride, ammonium sulfate, ammonium nitrate, ammonium acetate or ammonium oxalate. In step (ii), the dilute mineral acid is hydrochloric acid, sulfuric acid, nitric acid, acetic acid or oxalic acid.

PN CN112250110-A
TI Preparing ammonium metavanadate without waste water discharge, comprises e.g. carrying out desorption or back extraction of loaded resin or loaded organic phase, adding ammonium bicarbonate, and adding into calcium oxide.
AU LI Q
   ZHOU L
   ZHANG G
   WU S
   ZENG L
   CAO Z
   GUAN W
AE UNIV CENT SOUTH (UYCS-C)
GA 202111244J
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing ammonium metavanadate without waste water discharge, comprises (i) carrying out desorption or back extraction of loaded resin or loaded organic phase that adsorbs vanadium through the conversion solution to obtain desorption liquid or back extraction liquid, (ii) adding ammonium bicarbonate into the desorption liquid or stripping liquid of step (i) or passing carbon dioxide and ammonia into the solution for vanadium precipitation to obtain ammonium metavanadate and crystallization mother liquor, (iii) adding the crystallization mother liquor of step (ii) into at least one calcium oxide or calcium hydroxide for conversion, and returning the transformed liquid into the next round of desorption or back extraction.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing ammonium metavanadate without waste water discharge.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: reduces waste water discharge; avoids the discharge of a large amount of ammonia nitrogen mixed wastewater during the preparation of ammonium metavanadate by traditional ion exchange and extraction processes; greatly reduces the consumption of alkali in the process of desorption or stripping; reduce production costs; and is suitable for industrial production.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: In the step (ii), the addition amount of ammonium bicarbonate is 1-3 times the theoretical amount, the vanadium precipitation time is 0.1-5 hours, and the vanadium precipitation temperature is 0-60 degrees C. In step (iii), the addition amount of at least one calcium oxide or calcium hydroxide is 1-3 times the theoretical amount, the conversion time is 0.5-4 hours, and the conversion temperature is 20-100 degrees C. The method comprises adding at least one sodium carbonate or sodium hydroxide when the transformed liquid is returned to the next round of desorption or back extraction. Preferred Components: In step (i), the supported resin is weakly basic ion exchange resin. The loaded organic phase is weakly alkaline extractant. The weakly alkaline extractant includes tertiary amine extractant, primary amine extractant or secondary amine extractant. The conversion liquid is sodium carbonate solution and/or sodium hydroxide solution.
