PN DE102019209044-A1
TI Producing fired end products from natural, carbonate-containing, granular substances as educt, comprises forming first reaction zone, heating first reaction zone, discharging hot gases formed in reaction zone and discharging hot end product.
AU STUMPF T
AE LITHERM TECHNOLOGIES GMBH (LITH-Non-standard)
GA 2020C9590X
AB    NOVELTY - Producing fired end products from natural, carbonate-containing, granular substances as educt, comprises: (a) forming a first reaction zone in the form of a fluidized bed or a fluidized bed with the starting material and a fluidizing medium which contains water vapor; (b) heating the first reaction zone to at least one or more predetermined temperature ranges over a predetermined residence time for burning the starting material in the first reaction zone; (c) discharge of hot gases formed in the first reaction zone, the carbon dioxide and contain water vapor from the first reaction zone; (d) discharging the hot end product from the first reaction zone after the predetermined residence time; and (e) using the heat contained in the hot gases and/or the hot end product from the first reaction zone to preheat the starting material and/or the fluidization medium.
   USE - The method is useful for producing fired end products from natural, carbonate-containing, granular substances as educt.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is carried out in simple and efficient manner.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for device
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The water vapor is used as the fluidization medium. The heat is recovered by condensation of the water vapor contained in the hot gas and the condensation results in a material separation of carbon dioxide. The water obtained during the condensation is used again to generate water vapor for the fluidization medium. The heating of the first reaction zone takes place through the combustion of fuels and/or through the use of electrical energy, preferably from renewable sources. The first reaction zone is heated by means of internals in the reaction zone and in particular by heat pipes. The end product is sintered in a second reaction zone in which predetermined temperature ranges of 400-1500 degrees C and predetermined residence times. The process steps (c) and (d) are also carried out for the second reaction zone. The process step (e) is also carried out for the second reaction zone. The different fired end products, e.g. soft, hard and medium fired, are produced by the predetermined temperatures and dwell times. The start-up and shutdown air and/or carbon dioxide is used as a fluidizing medium.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INSTRUMENTATION AND TESTING - Preferred Components: The heat generating device is a combustion device to which a fuel can be fed by means of a fuel feed line and an oxidizing agent can be fed by means of a first gas feed line, the fuel and/or the oxidizing agent can be preheated by the heat recovery device. The calciner comprises a second reactor vessel in which a second reaction zone is formed for further thermal treatment, preferably sintering of the product from the first reactor vessel. The first reactor vessel is connected to the second reactor vessel via an intermediate product line, and a product discharge is provided for discharging the end product from the second reactor vessel. The heat transfer device comprises heat pipes for coupling the heat from the process heat generating device into the first reactor vessel. The heat recovery device has a condenser for the condensation of water vapor contained in the hot gas from the first reactor container and the material separation of carbon dioxide from the hot gases. The device via a fluidizing agent feed line air and/or carbon dioxide can be fed to the first reactor vessel.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

PN JP6792092-B1; JP2021127256-A
TI Calcium aluminate type filler for e.g., aqueous resin, comprises aqueous-based blast-furnace-slag liquid which blocks hydration reaction by adding blocking material to blast furnace slag, and curing agent which promotes hydration reaction.
AU SUGIYAMA M
   SUGIYAMA N
   TAKEI A
AE FUKKO KK (FUKK-Non-standard)
GA 2020B6188K
AB    NOVELTY - A calcium aluminate type filler comprises (A) aqueous-based blast-furnace-slag liquid which blocks hydration reaction by adding blocking material to blast furnace slag, and (B) curing agent (hydraulic reaction initiator) which releases blocking material that blocks the hydration reaction of aqueous-based blast-furnace-slag liquid and promotes hydration reaction, where liquid mixture of components (A) and (B) is accommodated in a container, and sealed.
   USE - A calcium aluminate type filler for aqueous resin and aqueous coating material (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - Generation amount of carbon dioxide and waste material and amount of energy consumption can reduced. When the blast-furnace-slag aqueous solution and the calcium aluminate type filler are added as a modifier to existing general-purpose resin and commercially available coating material, a uniform lusterless coating surface is obtained; drying rate of a coating film is accelerated; a strong adhesivity is acquired; improvement of heat-resistant performance and fireproof performance is obtained; and after coating, when overlapping a coating surface, improvement of a blocking resistance is obtained.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - CERAMICS AND GLASS - Preferred Components: Components (A) and (B) are separately accommodated in a container, and are mixed on the spot before use. The curing agent is carbonated and selected from sodium carbonate, potassium carbonate, lithium carbonate, and/or sodium silicate (liquid water glass). Preferred Processes: By mixing blast-furnace-slag powder with sodium silicate aqueous solution and accommodating in a container, the sodium silicate functions as a blocking material and further as a curing agent (hydraulic reaction initiator) for releasing the blocking material. The blocking material is added to the blast furnace slag to block the hydration reaction containing oxo acids and/or oxo acid salts.

PN CN111632606-A
TI Preparation of cobalt sulfide-cerium dioxide nitrogen reduction catalyst for ammonia synthesis, involves hydrothermally reacting cobalt and cerium reagent in specific reaction solution, washing, calcining, adding sulfur and curing.
AU JU H
   ZHOU J
   REN X
   FAN D
   SUN X
   KUANG X
   WANG B
   ZHANG Y
   WEI Q
AE UNIV JINAN (UNJN-C)
GA 2020916762
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of multilayer stacked nano-sheet cobalt sulfide-cerium dioxide nitrogen reduction catalyst involves adding predetermined amount of cobalt and cerium reagent to a specific reaction solution, obtaining a pre-reaction solution, subjecting the solution to hydrothermal processing by heating at predetermined temperature for predetermined time, naturally cooling, washing, drying, collecting cobalt-cerium precursor, place the precursor in a tube furnace, introducing nitrogen, calcining, and adding sulfur powder as a vulcanizing reagent agent for curing.
   USE - Preparation of multilayer stacked nano-sheet cobalt sulfide-cerium dioxide nitrogen reduction catalyst used for ammonia synthesis by electrochemical nitrogen reduction.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables economical, simple and non-toxic preparation of multilayer stacked nano-sheet cobalt sulfide-cerium dioxide nitrogen reduction catalyst having excellent stability and catalytic activity without using noble metals e.g. gold, ruthenium, silver and rhodium. The catalyst enables environmentally-friendly ammonia synthesis by electrochemical nitrogen reduction with high yield of 40 mu ghour-1mg-1 catalyst relative to standard hydrogen electrode at -0.2 V and Faraday efficiency of 10.2%, without emission of carbon dioxide.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - CHEMICAL ENGINEERING - Preferred Apparatus: The single-chamber membrane electrode nitrogen reduction electrocatalytic test system comprises a fixed base, a membrane electrode comprising the catalyst, a miniature funnel receiver, an ammonia solution storage tank, the electrolyte, a single-chamber electrolytic cell, a liquid inlet pipe, the counter electrode, the reference electrode, a pressure balance outlet, a working electrode lead, a micro dropper, and nitrogen delivery channel.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The specific reaction solution is a mixed solution of urea and thiourea. The cobalt source is one or more chosen from cobalt(II) nitrate hexahydrate, cobalt chloride, cobalt(II) acetylacetonate, cobalt sulfate, and cobalt acetate. The concentration of the cobalt source solution is 0.005-0.04 mol/L. The cerium source is one or more chosen from cerium ammonium nitrate, cerium(III) nitrate hexahydrate, cerium(III) chloride heptahydrate, cerium sulfate, and cerium acetate. The molar ratio of the cobalt source and the cerium source is 10:1-1:1. The vulcanizing reagent is sublimed sulfur. The mass ratio of the vulcanizing reagent and the cobalt-cerium precursor is 1:10-1:100. Preferred Process: The cobalt-cerium pre-reaction solution is processed at 100-180 degrees C for 6-14 hours. The curing of the precursor is performed at 300-600 degrees C a heating rate of 0.5 degrees C/minute for 1-6 hours. The catalyst is subjected to single-chamber membrane electrode nitrogen reduction electrocatalytic performance test by configuring the catalyst into 0.1-5 mg/mL ink, dripping resultant onto proton exchange membrane, drying at room temperature, coating surface of the dried film with Nafion (RTM: sulfonated tetrafluoroethylene-based fluoropolymer-copolymer) solution, obtaining electrocatalytic nitrogen reduction membrane electrode, and performing electrocatalytic nitrogen reduction process using electrocatalytic nitrogen reduction membrane electrode as working electrode, graphite electrode as counter electrode, silver/silver chloride electrode as reference electrode and 0.1-2 mol/L sodium sulfate-lithium perchlorate mixed solution as electrolyte.

PN CN111363570-A; CN111363570-B
TI Preparing methane-rich gas from waste by placing waste in thermal reactor and pyrolyzing, using semi-coke to catalytically reform volatile matter in reforming reactor, and passing synthesis gas and catalyst to methanation reactor.
AU CHEN D
   LUO J
   YIN L
   FENG Y
   DAI X
AE UNIV TONGJI (UYTJ-C)
GA 202063629L
AB    NOVELTY - Method for preparing methane-rich gas from waste involves (a) placing the waste in a thermal reactor and pyrolyzing it at 400-700 degrees C to obtain the solid-phase product pyrolysis semi-coke and volatiles, (b) using 60-75% semi-coke to catalytically reform the volatile matter in the reforming reactor to synthesis gas rich in hydrogen and carbon monoxide/carbon dioxide, loading 25-40% semi-coke with active components in the catalyst preparation system, carry out roasting and activating to obtain catalyst, and (c) passing the synthesis gas and the catalyst to the methanation reactor for reaction to obtain methane-rich gas, where the methane concentration in the methane-rich gas is higher than 35%, and the hydrogen concentration is lower than 20%.
   USE - The method is used for preparing methane-rich gas from waste.
   ADVANTAGE - The method adjusts the pyrolysis reforming products from waste into methane-rich gas, has important practical significance and value, cleans the waste, and improves the quality and efficiency of waste recycling energy.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The salt solution in step (b1) comprises a saturated nitrate solution, a saturated nitrite solution, a sulfate solution, a carbonate solution, a basic carbonate solution, and/or an acetate solution, where the particle size of the semi-coke-based catalyst particles is 4-60 mesh. The gas introduced during the reduction in step (b2) is hydrogen or a synthesis gas rich in hydrogen and carbon monoxide/carbon dioxide, and the reduction time is 2-8 hours. The active component in step (b) comprises a mixture of nickel, iron, cobalt, ruthenium, molybdenum, chromium and/or lanthanum, and the method comprises removing 25-40% semi-coke by screening when preparing the catalyst. Preferred Method: The method for preparing catalyst further comprises directly mixing -coke and the oxides, hydroxides, basic carbonates and composite metal oxides of the active components, kneading, and shaping to complete the loading to obtain the catalyst. The method for preparing the catalyst step (b) comprises (b1) placing the semi-coke in the salt solution of the active component for sufficient immersion, filtering, and adding a small amount of chitosan or flour as a binder for extrusion molding to obtain semi-coke-based catalyst particles, and (b2) drying the semi-coke-based catalyst particles, and calcining at a temperature of 400-600 degrees C under an nitrogen atmosphere, and reducing by a gas at a temperature of 200-600 degrees C to obtain the catalyst. Preferred Device: The methanation reactor in step (c) comprises one of a fixed bed reactor, a fluidized bed reactor, a moving bed reactor, and a slurry bed reactor. Preferred Properties: The moisture content of the waste in step (a) is 12-42%, and the calorific value is less than or equal to 8-20 MJ/Nm3, and the waste comprises a mixture of domestic garbage, straw, waste forestry branches, sludge or livestock and/or poultry manure. Preferred Conditions: The gas is hydrogen, and the temperature is 300-400 degrees C, the reduction time is 2-4 hours, and the introduced gas is the synthesis gas rich in hydrogen and carbon monoxide/carbon dioxide, the temperature is 300-500 degrees C, and the reduction time is 3-8 hours. The reaction temperature in the methanation reactor in step (c) is 200-600 degrees C, the gauge pressure is 0-5.0 MPa, and the gas space velocity is greater than or equal to 32000 h-1.

PN CN111214704-A; CN111214704-B
TI Preparation of decellularized nerve graft involves immersing peripheral nerve in distilled water and solution containing surfactant in order, heating to remove protein of peripheral nerve and washing with sodium dodecyl sulfate solution.
AU YANG Y
   ZHAO Y
   ZHANG L
   LI G
   LING J
   YANG P
AE UNIV NANTONG (UYNT-C)
GA 202052068B
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of decellularized nerve graft involves cutting out peripheral nerve, removing external fat, blood vessels and connective tissue of peripheral nerve, immersing peripheral nerve in oscillating bath for 12 hours in distilled water, immersing peripheral nerve into solution containing surfactant, placing in high-pressure reaction kettle, passing carbon dioxide to discharge the air in the high-pressure reaction kettle, heating to 25-35 degrees C under pressure of 15-25 MPa to remove the protein of the peripheral nerve, washing with 1% sodium dodecyl sulfate solution for 3 hours and immersing in distilled water. The surfactant is Triton X-100 (RTM: t-octyl phenoxy polyethoxy ethanol) or Triton X-200 (RTM: sodium octylphenoxyethoxyethyl sulfonate), preferably Triton X-200 (RTM: sodium octylphenoxyethoxyethyl sulfonate).
   USE - Preparation of decellularized nerve graft.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is short and carried out under low temperature condition, adopts supercritical fluid extraction reaction with less dosage of chemical reagent for decellularization process, which effectively reduce the immunogenicity and the damage to the nerve structure, and also retains laminin in axon regeneration, which improves clinical effect.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The solution comprises 1-3 wt.% surfactant.

PN CN111197173-A; CN111197173-B
TI Electroplating preparation of non-noble metal            single atom doped two-dimensional material comprises            e.g. preparing two-dimensional material electrodes by            direct growth or transfer, using three-electrode            system.
AU SUN Z
   XUAN N
AE UNIV FUDAN (UYFU-C)
   UNIV FUDAN (UYFU-C)
GA 2020479096
AB    NOVELTY - Electroplating preparation of non-noble metal                single atom doped two-dimensional material                comprises (1) preparing two-dimensional material                electrodes by direct growth or transfer, (2) using                a three-electrode system, with two-dimensional                material as the working electrode, graphite rod as                the counter electrode, and saturated calomel as the                reference electrode, the electrolyte uses saturated                non-noble metal hydrogen oxide aqueous solution,                adjusting the system pH to 9-14, and controlling                the concentration of free metal ions in the system                at 1010-106 g/ml, adopting chronopotentiometry or                cyclic voltammetry to perform electrodeposition                under magnetic stirring, electrodeposition time is                0.1-24 hour, and (3) completing the electroplating,                washing the two-dimensional material electrode with                deionized water and drying with nitrogen.
   USE - The method is useful for electroplating                preparation of non-noble metal single atom doped                two-dimensional material.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: is simple, easy to implement,                reacts at room temperature, and adjusts the type                and concentration of doped ions accurately. The                non-noble metal-doped two-dimensional material: has                high electrocatalytic hydrogen evolution and                electrocatalytic carbon dioxide reduction                performance.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The two-dimensional                  material is a transition metal chalcogenide                  compound transition metal dichalcogenides,                  nitrogen-doped reduced graphene oxide NRGO or                  transition metal carbon group compound MXene. The                  transition metal chalcogenide compound is MX2,                  where M is titanium, zirconium, hydrogen                  fluoride, vanadium, niobium, tantalum,                  molybdenum, tungsten, rhenium or platinum, X is                  sulfur, selenium or tellurium, in the transition                  metal carbon group compound MXene, M is                  molybdenum, titanium, vanadium, niobium or                  tantalum. The non-noble metal is iron, cobalt,                  nickel, copper or zinc. The metal hydroxide is                  cobalt hydroxide. Preferred Method: In the                  process, controlling the magnetic stirring speed                  from 100-600 rotations/minute. In the process,                  separating the metal hydroxide precipitate and                  the two-dimensional material electrode by filter                  paper or ion semi-permeable membrane.

TI Determination of carbon stable isotope of ethyl            acetate, ethyl caproate and ethyl lactate in scent-type            liquor involves injecting scent-type liquor in column,            placing components in gas chromatography-mass            spectrometer and calculating.

   YAN Z
   HU J
   MA Y
   REN L

GA 202025785M
AB    NOVELTY - Determination of carbon stable isotope of                ethyl acetate, ethyl caproate and ethyl lactate in                scent-type liquor involves injecting scent-type                liquor in capillary column of gas chromatography to                separate ethyl acetate, ethyl caproate, ethyl                lactate and other components in the liquor, placing                ethyl acetate, ethyl caproate, and ethyl lactate in                gas chromatography-combustion isotope ratio mass                spectrometer using Backflush function of                spectrometer, measuring the delta 13C value of the                reference material, recalibrating and calculating                the carbon stable isotope delta 13C ratio of ethyl                acetate, ethyl caproate, and ethyl lactate in                scent-type liquor. AU KIM C

   LIM Y S
AE SUN & SEA CO LTD (SUNS-Non-standard)
GA 202023937X
AB    NOVELTY - Purifying water, comprises treating water contaminated with algae in a water treatment plant by applying high voltage micropulse to the water flowing into a first treatment space of a water treatment plant to destroy and settle the algae's air bubbles contained in the water; discharging supernatant in water; introducing a flocculant into the supernatant flowing into a second treatment space of the water purification plant, and precipitating the algae by agglutinating reaction between the algae remaining in the supernatant and the flocculant.
   USE - The method is useful for purifying water, e.g. rivers, lakes, and groundwater.
   ADVANTAGE - The method primarily removes algae from the sedimentation site, reduces the alkalinity of the raw water to promote the aggregation reaction, and reduces the input amount of the pH adjusting agent (carbon dioxide) to lower the pH of the raw water; and lowers the zeta potential of algae to promote the coagulation reaction by the coagulant.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for a waterworks water treatment system for effectively treating water contaminated with algae.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ENVIRONMENT - Preferred Components: The high voltage micropulse applied for the high voltage micropulse discharge has a voltage of 5-30 kV and a pulse width of 6-100 us.

AU DONG X

   GONG M
   ZHAO Y
   HU Q
AE TECH INST PHYSICS & CHEM CHINESE ACAD (CAPC-C)
GA 202009901J
AB    NOVELTY - The device has a constant temperature module connected with a variable volume module and a visual balance kettle. The variable volume module is provided with a shell, an end cover and an elastic element. The end cover is fixedly connected with the elastic element, where the shell and the end cover are filled with pressurized fluid. The visual balance kettle is provided with a balance body and an observing lens. A measuring module is connected with an outer side of a bath. PT P
PN CN110627083-A; CN110627083-B
TI Preparing amino-functionalized MCM mesoporous            molecular sieve useful for gas adsorption and            separation, comprises e.g. mixing organic template,            silicon source and alkali source, crystallizing,            removing liquid phase and drying solid phase.
AU WU K
   REN X
   LIU Y
   JIA Z
   YANG G
AE CHINA PETROLEUM & CHEM CORP (SNPC-C)
   SINOPEC BEIJING RES INST CHEM IND (SNPC-C)
GA 202002590D
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing amino-functionalized MCM mesoporous                molecular sieve comprises (i) mixing organic                template, water, silicon source and alkali source                into a gel, and hydrothermally crystallizing the                gelled mixture, performing solid-liquid separation,                washing the separated solid phase and drying to                obtain MCM molecular sieve raw powder, (ii) mixing                raw MCM molecular sieve powder with passivation                agent to passivate, adding amino modifier,                reacting, subjecting the product to solid-liquid                separation, washing the separated solid sample and                drying to obtain dried product, and (iii) mixing                the dried product with extractant for extraction,                removing the liquid phase, and drying the solid                phase.
   USE - The molecular sieve is useful for gas                adsorption and separation, preferably to adsorb and                separate acid gases i.e. carbon dioxide                (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method does not damage the structure and                crystallinity of the molecular sieve channels and                can graft amino groups inside the molecular sieve                channels.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for                amino-functionalized MCM mesoporous molecular sieve                prepared by the above method.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: In the step (i), the                  molar ratio of each component in the mixture is                  expressed as silicon dioxide: a-water: b-R:                  c-OH-, where R is an organic template agent and                  the value of a is 80-160, preferably 100-140, the                  value of b is 0.1-0.7, preferably 0.2-0.5, the                  value of c is 2-7, preferably 4-5. The organic                  template agent comprises cationic surfactants                  having 12-20 carbon atoms, preferably cationic                  surfactants having 12-16 carbon atoms, more                  preferably hexadecyltrimethylammonium bromide,                  hexadecyltrimethylammonium chloride and                  hexadecyltriethyl ammonium bromide and/or the                  silicon source comprises white carbon black,                  ethyl orthosilicate, sodium silicate and silica                  sol and/or the alkali source comprises sodium                  hydroxide, tetramethylammonium hydroxide and                  aqueous ammonia. In the step (ii), the                  passivation agent includes an organosilane,                  diphenyldichlorosilane, trimethylchlorosilane and                  dimethyldichlorosilane, where the organosilane is                  represented by the general formula RaRbRcSiRd,                  where Ra, Rb, Rc and Rd are the same or                  different, independently taken from hydrogen,                  halogen, C1-C20 alkyl, C1-C20 alkoxy, C3-C20                  cycloalkyl, C6-C20 aryl and C1-C20 haloalkyl and                  Ra, Rb, Rc, and Rd are not hydrogen and/or                  halogen at the same time, preferably, Rd is                  halogen and Ra, Rb and Rc are not hydrogen and/or                  halogen at the same time and/or the ratio of the                  molar amount of passivating agent to MCM                  molecular sieve raw powder is (0.001-0.04):5,                  preferably (0.002-0.02):5. In the step (ii), the                  amino modifier is organosilane with an amino                  structure, preferably contains at least one of                  3-aminopropyltrimethoxysilane, 3-(phenylamino)                  propyltrimethoxysilane and                  3-aminopropyltriethoxysilane and/or the ratio of                  the molar amount of the amino modifier to the                  mass of the MCM molecular sieve raw powder is                  (0.01-0.1):5. The organic solvent is added when                  the amino modifier is added and it is preferred                  that the organic solvent contains at least one of                  alcohol compounds. In the step (iii), the                  extractant is an ether, preferably the mass ratio                  of the extractant to the MCM molecular sieve raw                  powder is 4: 1-2:1 and the extraction time is 2-4                  hours. The MCM molecular sieve raw powder is                  MCM-41 molecular sieve raw powder. Preferred                  Method: In the step (i), the the gelling                  temperature is 0-60 degrees C, preferably 40-60                  degrees C, the temperature of the hydrothermal                  crystallization is 110-140 degrees C, preferably                  120-130 degrees C and the time of the                  hydrothermal crystallization is 72-108 hours,                  preferably 84-100 hours. In the step (ii), the                  passivation temperature is 10-90 degrees C,                  preferably 50-70 degrees C and the passivation                  time is 0.5-20 hours, preferably 4-7 hours. In                  the step (ii), the temperature of the reaction is                  60-120 degrees C, preferably 80-100 degrees C and                  the reaction time is 4-8 hours, preferably 5-7                  hours. In the step (i)-(iii), the drying                  temperature is 100-140 degrees C, preferably                  110-130 degrees C.
DC E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); J01 (Separation - including evaporation, crystallisation, solvent extraction, chromatography, dialysis, osmosis including drying gases and/or vapours, and separation of solids from gases, liquids and other solids. Isotope separation, filter materials (including molecular sieves for separation), and centrifuges (except where used for analysis) (B01D, B03, B04, B07B).)

PN CN110600738-A; CN110600738-B
TI Preparing low-temperature lithium ion battery hard carbon cathode material comprises e.g. washing raw materials, drying, filling dried raw materials in a box, placing the box in a high-temperature oven, passing the protective gas.
AU LUO W
   YIN H
   ZHAO Z
   CHEN G
AE YANGZHOU HUADA NEW ENERGY CO LTD (YANG-Non-standard)
GA 202001240M
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing low-temperature lithium ion battery hard carbon cathode material comprises (i) washing raw materials, then drying to obtain dried raw materials, where raw materials are graphite, coal, wood, bamboo carbon-rich element or industrial organic macromolecular resin, (ii) filling dried raw materials of step (i) in a box, placing the box in a high-temperature oven, passing the protective gas, exhaust air from the high temperature furnace, and (iii) heating the high temperature furnace at 1200-1600 degrees C for pyrolysis, mixing a reactive gas into the protective gas to obtain final product, where pyrolysis time is 1-6 hours, reaction gas is carbon dioxide, and penetration ratio of the reaction gas and protective gas is 0.02-0.1:1.
   USE - The material is useful for lithium ion battery with high energy density and excellent rate performance (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method: has short-range ordered, long-range disorder, lower than surface, high tap density, and excellent electrochemical performance; suitable for low temperature environment; and method: has simple preparation process and low cost.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The protective gas is nitrogen or argon. In step (ii), the box is an alumina crucible. In step (iii), mixed gas flow rate is 100-500 ml/minute. Preferred Method: In step (i), drying temperature is 100-220 degrees C, and drying time is 5-10 hours. The heating rate of the high-temperature furnace is 1-3 degrees C/minute.

PN CN110559850-A; CN210993766-U
TI Air purifier, has reaction chamber provided with            heating mechanism, guide pipe connected with input            pipe, and reaction component connected with output pipe            to form transportation channel of output pipe.
AU YAN W
AE ZHONGSHAN LINGYU MACHINERY CO LTD (ZHON-Non-standard)
GA 2019A6750R
AB    NOVELTY - The purifier has a shell provided with a                reaction component, a heat exchange mechanism, a                drying mechanism and a guide pipe. The heat                exchange mechanism is provided with an input pipe                and an output pipe. The input pipe is connected                with the output pipe. The reaction component is                fixed with a reaction chamber. The reaction chamber                is connected with the input pipe and the output                pipe. The reaction chamber is provided with a                heating mechanism. The guide pipe is connected with                the input pipe. The reaction component is connected                with the output pipe to form a transportation                channel of the output pipe. PN CN110451545-A; CN110451545-B
TI Preparing rod-shaped calcium carbonate useful for polyethylene breathable film, comprises e.g. calcining limestone, sending calcium hydroxide suspension into carbonation reactor, carrying out carbonation reaction, drying and packaging.
AU YANG A
   ZHU Y
   LI Y
   MAN Z
AE GUANGXI HUANA NEW MATERIAL TECHNOLOGY CO (GUAN-Non-standard)
   GUANGXI HUANA NEW MATERIAL TECHNOLOGY CO (GUAN-Non-standard)
GA 201998029J
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing rod-shaped calcium carbonate comprises (i) calcining limestone at 1100-1250 degrees C into quicklime, digesting quicklime to obtain calcium hydroxide, and configuring refined calcium hydroxide into calcium hydroxide suspension with a specific gravity of 1.03-1.05; (ii) sending calcium hydroxide suspension into the carbonation reactor, adding the tetrasodium pyrophosphate solution, stirring, passing mixed gas containing carbon dioxide, , adding Tween-20 (RTM: Polysorbate 20) when the pH of the reaction system drops to 10.5, reacting continuality the reaction until the pH value of the reaction system drops to 6.5-7 and carrying out carbonation reaction to obtain calcium carbonate slurry; and (iii) aging calcium carbonate slurry at 60-80 degrees C for 5-8 hours, treating surface with sodium stearate, drying, crushing, classifying and packaging.
   USE - The rod-shaped calcium carbonate is useful for polyethylene breathable film (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method: produces calcium carbonate with rod and regular shape, good dispersibility, stable extrusion, processing fluidity and compatibility; and improves mechanical properties, high elongation, uniform micropore distribution, breathable and waterproof effect for film.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The adding amount of the tetrasodium pyrophosphate solution is 0.1-1 wt.% of dry mass of calcium hydroxide. The concentration of the tetrasodium pyrophosphate is 0.5 mol/liter. The using amount of Tween-20 (RTM: Polysorbate 20) is 0.1 -1 wt.% dry mass of calcium hydroxide in the suspension. Preferred Method: The carbonation reaction is carried out at 800-1000 r/min, adding Tween-20 (RTM: Polysorbate 20) and controlling stirring at 200-300 r/min. The method comprises controlling volume concentration of carbon dioxide in the mixed gas to 30-35%, and the flow rate at 2-4 m3/h.

AB    NOVELTY - Method for prolonging shelf life of garlic involves (i) selecting garlic which is free of mechanical damage, disease and insect pests in the garlic just picked, peeling, placing the garlic in distilled water for cleaning, air-blowing and air-drying, (ii) soaking the cleaned garlic in prepared preservative solution for 20-40 minutes, draining off after the garlic is soaked, and performing air drying using a blower, (iii) pre-cooling the air-dried garlic, and fumigating using 1-methylcyclopropene after pre-cooling, (iv) packaging the fumigated garlic in a fresh-keeping bag, filling nitrogen, carbon dioxide and oxygen to the fresh-keeping bag, and immediately binding the garlic to the fresh-keeping bag, and (v) placing the packaged garlic in a fresh-keeping storehouse, controlling the temperature to be -1 to -4 degrees C, and the humidity to be 60-75%.
   USE - Method for prolonging shelf life of garlic.
   ADVANTAGE - The method controls the storage environment, prolongs the fresh-keeping period and ensures the high quality of the garlic.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - FOOD - Preferred Components: The preservative in step (ii) is 1-3 pts. wt. citric acid, 0.2-0.6 pt. wt. phytic acid, 3-8 pts. wt. trehalose, 1-5 pts. wt. propolis, 0.5-1.6 pts. wt. calcium stearate, 0.02-0.1 pt. wt. sodium chloride and 70-85 pts. wt. water. Preferred Composition: The volume ratio of garlic and preservative is (3-5):1. The concentration of 1-methylcyclopropene in step (iii) is 0.5-1 ug/l, and the fumigation time is 20-26 hour. In step (iv), the content of oxygen is 4-6%, carbon dioxide is 12-18% and nitrogen is 70-86%. The gap maintained between each bag of garlic in step (v) is 5-10 cm.

The                degradable resin is one or more of                polyadipate/butylene terephthalate and polylactic                acid.
   USE - Foamed midsole material.
   ADVANTAGE - The environmentally-friendly foaming midsole                material has excellent degradability, high                bio-based content and excellent performance. The                midsole material recycles the discarded resources,                reduces the use of the non-renewable petroleum                resources, and the degradable portion reduces the                carbon dioxide emissions, thus achieving energy                saving and carbon reduction.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a                preparation of a foamed midsole material, which                involves kneading and granulating the ethylene                vinyl acetate composite material in turn, and then                foaming.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The active agent                  comprises 1.1-1.3 pts. wt. zinc oxide, 0.7-0.9                  pts. wt. stearic acid and 0.9-1.1 pts. wt. zinc                  stearate.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The ethylene vinyl                  acetate composite material comprises 20-30 pts.                  wt. bio-based ethylene propylene diene monomer                  rubber. Preferred Components: The crosslinking                  agent is a peroxide crosslinking agent. The                  peroxide crosslinking agent is one or more of                  dicumyl peroxide and                  1,4-di-tert-butylperoxyisopropylbenzene. The                  blowing agent is one or more of azodicarbonamide                  and 4,4-oxodiphenylsulfonylhydrazide.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Composition: The ethylene vinyl                  acetate copolymer has a vinyl acetate mass                  content of 26-40%.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - TEXTILES AND PAPER - Preferred Components: The plant fiber powder                  is one or more of aspen powder, hemp powder and                  corn cob powder.

TI Powder of foam, having specific particle size and diameter of pores useful for vacuum insulation panels, oil absorbing materials, insulating material and lightweight construction components, and for producing moldings by thermal compression.
AU OBERHOFFER R
   MUELLER A
AE SUMTEQ GMBH (SUMT-Non-standard)
GA 201946088D
AB    NOVELTY - Powder of foam, having the median particle size of 0.5-1000 mu m, and median of the diameter of the pores of the particles of 10-500 nm, is claimed.
   USE - The powder is useful for vacuum insulation panels, oil absorbing materials, insulating material and lightweight construction components, and for producing moldings by thermal compression (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The powder has improved thermal insulation properties.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for:
   (1) composition containing powder and opacifiers; and
   (2) preparing the powder, comprising (a) adding crosslinker to monomer or a mixture of monomers to obtain a monomer mixture, (b) polymerizing the monomer mixture, (c) drying the resulting polymer, (d) swelling the polymer with a swelling agent, (e) placing the swollen polymer in carbon dioxide atmosphere with a pressure of 50-500 bar and a temperature of 0-120 degrees C, (f) the pressure is lowered after a waiting time of 10-180 minutes to the atmospheric pressure, where the polymer foam is obtained, (g) the obtained polymer foam is ground.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The foam is a polymer foam, preferably a partially crosslinked polymer foam. The density of the foam is 30-300 kg/m3. The specific surface area is 5-500 m2/g, preferably 20-200 m2/g. The FOM factor is at least 0.01 and less than 1.2, which is defined as given in the specification. The opacifier comprises graphite, carbon black, multiwall carbon nanotubes, fullerene, grapheme and/or metal oxide. The clouding agent has an average particle size in the median of 0.01-5 mu m. Preferred Method: After step (g), (h) an opacifier is added to obtain the composition. The monomer mixture is polymerized in step (b) in water as a suspension. The swelling agent is ketone, preferably acetone.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

TI Preparation of polyoxyalkylene macromers used for            producing polyoxyalkylene brush polymer, involves            reacting hydrogen-functional initiators comprising            carbon-carbon double bond which is portion of cyclic            structure and alkylene oxides.
AU VOR D E L
   MUELLER T E
   GUERTLER C
   SUBHANI M A
   KOEHLER B
   LEITNER W
   MULLER T E
AE COVESTRO DEUT AG (CVST-C)
   COVESTRO DEUT AG (CVST-C)
   COVESTRO GERMANY AG (CVST-C)
   COVESTRO DEUT AG (CVST-C)
   COVESTRO INTELLECTUAL PROPERTY GMBH & CO (CVST-C)
   COVESTRO INTELLECTUAL PROPERTY GMBH & CO (CVST-C)
GA 2019461799
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of polyoxyalkylene macromers                involves reacting one or more hydrogen-functional                initiators and one or more alkylene oxides in the                presence of a catalyst. At least one of the                hydrogen-functional initiators comprises a                carbon-carbon double bond which is portion of a                cyclic structure. The initiator substance and the                alkylene oxide are metered continuously into the                reactor during the reaction.
   USE - Preparation of polyoxyalkylene macromers used                for producing polyoxyalkylene brush polymer for                manufacturing crosslinked polyoxyalkylene polymer                (all claimed) for elastomer applications.
   ADVANTAGE - The method provides high molecular weight                polymer with improved selectivity and                conversion.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the                following:
   (1) polyoxyalkylene macromers, which comprises                3-35 wt.%, preferably 5-25 wt.% carbon                dioxide;
   (2) production of polyoxyalkylene brush                polymer, which involves reacting the                polyoxyalkylene macromer with an olefin metathesis                catalyst in the presence of a olefin metathesis                catalyst; and
   (3) crosslinked polyoxyalkylene polymers,                which is obtained by reacting hydroxy-terminated                polyoxyalkylene brush polymers with                polyisocyanates, or reacting hydroxy-terminated                polyoxyalkylene brush polymers with polycarboxylic                acids or cyclic carboxylic acid anhydrides, or                free-radical crosslinking of polyoxyalkylene brush                polymers.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The                  hydrogen-functional initiator is a                  cyclopentene-based compound of formula (I). The                  hydrogen-functional initiator is one or more                  compounds chosen from norbornene carboxylic acid,                  hydroxy norbornene carboxylic acid, hydroxypropyl                  norbornene carboxylic acid, hydroxybutyl                  norbornene carboxylic acid, hydroxynorbornene,                  hydroxymethylnorbornene, cyclopentenecarboxylic                  acid, cyclooctenecarboxylic acid,                  cyclodecenecarboxylic acid, cyclopentenol and                  cyclooctenol. The alkylene oxide is one or more                  compound chosen from propylene oxide, ethylene                  oxide, 1-butylene oxide, 1-hexene oxide,                  1-dodecene oxide, epichlorohydrin, methyl                  glycidyl ether, ethyl glycidyl ether, butyl                  glycidyl ether, dodecyl glycidyl ether,                  tetradecyl glycidyl ether, methoxyethyl glycidyl                  ether, methoxyethoxyethyl glycidyl ether, allyl                  glycidyl ether, phenyl glycidyl ether, cresyl                  glycidyl ether, furfuryl glycidyl ether, benzyl                  glycidyl ether and tetrahydrofurfuryl glycidyl                  ether. The olefin metathesis catalyst is one or                  more ruthenium-carbene complex(s) chosen from                  dichloro(1,3-bis(2,4,6-trimethylphenyl)-2-imidazolidinylidene)(benzylidene)bis(3-bromopyridine)ruthenium(II),                  dichloro(1,3-bis(2,4,6-trimethylphenyl)-2-imidazolidinylidenes)(2-isopropoxyphenylmethylene)ruthenium                  (II),                  dichloro(benzylidenes)bis(tricyclohexylphosphines)ruthenium                  (II) and dichloro(1,3-bis                  (2-methylphenyl)-2-imidazolidinylidenes)(2-isopropoxyphenylmethylene)ruthenium                  (II).o = 0-8;R1-R5 = H, 1-22C alkyl, 6-14C aryl, 7-14C                  aralkyl or alkylaryl, 5-12C cycloalkyl, ester, or                  -COOR6;R6 = 1-22C alkyl, 6-14C aryl, 7-14C aralkyl                  or alkylaryl or 5-12C cycloalkyl;R1+R3 = 1-3C-alkylene bridge or ether                  bridge;p = 1-6;X = carboxy or OH, 1-22C alkyl (optionally                  substituted with carboxy or OH), or 6-14C aryl                  (optionally substituted with carboxy or OH) or                  -COOAlkOH moiety;andAlkOH = 2-12C hydroxyalkyl.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Process: The reaction is carried                  out in the presence of a double metal cyanide                  catalyst and carbon dioxide. The method involves                  adding hydrogen-functional initiator and/or a                  suspending agent which does not contain                  hydrogen-functional groups in a reactor each                  optionally together with double metal cyanide                  catalyst, optionally adding a portion of alkylene                  oxide to the obtained mixture at a temperature of                  90-150 degrees C, and continuously adding one or                  more hydrogen-functional initiator during the                  reaction in the reactor.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Properties: The polyoxyalkylene                  macromer has a number average molecular weight of                  500-1000000 g/mol, preferably 1000-200000 g/mol%                  measured by gel permeation chromatography, and a                  glass transition temperature of -80 degrees C to                  -1 degrees C, preferably -70 degrees C to -5                  degrees C.

PT P
PN CN109796614-A
TI Preparing regenerated cellulose film material useful in the packaging industry, comprises e.g. mixing cellulose, super strong organic alkali, polar organic solvent and alcohol reagent, defoaming cellulose solution and drying.
AU XIE H
   CAI L
   ZHANG L
   XU Q
AE UNIV GUIZHOU (UYGZ-C)
GA 2019474300
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing regenerated cellulose film material comprises (i) mixing cellulose, super strong organic alkali, polar organic solvent and alcohol reagent to obtain mixture, passing carbon dioxide into mixture at 0.1-8 MPa, reacting at 20-80 degrees C for 0.5-10 hours to obtain homogeneous phase of cellulose solution, (ii) defoaming the cellulose solution by ultrasonic or centrifugation, pouring on a flat support layer and slowly scraping the solution with spatula, (iii) placing support layer with cellulose solution in a coagulation bath, allowing to stand, controlling coagulation bath temperature at 10-60 degrees C to obtain film, (iv) removing the film from the support layer, and placing in a clean, identical coagulation bath, and (v) removing the film, washing in deionized water for multiple times and drying to obtain final product.
   USE - The film material is useful in the packaging industry (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The film material has simple method, high efficiency, convenient operation, high transparency and excellent mechanical strength. The method can recycle the solvent; and is energy saving and environmentally-friendly.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The cellulose is microcrystalline cellulose, alpha -cellulose, cotton, wood pulp, bamboo pulp, and cellulose obtained from the separation of agricultural and forestry straw or crop seed hull, and cellulose is of formula (I). In step (i), mixture includes 2-15 wt.% cellulose; 2-20 wt.% super strong organic base, 2-70 wt.% alcohol reagent and polar organic solvent (balance amount). The super strong organic base includes 2,3,4,6,7,8,9,10-octahydropyrimido(1,2-a)azepine, 2,3,4,6,7,8-hexahydropyrrolo(1,2-a)pyrimidine, 2,3-N',3-N',3-N,3-N,5-N',5-N',5-N,5-N-nonamethyl-2,4,6-triaza-3l(5)-phospha-5l(5)-phosphaoct-3-ene-3,3,5,5-tetramine, 3,5,6,7-tetrahydro-2H-pyrrolo(1,2-a)imidazole compound of formula (II) or methanetriamine compound of formula (III). The polar organic solvent is dimethyl sulfoxide, N-methylpyrrolidone, N,N-dimethylimidazolidinone, N,N-dimethylformamide, N,N-dimethylacetamide, N,N-diethylacetamide, 2-pyrrolidone, 2-azacyclohexanone, epsilon-caprolactam, N,N-dimethylpropenylurea, sulfolane and/or piperylene sulfone. The alcohol reagent includes CnH2n+1-OH saturated monohydric alcohol reagent, where n is ~1-10, CnH2n-(OH)2 saturated glycol reagent, CnH2n-1-(OH)3 saturated triol reagent or CnH2n-2-(OH)4 saturated tetrahydric alcohol reagent. Preferred Method: In step (ii), defoaming the cellulose solution by centrifugation at rotational speed of 500-16000 revolutions/minute for 0.5 minutes to 12 hours; and when the cellulose solution is ultrasonically defoamed, power range is from 100 to 5000 W, the time is 0.5 minutes to 24 hours, and the temperature is 5-70 degrees C; support layer is glass plate, stainless steel plate or polytetrafluoroethylene plate; and thickness is 10-5000 mu m. The coagulation bath comprises 3 categories, (i) methanol, ethanol, propanol, butanol, isopropanol, tert-butanol or water; (ii) aqueous hydrochloric acid and sulfuric acid solution, the concentration of solution is 0.5-10 wt.%; (iii) aqueous potassium hydroxide and sodium hydroxide solution, and the solution concentration is 0.5-50 wt.%; allowing to stand in the coagulation bath at 3 minutes to 10 hours. In step (v), drying method is vacuum drying, freeze drying or under stable air at room temperature.m = 50;n = 1 or 2;R = H, methyl or ethyl;R1 = H, methyl, 1-6C alkyl, 3-10C ethoxy polyethylene glycol, methoxypolyethylene glycol, 1-6C hydroxylated alkyl or 4-8C polyethylene glycol; andR2-R5 = H, methyl or ethyl.

Thus starch would be excluded, but chemically modified starch included).); A14 (Polymers of other substituted monoolefins; including PVC, PTFE.); A25 (Polyurethanes; polyethers.)

PN CN109770268-A
TI Conditioning method of beer weever fillet involves placing 3 times weever into acetylacetic ether enzyme solution, soaking, subjecting into bacteriostatic process, seasoning, drying, packing and storing by freezing.
AU YANG G
AE YANG G (YANG-Individual)
GA 201947073Q
AB    NOVELTY - Conditioning method of beer weever fillet comprises placing 3 times weever into acetylacetic ether enzyme solution at room temperature for 60 minutes, taking out and placing into sterile water for 10 minutes; placing pre-treated weever into 3 times caraway water, soaking at room temperature for 50-60 minutes, taking out and placing into 5-7 mg/L ozone water for 2-3 minutes; removing weever, subjecting into bacteriostatic process, seasoning by placing weever into seasoning solution at mass-volume ratio of 1:2 at 4 degrees C for 4 hours; drying at room temperature under 1000 m3/hour air speed for 1 hour; and drying conditioning beer seasoning and weever, packing with gas high barrier property packaging bag with carbon dioxide and nitrogen at mass ratio of 3:7 and storing by freezing at 3 to - 4 degrees C. The preparation of seasoning solution comprises dissolving 30% beer, 6% salt, 1% sugar, 0.5% monosodium glutamate into water.
   USE - Method for conditioning beer weever fillet.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is simple and has low cost.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - FOOD - Preferred Method: The preparation of enzyme solution comprises dissolving 50U/mL lipase B4000 and 20U/mL lipase P1000 into water. The preparation of caraway water comprises cleaning fresh caraway vegetable, cutting into less than 1 cm sections, and adding sliced caraway into water at mass-volume ratio of 1:15 g/L.

PN EP3476984-A1; WO2019081771-A1; EP3701068-A1; US2020291583-A1
TI Artificial turf fiber comprises polymer material, where polymer material comprises first flame retardant, and first flame retardant is salt which has positive enthalpy of solution in water, and polymer material comprises hydrocarbon.
AU SCHMITZ D
   SICK S
   SANDER D
   JANSEN B
   LOHR I
AE POLYTEX SPORTBELAEGE PRODN GMBH (POLY-Non-standard)
   POLYTEX SPORTBELAEGE PRODN GMBH (POLY-Non-standard)
   POLYTEX SPORTBELAEGE PRODN GMBH (POLY-Non-standard)
GA 2019372817
AB    NOVELTY - Artificial turf fiber comprises polymer material, where polymer material comprises first flame retardant, and first flame retardant is salt which has positive enthalpy of solution in water. The polymer material comprises 30 wt.% hydrocarbon that combusts into carbon dioxide and water. The polymer material comprises second flame retardant. The second flame retardant is reducing agent that reacts with oxygen at temperatures above threshold temperature. The threshold temperature is 100 degrees C or higher. The reducing agent is elementary metal.
   USE - Artificial turf fiber.
   ADVANTAGE - The artificial turf fiber has high mechanical wear, high thermal cycling or thermal aging.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for producing artificial turf fiber, which involves:
   (A) creating mixture, mixing polymer, using first flame retardant and second flame retardant, reducing second flame retardant that reacts with oxygen, where threshold temperature is 100 degrees C or higher;
   (B) extruding mixture into polymer film or into mono-filament; and
   (C) processing film or mono-filament or fibrillated tape which is generated from film for trans-forming the film or monofilament or fibrillated tape into artificial turf fiber.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The first flame retardant is potassium io-dide. The elementary metal is tungsten. Preferred Conpositions: The polymer material comprises 1 wt.%, preferably 8 wt.% reducing agent, 1 wt.%, preferably 8 wt.% first flame retardant. The polymer material comprises third flame retardant. The third flame retardant is hindered amine light stabilizer. The hindered amine light stabilizer is 1,3-propanediamine, N,N-1,2-ethan-ediylbis, cyclohexane and peroxidized N-butyl-2,2,6,6-tetramethyl-4-piperidi-namine-2,4,6-trichloro-1,3,5-triazine. Preferred Process: The mixture further involves intermixing second flame retardant in mix-ture, and intermixing third flame retardant in mixture, extruding mixture into monofilament, quenching monofilament, reheating monofilament, stretching reheated monofilament, forming monofilament into artificialturf fiber, transforming film into fibrillated tape, forming fibrillated tape into artificial turf fiber and incorporating artificial turf fiber into artificial turf backing.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.
DC A94 (Semi-finished materials - fibres, films, foams.); A32 (Polymer fabrication - such as moulding, extrusion, forming, laminating, spinning.); F01 (Threads and fibres - natural or artificial; spinning - including the production of mineral and carbon fibres (D01).)

PN CN109512879-A
TI Preparation of isoflavone-increased granules by crushing pine leaves, radix puerariae and dried orange peel, sieving, extracting, adding methanol absorption liquid, eluting with distilled water, washing column with ethanol and concentrating.
AU WANG Q
   QI J
   RUAN X
   ZHENG B
AE NINGBO TAIKANG HONGDOUSHAN BIO ENG CO LT (NING-Non-standard)
GA 201930503F
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of isoflavone-increased granules comprises mixing traditional Chinese medicine raw materials comprising pine leaves, radix puerariae and dried orange peel at mass ratio of 4.5-4.75:4.5-4.75:0.5-1, crushing, sieving through 45-60 mesh sieve, adding to supercritical extraction kettle, extracting at 35-55 degrees C and at carbon dioxide pressure of 30-60 MPa and extractant flow rate of 0.1-0.6 mL/minute for 2-4 hours, adding methanol absorption liquid at ratio of 1 mL:2 g, adding to D101 type macroporous resin column chromatography, eluting with 2 bed volume/hour distilled water until effluent is colorless, washing column with 10 column volumes of 45 wt.% ethanol, concentrating to near dryness at 450-500 Pa, and drying to 105 degrees C for 24 hours. The extractant comprises ethanol, petroleum ether and water at mass ratio of 5:1:4.
   USE - Method for preparation of isoflavone-increased granules.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is simple. The granules have increased isoflavone content and strong uric acid lowering activity.

AB    NOVELTY - Preparing gold/graphitic carbon nitride                two-dimensional thin layer composite photocatalyst                comprises (i) placing urea in alumina crucible and                calcining in muffle furnace to obtain                two-dimensional thin layer of graphitic carbon                nitride precursor, (ii) adding obtained precursor                into nitric acid solution, stirring under constant                temperature, washing the resulting solid to                neutral, vacuum drying, performing secondary                calcination, cooling to room temperature and                grinding to obtain two-dimensional thin layer                graphitic carbon nitride nanosheets, and (iii)                adding obtained nanosheet into deionized water,                ultrasonically dispersing under constant                temperature, adding aqueous chloroauric acid                solution, stirring, adding configured potassium                tetrahydroborate solution and stirring the reaction                mixture continuously to obtain final product.
   USE - The composite photocatalyst is useful for                degrading rhodamine B organic molecules in water                pollutants and reducing carbon dioxide molecules in                air pollutants (all claimed).

TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The step (i) comprises                  calcining in muffle furnace from room temperature                  to 550 degrees C, heating at a rate of 5 degrees                  C/minute, and calcination time at 550 degrees C                  is 4 hours. The container (1) of filling matter is removed from position essentially over lifting platform (2).
   USE - Method for filling of container containing fluid.

PN CN109021281-A; CN109021281-B
TI Composite foaming agent for preparing polyurethane            foam, comprises component consisting of polyether            polyol, modified ester polyol and aniline            carbonate-ethanolamine hydrogen carbonate mixture, and            component consisting of butene compound.
AU UNCOLUMN I
AE BEIJING JINGDA LVYE INTELLECTUAL PROPERT (BEIJ-Non-standard)
   GUANGXI SIBAIXIANG TECHNOLOGY IND CO LTD (GUAN-Non-standard)
GA 2018A5173R
AB    NOVELTY - A composite foaming agent comprises a                component (A) consisting of 100 pts. wt. polyether                polyol (A1) comprising 10-20 pts. wt. modified                polyoxypropylene polyoxyethylene polyol, 5-10 pts.                wt. modified ester polyol (A2) and 10-30 pts. wt.                aniline carbonate-ethanolamine hydrogen carbonate                mixture (A3) as a carbon dioxide donor, and a                component (B) consisting of 5-20 pts. wt. butene                compound (B1) substituted with 4, 5 or 6 fluorine                atoms, component (B2) containing 1-25 pts. wt.                other physical blowing agent and 0.5-10 pts. wt.                deionized water, and 0.5-10 pts. wt. adjuvant (B3)                comprising a catalyst, a surfactant and optionally                other additives. The mass ratio of the aniline                carbonate to the ethanolamine bicarbonate is 2-5:1.                The aniline carbonate-ethanolamine bicarbonate                mixture has water content of 15 wt.% or less.
   USE - Composite foaming agent for preparing                polyurethane foam (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The composite foaming agent has excellent                foaming properties, and provides polyurethane foam                having excellent thermal insulation property and                mechanical strength.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for use of                composite foaming agent for preparing polyurethane                foam.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: Aniline carbonate of                  component (A3) is prepared by adding an aniline                  compound, in which 1-2 amino groups on the                  benzene ring are substituted with a substituent                  (-(CH2)nCOONH4) and a water-glycol mixed solvent                  to a reactor, stirring, replacing the air in the                  reactor with nitrogen, adding ethylamine,                  increasing the temperature to 50-55 degrees C,                  adding propylene oxide or ethylene oxide,                  reacting under constant pressure of 0.15-0.3 MPa                  for 8-10 hours, distilling off the excess solvent                  under reduced pressure, and producing aniline                  carbonate having water content of less than 15                  wt.% and viscosity of 300-500 cps. Ethanolamine                  bicarbonate is prepared by adding a mixture of                  ammonium hydrogen carbonate, water, ethylene                  glycol amine and ethanolamine to a reactor,                  adding propyl formate, stirring, replacing the                  air in the reactor with nitrogen, stirring,                  adding propylene oxide or ethylene oxide,                  reacting at 50-60 degrees C and 0.1-0.3 MPa for                  6-10 hours, cooling, removing solvent and                  unreacted substance under reduced pressure, and                  producing ethanolamine-based hydrogen carbonate                  mixture having water content of less than 10 wt.%                  and viscosity of 200-300 cps. The composite                  foaming agent is mixed with a blocked isocyanate                  prepolymer obtained from the polyether polyol                  carbonate compound, and foamed to produce                  polyurethane foam. The polyether polyol carbonate                  is prepared by adding polyether polyol to a                  high-pressure reactor containing a metal cyanide                  complex catalyst and an additive, heating at 130                  degrees C, supplying carbon dioxide, adding                  propylene oxide, reducing temperature to 105                  degrees C at rate of 1 degrees C/minute, stirring                  for 0.5-1 hour, and naturally cooling. An                  isocyanate mixture comprising 65-75 wt.%                  diphenylmethane diisocyanate and 25-35 wt.%                  polyphenyl polymethylene polyisocyanate having                  isocyanate group content of 30-35 wt.% is mixed                  with polyether polyol having hydroxyl value of                  38-40 mg potassium hydroxide/g, and polyether                  polyol carbonate is added and mixed to produce                  blocked isocyanate prepolymer.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Method: The modified                  polyoxypropylene polyoxyethylene polyol is                  prepared by adding polyoxypropylene                  polyoxyethylene polyol and methyl                  hexahydrophthalic anhydride to a reactor, adding                  a catalyst, replacing the air in the reactor with                  nitrogen, heating at 125-130 degrees C, adding                  hydroquinone and glycidyl methacrylate, reducing                  the temperature to 100-105 degrees C,                  heat-preserving for 1-2 hours, adding                  2-methylimidazole when content of the epoxide in                  the reaction product is less than 1 wt.%,                  increasing the temperature to 120 degrees C, and                  cooling to room temperature. The modified ester                  polyol is prepared by adding ethylene oxide                  monoacrylate, N-methylimidazole and lower alkyl                  alcohol to a reactor, replacing the air in the                  reactor by nitrogen, reacting at 30 degrees C for                  15-30 minutes, heating at 42-45 degrees C,                  distilling off the solvent under reduced                  pressure, and concentrating.

TI Preparation of silicone special coating, by mixing phosphogypsum, fly ash, additives and modifier, subjecting clinker to water-milling dissolution, filtering, adding hydrochloric acid, mixing high-purity alumina powder and filtering.
AU CHEN X
   LI J
   WANG S
   LI M
AE UNIV GUIZHOU (UYGZ-C)
GA 2018A2602X
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of silicone special coating involves mixing phosphogypsum, fly ash, additives and modifier, crushing to make raw materials, roasting in kiln to obtain clinker, subjecting clinker to water-milling dissolution in liquid-solid ratio of 4-6:1, solid-liquid separating, subjecting residue to flotation, separating sulfide, placing sulfide in 30-50% oxygen-rich environment, calcining at 800-1200 degrees C for 3-5 hours, catalyzing flue gas generated by calcination by vanadium pentoxide, adding calcium oxide, stirring at constant temperature, filtering to obtain high-purity sodium metaaluminate solution, introducing high-purity carbon dioxide gas into high-purity sodium aluminate solution, filtering to obtain white precipitate, adding hydrochloric acid, washing with water to obtain high-purity aluminum hydroxide, calcining to obtain high-purity aluminum hydroxide to obtain high-purity alumina powder, mixing high-purity alumina powder, grinding and filtering.
   USE - Preparation of silicone special coating.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables simple and economical preparation of silicone special coating with improved electromagnetic shielding function, antistatic property, and wear resistance.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - CHEMICAL ENGINEERING - Preferred Components: The kiln comprises industrial rotary kiln, industrial tunnel kiln or industrial vertical kiln.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The additive comprises sodium carbonate, sodium sulfate or caustic soda. The modifier comprises anthracite, carbon or coal gangue.

PN CN108946722-A; CN108946722-B
TI Preparing spherical activated carbon useful e.g. as adsorbent, involves carbonizing spherical polymer or spherical carbonaceous biomass material, followed by carrying out activation reactions under atmosphere containing e.g. inert gas.
AU CHANG M
AE SHENZHEN GLOBAL GREEN LAND NEW MATERIALS (SHEN-Non-standard)
GA 2018A0555Q
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing a spherical activated carbon involves: (1) carbonizing a spherical polymer or a spherical carbonaceous biomass material; and (2) introducing the product obtained in the step (1) into an atmosphere containing an inert gas and water vapor to carry out first activation reaction; and (3) subjecting the product obtained in the step (2) to a second activation reaction in an atmosphere containing an inert gas, water vapor and carbon dioxide to prepare the product with pore diameter of 3-8 nm.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing a spherical activated carbon which is useful as an adsorbent for adsorbing one or more of harmful gases such as carbon monoxide, hydrogen sulfide, hydrochloric acid, sulfur dioxide and nitric oxide, and in a food industry e.g. as preparation and/or discoloration of food (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables economical preparation of the a spherical activated carbon with high yield and excellent absorption characteristics.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for the spherical activated carbon obtained by the method, which has a pore diameter of 3-8 nm, preferably 4.5-6 nm.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The carbonization step (1) is carried out in an inert atmosphere or in a mixed atmosphere of an inert gas and oxygen, which is chosen from an atmosphere containing oxygen, or an air atmosphere. The carbonization is carried out at a gradient-rising temperature. Preferred Condition: When the sulfonation is carried out before the carbonization step, the initial temperature of the carbonization step is equal to or higher than the final temperature of the sulfonation temperature. The temperature of the sulfonation step is 60-300 degrees C, preferably 80-150 degrees C. The carbonization temperature is 100-650 degrees C, preferably 150-600 degrees C. The carbonization step is carried out sequentially in two or more temperature regions, preferably 2-10 temperature regions. The temperatures of the temperature regions are different from each other. The temperature of the first temperature region is 100-450 degrees C, preferably 150-400 degrees C. The temperature of the second temperature region is higher than the first temperature region, and is 500-650 degrees C, preferably 550-600 degrees C. The carbonization time is 30 minutes to 10 hours, preferably 2-6 hours. The temperature of the first activation treatment in the step (2) is 500-1100 degrees C, preferably 850-950 degrees C. The time of the first activation treatment is 1-24 hours, preferably 6-12 hours. The temperature of the second activation treatment in the step (3) is 800-1200 degrees C, preferably 850-950 degrees C. The time of the second activation treatment in the step (3) is 1-10 hours, preferably 3-8 hours. Preferred Components: In the step (1), the carbonaceous biomass material is chosen from a husk, a coconut shell, a bagasse, and a wood. The inert gas is chosen from a mixture of one or more of nitrogen, helium, and argon.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Method: In the step (1), the polymer is produced by mixing a monomer and an initiator to carry out a polymerization reaction, where the polymer is a sulfonated polymer or a non-sulfonated polymer. When the non-sulfonated polymer is used, the sulfonation is carried out in situ during the sulfonation and/or carbonization process prior to the carbonization step. The sulfonation is carried out by contacting the non-sulfonated polymer with the sulfonating agent chosen from one or more of sulfuric acid (such as concentrated sulfuric acid), fuming sulfuric acid, and sulfur trioxide, where the total weight ratio of the non-sulfonated spherical polymer to the sulfonating agent is from 3:1-1:3, preferably 1:1-1:1.5.

PN CN108707504-A
TI Preparing tea seed oil comprises e.g. screening and grading dried tea seed, husking, screening, drying, winnowing and cooling, sealing seed oil, packing the pre-treated and then placing tea seeds taken from the cold storage.
AU LI G
   WANG B
AE MACHENG HONGYU AGRIC DEV CO LTD (MACH-Non-standard)
GA 201886798U
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing tea seed oil comprises (i) screening and grading dried tea seed, husking, screening, drying, winnowing and cooling to room temperature, (ii) sealing seed oil, packing the pre-treated at -5 degrees C, (iii) placing tea seeds taken from the cold storage to room temperature, crushing, fumigating tea seed powder with steam for 15 minutes and cooling to 50 degrees C, (iv) extracting the oil from the pretreated tea seed powder using a supercritical carbon dioxide extraction process, (v) allowing the oil to stand for 3 hours, coarse filtering using a vertical slag filter and fine filtering using a plate and frame filter, (vi) adding filtered oil into a crystallization tank, passing nitrogen gas, stirring at 7-10 degrees C, at a speed of 10-15 revolution per minute for 6-8 hours and multi-channel filtering at 10-13 degrees C, and (vii) subjecting tea seed oil to the above treatment is nitrogen-filled.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing tea seed oil.
   ADVANTAGE - The oil: has high yield and quality.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Method: In step (i), screening includes: sieving and magnetic separating to remove iron and then drying in a blast air drying box at 30 degrees C until the water content is less than 8%. In step (iii), sterilizing pulverized tea seed powder by ultraviolet lamp irradiation, fumigating with steam for 15 minutes and then cooling to 50 degrees C. In step (iv), extraction temperature of the supercritical carbon dioxide extraction process is 35-55 degrees C, extraction pressure is 25-35 MPa, and extraction time is 1-2 hours; separated by two-stage decompression, where primary pressure separation temperature is 35-55 degrees C, separation pressure is 8-15 MPa, secondary pressure separation temperature is 30-50 degrees C and separation pressure is 4-8 MPa. In step (vi), multi-pass filtration was performed by multi-channel filtration using 60 mesh, 80 mesh, 100 mesh, and 200 mesh screens. In step (vii), nitrogen filling pressure is 0.05-0.1 MPa, and purity of the nitrogen gas charged is 99.9%. In step (i), fuel of the blast drying oven is a dried tea husk.

PT P
PN WO2018183815-A1; US2021102141-A1; US11352380-B2
TI Obtaining isolated phospholipid enriched krill composition by contacting crude krill composition with alcohol, isolating phospholipid enriched layer and non-phospholipid enriched layer, and removing alcohol from phospholipid enriched layer.
AU JOHNSON J H
AE AVOCA LLC (AVOC-Non-standard)
   AVOCA LLC (AVOC-Non-standard)
GA 201877677U
AB    NOVELTY - An isolated phospholipid enriched krill composition is obtained by (a) contacting crude krill composition with alcohol in an amount sufficient to provide phospholipid enriched layer and non-phospholipid enriched layer; (b) forming phospholipid enriched layer and non-phospholipid enriched layer, where phospholipid enriched layer is above the non-phospholipid enriched layer; (c) isolating phospholipid enriched layer and non-phospholipid enriched layer; and (d) removing alcohol from phospholipid enriched layer.
   USE - The method is useful for obtaining an isolated phospholipid enriched krill composition.
   ADVANTAGE - The isolated phospholipid enriched composition provides beneficial characteristics, including good flowability.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for obtaining isolated phospholipid enriched krill composition which involves producing crude krill composition from krill by using acetone or supercritical carbon dioxide extraction; contacting crude krill composition with alcohol in an amount sufficient to provide phospholipid enriched layer and non-phospholipid enriched layer, where phospholipid enriched layer is above non-phospholipid enriched layer; forming phospholipid enriched layer and non-phospholipid enriched layer; isolating phospholipid enriched layer and non-phospholipid enriched layer; and removing alcohol from phospholipid enriched layer.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Processes: Crude krill composition is produced from krill by organic extraction. Water immiscible solvent, i.e. heptane, is added to alcohol and crude krill composition in step (a), and water immiscible solvent is removed in step (d). Gravity separation or centrifuge is used in step (b) to form phospholipid enriched layer and non-phospholipid enriched layer. The process is performed in the absence of water immiscible solvent or in the absence of supercritical carbon dioxide. Evaporator is used in step (d) to remove the alcohol. The method further includes step (e) combining non-phospholipid enriched layer and phospholipid enriched layer to produce a phospholipid enriched krill composition with a desired phospholipid content, where alcohol is removed after combining. Preferred Compositions: Alcohol is selected from methanol, ethanol, any isomer of propanol, and any isomer of butanol, preferably ethanol in a concentration of 90-99%. Alcohol concentration is less than or equal to 95 vol.%. Ratio of alcohol to crude krill composition is 1:1-10:1, preferably 5:1-20:1. Isolated phospholipid enriched krill composition comprises greater than or equal to 45% phospholipid.

AE PANDYA H (PAND-Individual)

AB    NOVELTY - The power conversion method involves generating drive current in first winding of electromagnets (141a,141b) in motor mode that converts input electrical power to output mechanical power, time-varying drive current flowing through detector in response to strength of magnetic field from nearest permanent magnet, rotating rotor (130) in response to drive current in motor mode, removing drive current from first winding in generator mode that converts input mechanical power to output electrical power, and inducing load current through second winding to electrical load (80) in response to torque applied to rotor in generator mode.

AU ZHANG H
AE DANYANG WOTE NEW MATERIAL TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (DANY-Non-standard)
GA 2018717630
AB    NOVELTY - An aqueous coating comprises 1-5 pts. wt. ureidopyrimidinone (UPy), 10-20 pts. wt. graphene oxide powder, 10-30 pts. wt. wollastonite powder, 3-15 pts. wt. ceramic powder, 5-15 pts. wt. aqueous acrylic dispersion and 50-80 pts. wt. aqueous epoxy emulsion.
   USE - Aqueous coating.
   ADVANTAGE - The coating has dense network structure, improved solvent resistance, excellent corrosion resistance, improves surface hardness and stain resistance.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for preparation of the aqueous coating, which involves mixing graphene oxide, UPy, and deionized water, ultrasonically processing at 80 degrees C for 30 minutes, redispersing in high-speed disperser to obtain dispersed mixture, extracting in carbon dioxide supercritical fluid to obtain UPy surface functionalized graphene oxide powder, mixing UPy surface functionalized graphene oxide powder, wollastonite powder, ceramic powder, aqueous acrylic dispersion and aqueous epoxy emulsion in high speed dispersing machine for more than 30 minutes to obtain aqueous coating main material, mixing filler, surfactant, defoamer, leveling agent, dispersant, thickener and deionized water in high-speed dispersing machine for more than 30 minutes to obtain aqueous coating additive materials, and mixing aqueous coating main material and aqueous coating additive materials for 60 minutes.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The filler comprises titanium dioxide, talc, calcium carbonate, and/or barium sulfate.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Component: The dispersant is sodium methylenebisnaphthalene sulfonate.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The aqueous coating further comprises 1-3 pts. wt. filler, 0.1-1 pt. wt. surfactant, 0.1-1 pt. wt. defoamer, 0.1-1 pt. wt. leveling agent, 0.1-1 pt. wt. dispersant, 0.01-0.9 pt. wt. thickener and 20-80 pts. wt. deionized water. The surfactant is polyoxyethylene ether. The defoamer comprises emulsified silicone oil, polyoxypropylene glyceryl ether and/or polydimethylsiloxane. The leveling agent is polymethylphenylsiloxane. The thickener is polyurethane thickener, sodium polyacrylate, polyvinyl alcohol, polyacrylamide, organic bentonite and/or diatomite.
