TI Preparing microbial feed additive comprises e.g.            mixing bran, corn powder, maltodextrin, glucose, urea,            magnesium sulfate, sodium chloride, potassium            dihydrogen phosphate, tryptone, and water to obtain            culture medium and inoculating strain.
AU 
AE QINGDAO BAIRUIJI BIOLOGICAL ENG CO LTD (QING-Non-standard)
GA 201870811F
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing microbial feed additive comprises                (i) mixing 4-8 pts. wt. bran, 1-5 pts. wt. corn                powder, 2-4 pts. wt. maltodextrin, 1-5 pts. wt.                glucose, 2-5 pts. wt. urea, 0.2-0.4 pts. wt.                magnesium sulfate, 0.1-0.3 pts. wt. sodium                chloride, 0.2-0.6 pts. wt. potassium dihydrogen                phosphate, 0.5-1.5 pts. wt. tryptone, and 50 pts.                wt. water to obtain culture medium, (ii)                inoculating 2-3 wt.% strain into the culture                medium, introducing carbon dioxide, and sealing to                ferment, (iii) adding 30-40 wt.% protective agent                into the fermentation broth, and drying to obtain                the product, where the strain is Lactobacillus                delbrueckii, Bacillus subtilis, Saccharomyces                cerevisiae and dark green Trichoderma according to                the weight ratio of 1:2:2:1.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing microbial                feed additive.
   ADVANTAGE - The additive: increases content of nutrient                substance in feed; and improves disease resistance,                survival rate, growth rate and nutritional value of                an animal feed.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - FOOD - Preferred Composition: The pH of the medium                  is 5-7. In the step (ii), the fermentation                  conditions are 30-35 degrees C for 48 hours. The                  protective agent is a mixture of mannitol, sodium                  alginate and citric acid in the weight ratio of                  3:2:0.5. In the step (iii), it is also require to                  add 5-10 wt.% traditional Chinese medicine                  composition into the fermentation broth. The                  traditional Chinese medicine composition is                  prepared by pulverizing a mixture of Astragalus,                  tangerine peel, hawthorn and Jerusalem artichoke                  into 80 mesh. The weight ratio of Astragalus,                  tangerine peel, hawthorn and Jerusalem artichoke                  is 2:2:1:1.

PN CN108406434-A
TI Hard alloy cutting method for delivering oil lubricants and boundary lubricants for cutting applications, involves spraying ultra-low temperature nitrogen mixed with lubricant from nozzle onto cutting tool for hard alloy cutting process.
AU XIONG W
   WANG C
AE DONGGUAN ARMORINE ENERGY-EFFICIENT & ECO (DONG-Non-standard)
GA 2018659966
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves mixing lubricant with super low temperature nitrogen before delivering to the workpiece. The ultra-low temperature nitrogen is delivered to the nozzle. The ultra-low temperature nitrogen mixed with lubricant is sprayed by the nozzle onto the cutting tool for hard alloy cutting process. The metal debris formed during the cutting process at ultra-low temperature nitrogen is evacuated during delivery. The vegetable oil and mannitol are used as lubricants. The cutting heat generated by the work piece and the cutting tool during the cutting process is cooled during super critical carbon dioxide delivery.
   USE - Hard alloy cutting method suitable for delivering lubricant, boundary lubricants and extreme pressure lubricant for cutting applications.
   ADVANTAGE - The efficiency and recovery of hard alloy cutting is improved. The amount of raw material required for manufacturing product is reduced. The problem caused by traditional metal machining liquid is avoided. The effective recovery system for oil and surfactant is obtained by using ultra-low temperature nitrogen as the solvent effective recovery system.

PN CN108384556-A; CN108384556-B
TI Preparation of degradable sand-fixing agent by reacting toluene, trimethylolpropane trimethacrylate, sieved granules including acetone, and catalyst, filtering, rotary evaporating, and mixing with palm powder and biological particles.
AU WANG Y
   SHI Y
   LIU H
   WANG J
AE WANG Y (WANG-Individual)
   SUN M (SUNM-Individual)
GA 2018642458
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of a degradable sand-fixing agent comprises uniformly mixing peat soil, glucose, and water in a container at mass ratio of 3:1:2, sealing at 40-45 degrees C, standing, spray-drying, ball-milling, and sieving to obtain granules; reacting 70-80 pts. wt. acetone, 30-35 pts. wt. 1, 4-butanediol, 22-26 pts. wt. L-lactide, and 8-11 pts. wt. dibutyltin dilaurate under carbon dioxide protection, heating to 115-125 degrees C for 16-18 hours, cooling to room temperature, and recycling acetone; reacting 100-110 pts. wt. toluene, 50-60 pts. wt. trimethylolpropane trimethacrylate, 43-48 pts. wt. toluene diisocyanate, 35-40 pts. wt. sieved granules, and 3-6 pts. wt. catalyst, stirring at 60-70 degrees C for 3-5 hours, and adding 13-19 pts. wt. triethanolamine, heating to 75-80 degrees C, continuously stirring for 10-15 hours, cooling to room temperature; and filtering, recycling catalyst, rotary evaporating, and mixing residue with palm powder and biological particles at a mass ratio of 6:3-5:2.
   USE - Method for preparing a degradable sand-fixing agent.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Component: The biological particles are prepared by carrying out ultrasonic vibration of bentonite and hydrochloric acid at a mass ratio of 1:2, filtering, calcining filter cake at 700 degrees C, crushing, sieving, mixing with the sieved materials and 0.3 mol/L glucose solution at a mass ratio of 1:4, standing, and spray-drying to obtain dried particles; uniformly mixing 150-160 pts. wt. water, 30-45 pts. wt. humus soil, 15-17 pts. wt. yeast extract, and 10-13 pts. wt. peptone, sealing and fermenting for 1-3 days, collecting fermentation mixture, filtering, and standing filtrate for 18-22 hours; and adding dried particles to 30-40 mass% filtrate, uniformly stirring, standing, freeze-drying, adding zein solution, where the mass ratio of the freeze-dried material and the zein solution is 1:3, and freeze-drying.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Component: The catalyst is chromium oxide, or boron carbide.
DC D16 (Fermentation industry - including fermentation equipment, brewing, yeast production, production of pharmaceuticals and other chemicals by fermentation, microbiology, production of vaccines and antibodies, cell and tissue culture and genetic engineering.); A97 (Miscellaneous goods not specified elsewhere - including papermaking, gramophone records, detergents, food and oil well applications.); A14 (Polymers of other substituted monoolefins; including PVC, PTFE.); ; G04 (Miscellaneous compositions - including luminescent and tenebrescent materials, de-icing/de-misting compositions, mastics, heat transfer compositions and aerosol-can filling mixtures (C09H).)

PN CN108264554-A
TI Preparation of fishmeal collagen, involves            freezing fresh yellow croaker fish, freeze-drying,            crushing, extracting, crushing, adding distilled water,            mixing, adjusting pH, stirring, extracting,            centrifuging, taking supernatant and repeating.
AU XING X
AE PUJIANG ANGBAO BIOTECHNOLOGY CO LTD (PUJI-Non-standard)
GA 201857534U
AB    NOVELTY - The preparation of fishmeal collagen involves                freezing fresh yellow croaker fish in a                refrigerator, freeze-drying in a vacuum freeze                dryer for 36-48 hours, crushing, performing                supercritical carbon dioxide extraction for 60-90                minutes for recovering oil to obtain fish pulp and                crushing to obtain fish egg skim powder, weighing                fish egg skim powder, adding distilled water and                uniformly mixing to prepare skim powder solution,                adjusting pH using sodium hydroxide solution,                stirring and extracting for 60-120 minutes,                centrifuging, taking supernatant, repeating                operation after centrifuging, combining                supernatant, adjusting pH of supernatant using                hydrochloric acid solution, centrifuging at                8000-10000 rpm for 10-15 minutes, leaving still to                form precipitate, washing precipitate using 95-98%                ethanol solution and distilled water 2-3 times,                respectively, adjusting pH of solution to neutral,                freezing and drying to obtain frozen powder                fishmeal collagen and storing at low                temperature.
   USE - Preparation of fishmeal collagen.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables preparation of fishmeal                collagen with high protein content and extraction                efficiency, stable property storage, low content of                polysaccharide, and high quality food protein                resource, by simple and easy processes.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: In the supercritical                  carbon dioxide extraction, the extracting                  pressure is 35-45 MPa, and the temperature is                  50-55 degrees C. In the alkali extraction step,                  the extraction temperature is 50-52 degrees C. In                  the alkali extraction step, the centrifugal                  rotating speed is 4500-5000 rpm and the                  centrifuging time is 15-25 minutes. In the acid                  precipitation step, the temperature of the                  low-temperature storage is -10 to -20 degrees C.                  Preferred Composition: The ratio of material to                  liquid when adding distilled water is 1:(15-18).                  The skim powder solution further contains 2.2-2.3                  Angstrom methyl p-tert-butylbenzoate and 0.5-0.52                  Angstrom (R)-( alpha )-methylbenzylamine.                  Preferred Properties: In the alkali extraction                  step, the pH value at the time of extraction is                  10.8-11. In the acid precipitation step, the pH                  value is 4.2-4.3.

PN CN108266287-A
TI Wear-resistant coating for piston ring, comprise four-layer structure, which has adhesive layer, main wear layer, assistant wear layer and buffer layer in order, where main wear layer is a Chromium Aluminium Nitride/Tungsten disulfide.
AU YANG H
AE ANHUI HAOFENG IND CO LTD (ANHU-Non-standard)
GA 2018574996
AB    NOVELTY - Wear-resistant coating comprise four-layer structure, which has an adhesive layer (2), main wear layer (3), assistant wear layer (4) and buffer layer (5) in order. The main wear layer is a Chromium Aluminium Nitride/Tungsten disulfide (CrAlN/WS2) multilayer coating comprising Chromium Aluminium Nitride (CrAlN) layer and Tungsten disulfide (WS2) layer alternately. The assistant wear layer is a Chromium/Chromium(II) oxide (Cr/CrO) multilayer coating in which chromium layer (Cr) and chromium(II) oxide layer are alternately formed.
   USE - Wear-resistant coating for piston ring.
   ADVANTAGE - The wear-resistant coating has excellent mechanical, tribological and corrosion resistance, reduces the friction abrasion consumption of energy, improves fuel economy and reduce carbon dioxide emission, and using CrAlN/WS2 multilayer coating provides high hardness and low friction coefficient as the main wearing layer, and further improves the stability and service life of the engine work.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of a wear-resistant coating
   Adhesive layer (2)
   Main wear layer (3)
   Assistant wear layer (4)
   Buffer layer (5)
   Piston ring substrate (5)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - METALLURGY - Preferred Conditions: The coating layer of the CrAlN/WS2 multilayer coating adjacent to the bonding layer is a CrAlN layer, and the outermost layer is a WS2 layer. The thickness of the CrAlN/WS2 multilayer coating is 5-10 mu m, where each CrAlN layer has a thickness of 10 nm, and each WS2 layer has a thickness of 1-2.5 nm. The coating layer of the Cr/CrO multilayer coating adjacent to the main wear layer is a chromium layer, and the outermost layer is a chromium(II) oxide layer. The thickness of the Cr/CrO multilayer coating is 2.5-5 mu m, where each Cr layer has a thickness of 5 nm, and each CrO layer has a thickness of 0.5-1 nm. The adhesive layer is coated on the outer surface of the piston ring base and is a transition metal chromium layer. The transition metal Cr layer has a thickness of 1-2 mu m. The buffer layer is coated on the outer surface of the assistant wear layer and is a copper layer. The copper layer has a thickness of 2-5 mu m.

PN CN108213043-A; CN108213043-B
TI Method for comprehensively treating chromium slag,            involves crushing chromium slag, pulverizing, sieving,            passing slurry to grit chamber with magnetic field,            followed by adding sediment, performing carbonation            reaction and filtering.
AU LIN Z
   LIU Z
   LIU W
   LIU X
   DENG H
AE UNIV SOUTH CHINA TECHNOLOGY (UYSC-C)
GA 201853494T
AB    NOVELTY - A chromium slag comprehensively treating                method involves sequentially crushing chromium                slag, pulverizing, sieving, and adding to the                pulping equipment with the aqueous phase, and                ultrasonically pulverizing under mechanical                stirring and ultrasonic conditions. The slurry is                passed through a grit chamber equipped with an                external magnetic field, and the sediment at the                bottom of the pool is collected for use, and the                slurry of the grit is acid-digested to obtain a                dilute chromium solution and an undissolved solid                residue. The sediment and the aqueous phase are                added to the pressure device, followed by                introducing a partial pressure of carbon dioxide to                0.1-1 MPa, performing a carbonation reaction under                ultrasonic conditions, and filtering to obtain a                filter residue and a filtrate, washing filter                residue obtained with water, and subjecting to                pressure filtration to obtain a detoxified                slag.
   USE - Method for comprehensively treating chromium                slag.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables comprehensively treating                chromium slag with high processing efficiency by                reducing burden of subsequent solid phase                processing and improving the process.

TI Sand drain method, involves supplying electric power to first and second heat lines by connecting power source.
AU HWANG W S
AE YOOGU E & C (YOOG-Non-standard)
GA 201847645W
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves forming a sand mat on a soft ground and loading a load to promote consolidation of the soft ground. The piercing position for a sand pile is determined and displayed on a soft ground. The first heat line is mounted for supplying heat and avoids puncturing position. The sand mat is constructed on the first hot line and the soft ground. The casing is pierced while penetrating the perforation position. The second heat line is penetrated for supplying heat to the casing. The sand is placed in the casing into which the second hot wire is introduced to construct a sand pile. The casing is withdrawn. The sand mat is filled so that a load is deposited on the sand mat. The electric power is supplied to the first and second heat lines by connecting the power source.
   USE - Sand drain method.
   ADVANTAGE - The method fastly and efficiently accelerates the drainage of the soft ground by applying hot wire to the sand pile, shortens the consolidation time, reduces the carbon dioxide emissions and prevents the environmental pollution.

TI Detecting impurity 2,4-dichlorobenzaldehyde in lozenges by preparing sample, injecting into chromatographic column for ultra-performance convergence chromatography, and analyzing results based on obtained graph.
AU DHIR N
   CHAKRABORTY D
   KAUR V
AE DHIR N (DHIR-Individual)
   CHAKRABORTY D (CHAK-Individual)
   KAUR V (KAUR-Individual)
GA 2018471537
AB    NOVELTY - Impurity 2,4-dichlorobenzaldehyde (DBZ) is detected in lozenges by using ultra performance convergence chromatography (UPCC), which involves (a) sample preparation of dissolving lozenges in water, adding equal volume of n-heptane (compensating the volume of internal standard), shaking mechanically for 1 hour to allow the layers to separate, and transferring lower layer to a separate container; (b) injection into chromatographic column for UPCC under test conditions of 50-55 ppm concentration gradient of DBZ in n-heptane, carbon dioxide as mobile phase (A), acetonitrile (ACN):isopropyl alcohol (IPA) (60:40) as mobile phase (B), column temperature of 45 degrees C, sample temperature of 15 degrees C, injection volume of 2 mu l, Waters Acquity UPC2 Torus column with dimensions of 30x 150 mm, 1.7 um, n-heptane as diluents, wavelength of 220 nm, and photodiode array detector (PDA) as detector; and (c) analysis of test results as per the graph obtained.
   USE - Detection of impurity 2,4-dichlorobenzaldehyde (DBZ) in lozenges.
   ADVANTAGE - The analytical method is faster, avoids use of reagents which are harmful for environment, and is also cheaper than existing methods.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Processes: Sample preparation involves taking 2 lozenges, weighing, dissolving in 40 ml water, adding 4 ml internal standard containing DBZ, 44 ml n-heptane (compensating the volume of internal standard), mechanically shaking for 1 hour to allow layers to separate, and transferring lower layer to a separate container.

PN WO2018083433-A1
TI Method for manufacturing steel product for use in            manufacturing car, involves guiding preheated steel            scrap along with proportionate amount of ultra-low            carbon refined iron slab into melt circulation            loop.
AU WARNER N A
AE WARNER N A (WARN-Individual)
GA 2018363745
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves guiding preheated steel                scrap along with a proportionate amount of                ultra-low carbon refined iron slab (6) into a melt                circulation loop (1) which is provided with a                gas-lift pumping unit (7) to circulate heated                molten iron or steel carrier medium (3). The                steel/iron melt is refined by vacuum evaporation.                The non-ferrous metal contaminates such as copper                and tin are recovered by adaptation of vacuum                dezincing process. The desired amount of carbon is                added to overflowing ferrous melt to satisfy                rigorous compositional specification of desired                amount of steel product.
   USE - Method for manufacturing steel product for use                in manufacturing car.
   ADVANTAGE - The emission of carbon dioxide during                manufacturing of steel product is prevented. The                need of electric arc furnace with graphite                electrodes for steel production is avoided. The                energy consumption of the steelmaking plant is                reduced.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the                heated steel melt circulation loop.
   Melt circulation loop (1)
   Molten steel carrier medium (3)
   Heavy current connector (5)
   Slab (6)
   Gas-lift pumping unit (7)

TI Preparing N'-long-chain-alkyl-N,N-diethylacetamidine used for synthesizing the pharmaceutical composition involves dissolving N-long-chain-alkyl ethylene imide ester and diethylamine in organic solvent, heating, and reducing pressure.
AU HOU Q
   GUO D
   ZHAO Y
   XU Y
   WANG F
   LUO J
AE PETROCHINA CO LTD (CNPC-C)
GA 201828013V
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing N'-long-chain-alkyl-N,N-diethylacetamidine involves dissolving N-long-chain-alkyl ethylene imide ester and diethylamine in 0-99 wt.% organic solvent at 5-40 degrees C temperature, in which the molar ratio of N-long chain alkyl ethylene imide ester and diethylamine is 1:1-1:100, heating to 20-100 degrees C temperature under normal pressure to react for 0.1-100 hours, reducing the pressure to 0.01-0.08 mega-pascal, reacting for 0.1-24 hours at 20-120 degrees C temperature, and stopping the reaction to obtain the desired N'-long-chain-alkyl-N,N-diethylacetamidine.
   USE - Method for preparing N'-long-chain-alkyl-N,N-diethylacetamidine used as carbon dioxide/nitrogen response switch surfactant in the oil extraction, used in preparing synthetic antibiotic, herbicide, insect repellent, miticide, diuretics, hypoglycemic agent or anticancer drug, or used for synthesizing the pharmaceutical composition for treating cardiovascular and cerebrovascular diseases (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The AAA method for preparing N'-long-chain-alkyl-N,N-diethylacetamidine is simply and eco-friendly.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The organic solvent is organic amine solution and/or aprotic solvent. The organic solvent is at least one triethylamine, tripropylamine, 4-dioxane and acetonitrile. The N'-long-chain-alkyl-N,N-diethylacetamidine is represented by formula (I),,R=CnH2n+1; andn= not less than 7.. Preferred Composition: The N-long-chain-alkyl ethylene imide ester and diethylamine in the molar ratio of 1:1-1:80, preferably, 1:20. Preferred Conditions: The normal pressure is an absolute pressure of 0.08-0.15 mega-pascal, preferably, 0.1 mega-pascal.
   ACTIVITY - Herbicide; Insect Repellent; Diuretic; Antidiabetic; Hypoglycemic; Cardiovascular-Gen; Cerebroprotective.

PN CN107552061-A
TI Active carbon-based sulfur-tolerant shift catalyst comprises aluminum oxide, calcium oxide, carbon, calcium oxide, and molybdenum oxide, which has high activity, long life span, and has high air speed and severe condition of capability.
AU QI H
   ZHAO Q
   BAI Z
   WANG H
   JIANG J
   XUE H
AE SINOPEC CORP (SNPC-C)
GA 2018049005
AB    NOVELTY - Active carbon-based sulfur-tolerant shift catalyst comprises 4-7 wt.% aluminum oxide, 2-3 wt.% calcium oxide, 77-85 wt.% carbon, 2-4 wt.% calcium oxide, 7-9% wt.% molybdenum oxide.
   USE - Active carbon-based sulfur-tolerant shift catalyst.
   ADVANTAGE - The active carbon-based sulfur-tolerant shift catalyst has high activity, long life span, stable function, high resist temperature, and has high air speed and severe condition of capability.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for preparing active carbon-based sulfur-tolerant shift catalyst, which involves:
   (A) adding calcium hydroxide aqueous solution in reaction kettle, placing activated carbon and pseudoboehmite into reaction kettle, stirring carbon dioxide, and filtering material to obtain a filtered precipitate;
   (B) drying precipitate, crushing, placing in kneader, adding mixture of water and concentrated sulfuric acid, kneading, extruding, drying, and calcining to obtain a carrier; and
   (C) dissolving cobalt nitrate and ammonium heptamolybdate in ammonia to obtain impregnation solution, impregnating the carrier, naturally drying to obtain final product.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The calcium hydroxide aqueous solution is prepared by placing calcium hydroxide powder in water, maintaining constant temperature at 10 degrees C, and stirring supernatant to obtain final product. The drying is carried out at 110 130 degrees C. The calcination is carried out at 750-95 degrees C for 2-3 hours under anaerobic conditions. The ratio of ammonia, cobalt nitrate, and ammonium heptamolybdate is 40-80:6.5-13:6.2-12.4. Preferred Composition: The water and concentrated sulfuric acid mixture of water per 100 milliliter comprises 2-2.2 milliliter of concentrated sulfuric acid. Preferred Process: The method for preparing active carbon-based sulfur-tolerant shift catalyst involves placing calcium hydroxide powder in water, maintaining a constant temperature of 8-12 degrees C, standing, stirring, separating supernatant, placing in a reaction vessel at the same temperature, adding activated carbon, and aluminum stone, stirring for 5-15 minutes, passing through the carbon dioxide, stirring for 100 -140 minutes, and discharging mixture from the kettle and filtering to obtain filtered precipitate. The precipitate is dried at 110-13 degrees C, crushed to 200 mesh, placed in a kneader, added a mixture of water and concentrated sulfuric acid, kneaded for 30-50 minutes, extruded, dried under anaerobic conditions, calcined at 750-950 degrees C for 2-3 hours to obtain the carrier. The cobalt nitrate and ammonium heptamolybdate are dissolved in ammonia to make an impregnation solution, impregnated carrier in the same amount, dried naturally, calcined at 450-550 degrees C in anaerobic condition for 2-3 hours to obtain final product.

AB    NOVELTY - The apparatus (100a) comprises: a first gas                supply unit (30) for supplying a first gas                containing an oxygen gas; a second gas supply unit                (31) for supplying a second gas containing at least                one of a carbon dioxide gas, a nitrogen gas and a                nitrogen oxide gas; an discharge unit (32) for                subjecting a gas comprising the first gas supplied                by the first gas supply unit and the second gas                supplied by the second gas supply unit to an                electrical discharge treatment to produce a third                gas containing an ozone gas; and an ozone water                production unit (34) for dissolving the third gas                in dissolution water to produce ozone water. The                apparatus includes: a control unit for controlling                the amount of by-products produced by subjecting                the second gas to the electrical discharge                treatment; and a sodium hypochlorite solution                supply unit which supplies sodium hypochlorite                solution. PN CN112592743-A; CN112592743-B
TI Desulfurization of coke oven gas wet absorption            method by cooling washing liquid passing through            cooling pipe, crystallizing and precipitating tar and            naphthalene in coal gas, and connecting section of            cooling pipe to Venturi scrubber.
AU YANG Z
   ZHOU C
   WU X
AE JIANGSU KUNPENG ENVIRONMENTAL PROTECTION (JIAN-Non-standard)
GA 202136075Q
AB    NOVELTY - Desulfurization method of coke oven gas wet                absorption method involves further cooling washing                liquid passing through second section of cooling                pipe, crystallizing and precipitating tar and                naphthalene in coal gas on second cooling pipe,                increasing gas temperature, and further purifying                tar and naphthalene in gas by blower; passing                pre-treated coal gas into desulfurization tower,                compressing gas, atomizing and spraying                ethanolamine absorption liquid through rotary                atomizer on top of desulfurization tower, setting                rotating speed of rotary atomizer, atomizing                absorption liquid, passing gas through purification                channel formed by baffles to fully contact                ethanolamine absorption liquid to remove hydrogen                sulfide and carbon dioxide gas in gas, and                discharging gas washed by absorption liquid from                top of desulfurization tower into ammonia washing                tower; and collecting and heating sprayed                desulfurization rich liquid, and sending heated                desulfurization rich liquid to top of desorption                tower.
   USE - The method is use for desulfurizing of coke                oven gas wet absorption method.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables desulfurization of coking                furnace gas with high sulfur removing efficiency,                by simple and economical process.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Desulfurization method of coke oven gas wet                absorption method involves:
   (A) further cooling washing liquid passing                through second section of cooling pipe, cooling gas                temperature to 5-10 degrees C, crystallizing and                precipitating tar and naphthalene in coal gas on                second cooling pipe, connecting second section of                cooling pipe to two Venturi scrubbers, installing                blower and heater at intake of Venturi scrubber,                increasing gas temperature to 15-20 degrees C,                further purifying tar and naphthalene in gas by                blower, and finishing gas pre-treatment;
   (B) passing pre-treated coal gas into                desulfurization tower, compressing gas to pressure                of 18-25 KPa, atomizing and spraying 30-60 wt.%                ethanolamine absorption liquid through rotary                atomizer on top of desulfurization tower, setting                rotating speed of rotary atomizer to 1000-1500                revolutions/minute (rpm), atomizing absorption                liquid to 50 mu m, controlling temperature to 30-55                degrees C, passing gas through purification channel                formed by baffles to fully contact ethanolamine                absorption liquid to remove hydrogen sulfide and                carbon dioxide gas in gas, and discharging gas                washed by absorption liquid from top of                desulfurization tower into ammonia washing tower;                and
   (C) collecting and heating sprayed                desulfurization rich liquid by heat exchanger at                bottom of desulfurization tower, sending heated                desulfurization rich liquid to top of desorption                tower, spreading through spraying head, passing hot                steam into desorption tower through steam aeration                head at bottom end of desorption tower, fully                contacting desulfurization rich liquid with hot                steam, heating rich desulfurization liquid heated                by hot steam, releasing acid gas, collecting acid                gas by collection chamber at top of desorption                tower, converting acid gas to sulfuric acid and                sulfur products, passing remaining absorption                liquid through slag removal tank at bottom of                desorption tower to remove residue, and flowing                back to top of desulfurization tower.
   An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a                desulfurization device.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The ethanolamine                  absorption liquid comprises 60 wt.%                  methyldiethanolamine, 5% polyethylene glycol                  dimethyl ether, 30 wt.% diisopropanolamine and 5                  wt.% water in distillation thin tube pressurizing                  to 2.5 MPa.

PN CN112357931-A
TI Preparation of integral controllable pore structure sodium potassium molecular sieve for separation of carbon dioxide, involves mixing calcined kaolin and water, extruding to form dry kaolin strip, immersing strip in mixed lye and aging.
AU FENG Q
   SUN Y
   ZENG X
   ZHANG L
   LI H
   QU X
   ZHONG D
   SU S
   MENG G
AE CENERTECH TIANJIN CHEM RES & DESIGN INST (CENE-Non-standard)
   CNOOC ENERGY TECHNOLOGY & SERVICES LTD (CNOO-C)
GA 2021182485
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of integral controllable pore structure sodium potassium molecular sieve involves grinding amorphous kaolin and passing through 100-150 mesh sieve, calcining at 600-850 degrees C for 3-5 hours, mixing calcined kaolin and distilled water in mass ratio of 1.5-2.5 at room temperature, extruding to obtain kaolin strip, drying at 110-120 degrees C, preparing mixed lye of sodium hydroxide and potassium hydroxide with water according to ratio of sodium oxide and potassium oxide to silicon dioxide is 0.40-0.70, controlling ratio of the two substances of sodium oxide as sodium source to sodium oxide and potassium oxide as potassium source to be 0-1, calculating quality of the mixed lye based on the water absorption rate of the dry kaolin strip from 48-50%, immersing dry kaolin strip in mixed lye solution for absorption at room temperature, adding to PTFE-lined reactor, aging at 30-40 degrees C for 20-30 hours, crystallizing, cooling to room temperature, washing with deionized water and drying.
   USE - Preparation of integral controllable pore structure sodium potassium molecular sieve for adsorption and separation of carbon dioxide from natural gas (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The preparation method is simple, easy and economical, and produces integral controllable pore structure molecular sieve with controlled pore structure, active site, strong catalytic effect and low water consumption.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparation of integral controllable pore structure sodium potassium molecular sieve involves grinding amorphous kaolin and passing through 100-150 mesh sieve, calcining at 600-850 degrees C for 3-5 hours, mixing calcined kaolin and distilled water in mass ratio of 1.5-2.5 at room temperature, extruding to obtain kaolin strip, drying at 110-120 degrees C, preparing mixed lye of sodium hydroxide and potassium hydroxide with water according to ratio of sodium oxide and potassium oxide to silicon dioxide is 0.40-0.70, controlling ratio of the two substances of sodium oxide as sodium source to sodium oxide and potassium oxide as potassium source to be 0-1, calculating quality of the mixed lye based on the water absorption rate of the dry kaolin strip from 48-50%, immersing dry kaolin strip in mixed lye solution for absorption at room temperature, adding to PTFE-lined reactor, aging at 30-40 degrees C for 20-30 hours, crystallizing at 90-120 degrees C for 5-10 hours, cooling to room temperature, washing with deionized water and drying to form NaKA molecular sieve. The reaction materials are added such that the molar ratio of silicon dioxide, aluminum oxide and sodium oxide is 1:0.35-0.55:0.43-0.70 and the amount of water to be added is calculated by formula: m(dry kaolin strip)x water absorption-m(NaOH+KOH).
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The supplementary silicon source used in method is white carbon black, fumed silica or sodium silicate, and supplementary aluminum source used in method is sodium metaaluminate, aluminum sulfate or aluminum hydroxide. The sodium source is sodium metaaluminate or sodium hydroxide, and the potassium source is potassium hydroxide.

PT P
PN CN112047317-A
TI Preparing sodium hypophosphite product by adding yellow phosphorus and slaked lime to reactor, then adding water, heating to get calcium hypophosphite solution, then adding sodium carbonate solution, and carrying out metathesis reaction.
AU ZHU X
   FENG Z
   LIU S
   GAO H
   QIAN X
AE JIANGSU KANGXIANG IND GROUP CO LTD (JIAN-Non-standard)
   JIANGSU FINE CHEM IND CO LTD (JIAN-Non-standard)
GA 2020C7188C
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing high-grade sodium hypophosphite product involves: (1) adding yellow phosphorus and slaked lime to a clean reactor, then adding water, and stirring into a slurry; (2) heating the slurry in reactor to generate calcium hypophosphite solution, generating phosphine gas during reaction, stirring, and filtering to remove unreacted solids; (3) adding the calcium hypophosphite solution to a stirring tank, then introducing carbon dioxide, and stirring to remove calcium hydroxide in the calcium hypophosphite solution; (4) adding a sodium carbonate solution to the calcium hypophosphite solution, continuously stirring to carry out metathesis reaction to generate sodium hypophosphite, filtering sodium hypophosphite and again filtering to remove calcium carbonate in the filtrate; and (5) adding the filtrate to a purification bottle, then adding purifying agents, stirring and reacting, purifying the filtrate, and sampling the clear liquid to measure the end point.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing high-grade sodium hypophosphite product.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables convenient preparation of sodium hypophosphite product with high quality.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing high-grade sodium hypophosphite product involves: (1) adding same amount of yellow phosphorus and slaked lime to a clean reactor, then adding water, and using a stirring rod to stir to obtain a slurry; (2) using an alcohol heating lamp to heat the slurry in the reactor for a period of time to generate a calcium hypophosphite solution, generating phosphine gas during reaction, using a safety device during the reaction, stirring for 1-2 hours, stopping the reaction when no gas is released, and using a filter to remove unreacted solids; (3) adding the calcium hypophosphite solution to a stirring tank, then introducing carbon dioxide, and starting the stirring tank to stir to remove calcium hydroxide in the calcium hypophosphite solution; (4) adding a sodium carbonate solution to the calcium hypophosphite solution, continuously stirring to carry out metathesis reaction to generate sodium hypophosphite, filtering sodium hypophosphite and again filtering to remove calcium carbonate in the filtrate; (5) adding the filtrate to a purification bottle, then adding purifying agents, stirring and reacting, purifying the filtrate, and sampling the clear liquid to measure the end point; (6) adding the extracted clear liquid sample to a reaction vessel, then adding 0.5-1 g sodium carbonate solution, leaving still for 0.5 hour, and observing that the clear liquid is very turbid, if not, the end is reached; (7) adding the purified filtrate to a vacuum evaporator, vacuum concentrating the filtrate, and filtering the filtrate again to remove the calcium carbonate precipitate in the filtrate until a crystalline film appears on the surface of the filtrate; and (8) cooling the filtrate, then leaving still for 1-2 hours to produce crystals, adding the filtrate after crystallization to a centrifuge, and starting centrifuge to remove mother liquor.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - CHEMICAL ENGINEERING - Preferred Device: The safety devices include goggles, filter masks and gloves, and are provided to maintain indoor ventilation during heating.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The heating temperature of the reactor is 90-98 degrees C. The time for the purification reaction is 0.5-1 hour. The filtrate is concentrated by vacuum evaporation to 10-20 degrees Baume, and the vacuum degree inside the vacuum evaporator is maintained at 0.1-0.3 MPa. The centrifuging time is 0.3-0.5 hour. Preferred Components: The mass ratio of yellow phosphorus and slaked lime is 1:2-1:4. The purifying agents are used for removing arsenic and heavy metals in a mass ratio of 1:1.
DC E34 (Compounds of Li, Na, K, Rb, Cs, Fr.); E33 (Compounds of Be, Mg, Ca, Sr, Ba, Ra, Sc, Y, La, Ac, Al, lanthanides (Rare-earths), Th.)
MC E31-K04

PT P
PN CN111925846-A
TI High-efficiency desulfurization ether and thiophene solvent for removing sulfide and thiophene, comprises activated Methyl diethanolamine solvent, compound amine, strong heterocyclic amine release agent, defoamer and anti-corrosion agent.
AU TANG G
   LIANG Y
   LIU Q
   HAN M
   ZHAO Q
   KANG L
   LAN L
   CAO W
   QI Y
   LI P
   MA Y
   ZHAO H
   LI C
AE CHINA PETROLEUM ENG CO LTD (CNPC-C)
   CHINA PETROLEUM ENG & CONSTR CORP (CNPC-C)
GA 2020B5441K
AB    NOVELTY - High-efficiency desulfurization ether and thiophene solvent comprises 10-30 wt.% activated Methyl diethanolamine (MDEA) solvent, 15-35 wt.% compound amine, 35-50 wt.% strong heterocyclic amine release agent, 0.01-0.15 wt.% defoamer, 0.5-2 wt.% anti-corrosion agent. The total mass percentage is 100%.
   USE - High-efficiency desulfurization ether and thiophene solvent for removing sulfide, thiophene, and portion of hydrogen sulfide and carbon dioxide in the feed gas selected from natural gas, petroleum refinery gas, liquefied petroleum gas or synthesis gas (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The high-efficiency desulfurization ether and thiophene solvents comprises heterocyclic amine strong release agent that contains amines, hydroxyl groups, ether bonds and other functional groups that have strong desulfurization and thiophene removal. The solvent is used in conjunction with activated MDEA solvent and complex amine, so that the solvent can remove more than 98% of the sulfide and thiophene in the mixed gas. Under the premise of thiophene removal effect, it can also greatly remove other organic sulfur compounds and acid gas components. The solvent has a wide range of applications, and has the advantages of large acid gas load, low sulfur content in the purified gas, stable solvent performance, renewable recycling, and no corrosion of equipment.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) using the high-efficiency desulfurization ether and thiophene solvents to remove sulfide, thiophene, and portion of hydrogen sulfide and carbon dioxide in the feed gas, where the raw material gas is natural gas, petroleum refinery gas, liquefied petroleum gas or synthesis gas; and
   (2) a system for efficiently removing sulfide and thiophene, which includes absorption tower, flash tower, barren and rich liquid heat exchanger , the regeneration tower, the lean liquid booster pump and the cooler I, where the bottom of the absorption tower is connected to the inlet of the flash tower, the outlet of the flash tower is connected with the lean and rich liquid heat exchanger and then connected with the top of the regeneration tower; the bottom of the regeneration tower is connected with the lean and rich liquid heat exchanger , barren and rich liquid heat exchanger is connected to the lean liquid booster pump and the cooler I in sequence, and then connected to the upper end of the absorption tower; a filter is also arranged between the cooler I and the absorption tower; the top outlet of the regeneration tower is connected to the second cooler, and the second cooler is connected to the third separator; the top of the third separator is connected with the acid gas outlet, and the bottom of the third separator is connected with the upper end of the side of the regeneration tower; and a loop is arranged at the lower end of the side of the regeneration tower, and a boiler is arranged on the loop.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The activated MDEA solvent is composed of N-methyldiethanolamine and an activator. The activator is selected from butyl alcohol amine, diethanol amine, piperazine, imidazole, and methyl imidazole, or their mixture. The compound amine is a combination of strongly selected sterically hindered amine and scopolamine in a mass ratio of 4-6:1-3. The sterically hindered amine is selected from1,3-bis(dimethylamine)-2-propanol, 1,3-bis(diethylamine)-2-propanol, 1,3-bis(tert-butylamino)-2-propanol, tert-butylaminoethoxyethanol, butylamino diethyl ether or their mixture. The heterocyclic amine strong release agent is selected from 2-hydroxybenzimidazole, 2-(2-Methoxyphenylamino)-methyl-1,3 tetrahydrothiazole, 6-amino-1,4-benzodioxane, 1-(1,4-dioxan-2-yl)-N-methylmethylamine or their mixture. The defoamer is polyether modified silicone oil and, or methyl silicone oil. The anti-corrosion agent is sodium borate, boric acid corrosion inhibitor, imidazole dithiourea, 2-aminoimidazolinones or their mixture. Preferred Conditions: The mass ratio of MDEA to activator is 8-12:1. Efficient desulfurization ether and thiophene solvent are used directly or diluted with water for raw gas. When diluted with water, the mass concentration of the solvent is 50-70%. The removal of sulfide, thiophene, portion of hydrogen sulfide and carbon dioxide in the feed gas is performed at temperature of 20-55 degrees C and pressure of 2.5-6.0 MegaPascal. The mass ratio of the total mass of the high-efficiency desulfurization ether and thiophene solvent to the organic sulfide in the feed gas is 100:0.2-1. The ratio of the total volume of the high-efficiency desulfurization ether and thiophene solvent to the volume of hydrogen sulfide in the feed gas is 100:0.2-3. The application also includes the absorption of sulfide in the feed gas. The high-efficiency desulfurization ether of thiophene and other organic sulfides and the thiophene solvent are regenerated to obtain a regenerated high-efficiency desulfurization ether, and thiophene solvent, where its regeneration pressure is 0.1-0.2 MegaPascal, and the regeneration temperature is 105-125 degrees C.

Chemical aspects of catalytic exhaust systems for cars are included as well as liquid or gaseous fuels of non-petroleum origin e.g. methanol or ethanol-based fuels. Combustion improvement additives for liquid fuels are included (C10L).); A97 (Miscellaneous goods not specified elsewhere - including papermaking, gramophone records, detergents, food and oil well applications.)

PN CN211871870-U; CN112920830-A
TI Device for recycling carbon two fractions in refinery dry gas, has compressor whose outlet is connected with inlet of heat exchanger, and partition wall whose two sides are respectively provided with condenser.
AU HE S
   HOU J
   CHEN J
   WANG Y
   LI T
   WANG H
   MA S
   ZHOU Y
   WAN Z
AE PETROCHINA CO LTD (CNPC-C)
GA 2020B2528N
AB    (CN211871870-U)   NOVELTY - The invention claims a system for recycling carbon two fraction in refinery dry gas by using partition tower, comprising a compressor, a heat exchanger, a partition tower; the outlet of the compressor is connected with the inlet of the heat exchanger; the outlet of the heat exchanger is connected with the inlet of the partition tower. The system for recycling carbon two fraction in refinery dry gas of the invention is simple in process, short in flow and low in energy consumption. Recovery of the carbon two fraction (mainly ethylene and ethane) in refinery dry gas is realized by using single tower. the refinery dry gas enters from the middle part of the absorption tower after being compressed and cooled; the absorbent enters from the tower top of the absorption tower; it is contacted with the dry gas of the refinery in counter-current manner; and the fraction is absorbed by the carbon two or more; the carbon dioxide is fractionated from the tower of the absorption tower, purified and sent to the ethylene device to realize the separation and recovery of ethane and ethylene. C3 and more than C3 of low carbon olefin and absorbent are distilled from the tower kettle, tower top gas into the fuel gas system or other utilization device.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The diagram shows a schematic diagram of the method for recycling the 2C fraction in the dry gas of refinery using bulkhead tower.
EA    NOVELTY - The method involves compressing refinery dry gas after cooling, and entering a bulkhead tower. An absorbent is entered from a tower top of a partition wall tower. The refinery dry gas is countercurrent contacted, and 2C fraction is absorbed to form a mixed fraction. The mixed fraction is separated by the partition wall tower to obtain a tower top gas phase fraction. 2C fraction and 3C fraction in the tower are compressed to cool the compressed dry gas to -5 to 35 degrees C. The gas phase fraction at the top of the tower is hydrogen, nitrogen, oxygen, carbon monoxide, carbon dioxide, hydrogen sulfide and methane. The absorbent is 3C, 4C, and 5C fractions. The 3C fraction is propylene and/or propane. The C4 fraction is n-butane, isobutane, n-butene and/or isobutene and the 5C fraction is n-pentane, isopentane and/or pentene.
   USE - The process is useful for recycling C2 fraction in refinery dry gas by using bulkhead tower.
   ADVANTAGE - The method has simple process, short flow and low energy consumption and uses single tower to realize the separation and recovery of ethane and ethylene.

PN CN111838482-A
TI Preparation of long-acting anti-fatigue salt water            involves preparation of anti-fatigue mixed solution,            preparation of anti-fatigue concentrated solution,            sugar liquid preparation, material mixing, and soda            water preparation.
AU ZHOU R
AE ANHUI KANGZHIWEI BIOTECHNOLOGY CO LTD (ANHU-Non-standard)
GA 2020A94462
AB    NOVELTY - The preparation of long-acting anti-fatigue                salt water comprises grinding and crushing ginseng,                radix astragali, safflower, angelica and                pseudoginseng, putting in high pressure reaction                kettle at 100 degrees C high temperature,                filtering, high speed centrifuging to obtain                supernatant fluid as anti-fatigue mixed solution;                adding the anti-fatigue mixed liquid into active                carbon, freeze-drying, alcohol washing treatment,                adding tea polyphenol and mixed vitamin, vacuum                concentrating to obtain anti-fatigue concentrated                solution; mixing white granulated sugar and                deionized water to obtain sugar solution; putting                the sugar liquid into preparing tank, then mixing                sodium benzoate, sodium citrate, salt, citric acid,                edible essence and anti-fatigue concentrated                solution, adding water to dissolve; and mixing                resulting mixed material, adding carbon dioxide,                filling, and then sterilizing and detecting to                obtain long-acting anti-fatigue salt water.
   USE - The method is for preparing long-acting                anti-fatigue salt water.
   ADVANTAGE - The method overcomes the shortage of the                existing technology, effectively improves fatigue                resistance of the beverage, and timely supplements                the loss of human body fluid during exercise in                time, and has high nutritive value, good stability,                good flavor and taste.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - The preparation of long-acting anti-fatigue                salt water comprises (A) selecting ginseng, radix                astragali, safflower, angelica and pseudoginseng,                slicing, adding deionized water, grinding and                crushing, putting in high pressure reaction kettle                at 100 degrees C high temperature, keeping the                temperature for 2-2.5 hours under the pressure of                10-12 MPa, taking out and filtering when it is hot,                putting the filtrate in the centrifuge at rotating                speed of 12000-14000 revolutions/minute (rpm) for                high speed centrifuging for 5-8 minutes, obtaining                supernatant fluid as anti-fatigue mixed solution                for further use; (B) adding the anti-fatigue mixed                liquid into the active carbon for decoloring                treatment, then filtering, and then putting the                filtrate in the freezing chamber for freeze-drying,                obtaining freeze-dried powder, then adding 75%                ethanol solution for alcohol washing treatment,                then recycling ethanol solution, adding 8 times                volume of deionized water into the alcohol washing                substance and then adding tea polyphenol and mixed                vitamin; uniformly stirring in 45-50 degrees C                water bath, vacuum concentrating to 1/6-1/8 of the                original volume, obtaining anti-fatigue                concentrated solution; (C) mixing the white                granulated sugar and deionized water, placing at                high temperature of 90-95 degrees C, stirring and                dissolving to obtain mixed solution, after cooling                the mixed solution to 40-45 degrees C, filtering                and diatomite filtering to obtain sugar solution;                (D) putting the sugar liquid into preparing tank,                then mixing sodium benzoate, sodium citrate, salt,                citric acid, edible essence and anti-fatigue                concentrated solution, adding water to dissolve,                filtering, obtaining the mixed filtrate into the                preparing tank, then adding deionized water to                constant volume, obtaining mixed material; and (E)                mixing the mixed material by soda-water mixer,                adding carbon dioxide, then filling by equal                pressure mode, and then sterilizing and detecting                to obtain long-acting anti-fatigue salt                water.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - FOOD - Preferred Method: The mass ratio of ginseng,                  radix astragali, safflower, Angelica, Panax                  notoginseng and deionized water is 2-4: 1-3: 1-3:                  1-2: 3-5: 4-6: 20-25. The mixed vitamin in step                  (B) is the mixture of vitamin C, vitamin B5,                  vitamin B6 and vitamin E at a mass ratio of 6: 2:                  1: 2. The mass ratio of tea polyphenol and mixed                  vitamin is 6: 1-1.6: 0.8-1.2. The mass ratio of                  white granulated sugar and de-ionized water is 1:                  3. The mixed material comprises 0.02-0.03% sodium                  benzoate, 0.03-0.04% sodium citrate, 0.14- 0.16%                  salt, 0.14-0.18% citric acid, 0.03-0.04% edible                  essence, 6.4-8.2% anti-fatigue concentrated                  solution, and balance deionized water.
   ACTIVITY - CNS-Gen; Muscular-Gen.
DC B04 (Natural products and polymers. Including testing of body fluids (other than blood typing or cell counting), pharmaceuticals or veterinary compounds of unknown structure, testing of microorganisms for pathogenicity, testing of chemicals for mutagenicity or human toxicity and fermentative production of DNA or RNA. PN CN111777035-A
TI Self-heating type lignite chemical chain            gasification method based on copper-based composite            oxygen carrier used to reaction system, by pyrolysis            reacting lignite, reducing copper-based composite            oxygen carrier, and oxidizing pyrolysis gas.
AU XIAO R
   HU J
AE UNIV SOUTHEAST (UYSE-C)
GA 2020A31677
AB    NOVELTY - Self-heating type lignite chemical chain                gasification method based on copper-based composite                oxygen carrier involves: adding lignite raw                material into fuel reactor, rapid pyrolysis                reacting to generate pyrolysis gas, coke and tar                comprising hydrogen, carbon monoxide and methane,                reducing copper-based composite oxygen carrier by                pyrolysis gas and small molecular gas generated by                pyrolysis, releasing heat in process of reducing,                oxidizing pyrolysis gas and small molecular gas to                generate carbon dioxide and water to discharge, and                entering reduction product copper/iron/ferrous                oxide of coke and composite oxygen carrier into                gasification reactor; gasification reacting coke to                produce synthesis gas comprising carbon monoxide                and hydrogen, reacting iron/ferrous oxide in                introduced composite oxygen carrier with water                vapor to produce hydrogen by steam-iron method to                generate hydrogen, partially oxidizing iron, and                collecting and utilizing hydrogen and carbon                monoxide synthesis gas.
   USE - The method is used for self-heating type                lignite chemical chain gasifying based on                copper-based composite oxygen carrier used to                reaction system.
   ADVANTAGE - The method solves problem of low synthesis gas                quality in chemical chain gasification system, too                high circulating fluidized bed circulating rate in                actual operation process, reduces circulating                fluidized bed circulating rate, realizes                self-heating of gasification system, and ensures                safe and stable operation of circulating fluidized                bed.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Self-heating type lignite chemical chain                gasification method based on copper-based composite                oxygen carrier involves:
   (A) adding lignite raw material into fuel                reactor through fuel feeder, rapid pyrolysis                reacting under inert carrier gas atmosphere to                generate pyrolysis gas, coke and tar comprising                hydrogen, carbon monoxide and methane, reducing                copper-based composite oxygen carrier by pyrolysis                gas and small molecular gas generated by pyrolysis,                releasing heat in process of reducing to partially                provide heat needed by pyrolysis, oxidizing                pyrolysis gas and small molecular gas to generate                carbon dioxide and water to discharge, and entering                reduction product copper/iron/ferrous oxide of coke                and composite oxygen carrier into gasification                reactor;
   (B) gasification reacting coke to produce                synthesis gas comprising carbon monoxide and                hydrogen, reacting iron/ferrous oxide in introduced                composite oxygen carrier with water vapor to                produce hydrogen by steam-iron method to generate                hydrogen, partially oxidizing iron, collecting and                utilizing hydrogen and carbon monoxide synthesis                gas, and directly entering partially oxidized                composite oxygen carrier comprising ferrous ferric                oxide and copper that has not participated in                reaction into air reactor; and
   (C) oxidizing and regenerating composite                oxygen carrier entering air reactor with air                reactor, releasing large amount of heat, carrying                into fuel reactor through copper-iron-based                composite oxygen carrier as carrier, and finishing                circulating heat and oxygen supply of entire                reaction device.
   The reaction system comprises fuel reactor,                air reactor and gasification reactor. The tar is                catalyzed and cracked by composite oxygen carrier                from air reactor to be small molecule gas.

PN CN111450826-A
TI Preparation of Mott Schottky junction catalyst used for photocatalytic reduction of carbon dioxide involves dissolving soluble metal salts, mixing with precipitant, crystallizing with hydrotalcite colloid, centrifuging, and drying.
AU FENG J
   LI D
   CHENG L
   FAN J
   LIU Y
AE UNIV BEIJING CHEM TECHNOLOGY (UYBC-C)
GA 2020728037
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of Mott Schottky junction catalyst comprises dissolving first soluble metal salt and second soluble metal salt in deionized water, and adding titanium tetrachloride in hydrochloric acid solution; dissolving precipitant in deionized water; adding first mixed solution and precipitant solution to rotating liquid film reactor and adjusting width of slit, rotor speed, and rotation time; adding hydrotalcite colloid, crystallizing, taking out, cooling to room temperature, washing with deionized water until the supernatant pH is neutral, drying, and grinding; and adding noble metal-loaded hydrotalcite precursor powder to the solvent, stirring, centrifuging, alternately washing with ethanol and water until the solvent on the surface of the filtered material is completely removed, and drying.
   USE - The method is used for preparing Mott Schottky junction catalyst for photocatalytic reduction of carbon dioxide (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The catalyst absorbs sunlight, promotes high dispersion of active metal components, and has good reusability.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparation of Mott Schottky junction catalyst comprises:
   (A) dissolving first soluble metal salt and second soluble metal salt in deionized water, and adding titanium tetrachloride in hydrochloric acid solution;
   (B) dissolving precipitant in deionized water, where precipitant is sodium hydroxide, potassium hydroxide, sodium carbonate, or sodium bicarbonate at concentration of 0.2-1 mol/L;
   (C) adding first mixed solution and precipitant solution to rotating liquid film reactor and adjusting width of slit, rotor speed to 5000-6000 revolutions/minute (rpm), and rotation time to 2-5 minutes;
   (D) adding hydrotalcite colloid, crystallizing at 130-150 degrees C for 24-48 hours, taking out, cooling to room temperature, washing with deionized water until the supernatant pH is neutral, drying at 60-90 degrees C for 12-24 hours, and grinding; and
   (E) adding noble metal-loaded hydrotalcite precursor powder to the solvent, stirring for 24-48 hours, centrifuging at 9600-10000 rpm, alternately washing with ethanol and water until the solvent on the surface of the filtered material is completely removed, and drying at 60 degrees C for 12-24 hours.
   The molar ratio of first metal salt to second metal salt is 2-4:1. The molar concentration of titanium(IV) ion is 0.04-0.06 mol/L. The first first metal salt is magnesium(II) ion, zinc(II) ion, copper(II) ion, nickel(II) ion, cobalt(II) ion, manganese(II) ion, nitrate, or chloride. The second metal salt cobalt(II) ion, nickel(II) ion, aluminum(III) ion, chromium(III) ion, cerium(III) ion, gallium(III) ion, indium(III) ion, nitrate, or chloride. The metal salt mixture is prepared with precious metal of concentration of 5-50 mmol/L. The precious metal salt is platinum containing compound of formula H2PtCl4, H2PtCl6(Pt(NH3)4)Cl2, Pd(NH3)2Cl2, Pd(NO3)2, or Na2PdCl4, chloroauric acid, sodium tetrachloroaurate, chloroiridic acid, sodium hexachloroiridate, rhodium trichloride hydrate, rhodium(II) acetate, silver nitrate, and/or silver acetate. The solvent is formamide, N,N-dimethylformamide, or n-butanol.

PN CN110938262-A
TI Preparing rubber air passage pipe useful for air source transcritical carbon dioxide heat pump, comprises e.g. adding Camellia oil, adding methanol and p-toluenesulfonic acid, heating, molding and performing vulcanization treatment.
AU LI S
AE ANHUI HUANQIUXING NEW ENERGY TECHNOLOGY (ANHU-Non-standard)
GA 202027539X
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing rubber air passage pipe comprises (i) adding Camellia oil to the reaction kettle, adding methanol and p-toluenesulfonic acid to the reaction kettle, heating to 80-85 degrees C, stirring at 200 revolutions/minute for 40 minutes; (ii) adding primary product to the reactor under the protection of inert gas, adding methanol, sodium hydroxide and rare earth chloride to the reactor; (iii) dissolving diethylene triamine in an ethanol solution with a mass fraction of 30%; (iv) adding ethylene propylene diene monomer rubber, zinc oxide, organic ester auxiliary, compound crosslinking agent, vulcanizing agent, and white carbon black to the mixer in order to obtain the mixed rubber;(v) injection molding the mixed rubber, pouring it into a mold, keeping it at 80 degrees C for 5 minutes, and then opening the mold to obtain a rubber tube blank; and (v) adding rubber tube blank obtained above to vulcanizing agent for vulcanization treatment.
   USE - The pipe is useful for air source transcritical carbon dioxide heat pump.
   ADVANTAGE - The method has larger bond energy larger than the disulfide bond and polysulfide bond and physical performance degradation; and reduces the number of sulfur-sulfur bonds.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing rubber air passage pipe comprises (i) adding Camellia oil to the reaction kettle, adding methanol and p-toluenesulfonic acid to the reaction kettle, heating to 80-85 degrees C, stirring at 200 revolutions/minute for 40 minutes, performing high-speed centrifugation for 30 minutes, allowing to stand for 2 hours, taking the upper oil phase and drying to constant weight to obtain the primary product; (ii) adding primary product to the reactor under the protection of inert gas, adding methanol, sodium hydroxide and rare earth chloride to the reactor, stirring at 1200 revolutions/minutes for 3-4 hours, and terminating the reaction, performing high-speed centrifugation for 30 minutes for 2 hours, taking the upper oil phase, and then distilling under reduced pressure to obtain an organic ester auxiliary agent; (iii) dissolving diethylene triamine in an ethanol solution with a mass fraction of 30% to obtain a diethylene triamine solution, heating to 50-55 degrees C, and then transferring to diethylene, adding phosphate ester and zinc borate to the triamine solution, stirring the reaction at 500 revolutions/minuttes for 2 hours, and then drying to constant weight to obtain a composite crosslinking agent;(iv) adding ethylene propylene diene monomer rubber, zinc oxide, organic ester auxiliary, compound crosslinking agent, vulcanizing agent, and white carbon black to the mixer in order to obtain the mixed rubber;(v) injection molding the mixed rubber, pouring it into a mold, keeping it at 80 degrees C for 5 minutes, and then opening the mold to obtain a rubber tube blank; and (v) adding rubber tube blank obtained above to vulcanizing agent for vulcanization treatment.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: In the step (i), the mixed weight ratio of camellia oil, methanol and p-toluenesulfonic acid is 120-130:75-78:1-1.5. In the step (ii), the mixed weight ratio of the primary product, methanol, sodium hydroxide and rare earth chloride is 100: 30: 5: 0.12-0.15. The chlorinated rare earth is cerium chloride. The inert gas is nitrogen and/or neon. In the step (iii), the mixing ratio of the diethylenetriamine and ethanol solution is 105 g:500-550 ml, the added amount of the phosphate ester is 15-18 wt.% of diethylenetriamine and the added amount of zinc borate is 3-5 wt,% of diethylenetriamine.In the step (iv), the mixing weight ratio of EPDM rubber, zinc oxide, vulcanizing agent and white carbon is: 112-116:5-6:6-8:12-15, the added amount of the organic ester auxiliary is 5-8 wt.% of EPDM rubber and the added amount of the composite crosslinking agent is 3-4 wt.% of EPDM rubber. Preferred Method: In the step (iv), the temperature of the internal mixing process is 157-160 degrees C and the internal mixing time is 1-1.6 hours. In the step (vi), the temperature of the vulcanization treatment is 140-146 degrees C for 8-10 minutes.

TI Recycling of waste cathode carbon block in            electrolytic aluminum electrolysis tank comprises e.g.            pulverizing the waste cathode carbon block to a carbon            powder, performing heat treatment of mixed solid            powder, and absorbing the gas.
AU LUO X
   QIN Z
   ZHOU Z
   CHEN X
   XIE X
   SU T
   HE Z
AE UNIV GUANGXI (UYGU-C)
   UNIV GUANGXI CHINESE MEDICINE FIRST AFFI (UGCT-C)
GA 201965141P
AB    NOVELTY - Recycling of waste cathode carbon block in                electrolytic aluminum electrolysis tank comprises                e.g. pulverizing the waste cathode carbon block to                a carbon powder having a particle size of less than                45 mesh by using a crusher and a ball mill, adding                5A type or 10X type zeolite molecular sieve having                a carbon powder mass of 5-20 wt.% having particle                size less than 2 mm to obtain a uniformly mixed                solid powder, performing the heat treatment for the                mixed solid powder at a temperature of 800-1600                degrees C for 30-120 minutes, absorbing the carbon                dioxide and nitrogen oxide gas generated by the                heat treatment by using 10% sodium hydroxide                solution to recover the salt solution of sodium                carbonate and sodium nitrate, adding concentrated                sulfuric acid at 80-120 degrees C to the                fluorine-containing solid obtained after the heat                treatment to obtain a solid matter, mixing three                products of salt solution and HF gas, and the solid                product, where the solid product is carbon powder                for recycling.
   USE - The method is useful for recycling of waste                cathode carbon block in electrolytic aluminum                electrolysis tank.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: comprises zeolite molecular sieve                as the heat treatment reaction mass, and plays the                role of catalyst and dispersant; realizes zero                discharge of harmful gas to obtain hydrofluoric                acid product with high added value; is economical                and environmentally friendly.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Recycling of waste cathode carbon block in                electrolytic aluminum electrolysis tank comprises                pulverizing the waste cathode carbon block to a                carbon powder having a particle size of less than                45 mesh by using a crusher and a ball mill, adding                5A type or 10X type zeolite molecular sieve having                a carbon powder mass of 5-20% by weight and a                particle size of less than 2 mm to the carbon                powder to obtain a uniformly mixed solid powder,                performing the heat treatment for the mixed solid                powder at a temperature of 800-1600 degrees C for                30-120 minutes, absorbing the carbon dioxide and                nitrogen oxide gas generated by the heat treatment                by using 10% sodium hydroxide solution to recover                the salt solution of sodium carbonate and sodium                nitrate, adding 98 wt.% concentrated sulfuric acid                at 80-120 degrees C to the fluorine-containing                solid obtained after the heat treatment to obtain a                solid matter, mixing three products of salt                solution and hydrogen fluoride gas, and the solid                product, where the solid product is carbon powder                used for recycling, adjusting the pH of the mixed                salt solution to 7 by adding 20% sodium hydroxide                solution to obtain sodium sulfate solution and                recovering, passing the hydrogen fluoride gas into                deionized water for absorption condensation to                obtain a hydrofluoric acid product.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The diagram shows flow diagram of recycling                of waste cathode carbon block in electrolytic                aluminum electrolysis tank.(Drawing includes                non-English language text
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The zeolite molecular                  sieve is type 5A zeolite molecular sieve.

PN CN109929637-A
TI Device for purifying gas based on principle of hydrate gas separation, comprises separation and removal device for carbon di-oxide in fuel gas, raw material gas separator, feed gas separator inlet port is connected with raw material.
AU LV X
   ZUO J
   ZHOU S
   LU D
   ZHAO H
   LI E
   WANG S
   QIAO D
   ZHU Z
AE UNIV CHANGZHOU (UNCZ-C)
GA 201959038N
AB    NOVELTY - Device for purifying gas based on the principle of hydrate gas separation, comprises a separation and removal device for carbon di-oxide in a fuel gas, a raw material gas separator, feed gas separator inlet port is connected with a raw material gas pipeline, and a raw material gas separator (1) air outlet is connected with a buffer tank (2). The hydration tower (9) is provided with an inlet nozzle and a refilling port, the middle section is provided with a carbon dioxide hydrate slurry outlet, and the top is provided with an outlet port, the inlet nozzle and the buffer tank. The air outlet is connected with a dehydrator (13) and decomposition tower (11) is set with a heater at the bottom, an outlet port and a carbon dioxide hydrate slurry inlet at the upper section of the decomposition tower and a water outlet at the bottom, where gas outlet is connected with a carbon di-oxide collection tank (12).
   USE - Device for purifying gas based on the principle of hydrate gas separation.
   ADVANTAGE - The device is suitable for purification treatment of natural gas and biogas, and also for separation of factory flue gas and the like, and also has good effect.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for purifying gas based on a gas separation principle of a hydrate method, which involves:
   (A) inputting H2S absorption liquid into the hydration tower through the liquid replacement port, and input CO 2 absorption liquid into the hydration tower through the liquid replacement port, where CO2 absorption liquid includes a mass fraction of 0.1-2 wt.% promoter, 10-30 wt.% tetrabutylammonium bromide, the balance is water;
   (B) removing raw material gas from the solid waste by the raw material gas separator and then entering the buffer tank, after the steady flow of the buffer tank, uniformly and stably to the hydration tower, and entering the H2S absorption liquid in the form of bubbling and forming H2S hydrate slurry;
   (C) generating H2S hydrate slurry in the hydration column;
   (D) injected H2S hydrate slurry generated by the hydration column into the decomposition column via a booster pump;
   (E) forming CO2 hydrate slurry in the hydration tower and injecting into the decomposition tower, and gradually flowing CO2 hydrate slurry to the lowermost layer;
   (F) passing CO2 through the hydration tower, refilling the rehydration port together with the hydration tower; and
   (G) removing residual gas, flowing out from the upper outlet of the hydration tower and then dehydrating through the dehydrator and entering gas pipeline.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows schematic view of device.
   Raw material gas separator (1)
   Buffer tank (2)
   Hydration tower (9)
   Decomposition tower (11)
   Carbon di-oxide collection tank (12)
   Dehydrator (13)

GA 201945279U
AB    NOVELTY - A tobacco material preparing method involves adjusting moisture of tobacco raw material, placing in a closed system, and heating, keeping, and grounding in a mortar to obtain a tobacco powder. The tobacco raw material is extracted with an aqueous ethanol solution, the solution is separated to obtain a tobacco extract, the extract is evaporated to obtain tobacco extract, dispersing aid is added to water, heated, the heat conductive material is dispersed in the dispersion aid solution, the solution and the polyol is mixed to form a mixed solution, and the tobacco powder is added to the mixed solution, mixed, the mixed system is fed into a single-screw extruder to continue mixing, and injected a foaming agent carbon dioxide to perform foam extrusion molding, the extrudate is dried and cut into a filamentous tobacco material with a cutter, a tobacco material for heating the non-combustion type tobacco product, and temperature is controlled to fluctuate between plus minus 2 degrees C by cooling water.
   USE - Method for preparing tobacco material for heating non-combustion type tobacco products.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables preparing tobacco material with better industrialization prospect, in simple and cost effective manner.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A tobacco material preparing method involves adjusting moisture of tobacco raw material to 15-20%, placing in a closed system, and heating at 150-200 degrees C for 1-6 hours, after the completion of the heating, keeping at room temperature for 10-40 hours, and grounding in a mortar to pass through a filter sieve having a particle diameter of 0.05-0.20 mm to obtain a tobacco powder. The tobacco raw material is extracted with an aqueous ethanol solution, and after the leaching, the solution is separated from the tobacco to obtain a tobacco extract, and the extract is evaporated under reduced pressure to remove ethanol and concentrated to obtain tobacco extract, dispersing aid is added to water to stirred and swelled, heated at 80-90 degrees C to make it evenly dispersed, the solution concentration is 2-4%, the heat conductive material is dispersed in the dispersion aid solution, the mass ratio of the flame retardant added to the tobacco extract is 1:1-1:4, the solution and the polyol is mixed to form a mixed solution, and the tobacco powder is added to the mixed solution, stirred, and mixed, the mixed system is fed into a single-screw extruder to continue mixing, and simultaneously injected a foaming agent carbon dioxide to perform foam extrusion molding, extrudate exhibits is a porous structure, the extrudate is dried and cut into a filamentous tobacco material with a cutter, a tobacco material for heating the non-combustion type tobacco product, the set temperature of the single-screw extruder is room temperature, and temperature is controlled to fluctuate between plus minus 2 degrees C by cooling water.
