PN CN109355230-A
TI Preparing selenium enrichment culture technology of Nostoc sphaeroides used for health foods, involves subjecting selenium-enriched culture technique of Nostoc sphaeroides, and full sunlight intensity between modified BG-110 liquid medium.
AU XIN M
   CHEN W
   PANG Y
   PANG F
   YANG L
AE QINGDAO HAORAN MARIN TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (QING-Non-standard)
GA 201920625B
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing selenium enrichment culture technology of Nostoc sphaeroides involves subjecting selenium-enriched culture technique of Nostoc sphaeroides at temperature of 22-25 degrees C, pH 7.8-8.2, optimal illumination at 80-100 mu E.m-2.s-1 and full sunlight intensity between the modified BG-110 liquid medium. The sucrose is added to the sodium selenite, and it is placed in a low concentration of selenium culture solution, in which the whole solution is dissolved in pure water during the preparation process of constant volume. The container used for culture is treated with ultraviolet disinfection for 10-14 hours, and oxygenation amount in the cultivation process is large, and the acclimation time is 2-3 months. The algae strain with strong selenium-enriching ability is selected as the selenium-enriched algae species.
   USE - Method for preparing selenium enrichment culture technology of Nostoc sphaeroides used for health foods.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to prepare selenium enrichment culture technology of Nostoc sphaeroides, which has therapeutic effects of clearing heat, eyesight, anti-aging and anti-infection, and preventive and auxiliary effects on cancer, heart disease, Keshan disease, cataract and atherosclerosis.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - The sodium selenite is added to the modified BG-110 liquid medium for the production of algae from Nostoc sphaeroides to make the final concentration of selenium reach 1-30 mg/L, per liter. The ratio of the wet weight of the culture solution is 2-6g to the reactor, and the air inflator is used to pass the carbon dioxide. The flow rate of the carbon dioxide is to ensure that the whole reaction liquid is in a gentle flow state, so that the genomic Nostoc sphaeroides grows normally in the selenium concentration. The culture temperature is maintained at 22-25 degrees C with pH 7.8-8, the best light between 80-100 mu E.m-2.s-1 and the total sunshine intensity, after three levels of expansion culture to get a maximum diameter of about 5mm fresh Nostoc sphaeroides under the conditions of suitable conditions. The fresh Nostoc sphaeroides is cultured up to 5 mm in diameter, and a stainless steel sieve with a diameter of 1.5 mm is selected, and the harvested culture is sieved. The sieved small-diameter culture is placed in the reactor and cultured. The fresh gems preserved on the sieve is thoroughly rinsed with water until the pH of the wash water reaches 7 and dried to obtain selenium-rich gems. The selenium-rich gems are subjected to the catering department as a meal, and the other portion is dried, as a raw material for other functional foods and health foods, it is further processed. The polysaccharides are subjected on the outer wall of the spheroidal cell of Nostoc sphaeroides are highly viscous, it is easy to hold the ware, when bonded together, the harvested spheres are dried in time to get desired product.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Conditions: The selenium-enriched culture technique is at a temperature of 22-23 degrees C and a pH of 7.9-8.1 (preferably, the selenium-enriched culture technique of the cemetery is at a temperature of 22 degrees C, pH 8). The container used for the culture is subjected to ultraviolet disinfection treatment for 11-13 hours (preferably, the container used for the culture is treated with ultraviolet disinfection for 12 hours). The domestication of algae strain is carried out in a modified BG-110 liquid medium, and the sodium selenite is added to the sodium selenite to in a low concentration selenium culture solution, and the concentration of the selenium culture solution is 1-30 mg/L, preferably, the concentration of the selenium culture solution is 10-20 mg/L, and more preferably, the concentration of the selenium culture solution is 12 mg/L. The selenium enrichment culture of Nostoc sphaeroides is carried out in a ratio of 3-5 g of wet weight per liter of the culture solution to the reactor (preferably, in a ratio of 4 g of wet weight per liter of the culture solution to the reactor). The selenium enrichment culture of Nostoc sphaeroides is replaced once every 10 days. The method of harvesting and post-processing for drying is dehydration or natural air drying.
   ACTIVITY - Cytostatic; Cardiant; Vasotropic.

PN CN109136910-A; CN109136910-B
TI Preparing high wear-resistant coating material useful for improving the lubricity and wear resistance of a pump body during movement, comprises e.g. mixing titanium powder and graphite powder, mixing by a ball mill and obtaining a mixture I.
AU LI L
   WANG C
   QIAN F
   LI X
   WANG X
AE HEFEI EVERGRANDE JIANGHAI PUMP IND CO (HEFE-Non-standard)
GA 201905442V
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing high wear-resistant coating material comprises (i) mixing 2-5 pts. wt. titanium powder and 1 pts. wt. graphite powder, mixing by a ball mill for 2-8 hours, obtaining a mixture I, mixing 2-4 pts. wt. TiCr-Cr3C2 and 1 pts. wt. WS2 powde and mixing in a ball mill for 2-8 hours to obtain a mixture II, (ii) mixing the mixture with methyl cellulose and apply it to the surface of the pump body, placing the pump body into the drying box to dry, using nitrogen gas as a shielding gas and a reaction gas, and a tungsten having a diameter of 3.2 mm as a cathode, the pump body as an anode, and the TIG welder as a heat source to perform a nitrogen arc cladding on the pump body and (iii) mixing the mixture with methyl cellulose, applying the surface of the pump body, drying the pump body, using the carbon dioxide laser to laser cladding in an argon or nitrogen atmosphere and obtaining the high wear resistant coating material on the surface of the pump.
   USE - The material is useful for improving the lubricity and wear resistance of a pump body during movement (claimed).
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing high wear-resistant coating material comprises (i) mixing 2-5 pts. wt. titanium powder and 1 pts. wt. graphite powder, mixing by a ball mill for 2-8 hours, obtaining a mixture I, mixing 2-4 pts. wt. TiCr-Cr3C2 and 1 pts. wt. WS2 powde and mixing in a ball mill for 2-8 hours to obtain a mixture II, (ii) mixing the mixture with methyl cellulose and apply it to the surface of the pump body after pretreatment after the coating is finished, placing the pump body into the drying box to dry, using nitrogen gas having a purity of not less than 99.999% as a shielding gas and a reaction gas, and a tungsten having a diameter of 3.2 mm as a cathode, the pump body as an anode, and the TIG welder as a heat source to perform a nitrogen arc cladding on the pump body and (iii) mixing the mixture with methyl cellulose, applying the surface of the pump body after the second step of treatment, drying the pump body in a drying oven after the coating is finished, using the carbon dioxide laser to laser cladding in an argon or nitrogen atmosphere and obtaining the high wear resistant coating material on the surface of the pump. INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for:
   (1) the high wear-resistant coating material is obtained by the above mentioned method; and
   (2) use of a highly wear-resistant coating material for improving the lubricity and wear resistance of a pump body during movement.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The average microhardness of the high wear-resistant coating is 1005HV0.2. The wear-resistant reinforcing phase of the high wear-resistant coating material is titanium carbide and TiWC2, and the self-lubricating phase is Ti2CS and chromium sulfide. Preferred Method: The pretreatment includes surface grinding, surface descaling and surface degreasing of the pump body, sanding the surface grinding and surface descaling with water sandpaper of not less than 1000 mesh and washing the surface degreasing uses anhydrous ethanol, dimethylformamide and deionized water at least once. The WS2 powder is obtained by dissolving the ammonium paratungstate in ammonia aqueous solution, maintaining the temperature of solution at 60-70 degrees C, continuously introducing vulcanized hydrogen gas for 2-6 hours, adding the mixed solution into low temperature environment for 12-24 hours after filtering, filtering the obtained crystal washing for multiple times by anhydrous ethanol and ether, drying in oven at 50 degrees C for 5 hours and performing the crushing process by the ball mill. The work condition of the carbon dioxide laser is as follows: the working pressure is 0.4-1.2Mpa, the laser power is 1-5kW, the scanning speed is 2-10 mm/seconds, the diameter of light spot is 2-6 mm and the energy density of laser is 50-250 J/mm3. The coating thickness of the high abrasion resistant coating material on the pump body is not greater than 2 mm.

PN WO2018234695-A1; FR3067719-A1; EP3642321-A1; FR3067719-B1
TI Continuous methanization in viscous manner in fermentor divided into compartments with closed gas and variable pressure skies, comprises e.g. introducing and transferring substrate to ferment, in first compartment.
AU BONHOMME M
AE BONHOMME M (BONH-Individual)
   BONHOMME M (BONH-Individual)
GA 201900131X
AB    NOVELTY - Continuous methanization in viscous manner in fermentor divided into compartments or fermentation tanks with closed gas and variable pressure skies, comprises e.g.: introducing and transferring substrate to ferment, where the substrate has high viscosity and comprises at least 12% dry matter, in first compartment maintained at hyperthermophilic temperature, preferably 65-72 degrees C, and in several compartments maintained at a temperature preferably thermophilic; mixing the fermentation substrate in the agitation sectors of each compartment by compressed biogas injection expanded under branch plates fixed to the floor of each compartment; regulating the transfer of the fermentation substrate from one compartment to another by the suction created by the flow of pressurized gas on either side of the partition wall of the compartments; and regulating the pH of the first compartment for hydrolysis and acidogenesis to 5.5, preferably hyperthermophilic temperature, to produce dihydrogen.
   USE - The method is useful for continuous methanization in viscous manner, which is used to produce biomethane.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: increases the methane content of biomethane by exogenous supply of dihydrogen, while allowing its reinjection into the natural gas network; and improves the economics of gas methanation, preferably in the biochemical production of methane from carbon dioxide and dihydrogen.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Continuous methanization in a viscous manner in a fermentor divided into compartments or fermentation tanks with closed gas and variable pressure skies, comprises: introducing and transferring a substrate to ferment, where the substrate has high viscosity and comprises at least 12% dry matter, in a first compartment maintained at a hyperthermophilic temperature, preferably 65-72 degrees C, and in several compartments maintained at a temperature preferably thermophilic; mixing the fermentation substrate in the agitation sectors of each compartment by compressed biogas injection expanded under branch plates fixed to the floor of each compartment; regulating the transfer of the fermentation substrate from one compartment to another by the suction created by the flow of pressurized gas on either side of the partition wall of the compartments; regulating the pH of the first compartment for hydrolysis and acidogenesis to 5.5, preferably hyperthermophilic temperature, to produce dihydrogen; adjusting the partial pressure of carbon dioxide in the closed gas sky of the other compartments to contain the pH, preferably below 7.3; re-circulating the biogas containing hydrogen produced in the first compartments through microporous ramps located on the floor preferably in the last compartment; and optionally, introduction of exogenous dihydrogen into the last compartment gas sky by the microporous ramps located on the floor of the fermenter of the last compartment to produce at least 95% biomethane. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for a methanation device designed to implement the continuous methanization process in a viscous manner, comprising a fermentor divided into compartments or closed fermentation tanks with closed gas and variable pressure.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The regulation of the pH of the fermentation substrate in at least the last compartment is carried out by adjusting the partial pressure of carbon dioxide in the gas sky, where the regulation of the carbon dioxide partial pressure is effected by adjusting the total pressure as a function of the carbon dioxide content and the gas head volume by means of variable opening valves on each gas sky. The comprises the regulation of the acid-base balance by the point opening of the valve on the transfer line between the compartments during agitation of the proximity sector generating the suction from one compartment to another and therefore the flow then the backflow of the substrate on the other side of the wall. The calculation of the volume of dihydrogen to be introduced by the microporous ramps is carried out according to the equation of four moles of hydrogen per mole of carbon dioxide by measuring the volume of solubilized carbon dioxide obtained by the calculating the volume of carbon dioxide introduced and maintaining the required carbon dioxide partial pressure.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

Combustion improvement additives for coal, peat and other nonhydrocarbon based fuels are included in this Section together with coal liquefaction and desulphurisation.)

PN CN109081339-A
TI Nitrogen-doped activated carbon prepared by            pretreatment of residue of kitchen waste, preliminary            carbonization of residue of kitchen waste, impregnation            of carbonized material, and activating carbonization,            for capturing greenhouse gas.
AU ZHANG Y
   LI P
   XIE Y
   HU M
   SUO J
AE UNIV SICHUAN (USCU-C)
GA 201901597U
AB    NOVELTY - Nitrogen-doped activated carbon for capturing                greenhouse gas capture, has a high carbon dioxide                capture capability. It is prepared by: (1)                pretreatment of residue of the kitchen waste:                drying and pulverizing residue of the kitchen                waste, and passing through a 60 mesh sieve; (2)                preliminary carbonization of residue of the kitchen                waste: placing the charcoal residue of the kitchen                waste in a tube furnace and carbonizing at 300-600                degrees C; (3) impregnation of the carbonized                material by potassium hydroxide solution:                impregnating the carbonized material with a mass                concentration of 5-30% potassium hydroxide                solution; (4) activating carbonization: placing the                impregnated mixture in an oven and drying at 105                degrees C; placing the dried material in a tube                furnace and activating at high temperature, and a                nitrogen atmosphere is used; and (5) washing and                drying: successively washing crude activated carbon                with a hydrochloric acid solution and deionized                water until the pH is 6.5-7.0, and drying in an                oven.
   USE - The nitrogen-doped activated carbon is useful                for capturing greenhouse gas.
   ADVANTAGE - The method not only improves the resource                utilization rate and the processing efficiency of                the kitchen waste, but also provides sustainable                and inexpensive raw material for preparing                activated carbon for capturing greenhouse                gases.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Nitrogen-doped activated carbon for capturing                greenhouse gas, has a high carbon dioxide capture                capability. It is prepared by: (1) pretreatment of                the residue of the kitchen waste: drying and                pulverizing residue of the kitchen waste, and                passing through a 60 mesh sieve; (2) preliminary                carbonization of the residue of the kitchen waste:                placing the charcoal residue of the kitchen waste                in a tube furnace and carbonizing at 300-600                degrees C; the heating rate is 5-10 degrees                C/minute, and the carbonization time is 0.5-3                hours, using a nitrogen atmosphere (flow rate of                4.8 L/hour); (3) impregnation of the carbonized                material by potassium hydroxide solution:                impregnating the carbonized material with a mass                concentration of 5-30% potassium hydroxide                solution; the volume ratio of the potassium                hydroxide solution is 1: 5-1:15, and the immersion                time is 15 hours; (4) activating carbonization:                placing the impregnated mixture in an oven and                drying at 105 degrees C; placing the dried material                in a tube furnace and activating at high                temperature, and a nitrogen atmosphere is used                (flow rate is 4.8 L/hour), the heating rate is 5-10                degrees C/minute, the activation time is 0.5-4                hours, the activation temperature is 500-700                degrees C, and finally the activated carbon crude                product is obtained; and (5) washing and drying:                successively washing crude activated carbon with a                hydrochloric acid solution and deionized water                until the pH is 6.5-7.0, and drying in an oven at                90 degrees C to obtain a nitrogen-doped activated                carbon.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The nitrogen-doped                  activated carbon is a waste material which is                  produced by a biological treatment process of a                  kitchen waste, and is synthesized by potassium                  hydroxide activation method. Preferred Component:                  The nitrogen element in the nitrogen-doped                  activated carbon is derived from a raw material                  (food waste residue) containing abundant                  nitrogen.

TI Preparing, preserving and recycling standard            sample of human fecal bacteria e.g. collecting stool            sample, transferring to anaerobic workstation, storing,            mixing fecal bacterial extract with glycerin and            complex protective agent and storing.
AU ZHU X
   XU Y
   HAN J
   XIANG S
   BAO X
   FENG X
   CHEN J
   CHEN Y
AE UNIV ZHEJIANG GONGSHANG (UYZG-C)
GA 2018A04901
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing, preserving and recycling standard                sample of human fecal bacteria comprises (i) taking                sample source from healthy and non-smoking person                who did not use antibiotics, probiotics and                probiotics or laxatives for the first 3 weeks, and                using as sample sources, (ii) urinating the donor                and collecting the feces during the sample                collection process, and transferring the stool                sample to the anaerobic workstation immediately,                (iii) extracting fecal bacteria in a sterile                anaerobic workstation, weighing the stool,                dissolving the stool sample in phosphate-buffered                saline solution, centrifuging to obtain fecal                bacterial extract, (iv) carrying out short-term                storage in a sterile anaerobic workstation, mixing                the fecal bacterial extract with glycerin,                freezing, storing to obtain short-term preservation                sample, (v) carrying out long-term preservation in                a sterile anaerobic workstation, mixing the fecal                bacterial extract and the complex protective agent,                and storing, and (vi) recovering.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing, preserving                and recycling standard sample of human fecal                bacteria.
   ADVANTAGE - The method ensures more than 85% viable                bacterial ratio.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing, preserving and recycling standard                sample of human fecal bacteria comprises (i) taking                sample source from healthy and non-smoking person                who did not use antibiotics, probiotics and                probiotics or laxatives for the first 3 weeks, and                using as sample sources, (ii) urinating the donor                and collecting the feces during the sample                collection process, and transferring the stool                sample to the anaerobic workstation immediately,                (iii) extracting fecal bacteria in a sterile                anaerobic workstation, weighing the stool,                dissolving the stool sample in phosphate-buffered                saline solution, centrifuging to obtain fecal                bacterial extract, (iv) carrying out short-term                storage in a sterile anaerobic workstation, mixing                the fecal bacterial extract with glycerin in a                ratio of 3:1-4:1, quickly freezing with liquid                nitrogen, storing in -80 degrees C refrigerator to                obtain short-term preservation sample, (v) carrying                out long-term preservation in a sterile anaerobic                workstation, mixing the fecal bacterial extract and                the complex protective agent in a volume of 1:5,                adjusting the pH to 6.6-6.8, magnetically stirring                for 15-20 minutes, vacuum-freeze-drying, and                storing the powder sample in a -80 degrees C                refrigerator to obtain long-term deposited samples,                and (vi) recovering and removing the deposited                fecal bacteria sample, placing in an anaerobic                incubator filled with 10% carbon dioxide and 90%                nitrogen for 37 hours at 37 degrees C, if the                short-term preservation sample is mixed with the                recovering fluid at a ratio of 1:2, and placing in                an anaerobic incubator filled with 10% carbon                dioxide and 90% nitrogen for 37 hours at 37 degrees                C, if the long-term preservation sample is added to                the recovering fluid in an amount of 8%.                INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for:
   (1) long-term preservation complex protective                agent, comprising the raw materials of complex                protective agent given below; and
   (2) recovering fluid, comprising the raw                materials of complex protective agent given                below
   .
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The complex protective                  agent comprises 60-65 g/l maltodextrin, 40-45 g/l                  fructose, 20-24 g/l sucrose, 40-45 g/l skim milk                  powder, 32-36 g/l corn alcohol soluble protein,                  20-22 g/l rice hydrolyzed peptide, 17-20 g/l fish                  skin protein, 10-12 g/l DHA, 8-10 g/l glycerol                  and 3-5 g/l ascorbic acid. The recovering fluid                  comprises 2-3 g/l dipotassium hydrogen phosphate,                  4-5 g/l sodium chloride, 0.0035-0.005 g/l iron                  sulfate heptahydrate, 0.3-0.45 g/l magnesium                  sulfate monohydrate, 0.3-0.4 g/l calcium chloride                  dehydrate, 0.8-1.4 g/l glucose, 1-1.2 g/l                  peptone, 0.035-0.05 g/l bile salt and 0.3-0.4 g/l                  cysteamine. Preferred Method: The gas environment                  of the anaerobic workstation in the step (iii) is                  10% carbon dioxide and 90% nitrogen. The process                  comprises dissolving every 10 g feces in 80 ml of                  0.01 M phosphate-buffered saline solution. The                  centrifugation is carried out at 600                  revolutions/minute for 1-2 minutes. The                  vacuum-freeze-drying conditions are: the cold                  shock treatment is carried out at 6 degrees C for                  5 hours; the pre-freezing temperature is -35                  degrees C; the frozen material thickness is 8 mm;                  the vacuum of the drying chamber is 30 Pa; the                  sublimation temperature is 28 degrees C; and the                  resolution temperature is 37 degrees C.

TI Processing small air volume volatile organic compounds waste gas by cryogenic series catalytic oxidation involves using oxidation gas temperature linkage diluting valve opening degree to control air inlet safety volatile organic compounds.
AU LUO S
   HUANG S
   YUAN A
AE NINGBO ZHONGRUI ENVIRONMENTAL PROTECTION (NING-Non-standard)
   HUANG S (HUAN-Individual)
GA 2021938533
AB    NOVELTY - Processing small air volume volatile organic compounds (VOCs) waste gas by cryogenic series catalytic oxidation involves combining condensing unit and catalytic oxidation unit into whole process, where condensing unit based on VOCs component contained in waste gas preset multiple condensing temperature, waste gas based on VOCs ingredient contained in condensing unit is cooled to corresponding condensing temperature, and waste gas is passed into catalytic oxidation unit. The catalytic oxidation unit uses oxidation gas temperature linkage diluting valve opening degree to control air inlet safety VOCs in the waste gas under the action of catalyst without flame oxidation to carbon dioxide and water.
   USE - Method for processing small air volume VOCs waste gas by cryogenic series catalytic oxidation.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to process common alkane, aromatic hydrocarbon, alcohol, ether, ketone, ester, aldehyde, organic acid and halogen-free waste gas, phosphorus, sulfur and VOCs waste gas, initial concentration of waste gas is from 1000 milligram/cubic meter (mg/m3) to saturated steam concentration, operation load elasticity is large, and operation is stable, safe and reliable.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The condensing unit comprises pre-cooler and cryocooler. The catalytic oxidation unit comprises heat exchange device and catalytic oxidation device. Preferred Conditions: The condensing unit and the catalytic oxidation unit are set with one-key start to realize fully automatic control. Preferred Process: The condensation temperature is such that the saturated gas concentration of the corresponding waste gas after condensation at the temperature is less than 50% lower explosive limit (LEL). The cryocooler uses liquid nitrogen as freezing liquid, and pre-cooler recovers amount of cold in waste gas after cryogenic cooling. The pre-cooler and cryocooler are installed up and down in series, and newly-inlet waste gas in pre-cooler enters and exits up and down. The cryocooler adopts coil-type cooling structure, refrigerant enters and exits from bottom of the tube, and waste gas enters and exits from the shell. The waste gas passes through pre-cooler to recover cold energy and merges with nourishing air to enter heat exchange device to exchange heat with oxidizing gas discharged from catalytic oxidation device and enters catalytic oxidation device for catalytic oxidation. The pre-cooler regularly passes in fixed amount of oxidizing gas to discharge hot air for defrosting. The heat exchange device is four-way tubular heat exchanger, and the waste gas goes through tube pass and oxidized gas goes through the shell pass.

GA 202191499S
AB    NOVELTY - Producing lime by combining waste coal with sintering plant, comprises pre-treating and sintering by (i) immersing waste coal in a sodium hydroxide solution, and conducting the first microwave heating, adding mixture of steam and carbon dioxide while stirring; (ii) applying second microwave heating to the waste coal that undergone the first microwave heating, heating after the solvent evaporates, and cooling to room temperature naturally; (iii) adding the cooled waste coal in a ball mill for ball milling to obtain pretreated waste coal; (iv) adding calcium carbonate to the pretreated waste coal to form a mixture, performing the first sintering, and performing the second sintering when no gas escapes from the system; and (v) keeping the temperature constant, sintering mixture after the second sintering for the third time, and the naturally cooling to room temperature to obtain a finished product.

GA 2021686704
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves utilizing a carbon dioxide                laser as an inducing light source, a polyimide film                as a precursor.    (2) outdoor alkyd varnish comprising UV radiation-resistant alkyd resin as film-forming component.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The triazine polybasic acid is of formula (I). The polyol is trimethylolpropane. The outdoor alkyd varnish comprises 70-90 wt.% UV radiation-resistant alkyd resin, 0.2-0.3 wt.% drier, 0.1-0.5 wt.% oxime compound and 10-15 wt.% organic solvent. The organic solvent is xylene. The oxime compound is 2-butanone oxime.R1 = benzene-1,3,5-tricarboxylic acid of formula (II);R2 = phenol moiety of formula (III);R3 = phenol moiety of formula (IV);R21-R24 = hydroxy; andR31-R34 = hydroxy.

PN CN112340763-A
TI Preparation of nano calcium carbonate for aqueous            ink, involves aging refined calcium hydroxide slurry,            adding crystal form control agent, carbonizing,            heating, adding modifier, modifying, filtering,            dehydrating, and depolymerizing.
AU ZHAO X
   AO Z
   LIAO J
AE JIANGXI HUAMING NANO CALCIUM CARBONATE (JIAN-Non-standard)
GA 2021176819
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of nano calcium carbonate,                involves aging refined calcium hydroxide slurry for                18-24 hours, removing slag using a vibrating screen                and a rotary liquid separator, adjusting the                concentration to 10-20 %mass, adjusting the                temperature to 20-35 degrees C, adding 0.3-1.5                %mass crystal form control agent (mixture of sodium                tripolyphosphate, dimer acid sodium, zinc sulfate,                and sucrose is 1:2:1:1), stir-mixing, introducing                20-40 vol.% carbon dioxide gas for bubbling                carbonization until the pH of the slurry is 6.5-7                to obtain 10-20 %mass boiled pulp, removing the                clinker, stirring, heating to 65-85 degrees C,                adding 2-8 %mass modifier (mixture of rosin                triquaternary ammonium salt, phenyltriethoxysilane,                and polyacrylamide is 1:1:0.2), adjusting the pH to                7-9, stirring and modifying for 60-90 minutes to                obtain a modified nano calcium carbonate                suspension, filtering and dehydrating, and drying                and depolymerizing.
   USE - Preparation of nano calcium carbonate for                aqueous ink.
   ADVANTAGE - The method provides nano calcium carbonate                having excellent compatibility, stability, fluidity                and dispersibility, no agglomeration, and strong                antiprecipitation ability.

PN CN112300419-A
TI Preparation of cellulose aerogel for e.g. protein adsorption, involves preparing aerogel using e.g. siloxane, initiator, environmentally compatible substances and cellulose nanofiber suspension, fully crosslinking in oven, and vacuum drying.
AU LI Y
   MU M
   YU H
   TANG J
AE UNIV ZHEJIANG SCI-TECH (UZST-C)
GA 202113735G
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of cellulose aerogel involves (1) adding siloxane and initiator to solvent, obtaining reaction system, (2) blowing nitrogen into the reaction system to isolate it from oxygen, polymerizing, adding solvents and environmentally compatible substances and initiators, aggregating, cooling to room temperature, obtaining a solution, (3) adding the solution to weakly polar organic solvent to precipitate, dissolving the precipitated material in a polar solvent, and repeating precipitation for many times, vacuum drying, obtaining intermediate product (A), (4) taking cellulose nanofiber suspension, adding cross-linking agent and the intermediate product (A), stirring at room temperature, obtaining intermediate product (B), (5) feeding the intermediate product (B) to the mold, directional freezing and freeze drying, obtaining aerogel, (6) fully crosslinking the aerogel in oven, using a solvent to wash away unreacted substances, and vacuum drying.
   USE - Preparation of cellulose aerogel for protein adsorption and desorption, dye adsorption, and directional transportation.
   ADVANTAGE - The aerogel material combines high specific surface area, biocompatibility, regular microstructure and environmental responsiveness (such as temperature, carbon dioxide, pH). The structure of aerogel has excellent stability.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The solvent in the step (1) is added in an amount of 10-30 mL. The solvent in the step (2) is added in an amount of 10-30 mL. The ratio of environmentally responsive substances and initiator is 50:1-100:1. Preferred Components: The environmentally responsive substance is chosen from one of the substances that N-isopropylacrylamide and N,N-dimethylaminoethyl methacrylate respond to environmental stimuli. The weakly polar organic solvent in the step (3) is chosen from to one of n-hexane, petroleum ether, and cyclohexane. The polar solvent is chosen from one of tetrahydrofuran and dimethyl sulfoxide. The solvent in step (6) used for cleaning is ethanol.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The siloxane is methylsiloxane with polymerization activity. The crosslinking agent is 1-1.5 wt.% methoxy siloxane. Preferred Composition: The ratio of siloxane and initiator is 50:1-100:1. The ratio of cellulose nanofiber and siloxane is 1:0.2-1:1. The ratio of cellulose nanofiber and intermediate product (B) is 1:1-1:3. Preferred Conditions: The polymerization in the step (2) is radical polymerization or atom transfer radical polymerization (ATRP). The nitrogen gas is passed for 5-10 minutes. In step (2), the polymerization is carried out at 50-70 degrees C for 8-10 hours. In step (2), the polymerization after adding raw materials is carried out for 10-12 hours. In step (3), the vacuum drying 3-10 times is performed at 20-35 degrees C for 24-48 hours. In step (4), the reaction is performed for 8-12 hours. In the step (5), the directional freezing is to use liquid nitrogen to freeze the aerogel on the thermally conductive metal from top to bottom, and the freeze-drying time is 36-48 hours. In the step (6), the temperature of the oven is controlled at 100-110 degrees C. After cleaning, the vacuum drying in step (6) is performed at 40-60 degrees C for 122-24 hours.

PN CN112088900-A
TI Graphene-based composite antibacterial and antiviral powder comprises weak-light photocatalyst powder, complexed silver ions, zinc ions, copper ions and graphene microflakes.
AU WANG Y
AE SHANGHAI XIFENG BIOTECHNOLOGY CENT (SHAN-Non-standard)
GA 2021007240
AB    NOVELTY - Graphene-based composite antibacterial and antiviral powder comprises weak-light photocatalyst powder, complexed silver ions, zinc ions, copper ions and graphene microflakes.
   USE - Graphene-based composite antibacterial and antiviral powder.
   ADVANTAGE - The graphene-based composite antibacterial and antiviral powder oxidizes and decomposes viruses, organic pollutants, odors and bacteria into carbon dioxide and water.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for preparing a graphene-based composite antibacterial and antiviral powder, which involves:
   (A) placing complexed silver ions, zinc ions and copper ions in an aqueous solution prepared by amino acids, inorganic salts and sugars through a metal ion carrier;
   (B) heating, so that the complexed silver ion, zinc ion and copper ion are freed in the prepared aqueous solution;
   (C) pouring the low-light photocatalyst powder into the aqueous solution containing metal ions, heating and stirring to mix;
   (D) placing the graphene microchips in the mixed aqueous solution, so that the metal ions and the low-light photocatalyst powder are loaded in the interlayer structure of the graphene microchips;
   (E) filtering out the graphene microchips, drying, heating; and
   (F) grounding to obtain graphene-based composite antibacterial and antiviral powder.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - PHARMACEUTICALS - Preferred Conditions: The graphene-based composite antibacterial and antiviral powder reacts with moisture in the air to generate active oxygen and hydroxyl radicals, active oxygen and hydroxyl radicals oxidize and decompose viruses, organic pollutants, odors and bacteria into carbon dioxide and water. The graphene-based composite antibacterial and antiviral powder absorbs the energy in the light source, activates the electrons on the particle surface, and escapes from the original orbit. The positively charged holes are formed on the surface, and the escaped electrons and holes react with water vapor in the air to generate active oxygen and hydroxyl radicals. The escaped electrons undergo a reduction reaction, and the holes undergo an oxidation reaction. The complexed silver ions, zinc ions and copper ions react with proteins, or replace metal ions in the enzyme, so that the enzyme is inactivated and the virus is killed. The particle size of the low-light photocatalyst powder is 20 nanometers. The use ratio of the graphene-based composite antibacterial and antiviral powder is 0.3-5%.

PN CN112095043-A
TI Removal of titanium from high-titanium ferrophosphorus alloy by crushing and grinding high-titanium ferrophosphorus alloy raw material block into high-titanium ferrophosphorus alloy powder, and placing into heating furnace, and heating.
AU ZHU X
   SU X
   WANG J
   TU H
   CHEN J
   HUANG X
AE UNIV CHANGZHOU (UNCZ-C)
   JIANGSU JIANGNAN FERROALLOY CO LTD (JIAN-Non-standard)
GA 2020C9073R
AB    NOVELTY - Removing titanium from high titanium ferrophosphorus alloy comprises (i) crushing and grinding high-titanium ferrophosphorus alloy raw material block to obtain high-titanium ferrophosphorus alloy powder with granularity of 10-400 meshes; (ii) placing the high titanium ferrophosphorus alloy powder into the tray, controlling the thickness of the high-titanium ferrophosphorus alloy powder to 1-10 cm, placing the tray in the heating furnace and continuously introducing carbon dioxide gas into the heating furnace at 750-1000 degrees C with melting point of 300-400 degrees C; (iii) placing the ferrophosphorus alloy into the melting furnace and melting temperature at 1400-1600 degrees C to obtain molten alloy liquid and slag floating on the alloy liquid surface; (iv) pouring the molten alloy liquid and slag into the cast steel mold together, naturally layering, cooling and forming; and (v) crushing the alloy blocks and slag blocks, and manually sort to obtain low-titanium-phosphorus-iron alloy ingots.
   USE - The method is useful for removing titanium from high titanium ferrophosphorus alloy.
   ADVANTAGE - The method efficiently and economically removes titanium from high-titanium ferrophosphorus alloy, without affecting the environment.

GA 2020C4129A
AB    NOVELTY - Producing vanadium pentoxide by one-step vanadium precipitation of vanadium-containing materials using dilute sulfuric acid as leaching agent, comprises determining leaching sulfuric acid concentration based on acid consumption of vanadium-containing material and residual acid concentration at the leaching end point, and adding fluorite powder as a leaching aid for repeated enrichment leaching, neutralizing and purifying the obtained circulating enrichment leachate with calcium powder to control certain sulfuric acid concentration, oxidizing the calcium powder neutralization and purification solution to vanadium under high acid condition using hydrogen peroxide, introducing carbon dioxide, adding wet polymerized polyvanadic acid, and forming vanadium precipitation by steam heating to produce crude vanadium, and returning vanadium precipitation mother liquor to first leaching to prepare acid, refining the crude vanadium and washing to produce vanadium pentoxide products containing grid.

PN CN111946335-A
TI Method for obtaining formation pressure based on downhole hydrocarbyl detection technology, involves performing formation hydrocarbyl detection in wellbore each time bottom hole pressure is reduced and setting pressure value.
AU LI Z
   LIANG Y
   YANG B
   YANG X
   LI S
   GAO R
   LEI Y
   JIANG L
   TANG G
   ZUO X
AE CHINA NAT PETROLEUM CORP (CNPC-C)
   CNPC CHUANQING DRILLING ENG CO LTD (CNPC-C)
GA 2020B7401G
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves using downhole hydrocarbyl detection tools to monitor the presence of formation hydrocarbyls in the wellbore. The formation hydrocarbyl detection is performed on the wellbore each time the bottom hole pressure is increased, until the concentration of hydrocarbyls in the formation in the wellbore is greater than 0 and not greater than a%. The first formation pressure is less than first pressure. The formation hydrocarbyl detection in the wellbore is performed each time the bottom hole pressure is reduced, until the formation hydrocarbyl concentration in the wellbore is greater than 0 and not greater than a%. The second formation pressure is greater than first pressure. The gas non- hydrocarbyl substance is comprised of carbon dioxide (CO2), hydrogen sulfide (H2S), water salt water and chloride ions.
   USE - Method for obtaining formation pressure based on downhole hydrocarbyl detection technology.
   ADVANTAGE - The downhole hydrocarbyl detection technology can be used to obtain the formation pressure in real time, and the measured data is highly accurate, economical and applicable, and can be used for oil and gas discovery and well control safety during drilling.
DC H01 (Obtaining crude oil and natural gas - including exploration, drilling, well completion, production and treatment. General off-shore platform and drilling technology is included together with the treatment of tar sands and oil shales (C10G, E21B).); Q49 (Mining (E21))
MC H01-B03B; Q49-A
IP E21B-047/06; E21B-049/00

PN CN111924175-A
TI Method for filling up spray cans, involves preparing a liquid material and preparing the tank and valve assembly along with pressing the prepared liquid material into the tank through the filling device and installing the nozzle button.
AU HE Y
   XIAO H
   LIU X
AE GUANGZHOU TAIDENG CAR ACCESSORY CO LTD (GUAN-Non-standard)
GA 2020B84269
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves preparing a liquid material. The tank body and valve assembly are prepared. The valve assembly is installed on the tank body. The propellant is pressed into the body, where propellant is selected from carbon dioxide, nitrogen dioxide, nitrogen, propane, butane or dimethyl ether. The prepared liquid material is pressed into the tank through the filling device. The nozzle button is installed on the valve assembly. The protective cover is installed on the valve assembly and outer side of the tank. The spray finished products are obtained. The spray finished products is put into quality inspection. The unqualified products are put into the waste collection tank for waste disposal and recycling.
   USE - Method for filling up spray cans.
   ADVANTAGE - The method involves putting the spray finished products into quality inspection and putting the unqualified products into the waste collection tank for waste disposal and recycling, and thus overall production costs are reduced along with improved utilization rate of spray cans.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of a spray can.

PN CN111792627-A; CN111792627-B
TI Recovering dilute sulfuric acid during production of methyl chloride by adding dilute sulfuric acid to stripping device, stripping, filtering dilute sulfuric acid, concentrating dilute sulfuric acid and returning concentrated sulfuric acid.
AU OUYANG J
   XIAO G
   HE Z
   WU Y
   SHI W
AE JIANGXI BLUESTAR ORGANIC SILICONE PLANT (JIAN-Non-standard)
GA 2020A6745Q
AB    NOVELTY - Method for recovering dilute sulfuric acid during the production of methyl chloride, involves (a) adding dilute sulfuric acid produced by methyl chloride production process to a stripping device through a low-pressure pipe, and passing low-pressure steam through the low-pressure pipe for the stripping reaction, (b) filtering the dilute sulfuric acid after stripping, (c) concentrating the filtered dilute sulfuric acid to obtain colorless and transparent sulfuric acid with a content of more than 98 wt.%, and optionally (d) returning the concentrated sulfuric acid to the production of methyl chloride for continued use.
   USE - The method is useful for recovering dilute sulfuric acid during the production of methyl chloride.
   ADVANTAGE - The method uses the oxidizing property of sulfuric acid itself to oxidize the organic substance and other impurities into carbon dioxide and water through the combination of stripping reaction and low-pressure steam, and the obtained concentrated sulfuric acid has high quality and few impurities, and is recycled, and reduces the production cost of methyl chloride, and does not generate secondary industrial waste, which reduces environmental pollution.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - CHEMICAL ENGINEERING - Preferred Device: The reaction device in the stripping reaction is equipped with a graphite cooler, an inserting bottom tube which is a hollow cylindrical tube, and a spherical device with a small hole is connected at the bottom, where the materials used are PTFE, titanium or enamel. Preferred Conditions: In step (a), the steam pressure of the low-pressure steam is 2-8 kg/cm3, preferably 4-6 kg/cm3, the temperature of the low-pressure steam in the stripping reaction is 100-180 degrees C, preferably 130-150 degrees C, the flow rate of low-pressure steam in the stripping reaction is 30-80 l/minute, preferably 40-60 l/minute, the introduction time of the low pressure steam in the stripping reaction is 2-8 hours, preferably 3-5 hours, and the reaction temperature in the stripping reaction is 120-170 degrees C, preferably 130-150 degrees C, where the reaction temperature in the stripping reaction is close to the temperature of the low-pressure steam, when the temperature is close, the temperature difference between the two is not greater than 10 degrees C. In step (c), the concentration temperature is 190-230 degrees C and a vacuum degree is 0.09-0.1 MPa.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The filter cloth is made of polypropylene and PTFE.
DC A97 (Miscellaneous goods not specified elsewhere - including papermaking, gramophone records, detergents, food and oil well applications.); E16 (Aliphatics - containing N and/or halogen.); E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).)

AU KATIYAR A
   KNIGHT T E
   PATIL P D
   ROHILLA N
AE DOW GLOBAL TECHNOLOGIES LLC (DOWC-C)
   DOW GLOBAL TECHNOLOGIES LLC (DOWC-C)

GA 202085134P
AB    NOVELTY - A sandstone-containing oil reservoir additive                composition comprises (a) nonionic wettability                alteration agent, and (b) injection fluid.
   USE - A sandstone-containing oil reservoir additive                composition for enhanced oil recovery from                sandstone-containing reservoir, such as                waterflooding operation and carbon dioxide flooding                operation for enhanced oil recovery (all                claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The composition provides a less costly                additive that can alter the wettability of                sandstone reservoir rocks to enhance the                effectiveness of enhanced oil recovery (EOR)                methods such as waterflooding.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A sandstone-containing oil reservoir additive                composition comprises (a) nonionic wettability                alteration agent of formula R-O-(C3H6O)x-(C2H4O)y-H                (I), and (b) injection fluid.
   R=4-10C alkyl residue from a branched                sec.-alcohol initiator;
   x=3-10; and
   y=1-20.
   An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for enhanced                oil recovery from sandstone-containing reservoir                which involves mixing the nonionic wettability                alteration agent with the injection fluid to form                the sandstone-containing oil reservoir additive                composition; injecting the sandstone-containing oil                reservoir additive composition into a                sandstone-containing oil reservoir; and extracting                oil from the sandstone-containing oil                reservoir.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The amount of nonionic                  wettability agent in sandstone reservoir additive                  composition is 0.01-20 wt.% based on the total                  weight of sandstone reservoir additive                  composition. Injection fluid is selected from sea                  water, engineered water, injection brine, and/or                  produced brine. Preferred Parameters: The                  temperature in the sandstone-containing oil                  reservoir is 0-100 degrees C.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The                  sandstone-containing oil reservoir additive                  composition further includes a hydrocarbon-based                  solvent selected from naphthene hydrocarbons,                  paraffin hydrocarbons, and/or hexane.
   Preferred Definitions:
   R=6-9C alkyl residue;
   x=3-8; and
   y=5-20.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

TI System for generating tunable ultrafast optical            pulses, has laser source from which beam is directed            for propagation within nonlinear medium, and filter            unit that is configured to select portion of pulse            output spectrum, respectively.
AU PICCOLI R
   SCHMIDT B E
   RAZZARI L
   JEONG Y
   MORANDOTTI R
AE INST NAT RECH SCI (NARE-Non-standard)
   INST NAT RECH SCI (NARE-Non-standard)
   INST NAT RECH SCI (NARE-Non-standard)
   INST NAT RECH SCI (NARE-Non-standard)
GA 202075490W
AB    NOVELTY - The system has a laser source, a nonlinear                medium of a third-order nonlinear susceptibility,                and a filter unit. A beam from laser source is                directed for propagation within nonlinear medium,                and filter unit is configured to select a portion                of a pulse output spectrum. An optical unit is                configured to adjust a size of the beam from the                laser source to a matching optical intensity in the                nonlinear medium. The nonlinear medium is one of                Argon, Nitrogen, Helium, Krypton, Xenon, and carbon                dioxide. The nonlinear medium is one of silica,                sapphire, and plasmas.
   USE - System for generating tunable ultrafast                optical pulses.
   ADVANTAGE - The system overcomes the restriction of the                temporal match between pump and signal pulses,                which imposes the use of high-intensity femtosecond                pump laser sources, optical parametric                chirped-pulse amplification (OPCPA) is                introduced.

TI Incinerator ash treatment system comprises dissolving tank, filter, freezing crystallization separation device, decarbonizer, reduction reactor, heating device and heat exchanger.
AU HU J
   CHENG L
   LIU L
   FANG Z
AE BEIJING MEIJING HUAXIA ENVIRONMENTAL TEC (BEIJ-Non-standard)
GA 2020745840
AB    NOVELTY - Incinerator ash treatment system comprises dissolving unit, freezing crystallization unit, acidification decarbonization unit, heavy metal removal unit, filtering unit and adsorption unit. The dissolving unit is used for dissolving ash of incinerator to be treated to obtain solution. The freezing crystallization unit is used for freezing and crystallizing solution from dissolving unit. The acidification decarbonization unit is used for acidifying and removing carbon dioxide from crystallization mother liquor discharged from freezing crystallization unit to remove carbonate and/or bicarbonate ions. The heavy metal removal unit is used for reducing and precipitating solution discharged from acidification decarbonization unit to remove heavy metals. The filtering unit is used for filtering effluent of heavy metal removal unit. The adsorption unit is used for adsorption treatment of filtrate of filtering unit.
   USE - Incinerator ash treatment system.
   ADVANTAGE - The system recovers salt in ash of incinerator, has high-quality usable additional products, concentrates impurities e.g. heavy metals in ash and reduces solid hazardous waste to be disposed.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of incinerator ash treatment system.

PN CN111429975-A
TI Method for calculating ablative properties of nitroamine propellant, involves determining peak temperature of inner wall of ablation tube, and calculating ablation wear of ablation tube and maximum pressure of reaction chamber.
AU WEI D
   JIN J
   YAN W
   ZHANG J
   LIU Y
   ZHOU J
AE XI'AN MODERN CHEM RES INST (CHNI-C)
GA 202070210Y
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves determining combustion products of nitroamine propellant including carbon dioxide, carbon monoxide, water, hydrogen, nitrogen and free species. The propellant ablative coefficient is calculated. The peak temperature of an inner wall of an ablation tube is determined. The amount of ablation and wear of the ablation tube is calculated under the action of nitroamine propellant gas and the maximum pressure of the reaction chamber. The ablation wear of the ablation tube is calculated under the action of nitroamine propellant gas according to formula relating average ablation wear of the ablation tube, ablation peak temperature of tube inner wall, propellant ablative coefficient and coefficient determined by the thermal hardness of the ablated tube material. The maximum pressure of the reaction chamber is calculated using power of the propellant per unit mass, filling density of the propellant, density of the propellant, and remaining volume of the propellant gas.
   USE - Method for calculating ablative properties of nitroamine propellant.
   ADVANTAGE - The ablative properties of high-energy nitroamine propellants with different formulations are calculated based on thermal, chemical and metallurgical factors.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - The free species include hydroxide, hydrogen, oxygen, nitrogen, and nitric oxide.
DC T01 (Digital Computers)
MC T01-J13
IP G16C-010/00

PN CN111389220-A
TI Method for treating volatile organic compounds in paste resin drying rear end gas, involves collecting droplets in overflow groove, and modifying photocatalyst used in photocatalytic oxidation in Titanium dioxide photocatalyst.
AU LIU B
   ZHANG Z
   CHEN F
   TANG Z
   LI J
   CAO H
   LI D
   ZHAO L
   LIU Y
   LIU L
   XU J
   LI M
   LIANG A
   GAO D
   YANG G
AE TANGSHAN SANYOU ALKALI-CHLORIDE CO LTD (TANG-Non-standard)
GA 2020668081
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves configuring a dried rear end gas of a paste resin to enter a gas scrubbing tower through an induced draft fan, where the rear end gas and an absorption liquid are fully contacted. A washed gas is passed into a vortex defogger. A rotating airflow is turned into the gas after passing through plate blades. Droplets are ejected at an elevation angle under an action of inertia to remove the droplets in the gas. The droplets are collected in an overflow groove. Volatile organic compounds in the gas are completely cracked to generate carbon dioxide and other inorganic substances after a cyclone defogging enters an Ultra Violet photocatalytic equipment. A photo-oxidation catalysis is divided into a direct photo-oxidation and a photo-catalytic oxidation. A light direct oxidation portion is configured to adopt multi-layer ultraviolet lamp with a wavelength of 185nm to 254nm. The photocatalyst used in photocatalytic oxidation is modified in a Titanium dioxide photocatalyst.
   USE - Method for treating volatile organic compounds in paste resin drying rear end gas.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - An anionic surfactant is comprises sodium lauryl sulfate, sodium dodecylbenzene sulfonate, sodium dioctyl succinate sulfonate or sodium lauryl sulfate, and the modified Titanium dioxide photocatalyst is made Zinc oxide, Cadmium Sulfide and Strontium titanate.

PN CN111357646-A
TI Tissue culture and rapid propagation of apple rootstock B9 comprises taking explants and inoculating into proliferation medium, taking cluster buds and inoculating into sugar-free rooting medium, cultivating under light.
AU LI Z
   WANG Y
   LAN L
   SHI X
AE SHAANXI QINGMEI BIOTECHNOLOGY CO LTD (SHAA-Non-standard)
GA 202063848U
AB    NOVELTY - Tissue culture and rapid propagation of apple rootstock B9 comprises (1) taking the explants and inoculating into the proliferation medium, light culture induces the formation of cluster buds, where the proliferation medium comprises MS medium, 25-35 g/l sucrose, 0.4-1.0 mg/l 6-BA and 0.3-0.5 mg/l naphthaleneacetic acid, pH 5.6-6.0, culture conditions includes light time 8-14 hour/day, light intensity 2500-3000 Lx, culturing time is 25-35 days, (2) taking the cluster buds with plant height greater than or equal to 2 cm and inoculating into sugar-free rooting medium, cultivating under light of 800-1500 ppm carbon dioxide to complete rooting, where the sugar-free rooting medium is hormone-free, sugar-free and agar-free 1/2 MS medium, culture conditions are : light culturing time is 10-12 hour/day, and light intensity is more than 3000 Lx.
   USE - The method is useful for tissue culture and rapid propagation of apple rootstock B9.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: controls the production of vitrified seedlings and enhances the proliferation rate, which can be quickly expanded.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - AGRICULTURE - Preferred Composition: In the step (1), the proliferation medium comprises Murashige and Skoog medium, 30 g/l sucrose, 0.8 mg/l 6-benzylaminopurine, and 0.4 mg/l naphthaleneacetic acid, pH 5.8. In the step (1), the propagation medium further comprises 1-3 g/l activated carbon, preferably 2 g/l activated carbon. In the step (1), the culture conditions comprise light time is 10-12 hour/day, light intensity 2800 Lx, and culture time is 30 days. Preferred Method: The step (1) further comprises after cultivating the explants to form lateral buds, adding far-infrared light side illumination, preferably, the far-infrared light side illumination time is 10-25 minute/day, more preferably 15 minute/day. The step (1), further comprises using a temperature difference between day and night to stimulate the growth of secondary shoots during the cultivation process, where the light cultivation temperature is 23-25 degrees C, and the dark cultivation temperature is 8-10 degrees C lower than the light cultivation temperature, more preferably 9 degrees C. In the step (2), the sugar-free medium further comprises vermiculite as a matrix, and the light intensity is 4000 Lx. In the step (1), cutting the stem segments with leaf buds from the sterile test tube seedlings as the explants, preferably, cultivating the sterile test tube seedlings, taking completely lignified B9 shoots, inducing buds, peeling off the shoot tips, inoculating MS blank medium after disinfection, dark culture first, and light culturing for 30-40 days, preferably 35 days to obtain the sterile test tube, among them, the light time is 10-12 hour/day, preferably 10 hour/day, the light intensity is 2500-3000 Lx, preferably 2800 Lx, and the culture temperature is 23-25 degrees C, preferably 24 degrees C. The B9 branches are winter branches, and no auxiliary agent is added during the induction of buds, refrigerating induced buds for 18-30 hours, preferably 24 hours, peeling off before the shoot tips. In the step (2), omitting the refining seedlings and directly transplanting the test tube seedlings obtained with full rooting.

PN JP2020070411-A; JP6978397-B2
TI Manufacture of polyurethane foam used as e.g. thermal insulation material, involves mixing composition comprising polyol and catalyst, and composition comprising polyisocyanate, adding foaming agent, reacting, foaming, and curing.
AU SEKI H
AE ASAHI YUKIZAI CORP (ASAY-C)
   ASAHI YUKIZAI KOGYO KK (ASAY-C)
GA 202037579W
AB    NOVELTY - Manufacture of polyurethane foam involves mixing a composition (A) comprising a polyol as a main component and an alkali metal carboxylate catalyst and/or quaternary ammonium salt catalyst as a catalyst, and a composition (B) comprising a polyisocyanate as a main component, reacting, foaming, and curing. The composition (A) and/or the composition (B) contain flame retardant (B1) containing red phosphorus. A foaming agent (F1) having threshold or subcriticality or containing liquid carbon dioxide is added to the composition (A) and/or the composition (B), and the reaction process is immediately carried out, after the addition process, at the manufacturing site of the polyurethane foam.
   USE - Manufacture of polyurethane foam used as reinforcing material used in civil work and thermal insulation material. Uses include but are not limited to electric refrigerator, building apartment, cold storage warehouse, and transfusion tube.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables manufacture of polyurethane foam with excellent flame retardance, due to efficient dispersion of red phosphorus with respect to reaction materials, high uniformity of density distribution, and suppressed occurrence of contractile difference in the interlayers and generation of curvature, by fast process.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Component: The alkali metal carboxylate catalyst is chosen from potassium octoate, potassium castorate, potassium acetate, and potassium 2-ethylhexanoate.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Process: The foaming process is carried out by a spray foaming method, after adding the foaming agent (F1) to the composition (A) and/or the composition (B). Preferred Composition: The composition (A) and/or the composition (B) further contain flame retardant (B2) chosen from phosphate ester, phosphate-containing flame retardant, stannate-containing flame retardant, bromine-containing flame retardant, boron-containing flame retardant, and metal hydroxide. The composition (A) contains 1-10 parts mass alkali metal carboxylate catalyst or quaternary ammonium salt catalyst, with respect to 100 parts mass polyol, when the catalyst is alkali metal carboxylate catalyst or quaternary ammonium salt catalyst, or 1-20 parts mass alkali metal carboxylate catalyst and quaternary ammonium salt catalyst, with respect to 100 parts mass polyol, when the catalyst is alkali metal carboxylate catalyst and quaternary ammonium salt catalyst, and 1-70 parts mass flame retardant (B1) and 3-90 parts mass flame retardant (B2), with respect to 100 parts mass polyol. The composition (A), the composition (B), or the foaming agent (F1) contains a foaming agent (F2) chosen from water, hydrocarbon, hydrofluorocarbon, and halogenated olefin. Preferred Properties: The difference between the whole density and the core density of the polyurethane foam is 0.1-12 kg/m3. The gross calorific value of the polyurethane foam is 8 MJ/m2 or less, when measured by heating at a radioactive heat intensity of 50 kW/m2 by a testing method based on ISO-5660 for 10 minutes or 20 minutes.

AU XU Y
   YOU Z
   YOU G
AE YANCHENG CROSS MACHINERY MFR CO LTD (YANC-Non-standard)
GA 202038903H
AB    NOVELTY - Rotary blade comprises straight blade bolted in cutter seat, bending section (2), mounting hole (3), and cutting edge. The straight blade and bending section are forge pieces. The bending section is stamped by stamping machine. The straight blade has end provided with three mounting holes and is bolted in cutter seat (4). The front ends of straight blade and bending section are provided with cutting edges. The straight blade and bending section are made of silicon manganese (60Si2Mn) steel, which is heated in whole salt bath is heated, oil-cooled quenched, degreased, and tempered in nitrate bath. The cutting edge has hardness HRC55. The straight blade has hardness HRC45. The cutting edge is formed via laser cladding using coating powder consisting of self-fusible iron-based alloy powder with particle size of 100 meshes under carbon dioxide laser with power of 3 kW, in which powder is added via powder feeder with powder feeding rate of 30 mg/minute.
   USE - Rotary blade.
   ADVANTAGE - The rotary blade is corrosion and wear resistant, has high-strength and long service life, reduces number of maintenance. It has special structure that improves soil crushing effect.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of rotary blade.
   Bending section (2)
   Mounting hole (3)
   Cutter seat (4)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - METALLURGY - Preferred Method: The salt bath is heated at 880 degrees C for 10-20 minutes. PN KR2020044334-A; KR2177423-B1
TI Producing paper-type leaflet, comprises e.g. grinding leaflet raw material by adding and dissolving calcium oxide with prepared leaflet material, and mixing obtained leaflets of calcium carbonate with prepared leaflets of calcium carbonate.
AU SEO Y B
   KANG L H
   AHN J
AE UNIV IND & ACADEMIC COOP IN CHUNGNAM NAT (UCHU-C)
   KIGAM KOREA INST GEOSCIENCE & MINERAL (KGEO-C)
GA 202036443T
AB    NOVELTY - Producing paper-type leaflet, comprises (a) grinding the leaflet raw material, pulping, fibrillation using a refiner, and drying after dehydration to prepare a leaflet stock, (b) adding and mixing 1-50 pts. wt. calcium oxide with 1 pts. wt. leaflet material, (c) dissolving leaflets and calcium oxide mixture in water, stirring to be uniformly mixed at 20-50 degrees C, injecting carbon dioxide to react with calcium oxide to form calcium carbonate on surface of leaflet stock to fill calcium carbonate, and (d) mixing the leaflets of calcium carbonate obtained in step (c) with the leaflets of calcium carbonate prepared in step (a).
   USE - The method is useful for producing paper-type leaflet.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enhances not only high retention effect without use of retention polymers, shows high whiteness and uniform combustion, contributes to reduction of greenhouse gases, improves quality of paper-making leaflets, reduces process energy, and prevents toxicity that occur during combustion.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for leaflet prepared by the above method.

PT P
PN CN111073754-A
TI Extracting sandalwood seed oil from Australian            sandalwood fruit by washing Australian sandalwood            fruit, crushing, adding wheat bran, subjecting to            ultrasonic pretreatment and extracting using hexane and            isopropanol as entraining agent.
AU LI H
   HUANG M X
   CHEN T
AE TIANJIN YUNSEE BIOTECHNOLOGY CO LTD (TIAN-Non-standard)
GA 202038908Y
AB    NOVELTY - Method for extracting sandalwood seed oil from                Australian sandalwood fruit involves (a) washing                Australian sandalwood fruit to remove impurities                and soil, drying under shade, controlling moisture                content to less than 10%, adding into vacuum drying                oven, spreading evenly, vacuum-drying at 55-65                degrees C and 10-200 Pa for 3-6 hours, and crushing                to 2-20 meshes using crusher, (b) adding wheat                bran, the weight ratio of Australian sandalwood                fruit to wheat bran is 10:1-1:3, and subjecting to                ultrasonic pretreatment at 30-50 degrees C for                10-20 minutes for 2-5 times, (c) adding the mixed                extraction raw material to supercritical extraction                kettle, and using carbon dioxide as a supercritical                fluid, and a mixed solution with a volume ratio of                hexane and isopropanol of 3:2 as an entraining                agent for extraction and separation, and (d)                extracting the mixture.
   USE - The method is useful for extracting sandalwood                seed oil from Australian sandalwood fruit.
   ADVANTAGE - The obtained oil and fat is clear and                transparent, and have better quality.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Method: The step (d) involves                  heating process and a boosting process, the                  heating process is performed by turning on power,                  turning on cooling water, activating refrigerant                  switch, opening extraction kettle and heating                  switch of separation kettle, and setting                  temperature of extraction kettle to 40-50 degrees                  C, preferably 45 degrees C and separation kettle                  to 30-50 degrees C, preferably 40 degrees C and                  the boosting process is performed by when                  temperature of freezer drops to 4-6 degrees C,                  when temperature of the extraction kettle and                  separation kettle reaches set temperature and is                  stable for 5-15 minutes, opening carbon dioxide                  gas cylinder and origin intake valve, exhausting                  air in extraction and separation system, when                  extraction pressure is consistent with storage                  tank pressure, starting carbon dioxide pump,                  gradually increasing pressure of extraction                  kettle to 20-40 MPa, setting separation kettle                  pressure to 5-15 MPa and setting extraction time                  to 1-8 hours. The method further involves when                  the extraction time is reached, collecting oil                  from separation kettle, detecting and analyzing                  by gas chromatography-mass spectrometry, and                  calculating extraction rate. The method involves                  separating carbon dioxide of sandalwood seed oil,                  recompressing from high-pressure pump to a                  supercritical state, and entering extraction                  tank. Amount of the entraining agent is 5-15%.                  The Australian sandalwood fruit and wheat bran                  are in weight ratio of 1:1. The method involves                  recovering wheat bran at end of supercritical                  extraction, separating, acidifying, and                  extracting protein after enzymatic                  hydrolysis.
DC D23 (Oils, fats and waxes - including fatty acids, essential oils, but excluding butter (substitutes) and montan wax (C11B, C).); D16 (Fermentation industry - including fermentation equipment, brewing, yeast production, production of pharmaceuticals and other chemicals by fermentation, microbiology, production of vaccines and antibodies, cell and tissue culture and genetic engineering.)

PT P
PN CN111060634-A
TI Dissolved organic carbon and dissolved total nitrogen isotope measurement involves taking quartz ultrafine fiber filter membrane into small pieces into headspace bottle, and pipette of filtered water into headspace vial with pipette.
AU XIAO H
   XIE P
   LUO L
AE UNIV EAST CHINA TECHNOLOGY (UDHL-C)
GA 202039322N
AB    NOVELTY - Dissolved organic carbon and dissolved total nitrogen isotope measurement involves taking 25 mm diameter quartz ultrafine fiber filter membrane into small pieces into a headspace bottle with a volume of 10 ml, and a 10 ml pipette of filtered water into the headspace vial with a pipette. 10 mu l hydrochloric acid is added into headspace bottle for 30 minutes to remove carbonate and carbon dioxide in the water sample. The headspace of the headspace bottle is sealed with a parafilm in a -20 degrees C refrigerator for 48 hours. The headspace bottle is removed from freeze dryer to freeze-dry to an anhydrous state. The obtained quartz ultrafine fiber filter is put in a tin cup to determine water-soluble organic carbon and water-soluble total nitrogen in the water sample.
   USE - Method for measuring dissolved organic carbon and dissolved total nitrogen isotope in water.
   ADVANTAGE - The method measures the dissolved organic carbon and dissolved total nitrogen isotope in water in a simple, cost-effective and eco-friendly manner.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The quartz ultrafine fiber filter membrane is torn apart layer by layer, and then placed in fluffy state. The sample or the working standard solution or the international standard solution can be directly freeze-dried after being taken out to prevent sample thawing. The working standard solution includes 180-220 mu g urea, 160-20 mu g sorghum flour, and 860-940 mu g glutamic acid, which can be prepared by dissolving the standard materials in 5 ml deionized water. The isotope standard substance solution includes 380-420 mu g ammonium sulfate (IAEA-N-1), 360-400 mu g nitrogen isotopes in ammonium sulfate (IAEA-N-2), 380-420 mu g nitrogen isotopes in ammonium sulfate (USGS 25), and 860-940 mu g carbon and nitrogen isotopes in l-glutamic acid (USGS 41A), which can be prepared by dissolving the standard materials in 5 ml deionized water.

AU MIAO M
   QIAO J
   WU X
AE UNIV HENAN CHINESE MEDICINE (HECO-C)
GA 2020209778
AB    NOVELTY - Topical essential oil comprises 4-6 mL trumpet creeper essential oil, 4-6 mL peach essential oil, 3-5 mL safflower oil, 3-5 mL ginseng essential oil and 3-5 mL cortex dictamni essential oil. The preparation involves mixing the components uniformly. The trumpet creeper essential oil is obtained by crushing trumpet creeper into a 10-20 mesh sieve coarse powder, adding solvent in 1 g:10-20 mL ratio, followed by supercritical carbon dioxide fluid extraction at 42-50 degrees C temperature and 20-25 MPa extraction pressure for 1.5-2.5 hours, and separation at 50-60 degrees C temperature under 4.5-5.5 MPa pressure to obtain the product. The preparation method of other essential oils is same as process for trumpet creeper essential oil.
   USE - Topical essential oil for moisturizing, and preventing skin dryness (claimed).

PT P
PN US2020071168-A1
TI Preparing mesoporous nitrogen-doped carbon, comprises forming composition by solubilizing nitrogen-containing polymer in aqueous solution of zinc chloride, drying solution, and heating composition after drying to carbonize polymer.
AU MATYJASZEWSKI K
   ZHANG J
   BOCKSTALLER M R
AE UNIV CARNEGIE MELLON (UYCM-C)
GA 202018962L
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing mesoporous nitrogen-doped carbon, comprises: forming a composition by solubilizing a nitrogen-containing polymer in an aqueous solution of zinc chloride; and drying the aqueous solution, where the method further comprises heating the composition after drying to a temperature to carbonize the nitrogen-containing polymer to form mesoporous nitrogen-doped carbon.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing mesoporous nitrogen-doped carbon.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: utilizes the zinc chloride as a dual role of a solubility enhancer and porogen and enhances the surface area as compared to conventional templated systems; and tunes pore size distributions in nanoporous carbons (NPCs) under facile and benign conditions that should promote the application of NPCs of metal-air batteries, fuel cells and carbon dioxide capture.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for a mesoporous nitrogen-doped carbon prepared by forming a composition by solubilizing a nitrogen-containing polymer in an aqueous solution of zinc chloride, drying the aqueous solution, and heating the composition after drying the aqueous solution to carbonize the nitrogen-containing polymer to form the mesoporous nitrogen-doped carbon.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The method further comprises dispersing many porogenic fillers in the aqueous solution of the composition prior to drying the composition. The composition is cast into a desired form before heating. Drying comprises freeze-drying. The composition is stabilized by heating at a temperature below 300 degrees C after drying and before heating the composition to carbonize the nitrogen-containing polymer, where the temperature to carbonize the nitrogen-containing polymer is less than 850 degrees C. Preferred Components: The porogenic fillers comprise at least one of silica particles, cellulose-based nanocrystals or filter paper. The mesoporous nitrogen-doped carbon comprises interconnected pores. The mesoporous nitrogen-doped carbon has a surface area of greater than or equal to 750 m2/g, preferably 1000 m2/g. The surface area arising from mesopores is at least 84%. The mesoporous nitrogen-doped carbon comprise both graphitic and disordered carbons. The graphitic carbons comprise catalytically active edge on pyridine oxide-N, pyrrolic- or pyridonic-N, and pyridinic-N (N-P) nitrogens.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The nitrogen-containing polymer is polyacrylonitrile (PAN). The degree of polymerization of the PAN is less than or equal to 100, preferably less than or equal to 50. The dispersity of the PAN is less than 2, preferably less than 1.3. The ratio of fillers to PAN is selected to provide sufficient PAN to fill the majority of the interstitial volume between the fillers, thus forming a coherent structure.
   EXAMPLE - Aqueous suspension of colloidal silica was dialyzed against water to 0 wt.%. Different ratios of polyacrylonitrile (PAN) and zinc chloride were added to 10 ml aqueous colloidal silica and the concentration of zinc chloride in the aqueous solution was adjusted to 60 wt.% to ensure complete solubility of the PAN. Mixture was stirred for 1 hour at room temperature until a transparent solution was formed. Silica/PAN solution was subjected to freeze-drying to yield solid silica/PAN composite. Composite was then stabilized under air at 280 degrees C followed by carbonization at 800 degrees C for 30 minutes under nitrogen to yield a silica-carbon composite. Obtained carbon samples were then stirred in 50 wt.% aqueous hydrogen fluoride solution for 12 hours to remove the silica template. After the addition of silica to the PAN/zinc chloride solution. When the silica/PAN ratio was high, 4.7, incomplete encapsulation and/or aggregation of silica nanoporous (NPs) occurred during the freeze-drying and carbonization processes and silica NPs of the resulting nanoporous carbon after the etching process. Further decrease of the silica/PAN ratio part of silica NPs were encapsulated by PAN chains to obtain nitrogen-doped carbon.
