PN CN110433863-A; CN110433863-B
TI Preparing catalyst used to prepare dimethyl            carbonate, by obtaining salen ligand from            3-tert-butyl-5-(4-pyridyl)salicylaldehyde, dripping            metal methanol solution to ligand to form complex and            mixing silica or alumina solvent with complex.
AU LU J
   LIU C
   LIU S
AE SHANDONG DEPU CHEM TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (SHAN-Non-standard)
   SHANDONG DEPU NEW MATERIAL SCI & TECHNOLOGY              CO LTD (SHAN-Non-standard)
GA 201996924R
AB    NOVELTY - Method for preparing a catalyst involves (a)                synthesizing a salen ligand starting from                3-tert-butyl-5-(4-pyridyl)salicylaldehyde and                ethylenediamine, (b) dissolved the Salen ligand in                anhydrous methanol, dripping a methanol solution of                a transition metal salt, stirring the mixture for                1-24 hours, heating under refluxing to obtain a                metal salen complex (MSalen) as reaction product,                (c) uniformly dispersing nano-silica or alumina                particles in a solvent, adding a silane coupling                agent, heating and refluxing under an inert gas as                protecting gas and evaporating to obtain silica or                alumina dry particles and (d) uniformly dispersing                the silica or alumina dry particles in a solvent,                adding the metal salen complex, heating under                refluxing in a presence of an inert shielding gas                and evaporating to obtain a nano-silica or alumina                particles loaded metal salen catalyst.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing a catalyst,                which is useful for synthesizing dimethyl                carbonate.
   ADVANTAGE - The catalyst promotes adsorption activity of                methanol, and increases reactivity of carbon                dioxide and methanol.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The step (a) involves                  dissolving 3-tert-butyl-5-(4-pyridyl)                  salicylaldehyde in absolute ethanol, dissolving                  ethylenediamine in absolute ethanol, mixing the                  3-tert-butyl-5-(4-pyridyl) salicylaldehyde                  ethanol solution in ethylenediamine ethanol                  solution, stirring, heating at 60-90 degrees C                  for 1-24 hours, stopping the reaction, cooling,                  filtering and recrystallizing filtered product                  from absolute ethanol. Preferred Composition: The                  volume ratio of 3-tert-butyl-5-(4-pyridyl)                  salicylaldehyde to absolute ethanol is 1:(4-50).                  The volume ratio of ethylenediamine to absolute                  ethanol is 1:(50-500). The molar ratio of the                  salen ligand to the transition metal salt is                  1:(1-3). The mass ratio of the silica or alumina                  to the silane coupling agent is 10:(1-100). The                  mass ratio of the metal salen complex to the dry                  particles of silica or alumina is 1:(5-50).                  Preferred Components: The transition metal salt                  comprises an acetate salt of transition metals                  comprising nickel, cobalt, copper, zirconium,                  molybdenum and vanadium. The silane coupling                  agent comprises an amino group-containing silane                  coupling agent. The solvent comprises anhydrous                  ethanol, anhydrous methanol and acetone.
DC E13 (Heterocyclics.); J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).)

AB    NOVELTY - The system has a coupled plasma reforming module (100) which includes a first reactor that is a dielectric discharge tube and an antenna that is an induction coil arranged to surround the first reactor. PN IN201841013978-A
TI Oxygen blow incinerator for incinerating sanitary napkins in e.g. hospital, has main structure provided with waste collection box, ash discharge door, air blower and waste inlet door, where box is formed with pyramidal configuration.
AU SUNDER R S
AE SUNDER R S (SUND-Individual)
GA 201990934R
AB    NOVELTY - The incinerator has a main structure provided with a waste collection box (3), an ash discharge door (4), a square chimney (1), an air blower (5) and a waste inlet door (2). The collection box is formed with a pyramidal configuration at uppers side and the chimney is connected to a top portion of the pyramidal structure, where angle of the pyramid is 45 degree such that maximum circulation is possible. The air blower lights up waste, where complete burning results lower emission without requiring external fuel.
   USE - Oxygen blow incinerator for incinerating sanitary napkins. Uses include but are not limited to hostel, hospital, house, school and college.
   ADVANTAGE - The incinerator allows the napkin waste to be burnt completely with less emission of carbon dioxide and toxic gases, realizes convenient management, and consumes less electricity.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a perspective view of an oxygen blow incinerator.
   Square chimney (1)
   Waste inlet door (2)
   Waste collection box (3)
   Ash discharge door (4)
   Air blower (5)
DC F07 (Other textile applications - include mechanical treatment of fabrics (D06C, F, G, H, J, L, M).); D22 (Sterilising, bandages, dressing and skin protection agents - including sterilising agents (other than for food), sutures, plaster casts, bioactive prostheses, contact lenses, diapers, animal litter, timber, preservatives, disinfectants, bactericidal detergents, deodorants, insect repellent compounds, moth proofers, sheep dip (A61L).); Q73 (Combustion equipment/processes (F23))

PT P
PN US2019309473-A1; US10787766-B2
TI Fractionation of lignocellulosic feedstock and production method of cellulose product by contacting lignocellulosic feedstock with fluid to obtain reaction slurry, recovering cellulose product, and contacting with caustic solution.
AU SUTTERLIN W R
   BLANCHARD C O
   CHECK C
AE INVENTURE RENEWABLES INC (INVE-Non-standard)
GA 201985346Q
AB    NOVELTY - Fractionation of lignocellulosic feedstock and production method of cellulose product comprises contacting lignocellulosic feedstock with fluid comprising water, carbon dioxide, and optionally compressed gas to obtain reaction slurry having temperature of 140 degrees C or 120-160 degrees C and pressure of 200 psi or 150-250 psi, recovering cellulose product, and washing and sieving pulp to separate and remove residual impurities; and contacting cellulose product with caustic (or alkaline) solution having pH of 10-14 at 120 degrees C for 1 minute to 1 hour, and increasing purity of cellulose product by removing portion of lignin and residual hemicellulose. The reaction slurry comprises solid cellulose product, and solubilized hemicellulose product.
   USE - The method is for fractionation of lignocellulosic feedstock and production of cellulose product.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is capable of handling range of lignocellulosic feedstocks, including agricultural and forest wastes, uses reduced amounts of water, produces pulps that consumes minimal amount of chemicals, produces little to no environmental contaminants, does not consume large quantities of toxic or harsh chemicals, and allows simple isolation of by-products, such as hemicellulose and lignin.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The method further comprises contacting solubilized hemicellulose product with acidic solution having pH of 1-4, hydrolyzing large portion of hemicellulose to hemicellulose monomers, and/or concentrating hydrolyzed hemicellulose through removal of water and other volatile impurities. The method further comprises recovering solid cellulose product from reaction slurry. The method further comprises utilizing hemicellulose hydrolysate product in portion as reaction medium for extraction of hemicellulose from fresh lignocellulosic feedstock. Preferred Components: The lignocellulosic feedstock comprises wood, optionally hardwood or softwood, cotton fiber, grass, straw, cane, agricultural residue, optionally corn cob, corn husk or sugar cane bagasse, residue generated in oil palm production process, optionally palm mesocarp fiber, empty fruit bunch, palm frond or palm trunk, rice husk and/or rice bran.
   ACTIVITY - None given.
   MECHANISM OF ACTION - None given.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example give.
DC F09 (Paper-making production of cellulose, chemical treatment of wood - including chipboard and fibre-board (D21).)
MC F05-A02A; F05-A02C

PN KR2019111646-A
TI Bio plastic pellets useful e.g. for extruded product, preferably wrapping paper and disposable products, comprise biodegradation catalyst, ginseng byproduct, polymer resin, oxidation promoter, plasticizer and compatibilizer.
AU MIN W K
   YOU Y
AE MIN W K (MINW-Individual)
   YOU Y (YOUY-Individual)
GA 2019862709
AB    NOVELTY - Bio plastic pellets comprise biodegradation catalyst including acetyl carbamate metal salts, unsaturated fatty acid, sodium bicarbonate and stearic acid, ginseng byproduct, polymer resin, oxidation promoter, plasticizer and compatibilizer.
   USE - The bio plastic pellets are useful for extruded product (claimed), preferably biofilaments for three dimensional printers, wrapping paper and disposable products.
   ADVANTAGE - The bio plastic pellet can lead to reduction of carbon dioxide on planet and reduction of limited petroleum resources.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for producing bio plastic pellets, comprising (a) adding poly lactic acid, ferric acetyl carbamate, aluminum acetyl carbamate, low-density polyethylene, linoleic acid, calcium carbonate, sodium bicarbonate and stearic acid into a mixer, mixing to dryness at high temperature, and extrusion molding to produce a pellet-shaped biodegradation catalyst, (b) hot air drying ginseng by-products until moisture content of less than or equal to 15% and then grinding to obtain powder, (c) adding biodegradation catalyst, ginseng by-product, linear low density polyethylene, behenic acid, triacetin and ethylene vinyl alcohol into a mixture, mixing and drying, and (d) preparing pellet using an extruder.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The acetyl carbamate metal salts include ferric acetyl carbamate, aluminum acetyl carbamate and/or manganese acetyl carbamate.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The compatibilizer includes glycidyl methacrylate, ethylene vinyl alcohol, polyvinyl alcohol and/or ethylene vinyl acetate.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Composition: The bio plastic pellets comprise 10-30 pts. wt. biodegradation catalyst, 5-20 pts. wt. ginseng byproduct, 50-70 pts. wt. polymer resin, 1-5 pts. wt. oxidation promoter, 1-5 pts. wt. plasticizer and 5-10 pts. wt. compatibilizer. Preferred Components: The polymer includes polyethylene vinyl acetate, polypropylene, polyacrylonitrile butadiene styrene, polyethylene, polyacetylene, polystyrene, polyurethane, polyamide, polyethylene terephthalate and/or polybutylene terephthalate. The biodegradation catalyst comprises 20-50 pts. wt. biomass, 0.1-2 pts. wt. acetyl carbamate metal salt, 30-50 pts. wt. binder resin, 1-5 pts. wt. unsaturated fatty acid, 10-50 pts. wt. calcium carbonate, 1-5 pts. wt. sodium bicarbonate and 1-5 pts. wt. stearic acid, preferably 30 pts. wt. poly lactic acid, 0.1 pts. wt. ferric acetyl carbamate, 0.3 pts. wt. aluminum acetyl carbamate, 40 pts. wt. low-density polyethylene, 5 pts. wt. linoleic acid, 20 pts. wt. calcium carbonate, 2 pts. wt. sodium bicarbonate and 2.6 pts. wt. stearic acid. The polymer resin is linear low-density polyethylene. The oxidation promoter is behenic acid. The plasticizer is triacetin. The compatibilizer is ethylene vinyl alcohol.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.
DC A32 (Polymer fabrication - such as moulding, extrusion, forming, laminating, spinning.); A18 (Addition polymers in general.); A23 (Polyamides; polyesters. (including polycarbonates, polyesteramides); alkyds; other unsaturated polymers.); A25 (Polyurethanes; polyethers.); A89 (Photographic, laboratory equipment, optical - including electrophotographic, thermographic uses.); A92 (Packaging and containers - including ropes and nets.)
MC A08-C03; A08-C09; A08-D02; A08-D05; A08-M02; A08-M10; A08-M10A; A08-P04; A08-R; A10-E09A; A11-A03; A11-B01; A11-B07; A12-W07

   LI X (LIXX-Individual)
GA 2019852442
AB    NOVELTY - The device has an environment parameter adjusting device and environment parameter detection device electrically connected with an embedded control system through a cable. The embedded control system is connected with an outdoor operation room through an internet monitoring chamber computer. A sterile washing pool comprises a sterilizing tank (21) and a cleaning pool (22). The cleaning tank comprises a tank body (221) and a water tap (224). The embedded control system is provided with an AVR single-chip microcomputer and an upper computer. The environment parameter detection device comprises a carbon dioxide concentration sensor, a wind speed sensor, a particulate matter concentration sensor, a temperature sensor and a humidity sensor.
   USE - Sterile operating room environment parameter control device.
   ADVANTAGE - The can detect multiple parameters at same time, and effectively adjust some of the parameters in an accurate and timely manner, and avoids manual operation and improves working efficiency of a medical staff.

PT P
PN CN110280224-A
TI Preparation of functionalized graphene-based aerogel used for adsorption of heavy metal ions, involves adding chitosan to graphene oxide dispersion, hydrothermally reacting to obtain wet gel, and soaking in solvent exchange solution.
AU ZHU J
   XIE Z
   REN H
   BI Y
AE UNIV SOUTHWEST SCI & TECHNOLOGY (USWS-C)
GA 201984192N
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of functionalized graphene-based aerogel involves adding chitosan to 1-3 mg/mL graphene oxide dispersion, adjusting pH 6-7, stirring to obtain mixed solution, adding mixed solution to high-pressure reaction kettle for hydrothermal reaction at 165-185 degrees C for 10-15 hours to obtain a wet gel, soaking wet gel 3 times in solvent exchange solution with alcohol and acetone for 24 hours each time, and supercritically drying with carbon dioxide.
   USE - Preparation of functionalized graphene-based aerogel used for adsorption of heavy metal ions e.g. divalent palladium, divalent copper, divalent cadmium, and divalent nickel (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method is simple, suitable for industrialization, carried out using inexpensive and readily available chitosan and graphene oxide as raw material. The graphene-based aerogel enhances the adsorption of heavy metal ions divalent palladium and divalent copper due to attachment of chitosan to the graphene oxide sheet to expose amino group and hydroxyl group to the surface of the aerogel to the greatest extent.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The mass ratio of graphene to graphene oxide in graphene oxide dispersion is 1-4:1. The acid used for adjusting the pH is hydrochloric acid, sulfuric acid, nitric acid, acetic acid or carbonic acid. The concentration of the acid is 0.5-1.5 mol/L. Preferred Process: The preparation method preferably involves pretreating mixed solution by placing in a sealed glass jar to carry out ultrasonically processing at 60-100 kHz, irradiating with electron beam irradiation apparatus. The irradiation voltage range is 5-25 MeV. The irradiation number range is 5-15 mA. The irradiation dose range is 200kGy-800 kGy. The graphene oxide dispersion is a carboxylated graphene oxide dispersion. The carboxylated graphene oxide dispersion is prepared by adding chloroacetic acid and ethylenediamine to the graphene oxide dispersion, and ultrasonically processing, passing sodium hydroxide atomized product through carrier gas and passing into a graphene oxide dispersion to carry out reaction to obtain mixture, washing with deionized water until the pH of the effluent is 7.0, filtering, and drying. The sodium hydroxide atomizer is prepared by adding 30-50 g/L sodium hydroxide solution to ultrasonic atomizer, ultrasonically atomizing sodium hydroxide solution at 5-15 kW and 2-3 MHz for 20-40 kg/hour. The carrier gas used in sodium hydroxide atomizer is ammonia gas. The flow rate of carrier gas is 1-3 L/minute. The mass ratio of graphene oxide in chloroacetic acid and graphene oxide dispersion is 1:30-60. The mass ratio of chloroacetic acid to ethylenediamine is 1:0.4-0.6. The mass ratio of chloroacetic acid to sodium hydroxide in sodium hydroxide atomizer is 1:0.6-0.8.
DC D15 (Chemical or biological treatment of water, industrial waste and sewage - including purification, sterilising or testing water, scale prevention, treatment of sewage sludge, regeneration of active carbon which has been used for water treatment and impregnating water with gas e.g. CO2, but excluding plant and anti-pollution devices (C02).); E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).); A97 (Miscellaneous goods not specified elsewhere - including papermaking, gramophone records, detergents, food and oil well applications.)
MC D04-A01F1; D04-A01F3; D04-A01P1; D04-B05A; E05-U06; E10-B01E; E10-C04F; E10-C04J2U; E11-Q02C; E31-B03D; E31-F05; E31-H05; E33-A03; E35-A; E35-D; E35-W; E35-X; J04-A03; A10-E09; A12-W11D; A12-W11F; A12-W11J

PN CN209433270-U
TI Mushroom quality preserving impairment cabinet body intelligent cloud control system, has ventilation controller connected with carbon dioxide sensor, electric door, fan and reader, and identification device provided with sound box that is fixed with sensor.
AU JI L
   LI S
   SUN Z
   MA W
AE HENAN LONGFENG EDIBLE FUNGUS IND RES INS (HENA-Non-standard)
   HENAN LONGFENG IND CO LTD (HENA-Non-standard)
GA 201982880X

PN CN110124488-A
TI Method for treating volatile organic compounds and exhaust gas reuse in biochemical treatment, involves processing remaining exhaust gas into pressure swing adsorption treatment and recycling obtained nitrogen and reusing.
AU LI H
   WANG Q
   SHENG Y
   LI Y
   WANG J
AE BEIJING SECCO KANGLUN ENVIRONMENTAL PROT (BEIJ-Non-standard)
GA 201972832J
AB    NOVELTY - Method for treating volatile organic compounds and exhaust gas involves collecting the tail gas generated by the biochemical treatment by the gas collecting hood and then entering the volatile organic compounds processing unit. The exhaust gas treated by the volatile organic compounds enters the absorption tank to obtain a sodium carbonate solution. The absorption tank processes the remaining exhaust gas into the pressure swing adsorption treatment. The obtained nitrogen is recycled and used, and the oxygen is treated by the ozone generator and returned to the ozone catalytic oxidation treatment.
   USE - Method for treating volatile organic compounds and exhaust gas reuse in biochemical treatment.
   ADVANTAGE - The method collects exhaust gas in the biochemical treatment tank, effectively processes volatile organic compounds and reduces air pollution. The tail gas in the biochemical treatment tank is collected, and nitrogen, oxygen and carbon dioxide in the tail gas are separated and used for gas floating and oil removal, ozone catalytic oxidation and chemical softening, and the tail gas is fully utilized and reused to reduce operating costs.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The pH of the wastewater in the anaerobic tank is 7-7.5, and the hydraulic retention time is 1-5 hours. The pH of the anoxic pond wastewater is 6-8.5, and the hydraulic retention time is 2-6 hours. The pH of the aerobic pond wastewater is 6-8.5, and the hydraulic retention time is 10-25 hours. Preferred Composition: The chemical oxygen demand removal rate of the wastewater after biochemical treatment reaches 80-95%. Preferred Process: The exhaust gas after the volatile organic compounds treatment is subjected to absorption tank treatment. The alkali solution is added to the absorption tank for absorbing carbon dioxide in the exhaust gas. The lye is preferably a dilute solution of sodium hydroxide. The obtained sodium carbonate solution has a concentration of greater than or equal to 10% and is reused for chemical softening.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The removal rate of volatile organic compounds is greater than or equal to 50%.

PN CN110057757-A; CN110057757-B
TI Method for constructing hemoglobin derivative identifying network, involves performing characteristics sequence processing operation, and constructing hemoglobin derivative identifying network by recognition model according to samples.
AU CHEN Z
   XIE Y
   WANG L
AE EDAN INSTR INC (EDAN-C)
GA 201966901K
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves obtaining multiple training samples with different hemoglobin derivative concentrations. Multiple characteristic sequences are obtained corresponding to the training samples. Dimension reduction processing operation of the characteristic sequence is performed according to the hemoglobin derivative concentrations. Characteristics sequence processing operation is performed after processing the dimension reduction. A hemoglobin derivative identifying network is constructed by the training recognition model according to the training samples. A mixture of different components is stored into a blood sample to obtain the training samples and contained with oxygen, carbon monoxide, carbon dioxide, nitrogen and iron cyanide. The hemoglobin derivative concentrations corresponding to the training samples are recorded. Absorption spectrum analysis of the training samples is performed to obtain the characteristic sequence.
   USE - Method for constructing a hemoglobin derivative identifying network.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables simplifying hemoglobin derivative identifying process.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for the following:
   (1) a method for identifying hemoglobin derivative
   (2) a device for constructing a hemoglobin derivative identifying network
   (3) a device for identifying hemoglobin derivative.

AB    NOVELTY - A high-purity barium carbonate is prepared by crushing barite into particles with particle diameter of less than 50 mu m, alkali washing, acid washing and water washing, drying to obtain barite powder, adding barite powder into thermal reduction reactor, introducing nitrogen to displace air in reactor, continuously filling nitrogen, heating to 1000-2000 degrees C, filling mixed gas of sulfur vapor and nitrogen into reactor, reacting, cooling to room temperature to obtain coarse barium sulfide and collecting gas containing large amount of sulfur dioxide and small amount of sulfur vapor, dissolving barium sulfide in deionized water, filtering to get barium sulfide clear liquid, stirring at 0-100 degrees C at speed of 0-1000 revolutions/minute, introducing carbon dioxide gas or slowly adding sodium carbonate solution in barium sulfide clear liquid to precipitation reaction, solid-liquid separating, washing solid, and drying.
   USE - The method is used for high-purity barium carbonate preparation.
   ADVANTAGE - The method has high raw material conversion rate and meets green production idea. The product has purity of greater than 99.5%.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The alkali is sodium hydroxide solution or potassium hydroxide solution. The acid is hydrochloric acid solution. The molar ratio of barite powder and sulfur vapor is 1:2-10. The sulfur vapor in mixed gas accounts for 5-95% of total volume of mixed gas. The mass ratio of coarse barium sulfide and deionized water is 5-500:1000. The molar concentration of sodium carbonate solution is 0.05-0.5 mol/L. Preferred Method: The mixed gas has flow rate of is 0.1-2.5 L/minute and total reaction time of 0.5-2 hours. The gas flow rate of carbon dioxide is 1-10 L/hour.

PT P
PN CN109908967-A; CN109908967-B
TI Composite carrier comprises activated carbon and a            basic carbonate, and the basic carbonate is basic            magnesium carbonate or a mixture of basic calcium            carbonate and basic magnesium carbonate.
AU ZHAO Y
   JIANG G
   LI B
   MA C
   WANG X
AE CHINA PETROLEUM & CHEM CORP (SNPC-C)
   SINOPEC DALIAN PETROLEUM & PETROCHEM RES (SNPC-C)
   SINOPEC DALIAN RES PETROLEUM & PETROCHEM (SNPC-C)
   CHINA PETROLEUM & CHEM CORP (SNPC-C)
GA 201957735R
AB    NOVELTY - Composite carrier comprises 35-90 wt.%,                preferably 40-80 wt.%, activated carbon and 10-65                wt.%, preferably 20-60 wt.% a basic carbonate. The                basic carbonate is basic magnesium carbonate or a                mixture of basic calcium carbonate and basic                magnesium carbonate.
   USE - Used as composite carrier.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is simple, good stability, and can                alleviate the problem of metal loss.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for                preparing the composite carrier, comprising (i)                mixing the activated carbon with the soluble                organic salt solution, and uniformly mixing to                obtain the material A, the soluble organic salt                solution being a soluble organic magnesium salt                solution or soluble organic a calcium salt solution                and a soluble organic magnesium salt solution, (ii)                introducing a carbonate solution or an alkaline                solution into the material A, mixing uniformly and                allowing to stand to obtain the material B, (iii)                subjecting the material B to solid-liquid                separation, drying separated solid phase and                calcining to obtain a material C, and (iv) mixing                the material C with water, reacting with carbon                dioxide gas, cooling, subjecting to solid-liquid                separation, drying the separated solid phase and                calcining.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The activated carbon                  is a powdery activated carbon, the particle size                  is 150-300 mesh, the specific surface area is                  500-3000 m2/g, the pore volume is 0.5-1.8 cm3/g,                  the average pore diameter is 0.5-4 nm, and the                  pore diameter is 1-2 nm. The pore volume accounts                  for more than 90% of the total pore volume. The                  properties of the composite carrier are as                  follows: a specific surface area of 150-1500                  m2/g, a pore volume of 0.1-1.2 cm3/g, and an                  average pore diameter of 1-8 nm. The composite                  carrier has two-stage pores, the pore diameter of                  the first-stage pores is 0.5-2 nm, the pore                  diameter of the second-stage pores is 2-50 nm,                  and the pore volume of the pores having a pore                  diameter of 0.5-2 nm 85% or less of the total                  pore volume, preferably 70-80%, and pores having                  a pore diameter of 2-50 nm account for 15% or                  more of the total pore volume, preferably 20-30%.                  In the step (i), the activated carbon is a                  powdery activated carbon having a particle size                  of 150-300 mesh, a specific surface area of                  500-3000 m2/g, a pore volume of 0.5-1.8 cm3/g, an                  average pore diameter of 0.5-4 nm, and a pore                  diameter of 1-2 nm, the pore volume of the pores                  accounts for more than 90% of the total pore                  volume. In the step (i), the soluble organic                  calcium salt is calcium gluconate, calcium                  acetate, calcium lactate, calcium amino acid,                  calcium L-aspartate and calcium L-threonate,                  preferably, calcium gluconate or calcium lactate.                  In the step (i), the soluble organic magnesium                  salt is magnesium gluconate, magnesium acetate,                  magnesium lactate, magnesium amino acid,                  magnesium L-aspartate and magnesium L-threonate,                  preferably, magnesium gluconate or magnesium                  lactate. In the step (i), the activated carbon                  and the soluble organic salt are mixed at a C:                  magnesium ion molar ratio of 4.3 to 84.7:1,                  preferably a C:M2+ molar ratio is 10-60:1, where                  M represents magnesium or calcium. In the step                  (ii), the carbonate is ammonium carbonate,                  potassium carbonate, sodium carbonate, preferably                  ammonium carbonate. In the step (ii), the                  carbonate solution with the concentration of                  0.1-1 mol/l. In the step (ii), the amount of the                  carbonate is a CO32-:M2+ molar ratio of 1 to                  1.2:1, preferably a CO32-:M2+ molar ratio of 1:1.                  In the step (ii), the alkaline solution is an                  inorganic alkaline solution. In the step (ii),                  alkaline solution is ammonia water, sodium                  hydroxide or potassium hydroxide. In the step (i)                  and (ii), the alkaline solution is introduced                  into the material, and the pH is adjusted to 8-9.                  In the step (ii), the alkaline solution is                  OH-:M2+ molar ratio of 2 to 4:1, preferably                  OH-:M2+ molar ratio is 2 to 2.5:1, where M                  represents magnesium or calcium and magnesium.                  Preferred Method: In the step (iii), the drying                  temperature is 70-110 degrees C, preferably                  80-100 degrees C, and the drying time is 2-6                  hours, preferably 3-4 hours. In the step (iii),                  the calcination carried out under nitrogen or an                  inert atmosphere, and the inert atmosphere is                  argon gas and helium gas. In the step (iii), the                  calcination temperature is 500-1200 degrees C,                  preferably 600-900 degrees C, and the calcination                  time is 2-8 hours, preferably 3-5 hours. In the                  step (iv), the material C is mixed with water at                  60-100 degrees C. In the step (iv), the partial                  pressure of carbon dioxide is 0.08-1 MPa. In the                  step (iv), the pH of the reaction end point is                  7-10, preferably 8-9. In the step (iv), the                  reaction temperature is 15-150 degrees C. In the                  step (iv), the drying temperature is 40-100                  degrees C, preferably 50-60 degrees C, the drying                  time is 3-24 hours, preferably 6-8 hours. In the                  step (iv), the baking in nitrogen or an inert                  atmosphere, the inert atmosphere is argon gas,                  and helium gas. In the step (iv), the baking                  temperature is 80-220 degrees C, preferably                  150-200 degrees C, and the baking time is 2-12                  hours, preferably 3-8 hours.

PN CN109897209-A; CN109897209-B
TI Epoxy resin microporous material preparation involves dissolving epoxy resin and curing agent in methylene chloride with stirring uniformly, and then removing solvent to obtain an epoxy resin or curing agent mixed powder.
AU ZHANG G
   SHANG Z
   FAN X
   GAO Q
   SHI X
   QIN J
AE UNIV NORTHWESTERN POLYTECHNICAL (UNWP-C)
   UNIV NORTHWESTERN POLYTECHNICAL (UNWP-C)
GA 201956295X
AB    NOVELTY - Epoxy resin microporous material preparation involves dissolving epoxy resin and curing agent in methylene chloride with stirring uniformly, removing solvent to obtain an epoxy resin or curing agent mixed powder, and then pressing the mixed powder in a hot press at a high temperature to obtain an epoxy resin sheet. The obtained epoxy resin sheet is placed in an autoclave under supercritical carbon dioxide introduction for saturation adsorption at 35-65 degrees C for 12-72 hours. The CO2 saturated epoxy resin sheet is received after relieving the autoclave, and then placed in a constant temperature oil bath for foaming and setting with cold water.
   USE - Method for preparing epoxy resin microporous material.
   ADVANTAGE - The method prepares the epoxy resin microporous material in a simple, cost-effective and eco-friendly manner with good bimodal distribution, good practicability, an average cell diameter of 1.21 1.40 mu m, and average diameter of 13.1 29.7 mu m.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a graphical representation depicting gel fraction-curing agent dosage of the prepared epoxy resin sheet. (Drawing includes non-English language text).
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Conditions: The epoxy resin is a bisphenol A type epoxy resin having a molecular weight of 1000-2000 g/mol. The curing agent is ethylenediamine, hexamethylenediamine or 4,4-diaminodiphenylmethane.

AB    NOVELTY - A nickel-cobalt-manganese ternary precursor comprises beta -type nickel-cobalt-manganese hydroxide core having compact structure of fine particles, and alpha -type nickel-cobalt-manganese hydroxide having porous structure of thin sheets.
   USE - Nickel-cobalt-manganese ternary precursor.
   ADVANTAGE - The nickel-cobalt-manganese ternary precursor has improved physical properties, and high tap density.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A nickel-cobalt-manganese ternary precursor comprises beta -type nickel-cobalt-manganese hydroxide core having compact structure of fine particles, and alpha -type nickel-cobalt-manganese hydroxide having porous structure of thin sheets. The general formula of core is Ni2+1-x-yCo2+xMn2+y(OH)2, and shell is (Ni2+1-x-yCo3+xMn3+y(OH)2)CO32-(x+y)/2, where 0.17 less than or equal to (x+y) less than or equal to 0.33. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for preparation of nickel-cobalt-manganese ternary precursor involves using mixed solution of nickel, cobalt, and manganese, precipitant solution, complexing agent solution, dilute acid, and mixed gas of carbon dioxide, and oxygen.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Properties: The fine particles has average particle diameter of 5-18 mu m, tap density of 1.9-2.5 g/cm3, and specific surface area of 8-30 m2 /g. The thickness of shell is 2-7 mu m. Preferred Components: The nickel salt, cobalt salt, and manganese salt in the mixed solution of nickel-cobalt-manganese is atleast one of sulfate, nitrate, and chloride. The complexing agent is at least one of aqueous ammonia , ammonium sulfate, ammonium nitrate, and ammonium chloride. The precipitant in the precipitant solution is atleast one of sodium hydroxide, and potassium hydroxide. The dilute acid is atleast one of dilute sulfuric acid, dilute hydrochloric acid, and dilute nitric acid. Preferred Composition: The molar ratio of Ni2+ :( Co2+ + Mn2+) in the nickel-cobalt-manganese mixed solution is (2.03-4.88):1. The ammonia content in the reaction liquid is 1-2 mol/L. The concentration of the precipitant solution is 3-10 mol/L. The volume concentration of carbon dioxide in the mixed gas of carbon dioxide, and oxygen is 10-20%. The molar ratio of carbon dioxide to the sum of cobalt, and manganese ions is (0.5-1):1.

A primary catalyst for catalytically reacting Carbon monoxide (CO) and nitrogen oxide (NO) in the flue gas to produce N2 and carbon dioxide (CO2) in the upstream of the two-stage catalytic chamber, an air duct for incorporation of air in the middle of the middle, and a CO for remaining in the flue gas are provided downstream catalytic combustion secondary catalyst.
   USE - Radiant tube discharge system for reducing nitrogen oxide emissions.

   Radiant tube (1)
   Heat exchanger (2)
   Two-stage catalytic chamber (3)
   Stage catalyst (4)
   Air pipeline (5)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The air flow valve and a gas flow valve are respectively disposed on the air supply pipe and the gas supply pipe connected to the radiant tube low NOx burner. A control device and a detection mechanism is provided for temperature, pressure, and flow disposed on the discharge conduit, the control device being coupled to the detection mechanism, the dual stage catalytic chamber, and the radiant tube low NOx burner, respectively. The radiant tube burner matched with the radiant tube is a low NOx burner of the radiant tube. The flue gas generated by the radiant tube is firstly recovered by the heat exchanger of the radiant tube low NOx burner, and then passed through an external heat exchanger. Secondary waste heat recovery. The CO and NO in the flue gas are catalytically reacted to generate N2 and CO2, and the paired NO is removed at one time, and then the secondary catalyst is used to catalyze the remaining CO in the flue gas after is mixed with air, and discharged into the atmosphere through a smoke exhaust fan and a chimney. The ratio of air and gas passing through the radiant tube low NOx burner is controlled to ensure that the residual oxygen value and CO value in the flue gas generated by the combustion are at a set level. The temperature, pressure and flow rate of the flue gas generated by the combustion are detected to control the combustion process and the two-stage catalytic process.

PT P
PN CN109207787-A; CN109207787-B
TI Preparation of autogenous graphene-reinforced            magnesium-based composite material involves melting            matrix alloy, introducing gas, mechanically agitating,            passing high-energy ultrasonic waves and hydrothermally            solidifying.
AU WANG X
   LI X
   LENG X
   HU X
   WU K
AE HARBIN INST TECHNOLOGY (HAIT-C)
GA 201908050G
AB    NOVELTY - The preparation of autogenous                graphene-reinforced magnesium-based composite                material involves (1) melting matrix alloy in a                crucible, (2) continuously introducing gas with                flow rate of 0.5-5 l/minute into semi-solid matrix                alloy melt for 5-40 minutes, mechanically agitating                at 580-700 degrees C and stirring at 500-3000 rpm                for 10-20 minutes, (3) adjusting alloy to                semi-solid temperature of 580-620 degrees C,                stirring for 10-30 minutes by semi-solid stirring,                (4) passing high-energy ultrasonic waves into the                alloy melt to promote the dispersion of graphene,                (5) hydrothermally solidifying the alloy melt to                prepare composite material, subjecting the prepared                composite material to solution treatment and hot                extruding to prepare extruded composite material,                where the base alloy is magnesium-zinc alloy,                magnesium-calcium alloy or magnesium-lithium alloy,                and the gas is mixed gas of carbon dioxide and                nitrogen.
   USE - Preparation of autogenous graphene-reinforced                magnesium-based composite material.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables preparation of autogenous                graphene-reinforced magnesium-based composite                material ensuring increased wettability and                enhanced material performance.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - METALLURGY - Preferred Process: In step (5), the                  water-cooling solidification of alloy melt                  involves placing the crucible containing alloy                  melt in water and water-cooling at rate of 10-400                  mm/minute.
DC M26 (Non-ferrous alloys - including alloy production and composition (C22C).); M29 (Changing the physical structure of nonferrous metals and alloys - including tempering, annealing, work-hardening and recrystallising (C22F).)

PN CN109179489-A; CN109179489-B
TI Preparation of two-dimensional ultra-thin stannous sulfide nanosheet by connecting stannous sulfide block to electrode as working electrode, and using platinum sheet as counter electrode and sodium sulfate solution as electrolyte.
AU HOU Y
   CHEN H
   LEI L
AE UNIV ZHEJIANG (UYZH-C)
GA 201909363A
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of two-dimensional ultra-thin stannous sulfide nanosheet comprises connecting stannous sulfide block to electrode as working electrode using conductive copper tape, using platinum sheet as counter electrode and sodium sulfate (Na2SO4) solution as electrolyte, immersing the stannous sulfide block and platinum sheet in the electrolyte, alternately applying a negative voltage and a positive voltage to the working electrode, vacuum filtering, collecting stannous sulfide flakes in the electrolyte, washing, drying, dispersing the dried stannous sulfide flakes in a dispersion solvent, ultrasonically crushing,centrifuging the suspension liquid, and freeze drying.
   USE - Preparation of two-dimensional ultra-thin stannous sulfide nanosheet used for electrochemical reduction of carbon dioxide (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method is simple and suitable for large-scale production, has high efficiency, low cost, controllable product quality, and good repeatability, and provides two-dimensional ultra-thin stannous sulfide nanosheet, which has excellent electrochemical performance and excellent acid selectivity.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for the two-dimensional ultra-thin stannous sulfide nanosheet having average thickness of not greater than 6 nm.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ELECTRICAL POWER AND ENERGY - Preferred Method: The negative voltage is - 1 to - 5 V. The positive voltage is + 1 to + 5 V. The absolute value ratio of the positive voltage and negative voltage is 0.5-2. The positive voltage application time for each time is 0.1-10 minutes. In the step of repeatedly and alternately applying positive voltage and negative voltage, the application time of the negative voltage is 0-10 minutes longer than the positive voltage application time.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The stannous sulfide block is prepared by mixing sulfur powder and tin powder, fully grinding the mixture, sealing in a vacuum quartz glass tube, heating the mixture at 400-600 degrees C for 12-36 hours, where heating rate is 1-10 degrees C/minute, and naturally cooling.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The dispersion solvent is an organic solvent miscible with water.

PN CN108816011-A
TI Method for treating plasma-bound water vapor volatile organic compound gas, involves collecting volatile organic compounds, followed by releasing as compressed gas and exciting volatile organic compound gas by electric field.
AU YIN H
AE YIN H (YINH-Individual)
GA 2018920407
AB    NOVELTY - A plasma-bound water vapor volatile organic compound gas treating method involves collecting volatile organic compounds and releasing as compressed gas, exciting the volatile organic compound gas by an electric field having an intensity of 22.2-22.4 kV/cm to form plasma while decomposing the volatile organic compound into small molecular organic substances of easily oxidatively degraded. The resulting plasma is introduced into the oxidation reaction chamber, sprayed the water vapor in the reaction chamber towards the surface, utilized water molecules to generate a large amount of hydroxyl radicals under the catalytic action of plasma having strong oxidizing ability for 13-15 minutes to completely oxidize the volatile organic compound to carbon dioxide and water within 2-3 minutes after the air is introduced, and cooled the purified gas by cold water spray.
   USE - Method for treating plasma-bound water vapor volatile organic compound gas.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables treating the plasma-bound water vapor volatile organic compound gas with very strong oxidation ability, and no formation of secondary pollution.

PN CN108770592-A; CN108770592-B
TI Medium useful for cultivating Pholiota limonella,            comprises corn cob, bran, glucose and gypsum.
AU WANG L
   YAO L
   ZHAO L
   ZHANG J
AE UNIV HEBEI NORMAL (UHEB-C)
GA 2018900937
AB    NOVELTY - Medium comprises 76-80 pts. wt. corn cob,                18-22 pts. wt. bran, 0.9-1.1 pts. wt. glucose and                0.9-1.1 pts. wt. gypsum. The medium has a water                content of 55-65 wt.%.
   USE - The medium is useful for cultivating                P.limonella.
   ADVANTAGE - The medium provides nutrients such as carbon                source, nitrogen source, inorganic salt, water and                growth factor for the growth of P.limonella,                increases yield of P.limonella fruiting body by                170-190 g/bag, which is 55-73% higher than that of                the control group.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for method                for cultivating Pholiota limonella, which involves                (a) sterilizing a bag containing the cultivation                medium and cooling the sterilized bag to less than                25 degrees C, inoculating P.limonella to obtain                P.limonella rod, (b) subjecting P.limonella rod to                spawn cultivation at 23-27 degrees C and ambient                humidity of 60-70% for 30-40 days to obtain                P.limonella spawn, and (c) removing and subjecting                P.limonella spawn to mushroom culture at 10-25                degrees C and ambient humidity of 80-90% for 12-16                days under volume concentration of carbon dioxide                in the environment of less than 0.1%to obtain                P.limonella fruiting body.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Composition: The medium comprises                  77-79 pts. wt. corn cob, 19-21 pts. wt. bran,                  0.95-1.05 pts. wt. glucose and 0.95-1.05 pts. wt.                  gypsum. Preferred Components: The medium has a                  water content of 58-62 wt.%. Preferred Method: In                  step (a), preparing P.limonella strain by (i)                  inoculating P.limonella mycelium onto the PDA                  medium and incubating medium at 23-27 degrees C                  for 12-14 days to obtain the P.limonella mother                  mycelium, (ii) inoculating the mother mycelium                  into the seed culture medium and culturing medium                  at 23-27 degrees C for 25-27 days and obtaining                  P.limonella species, where before inoculating the                  mother mycelium into the seed culture medium, the                  mother mycelium is further expanded for 8-10 days                  and the seed culture medium comprises 97-99 pts.                  wt. corn kernels and 1.8-2.2 pts. wt. gypsum                  powder and the water content of the seed culture                  medium is 58-62 wt.%. The medium for expanding                  the culture is a mother culture medium. The                  mother culture medium comprises 18-22 pts. wt.                  corn flour, 10-22 pts. wt. glucose, 2.9-3.1 pts.                  wt. peptone, 2.9-3.1 pts. wt. potassium                  dihydrogen phosphate, and 1.45-1.55 pts. wt.                  magnesium sulfate. 19 to 21 pts. wt. agar and                  980-1200 pts. wt. water and the pH of the mother                  medium is 5.2-5.8. The expanding culture is                  performed by alternately culturing under light                  and dark condition, the light and dark alternate                  manner is performed by culturing in accordance                  with light for 11-13 hours and culturing for                  11-13 hours under dark. In step (b), the ambient                  humidity of the seed culture is 60-70 wt.%. The                  P.limonella is P.limonella (CGMCC No. 15189). In                  step (a), the mushroom-raising method of the                  mushroom culture is de-bagged mushroom.

GA 2018832657
AB    NOVELTY - The boiler has a compressor (M1) for compressing a heat pump type loop (34) using a refrigerant e.g. R744 type compressible fluid and carbon dioxide. A fuel burner (11) delivers calories in the compressible fluid from a downstream of the compressor. The compressor is designed as a thermal compressor, which includes a piston compression stage. The fuel burner is provided with a hot source and a heating circuit for forming a cold source. An exchanger (5) is equipped with a thermal interface that is arranged between a compressible fluid circuit (31) and a heating circuit (30).

   LUNDMARK S
AE PERSTORP AB (PEST-C)
GA 201890700A
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves computing a carrying structure and a outer surface of a digital three dimensional model. The carry structure is printed with thermoplastic material at low resolution. The outer surface is printed with high resolution. The printed thermoplastic material is cooled and set by a cooling nozzle. Effluent is supplied to the cooling nozzle, where the effluent is selected from liquid carbon dioxide, liquid water and cooled air. Position of the cooling nozzle and amount of effluent ejected from the cooling nozzle is guided through algorithms. The thermoplastic material comprises polyester selected from polylactic acid and polycaprolactone. The thermoplastic material is selected from group consisting of poly caprolactones, lactides, allylics, benzimidazoles, carbonates, ether ether ketones, ether imides, alkylenes, alkylene phthalates, alkylene oxides, alkylene sulfides, propylene or butylene, styrenes, vinyl or vinylidene halides, chlorides and fluorides.

AU HONG G
   LIANG X

AE SHANGHAI TECH PHYSICS INST CHINESE ACAD (CATP-C)
GA 201879372F
AB    NOVELTY - The detector has an optical fiber combiner (5) collecting and mixed sun light and local oscillation light by a high-speed photoelectric detector (9) for outputting coherent detection electric signal, where the output electric signal comprising heterodyne interference from radio frequency signal and direct current signal. A multi-stage radio frequency amplifier (11) receives a radio frequency signal. A square law detector (12) detects an envelope signal when the radio frequency signal reaches sufficient magnitude. A microprocessor (16) generates a sawtooth wave signal (8). TI Automatic flue-gas pollutant monitoring system, has sampling part is provided with sampling pipeline, and controller for controlling first and second electromagnetic valves according to detecting threshold of pollutant concentration.
AU JIANG C

AB    NOVELTY - The system has a data analysis device for determining pollutant concentration in a smoke channel according to a detection result of an on-line sensor device. A sampling part is provided with a sampling pipeline that is provided with a smoke channel inlet and an air inlet. The smoke channel inlet and the air inlet are respectively provided with first and second electromagnetic valves. A controller controls the first and second electromagnetic valves according to detecting threshold of pollutant concentration by an on-line sensing device, where the pollutant concentration comprises sulfur dioxide, carbon dioxide, nitric oxide, anhydrous ammonia and nitrogen Dioxide. The on-line sensor device is selected as a diode array detector and a GFC detector or a differential spectrum detector.

   ADVANTAGE - The system can monitor concentration of pollutants in pollution and detect whether flue gas pollutant exceeds specific range, and has high accuracy and better environmental protection effect.

AU WILDENAUER F

GA 2018704127
AB    NOVELTY - The apparatus (10) has a container (11) which is suitable for receiving a liquid (20) and is provided with an opening (12). The light entering through the opening from the outside of the container reaches the liquid surface (22) of the liquid received in the container. A vibration device (30) is provided for generating turbulence in the liquid accommodated in the container.
   USE - Used for producing phototrophic organisms (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The growth of phototrophic organisms in water is essentially influenced by the available light, carbon dioxide and nutrients as well as the water temperature. The turbulent flow is generated and the laminar flow is prevented in the liquid surface. The incident light reaches the maximum number of organisms and thus, the increase of the growth rate of the organisms is increased. The low energy consumption is provided to avoid sedimentation and to manage the turbulent exchange many kinds of algae sensitive to shear forces, which are mainly generated by pumps. The vibration device is mechanically brought into contact with the liquid.

PT P
PN CN108469423-A
TI Detection of trace sulfur element content in cobalt carbonate and cobalt oxalate involves burning cobalt powder and flux, oxidizing sulfur and carbon, determining sulfur dioxide concentration, and obtaining content of sulfur and carbon.
AU LI W
   CHEN Q
   FANG L
AE NANJING HANRUI COBALT CO LTD (NANJ-Non-standard)
GA 2018700261
AB    NOVELTY - Detection of trace sulfur element content in cobalt carbonate and cobalt oxalate involves reducing cobalt-containing raw material comprising cobalt carbonate and cobalt oxalate, in a reducing gas to prepare cobalt powder, placing the cobalt powder and flux in a closed high-frequency furnace for combustion, oxidizing the sulfur element to sulfur dioxide, and carbon to carbon dioxide, determining the concentration of sulfur dioxide by infrared absorption method, and obtaining the mass percentage of sulfur element and carbon element in the cobalt powder. The mass percentage of sulfur element in the cobalt-containing raw material is calculated by the ratio of product of mass percentage of sulfur element in the cobalt powder and mass of cobalt powder, to mass of cobalt-containing raw material.
   USE - Detection of trace sulfur element content in cobalt carbonate and cobalt oxalate.
   ADVANTAGE - The method detects trace of sulfur element content with high accuracy, due to zero consumption of sulfur element during reduction process, and the content of sulfur element in cobalt-containing raw material remains constant in cobalt powder.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The reduction reaction of the cobalt-containing raw material is carried out using protective gas comprising nitrogen gas with purity of 99% or more, and reducing gas comprising hydrogen gas with purity of 99.5% or more.
DC E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).)

   USE - The fertilizer is useful in preparation of a plant fertilizer and/or a plant fertilizer additive (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The agent improves the soil structure, improving soil fertility, promoting plant growth and increase crop disease resistance and reduces the crop diseases in crops.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for producing fertilizer using food waste, comprising (i) uniformly mixing the food waste with lime, tourmaline powder and kieselgur and crushing, introducing carbon dioxide, where the mass ratio of food waste with lime, tourmaline powder and kieselgur is (100-300):(5-10):1(20-50), (ii) mixing product and fermenting agent for fermenting the compost after mixing, where the amount of the cellulase is 5-10 g in each kilogram of food waste, the amount of the protease is 8-15 g, the amount of Bacillus megatheriumis 2-5 g, the amount of Candida is 5-8g, and (iii) uniformly mixing fermented product with nutrient substance, granulating and drying; #the fertilizer is prepared by the above method.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - AGRICULTURE - Preferred Components: The microorganism further comprises Lactobacillus acidophilus and the mass ratio of cellulose, protease, Bacillus megatherium, Candida and Lactobacillus acidophilus is (5-10):(8-15):(2-5):(5-8):(3-5). The raw material comprises ammonium dihydrogen phosphate and the mass ratio of the food waste and phosphate is (100-300):1. Preferred Method: In the step (i) the condition of the carbon dioxide specifically comprises the quantity of the carbon dioxide is 510-560 ml/h, the time is 20-50 minutes. In the step (ii) the compost fermenting condition specifically comprises the PH value is 5.8-6.5, the time is 13-18 days, the stacking is 3-5 m, the width is 2-3 m, the height is 1-1.5 m bar buttress-shaped stack and the composting environment temperature in fermentation is 25-30 degrees C. In the step (iii), the nutrient substance comprises 1-3 pts. wt. ammonium molybdate, 3-7 pts. wt. cobalt chloride, 1-2 pts. wt. boric acid, 1-2 pts. wt. potassium iodide, 0.5-1.5 pts. wt. manganese sulfate, 8-15 pts. wt. spirulina, 10-25 pts. wt. bentonite and 1-2 pts. wt. brown algae powder, where the preparation method of the nutrient substances comprises mixing the raw materials after grinding and mixing uniformly, the further comprises 6-9 pts. wt. potash feldspar, 5-10 pts. wt. bitter wood and 1-2 pts. wt. tobacco, where the mass ratio of the nutrient food waste to material is (2-5):1. In the step (iii), the granulation comprises uniformly mixing with the nutritional substance to obtain the product into particles of 1-10 mm by pelletizer. In the step (iii), the drying condition comprises the vacuum degree is 0.09-0.07 MPa, the temperature is 35-45 degrees C and the time is 30-100 minutes.
   ACTIVITY - Fertilizer.

   TAKEUCHI K
   MIHARA M
   HORIGUCHI T
AE TORAY IND INC (TORA-C)
   TORAY IND INC (TORA-C)
GA 201862056G
AB    NOVELTY - A carbon film for gas separations is                manufactured by infusibilizing precursor fiber                containing polyacrylonitrile and carbonizing                infusibilized fiber. Infusibilizing process is                performed on the precursor fiber so that nitrile                consumption index (K1) of infusibilized fiber                surface is greater than or equal to 0.5, and                nitrile consumption index (K2) at a depth of 10 mu                m from the infusibilized fiber surface is greater                than or equal to 0.5. Nitrile consumption index is                determined by measuring absorbance of C-H vibration                (2940 cm-1) and absorbance of nitrile group (2240                cm-1) by micro-attenuated total reflectance (ATR)                method using infrared spectrometer, and calculating                nitrile consumption index according to equation                absorbance at 2240 cm-1/absorbance at 2940                cm-1.
   USE - Manufacture of carbon film for gas                separations, preferably for carbon dioxide                separation (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - Gas transmission rate can be further increased                in the polyacrylonitrile-based carbon film for gas                separation.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for:
   (1) manufacture of infusibilized fiber, which                is a precursor of the carbon film for gas                separations, which involves infusibilizing                precursor fiber containing polyacrylonitrile;                and
   (2) infusibilized fiber.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: Precursor fiber has                  porous precursor structure inside and has average                  fiber diameter of 20-5000 mu m. Precursor fiber                  is a hollow cross-section fiber with film                  thickness of 10-500 mu m, or is core-sheath                  structure fiber containing dissipating resin                  which forms a hollow portion in a core portion                  due to dissipation. Precursor fiber is obtained                  from resin mixture which mixes dissipating resin                  with polyacrylonitrile-type resin. Preferred                  Properties: Nitrile consumption index K1 of                  infusible fiber surface is 0.6-0.9, and nitrile                  consumption index K2 is 0.55-0.9. Preferred                  Conditions: In the infusibilizing process, when                  150 degrees C less than or equal to T1 less than                  or equal to T2 less than or equal to 350 degrees                  C, where T1 is 1st infusibilizing temperature,                  and T1 is infusibilizing temperature, the process                  is carried out so that S1+S2=1-50 minutes, where                  S1 is 1st infusibilizing time, and S2 is 2nd                  infusibilizing time.

PN CN108213453-A
TI Preparation of multi-metal nanomaterial by mixing bismuth nitrate, tin nitrate, barium sulfate and ethylene glycol monobutyl ether to obtain mixed solution, mixing with powder including sodium humate solution, and hydrothermal reacting.
AU YE J
AE YE J (YEJJ-Individual)
GA 201853485R
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of multi-metal nanomaterial comprises taking 3-6 pts. wt. bismuth nitrate, 4-9 pts. wt. tin nitrate and 2-6 pts. wt. barium sulfate, adding 20-30 pts. wt. ethylene glycol monobutyl ether, and stirring at 30-50 degrees C for 30-40 minutes to obtain first mixed solution; stirring 10-20 pts. wt. sodium humate solution into 4-8 pts. wt. tetrachlorosilane, 3-7 pts. wt. antimony trioxide and 10-20 pts. wt. absolute ethanol, stirring, reacting for 30-50 minutes at 70-80 degrees C, filtering and drying to obtain powder; adding powder into mixed solution, performing ultrasonic treatment for 10-20 minutes, putting into PTFE-lined hydrothermal kettle, reacting at 80-120 degrees C for 4-6 hours to obtain second mixed solution, and controlling filling ratio of hydrothermal kettle at 25-40%; and placing mixed solution containing 15 wt.% carbon monoxide and 85 wt.% carbon dioxide atmosphere, and roasting for 3-6 hours at 750-850 degrees C.
   USE - Method for preparing multi-metal nanomaterial.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - METALLURGY - Preferred Components: The multi-metal nanomaterial comprises 5 pts. wt. cerium nitrate, 7 pts. wt. tin nitrate, 5 pts. wt. barium sulfate, and 25 pts. wt. ethylene glycol monobutyl ether. The powder comprises 15 pts. wt. sodium humate solution, 6 pts. wt. silicon tetrachloride, 5 pts. wt. antimony trichloride, and 15 pts. wt. absolute ethanol.

PN CN108070630-A
TI Preparing high-purity flaxseed protein comprises            e.g. de-gumming, petroleum ether degreasing, separating            flaxseed protein from flaxseed by foam separation,            determining the foam layer and the residual liquid            volume and drying.
AU WANG F
   ZHANG Z
   DENG F
   GE J
AE GUANGZHOU LIZHONG BIOTECHNOLOGY CO LTD (GUAN-Non-standard)
GA 2018434187
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing high-purity flaxseed protein                comprises (1) mixing cleaned flaxseed and water                according to mass ratio of 1:5-6, using acid                adjusting the pH value to 3.8-4.2, adding enzyme,                carrying out enzymolysis at 40-50 degrees C for 4-5                hours, and degumming under 250 revolutions/minute                condition, (2) drying the de-gummed flax seed,                crushing, degreasing using petroleum ether at room                temperature to obtain defatted flaxseed meal, (3)                configuring flaxseed meal to extract liquid,                pouring into foam separator, introducing air or                carbon dioxide, collecting the foam until there is                no foam is generated, and collecting bubbles after                bubbling, (4) determining the foam layer and the                residual liquid volume, measuring raw liquid, and                content of protein in the residue and foam layer,                and (5) drying the resultant flax seed protein by                freeze-drying to obtain flax seed protein.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing high-purity                flaxseed protein.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: is simple, economical and                environmentally friendly; has high yield; and                ensures high purity flax seed protein.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Components: The enzyme is                  alpha-amylase, beta-amylase, glucoamylase,                  protease, cellulose and/or pectinase. Preferred                  Method: In the step (1), the adding amount of the                  enzyme is 100 U/g, where the adding quantity of                  enzyme to dry basis. In the step (2), crushing is                  to 70 mesh sieve. In the step (3), extract liquid                  preparation comprises mixing flax seed powder and                  sodium chloride solution uniformly according to                  mass ratio of 10-18:1, adjusting the pH value to                  3-3.5 by acid, extracting for 0.5-2 hours at the                  temperature of 40-60 degrees C to obtain flax                  seed meal extract solution, where the                  concentration of the sodium chloride solution is                  0.6-0.8 mol/l. In the step (4), the measuring raw                  liquid, the content of protein in the residue and                  foam layer is by Coomassie brilliant blue                  method.

PT P
PN CN107988288-A; CN107988288-B
TI High-density fermentation method for producing Propionibacterium bacteriocin, inoculating Propionibacterium freudenreichii into modified peptone yeast glucose medium, culturing, and performing fermentation using e.g. carbon source.
AU ZHENG L
   WANG L
   QI B
   WANG J
   XU T
AE CHANGSHU INST TECHNOLOGY (CHGS-C)
GA 2018366254
AB    NOVELTY - High-density fermentation method for producing Propionibacterium bacteriocin, involves (a) inoculating single colonies of Propionibacterium freudenreichii strain CS1420 into modified peptone yeast glucose medium for activating and anaerobically culturing strain at 30 degrees C for 2.5 days, (b) inoculating 10% seed solution into flask containing standard lactose broth medium and anaerobically culturing at 30 degrees C for 2.5 days, and (c) inoculating 10% expanded seed liquid into a 5 l fermenter containing 2 l SLB medium, fermenting at 30 degrees C and 100 rpm, and introducing carbon dioxide at 0.05 MPa and controlling pH to 6, supplementing 100 ml carbon sources and nitrogen sources from 96th hour of fermentation for every 24 hours for 5 times, where the carbon source (2-4 g/l) is 75% sodium lactate and 25% glucose and the nitrogen source is a yeast solution and corn vinasse solution in a volume ratio of 1:(10-50) and mass ratio of grain and distilled water of vinasse solution is 1:4.
   USE - The high-density fermentation method is useful for producing Propionibacterium bacteriocin.
   ADVANTAGE - The method produces Propionibacterium bacteriocin with high yield.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Method: In step (c), the concentration of carbon source is 2.5 g/l. The volume ratio of yeast solution and corn vinasse solution is 1:30. The method involves determining antibacterial activity by centrifuging the fermentation broth at 4 degrees C and 6000 rpm for 15 minutes, taking the supernatant, adjusted the pH to 7 using 10% sodium hydroxide and pH to 5.5 using 10% tartaric acid, ultrafiltering the fermentation broth using an ultrafiltration membrane with a cut-off of 3000 u to remove high molecular weight proteins, and heteropolypeptides, obtaining crude bacteriocin extract, and diluting using sterile lactose broth (LB) solution, performing 2N serial dilution, adding different dilutions of antibacterial substances into 96-well plate having total volume of each well of 200 mu l, where 20 mu l of antibacterial substance with different dilutions, 180 mu l of indicator bacteria solution, 20 mu l blank LB liquid medium and 180 mu l indicator bacteria liquid are added as controls, incubating the plate at 30 degrees C and measuring the absorbance value of each well at 600 nm at regular intervals, determining the absorbance at optical density (OD)600 nm using standard enzyme solution, when the dilution at OD600 is half the absorbance of the control group, then it is defined as a unit of antibacterial activity in Au/ml. The corn vinasse solution is prepared by mixing wine lees and distilled water in a mass ratio of 1:4, stirring at 30 degrees C for 3 hours, centrifuging at 4200 rpm for 5 minutes and filtering mixture. The feeding speed is 10 ml/minute. The SLB medium formulation comprises 10 g trypsin hydrolyzed casein, 5 g yeast extract, 10 g sodium lactate and 1000 ml distilled water, where the pH of medium is 7-7.2.
DC B04 (Natural products and polymers. Including testing of body fluids (other than blood typing or cell counting), pharmaceuticals or veterinary compounds of unknown structure, testing of microorganisms for pathogenicity, testing of chemicals for mutagenicity or human toxicity and fermentative production of DNA or RNA. General compositions.); D16 (Fermentation industry - including fermentation equipment, brewing, yeast production, production of pharmaceuticals and other chemicals by fermentation, microbiology, production of vaccines and antibodies, cell and tissue culture and genetic engineering.); E13 (Heterocyclics.)

PN US2018120276-A1
TI Sensing device for detecting gas molecules, comprises LED light generating component, color sensor which is arranged such that no light emitted from LED component directly impinges on it, and removably attached gas molecule sensing film(s).
AU KANE J
   HARRIS J
   BIAGIONI J
   BORTZ T
AE POLESTAR TECHNOLOGIES INC (POLE-Non-standard)
GA 201833888P
AB    NOVELTY - A sensing device comprises at least one LED light generating component, at least one color sensor, and at least one removably attached gas molecule sensing film (52). The LED component and the color sensor are situated such that no light emitted from the LED component directly impinges onto the color sensor.
   USE - Sensing device is used for detecting gas molecules e.g. carbon dioxide and/or oxygen (claimed). Uses include but are not limited to scuba diving equipment, medical equipment, airplanes and space vehicles.
   ADVANTAGE - The sensing device has improved optical properties and sensing of gas molecules. The color sensor receives only light which is emitted from the LED and reflected off of the sensing film, thereby improving the sensitivity of the sensor. An opaque component in the sensing device is capable of preventing light from reaching the color sensor directly without impinging onto and/or into the sensing film.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows an exploded view of the sensing device.
   Protective cap (12)
   Main housing (14)
   Connector component (36)
   Sensing film (52)
   Substrate (58)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ELECTRONICS - Preferred Arrangement: The sensing device further comprises at least one nonreactive colorant or fluorescent component.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The gas molecule sensing film comprises at least one of a gas molecule reacting composition, a substrate (58) and an adhesive. The gas molecule reacting composition comprises at least one of a gas molecule reacting component which is capable of causing the composition to either change color when exposed to and reacted with a selected gas molecule or is capable of causing a change in the fluorescent properties of the composition when exposed to and reacted with a selected gas molecule. The gas molecule reacting composition further comprises at least one gas permeable host polymer and at least one base. The base is chosen from alkali metal hydroxides, alkaline earth metal hydroxide, tetra-coordinated ammonium hydroxides, amines, cyclic amines, and tetra-coordinated phosphorus hydroxides. The gas molecule reacting component is an indicator dye chosen from pH indicator, metacresol purple, thymol blue, cresol red, phenol red, xylenol blue, mixture of cresol red and thymol blue in a ratio of 3:1, bromothymol blue, neutral red, phenolphthalein, rosolic acid, alpha -naphtholphthalein, orange I, bromocresol purple, bromophenol red, p-nitrophenol, m-nitrophenol, curcumin, quinoline blue, thymolphthalein, 8-hydroxypyrene-1,3,6-trisulfonic acid and trisodium salt. The gas molecule reacting composition comprises at least one fluorescent component which is capable of reacting with a selected gas molecule to alter the fluorescence properties of the component when the LED light impinges onto and/or into the film which has been exposed to the selected gas molecules. The fluorescent component is chosen from a platinum group metal complex platinum group metal complexes of 1,10-phenanthroline and bipyrene derivatives including for example, ruthenium tris(4,7-diphenyl-1,10-phenanthroline) and osmium tris(4,4'-diphenyl-2,2'-bipyridine), metalloporphyrin complexes comprising platinum meso-tetra-perfluoroaromatic-porphyrin, vanadyl octaethylporphyrin, palladium tetrakis(4-sulfonatophenyl)porphyrin, 1,3-dihydroxypyrene-6,8-disulfonate, fluorescein, umbelliferone, 4-methylumbelliferone, 3-benzothiazoylbelliferone, 7-hydroxycoumarin-3-carboxylic acid, 1-naphthol-2-sulfonate, 1-naphthol-4-sulfonate, 2-naphthol-6-sulfonate, 7-hydroxyflavone, 7-hydroxyisoflavone, 3-hydroxyxanthone, 3,6-dihydroxyxanthone, 7-hydroxy-4-methylchromon, 7-hydroxyepidone, 3-hydroxy-4-methylcoumarin, 8-hydroxy-1, 3,6-trisulfonate, 3-hydroxyxacridone, salicylaldehyde semicarbazone and 2-hydroxycinnamic acid.

Preferred Method: The preparation of                  catechol-modified chitosan comprises: (i)                  dissolving CS in acid solution (hydrochloric                  acid, pH 1-2) and stirring for 4-6 hours to                  obtain CS solution, adjusting the pH of CS                  solution to 5, then adding cyclohexyl acrylate                  into the CS solution, where the molar ratio of                  cyclohexyl acrylate and CS is 0.8-1, stirring                  until the solution is clear to obtain a                  solution-A; (ii) dissolving                  1-ethyl-3-(3-dimethylaminopropyl)carbodiimide                  hydrochloride in first solvent (ethanol),                  drop-wisely adding solution-A at a rate of 1                  drop/second, stirring, where the molar ratio of                  1-ethyl-3-(3-dimethylaminopropyl)carbodiimide                  hydrochloride to cyclohexyl acrylate is 1-2,                  carrying out coupling reaction at 37 degrees C                  for 12-24 hours, maintaining a pH of 5, dialyzing                  with 1 wt.% sodium chloride, hydrochloric acid                  (pH 5) and deionized water to remove reaction                  substances and by-products, then lyophilizing and                  drying.

   USE - The method is useful for growing H.erinaceus in greenhouse using air source heat pump.
   ADVANTAGE - The method obtains H.erinaceus having rich nutrition and high survival rate.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Method for growing Hericium erinaceus in greenhouse using air source heat pump involves (a) preparing a culture medium required for bag cultivation of H.erinaceus, (b) preparing various materials by mixing various materials, and controlling water content of culture material at 63-65% and pH value at 8-8.5, (c) packing culture material in a plastic bag i.e. 900-1000 g culture material per bag, putting filled plastic bag on the collar to form a cultivation bag, where plastic bag is a polypropylene plastic bag with a size of 17 cmx 35 cm and a thickness of 0.0045 cm, (d) using high pressure sterilization, moving to sterilization cart to stack, pushing to sterilization pot for sterilization, steaming pot to raise the temperature to 100 degrees C, keeping it warm for 20 minutes, heating to 121 degrees C for 2 hours, moving sterilized cultivation bag to a clean cooling position and cooling to below 25 degrees C, (e) inoculating culture bag in culture room, and (f) (i) placing inoculated cultivation bag in a dark cultivation room, controlling indoor temperature to 22-24 degrees C and relative air humidity to 60-70%, and cultivating in environment for 25 days, (ii) moving cultivation bag to fruiting room, removing the collar and tampon on the first day, maintaining bag intact, inserting cultivation bag into the fruiting grid, adjusting the indoor temperature to 15-17 degrees C, increasing internal circulation, and cultivating in the dark, (iii) controlling indoor temperature to 14-15 degrees C with temperature of 90-95% and carbon dioxide content to below 800 ppm on days 2-5 when small primordium begins to form, where light condition is 5 hours a day, and intensity is 50-100 lux, (iv) stimulating formation of fruit bodies on days 6-8, and controlling room temperature at 12-14 degrees C with temperature of 85-90%, carbon dioxide content to below 800 ppm, light for 5 hours per day, intensity of 50-100 lux, (v) controlling indoor temperature to 12-14 degrees C on 9th day with temperature of 80-85%, and carbon dioxide content to below 800 ppm, (vi) harvesting on the 10th day when first tide of mushroom fruiting is over, (vii) increasing temperature and reducing humidity from the 11th to the 12th day, controlling the second tidal mushroom, controlling temperature 17-18 degrees C and humidity to 70-80%, increasing internal circulation, and cultivating in the dark, (viii) controlling indoor temperature to 14-15 degrees C from 13th to 16th day when small primordium begins to form, with temperature of 90-95%, carbon dioxide content to below 800 ppm, light of 5 hours a day and intensity of 50-100 lux, (ix) stimulating formation of fruit bodies on days 17-18, controlling room temperature at 12-14 degrees C with temperature of 85-90%, carbon dioxide content to below 800 ppm, light for 5 hours per day, and intensity of 50-100 lux, (x) controlling indoor temperature to 12-14 degrees C on the 19th day, with temperature of 80-85% and the carbon dioxide content to below 800 ppm, and (xi) harvesting on the 20th day when second tide mushroom fruiting is over.
