PN CN114634946-A
TI Constructing Pichia pastoris genetic engineering            bacteria for improving methanol assimilation rate and            fixing carbon dioxide in Pichia pastoris, involves            amplifying respective corresponding sequences of            primers by using polymerase chain reaction.
AU CHEN K
   MA C
   WANG J
   GUO Y
   WANG X
AE UNIV NANJING TECHNOLOGY (UNTY-C)
GA 202284382P
AB    NOVELTY - Constructing Pichia pastorisgenetic                engineering bacteria involves designing primers                corresponding to reductive glycine pathway related                gene MIS1, reductive glycine pathway related gene                GCV1, reductive glycine pathway related gene GCV2                and reductive glycine pathway related gene GCV3                respectively, and amplifying respective                corresponding sequences of the primers by using                polymerase chain reaction (PCR).
   USE - Method for constructing Pichia pastoris                genetic engineering bacteria for improving methanol                assimilation rate and fixing carbon dioxide in                Pichia pastoris (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to modify methanol                metabolism path of Pichia pastoris, a dissimilatory                path is knocked out, and reductive glycine path is                reconstructed, so that carbon atom loss caused by                carbon dioxide generated by dissimilatory is                eliminated, and the utilization efficiency of                carbon atoms is improved. The preferred methanol                dissimilation pathway of the Pichia pastorisis                subjected to compartmentalization rational design                in order to further improve the assimilation of                methanol and carbon dioxide, biomass of a strain                subjected to metabolic modification is improved by                8.7%, and gene engineering strain has the same                methanol consumption rate but obvious formic acid                accumulation of about 0.66 g/L compared with the                original Pichia pastorisGS115, and further proves                that more carbon is used for central metabolism by                the Pichia pastorisgene engineering strain.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Constructing Pichia pastoris genetic                engineering bacteria involves designing primers                corresponding to reductive glycine pathway related                gene MIS1, reductive glycine pathway related gene                GCV1, reductive glycine pathway related gene GCV2                and reductive glycine pathway related gene GCV3                respectively, and amplifying respective                corresponding sequences of the primers by using                polymerase chain reaction (PCR), where MIS1, GCV1,                GCV2 and GCV3 used in recombinant plasmids are                derived from Pichia pastoris GS115 endogenous                genes. An formate dehydrogenase (FDH) gene in a                dissimilatory pathway in Pichia pastoris GS115 is                knocked out by using clustered regularly                interspaced palindromic repeats (CRISPR)/Cas9 gene                knock-out technology to obtain recombinant strain                GS 115-delta FDH. The recombinant plasmids, such as                pGAP-MIS1-P2A-GCV1, and pGAP-GCV2-P2A-GCV3 are                constructed and plasmid pET28 a-His is knocked out.                The recombinant plasmids pGAP-MIS1-P2A-GCV1 and                pGAP-GCV2-P2A-GCV3 are introduced into Pichia                pastoris GS 115-delta FDH, and double-crossing                homologous recombination is carried out by using                knock-out plasmid pET28a-His to knock out DAS genes                in the Pichia pastoris, so that the reductive                glycine pathway replaces the original methanol                assimilation pathway. A recombinant plasmid                pIC9K-FLD-P2A-FGH-PTS1 is constructed, PCR                amplification on genes FLD and FGH of a methanol                catabolism pathway of Pichia pastoris GS115 is                carried out by using specific primers, and PTS1                signal peptide is added at a sequence C end to                position the catabolism pathway in peroxisomes. The                recombinant plasmid pIC9K-FLD-P2A-FGH-PTS1 is                introduced into constructed recombinant Pichia                pastoris, compartmentalized positioning expression                of a methanol dissimilation pathway of the Pichia                pastoris is realized, Pichia pastoris containing                the recombinant plasmid is obtained, and                abbreviated as recombinant bacteria.
   An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for an                application of the Pichia pastoris genetically                engineered bacteria, which is used for improving                methanol assimilation rate and fixing carbon                dioxide, which specifically involves selecting                positive clone of Pichia genetic engineering                bacteria, culturing overnight in a shaking table,                transferring to M9 culture medium, culturing by                taking methanol as a unique carbon source, taking                samples every 24 hours during the cell culture to                draw a growth curve as oprtical density at 600                nanometer (OD600) and measuring, and determining                the biomass.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Conditions: The conditions used                  for the PCR amplification are denaturation at                  95&#8451; for 2 minutes, 30 cycles of                  annealing at 95&#8451; for 20 seconds,                  55&#8451; for 20 seconds, and 72&#8451;                  for 10 seconds, and extension for 5 minutes at                  72&#8451;. The Pichia pastoris GS115 is                  derived from a commercial bacteria bank ATCC                  201178.Preferred Sequences: The upstream primer                  used for the reductive glycine pathway-related                  gene MIS1 is MIS1-F that has a nucleic acid                  sequence of 55 nucleotides of (SEQ ID No: 17),                  given in the specification, and downstream primer                  MIS1-R has a nucleic acid sequence of 42                  nucleotides of (SEQ ID No: 18), given in the                  specification. The amino                  acid sequence of the reductive glycine                  pathway-related gene MIS1 used for constructing                  the recombinant plasmid pGAP-MIS1-P2A-GCV1 is                  (SEQ ID No: 1), given in the specification, and                  amino acid sequence of reductive glycine                  pathway-related gene GCV1 is (SEQ ID No: 2),                  given in the specification. The amino acid                  sequence of the reducing glycine pathway related                  gene GCV2 used for constructing the recombinant                  plasmid pGAP-GCV2-P2A-GCV3 is (SEQ ID No: 3),                  given in the specification, and the amino acid                  sequence of the reducing glycine pathway related                  gene GCV3 is (SEQ ID No: 4), given in the                  specification. The recombinant plasmid                  pIC9K-FLD-P2A-FGH-PTS1 is constructed, where                  amino acid sequence of the reducing glycine                  pathway related gene FGH is (SEQ ID No: 5), given                  in the specification, and amino acid sequence of                  the reducing glycine pathway related gene FLD is                  (SEQ ID No: 6). The nucleic acid sequence of the                  upstream primer used by the dissimilatory pathway                  related gene FGH is (SEQ ID No: 13), given in the                  specification, and nucleic acid sequence of the                  downstream primer FGH-R is (SEQ ID No: 14), given                  in the specification. The nucleotide sequence of                  the upstream primer FLD-F used by the                  dissimilatory pathway related gene FLD is (SEQ ID                  No: 15), given in the specification, and the                  nucleotide sequence of the downstream primer                  FLD-R is (SEQ ID No: 16), given in the                  specification.
DC B04 (Natural products and polymers. PN CA2988458-A1
TI Process and equipment for producing reducing gas e.g. hydrogen and carbon monoxide, involves using raw material e.g. coal, domestic garbage, annual crop residue material, and fuel oil, and oxidant by total reduction or partial oxidation.
AU NABI G
AE NABI G (NABI-Individual)
GA 2019514648
AB    NOVELTY - Process and equipment for producing reducing gas involves using raw materials, such as coal, domestic garbage and annual crop residue material, fuel oil and their gas condensate of single or variable admixtures, and an oxidant which is highly-pure oxygen or oxygen comprising some nitrogen by a totally reducing or low degree partial oxidation process. The obtained product gases are cleaned from the gaseous impurities of sulfur in a desorption tower. Absorption of the gases is carried out after the gases are cooled (desorbed) in a stripper. Sodium hydroxide is reacted with the impurity material and this partially reacted solution is pumped to concentration tower where precipitated material is filtered out and lean caustic solution is recycled. The filtered solid material is dried and converted to completely oxidized form and reacted with calcium hydroxide to obtain sodium hydroxide for reuse. The obtained mixture of gases is separated into carbon monoxide and hydrogen gases.
   USE - Process and equipment for producing reducing gases, such as carbon monoxide for producing carbon dioxide, hydrogen for producing aluminum and silicon, purifying electronic scrap, steel scrap and recovering tramp elements, noble metals and platinum group of metals, and solid carbon for power generation, industrial production, and synthesis of chemicals and new material. The carbon monoxide and hydrogen is converted to methanol which is used for producing dimethyl ether, ethylene, acetic acid, formic acid and propylene (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables manufacture of highly-pure and highly-potential reducing gas having high calorific value by environmentally-friendly and simple process, and converts the obtained impurities into useful byproducts.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Process and equipment for producing reducing gas involves using raw materials, such as coal, domestic garbage and annual crop residue material, fuel oil and their gas condensate of single or variable admixtures, and an oxidant which is highly-pure oxygen or oxygen comprising some nitrogen by a totally reducing or low degree partial oxidation process. The gasification of charge material is carried out in a refractory lined steel reactor equipped with attachments to measure and control reactor and reaction parameters. The combined material and oxygen are introduced to form products and carbon dioxide, water to control the temperature. The obtained product gases are cleaned from the gaseous impurities of sulfur using sensible heat of the gases for their purification in a desorption tower. Absorption of the gases is carried out after the gases are cooled (desorbed) in a stripper. Sodium hydroxide is reacted with the impurity material and this partially reacted solution is pumped to concentration tower where precipitated material is filtered out and lean caustic solution is recycled by adding additional caustic solution. The filtered solid material is dried and converted to completely oxidized form and reacted with calcium hydroxide to obtain sodium hydroxide for reuse. The obtained mixture of gases comprising carbon monoxide and hydrogen is separated into carbon monoxide and hydrogen gases and converted into pure hydrogen and carbon dioxide. The carbon dioxide is directly liquefied by compression expansion cycle and to avoid high compression of carbon dioxide a primary cycle of nitrogen cooling is added. Some of the mixture of gases comprising carbon monoxide and hydrogen can be converted to methanol, and methanol can be converted to dimethyl ether, ethylene and propylene by catalytic dehydration, and to acetic acid and formic acid by interlinked process.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of purification of coal.
   Coal storage (1)
   Crushing and grinding of coal (2)
   Reaction of coal with sodium hydroxide (3)
   Reaction of washed coal with mixed acids (4)
   Separation of dissolved impurities from coal (5)

TI Processing comprehensive utilization by-products in producing waste A by AW, comprises adding coke for calcination to form tungsten oxide and carbon dioxide, leaching, extracting, adding, crystallizing, cooling, transferring, and pumping.
AU OUYANG H
   LEI Y
   LI X
   YANG J
   PENG Y
   JIANG C
   CAO Y
   CAO C
   LONG C
   HUANG F
AE HUNAN MAOTIAN CENTURY NEW MATERIAL CO (HUNA-Non-standard)
GA 2019113083
AB    NOVELTY - Processing comprehensive utilization by-products in producing waste A by AW, comprises e.g. (i) adding coke to WC for calcination to form tungsten oxide and carbon dioxide; (ii) leaching tungsten oxide with sodium hydroxide solution to obtain sodium tungstate solution; (iii) extracting with quaternary ammonium salt organic relative to sodium tungstate solution, and separating; (iv) back-extracting organic phase supporting tungsten with ammonium hydrogen carbonate to obtain ammonium tungstate solution and quaternary ammonium salt organic phase; (v) adding calcium oxide to raffinate to form caustic slag and causticizing liquid, preheating, transferring; (vi) collecting, combining and introducing into gas deposition chamber, collecting, spraying, dedusting, and introducing; (vii) subjecting ammonium tungstate solution to evaporative crystallization to obtain APT and ammonia-containing vapor, cooling, transferring, and transferring; and (viii) pumping.
   USE - The method is useful for processing comprehensive utilization by-products in producing waste A by AW.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is economical; and reduces greenhouse effect.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Processing comprehensive utilization by-products in producing waste A by AW, comprises adding 2 wt% coke to WC for calcination to form tungsten oxide and carbon dioxide; (ii) leaching the tungsten oxide with sodium hydroxide solution to obtain sodium tungstate solution; (iii) extracting with quaternary ammonium salt organic relative to sodium tungstate solution, and separating organic phase and raffinate loaded with tungsten; (iv) back-extracting the organic phase supporting tungsten with ammonium hydrogen carbonate to obtain ammonium tungstate solution and quaternary ammonium salt organic phase, and returning the quaternary ammonium salt organic phase without treatment; (v) adding calcium oxide to the raffinate to form caustic slag and causticizing liquid, and preheating the caustic slag to 150-200 degrees C in a microwave oven, and transferring to a kiln for calcination, producing calcium oxide and carbon dioxide, where the calcium oxide is used for repeated causticization of the raffinate, and the causticizing liquid is returned for leaching of the sodium tungstate solution; (vi) collecting the flue gas formed in the calcination, combining and introducing into the gas deposition chamber, and collecting the dust with a bag filter, spraying the water and dedusting, introducing the purified mixed gas into the plate heat exchanger through the induced draft fan to cool down and pressurizing by a supercharger to 0.1-0.14 MPa into a carbonizer to produce ammonium bicarbonate; (vii) subjecting the ammonium tungstate solution to evaporative crystallization to obtain APT and ammonia-containing vapor, and introducing the ammonia-containing vapor into the ammonia gas absorption tower, cooling using cooling device in the tower, increasing non-condensable gas, falling the condensate into the bottom of tower and pumping out, and cooling by the plate heat exchanger outside the tower, and transferring to the absorption tower to make the spray liquid absorb the non-condensed ammonia gas, and circulating absorption, when the ammonia water concentration is greater than 3 mol/l, transferring the dilute ammonia water to the ammonium hydrogen carbonate circulation tank; and (viii) pumping out the diluted ammonia water from the ammonium bicarbonate circulation tank, and cooling by a plate heat exchanger, and introducing into a carbonizer and reacting with carbon-di-oxide gas, and introducing dilute ammonium hydrogen carbonate solution into an ammonium hydrogen carbonate circulation tank, pumping out the dilute ammonium bicarbonate solution from the circulation tank and continuing to absorb the carbon-dioxide gas after heat exchange through the plate heat exchanger, so that the cycle is absorbed until the total C concentration is greater than 2 mol/l, diluting the dilute ammonium bicarbonate solution, adjusting the ratio with liquid ammonia and solid ammonium bicarbonate, and returning for use as a stripping agent.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: In the step (vi), the combined flue gas and the ammonia-containing vapor are not treated, introducing directly into the stripper using the high-temperature stripping of carbon-dioxide, spraying the carbon-dioxide entrained micro-particles and removing by the ammonium bicarbonate solution in the tower, producing the top of the tower a high concentration of ammonium bicarbonate solution, and filtering dilute ammonium bicarbonate solution at the bottom of the tower through a precision filter and transferring to the top of the tower for circulation absorption of the spray liquid.
DC E37 (Mixtures of many components; inorganic reactions and processes of general applicability.); J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).)

PT P
PN DE102018104595-A1; EP3428130-A1; EP3428130-B1
TI Gasifying and converting moist biomass with supercritical water, comprises diluting biomass to be gasified, depending on water content with water or with enriched dry mass biomass, preconditioning by crushing and mixing biomass suspension.
AU GOMES J
   CLAUSNITZER M
AE VPC GMBH (VPCV-Non-standard)
   VPC GMBH (VPCV-Non-standard)
GA 2019035605
AB    NOVELTY - Gasifying and converting moist biomass with supercritical water, where an educt of the gasification plant comprises of dry matter and water, and to achieve a constant biomass concentration, comprises diluting the biomass to be gasified, depending on the water content with water or with enriched dry mass biomass, preconditioning by crushing and mixing the biomass suspension to obtain a pumpable, uniform starting material, which is then brought to a pressure of less than 20 bar, feeding to a first heat exchanger, which heats the suspension to less than 180 degrees C and passing the biomass suspension into the liquefaction reactor. The temperature is maintained at the inlet temperature for 25 minutes, and then the suspension is brought to the gasification pressure of less than 290 bar and subsequently the biomass mixture passes through a second heat exchanger in which biomass mixture is heated by heat transfer.
   USE - The method is useful for gasifying and converting moist biomass with supercritical water.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables smooth recovery of electrical energy in the gasification of wet biomasses with supercritical water.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Gasifying and converting moist biomass with supercritical water, where an educt of the gasification plant comprises of dry matter and water, and to achieve a constant biomass concentration, comprises diluting the biomass to be gasified, depending on the water content with water or with enriched dry mass biomass, preconditioning by crushing and mixing the biomass suspension to obtain a pumpable, uniform starting material, which is then brought to a pressure of less than 20 bar, feeding to a first heat exchanger, which heats the suspension to less than 180 degrees C and passing the biomass suspension into the liquefaction reactor. The temperature is maintained at the inlet temperature for 25 minutes, and then the suspension is brought to the gasification pressure of less than 290 bar and subsequently the biomass mixture passes through a second heat exchanger in which biomass mixture is heated by heat transfer with the gasification product to a temperature of less than 500 degrees C and the water is in the supercritical state and a gasification reaction begins, and then, after passing through the gasification reactor, the biomass is largely converted into gases, which contains gases e.g. carbon dioxide, methane, hydrogen and ethane and continues to exist and gasification product mainly from unreacted supercritical water and dissolved salts and non-gasified organic particles, and subsequently the precipitated salts are separated by a bottom draw as a salt solution, and then the product is cooled by heat recovery in the at the heat exchangers to 275-65 degrees C at unchanged high pressure level. From a temperature of 374 degrees C water is present in the liquid state and can be separated from the product gas after heat recovery in a water separator. The existing product gas comprises carbon dioxide, methane, hydrogen and ethane, and the necessary carbon dioxide separation for gas treatment takes place then by cooling, and by a chiller. The product gas is cooled to a temperature below 30 degrees C, since at this temperature and the prevailing high pressure, both carbon dioxide and ethane are liquid and are separated in the carbon dioxide and ethane separators by phase separation. The remaining fuel gas, which is composed of methane and hydrogen, can be utilized in the power generation process independent of the gasification process.
TF EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.
DC H09 (Fuel products not of petroleum origin - excluding coal handling, preparation or mining, but including coking, briquetting, peat processing synthesis, gas production, coal gasification. Combustion improvement additives for coal, peat and other nonhydrocarbon based fuels are included in this Section together with coal liquefaction and desulphurisation.)

PT P
PN WO2018027322-A1; CA3033122-A1; US2019186027-A1
TI Producing glycerol carbonate involves applying electrochemical potential between anode and cathode immersed in electrolyte containing carbon dioxide and glycerol to transform carbon dioxide and glycerol into glycerol carbonate.
AU KIRK D
   HOE H H
AE UNIV TORONTO GOVERNING COUNCIL (UTOR-C)
   UNIV TORONTO GOVERNING COUNCIL (UTOR-C)
GA 201813189V
AB    NOVELTY - Glycerol carbonate (508) is produced by providing electrolyte containing carbon dioxide (CO2) (402) and glycerol (401) in electrochemical reaction unit; and applying electrochemical potential between anode and cathode immersed in electrolyte to electrochemically transform CO2 and glycerol into glycerol carbonate.
   USE - Production of glycerol carbonate.
   ADVANTAGE - Providing reduced viscosity and/or temperature conditions can advantageously enhance production. Electrochemical conversion has high conversion efficiency and low cost due to lower operating temperatures and use of non-noble catalysts. Electrochemical formation of glycerol carbonate from glycerol and potassium carbonate is demonstrated without high cost of conditions required for conventional chemical processes. Such carbon fixation, which uses captured carbon dioxide, can facilitate closing greenhouse emissions loop, reducing greenhouse accumulation in the atmosphere.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for: (1) system for producing glycerol carbonate (508) comprising electrochemical reaction unit which has reaction chamber, anode and cathode disposed within reaction chamber, inlet for providing electrolyte containing glycerol (401) and dissolved CO2 (402) into reaction chamber, power source coupled to anode and cathode and configured to create electric potential to induce electrochemical reduction and oxidation reactions and produce reaction mixture containing glycerol carbonate, and outlet for releasing reaction mixture; extractor which has reaction mixture inlet in fluid communication with outlet of electrochemical reaction unit for receiving reaction mixture; solvent inlet for receiving solvent capable of solubilizing glycerol carbonate, extraction chamber in fluid communication with reaction mixture inlet and solvent inlet and configured to enable direct contact between reaction mixture and solvent to enable glycerol carbonate to dissolve into solvent and produce glycerol carbonate depleted fraction containing glycerol and glycerol carbonate enriched fraction containing the solvent, 1st outlet for releasing glycerol carbonate enriched fraction, and 2nd outlet for releasing glycerol carbonate depleted fraction; and absorption reactor which has gas inlet for receiving CO2-containing gas, liquid inlet for receiving absorbent solution containing glycerol, carbonate salt, and material derived from glycerol carbonate depleted fraction, absorption chamber in fluid communication with gas inlet and liquid inlet and configured to enable direct contact between CO2-containing gas and absorbent solution to form loaded absorbent, and absorbent outlet for releasing loaded absorbent and being in fluid communication with inlet of electrochemical reaction unit such that loaded absorbent forms a part of the electrolyte; (2) electrochemical process for integrating CO2 into an ionizable organic compound to form reaction product, which involves providing electrolyte containing ionizable organic compound, salt, and CO2 in the form of CO2/carbonic-acid/bicarbonate/carbonate system, and applying electrochemical potential between anode and cathode immersed in electrolyte to induce simultaneous electrochemical reduction and oxidation reactions of CO2 and ionizable organic compound, respectively, and form the reaction product, and optionally, controlling electrolyte viscosity sufficiently low to enable free fluid flow of the electrolyte; (3) production of glycerol carbonate which involves providing electrolyte containing CO2 and glycerol in electrochemical reaction unit, electrochemically reducing CO2 to promote formation of glycerol carbonate in electrolyte, and controlling electrolyte viscosity sufficiently low to enable free fluid flow of electrolyte; and (4) production of glycerol carbonate which involves simultaneous electrochemical reduction of CO2 and oxidation of glycerol within a common electrolyte fluid, and allowing contact between electrochemical reaction products to form a reaction mixture comprising the glycerol carbonate.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a process flow diagram of the production of glycerol carbonate.
   Glycerol (401)
   Carbon dioxide (402)
   Bubble column reactor (406)
   Electrochemical cell (502)
   Glycerol carbonate (508)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: Electrolyte comprises water. Electrolyte comprises monovalent cations such that CO2 is partly in the form of dissolved CO2/bicarbonate/carbonate ions of the monovalent cations. Monovalent cations comprise potassium. Monovalent cations comprise sodium. Anode is made of nickel. Anode comprises a nickel coating. Cathode is made of nickel. Cathode comprises a nickel coating. Anode and cathode are made of and/or coated with non-noble metal or carbon that is in contact with the electrolyte. Absorbent solution comprises water, potassium, and glycerol. Gaseous CO2 is part of a gas stream containing other components. Other components consist of inert components and optional trace components. Gaseous CO2 is part of a gas stream that is substantially pure CO2 gas. Salt comprises potassium carbonate or sodium carbonate. Electrolyte contains alkaline metal hydroxide prepared with dissolved CO2. Preferred Processes: The method further includes providing electrolyte at an electrolyte temperature of greater than or equal to 40 degrees C, preferably 35-50 degrees C. The method further includes reducing electrolyte viscosity by heating electrolyte and/or diluting electrolyte. The method further includes controlling electrolyte viscosity at less than or equal to 300 cP. The method further includes producing loaded solution containing CO2 and supplying loaded solution to electrochemical reaction unit. Loaded solution is produced by supplying CO2-containing gas to an absorption reactor; supplying absorbent solution to absorption reactor; directly contacting CO2-containing gas and absorbent solution in the absorption reactor to cause CO2 gas to dissolve in the absorbent solution and form bicarbonate/carbonate ions; and withdrawing loaded solution from absorption reactor. CO2 is introduced to electrochemical cell as a gas. CO2 is provided by injecting gaseous CO2 directly into the electrolyte. Gaseous CO2 is injected in the form of bubbles. Bubbles are injected at a bottom of the electrochemical reaction unit. Gaseous CO2 is provided with a gas temperature for heating the electrolyte. Gaseous CO2 is injected via a gas diffuser. Gaseous CO2 is injected into a bottom of the electrochemical reaction unit in spaced-apart relation from the anode and the cathode. CO2 is introduced via a gas permeable membrane that is in contact with the electrolyte. The method further includes mixing electrolyte while electrochemical potential is applied between anode and cathode to induce fluid flow. Mixing is performed by mixing element disposed within electrochemical reaction unit. The method further includes removing water from process stream, a part of which is used as a part of the electrolyte. Electrolyte viscosity is controlled by heating electrolyte above a threshold temperature of 30 degrees C, 35 degrees C, or 40 degrees C. Electrolyte viscosity is controlled by diluting electrolyte above a dilution threshold. Dilution is performed with water. Electrolyte viscosity is performed to provide electrolyte viscosity below 600 cP, 500 cP, 400 cP, 300 cP, 250 cP, 200 cP, or 150 cP. Preferred Device: Absorption reactor is a bubble column reactor. Preferred Conditions: Absorption reactor is operated at 15-70 degrees C, preferably 20-40 degrees C. Absorption reactor is operated at substantially atmospheric pressure conditions. Electrochemical reaction unit is operated at a pressure of 0-5 bars, preferably 1-4 bars. Electrochemical reaction is operated at a reaction temperature of 100-120 degrees C. Electrochemical reaction temperature is below boiling point of electrolyte. Electrochemical reaction is performed without pressurization. Dilution threshold is 5 vol.% water, 10 vol.% water, 15 vol.% water, 20 vol.% water, or 25 vol.% water. Preferred Parameters: Electrolyte is basic with pH of 8-14, preferably 10-14. Electrolyte is mildly acidic with pH of 5-7.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Processes: The method further includes separating glycerol carbonate from electrolyte by withdrawing reaction mixture containing electrolyte and glycerol carbonate from electrochemical reaction unit; and subjecting reaction mixture to solvent extraction by contacting reaction mixture with solvent capable of solubilizing the glycerol to produce glycerol carbonate depleted fraction containing glycerol and glycerol carbonate enriched fraction containing solvent. Solvent extraction involves supplying reaction mixture and solvent to extractor to promote transfer of glycerol carbonate into the solvent phase; and removing glycerol carbonate depleted fraction and glycerol carbonate enriched fraction from extractor. Reaction mixture and solvent are passed counter-currently with respect to each other in the extractor. The method further includes subjecting glycerol carbonate enriched fraction to solvent recovery to produce recovered solvent fraction and glycerol carbonate fraction. The method further includes recycling a portion of recovered solvent fraction back into the solvent extraction. Solvent recovery comprises distillation. Solvent recovery is performed in a packed distillation column. The method further includes recovering glycerol from a portion of glycerol carbonate depleted fraction. Glycerol is recovered by supplying glycerol carbonate depleted fraction to evaporator to produce condensate stream and glycerol enriched stream. The method further includes removing water from glycerol carbonate depleted fraction to produce glycerol enriched stream. Water is removed by evaporation. Water is removed by contacting glycerol carbonate depleted fraction with absorbent material that absorbs a portion of the water to produce a water-loaded absorbent material. The method further includes separating water-loaded absorbent material from glycerol enriched stream, subjecting water-loaded absorbent material to regeneration to remove water and produce regenerated absorbent material, and reintroducing regenerated absorbent material for contacting glycerol carbonate depleted fraction. The method further includes supplying a portion of glycerol enriched stream back into absorption reactor as part of the absorbent solution. The method further includes adding glycerol make-up stream to glycerol enriched stream upstream of absorption reactor to form absorbent solution. Step of removing water is performed on glycerol carbonate depleted fraction to produce water-depleted glycerol enriched stream. Step of removing water is performed on liquid solution before feeding the same into the electrochemical reaction unit. Step of removing water is performed on reaction mixture withdrawn from electrochemical reaction unit before solvent extraction. CO2/carbonic-acid/bicarbonate/carbonate system is supplied as liquid solution into an electrochemical reaction unit. Preferred Components: Solvent comprises an alcohol, e.g. propanol or isopropanol. Electrolyte is free of amine- based and/or carbamate-forming compounds. Electrolyte consists essentially of glycerol, CO2 in the form of CO2/carbonic-acid/bicarbonate/carbonate system, and potassium or sodium cations, and optionally water. Liquid solution is the loaded absorbent produced by the absorption reactor. Ionizable organic compound contains alcohol, and reaction product contains carbonate ester. Ionizable organic compound contains glycerol, and reaction product contains glycerol carbonate. Alcohol contains methanol, and carbonate ester compound comprises dimethyl carbonate. Alcohol comprises diol and/or triol. Alcohol comprises aryl or alkyl group. Preferred Parameter: Ionizable organic compound has viscosity of greater than or equal to 3900 cP at 10 degrees C, greater than or equal to 1400 cP at 20 degrees C, and/or greater than or equal to 600 cP at 30 degrees C.

PN US2022314787-A1
TI Integrated fill system for housing material used            as fuel source for vehicle, such as bus, has draw line            whose first end is coupled to second end of container            through second valve, where second valve allows flow of            compressed gas out of container.
AU RIKE J B
AE WORTHINGTON IND INC (WORT-Non-standard)
GA 2022C7804K
AB    NOVELTY - The integrated fill system (100) has a                container (102) to house compressed gas, where the                container has two ends (101,103) opposite to each                other. Two neck mounts receive the ends of the                container and restrict movement. A housing (104)                encases the container, and the neck mounts. A fill                line (106) has an end coupled to one of the ends by                a first two-way valve (110), where the first valve                allows flow of the compressed gas into the                container. A filter is arranged proximate to the                fill line to prevent contaminants e.g. carbon                dioxide, from entering the container by the filter.                A draw line (108) includes a first end and a second                end, where the first end is coupled to the second                end of the container by a second valve (112) that                is configured to allow flow of the compressed gas                out of the container.
   USE - Integrated fill system for housing a material                used as a fuel source for a vehicle. Uses include                but are not limited to a semi-truck, a semi-trailer                truck, a tractor-trailer, a transfer truck, an                18-wheeler, a truck, a class 8 vehicle, an                automobile, farm equipment, industrial equipment,                construction equipment, van, Sport Utility Vehicle                (SUV), a truck that carriers a load and/or freight,                a cement truck, a delivery truck, a tractor, a                flat-bed truck, and a bus.
   ADVANTAGE - The system utilizes an integrated fill system                for a container of a vehicle that utilizes an                alternative fuel as a fuel source, so that the                vehicle can utilize a combustible fuel engine, a                battery powered engine, or a combination of                combustible and battery powered engines in an                efficient manner.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a fuel                storage system for a vehicle.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the                integrated fill system.
   100Integrated fill system
   101,103Ends of container
   102Container
   104Housing
   106fill line

   112Second valve

PT P
PN US2022259130-A1; WO2022232493-A1
TI Producing alkaline-earth oxide and carboxylic acid            from alkaline earth cation-carboxylic acid anion salt            involves separating alkaline-earth-sulfite solid from            aqueous carboxylic acid solution, and calcining            alkaline-earth-sulfite solid to produce            alkaline-earth-oxide.
AU NOVEK E
AE INNOVATOR ENERGY LLC (INNO-Non-standard)
   INNOVATOR ENERGY LLC (INNO-Non-standard)
GA 2022A5781N
AB    NOVELTY - Producing an alkaline-earth oxide and a                carboxylic acid from an alkaline earth                cation-carboxylic acid anion salt involves reacting                an alkaline-earth cation-carboxylic acid anion salt                with aqueous sulfur dioxide to produce aqueous                alkaline-earth-bisulfite and aqueous carboxylic                acid solution, desorbing sulfur dioxide from the                aqueous alkaline-earth-bisulfite and aqueous                carboxylic acid solution to produce                alkaline-earth-sulfite solid and aqueous carboxylic                acid solution; separating the                alkaline-earth-sulfite solid from the aqueous                carboxylic acid solution; and calcining the                alkaline-earth-sulfite solid to produce                alkaline-earth-oxide, or cement.
   USE - Method for producing an alkaline-earth oxide                and a carboxylic acid from an alkaline earth                cation-carboxylic acid anion salt.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is cost effective, reduces energy                consumption (OPEX) and construction (CAPEX) cost.                The method does not require an air separation unit                or post-combustion carbon dioxide (CO2) capture to                produce pure CO2. The pure CO2 is produced at a                high pressure and/or relatively low temperature                and/or with relatively low water vapor                concentration.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for:
   a. a method for producing calcium oxide and                carboxylic acid from a calcium cation-carboxylic                acid anion salt, which involves: i. reacting a                calcium cation-carboxylic acid anion salt with                aqueous sulfur dioxide to produce aqueous calcium                bisulfite and aqueous carboxylic acid; ii.                desorbing sulfur dioxide from aqueous calcium                bisulfite and aqueous carboxylic acid solution to                produce calcium sulfite solid and aqueous                carboxylic acid solution; iii. separating calcium                sulfite solid from aqueous carboxylic acid                solution; and iv. calcining calcium sulfite solid                to produce calcium oxide, or cement; and
   b. a method for producing calcium oxide and                citric acid from calcium citrate, which involves                reacting calcium citrate with aqueous sulfur                dioxide to produce aqueous calcium bisulfite and                aqueous citric acid; desorbing sulfur dioxide from                the aqueous calcium bisulfite and aqueous citric                acid solution to produce calcium sulfite solid and                aqueous citric acid solution; separating the                calcium sulfite solid from the aqueous citric acid                solution; and calcining the calcium sulfite solid                to produce calcium oxide, or cement.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The alkaline-earth                  cation comprises calcium cation and magnesium                  cation.Preferred Conditions: The aqueous carboxylic                  acid solution is separated into isolated                  carboxylic acid and water. Sulfur dioxide is                  absorbed with water to generate aqueous sulfur                  dioxide. The method further involves absorbing                  the desorbed sulfur dioxide in water to produce                  aqueous sulfur dioxide, calcining the                  alkaline-earth-sulfite solid produces sulfur                  dioxide, and reacting the alkaline-earth-oxide                  with water to produce alkaline-earth-hydroxide.                  The reaction also produces heat. The                  alkaline-earth-hydroxide is further reacted with                  a solution comprising a carboxylic acid to form                  alkaline-earth cation-carboxylic acid anion salt,                  where at least a portion of the alkaline-earth                  cation-carboxylic acid anion salt is a solid. The                  alkaline-earth cation-carboxylic acid anion salt                  is separated using solid-liquid separation. The                  process is conducted in the presence of a low                  diatomic oxygen environment. The calcination is                  conducted under conditions to substantially                  prevent the formation of sulfate. The method                  further involves producing a precipitated                  alkaline-earth-carbonate from the                  alkaline-earth-hydroxide. The method for                  producing an alkaline-earth oxide and a                  carboxylic acid from an alkaline earth                  cation-carboxylic acid anion salt further                  involves producing a precipitated                  alkaline-earth-carbonate from                  alkaline-earth-hydroxide. The precipitated                  alkaline-earth-carbonate comprises calcium                  carbonate, magnesium carbonate, or both. The                  alkaline-earth-hydroxide is dissolved in water.                  The alkaline-earth-hydroxide comprises calcium                  hydroxide. The isolated water comprises dissolved                  sulfur dioxide.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The carboxylic acid                  anion salt comprises citric acid anion                  salt.

PT P
PN US2022250954-A1
TI Flow through electrode, capacitive deionization            system for effectively removing nitrate from mixture of            ions in fluid, comprises water permeable dielectric            sandwiched between the electrodes, and electrical            circuit for applying direct current voltage across the            electrodes.
AU STADERMANN M
   PHAM T A
   LOEB C
   HAWKS S
   HERNANDEZ M C
   CAMPBELL P
AE LAWRENCE LIVERMORE NAT SECURITY LLC (USGO-C)
GA 2022A1986T
AB    NOVELTY - Flow through electrode, capacitive                deionization (FTE-CDI) system (100) comprises a                pair of electrodes (102, 104) arranged generally                parallel to one another, a water permeable                dielectric (114) arranged between the electrodes so                as to be sandwiched between the electrodes, an                electrical circuit (110) for applying a direct                current voltage across the electrodes, and at least                one of the electrodes formed from a carbon material                having a hierarchical pore size distribution. The                hierarchical pore size distribution includes a                first multiple nano-sized pores having a width of                no more than about 1 nm, and a second multiple                pores having micron-sized pores that enable a flow                of water to be pushed through the electrode. The                first multiple pores form adsorption sites for                nitrate molecules carried in the water flowing                through the at least one electrode.
   USE - Flow through electrode, capacitive                deionization (FTE-CDI) system for effectively                removing nitrate from a mixture of ions in fluid                (water) flowing through the system.
   ADVANTAGE - The system uses a new carbon material which                has a hierarchical pore size distribution, that                includes multiple sub-nanometer scale pores to                provide adsorption sites, while another set of                pores are included which form micron-sized pores                through which water can be pushed at relevant flow                rates without requiring a substantial amount of                energy. The material can be used in a different                geometry, so rather than passing water between the                electrodes, the water is pushed through the                electrodes. The salt is actively pushed into and                out of the capacitor, which reduces desalination                time substantially.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method                for making a carbon aerogel electrode material,                which involves: i. making a wet organic sol-gel                form; ii. carbonizing the sol-gel form at a                temperature of 900-1000&#176;C, for 2-4 hours;                and iii. activating the carbonized sol-gel under                carbon dioxide flow, for 0.5-1.5 hours, at                900-1000&#176;C.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a high level block diagram                of a FTE-CDI system.
   100Flow through electrode, capacitive                deionization (FTE-CDI) system
   102,104Electrodes
   110Electrical circuit
   114Water permeable dielectric
   138,140Current collectors
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: Both of the pair of                  electrodes comprise carbon material having the                  hierarchical pore size distribution. The                  electrodes comprise a carbon aerogel. The                  dielectric comprises a non-conductive, water                  permeable paper, or a polymer membrane which                  allows a flow of water. The header plates                  comprises an ultraviolet transparent acrylic                  material. The wet organic sol-gel form is a                  resorcinol-formaldehyde sol-gel. Preferred                  Process: The method for making a wet organic                  sol-gel form involves making an aqueous solution                  of resorcinol and formaldehyde, mixing the                  aqueous solution with an alcohol (preferably                  methanol) and an acid (preferably acetic acid) to                  make a sol-gel precursor, curing the sol-gel                  precursor in a mold, and aging the sol-gel. The                  wet organic sol-gel form is a sheet having a                  thickness of 300-700 &#956;m. The sheet is                  cut from a block of aged resorcinol-formaldehyde                  sol-gel.

First end                of a supporting element (4) is fixedly connected to                the single stone layer, and second end of the                supporting element is supported on a sapling. A                solid layer wedge (5) penetrates through the single                stone layer, and is wedged into the river bank.                Adjacent single stone layers are fixed by tying a                steel wire.
   USE - Waterproof soil loss device for riparian                vegetation restoration.
   ADVANTAGE - The soil surface is fixed through the single                stone layer, water and soil loss under rainwater                washing is reduced, the root system of the sapling                grows downwards through the blocking of the root                bundling barrel, the root puncturing depth is                improved, the sapling is supported through the                supporting element, and the lodging resistance of                the sapling is improved. The mounting difficulty is                reduced, the sliding during laying of the crushed                stone is reduced, the using amount of the crushed                stone is correspondingly reduced, the environment                is protected, the root bundling barrel is formed of                starch plastic, the carbon dioxide is formed after                the starch plastic is decomposed, and the harm to                the soil is reduced.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic diagram of a                waterproof soil loss device for riparian vegetation                restoration.

TI Synthesis of zinc dicarboxylate catalyst for            synthesizing zinc-based compound, involves adding base            solution to zinc precursor solution, separating            precipitate from supernatant solution and reacting            zinc-based compound with dicarboxylic acid.
AU TANG Z
   PESCARMONA P P
   SARTORE L
   PANDINI S
   GNALI L
   SALA G
AE EPOX-CO2 SRL (EPOX-Non-standard)
   RIJKSUNIV GRONINGEN (UYRJ-C)
   UNIV BRESCIA (UYBR-Non-standard)
GA 2022791286
AB    NOVELTY - Synthesis of zinc dicarboxylate catalyst                involves providing a solution of zinc precursor                (I), adding a base solution to the zinc precursor                solution and obtaining a precipitate, separating                the precipitate from the supernatant solution and                heat treating the obtained solid at 30-90 degrees C                for 20-60 hours, preferably 150-400 degrees C for                20 minutes-20 hours, obtaining a zinc-based                compound containing a mixture of zinc oxide and                zinc hydroxide-based compound (II) and reacting the                zinc based compound with a saturated aliphatic                dicarboxylic acid.
   USE - Synthesis of zinc dicarboxylate catalyst for                synthesizing zinc-based compound and polyalkylene                carbonate (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method provides zinc dicarboxylate                catalyst having high degree of purity and high                selectivity and excellent catalytic activity                towards the heterogeneous catalytic                copolymerization reaction for the synthesis of                polyalkylene carbonate.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Synthesis of zinc dicarboxylate catalyst                involves providing a solution of zinc precursor of                formula (I): Zn(L)2-nH2O, adding a base solution to                the solution of the zinc precursor and obtaining a                precipitate, separating the precipitate from the                supernatant solution and heat treating the obtained                solid at 30-90 degrees C for 20-60 hours,                preferably 150-400 degrees C for 20 minutes-20                hours, obtaining a zinc-based compound containing a                mixture of zinc oxide and zinc hydroxide-based                compound of formula (II): Znx(OH)y(L)z.nH2O and                reacting the zinc based compound with a saturated                aliphatic dicarboxylic acid.
   n = 0-20;
   L = NO3, CH3CO2, (SO4)0.5, halide or acetyl                acetone;
   x = 1-5;
   y = 1-8;and
   z = 1-2.
   INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the                following:
   (1) synthesis of zinc-based compound;                and
   (2) synthesis of polyalkylene carbonate by                heterogeneous catalytic copolymerization reaction,                which involves reacting carbon dioxide with an                alkyl epoxide containing at least 3 carbon atoms,                preferably in the absence of an organic solvent and                in the presence of the zinc dicarboxylate catalyst                and heating at 30-150 degrees C at pressure of 1-80                bar for 1-40 hours.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Property: The mass ratio of the                  zinc oxide to the zinc hydroxide-based compound                  (II) is 0.1-10, preferably 0.2-5.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Component: The synthesis of zinc                  carboxylate catalyst selected from zinc                  glutarate, zinc adipate and zinc succinate and                  the dicarboxylic acid is selected from glutaric                  acid, adipic acid and succinic acid. The                  polyalkylene carbonate is polypropylene carbonate                  and the alkyl epoxide is propylene oxide.                  Preferred Process: Synthesis of polyalkylene                  carbonate involves heating at 30-150 degrees C at                  pressure of 1-80 bar for 1-40 hours.

The second                composite yarn color mixing unit contains 14 weft                yarns.

The degradable                polyester yarn-dyed fabric which is beneficial to                degradation is more environment-friendly. in the                basic tissue 311/111 composite twill, the warp and                weft direction tissue circulation is only 8, the                scheme adopts degradable yarn-dyed yarns, the                tissue circulation is expanded by multiple times,                and the pattern is expanded by multiple times, so                that the fabric tissue structure is full and not                rigid. The fabric can be undyed, the discharge of a                large amount of waste water and carbon dioxide is                reduced, the fabric is environment-friendly and                healthy, and the color fastness of the fabric is                high.

AU BEYER K
   GRIMM K D
AE GRIMMS GARDENS LLC (GRIM-Non-standard)
GA 202257589P
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves receiving water from subsurface soil into a dripline through an emitter during a collection period (402). A pressurized air source is connected (404) to the subsurface irrigation system. Pressurized air is introduced (414) into the dripline during a flush period. The water is expelled from the subsurface irrigation system through a flush valve with pressurized air, where the collection period lasts between 1-12 hours in between flush periods. The subsurface irrigation system is provided with a flush pipe that is connected with the driplines at a first end.
   USE - Method for aerating and drying subsurface soils with a subsurface irrigation system for micro-irrigation of plant crops, gardens, orchards and other food and non-food applications.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables actively and passively removing water from the subsurface soil, drying wet spots caused on fields by higher precipitation or localized flooding to improve soil dynamics through aerating and water removal from soils, utilizing a gas delivery system for agrochemicals in proximity to plant root mat zones to limit crop yield stress by drying high-water holding capacity soils, such as loam or silty clay loans, aiding plant growth, waste management, carbon tax credits, and increased yields by utilizing reclaimed industrial byproduct, such as carbon dioxide to reduce amount of total nutrients, such as nitrogen, phosphorus, potassium, sulfur, molybdenum, boron, and zinc, that may leach to lower levels of the soil profile, and hence reducing wait times for planting or harvesting crops due to moist fields.

The remainder of the composition is iron and unavoidable impurities resulting from the smelting. The steel sheet has a microstructure comprising in surface fraction, 0-45% ferrite; 20-50% retained austenite; 5-80% annealed martensite; less than or equal to 5% fresh martensite; a carbon (C)A and manganese (MP)A content in austenite, expressed in wt.%, such that the ratio (((C)A)2\(MP)A)/((C%)2x Mn%) is 4.5-11.0; and a carbides density of less than or equal to 4x 106/mm2.

The microstructure comprises 5-25% ferrite, 25-50% retained austenite and 25-70 wt.% annealed martensite. The ferrite is present and is equiaxed. The microstructure comprises no ferrite, 25-45% retained austenite and 55-75% annealed martensite. The steel has a carbon equivalent Ceq of less than or equal to 0.4%. the carbon equivalent being defined as Ceq=C% + Si%/55 + Cr%/20 + Mn%/19 -Al%/18 + 2.2P% - 3.24B% - 0.133x Mn%x Mo% with elements being expressed by wt.%. Preferred Parameters: The tensile strength is greater than or equal to 900 MPa; the uniform elongation UE is greater than or equal to 11.0%; the yield strength YS is greater than or equal to 700 MPa and the total elongation TE, YS, UE, and TS are such that ((YS-200)x UE +(TS - 300)x TE)/(C%x Mn%) is greater than or equal to 29 000. The liquid metal embrittlement (LME) index is less than or equal to 0.36.

AE WUXI HUITONG LIGHTWEIGHT MATERIALS CO (WUXI-Non-standard)
GA 202219507S
AB    NOVELTY - Beads, prepared by composite particles through                high temperature autoclave foaming method, is                claimed. The composite particles include a core                layer and a skin layer, the core layer includes                40-60 wt.% polypropylene A, 20-40 wt.%                polypropylene B, 15-30 wt.% polypropylene C, 0.05-2                wt.% expansion aid, 0.05-0.5 wt.% cell nucleating                agent and 0.5-3 wt.% maleic anhydride modified                polypropylene, and the skin layer includes 90-100                wt.% polyolefin D, and 0-10 wt.% other auxiliary                agents.
   USE - The beads is useful for heat preservation and                heat insulation, buffer package and automobile                light weight field.
   ADVANTAGE - The beads: is energy-saving, and expanded; has                15-120 g/l bulk density, 8-15 J/g melting peak                endothermic enthalpy value of first DSC melting                curve higher than intrinsic melting point, good                compatibility and excellent apparent quality.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included                for:
   (1) preparing beads, comprising (i) mixing                polypropylene A, polypropylene B, polypropylene C,                co-expansion agent, cell nucleating agent, and                maleic anhydride modified polypropylene, twin-screw                extruding and granulating to obtain core layer                masterbatch, (ii) uniformly mixing polyolefin D and                other additives, twin-screw extruding and                granulating to obtain skin layer masterbatch, (iii)                adding core layer masterbatch and skin layer                masterbatch to core layer extruder and skin layer                extruder of twin-single-screw co-extrusion unit,                plasticizing, simultaneously spinning and                granulating co-extrusion die to obtain composite                particles with skin layer covering core layer, and                (iv) adding composite particles together with                dispersant and water into autoclave, adding carbon                dioxide after sealing, keep stirring, raising                temperature of autoclave to foaming temperature,                adjusting pressure in kettle to foaming pressure,                keeping at this temperature and pressure for 5-30                minutes, releasing pressure and discharging                material into normal pressure foaming pipeline for                foaming, where the temperature of atmosphere in                foaming pipe is 85-98degreesC, and the time for                material in foaming pipe is 4-15 seconds, obtaining                expanded beads.
   (2) molded part of expanded polypropylene                beads.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The melting point of                  polypropylene A is 120-130degrees Celsius, and                  the melt index is 5-10 g/10 minutes, the melting                  point of polypropylene B is 130-140degrees                  Celsius, and the melt index is 5-12 g/10 minutes,                  the melting point of polypropylene C is                  140-152degrees Celsius, and the melt index is                  5-30 g/10 minutes, and the melting point of                  polyolefin D is 104-122degrees Celsius, and the                  melt index is 4-15 g/10 minutes. The                  polypropylene A, polypropylene B and                  polypropylene C is random copolymerization                  polypropylene, preferably the product of                  metallocene catalyst series, the flexural modulus                  of polypropylene A is greater than or equal to700                  MPa, the flexural modulus of polypropylene B is                  greater than or equal to900 MPa, the flexural                  modulus of polypropylene C is greater than or                  equal to1200 MPa, the polyolefin D includes                  LLDPE, LDPE, HDPE, ternary random copolymer PP,                  POE, POP and/or OBC. The cell nucleating agent                  includes polytetrafluoroethylene powder, zinc                  borate, talc, barium sulfate, calcium carbonate,                  calcium stearate and/or silicon dioxide, and the                  particle size of cell nucleating agent is 10-15                  mum. The auxiliary expanding agent is hydrophilic                  substance. The auxiliary expanding agent is                  potassium aluminum sulfate, and the average                  particle size is 1-15 mum. The skin layer                  accounts for 3-8% of the total mass of composite                  particles, and the length of composite particles                  is 1.2-2.5 mm, and the unit weight is 0.5-1.8 mg.                  The molding pressure of molded part is less than                  or equal to1.4 bar, and the molding production                  can be carried out on an EPS molding machine, the                  surface quality of molded part is excellent, the                  density of the molded part is 58-62 g/l, and the                  compressive stress at 50% strain is greater than                  or equal to500 KPa.

TI Preparing trophoblast utilizing genetic engineering modification comprises e.g. constructing pLV-mbIL-21-OX40LCD48 lentiviral vector plasmid, extracting recombinant lentivirus, infecting K562 cells, sorting and subcloning, and sterilizing to obtain K562-ZHX trophoblast cells.
AU YE H
   WU J
   XIA Y
AE ZHONGHAIXIA FUJIAN CELL BIOTECHNOLOGY CO (ZHON-Non-standard)
GA 202207802R
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing trophoblast utilizing genetic engineering modification comprises (1) constructing pLV-mbIL-21-OX40LCD48 lentiviral vector plasmid, (2) using pLV-mbIL-21-OX40L-CD48 lentiviral vector plasmid to prepare recombinant lentivirus, and (3) extracting recombinant lentivirus, infecting K562 cells, sorting and subcloning, and sterilizing to obtain K562-ZHX trophoblast cells.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing trophoblast utilizing genetic engineering modification, which is used for in vitro expansion and culture of natural killer (NK) cells (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method: can prepare and utilize K562 cells to stably express membrane-bound interleukin 21 (mbIL21), OX40L molecule and CD48 molecule at the same time to prepare an engineered cell line, which is K562ZHX trophoblast, then irradiated the engineered cell line with cobalt 60 to obtain trophoblast-expanded NK cells, the NK cell activity obtained by method is increased by 3 times and the cell expansion can be increased by more than 300 times, can save a lot of time and cost, and can lay a practical foundation for obtaining NK cells with high purity, high expansion multiple and high killing activity.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for in vitro expansion and culture of NK cells, comprising (S6) extracting PBMC cells, and (S7) In vitro expansion of NK cells: (S71) taking K562-ZHX trophoblasts, reviving them in a water bath at 37degrees Celsius, washing using phosphate-buffered saline and centrifuging to obtain washed K562-ZHX trophoblasts, (S72) adding PBMC cells to the culture flask containing NK cell complete medium, resuspending, then adding the washed K562-ZHX trophoblast cells, uniformly mixing to form cultured cell solution I, transferring the cultured cell liquid I to an incubator, cultivating at 37degrees Celsius under 5% carbon dioxide conditions, when the culture reaches the third day, centrifuging and discarding the supernatant, adding NK cell complete medium, and continuing to culture after uniformly mixing, and (S73) when cultured to the 7th day, adding NK cell complete medium to the culture flask to make the cell density of PBMC cells 2x106 cells/ml, adding the washed K562-ZHX trophoblasts, uniformly mixing the cells, and culturing for 7 days to obtain NK cells.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Method: The step (1) specifically comprises (S1) obtaining the gene coding sequences of human IL-21, human OX40L, and human CD48, (S2) connecting the coding sequence of human IL-21 gene, the coding sequence of PGK promoter, the coding sequence of human OX40L gene, the coding sequence of T2A self-cleaving peptide, and the coding sequence of CD48 gene in series, and obtaining mbIL-21-PGK-OX40LT2A-CD48 gene sequence, where the mbIL-21-PGK-OX40L-T2ACD48 gene sequence is (SEQ ID NO. 6), and (S3) constructing the mbIL-21-PGK-OX40L-T2A-CD48 gene sequence into a plasmid vector to obtain the pLV-mbIL-21-OX40L-CD48 lentiviral vector plasmid. The step (S3) specifically comprises (S31) carrying out BamH I, Xmal I double enzyme digestion with mbIL-21-PGK-OX40L-T2A-CD48 gene sequence, and electrophoresis recovery, (S32) double digesting the lentiviral transfer plasmid pLV-EF1a-IRES-Puro with BamH I and Xmal I, and recovering the linearized backbone plasmid by electrophoresis, and (S33) connecting the digested mbIL-21-PGK-OX40L-T2A-CD48 gene to the digested pLV-EF1a-IRES-Puro backbone plasmid by Ligation High Ver2 ligase, transforming into competent E.coli (DH5alpha) cells and purifying to obtain pLVmbIL-21-OX40L-CD48 lentiviral vector plasmid. The step (2) is specifically: (S4) preparing recombinant lentivirus: (S41) inoculating 293T cells in a culture plate containing DMEM complete culture medium, and incubating at 37degrees Celsius under 5% carbon dioxide conditions to obtain a culture plate containing 293T cells, (S42) the next day, mixing the pLVmbIL-21-OX40L-CD48 lentiviral vector plasmid, psPAX-1 plasmid, and pMD2.G plasmid, diluting the Opti-MEM medium to 1 mug/mul to obtain a dilution, taking tube A, adding serum-free Opti-MEM medium and transfection reagent, vortexing and mixing for 10 seconds to obtain solution A, taking tube B, adding serum-free Opti-MEM medium, diluent, and P3000 reagent, vortexing for 10 seconds to obtain solution B, thoroughly mixing solution A and solution B, incubating at room temperature for 15 minutes, dropping into a culture plate containing 293T cells, incubating at 37degrees Celsius under 5% carbon dioxide for 6-8 hours, discarding the culture medium in the culture plate, adding DMEM complete culture medium, and continuing to incubate at 37degrees Celsius and 5% carbon dioxide, (S43) culturing for 24 hours, collecting the first batch of cell supernatants, adding DMEM complete medium, and continuing to incubate at 37degrees Celsius and 5% carbon dioxide, and (S44) culturing for 52 hours, collecting the second batch of cell supernatant, mixing with the first batch of cell supernatant and centrifuging, collecting and transferring the supernatant, and (S5) concentrating the supernatant to obtain a recombinant lentivirus. The step (3) specifically comprises sequentially adding the recombinant lentivirus and polybrene according to the final MOI value of the composite recombinant lentivirus is 5, uniformly mixing to form a recombinant lentivirus mixture, infecting K562 cells with the recombinant lentivirus mixture, sorting and sub-cloning for 72 hours after infection to obtain the K562-ZHX engineered cell line, and sterilizing the K562-ZHX engineered cell line by cobalt-60 irradiation to obtain K562-ZHX trophoblast cells. The step (S5) specifically comprises filtering the collected supernatant, adding Lenti-XConcentrator, incubating at 4degrees Celsius for 12 hours, then centrifuging at 1500 revolutions/minute for 45 minutes, discarding the supernatant, resuspending after dilution, sub-packing to obtain recombinant lentivirus. In the step (S5) performing titer detection after concentration, and the specific steps of titer detection are as follows: infecting k562 cells with the recombinant lentivirus, and detecting the titer of the recombinant lentivirus by flow cytometry. When NK cells are expanded in vitro, the number of PBMC cells and K562-ZHX trophoblasts is 1:1, and the cell density ratio of PBMC cells and K562-ZHX trophoblasts is 1:1.Preferred Components: In the step (S1), the gene coding sequence of human IL-21 comprises fully defined sequence of 462 nucleotides (SEQ ID NO. 1), the gene coding sequence of human OX40L comprises fully defined sequence of 552 nucleotides (SEQ ID NO. 2), and the gene coding sequence of human CD48 comprises fully defined sequence of 732 nucleotides (SEQ ID NO. 3).

AB    NOVELTY - The system has a common model that is                accessible and configured by technicians during                setup of the BMS for the building. The common model                is set to define an ontology of multiple devices                installed at the building, and is configured to                receive hardware agnostic point information and                provide hardware agnostic control commands for                controlling multiple devices installed at the                building. A gateway is communicatively coupled to                devices installed at the building, and is                configured toreceive point information from the                devices installed at the building and convert the                received point information into hardware agnostic                point information before sending the hardware                agnostic point information to the common model. The                hardware agnostic control commands are received                from the common model, and the received hardware                agnostic control commands are converted into                hardware specific control commands before sending                the hardware specific control commands to the                appropriate devices installed at the                building.

PN CN113651725-A
TI System for preparing dicyandiamide by reuse of melamine tail gas, has reactor, filter, polymerization reactor, second filter, crystallizer and trap, reactor is connected to the filter, polymerization reactor, second filter sequence through the pipeline, reactor is connected to the flue gas system.
AU LI G
   QIN Y
   MO Y
   SONG G
   KONG D
   TANG Y
AE SICHUAN GOLDEN-ELEPHANT SINCERITY CHEM (SICH-Non-standard)
GA 2021D9679G
AB    NOVELTY - System for preparing dicyandiamide by reuse of melamine tail gas, has reactor, filter-1, polymerization reactor, filter-2, crystallizer and trap, reactor is connected to the filter-1, the polymerization reactor, and the filter-2 in sequence through the pipeline, connection of crystallizer and trap, the reactor is connected to the flue gas system, the process cold air is connected to the crystallizer through a cold air compressor, the trap is connected to the crystallizer through a cold gas compressor or connected to the reactor through a compressor.
   USE - System for preparing dicyandiamide by reuse of melamine tail gas.
   ADVANTAGE - Dicyandiamide solid obtained by the method is simple process flow, environmental protection, high raw material utilization rate, byproduct steam, low energy consumption and cost saving on the basis of ensuring high product quality and purity.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for method for preparing dicyandiamide by reusing melamine tail gas using the system involves passing the part of the melamine tail gas into the crystallizer through a cold compressor as process cold gas, and passing part is into the reactor, while a small amount of carbon dioxide gas is passed into the reactor, and the two react in the reactor to obtain cyanamide gas. The cyanamide gas enters the filter-1, after filtering the solid impurities carried out, passing into the polymerization reactor. Passing the cyanamide gas into the polymerization reactor is polymerized in the polymerization reactor to obtain dicyandiamide gas. Dicyandiamide gas enters the filter-2, after filtering the solid impurities carried out, it is sent to the crystallizer. After the process cold air of the air-conditioning compressor and mixing dicyandiamide gas in the crystallizer for cooling and crystallization, the gas entrains the dicyandiamide crystals into the trap to complete the gas-solid separation, and collecting the dicyandiamide solid, and using part of the separated gas is process gas is reused, and part of it is used as process tail gas after treatment.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: Melamine tail gas contains carbon dioxide 30-70%, ammonia 20-60% and balance of a small amount of isocyanic acid and nitrogen
