PN CN113522336-A
TI Preparing aluminum single atom-loaded            nitrogen-doped carbon material of catalyst used in            photothermal catalytic synthesis of cyclic carbonate,            involves mixing solution comprising 1,10-phenanthroline            and solvent and solution comprising aluminum source and            solvent, mixing with melamine and calcining.
AU LIN Y
   PENG H
   ZHANG Q
   YANG Q
   LU Z
   CHEN L
AE CHINESE ACAD SCI NINGBO MATERIALS TECHNO (CANM-C)
   NINGBO HANGZHOU BAY NEW MATERIALS RES (NING-Non-standard)
GA 2021C8872L
AB    NOVELTY - A preparation method of nitrogen-doped carbon                material loaded with aluminum single atom, involves                providing a solution (I) comprising                1,10-phenanthroline and solvent, providing a                solution (II) comprising an aluminum source and a                solvent, mixing the solution (I) and solution (II),                quickly mixing with melamine for grinding while                removing solvent to obtain solid composite, and                calcining the solid composite two times under inert                atmosphere.
   USE - Preparation method of aluminum single                atom-loaded nitrogen-doped carbon material of                catalyst used in photothermal catalytic synthesis                of cyclic carbonate (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables preparation of aluminum                single atom-loaded nitrogen-doped carbon material                with stable material structure and excellent                photo-thermal absorption and conversion effect, and                which is repeatedly used for more than 5 times, by                simple process. The catalyst activity is constant.                The stable, efficient and environmentally-friendly                heterogeneous catalyst is provided.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the                following: 1. the nitrogen-doped carbon material                loaded with aluminum single atom, which is prepared                by uniformly dispersing aluminum with a high                content in a monoatomic state in the nitrogen-doped                carbon material loaded with aluminum single atom,                where the content of aluminum single atom in the                nitrogen-doped carbon material loaded with aluminum                single atom is 10-20 wt.%, and the aluminum and                nitrogen in the nitrogen-doped carbon material                loaded with aluminum single atom forms an aluminum                nitride coordination structure; 2. the application                of nitrogen-doped carbon material loaded with                aluminum single atom in photothermal catalytic                synthesis of cyclic carbonate; 3. a catalyst for                photothermal catalytic synthesis of cyclic                carbonate, which comprises nitrogen-doped carbon                material loaded with aluminum single atom; and 4. a                method for photothermal catalytic synthesis of                cyclic carbonate, which involves providing a mixed                reaction system comprising an epoxy compound,                catalyst, a cocatalyst, and organic solvent,                introducing carbon dioxide into the mixed reaction                system under illumination condition, and stirring                at normal pressure for cycloaddition reaction to                obtain cyclic carbonate.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The molar ratio of                  the 1,10-phenanthroline to the aluminum source is                  (1-6):1. Preferred Components: The aluminum                  source includes aluminum nitrate. The co-catalyst                  comprises tetrabutylammonium bromide and/or                  tetrabutylammonium chloride. Preferred Process:                  The method further involves heating the solid                  composite at a selected heating rate of                  2-8degreesC/minute from room temperature to                  500-600degreesC under the inert atmosphere, and                  calcining for 2-4 hours to obtain an intermediate                  product, grinding the intermediate product under                  the inert atmosphere, raising the temperature                  from room temperature to 800-900degreesC at a                  selected heating rate, and calcining for 2-4                  hours to obtain nitrogen-doped carbon material                  loaded with aluminum single atom.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The solvent comprises                  one or combination of methanol, ethanol, and                  isopropanol. The epoxy compound comprises one of                  epibromohydrin, epichlorohydrin, glycidol,                  glycidyl phenyl ether, and styrene oxide. The                  organic solvent comprises one or more of                  acetonitrile, N,N-dimethylformamide, and                  N,N-dimethylacetamide. Preferred Property: The                  full-spectrum optical power density of the light                  source is 200-400 mW/cm2. Preferred Composition:                  The molar ratio of the solvent to aluminum source                  is (60-120):1. The molar ratio of the melamine to                  the aluminum source is (20-50):1. The ratio of                  epoxy compound, catalyst, co-catalyst and organic                  solvent is 1 mmol:(30-33) mg:0.1 mmoL:(2-3) mL.                  Preferred Conditions: The temperature of the                  cycloaddition reaction is 41-62degreesC. The                  stirring time at normal pressure is 8-36                  hours.

PN RU2756258-C1
TI GAS SEPARATION DEVICE FOR CREATING A BREATHABLE FIRE-SUPPRESSING HYPOXIC ATMOSPHERE.
AU KOTLIAR I K
AE KOTLIAR I K (KOTL-Individual)
GA 2021B5675S
AB    NOVELTY - Invention relates to fire-fighting equipment, namely to methods and devices for the prevention and exclusion of fire, fire containment with a limited supply of extinguishing agent in the form of a life-supporting hypoxic atmosphere controlled by signals from the danger zone. The gas separation device for creating a breathable fire-suppressing hypoxic atmosphere contains a working cabinet in which a gas distribution module is located, connected to a compressed air supply compressor with a pressure of 3-11 bar, equipped with a compressed air dryer and fine filters, while the gas separation module is made with the possibility of separating compressed air according to the degree of oxygen saturation into permeate-oxygen-enriched composition of the atmosphere and retentate-hypoxic composition, depleted in oxygen, with the possibility of its separation into a gas mixture, including: oxygen from 10% to 16%; inert gases; water vapor; carbon dioxide in quantities suitable for breathing with constant use as a fire-preventing atmosphere, or a gas mixture with an oxygen content of 9% to 12% for occasional use as a fire extinguishing agent. The gas separation device contains a gas separation module, including a hollow housing, in which a block of hollow-fiber tubular membranes is placed, consisting of a bundle of porous polymer fiber with a 10-100 nm thick gas separation layer applied to the outer surface, with the possibility of supplying a separated fraction: permeate of the separated gas into the inter-tube space and a hypoxic fraction: retentate in the form of oxygen-depleted air-into the outlet cavity of the gas distribution module. The block of hollow fiber membranes is hermetically fixed with the ends by means of epoxy collectors in the specified housing. The gas separation module is designed to operate in three modes of separation of the input air. The gas separation device is equipped with throttling means in the form of a system of pressure adjustment valves and oxygen content settings, and connected to a control panel equipped with oxygen sensors installed in protected rooms, it is equipped with a sensor and a temperature and humidity controller.
   USE - Fire protection.
   ADVANTAGE - Increase in the fire protection of premises while maintaining the life support of the atmosphere.

TI Iron bath melting reduction process gas modifying device useful in e.g. metallurgy industry, comprises melting reducing furnace with outlet flue and hot air furnace with flue gas outlet, where coal powder blowing system comprises coal powder spray gun and coal powder pipeline.
AU ZHANG Q
   MA Y
   YUAN X
   WEI Z
   ZHANG G
AE SHANDONG MOLONG PETROLEUM MACHINERY CO L (SHAN-Non-standard)
GA 2021B4022Y
AB    NOVELTY - Iron bath melting reduction process gas modifying device comprises melting reducing furnace with outlet flue and hot air furnace with flue gas outlet. A coal powder blowing system provided with coal powder spray gun and coal powder pipeline. The coal powder spraying gun is arranged in outlet flue. A flue gas conveying system is provided with pressurizing flue air storage tank and flue oil pipeline. A pressurizing flue fuel storage tank is arranged on the flue liquid pipeline. The end of the pressurizing flue fluid storage tank communicates with the outlet of the outlet. Another end of pulverized coal pipeline is connected with the pulverization coal pipeline, so that the gas discharged by the outlet is used as gas source of the coal powder shooting gun. The pressurizer is arranged at the bottom surface of the gas storage tank. The device further comprises controller.
   USE - The device is useful in metallurgy industry, pyrolysis of natural gas, gasification reaction using carbon, and two methods for high temperature environment of reaction, where the first method is simple but limited by natural gas resource, and the second method can good use of our countries rich coal resources.
   ADVANTAGE - The device directly sprays the coal powder into the melting reduction furnace and the hot blast furnace, reduces the production cost and the dangerous coefficient of the technique, reduces the temperature of the coal gas, improves the heat value of the gas, and realizes the efficient utilization of the energy. The reaction condition of gas modification in the application is oxygen free environment, and the chemical reaction modified by gas utilizes the water and carbon dioxide in the gas. The device improves the content of carbon monoxide and hydrogen in the coal gases, reduces the gas oxidation degree, improves the reduction characteristic of coal gas so as to further improve coal gas heat value, and is convenient for downstream reasonable use of user. The coal blowing system uses the air heating furnace outlet to discharge the smoke as the gas source.

   LYS I
AE AGNETIX INC (AGNE-Non-standard)
GA 2021A2271J
AB    NOVELTY - The fixture (1000) has: an aluminum housing; at least one light source mechanically supported by the aluminum housing; at least one copper pipe thermally coupled to the aluminum housing to carry a fluid coolant (800), where during operation of the lighting fixture the fluid coolant flowing through the copper pipe extracts heat generated by the lighting fixture; Power over Ethernet (PoE) ports (1008A); at least one Universal Serial Bus (USB) port; and at least one integrated sensor assembly coupled to at least one of the PoE ports or the USB port, the integrated sensor assembly comprising sensors including: an air temperature sensor; a visible light sensor; a near infrared (NIR) sensor; a relative humidity sensor; a camera; a carbon dioxide sensor; and/or an infrared (IR) sensor.

The lighting fixture substantially reduces the costs typically associated with purchasing the lighting fixture hardware and installation, and provides substantial savings to the customer. PT P
PN US2021260317-A1
TI System for monitoring ventilation of patient at            scene of emergency, has processor that determines            ventilation quality parameters based on gas flow volume            calculated based on sensed gas flow rate and gas            pressures.
AU CLOUTIER J P
   HERKEN U
   SILVER A E
   FREEMAN G A
AE ZOLL MEDICAL CORP (ZOLL-C)
GA 202198914N
AB    NOVELTY - The system (100) has a manual patient                ventilation unit that defines an airflow path. A                feedback device comprises a processor arranged to                receive and process the data generated by the                sensor, determine when a ventilation breath has                been administered to the patient and estimate a                tidal volume of the administered ventilation breath                based on the processed data, compare the estimated                ventilation tidal volume to a target ventilation                tidal volume range, and provide ventilation                feedback on the visual display, the feedback                providing an indication of whether the estimated                tidal volume of the administered breath falls                within the target ventilation tidal volume range                and providing a timing indication representing a                time until a subsequent ventilation should be                provided to the patient. A monitoring sensor senses                data representative of end tidal carbon dioxide,                oxygen saturation, blood oxygen concentration or                ventilation/perfusion ratio of patient.
   USE - Medical system for assisting rescuer in                monitoring ventilation of patient e.g. victim at                scene of emergency. Uses include but are not                limited to cardiac arrest, cardiac monitoring,                intravenous cannulation, intraosseous access,                surgical cricothyrotomy, needle cricoryrotomy and                advanced medication administration through parental                and enteral routes.
   ADVANTAGE - The feedback unit provides feedback to the                rescuer based on the ventilation quality parameters                including spirometric measurements, thus providing                feedback to a rescuer with feedback if the rescuers                are compressing too hard or too soft, and too fast                or too slow, as compared to set standards and                protocols. The system provides a feedback unit to                display spirometric information in the form of                side-by-side numerics as simultaneous display of                flow rate and volume, so that the feedback unit can                display a single value that provides a measure of                the squareness of a spirometry curve based on                spirometry measurements in an effective                manner.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the                system for responding to an emergency medical                condition.
   Medical system for assisting rescuer in                providing manual ventilations to patient,                (100)
   Victim (102)
   Ventilation bag (104)
   Airflow sensor (106)
   Central server system (120)
DC B07 (General - tablets, dispensers, catheters (excluding drainage and angioplasty), encapsulation etc, but not systems for administration of blood or saline or IV feeding etc.); P34 (Sterilising, syringes, electrotherapy (A61L, M, N).); S05 (Electrical Medical Equipment); T01 (Digital Computers)
MC B11-C11; B11-C08K; B12-K04G; B05-C08; B05-C04; P34-A02; S05-B04; S05-A01B; T01-J06A; T01-E01C
IP A61M-016/00; A61N-001/39

PT P
PN CN113107085-A
TI Decoration plate comprises wrapping edge installed with bamboo carbon adsorption substrate that is installed with fireproof layer composed of closed-cell perlite, volcanic ash, cement, asbestos fiber, talc, sodium bicarbonate and water.
AU TAN F
AE TAN F (TANF-Individual)
GA 202181753K
AB    NOVELTY - Decoration plate comprises a wrapping edge (1) whose inner part is installed with bamboo carbon adsorption substrate (2). An inner part of the wrapping edge is located above and below the bamboo carbon adsorption substrate that is installed with a fireproof layer (3). An end of the fireproof layer is connected with the bamboo carbon adsorption substrate. Top and bottom parts of the wrapping edge are installed with a waterproof layer (4). An end of the waterproof layer is connected with the fireproof layer. The fireproof layer comprises 20-40 pts. wt. closed-cell perlite, 15-20 pts. wt. volcanic ash, 15-20 pts. wt. cement, 5-15 pts. wt. asbestos fiber, 5-10 pts. wt. talc, 4-8 pts. wt. sodium bicarbonate, 2-4 pts. wt. calcium bicarbonate, 2-4 pts. wt. barium sulfate, 5-15 pts. wt. aluminum oxide and 40-50 pts. wt. water. The bamboo carbon adsorption substrate comprises 20-40 pts. wt. bamboo carbon particles, 15-20 pts. wt. binder, 20-30 pts. wt. diatom ooze and 10-15 pts. wt. fine sand.
   USE - Used as decoration plate.
   ADVANTAGE - The plate: absorbs formaldehyde, indoor combustion product i.e. carbon monoxide and carbon dioxide, air microorganism, inhalable particle and organic volatile in air by using the bamboo carbon adsorption substrate, which effectively purifies harmful substance in air; ensures fire-proof, flame-retardant, heat preservation, sound absorption and noise reduction effect by using the diatom ooze; increases fire resistance and avoids occurrence of dangerous accident by using the fireproof layer; avoids damp intrusion by using the waterproof layer, which prevents interior of decoration plate from being corroded by moisture, thus avoiding occurrence of mildew phenomenon; and radically eliminates pollution source without improving function to living environment.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for preparing the decoration plate by utilizing wrapping edge, bamboo carbon adsorption substrate, fireproof layer, waterproof layer and foaming agent.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic representation of decoration plate.
   Wrapping edge (1)
   Bamboo carbon adsorption substrate (2)
   Fireproof layer (3)
   Waterproof layer (4)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The foaming agent is aluminum powder. The waterproof layer comprises film-forming auxiliary agent, ammonia water, mildew-proof preservative, thickening agent, antifoaming agent, mixed filler, dispersing agent and wetting agent.
DC L02 (Refractories, ceramics, cement - includes manufacturing methods, limes, soil preparation for (road) building, magnesias and slags, cements, mortars, concretes, abrasives, thermal or acoustic insulation (non)oxide ceramics and ceramic composites, but not brick making, concrete mixers or casting or pottersâ€™ wheels (C04).); Q43 (General building constructions (E04B))
MC L02-A07; L02-B08; L02-C08; L02-D15A; L02-J02C; Q43-A99; Q43-F
IP E04B-001/04; E04B-001/62; E04B-001/94; C04B-028/02; C04B-028/00; C04B-026/00; C04B-038/02; C04B-111/28; C04B-111/40

PT P
PN WO2021116455-A1; FR3104565-A1; FR3104565-B1; WO2021116455-A8; EP4072997-A1; IN202217032938-A
TI Carbonaceous material used in electrode for            rechargeable battery or cell e.g. sodium ion or lithium            batteries comprises hard and non-porous carbon            particles having spherical morphology.
AU GHIMBEU C
   BEDA A
   TABERNA P
   SIMON P
   MORCRETTE M
   RABUEL F
   CAMELIA G
   ADRIAN B
   PIERRE-LOUIS T
   PATRICE S
   MATHIEU M
   FRANCOIS R
AE CENT NAT RECH SCI (CNRS-C)
   UNIV HAUTE ALSACE (UYHA-Non-standard)
   UNIV TOULOUSE III SABATIER PAUL (UTOS-C)
   UNIV PICARDIE VERNE JULES (UYPI-Non-standard)
   CENT NAT RECH SCI (CNRS-C)
   CENT NAT RECH SCI (CNRS-C)
   UNIV HAUTE ALSACE (UYHA-Non-standard)
   UNIV TOULOUSE III SABATIER PAUL (UTOS-C)
   UNIV PICARDIE VERNE JULES (UYPI-Non-standard)
GA 2021660503
AB    NOVELTY - Carbonaceous material comprises hard and                non-porous carbon particles having a spherical                morphology. The material has an interreticular                distance d002 of greater than 3.6 Angstrom , a                total specific surface area of less than 75 m2/g,                measured by the Brunauer-Emmett-Teller (BET)                nitrogen method and a total specific surface of                1-100 m2/g, preferably 1-60 m2/g, measured by the                BET carbon dioxide method.
   USE - The carbonaceous material is useful: in an                electrode (claimed) for rechargeable battery (or                cell), preferably for sodium ion or lithium                batteries; and as an additive and combined with at                least one other materials in a device, preferably                an electrochemical devices e.g. electrochemical                cells, cells, batteries and/or sodium ion                accumulators.
   ADVANTAGE - The material: has irreversible initial                capacity, which capacity is lower compared to other                similar hard carbon materials; provides simple and                inexpensive electrodes; has a low active surface                and oxygen-based functional groups; provides                improved corrosion resistance and good electronic                conductivity; and is economical.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for                manufacturing a non-porous carbonaceous material                comprising (a) mixing at least an amine catalyst                comprising triethylenediamine, quinuclidine,                trimethylamine and/or hexamethylenetetramine, a                hydroxylated aromatic compound comprising phenol,                resorcinol and phloroglucinol, catechin,                pyrogallol, hydroxyquinol, gallic acid or                polyphenols e.g. tannins, an aldehyde compound                comprising glyoxal, formaldehyde, acetaldehyde, a                ketoacid, glyoxylic acid, pyruvic acid and/or                2-methyl-3-oxopropanoic acid in the presence of a                polar protic solvent, (b) maturing the mixture                obtained at the end of step (a), preferably at a                temperature of 20-35 degrees C, e.g. for 0.5-5 days                to obtain a solid phase comprising spheres of                phenolic resin and a liquid phase, (c) controlling                separation of the solid phase from the liquid                phase, (d) polymerizing and/or drying the solid                phase for 1-48 hours resulting in the formation of                a dry phenolic resin, (e) carbonizing the dry                phenolic resin, preferably in the presence of a                flow of inert gas at a temperature of 1200-2000                degrees C to obtain a non-porous carbonaceous                material and (f) optionally recovering the                carbonaceous material.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The interreticular                  distance is 3.7-4 Angstrom , preferably 3.7-3.8                  Angstrom . The total specific surface area,                  measured by the BET carbon dioxide method is 1-50                  m2/g, preferably 1-3.5 m2/g. The material has: a                  packed density greater than 0.7 g/cm3, e.g. 0.72                  g/cm3 at 1.1 g/cm3, preferably greater than 0.84                  g/cm3; an active surface area (ASA) of less than                  12 m2/g, preferably less than 5 m2/g; less than                  0.5 mmol/g, preferably less than 0.05 mmol/g an                  oxygen-based functional group. The particles have                  a size of 0-15 mu m, preferably 0-10 mu m. The                  amine catalyst is triethylenediamine and the                  hydroxylated aromatic compound is preferably                  phloroglucinol or condensed tannin and/or the                  aldehyde compound is preferably glyoxylic acid.                  Preferred Method: The polymerization step is a                  thermal step, preferably by heating at a                  temperature of 70-150 degrees C. The maturing                  step (b) is carried out in the absence of                  agitation. The separation step (c) is carried out                  without centrifugation.
   EXAMPLE - 4.1 g Phloroglucinol and 3.6 g glyoxylic acid                were dissolved in 200 ml water with mechanical                stirring (30 minutes/1 hour) at room temperature                (25 degrees C) in a flask. Then, 1.8 g                triethylenediamine (TEDA) was added to this mixture                and stirred for 5-10 minutes until complete                dissolution. The addition of TEDA as a crosslinking                agent/catalyst causes the formation of a turbid                solution of phenolic resin spheres. The mixture was                left to stand for 24 hours. The spheres increase in                size and weight and settle on the bottom of the                balloon. Two phases were observed a solid phase                (composed of polymer spheres) and a liquid phase.                The latter was removed and the solid phase                recovered (without centrifugation, by simple                pouring) and dried in an oven at 80 degrees C for                12 hours to evaporate the water and to better                crosslink the resin spheres. A pyrolysis step was                then carried out in an inert atmosphere (argon),                heating 6.5 g the resin obtained with a heating                rate of 5 degrees C/minutes up to 1300 degrees C,                1400 degrees C, 1500 degrees C or 1600 degrees C                and once the desired temperature had been reached,                a level of 1 hour was made, then a natural cooling                step was carried out to obtain 2.5 g the hard                carbon. The hard carbon was subsequently used                without the need for a grinding step to make the                particle size uniform. The materials obtained were                called acetone butanol ethanol (ABE) 1300, ABE                1400, ABE 1500 and ABE 1600.
DC A85 (Electrical applications.); A21 (Epoxides; aminoplasts; phenoplasts.); L03 (Electro-(in)organic - chemical features of conductors, resistors, magnets, capacitors and switches, electric discharge lamps, semiconductor and other materials, batteries, accumulators and thermoelectric devices, including fuel cells, magnetic recording media, radiation emission devices, liquid crystals and basic electric elements. Growing of single crystals of semiconductors and their doping are included, but semiconductor devices, where the manufacture is not claimed are excluded. Electrography, electrophotography, magnetography, electrolysis, electrophoresis, power plant, X-ray and plasma-techniques, ion exchange resins, polyelectrolytes, electroplating, metal electrodeposition, electroforming, anodising, electrolytic cleaning, cathodic protection and electrolytic or electrothermic production or refining of metals are all covered elsewhere (Sections G, J, K and M).); X16 (Electrochemical Storage)
MC A05-C01B; A08-S02; A10-E05B; A11-C02; A12-E06A; L03-A02B; L03-E01B3; L03-E01B5B; L03-E03; L03-E06; L03-E08; L03-J01; X16-B01F1; X16-E01J; X16-M

PT P
PN CN112901295-A; CN214464460-U
TI Mixture trans-critical cycle waste heat recovery system has medium-temperature flue gas heater whose high-temperature side outlet is communicated with high-temperature side inlet of low-temperature flue gas heater.
AU GAO W
   LI H
   ZHANG L
   WU S
   LI K
   ZHANG C
AE XIAN THERMAL POWER RES INST CO LTD (CHHN-C)
   XIAN THERMAL POWER RES INST CO LTD (CHHN-C)
GA 202167952W
AB    NOVELTY - The system has a low-temperature flue gas heater (9) whose low-temperature side outlet merges with the cold-side outlet of the medium-temperature regenerator (3) and is connected to the cold-side inlet of the medium-temperature flue gas heater (10). The outlet of the low-temperature turbine (11) merges with the hot-side outlet of the high-temperature regenerator (2) and is connected to the hot-side inlet of the medium-temperature regenerator. The low-temperature side outlet of the high-temperature flue gas heater (12) is connected with the inlet of the high-temperature turbine (1). The high-temperature side outlet of the high-temperature flue gas heater is communicated with the high-temperature side inlet of the medium-temperature flue gas heater. The high-temperature side outlet of the medium-temperature flue gas heater is communicated with the high-temperature side inlet of the low-temperature flue gas heater.
   USE - Mixture trans-critical cycle waste heat recovery system for power generation system.
   ADVANTAGE - The system uses the water pump to replace the compressor in the traditional carbon dioxide Brayton cycle. The power consumption of the compression liquid is much smaller than the power consumption of the compressor. The temperature of the high pressure water is improved. The system avoids the problem that the low-temperature working medium heat absorption is not matched with the heat source heat release amount. The system avoids the phenomenon of vaporizing in the process of liquid in the process of gas compression. The system improves the stability of the device safety.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for an operation method of mixture trans-critical cycle waste heat recovery system.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of a mixture trans-critical cycle waste heat recovery system.
   High-temperature turbine (1)
   High-temperature regenerator (2)
   Medium-temperature regenerator (3)
   Low-temperature flue gas heater (9)
   Medium-temperature flue gas heater (10)
   Low-temperature turbine (11)
   High-temperature flue gas heater (12)
DC Q51 (Internal combustion engines, reciprocating engines, rotary engines (F01, F02B,D,F,G,M,N,P))
MC Q51-C

PT P
PN CN112869176-A
TI Plant-based meat hamburger cake comprises water, soybean oil, soybean silk protein, mushroom paste, wheat protein, thickening agent, beef flavor essence, soybean isolated protein, beet red, apple juice, and edible salt.
AU HUANG X
   QI M
   LIN F
AE BIFEIAI FOOD CO LTD (BIFE-Non-standard)
GA 202164766F
AB    NOVELTY - Plant meat hamburger cake comprises 40-50 pts. wt. water, 10-20 pts. wt. soybean oil, 10-20 pts. wt. soybean silk protein, 4-7 pts. wt. mushroom paste, 3-5 pts. wt. wheat protein, 2-3 pts. wt. thickening agent, 2-3 pts. wt. beef flavor essence, 1-2 pts. wt. soybean isolated protein, 0.1-1 pts. wt. beet red, 0.1-2 pts. wt. apple juice, 0.1-1 pts. wt. white granulated sugar, 0.1-1.5 pts. wt. edible salt and 0.1-1 pts. wt. yeast extract.
   USE - Plant-based meat hamburger cake.
   ADVANTAGE - The plant meat hamburger cake has high protein, zero cholesterol, low saturated fatty acid, similar taste to the structure of the meat, the flavor and the beef are close to each other, and the juicy is sufficient.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for preparing a vegetable meat burger cake, which involves:
   (A) soaking the soybean silk protein with ice water until it is fully expanded, putting it in a dehydrator for dehydration, putting it into a chopper and chopping it into filaments, and taking it out for later use;
   (B) adding water to the chopper and turn on the low-speed chopping mode;
   (C) chopping with chopper to the speed of 300-400r/min, and slowly adding thickener, after completion, increasing speed of the chopper to 1500-2000 revolution per minute to obtain slurry, after mixing uniformly;
   (D) slowly adding soybean oil to the slurry, keeping the speed of the chopper for 5-10 minutes to make an emulsified slurry;
   (E) adding the emulsified pulp to the mixer and turning on the stirring;
   (F) adding the processed soy protein silk, and stirring uniformly;
   (G) adding mushroom puree, soy protein isolate, beef flavor, beet red, apple juice, white sugar, edible salt, yeast extract, and using carbon dioxide to cool down and vacuum to mix uniformly; and

AB    NOVELTY - Aerated concrete comprises 78.8-88.8 wt.%                steel slag, 5.9-16.8 wt.% slag, 1-4 wt.% red mud,                0.3-0.6 wt.% activator, 0.3-0.8 wt.% additive,                0.7-1.5 wt.% foam stabilizing agent and 1-4 wt.%                foaming agent.
   USE - Aerated concrete.
   ADVANTAGE - The aerated concrete has excellent synergistic                effect of alkali excitation and accelerated                carbonization.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method                for preparing the aerated concrete, involving (a)                drying and crushing the steel slag and red mud,                mixing with additive and grinding to an 80 mu m                sieve residue less than 2%, and mixing with slag                and foam stabilizing agent to obtain a composite                powder, (b) spraying 5% powder with water into the                composite powder, evenly stirring and placing in an                environment at room temperature of greater than or                equal to 90% for 24 hours, (c) uniformly mixing                activator and water in ratio of water/sodium oxide                ratio of 225:1-500:1 to obtain an activator                solution, (d) adding the powder prepared in step                (b) to the activator solution according to the                ratio of the liquid-to-solid mass ratio of                0.45-0.5, and uniformly stirring to obtain an                alkali-activated slurry, (e) adding foaming agent                to the alkali-activated slurry, and rapidly                stirring for 10 seconds at a speed of 1000 r/min to                obtain an aerated concrete slurry, (f) quickly                pouring the aerated concrete slurry into a mold for                pouring, transferring to a static room with a                normal temperature and a humidity of greater than                or equal to 90% for aeration and leaving still for                24 hours, (g) demolding and cutting to obtain an                aerated concrete body of the required size after                the static curing is finished, transferring the                body to a carbonization kettle with a temperature                of 60 plus minus 5 degrees C, a humidity of 50 plus                minus 5%, and a carbon dioxide concentration of                10-20% for carbonization for 18-24 hours, and (h)                taking the aerated concrete treated in step (g) out                of the kettle for inspection, packing into storage,                and naturally curing for 5 days to obtain the                finished product.

The activator is                  commercially available industrial-grade liquid                  sodium silicate or water glass, and with a                  modulus of 2.2-2.5.    ZHANG Z

   LI M

   GUO G
AE PETROCHINA CO LTD (CNPC-C)
GA 2020C7891D
AB    NOVELTY - Graphene-modified solvent-free epoxy anticorrosive coating includes first component and a second component. The first component includes 1 pts. wt. of polyether modified epoxy resin, 0.8-1 pts. wt. of phenolic epoxy resin, 0.005-0.01 pts. wt. ethylenediamine grafted modified graphene, 1.55-1.96 pts. wt. of pigment and filler, 0.02-0.03 pts. wt. of defoaming agent, 0.01-0.02 pts. wt. of dispersant and 0.01-0.02 pts. wt. leveling agent and the second component includes an epoxy curing agent.
   USE - Graphene-modified solvent-free epoxy anticorrosive coating for oil and casing anticorrosion.
   ADVANTAGE - The graphene-modified solvent-free epoxy anticorrosive coating has good physical and chemical properties, no cracks in the anti-1.5 degrees bending coating, 8 joules impact resistance, abrasion resistance less than 50 mg, cathodic stripping resistance less than 12 mm, high temperature and high pressure paint film intact, and excellent chemical resistance, and coating can be used for internal and external corrosion protection of steel tubing and casing, and can adapt to high temperature, high pressure, hydrogen sulfide (H2S) and carbon dioxide (CO2) environments.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a manufacturing method of graphene modified solvent-free epoxy anticorrosive coating, which involves:
   (A) putting the phenolic epoxy resin into the oven for preheating;
   (B) heating the pigments and fillers to remove water;
   (C) mixing the phenolic epoxy resin and polyether modified epoxy resin, defoamer, dispersant and leveling agent, and stirring, add the modified graphene and pigments while stirring, and continue to stirring to make the components uniformly dispersed;
   (D) obtaining the first component of the graphene modified solvent-free epoxy anticorrosive coating after grinding;
   (E) mixing the modified aromatic amine curing agent and the phenolic curing agent, and then stirring uniformly to obtain the second component of the graphene modified solvent-free epoxy anticorrosive coating; and
   (F) mixing the first component and the second component uniformly to obtain the graphene modified solvent-free epoxy anticorrosive coating.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Conditions: The weight ratio of the first component to the second component is 1:(0.2-0.3). Preferred Process: The preparation method of ethylenediamine grafted modified graphene involves adding flake graphite to sulfuric acid, slowly adding potassium permanganate while stirring, controlling the reaction temperature to always be less than 5 degrees C and placing to obtain a dark green liquid. The obtained dark green liquid is added to deionized water, stirred uniformly, and raised the temperature to 95 degrees C. The hydrogen peroxide is added while it is hot, kept to react, filtered, washed, and dried to obtain a graphene sample. The obtained sample is added to dimethyl formamide (DMF), and ultrasonically processed to obtain a suspension. The ethylenediamine and bicyclohexylcarbimide are added, mixed ultrasonically and reacted at 120 degrees C, and added absolute ethanol. The upper layer of clear night is removed, filtered the lower layer of precipitation, and washed with ethanol and deionized water, and dried the obtained sample to obtain modified graphene. Preferred Components: The pigments and fillers include barium sulfate, talc, titanium dioxide, mica powder, and fumed silica.

TI Environmentally-friendly, pollution-free and high-filled natural inorganic mineral packaging material comprises polypropylene, polyethylene, graded natural inorganic mineral, lubricant, oxidative degradation agent and wetting dispersant.
AU YANG L
AE DONGGUAN LIGE AUTOMATION MACHINERY CO (DONG-Non-standard)
GA 2020B3047M
AB    NOVELTY - Environmentally-friendly, pollution-free and high-filled natural inorganic mineral packaging material comprises 8-18 pts. wt. polypropylene, 10-25 pts. wt. polyethylene, 55-75 pts. wt. graded natural inorganic mineral, 2-9 pts. wt. lubricant, 2-6 pts. wt. oxidative degradation agent and 2-8 pts. wt. wetting dispersant.
   USE - Used as environmentally-friendly, pollution-free and high-filled natural inorganic mineral packaging material.
   ADVANTAGE - The packaging material: has rich sources of raw materials and high dispersion and compatibility and long shelf life of six months; is non-toxic and harmless; can be widely used in industrial product packaging and other fields and degraded into fragments, inorganic mineral powder, carbon dioxide, water, etc; and does not cause secondary pollution to the environment during incineration.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for preparing an environmentally-friendly, pollution-free, high-filling natural inorganic mineral packaging material, comprising (i) drying natural inorganic minerals to a moisture content of less than or equal to 0.5%, if the moisture content exceeds the standard and drying the inorganic minerals at 80-150 degrees C until the test is qualified; (ii) grinding polypropylene, polyethylene, lubricants, oxidative degradation agents and wetting and dispersing agents into powders, and activating the graded natural inorganic minerals; (iii) eliminating static electricity on the materials of step (ii) to prevent material agglomeration; (iv) adding graded natural inorganic minerals into the high-mix system, programming the temperature for heating and stirring, and adding wetting and dispersing agents, oxidative degradation agents, and lubricants in sequence by stirring; (v) adding partially ground polypropylene and polyethylene into the high-mix system for pre-plasticization; (vi) heating the pre-plasticized raw material, smelting in an internal mixer, and extruding and granulating using plastic extruder to obtain raw material particles; (vii) subjecting the particles in step (vi) and the remaining polypropylene and polyethylene after grinding to secondary pelletization through a plastic extruder to prepare an environmentally-friendly master batch; and (viii) screening the environmentally-friendly master batch, placing in a plastic film blowing machine for film blowing and slitting, cutting the plastic film and combining thermally into packaging materials.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The graded natural inorganic mineral comprises at least one of calcium carbonate, magnesium carbonate, talc powder, calcium sulfate, barium sulfate, mica powder, kaolin or titanium dioxide, where inorganic powder has a particle size of 500-3000 meshes in a certain proportion. In the step (vii), the particle size of the secondary granulation is 1.5-5 mm. Preferred Method: In the step (iv), the heating is carried out at 60-180 degrees C. In the step (vi), the temperature of heating and mixing is carried out at 180-200 degrees C. In the step (vi), the temperature of the extruder is controlled at 140-180 degrees C.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The oxidative degradation agent comprises at least one of fatty acid metal salt, oleic acid metal salt, acetylacetone metal salt or tetramethyl-4,4-diaminomethanone, which can absorb ambient light, transfers heat and other energy to chemical energy and breaks polyethylene and polypropylene molecular chains.

PN CN110980696-A
TI Pinewood-based graphene quantum dot used e.g. in            cell fluorescence probes, is prepared by hydrothermally            carbonizing using pinewood, centrifuging and dialyzing            to separate and purify graphene quantum dots, and            freeze-drying solid powder.
AU SONG X
   ZHAO S
   CHAI X
   LIU Z
   HE H
   WU M
AE UNIV GUANGXI (UYGU-C)
GA 202031485C
AB    NOVELTY - A pinewood-based graphene quantum dot is                prepared by using pinewood as raw material,                hydrothermally carbonizing to prepare pine-based                graphene quantum dot solution, centrifuging and                dialyzing to separate and purify graphene quantum                dots, and freeze-drying solid powder.
   USE - The pinewood-based graphene quantum dot is                used in water heavy metal detection for detecting                iron (III) ion concentration in water and cell                fluorescence probes (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The product has simple preparation method,                good luminescence stability and high                biocompatibility.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for:
   (1) preparation of the pinewood-based graphene                quantum dot comprising using pinewood as raw                material, grinding into solid pinewood powder,                passing through 200-800 mesh sieve, moving to                hydrothermal reaction kettle, adding ultrapure                water, mixing, heating, holding, performing ice                water bath on reaction kettle to stop hydrothermal                reaction to obtain pinewood solid-liquid mixture,                removing pinewood coarse residue to get                supernatant, separating and purifying supernatant                by centrifugation to obtain pinewood-based graphene                quantum dot solution, dialyzing and separating                using dialysis bag, periodically replacing                dialysate for 6-12 hours, and freeze-drying;
   (2) use of pinewood-based graphene quantum dot                to detect iron (III) ion concentration in water                comprising formulating pinewood-based graphene                quantum dot solid powder to pinewood-based graphene                quantum dot aqueous solution, mixing with water                sample, reacting at room temperature for 15                minutes, detecting fluorescence intensity value at                excitation wavelength of 330 nm, and calculating                concentration of iron (III) ion in water sample to                be tested; and
   (3) use of pinewood-based graphene quantum dot                to cell fluorescence probes comprising formulating                pinewood-based graphene quantum dot solid powder to                pinewood-based graphene quantum dot aqueous                solution, filtering using 0.22 mu m sterilizing                filter film, using HeLa cells as observation                object, placing filtered pinewood-based graphene                quantum dot aqueous solution and HeLa cells in high                glucose culture medium, culturing in constant                temperature culture box with 5% carbon dioxide for                24 hours, washing with pre-heated phosphate buffer                solution, observing HeLa cells under laser confocal                microscope, absorbing pinewood-based graphene                quantum dots by cells, receiving excitation wave,                emitting fluorescence, and revealing cell                structure.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The method involves using                  pinewood as raw material, grinding into solid                  pinewood powder, passing through 200-800 mesh                  sieve, moving to hydrothermal reaction kettle,                  adding ultrapure water, mixing, heating at                  140-200 degrees C for 0-5 hours, holding,                  performing ice water bath on reaction kettle to                  stop hydrothermal reaction to obtain pinewood                  solid-liquid mixture, removing pinewood coarse                  residue to get supernatant, separating and                  purifying supernatant by centrifugation for 5-10                  minutes at speed of 10000-24000                  revolutions/minute (rpm) to obtain pinewood-based                  graphene quantum dot solution, dialyzing and                  separating using dialysis bag for 1-3 days,                  periodically replacing dialysate for 6-12 hours,                  and freeze-drying for 1-2 days. Preferred                  Components: The solid-to-liquid ratio of pinewood                  powder and ultra-pure water is 1 g:3-8 mL.

PN DE102018124922-A1; DE102018124922-B4
TI Drive device for vehicle, has control device to add in-engine added amount of fuel to drive device in high-load operation to generate compensation control instruction for electric motor so that excess power of diesel engine that is absorbed.
AU VAN MOERGASTEL B
   VISSER S
   HERRMANN O
   NORGEL K
AE DENSO CORP (NPDE-C)
GA 202026612V
AB    NOVELTY - The device (10) has an exhaust gas after-treatment device (13) for treating an exhaust gas (34) of a diesel engine (11). The exhaust gas is arranged on an exhaust gas passage (14). A controllable fuel addition device (17) adds fuel to exhaust gas. A catalyst (20) removes nitrogen oxides (NOx) from exhaust gas. A control device (23) controls fuel addition device by adding fuel to exhaust gas. The air-fuel ratio in exhaust gas in region of catalyst is equal to a target value close to the stoichiometric equilibrium. A required amount for a fuel addition is determined. An additional control instruction is generated for the diesel engine. The in-engine added amount of fuel is added to the drive device in high-load operation to generate a compensation control instruction for an electric motor (12) so that an excess power of the diesel engine is absorbed by the electric motor. The catalyst is a three-way catalyst or a nitrogen oxides storage catalyst.
   USE - Drive device for vehicle e.g. hybrid diesel vehicle such as passenger cars or trucks.
   ADVANTAGE - The diesel engine is combined with an electric motor to save fuel and combustion emissions to create a hybrid drive with which a load distribution between the diesel engine and the electric motor is achieved. The drive device has lower production costs and can comply with the strict emission guidelines for carbon dioxide and nitrogen oxide emissions. The after-engine fuel addition can be controlled by a pilot control, while the in-engine fuel addition can be carried out by a regulation.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for an operating method of drive device.

TI Catalyst for producing unsaturated aldehyde, unsaturated carboxylic acid and conjugated diene, comprises molybdenum and element including other catalytically active components, and has preset ionization energy based on atomic ratio.
AU SHIGEKI O
   MOTOHIKO S
   SEIICHIRO F
   HIDEOMI S
   OKUMURA S
   SUGIYAMA M
   FUKUNAGA S
   SAKAI H
   FUKUNAKA S
   SAKAIHIDEOMI
AE NIPPON KAYAKU KK (NIPK-C)
   NIPPON KAYAKU KK (NIPK-C)
GA 202006532K
AB    NOVELTY - A catalyst comprises molybdenum as essential component and constituent elements including other catalytically active components. The ionization energy (I) (XIE) of each element other than molybdenum, ionization energy (I) (MoIE) of molybdenum, and atomic ratio (XC) of constituent element when ratio of molybdenum is taken as 12, satisfy specific relation (I).
   USE - Catalyst such as oxidation catalyst or an oxidative dehydrogenation catalyst for catalyzing isobutylene and t-butyl alcohol and reducing carbon monoxide, carbon dioxide, acetaldehyde, acetic acid, acrolein and formaldehyde for manufacturing unsaturated aldehyde compound such as methacrolein, unsaturated carboxylic acid compound such as methacrylic acid and methyl methacrylate and conjugated diene compound (all claimed) such as 1,3-butadiene and 1,3-pentadiene by gas-phase catalytic oxidation.
   ADVANTAGE - The catalyst has excellent activity and is manufactured with high yield and improves the process stability of a partial oxidation reaction accompanied by heat generation such as reduction of ( Delta T), and capable of manufacturing aldehyde compound, unsaturated carboxylic acid compound and conjugated diene compound with high yield even in region having high catalytic activity and without catalytic activity by gas-phase catalytic oxidation reaction.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A catalyst comprises molybdenum as essential component, and constituent elements including other catalytically active components. The ionization energy (I) (XIE) of each constituent element other than molybdenum, ionization energy (I) (MoIE) of molybdenum, and atomic ratio (XC) of constituent element when ratio of molybdenum is taken as 12 satisfy relation: 0.44 less than or equal to ( Sigma ((MoIE)-(XIE)x XC)) less than or equal to 1.53 (I). When (MoIE-XIE) is smaller than 0, the value is set to 0 in calculation. INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) manufacture of unsaturated aldehyde compound, an unsaturated carboxylic acid compound and a conjugated diene compound; and
   (2) manufacture of catalyst, which involves forming pre-fired powder which is obtained by baking dry powder which is obtained by drying the slurry containing each element in catalyst composition at 200-600 degrees C, and baking again at 200-600 degrees C.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Property: The standard enthalpy (X Delta H) of formation (kJ.mol-1) of an oxide of each element other than molybdenum, stoichiometric number (XS) of each element contained in the oxide and atomic ratio (XC) of constituent element satisfy relation of: 0.1 less than or equal to ( Sigma (-X Delta HXSx XC)x 10-3) less than or equal to 3.3 (II). Preferred Components: The catalyst comprises bismuth, iron, and cobalt and further comprises nickel, cesium, and catalytically active component comprising molybdenum-bismuth-iron-cobalt-nickel-alkali metal-alkaline metal oxide of formula: MoaBibFecCodNid1B'eCfD'gEhOx (III).B' = lithium, sodium, potassium, rubidium, cesium or thallium;C = boron, phosphorus, chromium, manganese, zinc, arsenic, niobium, tin, antimony, tellurium, lanthanum, cerium, praseodymium, neodymium, samarium, europium, tungsten, zinc, or lead;D' = silicon, aluminum, titanium or zirconium;O = oxygen;a, b,c,d,d1,e,f,g,h,x = atomic ratio of Mo, Bi, Fe, Co, A, B', C, D', E, and O, respectively;andX = numerical value determined by oxidation state of each element.When a is 12, then b is 0.1-10, c is 0.1-20, d is 0.1-20, d1 is 0.1-20, e is 0.05-1, f is 0-10, g is 0-30 and h is 0-5.
   EXAMPLE - 100 Parts mass ammonium heptamolybdate was completely dissolved in 380 parts mass pure water to obtain mother liquor (1). 3.7 parts mass cesium nitrate was dissolved in 42 ml pure water and was added to the mother liquor (1). 34 parts mass ferric nitrate, 103 parts mass cobalt nitrate and 10 parts mass nickel nitrate were dissolved in 78 ml pure water, and added to the mother liquor (1). 17 Parts mass bismuth nitrate was dissolved in aqueous solution of nitric acid, and added to the mother liquor (1). The obtained mother liquor (1) was dried by a spray drying method, and the obtained dried powder was pre-baked at 440 degrees C for 5 hours to obtain pre-baked powder. After adding 5 %mass crystalline cellulose to mixture and mixing, 33 %mass glycerol solution as a binder was added to perform tumbling granulation method to obtain unreacted powder. The active carrier was molded into a spherical shape so that the loading ratio was 40 %mass. The obtained spherical molded product with particle diameter of 4.4 mm was calcined at 520 degrees C for 5 hours, to obtain catalyst had excellent activity and yield of 84.1%.

TI Extracting and separating high-purity cannabidiol            comprises e.g. using two-stage analytical supercritical            carbon dioxide extraction system and adding dry            industrial hemp powder to extraction kettle and heating            extraction device.
AU ZHU C
   GE F
   SONG D
   TENG C
AE GUIZHOU AEROSPACE WUJIANG ELECTROMECHANI (CAES-C)
GA 202007696U
AB    NOVELTY - Extracting and separating high-purity                cannabidiol comprises e.g. using two-stage                analytical supercritical CO2 extraction system,                adding dry industrial hemp powder to extraction                kettle of the supercritical CO2 extraction device                and heating extraction device up and boosting to                extraction conditions, starting circular                extraction, maintaining constant temperature and                pressure, and collecting extract from resolving                kettle II after the extraction is completed to                obtain crude cannabidiol extract, adding crude                cannabidiol extract to thermostatic bath of a                molecular distillation device, heating up to the                required distillation temperature, adjusting degree                of vacuum to the required pressure, starting                distillation process and collecting cannabidiol                fractions to obtain cannabidiol enriched products,                dissolving cannabidiol fraction with a solvent,                filtering and applying to industrial chromatography                column, eluting with ethanol and collecting                cannabinol separation liquid and                concentrating.
   USE - The method is useful for extracting and                separating high-purity cannabidiol.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: is advanced, stable and feasible,                has high production efficiency, high purity and                yield, is environmentally-friendly and suitable for                mass industrial production; and content of                cannabidiol products obtained is greater than 99%,                total transfer rate is above 85%, and it does not                contain tetrahydrocannabinol.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Extracting and separating high-purity                cannabidiol comprises (i) using two-stage                analytical supercritical CO2 extraction system,                adding dry industrial hemp powder to extraction                kettle of the supercritical CO2 extraction device                and heating extraction device up and boosting to                extraction conditions, starting circular                extraction, maintaining constant temperature and                pressure to required extraction time, and                collecting extract from resolving kettle II after                the extraction is completed to obtain crude                cannabidiol extract; (ii) adding crude cannabidiol                extract obtained in step (i) to thermostatic bath                of a molecular distillation device, heating up to                the required distillation temperature, adjusting                degree of vacuum to the required pressure, starting                distillation process and collecting cannabidiol                fractions to obtain cannabidiol enriched products;                and (iii) dissolving cannabidiol fraction obtained                in step (ii) with a solvent, filtering and applying                to industrial chromatography column, eluting with                ethanol as an eluent, collecting cannabinol                separation liquid and concentrating under reduced                pressure at 60-70 degrees C.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: In the step (i), high                  pressure supercritical extraction is carried out                  at 40-60 MPa at 52-65 degrees C for 30-90                  minutes. In the step (i) adopting two-stage                  analysis, where pressure in analysis vessel I is                  16-20 MPa and temperature is 55-65 degrees C,                  pressure in analysis vessel II is consistent with                  circuit pressure, 5-6 MPa, and temperature is                  50-55 degrees C. In the step (ii) molecular                  distillation temperature of crude cannabidiol                  extract is 120-160 degrees C and vacuum degree is                  0.1-5 Pa. In the step (i) adopting two-stage                  analysis, pressure in the analysis vessel I is                  16-20 MPa, temperature is 55-65 degrees C,                  pressure in the analysis vessel II is consistent                  with the circuit pressure, 5-6 MPa and                  temperature is 50-55 degrees C. In the step (ii)                  molecular distillation temperature of crude                  cannabidiol extract is 120-160 degrees C and                  vacuum degree is 0.1-5 Pa. In the step (ii)                  rotation speed of the scraper is 200-300                  revolutions/minute. In the step (iii), dissolving                  cannabidiol-enriched product with required                  solvent and then adding to industrial                  chromatography separation column, chromatographic                  column is at least one of UniQ10, UniSil10,                  UniPSA10, UniPS10, and UniPSN10. In the step                  (iii) flow rate of eluent is 10-50 ml/minutes.                  Preferred Components: In the step (iii) size of                  the industrial chromatographic column is 50x 250                  mm. In the step (iii), volume fraction of eluent                  ethanol is 50-80%.

PN CN110484495-A; CN110484495-B
TI Isolating and culturing cells, by obtaining cartilage specimen, aseptically refrigerating specimen, adding pronase E, filtering, calculating cell counts and live cell ratio, and inoculating chondrocytes into cell culture medium.
AU DING L
   XIONG L
   ZHOU Z
   CUI M
   WANG Q
   WU M
AE UNIV JIANGNAN (UYJN-C)
GA 2019A03845
AB    NOVELTY - Method for isolating and culturing cells from synovial joint cartilage, involves obtaining cartilage specimen, aseptically refrigerating the specimen throughout whole process, cutting the cartilage specimen, placing the cartilage tissue piece in sterile spinner flask, adding pronase E with weight/volume ratio of 0.4% for first degradation of extracellular matrix proteins, mixing the materials for 90 minutes, using 0.02% collagenase IA to degrade extracellular matrix protein of the degraded cartilage tissue pieces, reacting the mixture in cell incubator for 16-20 hours, filtering the degraded solution to obtain chondrocyte suspension using cell sieve to remove undigested tissue or cell clusters, centrifuging to obtain chondrocyte, calculating cell counts and live cell ratios, and inoculating isolated chondrocytes in Dulbecco's modified eagle medium:nutrient mixture F-12 (DMEM/F-12) cell culture medium for primary culture, where the inoculation density is 2-4x 105 cells/cm2.
   USE - The method is useful for isolating and culturing cells from synovial joint cartilage (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method provides high yield of chondrocytes, and less pollution rate.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Method for isolating and culturing cells from synovial joint cartilage, involves obtaining cartilage specimen, aseptically refrigerating the specimen throughout whole process, cutting the cartilage specimen to obtain tissue pieces with a diameter of less than 0.5 cm, placing the cartilage tissue piece in sterile spinner flask, adding pronase E with weight/volume ratio of 0.4% for first degradation of extracellular matrix proteins, mixing the materials for 90 minutes, where the reaction conditions are: 37 degrees C, 5% carbon dioxide and humidity of 100%, using 0.02% collagenase IA to degrade extracellular matrix protein of the degraded cartilage tissue pieces, reacting the mixture in cell incubator for 16-20 hours, filtering the degraded solution to obtain chondrocyte suspension using cell sieve to remove undigested tissue or cell clusters, centrifuging at 800-1000 revolutions per minute (rpm) and room temperature for 8-10 minutes at low speed to obtain chondrocyte, calculating cell counts and live cell ratios, and inoculating isolated chondrocytes in Dulbecco's modified eagle medium:nutrient mixture F-12 (DMEM/F-12) cell culture medium for primary culture, where the inoculation density is 2-4x 105 cells/cm2.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Components: The Pronase E is prepared using sterile DMEM/F12, preferably protease XIV from Streptomyces griseus solution. The collagenase IA is obtained from Clostridium histolyticum solution prepared using sterile DMEM/F12. The rotation speed is 70-90 rpm. The pore size of the cell sieve is 40-70 mu m. The DMEM/F12 cell culture solution comprises 10% fetal bovine serum and 1% monoclonal antibody.

TI Strong osmosis type formaldehyde scavenging agent comprises film-forming auxiliary agent, plant extracting solution, acetic acid, water repellent agent, bentonite penetrant agent, activated carbon powder and sodium bicarbonate.
AU CHAI D
AE SHEYONG ENVIRONMENTAL TECHNOLOGY NINGBO (SHEY-Non-standard)
GA 201996934H
AB    NOVELTY - Strong osmosis type formaldehyde scavenging agent comprises 3-6 pts. wt. film-forming auxiliary agent, 25-45 pts. wt. plant extracting solution, 3-7 pts. wt. acetic acid, 1-3 pts. wt. water repellent agent, 2-6 pts. wt. bentonite, 3-5 pts. wt. penetrant agent, 11-16 pts. wt. activated carbon powder,8-18 pts. wt. sodium bicarbonate,4-12 pts. wt. bactericide, 5-15 pts. wt. olive oil, 16-24 pts. wt. milk and 120-200 pts. wt. deionized water.
   USE - Used as strong osmosis type formaldehyde scavenging agent.
   ADVANTAGE - The scavenging agent improves conventional scavengers; enhances the permeability of the scavenger; avoids bacterial mixing during use; causes mildew and odor; is simple, convenient in use and environmentally-friendly; and has better formaldehyde removal effect.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for preparing strong osmosis type formaldehyde scavenging agent comprising (i) adding plant extracting solution and deionized water to the agitator and stirring at a certain rotation speed for a period of time to obtain base liquid A and then adding hydrophobic agent and penetrant agent to base liquid A and stirring at a certain speed for a period of time to obtain base liquid B; (ii) adding acetic acid to the base liquid B prepared in the first step and then stirring at a certain rotation speed for a period of time, stirring uniformly, adding bentonite and activated carbon powder, and stirring at a certain rotation speed for a period of time to obtain semi-finished product A; (iii) directly adding the sterilizing agent and milk to the semi-finished product A, stirring at a certain speed for a period of time, adding olive oil, and then stirring at a certain speed for a period of time to obtain semi-finished product B; (iv) adding film-forming auxiliary agent to the semi-finished product and stirring at a certain speed for a period of time to obtain a semi-finished product C; and (v) injecting semi-finished product C into a sealed container at 4 degrees C, and then injecting carbon dioxide into the sealed container to pressurize the product to obtain a finished product.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The scavenging agent comprises 4 pts. wt. film-forming auxiliary agent, 35 pts. wt. plant extracting solution, 5 pts. wt. acetic acid, 2 pts. wt. water repellent agent, 5 pts. wt. bentonite, 4 pts. wt. penetrant agent, 14 pts. wt. activated carbon powder, 14 pts. wt. sodium bicarbonate, 8 pts. wt. bactericide, 10 pts. wt. olive oil, 20 pts. wt. milk and 160 pts. wt. deionized water. Preferred Components: The plant extracting solution is cactus, Chlorophytum comosum, Gerbera jamesonii, reed, ivy, sago Cycas and/or Chrysanthemum flower. Preferred Method: The bactericide is prepared by mixing nano silver with rose, Osmanthus, violet, jasmine, lemon, rose, carnation, lily and/or crape myrtle. The activated carbon powder is obtained by milling and crushing the bulk activated carbon, and then screening by using a 200 mesh sieve.

A cable (32) is fixed to a stem or a branch of a tree. An upper portion of an underground screw (34) is fixed to the ground through the lower perforation. Multiple pores are formed on an outer surface of a basalt material.

The device is formed with the gravel-filled layer so as to fill residue of the basalt material with the pores, so that growth of the tree can be ensured by injecting useful microorganisms between the pores.

PU powder, 30-45 pts. wt. environmentally-friendly plasticizer, 0.5-2 pts. wt. calcium-zinc stabilizer, 0.6-0.8 pt. wt. environmentally-friendly antioxidant, 2-8.5 pts. wt. elastomer, 3-6 pts. wt. far infrared ceramic powder, 8-17 pts. wt. terpene resin, 0.6-0.9 pt. wt. high-efficiency water reducing agent and 0.1-0.4 pt. wt. calcium lignosulfonate.
   USE - As degradable PU glove.

The electrical currents and voltages for the ultrasound generation, electrolysis and gas discharge for triggering the generation processes are provided (2). The preparation of the cavitation and electrolysis medium and decoupling of the released heat are performed (3). The detection and control of the process parameters and the control of the optimal operation in the processes are performed (4). The gases produced during the cavitation and electrolysis at cathode and anode are detected and made available for use in a post-reaction and catalytic recombination to increase the released heat.
   USE - Method for generating heat from the metal grid-supported, electromagnetic condensation of hydrogen atoms through cavitation in a hydrogen-containing liquid medium.
   ADVANTAGE - The cavitation in a liquid cavitation medium in the vicinity of metal surfaces is used as an excitation process to detect exothermic, metal-grid-supported, electromagnetic condensation processes of hydrogen, as an ecologically advantageous, sustainable, carbon dioxide-free and cost-effective heat source practically usable. The uncontrolled performance excursions are avoided.

PN CN110125146-A
TI Method for recycling antibiotic residues e.g. penicillin, involves collecting antibiotic residues followed by obtaining antibiotic slurry, performing catalytic wet air oxidation and determining total nitrogen and total carbon.
AU ZHOU T
   QIAO W
AE ZHOU T (ZHOU-Individual)
GA 201972817A
AB    NOVELTY - An antibiotic residues recycling method involves collecting antibiotic residues in storage tanks, diluting with water, adjusting solid content rate to 3-10% and pH to neutral, obtaining to-be-treated antibiotic slurry, placing the slurry in catalytic wet air oxidation reactor and closed reactor, utilizing external heating source to heats, introducing high pressure oxygen into the reactor followed by obtaining partially concentrated product, performing natural exhaustion of spent steam, determining total nitrogen and total carbon of the concentrated product, determining total nitrogen by Kjeldahl method, determining total carbon using total organic carbon analyzer, judging results, adding small amount of nitrogen source components, carbon source components and trace element components according to the results and utilizing fermented antibiotic medium for secondary use.
   USE - Method for recycling antibiotic residues e.g. cephalosporins, penicillin, macrolides, aminoglycosides and rifamycins (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables recycling antibiotic residues by reducing cost of disposal and environmental risks during or after disposal and completely disposing antibiotic residues.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An antibiotic residues recycling method involves collecting antibiotic residues in storage tanks, diluting with water, adjusting solid content rate to 3-10% and pH to neutral, obtaining to-be-treated antibiotic slurry, placing the slurry in catalytic wet air oxidation reactor and closed reactor, utilizing external heating source to heats, introducing high pressure oxygen into the reactor, such that under action of high temperature and pressure and catalyst, organic matter contained in the antibiotic slurry is oxidized with oxygen and oxidized products are carbon dioxide, water, nitrogen and sulfate ions, reacting to obtain gas-liquid mixture, subjecting the gas-liquid mixture to gas-liquid separation using gas-liquid separator, obtaining concentrated product, such that spent steam naturally discharges feed and preheats gas-liquid mixture and preheating the gas-liquid mixture using heat exchanger, separating using gas-liquid separator, obtaining partially concentrated product, performing natural exhaustion of spent steam, determining total nitrogen and total carbon of the concentrated product, determining total nitrogen by Kjeldahl method, determining total carbon using total organic carbon analyzer, judging results, adding small amount of nitrogen source components, carbon source components and trace element components according to the results and utilizing fermented antibiotic medium for secondary use.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The method involves utilizing treated slurry and oxygen in catalytic wet air oxidation treatment is 1:10-1:100.

TI Clean air generator for use in wall of building,            has inflow pipe for making flow of outdoor air passed            through lid of tank, and air supply pipe provided with            hose that is swing at different suspension heights to            produce irregular waves.

GA 2019682128
AB    NOVELTY - The generator has an air filtering unit (100)                for performing primary filtering during introducing                process of outdoor air into an inflow pipe (110) by                an air blower (120) while being installed in a                water tank (200). An air knife is fixed in an inner                side of the air filter unit and provided with                multiple air nozzles. The water tank is provided                with a tubular body. The inflow pipe makes flow of                outdoor air passed through a lid of the water tank.                Multiple air stones (230) are floatably installed                in the water tank that is fixed with air supply                pipe (250) such that the air stones are immersed in                water stored in the water tank. The air supply pipe                is provided with a flexible hose that is swing at                different suspension heights to produce irregular                waves.
   USE - Clean air generator for use in a wall of a                building.
   ADVANTAGE - The generator is installed at an inlet of a                wall of the building for blowing compressed air                along outside air direction for predetermined time                by the air knife so as to maintain filter function                of an outside filter portion through filter                cleaning and perform primary filtering and                secondary filtering of the outdoor air by the                outside filter portion and the air stones immersed                in the water of the water tank, thus increasing                amount of dissolved oxygen and improving water                quality. The generator deposits ultrafine dust and                heavy metals passed through micro bubbles generated                by the air stones immersed in water of the water                tank and supplies indoor air with clean air                including outdoor oxygen through natural                humidification without changing temperature of a                room so as to reduce generation of indoor air                pollution by indoor pollutants including carbon                dioxide, radon and formaldehyde and maintain                comfortable indoor space by reducing size of the                indoor space.

PN JP2019100623-A
TI Combustion system e.g. internal combustion engine, comprises combustion unit for burning hydrogen gas and fuel, hydrogen generation unit for producing hydrogen gas, and fuel storage unit for storing fuel other than hydrogen gas.
AU SAITO Y
   SAKASHITA H
AE SHINENE KK (SHIN-Non-standard)
   SADO SEIMITSU KK (SADO-Non-standard)
   MITSUBA KAKO KK (MITS-Non-standard)
GA 201952060C
AB    NOVELTY - A combustion system comprises a combustion unit for burning hydrogen gas and fuel other than hydrogen gas, a hydrogen generation unit for producing hydrogen gas, and a fuel storage unit for storing fuel other than hydrogen gas. The hydrogen generation unit comprises cylindrical structure in which at least at one end open, a substrate in which a heating chamber is arranged inside cylindrical structure, a catalyst arranged so as to cover portion of outer peripheral surface of the cylindrical structure of the substrate, in which a catalyst metal is supported on a carbon sheet, an exterior material which accommodates the substrate in which the catalyst is arranged, a discharge port arranged at end side in length direction of pipe of the substrate, and discharges hydrogen gas, and supply port for supplying methanol aqueous solution between the substrate and the exterior material, arranged at the other end side in length direction of the pipe of the substrate.
   USE - Combustion system e.g. internal combustion engine and boiler.
   ADVANTAGE - The combustion system effectively reduces carbon dioxide emitted from combustion unit.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A combustion system comprises a combustion unit for burning hydrogen gas and fuel other than hydrogen gas, a hydrogen generation unit for producing hydrogen gas, and a fuel storage unit for storing fuel other than hydrogen gas. The hydrogen generation unit comprises cylindrical structure in which at least at one end open, a substrate in which a heating chamber is arranged inside cylindrical structure, a catalyst arranged so as to cover portion of outer peripheral surface of the cylindrical structure of the substrate, in which a catalyst metal is supported on a carbon sheet, an exterior material which accommodates the substrate in which the catalyst is arranged, a discharge port arranged at end side in length direction of pipe of the substrate, and discharges hydrogen gas, and supply port for supplying methanol aqueous solution between the substrate and the exterior material, arranged at the other end side in length direction of the pipe of the substrate, in which the aqueous solution modification of supplied methanol aqueous solution based on the catalyst is carried out, and hydrogen gas is produced.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The combustion unit is boiler or internal combustion engine. The carbon sheet is carbon cloth or carbon felt. The catalyst metal is composite of metal group (s) chosen from platinum, palladium, ruthenium, nickel or copper, or platinum, palladium, ruthenium, nickel and copper, and metal of other different from metal of the metal group. The other metal contains manganese and/or zinc. The catalyst metal is solid solution containing platinum, ruthenium and/or nickel. The catalyst supports metal particles containing catalyst metal. The average primary particle diameter of the metal particles is 10 nm or less. Preferred Process: The combustion is performed by supplying hydrogen gas and fuel other than hydrogen gas to the combustion unit at a ratio at which the heat utilization efficiency at the time of combustion is maximized. The catalyst is wound by outer peripheral surface of the substrate and is made into laminated structure. In the catalyst, the supported amount of catalyst metal supported by the carbon sheet is 15-150 g/L. The catalyst reforms the supplied aqueous methanol solution in the form of a superheated liquid film. The aqueous methanol solution supplied from the supply port is preheated while being circulated in the length direction of the cylinder of the substrate, and the aqueous solution is reformed by the catalyst in a state of being heated to a temperature exceeding the boiling point.
DC E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).)

   TONG P
   LI M
