   DAI J
AE INST MEDICAL BIOLOGY CHINESE ACAD MEDICA (MSUM-C)
GA 201935789X
AB    NOVELTY - Medium comprises Dulbecco's Modified Eagle                Medium/nutrient mixture F-12 (DMEM/F12) complete                medium, as basal medium which is supplemented with                10% fetal bovine serum, 1% double antibody, 1%                microglia growth supplement (MGS) and 0.04% B27                supplement.
   USE - The medium is useful for isolating, culturing                and purifying treeshrew microglia cell in                vitro.
   ADVANTAGE - The medium ensures that the microglia cells                with high purity and strong vigor.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for method                for isolating, culturing and purifying treeshrew                microglia cell in vitro, which involves (a) taking                the cortical part of the 1-2 days old treeshrew                brain tissue, cutting the brain tissue, digesting                with 0.25% trypsin and visually observing that                there is no obvious brain tissue mass, (b) adding                the equal volume 10% fetal bovine serum and 1%                double antibody in DMEM/F12 complete medium to                terminate digestion, repeatedly blowing to form a                single cell suspension, filtering, centrifuging the                filtrate, discarding the supernatant, then adding                DMEM/F12 complete medium containing 10% fetal                bovine serum and 1% double antibody and                resuspending into a single cell suspension, (c)                inoculating the single cell suspension obtained in                the step (b) into a culture flask coated with                L-poly-lysine, culturing at 37 degrees C under 5%                carbon dioxide for 55-60 minutes in an incubator,                completely replacing with new DMEM/F12 complete                medium containing 10% fetal bovine serum and 1%                double antibody after 3 days of culture and then                changing the medium once in every 3 days and (d)                changing with new DMEM/F12 complete medium                containing 10% fetal bovine serum and 1% double                antibody half a day before cell purification,                sucking out half medium, placing thee cell culture                flask upright for 3-5 minutes, gently tapping the                flask, and when a large number of suspended cells                appear under the microscope, the tapping is stopped                to obtain a cell suspension, inoculating the cell                suspension into a culture flask coated with                L-poly-lysine, changing the medium after 30-35                minutes, and completely replacing with the                above-mentioned and the half medium previously                sucked out.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Method: The step (a) involves                  removing the surface blood vessel and the                  meninges from the treeshrew brain tissue and then                  removing the cerebellum and the brain stem to                  obtain the cortical part of the treeshrew brain                  tissue and digesting for 8-10 minutes while                  repeatedly blowing 15-20 times. The step (b)                  involves centrifuging at 1300 revolutions per                  minute (rpm) for 5 minutes. In the step (c) or                  (d), the cell culture flask coated with                  L-poly-lysine is prepared by adding 1 ml 0.1-0.5                  mg/ml L-polylysine to the cell culture flask,                  shaking until the L-poly-lysine evenly spread in                  the bottom of the flask, allowing to stand for 5                  minutes, aspirating the liquid, leaving the cell                  culture flask in a incubator at 37 degrees C                  under 5% carbon dioxide to dry overnight, and                  rinsing with phosphate buffered saline (PBS)                  before inoculation. In the step (c), the cell                  culture flask is a 25 cm2 culture flask. The step                  (d) involves performing cell purification after                  7-9 days of culture.

PN CN109504395-A; CN109504395-B
TI Agent used for bio-modifying saline-alkali soil, comprises modifier containing cellulase, ligninase, protease, and green Trichoderma suspension, and modifier containing water-soluble lubricant and temperature-sensitive hydrogel particles.
AU ZHAO W
   XU S
   CHEN W
   QIN T
AE SHAANXI BIO-AGRIC INST (SHAA-Non-standard)
GA 201928634B
AB    NOVELTY - A saline-alkali soil bio-modifying agent comprises a modifier (a) comprising 2-8 vol.% cellulase, 3-8 vol.% ligninase, 2-8 vol.% protease, 5-10 vol.% green Trichoderma suspension (s1), 5-10 vol.% tropical Candida tropicalis suspension (s2), 8-15 vol.% Bacillus megaterium suspension (s3), 8-12 vol.% Bacillus licheniformis suspension (s4), and remainder of biochemical fulvic acid solution, and a modifier (b) comprising 12-15 vol.% water-soluble lubricant, 7-9 vol.% water-soluble metal chelating agent, 2-4 vol.% hydrogel agent, 3-5 vol.% carbon dioxide foaming agent, 0.5-1.5 vol.% dispersant, 10-12 vol.% biomass extract, and remainder of temperature-sensitive hydrogel particles. The number of viable fungi in suspension (s1) is 108 cfu/mL or more, number of viable yeast in suspension (s2) is 108 cfu/mL or more, number of viable bacteria in suspension (s3) is 1010 cfu/mL or more, and number of viable bacteria in suspension (s4) is 1010 cfu/mL or more.
   USE - Agent is used for bio-modifying saline-alkali soil (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The saline-alkali soil bio-modifying agent improves saline-alkali soil condition, and plant yield.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for bio-modification of saline-alkali soil, which involves deep-plowing saline-alkali soil by 40-50 cm, adding 20 L per acre modifier (b) to water at 40-45 degrees C, ultrasonically-processing to obtain a mixture, applying the mixture on the bottom of the deep pit, in which foam thickness is 8-10 cm and soil thickness is 5-10 cm, adding 100-200 ml per acre modifier (a) to water to obtain a mixture, and soaking seeds in the mixture for 4-8 hours, and spraying the soaked seeds on the soil surface, before planting, or involves deep-plowing saline-alkali soil with a depth of 10-12 cm from the root of the plant of 15-20 cm, adding 10 L per acre modifier (b) to water at 40-45 degrees C, ultrasonically-processing, adding 5-10 L per acre modifier (a) to water to obtain a mixture, and spraying the soaked seeds on the soil surface, while planting.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Composition: The biomass extract is prepared by mixing a seaweed extract, an oil residue extract, a soybean meal extract, a sweet potato root extract, and chicory extract in a ratio of 1:3:2:1:2, and pyrolyzing at high temperature. The modifier (a) can also be fermented in 40-80 L/1000 kg farmyard manure for 30 days, and fertilized at 800-1500 kg per acre.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The modifier (a) is prepared by separately culturing Trichoderma viride, C. tropicalis, B.megaterium, and B.licheniformis to obtain culture solutions, inoculating the inoculated using 10-15 vol.% fermenter, performing liquid culturing at a culture temperature of 25-35 degrees C for 24-48 hours to obtain suspensions (s1)-(s4), mixing a cellulase solution, a ligninase solution, protease solution and biochemical fulvic acid solution with the suspensions (s1)-(s4). The modifier (b) is prepared by mixing the temperature-sensitive hydrogel particles and a hydrogel agent under the protection of an inert atmosphere, heat-treating at 100-130 degrees C for 20-30 minutes, cooling to 10-20 degrees C at a rate of 10 degrees C/minute for 10-20 minutes to obtain a mixture (m1), adding dispersant to the water-soluble metal chelating agent, uniformly stirring, adding 100-200 times by volume of purified water for dilution, adding the mixture (m1), introducing the obtained product into a dialysis bag, dialyzing at 4-10 degrees C for 12-24 hours, purging with nitrogen for 15-20 minutes under vacuum and 35-40 degrees C environment, adding the biomass extract liquid and the water-soluble lubricant, uniformly stirring, adding carbon dioxide foaming agent. Preferred Composition: The hydrogel agent is a mixture of bioactive silicone and microbial oil in a volume ratio of 1:2. The water-soluble lubricant is a hyaluronic acid-type lubricant, a chitosan-type lubricant, and/or an Aloe-type lubricant.

PN DE102017121159-A1
TI Double tube ventilation element for deacidification of water, used in ventilation system used in treatment of raw water, has outer tube that is provided with outer tube ventilation openings on upper side and drainage opening at bottom.
AU KOLITSCH S
   SEYDEL C
AE WKS TECH GMBH (WKST-Non-standard)
GA 201923927M
AB    NOVELTY - The element (1) has a horizontally oriented inner tube (2) that is provided with inner tube ventilation openings (6) on the underside of the inner tube and an air inlet opening for introducing air which is formed into the inner tube. The inner tube is arranged within an outer tube (3). The outer tube has outer tube ventilation openings (7) on the upper side and a drainage opening at the bottom. The sum of surfaces of the inner tube ventilation openings of the inner tubes is greater than or equal to the sum of surfaces of the outer tube ventilation openings of the outer tube.
   USE - Double tube ventilation element for deacidification of water, used in ventilation system (claimed) used in treatment of raw water.
   ADVANTAGE - The uniform pressure is formed across the entire length of the outer tube, thus the uniform air entry into the water to be processed is realized at a lower differential pressure. The pressure gradient within the outer tube is avoided. The ingress of water into the inner tube is prevented with the downwardly directed holes in the inner tube. The water in the tube is prevented from becoming permanent with the drainage openings in the outer tube. The water that enters the ventilation element is permanently replaced due to the special arrangement of the holes, so that the risk of microbial contamination is minimized. The need for complex chemical cleaning is avoided. The double pipe ventilation elements are mechanically cleaned. The time required for the cleaning operations is reduced and the need for redundancy of the ventilation elements is avoided. Hence the investment cost is effectively reduced. The two tubes are arranged in a horizontal plane, so that the air escaping from the double-tube ventilation element is optimally distributed within a shallow water bath, thus, the effective discharge of carbon dioxide from the water is ensured.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a ventilation system.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a side view of the double tube ventilation element.
   Double tube ventilation element (1)
   Inner tube (2)
   Outer tube (3)

PN CN109181274-A
TI Antibacterial composite additive for sole material, comprises Ziziphora clinopodioides, mint leaves, bamboo charcoal fiber, tin sulfate solution, sodium pyrophosphate, polyurethane stock solution, PTFE rubber powder and gum Arabic solution.
AU RAO R
   RAO K
AE JIESHOU SHENGZE SHOE IND CO LTD (JIES-Non-standard)
GA 2019093192
AB    NOVELTY - An antibacterial composite additive comprises 5-15 pts. wt. Ziziphora clinopodioides, 10-15 pts. wt. mint leaves, 20-30 pts. wt. bamboo charcoal fiber, 10-15 pts. wt. tin sulfate solution, 1-2 pts. wt. sodium pyrophosphate, 30-40 pts. wt. polyurethane stock solution, 3-6 pts. wt. PTFE rubber powder and 5-10 pts. wt. gum Arabic solution.
   USE - Antibacterial composite additive for sole material.
   ADVANTAGE - The composite additive has excellent antibacterial property by the synergistic effect of Z.clinopodioides and mint leaves combined with bamboo charcoal fiber, enhanced dispersing ability by modification of bamboo charcoal fiber, excellent wear resistance and reduced wear rate and is difficult to agglomerate.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for preparation of antibacterial composite additive, which involves (1) drying Z.clinopodioides and mint leaves in shade, pulverizing to a particle size of 1-10 mm by a pulverizer, then adding to supercritical carbon dioxide extraction tank for 3-4 hours to obtain extract, subjecting extract to secondary extraction with ethanol, evaporating to remove the solvent, stop evaporating when the solution is thick and obtaining Z.clinopodioides extract and mint leaves extract, (2) adding bamboo charcoal fiber to 20-30 times 2-3% sodium hydroxide solution, ultrasonically processing at 10-20 degrees C for 20-30 minutes, then adding tin sulfate solution, stirring at 50-60 degrees C for 2-3 hours, filtering, washing 2-3 times with deionized water, drying at 70-80 degrees C for 10-20 hours and calcining at 350-400 degrees C for 1-2 hours to obtain tin oxide-modified bamboo charcoal fiber, (3) adding Z.clinopodioides extract and mint leaves extract to tin oxide-modified bamboo charcoal fiber, reacting at 40-50 degrees C, stirring at 200-300 rpm for 10-20 minutes, then adding sodium pyrophosphate, polyurethane stock solution, PTFE rubber powder and gum Arabic solution, stirring at 500-600 rpm for 20-30 minutes to obtain spinning solution, spinning the spinning solution through a spinning machine, solidifying, molding and pulverizing to a fiber with length of 50-100 nm.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The mass fraction of sodium hydroxide solution is 2-3%. The concentration of tin sulfate solution is 5-10%, which contains 5-8% sulfuric acid.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Composition: The solid content of the polyurethane stock solution is 20-25%. The solid content of gum Arabic solution is 30-35%.

AU YE F
AE UNIV XIAN JIANTONG (UNIV-Non-standard)
GA 201903928S
AB    NOVELTY - Gradient composition coating comprises                titanium carbide coating including a large number                of gray reinforcing phase particles of titanium                carbide and a small amount of gray-white iron                substrate, where the reinforcing phase particles                have uniform distribution of titanium carbide,                regular shape, square or spherical shape, and                particle size of 1-5 &#956; m with obvious                angles and corner.
   USE - Used as gradient composition coating.
   ADVANTAGE - The coating has strong metallurgical bond                force; and can improve wear resistance of the                surface of the metal substrate.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for                preparing coating comprises (a) removing rust from                the surface of the substrate, ultrasonic cleaning                with alcohol or acetone, where the base body is low                carbon steel or low carbon alloy steel; (b)                applying a layer of recarburizing agent to the                collective surface after rust cleaning, and                adjusting the recarburizing agent to paste state                with alcohol or acetone and then coating on the                surface of the substrate; (c) coating a layer of                insulation coating with a thickness of 1.5-2.5 mm;                (d) placing metal substrate coated with the thermal                insulation coating obtained in step (c) in a high                frequency inductor for surface carbonization                treatment, and controlling surface carbonization at                900-950&#176; C for 36- 72 hours; (e) uniformly                covering the ferrotitanium powder on the substrate                after the carbonization treatment in the step (d);                (f) placing the substrate obtained in step (e) in                an oven and pre-drying at 30-50&#176; C for                10-30 minutes; (g) placing substrate pre-dried in                step (f) on a workbench, performing a laser                cladding with a carbon dioxide laser, the laser                power is 2500-3000 W, the scanning speed is 3-6                mm/seconds, and the spot size is 10 mm x 2 mm, the                multi-pass bonding rate is 15-35%, and the                thickness of the cladding layer is 1-2 mm; and (h)                placing substrate after laser cladding obtained in                step (g) in an atmosphere-protected holding furnace                for heat treatment, and finally cooling to room                temperature with the furnace to obtain a                composition wear-resistant titanium carbide coating                on the surface of the metal substrate.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The gradient                  composition coating has a micro hardness of                  1120-1530 HV0.1 and the coating has a wear                  resistance of 5.3-7.5 times that of the                  substrate. The carburant is HYCPC-01, HYCPC-02 or                  HYCPC-03. The thickness of carburant is 0.5-1.5                  mm. The ferrotitanium powder has a particle size                  of 500-900 mesh and a purity of 50-65%. The                  protective gas during the heat treatment is argon                  gas, and the gas flow rate is 5-8 ml/minutes.                  Preferred Composition: The coating comprises 10                  wt.% sodium silicate nonahydrate, 30-40 wt.%                  deionized water, 22-4 wt.% nano silicon monoxide,                  8-25 wt.% hollow ceramic beads, 3-9 wt.% aluminum                  silicate fiber, 8-28 wt.% silicon dixide aerogel                  slurry, 0-2 wt.% potassium hexatitanate whiskers,                  20-2 wt.% rutile titanium(II) oxide and 0-8 wt.%                  titanium sol. Preferred Method: In the step (h)                  heat treatment is carried out by placing the                  substrate after laser cladding in an                  atmosphere-protected holding furnace to raise the                  temperature from room temperature to                  1000-1150&#176; C with heating rate of                  6&#176; C/minutes for 8-15 hours.

TI Detecting long-term multi-modal information of living cells in situ by providing long-term multi-modal information detection system comprising microfluidic chip live cell in situ culture module, and multimode information detection module.
AU HUANG G
   FU R
   SU Y
AE UNIV TSINGHUA (UYQI-C)
GA 201899977C
AB    NOVELTY - Long-term multi-modal information of living cells in situ, is detected by providing long-term multi-modal information detection system comprising a microfluidic chip live cell in situ culture module, a multimode information detection module, a multidimensional motion control module, and digital information storage processing and display module. The microfluidic chip living cell in situ culture module is provided with a microfluidic chip, and the microfluidic chip is provided with a micro cell capture positioning unit, a cell culture fluid input/output control microvalve, a temperature control unit, a temperature feedback sensor, and a humidity feedback sensor and carbon dioxide (CO2) concentration feedback sensor. The living cells to be detected are input into the microcell capture positioning unit through the microfluidic pipeline, so that the living cells to be detected form a single cell array in the microcell capture positioning unit.
   USE - The method is useful for detecting long-term multi-modal information of living cells in situ (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method does not affect cell activity and can perform long-term in situ detection of various information such as organelles, molecules, and ions.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a long-term multi-mode information detection system for living cell in situ culture, comprising a microfluidic chip live cell in situ culture module, a multimodal information detection module, a multidimensional motion control module and digital information storage processing and display module; the microfluidic chip living cell in situ culture module is used for culturing living cells and maintaining a living cell growth activity state; the multi-mode information detecting module is configured to use white light interference hyperspectral analysis, absorption or emission spectral analysis, electrical impedance, cell autofluorescence and/or excitation fluorescence to analyze cell morphology, organelle, molecule and ion distribution of living cells; and the digital information storage processing and display module is configured to control, by the multi-dimensional motion control module, movement of living cells in the living cell in situ culture module of the microfluidic chip, and obtain three-dimensional spatial position information of the living cells.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Method: The method involves long-term in-situ non-destructive detection of cell morphology, organelles, molecules, and ion distribution information of living cells to be detected in the microfluidic chip by anti-cell, auto-fluorescence, and/or fluorescence excitation. By stimulating fluorescence, organelles, molecules, ion stains or labeled probes are used to generate emission fluorescence under the excitation of excitation light, and the spatial dynamic distribution of organelles, molecules and ions in living cells to be detected and information over time are obtained. Autofluorescence is generated by biotinylation or ultraviolet light excitation to obtain spatial dynamic distribution of organelles, molecules and ions in living cells to be detected and information over time. Preferred Components: The organelle, molecule, ion stain or labeling probe comprises protein fusion calcium ion probe, such as GCaMP, GCaMP6 or GCaMP-X; and the fluorescent protein probe of the molecule and ion in the cell is green fluorescent protein (GFP) or red fluorescent protein (RFP).

The main binder is a mixture of slag powder and ash in a weight ratio of 6: 4 to 5: 5.
   USE - Aluminosilicate-based inorganic binder composition used in high acid resistant concrete composition for high-acid-resistant concrete water channel (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The aluminosilicate-based inorganic binder composition improves the acid resistance of concrete and replaces existing cement, contributes to prevention of global warming by reducing carbon dioxide emissions, utilizes slag powder and ash, which are industrial byproducts to produce environmentally friendly and high added value products and is excellent in durability, thus reduced the maintenance cost of the water pipe and extends the maintenance and replacement cycle.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) a method for preparing aluminosilicate-based inorganic binder composition, which involves using main binder including slag powder and ash, using 1 to 20 pts. wt. secondary binder having sodium hydroxide and sodium silicate, and 0.01 to 5 pts. wt. curing retarder having sodium phosphate;
   (2) pulverizing the mixture by adding 0.01 to 1 pts. wt. pulverizing auxiliary agent to 100 pts. wt. the premixing mixture, where weight ratio of the slag powder and the ash is 6: 4 to 5: 5;
   (3) mixing pre-mixed mixture at a speed of 10 to 100 rpm for 0.5 to 20 minutes;
   (4) using a vibrating ball mill at a speed of 500 to 3,000 rpm and a ball of 5 to 20 mm;
   (5) a high acid resistant concrete composition, which comprises 100 pts. wt. aluminosilicate inorganic binder composition, 50 to 500 pts. wt. fine aggregate, 150 to 600 pts. wt. coarse aggregate, 1 to 150 pts. wt. mixed water and 0.01 to 20 pts. wt. admixture; and
   (6) a high-acid-resistant concrete water channel formed, which is formed by placing the high-acid-resistant concrete composition on a mold.

GA 2018456606

The corresponding data is uploaded to the DCS system of the IoT, and the data is collected through the processor into one monitoring data, and detection is performed once every hour, and the multiple monitored data are collected into a chart or a bar graph. The power supply during the entire monitoring process is the storage of electricity through the solar cells to ensure that the entire process is not affected by the external circuit.
   USE - Method for monitoring environment based on IoT.

The impact of weather and geographical conditions is reduced, and equipment life is improved.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - The positioning information is matched with the original environment image data, and the environmental monitoring data is perfected, which is accurately identified in a distributed control system (DCS) of the IoT. The monitoring is performed synchronously after the environmental monitoring data is obtained, and carbon dioxide, carbon monoxide, sulfur dioxide, hydrogen sulfide, nitric oxide, particulate matter 2.5mm or less, particulate matter 10 mm or less, wind speed, temperature, and humidity are detected by several monitoring instruments, and corresponding data are obtained. PN CN108002419-A
TI Preparing light calcium carbonate involves adding marble slag after crushing, placing into reactor with deionized water, adding concentrated sulfuric acid, collecting precipitate, and adding hydrochloric acid solution.
AU HUANG H
AE LIUZHOU JIHUI MACHINE DESIGN CO LTD (LIUZ-Non-standard)
GA 201837515L
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing light calcium carbonate involves adding 200 pts. wt. of marble slag after crushing, placing into reactor with 500 pts. wt. of deionized water, adding 15 pts. wt. of concentrated sulfuric acid, collecting precipitate. The 50-100 pts. wt. of 3% of hydrochloric acid solution is added, and stirred to form solution system. The 3 pts. wt. of barium metal chelating agent is added to chelate barium ion in solution system, and filtered. The 50 pts. wt. of sodium carbonate solution is added to precipitate filtrate. The precipitate is dried to obtain calcium carbonate powder crude product.
   USE - Method for preparing light calcium carbonate used in medicine and building decoration field.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to prepare light calcium carbonate that has high purity and wide source range, and in cost effective manner.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing light calcium carbonate involves adding 200 pts. wt. of marble slag after crushing, placing into reactor with 500 pts. wt. of deionized water, adding 15 pts. wt. of concentrated sulfuric acid, collecting precipitate. The 50-100 pts. wt. of 3% of hydrochloric acid solution is added, and stirred to form solution system. The 3 pts. wt. of barium metal chelating agent is added to chelate barium ion in solution system, and filtered. The 50 pts. wt. of sodium carbonate solution is added to precipitate filtrate. The precipitate is dried to obtain calcium carbonate powder crude product. The crude calcium carbonate powder is calcined into muffle furnace with rate of 50-65 degrees C/minute at 700-1000 degrees C for 2 hours to obtain calcium oxide powder. The 100 pts. wt. of calcium oxide powder and 5000 pts. wt. of de-ionized water is added into carbonization reactor. The bag is equipped with porous titanium dioxide catalyst and de-ionized water. The ultrasonic processing power is 1000-1500 Watts. The temperature is 40-50 degrees C under the condition of inletting carbon dioxide gas in the process of carbonization. The pressure of the reaction system is 2-4 megapascal. The mixture is reacted for 45 minutes to obtain precipitate calcium carbonate crystal, and hot air dried calcium carbonate crystal to obtain light calcium carbonate product.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The porous titanium dioxide catalyst is prepared by adding 100 parts of ethanol solution, 1 parts of surfactant and 2 parts of ferric sulfate into reactor, starting stirring device to stir at the speed of 200-500 revolution per minute, adding 10 parts of tetrabutyl titanate at the speed of 2 mL/minute, continuously reacting for 2-3 hours, decompressing, distilling, using absolute ethyl alcohol to precipitate, collecting the precipitate, and using pure water to wash for 25 minutes. The washing strength is 30-50L/(m2). The precipitate is placed in muffle furnace to calcine to obtain doped titanium dioxide catalyst with iron ions. Preferred Composition: The amount of ethanol solution is 65-75%. The amount of sodium carbonate solution is 15%. The water content of the light calcium carbonate finished product is 0.5-1%. Preferred Condition: The drying temperature is 110-120 degrees C. The drying time is 5-8 hours.

PN CN108011101-A; CN108011101-B
TI Large-size and uniformly doped oxido oxo alumane            ceric oxide preparation involves adding certain amount            of soluble aluminum salt to cobalt salt solution, and            then configuring corresponding amount of cobalt and            aluminum mixed salt solution.
AU TIAN L
   LIU R
   CHEN Q
   QIN C
   YU Y
   ZHOU E
   XIONG T
   ZHANG K
AE QUZHOU HUAYOUGU NEW MATERIALS CO LTD (QUZH-Non-standard)
   HUAYOU COBALT CO LTD (HUAY-Non-standard)
GA 201838627K
AB    NOVELTY - Large-size and uniformly doped                oxido(oxo)alumane ceric oxide preparation involves                adding certain amount of soluble aluminum salt to                cobalt salt solution, and then configuring                corresponding mixed amount of cobalt and aluminum                mixed salt solution. A certain amount of pure water                is injected into the reactor as bottom liquid, and                then adjusted pH to 7.0-8.0 with ammonium carbonate                or ammonium bicarbonate solution at 40-60 degrees C                with stirring at a stirring speed of 140-150                revolutions per minute.
   USE - Method for preparing large-size and uniformly                doped oxido(oxo)alumane ceric oxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The method prepares the large-size and                uniformly doped oxido(oxo)alumane ceric oxide in a                simple and cost-effective manner with high specific                capacity and excellent cycle performance.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Large-size and uniformly doped                oxido(oxo)alumane ceric oxide preparation involves                adding certain amount of soluble aluminum salt to                cobalt salt solution, and then configuring                corresponding mixed amount of cobalt and aluminum                mixed salt solution. A certain amount of pure water                is injected into the reactor as bottom liquid, and                then adjusted pH to 7.0-8.0 with ammonium carbonate                or ammonium bicarbonate solution at 40-60 degrees C                with stirring at a stirring speed of 140-150                revolutions per minute. A certain amount of                cobalt-aluminum mixed salt solution, ammonium                carbonate and ammonium bicarbonate solution are                simultaneously added to the reactor to cause                precipitation reaction. The reaction is carried out                at 40-60 degrees C. The aluminum-doped cobalt                carbonate slurryis dehydrated and then washed with                an ammonium bicarbonate or ammonium carbonate                solution or an aqueous solution having carbon                dioxide to remove corresponding anions. The                aluminum-doped cobalt carbonate is dried, and then                mixed with the obtained spherical aluminum-doped                cobalt carbonate on a push-plate kiln or a rotary                kiln at 300-500 degrees C. The obtained alumina is                calcined in an air atmosphere to prevent                precipitated alumina, and then get large-size and                uniformly doped oxido(oxo)alumane ceric                oxide.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic sectional                diagramof the preparing large-size and uniformly                doped oxido(oxo)alumane ceric oxide.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The aluminum-doped                  eutectic aluminum doping amount is 500-5000 parts                  per million. The cobalt salt is selected from                  cobalt chloride, cobalt sulfate, and cobalt                  nitrate. The aluminum salt is a soluble metal                  salt corresponding to the cobalt salt.

TI New substituted phenyl containing compounds used as a catalyst i.e. heterogeneous catalyst in organic chemistry reaction e.g. reduction reactions in the presence of hydrogen gas i.e. the hydrogenation of alkenes, alkynes or arenes.
AU HUC V G
   MARTINI C A R
   ABDELLAH I
   SCHULZ E
AE UNIV PARIS SUD (UYPI-C)
   CENT NAT RECH SCI (CNRS-C)
   UNIV PARIS SUD (UYPI-C)
   CENT NAT RECH SCI (CNRS-C)
   FRENCH NAT CENT SCI RES (CNRS-C)
   CENT NAT RECH SCI (CNRS-C)
   UNIV PARIS-SACLAY (UYPA-Non-standard)
GA 201833946M
AB    NOVELTY - Substituted phenyl containing compounds (I) are new.
   USE - (I) are useful as a catalyst (preferably heterogeneous catalyst) in an organic chemistry reaction, preferably: reduction reactions in the presence of hydrogen gas i.e. the hydrogenation of alkenes, alkynes or arenes; oxidation reactions in the presence of oxygen, the carbon-carbon bond formation reactions i.e. Suzuki, Heck and Stille reactions of Kumada and Sonogashira; carbon-heteroatom bond formation reactions i.e. carboneazote, carbon-oxygen and carbon-sulfur bond formation; carbonylation reactions in the presence of carbon monoxide i.e. Fischer-Tropsch reaction; or reactions of carboxylations in the gas phase in the presence of carbon dioxide (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - (I): are easily separable from the product; and can capable to avoid leaching of the metal during the reaction in which it is engaged and obtaining finished products containing a very small amount of metal (i.e. less than 5 ppm).
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Substituted phenyl containing compounds (I) of formula (a1)-(a4) are new.
   n = 7-20 or greater than 20 (preferably 21-220);
   X = linear or branched 1-10C alkyl, a polyethylene glycol comprising 1-5 ethylene glycol units or (linear or branched 0-10C alkyl)-aryl including phenyl or naphthyl;
   R1 = linear or branched 1-8C alkyl, linear or branched 1-8C O-alkyl or O-(linear or branched 0-3C alkyl)-aryl;
   (M)(m+) = metal in the oxidation state m or metal aggregate comprising a set of metals in the oxidation state m;
   m = 0-3;
   L = at least one neutral or negatively charged ligand bound to the metal;
   t = 0 (in (a2)) or 1 (in (a4));
   Z = Q;
   Q = phosphorus ligands including phosphines, phosphonites, phosphinites or N-heterocyclic carbenes including 1,3 imidazolylidene, 1,3-imidazolinylidene, 1,2,4-triazolylidene, 1,3-benzimidazolylidene, 1,2,4-triazolylidene or 1,3-thiazolylidene; and
   Q1 = azoliums including 1,3-imidazolium, 1,3-imidazolinium, 1,3-benzimidazoliums, 1,2,4-triazoliums, 1,3-thiazoliums or precursors of phosphorus ligands including sulfides of phosphine.
   An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for preparing substituted phenyl containing compounds (I).
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preparation (Claimed): Preparation of (I) comprises e.g. contacting substituted phenyl containing compound of formula (II) with metal containing compounds of formula (QA) (III) in the presence of a base, to give (a2).V1 = leaving group, preferably halo including Cl, Br or I or sulfonates including OSO2CH3, OSO2(C7H7) or OSO2CF3; andA = alkali metal including Na, K or Li or H.
   Preferred Definitions:
   R1 = n-octyl, t-butyl, O-benzyl or O-alkyl, preferably t-butyl or O-benzyl;
   n = 8; and
   M = Ni, Pd, Ru, Rh, Cu or Pt.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

PT P
PN CN107927203-A
TI Edible blend oil comprises pine nut oil, peony seed oil, wheat germ oil, sunflower seed oil, sesame oil, lycopene, butyl hydroxyanisole, tocopherol, sodium citrate, cyclohexalcohol phosphate and additive.
AU 
AE CHANGSHA SHANDAO NEW MATERIAL TECHNOLOGY (CHAN-Non-standard)
GA 2018321118
AB    NOVELTY - Edible blend oil comprises 12-15 pts. wt. pine nut oil, 8-11 pts. wt. peony seed oil, 5-8 pts. wt. wheat germ oil, 4-9 pts. wt. sunflower seed oil, 5-8 pts. wt. sesame oil, 0.5-1.2 pts. wt. lycopene, 0.2-0.6 pts. wt. butyl hydroxyanisole, 1-4 pts. wt. tocopherol, 2-4 pts. wt. sodium citrate, 0.5-1.2 pts. wt. cyclohexalcohol phosphate and 1.3-2.5 pts. wt. additive, where the additive is mixture of palm oleic acid and allicin obtained by refining and extracting palm oil, the weight ratio of tocopherol, sodium citrate, cyclohexalcohol phosphate and additive is 1-4:2-4:0.5-1.2:1.3-2.5.
   USE - Used as edible blend oil.
   ADVANTAGE - The edible blend oil reduces the oil acid value and peroxide value.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for preparing edible blend oil, comprising (1) taking refined palm oil, using carbon dioxide supercritical extraction method separating palmitoleic acid, (2) dissolving garlicin in ethanol, ultrasonic oscillating under 40-60 degrees C for 5-10 minutes, allowing to stand for 2-5 hours, where the power of the ultrasonic is 500-550 W and the frequency is 360-400 KHz, (3) mixing palmitoleic and allicin, adjusting its pH value to 5.6-6.8, distilling under is 1.2-2.3 KPa vacuum degree and 150-180 degrees C temperature condition, filtering for 2-3 times, taking the filtrate as additive, (4) taking pine seed oil, peony seed oil, wheat germ oil, sunflower seed oil and sesame oil, mixing completely, pumping into refrigerator, cooling to below 15 degrees C, filtering to obtain the mixed oil, and (5) adding lycopene, butyl hydroxyanisole, tocopherol, sodium citrate, cyclohexalcohol phosphate and additive into mixed oil, mixing uniformly, ultrasonic oscillating for 3-5 minutes, allowing to stand for 2-4 hours, filtering to remove filter residue, and distilling the mixed oil under 25-40 KPa vacuum degree and 130-140 degrees C temperature condition of for 2-3 times to obtain edible blend oil product.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The weight ratio of tocopherol, sodium citrate, cyclohexalcohol phosphate and additive is 3:3:1:2. The weight ratio of the palmitoleic and allicin is 1-3:2-5, preferably 2:3. Preferred Method: In the step (i), the temperature of supercritical carbon dioxide extraction is 160-170 degrees C, the extracting pressure is 5-10 MPa, the flow rate of carbon dioxide is 20-40 kg/hour, and the extracting time is 2-5 hours. The power of the ultrasonic wave in the step (5) is 220-250 W and the frequency is 90-100 KHz.
DC D13 (Other foodstuffs and treatment - including preservation of food, milk, milk products, butter substitutes, edible oils and fats, non-alcoholic beverages, artificial sweeteners, food additives and animal feed (A23B-L).); E19 (Other organic compounds general - organic compounds of unknown or indefinite structure; general mixtures of many types; organic reactions (e.g. nitration, resolution) when applied generally.)

PN CN107744594-A
TI Medical ultrasonic coupling agent comprises plant extract, sodium hydroxide, ethyl cellulose, sodium acetate, carbomer, glycerol, polyglutamic acid, and sterile deionized water.
AU DAI T
   SUN Z
   LI D
   LI J
   NI L
AE UNIV JILIN (UYJI-C)
GA 2018189658
AB    NOVELTY - A medical ultrasonic coupling agent comprises 5-10 wt.% plant extract, 0.2-0.5 wt.% 1 mol/L sodium hydroxide, 0.05-0.1 wt.% ethyl cellulose, 0.5-1 wt.% sodium acetate, 0.1-0.2 wt.% carbomer, 2-4 wt.% glycerol, 0.1-0.2 wt.% polyglutamic acid, and balance sterile deionized water. The plant extract comprises 15-20 pts. wt. luteolin, 8-15 pts. wt. Forsythia, 5-10 pts. wt. chamomile, 3-5 pts. wt. nightshade, 3-5 pts. wt. Rehmannia leaf, and 1-2 pts. wt. sapanwood.
   USE - Medical ultrasonic coupling agent.
   ADVANTAGE - The medical ultrasonic coupling agent permeates into the skin, has good conduction effect, good lubricating performance, high safety, no environment pollution, good imaging effect and image resolution, and has sterilizing, disinfecting, and antiinflammatory effects and is easy to unfold.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for preparation of medical ultrasonic coupling agent comprising: (A) weighing plant extract components; (B) taking Forsythia and Arctium lappa, mixing, crushing, sieving with 60-mesh sieve, and countercurrent supercritical carbon dioxide extracting; (C) weighing crushed luteolin, adding 6 times the amount of acetone solution, soaking for 2 hours, reflux-extracting for 3 times for 3 hours each time, combining all extracts, filtering, and concentrating; (D) weighing Solanum, freeze-drying for 12 hours at - 20 degrees C, crushing, adding into salt water, soaking for 6 hours, filtering, distilling for 3 hours, collecting distillate, and condensing until relative density of 1.3-1.4; (E) weighing sapanwood, drying, crushing, sieving, adding 60% ethanol at ratio of 1 g:15 mL, soaking for 2 hours, heat-preserving at 60 degrees C, centrifuging, and concentrating; (F) mixing prepared components and stirring; (G) taking coupling agent raw materials; (H) weighing carbomer, adding sterile deionized water, standing for 2 hours, and stirring for 30 minutes at 800 revolutions/minute (rpm); (I) weighing ethyl cellulose solution, adding glycerol and plant extract, and stirring; and (J) adding sodium hydroxide, stirring for 10 minutes at 500 rpm, adding sodium acetate, stirring for 30 minutes at 500 rpm, and standing for 24 hours.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The countercurrent supercritical carbon dioxide extraction is done at 35 MPa and 50 degrees C for 3 hours.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - PHARMACEUTICALS - Preferred Components: The plant extract comprises 20 pts. wt. luteolin, 10 pts. wt. Forsythia, 8 pts. wt. chamomile, 4 pts. wt. nightshade, 4 pts. wt. Rehmannia leaf, and 1 pt. wt. sapanwood.
   ACTIVITY - Antiinflammatory.
DC B04 (Natural products and polymers. Including testing of body fluids (other than blood typing or cell counting), pharmaceuticals or veterinary compounds of unknown structure, testing of microorganisms for pathogenicity, testing of chemicals for mutagenicity or human toxicity and fermentative production of DNA or RNA. General compositions.); B02 (Fused ring heterocyclics.); A96 (Medical, dental, veterinary, cosmetic.)

TI Method for processing data e.g. heart rate,            associated with patient to predict e.g. mortality risk,            involves transmitting predicted health condition of            subject to computing device, where predicted condition            is displayed at computing device.
AU TEKUMALLA L S
   RAJAN V
AE CONDUENT BUSINESS SERVICES LLC (XERO-C)
   CONDUENT BUSINESS SERVICES LLC (XERO-C)
GA 2018166789
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves receiving multi-view data                associated with a human subject from data sources                and/or a computing device (106) over a                communication network (114) by using a transceiver,                and predicting health condition of the human                subject with a trained classifier based on the                received multi-view data associated with the human                subject, by using a processor. The predicted health                condition of the human subject is transmitted to                the computing device over the network by a                transceiver, where the predicted health condition                of the human subject is displayed at the computing                device.
   USE - Method for processing data e.g. cholesterol                level, heart rate, blood pressure, breath                carbon-dioxide concentration, breath oxygen                concentration, stroke score, blood albumin level,                blood sodium level, total blood count, blood                glucose/sugar level, blood hemoglobin level, and                blood platelet count, associated with a human                subject i.e. patient, to predict health condition                e.g. mortality risk, disease risk, disease symptom,                onset of a disease, recovery from a disease, and                effect of medications for a disease (all claimed),                of the human subject by using a computing device                for healthcare and insurance applications. Uses                include but are not limited to a laptop, a personal                digital assistant (PDA), a tablet computer, a                smartphone and a phablet.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables updating/tuning the                classifier using the historical data such that the                classifier is able to predict the categories in the                historical data with greater accuracy so as to                efficiently process the multi-view data in real                time.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for the                following:
   (1) a system for processing data to predict                health condition of a human subject
   (2) a computer program product comprising a                set of instructions for processing data to predict                health condition of a human subject.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic block diagram                of a system environment for processing data to                predict health condition of a human subject.
   Application server (102)
   Database server (104)
   Computing device (106)
   Biosensors (108A-108C)
   Communication network (114)

TI Manufacture of functional cooling water used in vehicle engine, involves heating natural mineral water containing silicate, cooling, adding sodium adipate, reacting, filtering, obtaining liquid material, and mixing with water or antifreeze.
AU KIM C J
AE KIM C J (KIMC-Individual)
GA 201811190X

   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Manufacture of functional cooling water involves heating natural mineral water containing silicate mineral at 90-100 degrees C for 1-30 minutes to completely dissolve the silicate mineral, rapidly cooling to 0-10 degrees C, adding disodium adipate obtained by reacting adipic acid with sodium hydroxide, reacting the resultant reaction product at 40-90 degrees C for 1-10 hours, filtering the complex product to obtain a liquid material, and mixing the liquid material with 5-10 wt.% water or antifreeze. PN RU2775633-C1
TI METHOD FOR DEVELOPING A HIGH-VISCOSITY OIL OR            BITUMEN FIELD WITH GAS INJECTION.
AU ZVEZDIN E I
   AMERKHANOV M I
   AKHMETZIANOV F M
   AKHMETSHIN N M
AE TATNEFT SHASHIN PUBLIC STOCK CO (TATN-C)
GA 202296075T
AB    NOVELTY - Invention relates to the oil industry and can                be used in the development of deposits of                high-viscosity oil or bitumen. A method for                developing a high-viscosity oil or bitumen field                with gas injection includes placing horizontal                steam-cyclic wells on the periphery of the field,                placing paired horizontal injection and production                wells in the central part of the field, locating                temperature and pressure control devices in                horizontal production wells, injecting steam at an                early stage of development in horizontal                steam-cyclic and paired horizontal injection and                production wells for warming up the productive                formation, stopping the supply of steam for                thermocapillary impregnation, then the transfer of                horizontal production wells for production                extraction, horizontal injection wells - for steam                injection, operation of horizontal steam-cyclic                wells in cycles - sequential steam injection and                production extraction. At the stage of growth of                the steam chamber, the operating parameters of the                production well are determined - the liquid flow                rate, the water cut of the produced product and the                temperature of the liquid, pairs of horizontal                injection and production wells are isolated with                the production well operating parameters unchanged                or varying within no more than 8% during the month                of operation. Joint injection of gas with steam at                a concentration of 0.5 m3 of gas to 1 m3 of steam                is initiated into the selected paired injection                wells. Methane or carbon dioxide is used as the                gas. The development is continued, while repeating                the above operations for separating pairs of                horizontal injection and production wells with                unchanged or changing within no more than 8%                operating parameters of the production well during                the month of operation and joint injection of gas                with steam.
   USE - Oil industry.
   ADVANTAGE - Increasing the efficiency of developing a                high-viscosity oil or bitumen field by increasing                oil recovery by reducing the viscosity of oil and                increasing its mobility when gas is dissolved in                it, increasing the coverage of the reservoir by                development due to the lateral expansion of the                steam chamber and a corresponding increase in the                oil recovery factor, reducing the volume of                generated steam and partial replacement with gas,                as well as expanding the technological capabilities                of the development method through the use of                associated gas - methane or CO2, which is formed in                the process of steam generation at boiler                plants.
1 cl, 1 dwg

AB    NOVELTY - Dry extraction of an activated or defluorinated, or sintered concentrate of lithium aluminosilicate, comprises a primary rock comprising lithium, rubidium and caesium, or from a sewage treatment plant after removal of the minerals tin and tungsten, or only from a previously obtained semi-concentrate or concentrate of lithium mica, or spodumene after separation of heavy fluorine minerals, including topaz and calcium fluoride, heating at 500-800 degrees C for 0-60 minutes without or with the effect of sodium bicarbonate, sodium carbonate, sodium metasilicate, calcium oxide, calcium hydroxide, sodium bisulfate, sodium sulfate, calcium chloride, ammonium chloride, sodium chloride, aluminum potassium sulfate, or potassium alum is subjected to selected fluxes, with the possibility of opening the surface, i.e. activating the lithium raw material or the processing product, removing fluorine and other gaseous by-products from the mica.
   USE - The method is useful for smelting volatilization and leaching processes to extract sulfate, oxide, hydroxide, halide and carbonate from alkali metals, preferably lithium, rubidium and caesium (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method is simple and economical.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Dry extraction of an activated or defluorinated, or sintered concentrate of lithium aluminosilicate, comprises a primary rock comprising lithium, rubidium and caesium, or from a sewage treatment plant after removal of the minerals tin and tungsten, or only from a previously obtained semi-concentrate or concentrate of lithium mica, or spodumene after separation of heavy fluorine minerals, including topaz and calcium fluoride, heating at 500-800 degrees C for 0-60 minutes without or with the effect of sodium bicarbonate, sodium carbonate, sodium metasilicate, calcium oxide, calcium hydroxide, sodium bisulfate, sodium sulfate, calcium chloride, ammonium chloride, sodium chloride, aluminum potassium sulfate, or potassium alum is subjected to selected fluxes, with the possibility of opening the surface, i.e. activating the lithium raw material or the processing product, removing fluorine and other gaseous by-products from the mica, as well as from associated minerals of fluorine, and converting lithium aluminosilicate into the form of soluble or volatilizable salts, facilitating fine grinding of lithium mica, the possibility of increasing the mischmetal mica/additive mixture in favor of lithium-mica, the possibility of temperature reduction when using the leach residue agglomerate process and reduction of carbon dioxide emissions in smelting volatilization recovery processes of lithium salts.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The lithium mica or spodumene is heated with the selected fluxes, producing a melt without additives of the limestone or gypsum type, in which it softens, and lithium goes into a soluble state after a reaction with additive salts, after which the sintered lithium concentrate is finely ground and lithium is leached into solution, refined and precipitated as lithium carbide, lithium fluoride and lithium hydroxide. The semi-concentrate or concentrate of lithium mica, lepidolite and the ore spodumene is exposed to temperature by heating in a dryer, e.g. fluid or continuous dryer, or in a furnace, e.g. roller, belt, rotary, shaft or fluid furnace, with a movable or immobile burned layer provided with a protective layer of graphite or paint to prevent adhesion of lithium-mica, with which the surface of lithium mica or spodumene is opened and it goes into the amorphous state, with the simultaneous possibility of defluorination with the removal of unwanted fluorine, with the addition of flux facilitating and accelerating the removal of gaseous fluorine compounds from the produced melt at a lower temperature, and at the same time the lithium compounds with flux salt, e.g. carbonate, sulfate, hydroxy, chloride - react for their easier leaching or volatilization at lower heating temps erature, after which the processed, more brittle and better grindable activated or defluorinated lithium mica or rock is either ground ultrafinely below the size of 20-100 mu m and leached in a hot aqueous, acidic or alkaline solution or the activated Li mica after addition of flux is machined to the sintered state, followed by ultra-fine grinding and leaching of lithium, rubidium, and cesium compounds into a hot solution. The insoluble residue after leaching aluminosilicate contains active meta-aluminosilicate with pozzolanic activity, which is the more reactive with calcium hydroxide the less additives of the limestone, gypsum, magnesite, calcium chloride, sodium sulfate containing lithium-mica prior to heating in the leach residue agglomerate process or melt volatilization process. After the leaching, preferably in an acidic environment, lithium-mica is decolourised, with the result that the degree of whiteness and color of pozzolan is increased. The products of kaolin, clay, bentonite, marl, marley lime, magnesite, talc or slate type are used as binders or additives, increasing the amount of kaolin, clay, bentonite increasing the pozzolanic activity of meta aluminosilicate or pozzolan is increased.
   EXAMPLE - None given.

An open cycle absorption chiller system (114) comprises an absorber section operable to receive the flow of the exhaust gas from the turbo-expander and to mix the flow of exhaust gas with refrigerant solution within the absorber section, where the refrigerant solution operable to absorb water from the exhaust gas as the exhaust gas passes through the refrigerant solution and the absorber section routes the flow of exhaust gas to the turbo-compressor after the flow of exhaust gas is passed through the refrigerant solution.

   ADVANTAGE - The specific volume of flow of exhaust gas is significantly and efficiently reduced with as little pressure drop as possible after exiting the turbo-expander and prior to entering the turbo compressor. The overall efficiency of the bottoming cycle power system is increased. The volume and mass of water vapor in the flow of the exhaust gas are efficiently decreased. The energy consumption of the system is reduced. The carbon dioxide is captured in a flow of gas from the system with little energy consumption. The temperature of the gas is maintained above a temperature that will enable the gas to readily flow up its associated stack.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for: (1) a combined power system comprising: a primary power system comprising an internal combustion engine having a rotatable crankshaft; (2) a recycled plastic processing system comprising an extruder; (3) an open cycle absorption chiller system comprising: an absorber section operable to receive a flow of exhaust gas from a combustion source and to mix the flow of exhaust gas with a first refrigerant solution within the absorber section.

   CASALE SA (CASA-C)
GA 202263496T
AB    NOVELTY - Prilling (P1) of a urea melt (1), comprises distributing urea melt in the form of droplets in a prilling tower and urea droplets solidify as they fall down the tower in counter-current with upflowing air, and the urea melt comprises at least one calcium lignosulfonate and carboxymethyl starch as a prilling additive and the urea melt contains no added formaldehyde.
   USE - The process is useful for prilling of urea melt to produce formaldehyde-free urea product (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The process: can increase the mechanical properties of the urea prills and therefore can replace formaldehyde; utilizes calcium lignosulfonate and carboxymethyl starch can be used as additives and are fully safe, they do not pose health concerns, and their cost is similar to that of the common formaldehyde-containing solutions like UF80 and are economically viable; and provides formaldehyde-free urea, which is safe and economically acceptable.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for producing (P2) solid urea comprising reacting ammonia and carbon dioxide at a synthesis pressure obtaining a reaction effluent comprising urea, water and unconverted ammonium carbamate, processing the reaction effluent at a lower pressure to recover unconverted reagents, obtaining a purified aqueous solution of urea (2), subjecting the aqueous solution of urea to an evaporation process to remove water and obtain the urea melt, and the process further comprising adding at least one calcium lignosulfonate and carboxymethyl starch, as a prilling additive, to the purified urea aqueous solution or to the urea melt, where no formaldehyde is added to the purified aqueous solution or to the urea melt, so that the urea melt contains no added formaldehyde, converting the urea melt with the additive into a solid product by a prilling process, the prilling process includes distributing the urea melt in the form of droplets in a prilling tower and the droplets fall down the tower and solidify in the presence of a counter-current upflowing cooling air.

   Aqueous solution of urea (2)
   Recovery section (3)

   Evaporation section (5)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The urea melt was converted into droplets by means of at least one showerhead or by means of at least one rotating prilling bucket. The urea melt was produced in a urea synthesis plant, where urea is synthesized from ammonia and carbon dioxide at a synthesis pressure obtaining a reaction effluent containing urea, water and unconverted ammonium carbamate and the reaction effluent is processed in one or more recovery sections (3) at a lower pressure to recover unconverted reagents, obtaining a purified aqueous solution of urea, the aqueous solution being processed in an evaporation section (5) to remove water and obtain the urea melt, the prilling additive was added to the purified aqueous solution before evaporation or is added to the urea melt. In the process (P2): 0.1-1 wt.%, preferably 0.3-0.7 wt.% prilling additive was added to the aqueous solution or to the urea melt on dry basis relative to the urea; both calcium lignosulfonate and carboxymethyl starch were used as prilling additives, and they are added separately or as a mixture to the purified aqueous solution before evaporation or to the urea melt, where the urea is feed grade. Preferred Components: The total quantity of the prilling additive in the urea melt is 0.1-1 wt.%, preferably 0.3-0.7 wt.% on dry basis relative to the urea. The quantity of calcium lignosulfonate in the urea melt is 0.7 wt.% on dry basis relative to the urea. The quantity of carboxymethyl starch in the urea melt is 0.5 wt.% on dry basis relative to the urea. The urea melt comprises, as a prilling additive, calcium lignosulfonate and carboxymethyl starch, preferably in a ratio of 1:1 by weight. The urea melt comprises more than 98 wt.%, preferably more than 99 wt.% urea. The obtained urea prills: have an average diameter not greater than 2 mm, preferably bit greater than 1 mm; and are feed grade.
   EXAMPLE - None given.

PN CN114457115-A
TI Constructing recombinant baculovirus expressing goose astrovirus capsid protein open reading frame 2 involves using GAstV-ORF2 gene recombinant baculovirus expression vector, designing primers to respectively amplify ORF2 gene of goose astrovirus and pFastBacHTA linearized vector.
AU SHAO H
   XIE Q
   LI T
   WAN Z
   QIN A
   REN D
   YE J
   ZHANG X
   GAO W
AE UNIV YANGZHOU (UYYZ-C)
GA 202268561J
AB    NOVELTY - Constructing recombinant baculovirus expressing goose astrovirus capsid protein open reading frame 2 (ORF2) involves using GAstV-ORF2 gene recombinant baculovirus expression vector, designing primers to respectively amplify ORF2 gene of goose astrovirus (GAstV) and a pFastBacHTA linearized vector, connecting and transforming to escherichia coli DH5 alpha competent cells, coating the competent cells on an LB agar plate added with Amp+, culturing overnight, identifying positive clone by colony PCR, and constructing a recombinant donor pFastBacHTA-GAstV-ORF2.
   USE - Method for constructing recombinant baculovirus expressing goose astrovirus capsid protein open reading frame 2
   ADVANTAGE - The method helps to amplify the goose astrovirus capsid protein ORF2 full-length plate and construct a baculovirus recombinant vector, and transfect Sf9 cells to obtain baculovirus capable of expressing capsid protein ORF2.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Constructing recombinant baculovirus expressing goose astrovirus capsid protein open reading frame 2 (ORF2) involves using GAstV-ORF2 gene recombinant baculovirus expression vector, designing primers to respectively amplify ORF2 gene of goose astrovirus (GAstV) and a pFastBacHTA linearized vector, connecting and transforming to escherichia coli DH5 alpha competent cells, coating the competent cells on an LB agar plate added with Amp+, culturing overnight, identifying positive clone by colony PCR, and constructing a recombinant donor pFastBacHTA-GAstV-ORF2. The baculovirus expressing recombinant GAstV-ORF2 protein is packaged by transforming the successfully constructed pFastBacHTA-GAstV-ORF2 plasmid into a DH10Bac competent cell, and obtaining a recombinant positive clone by using a blue-white spot screening technology, selecting positive single clone, carrying out overnight amplification culture at 37degrees Celsius, extracting plasmid, simultaneously taking baculovirus transfer vector plasmid without target fragment as negative control, and verifying that the positive clone is baculovirus plasmid successfully carrying ORF2 gene by PCR amplification by utilizing pUC/M13 Forward and pUC/M13 Reverse primers. The baculovirus plasmid carrying the GAstV-ORF2 gene is transfected into Sf9 cells at 27degrees Celsius cultured in incubator under carbon dioxide (CO2) free condition for 5-7 days, and the cytopathic condition is observed every day. The cell supernatant is collected when cytopathic effect reaches 70%, and stored at -80degrees Celsius, and virus passage is carried out, and when the virus is transmitted to the third generation, then the virus is preserved as seed virus and amplification culture is carried out to obtain the recombinant baculovirus expressing the goose astrovirus capsid protein ORF2.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Conditions: The primers for PCR amplification of the linearized pFast-Bac-HTA linearized vector include upstream primer, downstream primer, upstream and downstream primers for PCR amplification of the goose astrovirus ORF2 gene comprises 27-42 nucleobases of (SEQ ID NOs: 1-4), that are taagaatctagagcctgcagtctcgag, and catgtaggcctttgaattccgcgcgcttcg. The ORF2 gene is cloned to a pFastBacHTA vector by a one-step cloning method to obtain pFastBacHTA-GAstV-ORF2. The constructed plasmid pFastBacHTA-GAstV-ORF2 is transformed into DH10Bac to obtain a baculovirus plasmid carrying the ORF2 gene. The baculovirus plasmid carrying the GAstV-ORF2 gene is transfected into Sf9 cells to obtain the recombinant baculovirus expressing the novel goose astrovirus capsid protein ORF2.

PN CN114243044-A
TI Gas diffusion layer for improving water management            ability of fuel cell, comprises supporting layer carbon            paper and micro-porous layer, where microporous layer            is multi-layer gradient structure.
AU LIU Y
   WU G
AE INST COAL CHEM CHINESE ACAD SCI (CAXI-C)
GA 202249279J
AB    NOVELTY - Gas diffusion layer comprises supporting layer                carbon paper and microporous layer. The microporous                layer is multi-layer gradient structure. The                micro-layer of cathode gas diffusion layer is                three-level gradient structure comprising                microporous a, microporous layer b, and microporous                layer c. The microporous layer a is coated with                hydrophobic agent and carbon powder mixed slurry on                carbon paper. The microphobic layer b is coated on                carbon nano-tube mixture slurry. A carbon nanotubes                are coated on microporous layer c, and carbon                powder mixture is mixed with the carbon nanofiber                slurry, the microporous layer b and the carbon                dioxide mixture are coated with hydrophilic                agent.
   USE - Gas diffusion layer used for improving water                management ability of fuel cell.
   ADVANTAGE - The gas diffusion layer has improved fuel cell                water management capability and simple operation                process, and improves fuel cell high efficiency                power generation performance.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method                of preparing gas diffusion layer for enhancing                water management of fuel cell, which                involves:
   a. selecting carbon nanotubes with diameter of                30-100 nm, and oxidizing carbon nanotubes by                adopting acid oxidation method or electrochemical                oxidation method;
   b. mixing carbon powder, dispersing agent,                isopropanol and hydrophobic agent according to                different proportions, and uniformly mixing by                stirring, ultrasound and sanding to prepare first                and second slurry, mixing oxidized carbon nano                tube, dispersing agent and isopropanol in                proportion, and uniformly mixing by stirring,                ultrasonic treatment and sanding to prepare                third;
   c. coating first slurry on surface of support                layer carbon paper and drying to form a microporous                layer a, coating second slurry on surface of                microporous layer a and drying to form microporous                layer b, coating third slurry on the surface of the                microporous layer b and drying to form a                microporous layer c, and finishing coating of anode                gas diffusion layer, coating third slurry on the                surface of the support layer carbon paper and                drying to form microporous layer c', coating second                slurry on the surface of the microporous layer c'                and drying to form a microporous layer b', and                finishing coating of the cathode gas diffusion                layer; and
   d. roasting cathode/anode gas diffusion layer                in nitrogen atmosphere to obtain gas diffusion                layer capable of improving water management                capability of the fuel cell.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows schematic structural view                of a gas diffusion layer. (Drawing includes                non-English language text).
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The carbon powder is                  acetylene black, Vulcan (RTM: Powdered carbon                  black) XC-72 and Ketjen black. The hydrophobic                  agent is polytetrafluoroethylene,                  tetrafluoroethylene-perfluoroalkyl vinyl ether                  copolymer, tetrafluoroethylene-ethylene                  copolymer, fluorinated ethylene propylene                  copolymer and polyvinylidene fluoride.

PN CN114213196-A
TI Recycling of acetylene sludge in acetylene production, involves collecting materials, livestock and poultry manure and plant residues, roots, stems and leaves, collecting calcium carbide slag produced in acetylene production, and reserving.
AU LU X
   ZHAO K
AE ANHUI HWASU CORP (ANHU-Non-standard)
GA 202246659J
AB    NOVELTY - Recycling of acetylene sludge in acetylene production involves collecting the materials, collecting the livestock and poultry manure and plant residues, roots, stems and leaves, collecting the calcium carbide slag produced in acetylene production, and reserving it for future use; material processing, sealing and composting livestock and poultry manure into organic rot, anaerobic fermentation of plant residues, roots, stems and leaves into plant base material, which is reserved for future use; removing the impurities, carry out screening and air separation of carbide slag to remove impurities; purifying and drying, purifying, dehydrating and drying the carbide slag to obtain dry carbide slag; mixing and stirring, discharging the dry carbide slag into the pulverizer, pulverizing it into powder, then preparing powdered calcium oxide, and stirring with the calcium carbide slag powder to obtain a mixed powder.
   USE - Method for recycling of acetylene sludge in acetylene production
   ADVANTAGE - The method provides the reducing soil acidity, improving soil structure, and the activity is stronger, no carbon dioxide, more environment-friendly, and has low cost.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Recycling of acetylene sludge in acetylene production involves collecting the materials, collecting the livestock and poultry manure and plant residues, roots, stems and leaves, collecting the calcium carbide slag produced in acetylene production, and reserving it for future use; material processing, sealing and composting livestock and poultry manure into organic rot, anaerobic fermentation of plant residues, roots, stems and leaves into plant base material, which is reserved for future use; removing the impurities, carry out screening and air separation of carbide slag to remove impurities; purifying and drying, purifying, dehydrating and drying the carbide slag to obtain dry carbide slag; mixing and stirring, discharging the dry carbide slag into the pulverizer, pulverizing it into powder, then preparing powdered calcium oxide, and stirring with the calcium carbide slag powder to obtain a mixed powder; secondary pulverization, pass the mixed powder material into a slag to ripen to obtain calcium hydroxide coarse material, and then carry out secondary pulverization to the coarse material to obtain calcium hydroxide powder; mixing raw materials, adding organic humus to the plant base material, stirring uniformly, and undergoing secondary fermentation to obtain fertilizer; and drying and granulating, adding calcium hydroxide powder into the fertilizer, slightly dissolving and stirring uniformly, then heating and drying by thermal radiation, and granulating on a roll.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - AGRICULTURE - Preferred Method: The method further involves after collecting livestock and poultry manure, the livestock and poultry manure is heated and pulverized and stirred to collect plants. After the residual roots, stems and leaves are pulverized to a length of 2-3 cm, high-density compaction and baling are carried out with a baler, and the carbide slag is collected, and the calcium carbide slag without any treatment is selected. The livestock and poultry manure is sealed and composted for 7-10 days to obtain organic rot, and in this process, the temperature is controlled at 40-50 degreesC, and ammonium bicarbonate is added, the plant residues, roots, stems and leaves are sealed and stacked, anaerobic fermentation is performed for 20-30 days, and the temperature is controlled at 15-25 degreesC. Before removing impurities, industrial waste slag fly ash is collected in a power plant, and the fly ash is treated as s proportion of 15 % is added to the calcium carbide slag to reduce the moisture content of the carbide slag, and in further process the calcium carbide slag with reduced moisture is put into the vibrating screen at a constant speed to remove impurities with large particles with a particle size greater than 8 cm. The cyclone is used to separate the carbide slag, and controlling the wind speed to 2-6 m/second, the pressure is 300-600 pa, and separating the coarse particles with a particle size greater than 5 cm. the method further involves using the suspension type drying device for dehydrating and drying, controlling the temperature at 100-120 degreesC., removing containing methanol, tetrahydrofuran, organic waste liquid of methyl acetate mixture, obtaining dry carbide slag with low water content to 1-2 %, at the same time, discharging the waste liquid into the purifying device for filtering liquid. The carbide slag powder and powdered calcium oxide are stirred in the stirrer, according to the mass ratio of 1:0.5-1:1, the stirring time is 20-30 degreesC., in acetylene production. The curing temperature is set to be 50- 80 degreesC., the reaction time is controlled at 30-80 minutes, and in the secondary crushing process, the control temperature is 25-500 degreesC., the crushing time is 15-30 minutes, and obtaining the calcium hydroxide powder. The adding fermentation bacteria for 15 days under the condition of 30-35 degreesC, 15 days, turning one time every 3 days, at the same time, water filling and ventilating, controlling the water content at 60 %, fermenting for one time, then ventilating and supplying oxygen to ferment for 15 days to obtain the fertilizer. The method further involves adding the calcium hydroxide powder into the fertilizer, mixing according to the mass ratio of 0.5:1.5-0.5:2.0, and adding water to stir at a constant speed of 1-3 hours by a stirrer, so that the calcium hydroxide powder is slightly dissolved in the fertilizer, then heating and drying, adjusting the water content of the mixture is 5-8 %, and finally granulating into spherical particles.

TI Constructing glioma macrophages ligand system comprises placing brain glioma tissue of fresh surgical excision in tissue preservation solution, rinsing tissue with dulbecco's phosphate-buffered saline, resuspending tissue with medium and inoculating on Transwell chamber, and culturing.
AU BIAN X
   YAN Z
   LIU Y
   PING Y
   SHI Y
   LIU Q
AE UNIV CHINESE PEOPLES LIBERATION ARMY NO (UYCH-C)
GA 202242581K
AB    NOVELTY - Constructing glioma macrophages ligand system comprises placing brain glioma tissue of fresh surgical excision in tissue preservation solution, immediately introducing to the laboratory at 4degrees Celsius, immediately processing the tissue, cutting off the tumor capsule and necrotic hemorrhagic tissue were with microscissors in the petri dish, rinsing the tissue for three times with dulbecco's phosphate-buffered saline and cutting to a size of 0.5-1 mm3, transferring to a centrifuge tube to resuspend the tissue with medium, centrifuging, and discarding the supernatant, adding erythrocyte lysate into the pellet obtained by centrifugation, mixing and lysing uniformly, centrifuging, aspirating the supernatant and resuspending with the culture medium and centrifuging, repeating for three times, aspirating the supernatant, resuspending the tissue with medium and inoculating on the Transwell chamber, and culturing.
   USE - The method is useful for constructing glioma macrophages ligand system.
   ADVANTAGE - The method has good phenotypic stability.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Constructing glioma macrophages ligand system comprises placing brain glioma tissue of fresh surgical excision in tissue preservation solution, immediately introducing to the laboratory at 4degrees Celsius, immediately processing the tissue, cutting off the tumor capsule and necrotic hemorrhagic tissue were with microscissors in the petri dish, rinsing the tissue for three times with dulbecco's phosphate-buffered saline and cutting to a size of 0.5-1 mm3, transferring to a centrifuge tube to resuspend the tissue with medium, centrifuging, and discarding the supernatant, adding erythrocyte lysate into the pellet obtained by centrifugation, mixing and lysing uniformly, centrifuging, aspirating the supernatant and resuspending with the culture medium and centrifuging, repeating for three times, aspirating the supernatant, resuspending the tissue with medium and inoculating on the Transwell chamber, culturing the chamber in a 37degrees Celsius, 5% carbon dioxide incubator, replacing the new medium every 2-3 days, transferring the Transwell chamber and adding into the culture system to continue the culture, where the culture system includes a liquid medium without phenol red neural basis, B-27 additive with vitamin A removed, 2-carboxyethyl phosphine hydrochloride, L-alanyl-L-glutamine, insulin transferrin-selenium-sodium pyruvate additive, macrophage colony stimulating factor, type II interferon, interleukin 4, human epidermal growth factor and human basic fibroblast growth factor.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Components: The culture system includes a liquid medium without phenol red neural basis, 0.5-3 wt.% B-27 additive with vitamin A removed, 1-5 mmol/l 2-carboxyethyl phosphine hydrochloride, 2-5 mmol/l L-alanyl-L-glutamine, 1-5 wt.% insulin transferrin-selenium-sodium pyruvate additive, 5-20 ng/ml macrophage colony stimulating factor, 10-50 ng/ml type II interferon, 10-50 ng/ml interleukin 4, 5-20 ng/ml human epidermal growth factor and 5-20 ng/ml human basic fibroblast growth factor and further comprises 1-5 wt.% insulin non-essential amino acid. The agent is used to rinse the tissue is phosphate buffered saline.Preferred Method: The centrifugation is carried out at 300-500 revolutions/minute for 3-10 minutes.

GA 2021B9010Y
