A second sewage bottle is connected to the first sewage bottle through a first water pump. Multiple first pipes are connected to the first sewage bottle. A filter bottle is arranged in a filter chamber. The filter bottle is connected to a medicine bottle and a third sewage bottle through a second water pump and a third water pump. A second conduit is arranged in the filter bottle. A support plate is arranged in the filter chamber. Multiple activated carbon filter elements are arranged on the support plate. The output end of a fourth duct is communicated with the outer wall of the box housing, and a valve is provided on the fourth duct.

The honeycomb activated carbon is collided with the exhaust gas, thus improves the efficiency of honeycomb activated carbon to adsorb exhaust gas. The device can quickly discharge the heat on the radiating surface of the semiconductor refrigeration fin out of the box. The sliding table drives the support plate to rise and fall, thus makes the upper end of the cylinder housing is separated from the first pipe, which is convenient for taking out the filter element.

PN US2021261489-A1; US11236033-B2
TI Producing alkaline-earth oxide and carboxylic acid            from alkaline earth cation-carboxylic acid anion salt,            comprises reacting alkaline-earth cation-carboxylic            acid anion salt with aqueous sulfur dioxide, desorbing            sulfur dioxide, separating the alkaline-earth-sulfite            solid, and calcining.
AU NOVEK E
AE INNOVATOR ENERGY LLC (INNO-Non-standard)
GA 2021987718
AB    NOVELTY - Producing (M1) alkaline-earth oxide and                carboxylic acid from alkaline earth                cation-carboxylic acid anion salt, comprises (i)                reacting alkaline-earth cation-carboxylic acid                anion salt with aqueous sulfur dioxide to produce                aqueous alkaline-earth-bisulfite and aqueous                carboxylic acid solution, (ii) desorbing sulfur                dioxide from the aqueous alkaline-earth-bisulfite                and aqueous carboxylic acid solution to produce                alkaline-earth-sulfite solid and aqueous carboxylic                acid solution, (iii) separating the                alkaline-earth-sulfite solid from the aqueous                carboxylic acid solution, and (iv) calcining the                alkaline-earth-sulfite solid to produce                alkaline-earth-oxide and/or cement.
   USE - The method is useful for producing                alkaline-earth oxide and carboxylic acid from                alkaline earth cation-carboxylic acid anion                salt.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: recycles calcium oxide in citric                acid production process; eliminates or greatly                reduces need for virgin calcium oxide input and                sulfuric acid input, waste products; significantly                reduces incremental costs, significantly reduces or                eliminates carbon dioxide emissions from citric                acid production; and utilizes iron sulfite or                manganese sulfite which is practically insoluble in                water if formation of bisulfite salts is avoided or                minimized by employing stoichiometric                concentrations of aqueous sulfur dioxide and/or                minimizing residence time, and acid gases with                regeneration or recovery properties.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included                for:
   (1) producing (M2) calcium oxide and                carboxylic acid from calcium cation-carboxylic acid                anion salt, comprising reacting calcium                cation-carboxylic acid anion salt with aqueous                sulfur dioxide to produce aqueous calcium bisulfite                and aqueous carboxylic acid, desorbing sulfur                dioxide from the aqueous calcium bisulfite and                aqueous carboxylic acid solution to produce calcium                sulfite solid and aqueous carboxylic acid solution,                separating the calcium sulfite solid from the                aqueous carboxylic acid solution, and calcining the                calcium sulfite solid to produce calcium oxide                and/or cement; and
   (2) producing (M3) calcium oxide and citric                acid from calcium citrate, comprising reacting                calcium citrate with aqueous sulfur dioxide to                produce aqueous calcium bisulfite and aqueous                citric acid, desorbing sulfur dioxide from the                aqueous calcium bisulfite and aqueous citric acid                solution to produce calcium sulfite solid and                aqueous citric acid solution, separating the                calcium sulfite solid from the aqueous citric acid                solution, and calcining the calcium sulfite solid                to produce calcium oxide and/or cement.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The method (M1) further                  comprises: separating aqueous carboxylic acid                  solution into isolated carboxylic acid and water;                  absorbing sulfur dioxide with water to generate                  aqueous sulfur dioxide for the reacting step;                  absorbing the desorbed sulfur dioxide in water to                  produce aqueous sulfur dioxide; absorbing the                  sulfur dioxide in water to produce aqueous sulfur                  dioxide; reacting the alkaline-earth-oxide with                  water to produce alkaline-earth-hydroxide;                  reacting the alkaline-earth-hydroxide with                  solution comprising carboxylic acid to form                  alkaline-earth cation-carboxylic acid anion salt;                  separating the alkaline-earth cation-carboxylic                  acid anion salt using solid-liquid separation;                  calcining is conducted under conditions to                  substantially prevent the formation of sulfate;                  producing precipitated alkaline-earth-carbonate                  from the alkaline-earth-hydroxide; and separating                  sulfur dioxide from the isolated water. In the                  method (M1): calcining said                  alkaline-earth-sulfite solid produces sulfur                  dioxide; reacting produces heat, where the heat                  is used in the process; separating comprises                  solid-liquid separation step; the process is                  conducted in presence of low diatomic oxygen                  environment; alkaline-earth-hydroxide is                  dissolved in water; isolated water comprises                  dissolved sulfur dioxide; and reacting produces                  heat, where heat is used desorb sulfur dioxide,                  and separate water.Preferred Components: In the method (M1):                  the alkaline-earth cation comprises calcium                  cation and magnesium cation; the carboxylic acid                  anion salt comprises citric acid anion salt; at                  least a portion of the alkaline-earth                  cation-carboxylic acid anion salt is a solid;                  precipitated alkaline-earth-carbonate comprises                  calcium carbonate and/or magnesium carbonate; and                  alkaline-earth-hydroxide comprises calcium                  hydroxide.
   EXAMPLE - Solid calcium carbonate was reacted with                sulfur dioxide-rich solution to produce calcium                sulfite, gaseous carbon dioxide and sulfur                dioxide-lean solution, the solid calcium sulfite                was separated from the sulfur dioxide-lean                solution, and the separated calcium sulfite was                thermally decomposed to produce gaseous sulfur                dioxide.

PN CN112293574-A
TI Chinese herbal medicine feed additive includes Amomum hainanensis, fructus alpiniaeoxyphyllae, licorice slices, lily, nutmeg, Magnolia officinalis, dandelion, Verbena, purse, and Chlorella.
AU YU M
   WANG G
AE HENGSHUI LIKANG BIOTECHNOLOGY CO LTD (HENG-Non-standard)
GA 2021167199
AB    NOVELTY - Chinese herbal medicine feed additive includes 10-100 pts. wt. of Amomum hainanensis, 20-70 pts. wt. of fructus alpiniaeoxyphyllae, 5-40 pts. wt. of licorice slices, 5-30 pts. wt. of lily, 3-50 pts. wt. of nutmeg, 10-50 pts. wt. of Magnolia officinalis, 5-30 pts. wt. of dandelion, 20-50 pts. wt. of Verbena, 10-40 pts. wt. of purse, and 8-10 pts. wt. of Chlorella.
   USE - Chinese herbal medicine feed additive.
   ADVANTAGE - The Chinese herbal medicine feed additive is safe, non-toxic and side effects, no drug residues, safe and reliable, can improve the immunity of animal pups, and at the same time has the functions of eliminating food and dehumidification, regulating qi and transforming accumulation, invigorating qi and strengthening the spleen, and promotes the healthy growth of animals and other functions, which can effectively prevent the occurrence of diarrhea and anorexia in young animals.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for preparing Chinese herbal medicine feed additive, which involves:
   (A) putting Amomum villosum in a dryer and heating to 30-40 degrees C, and after 30-60 minutes, heating to 40-50 degrees C, and when the fruit shrinks and softens, taking out and putting in a bamboo basket and pressing overnight;
   (B) making the peel and the seeds close together, and then drying them in a dryer at 60-70 degrees C, and then using a grinder to pulverize the dried Amomum villosum to powder;
   (C) washing the lily, dandelion, verbena, and purse, chopping, putting in a dryer at 50-70 degrees C for 2-4 hours, and then taking out and using a crusher to crushing into powder;
   (D) using a pulverizer to pulverize fructus alpiniaeoxyphyllae, licorice flakes, nutmeg, and magnolia into powder;
   (E) washing the chlorella with water and drying the surface attachments at room temperature, and then placing in an oven at 40-60 degrees C to dry the excess water;
   (F) cutting chlorella into pieces and ground into chlorella powder, and then placing the chlorella powder in a methanol-chloroform mixed solvent, and extracting the filtrate by refluxing;
   (G) evaporating the filtrate into a Chlorella extract, and extract the Chlorella extract using a supercritical carbon dioxide extraction method to obtain the Chlorella extract; and
   (H) stirring and mixing and the chlorella extract uniformly to obtain the feed additive product.

PN CN112159047-A; CN213506466-U
TI Device for collecting and recycling loss anaerobic sludge of anaerobic reactor, comprises loss sludge collecting and recycling device shell, and its middle is vertically provided with water inlet water distribution groove.
AU JING C
   SHAO T
AE SHANGHAI FUSEN ENVIRONMENTAL TECHNOLOGY DEV CO LTD (SHAN-Non-standard)
   SHANGHAI FUSEN ENVIRONMENTAL TECHNOLOGY (SHAN-Non-standard)
GA 202107005F
AB    NOVELTY - The device comprises a loss sludge collecting and recycling device shell (26). The middle of the loss sludge collecting and recycling device shell is vertically provided with a water inlet water distribution groove (5) whose top portion is a water inlet connection port, and its bottom is located below the water inlet water distribution groove which is provided with a tapered loss anaerobic sludge sedimentation collecting groove (20). The loss anaerobic sludge sedimentation collecting groove through the recycling outlet (21) is connected with the recycling pump front pipeline (23) through the reflux spiral centrifugal pump.
   USE - Device for collecting and recycling loss anaerobic sludge of anaerobic reactor.
   ADVANTAGE - The device effectively recycles the drained sludge carried in anaerobic effluent and return to the anaerobic reactor, ensures anaerobic system with high concentration of anaerobic sludge in anaerobic system, and greatly avoids the enterprise supplementing the huge cost of fresh granular sludge.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for collecting and recycling lost anaerobic sludge from an anaerobic reactor, which involves:
   (A) outputting anaerobic treated wastewater carrying the lost anaerobic sludge in the anaerobic reactor through the overflow outlet into the anaerobic effluent degassing standpipe, flowing anaerobic effluent into the anaerobic effluent degassing standpipe, dissolving unseparated carbon dioxide and biogas in the anaerobic effluent and attaching it to anaerobic sludge lost by small particles;
   (B) transporting sludge-containing wastewater by anaerobic effluent degassing riser, entering it to inlet water distribution tank, locating vertical inlet water distribution slot in inlet water distribution tank, and distributing waste water to be separated from mud and water on both sides;
   (C) separating small particles or flocculent anaerobic sludge contained in anaerobic effluent from the wastewater;
   (D) collecting effluent from the effluent collection tank, and discharging it to subsequent treatment facilities; and
   (E) using lost anaerobic sludge sedimentation collection tank to collect and store the lost anaerobic sludge separated from the anaerobic effluent and sequentially passing it through the reuse outlet and the front pipeline of the reuse pump, and then returning the return pipe to anaerobic reactor under the action of the return screw centrifugal pump.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of device.
   Water inlet water distribution groove (5)
   Tapered loss anaerobic sludge sedimentation collecting groove (20)
   Recycling outlet (21)
   Recycling pump front pipeline (23)
   Loss sludge collecting and recycling device shell (26)

TI Corneal limbus stem cell culture medium comprises fetal bovine serum, madecassoside, follistatin-related protein, transferrin, glutamine, penicillin mixture, insulin, hydrocortisone, cholera toxin and platelet-derived factor.
AU ZHAN Z
   QI G
   LIU S
AE GUANGDONG KANGDUN BIOLOGICAL ENG TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (GUAN-Non-standard)
GA 2021046603
AB    NOVELTY - Corneal limbus stem cell culture medium comprises 8-10 wt.% fetal bovine serum, 10-15 mu g/ml madecassoside, 0.3-0.8 mu g/ml follistatin-related protein, 10-20 mg/ml transferrin, 0.3-0.5 mg/ml glutamine, 2-3 ml 100x penicillin mixture, 5-8 mu g/ml insulin, 0.2-0.5 mu g/ml hydrocortisone, 10-15 ng/ml cholera toxin, 3-5 ng/ml platelet-derived factor and remaining of Dulbecco's modified Eagle medium/nutrient mixture F-12 (DMEM/F12) (1:1).
   USE - The culture medium is used for culturing corneal limbus stem cells (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The culture medium promotes growth of corneal limbus stem cells, up-regulates cell viability, increases cell proliferation activity, ensures excellent stability, completely delays the exhaustion of expansion capacity and inhibits their differentiation, and maintains the characteristics of limbus stem cells.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for culturing corneal limbus stem cells using the culture medium, which involves (i) using corneal limbus stem cell basic culture medium DMEM/F12 (1:1), adding fetal bovine serum, madecassoside, follistatin-related protein, transferrin, glutamine, 100x penicillin mixture, insulin, hydrocortisone, cholera toxin and platelet-derived factors, and mixing to prepare corneal limbus stem cell culture medium, (ii) cutting the corneal limbus stem cell tissue block into small pieces, rinsing with Hank's solution for 2-3 times in a sterile environment, adding 30-50 times the volume of digestive juice to digest for 10 minutes until the tissue is loose, mixing 1.25 g/l trypsin digestion solution with 0.2 g/l EDTA at a ratio of 1:1, discarding the digestion solution, washing with Hank's solution for 2-3 times, adding the corneal limbus stem cell culture medium of step (i), aspirating the cell culture medium and adding an equal amount of new corneal limbus stem cell culture medium, repeating for 1-2 times, centrifuging at 1000 rpm for 5 minutes, and discarding the supernatant, and (iii) adding the pelleted cells obtained in step (ii) to the corneal limbus stem cell culture medium to resuspend, plating in a perforated culture plate, culturing at 37 degrees C, 95% humidity, 5% carbon dioxide in an incubator, and changing the liquid next day to obtain the corneal limbus stem cells.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Composition: The concentration of the follistatin-related protein is 0.5-0.6 mu g/ml follistatin-related protein. The concentration of the madecassoside is 12-13 mu g/ml. The corneal limbus stem cell culture medium comprises 9 wt.% fetal bovine serum, 12-13 mu g/ml madecassoside, 0.5-0.6 mu g/ml follistatin-related protein, 15 mg/ml transferrin, 0.4 mg/ml glutamine, 2.5 ml 100x penicillin mixture, 7.5 mu g/ml insulin, 0.3 mu g/ml hydrocortisone, 12 ng/ml cholera toxin, 4 ng/ml platelet-derived factor and remaining of DMEM/F12 (1:1). The added amount of the corneal limbus stem cell culture medium in step (ii) is 5-6 times the volume of the corneal limbus stem cells. The added amount of the corneal limbus stem cell culture medium in step (iii) is 2-3 times the volume of the precipitated cells. Preferred Conditions: In step (iii), the seeding density of the resuspended cells in the perforated culture plate is 1x 105 cells/cm2.

TI Device for treating wastewater by using electrolysis combined with biochar-LDH composite material comprises rotor of thermostatic magnetic stirrer set in high-salt organic wastewater to be treated in electrolytic cell.
AU LIU Q
   ZHANG P
   ZHANG S
   HOU D
AE UNIV SHANGHAI (USHN-C)
GA 2020B3022N
AB    NOVELTY - Device for treating wastewater by using electrolysis combined with biochar-LDH composite material comprises DC stabilized power supply (1), cathode graphite plate (2), anode graphite plate (3), electrolytic cell (4) and constant temperature magnetic stirrer (5). A rotor of the thermostatic magnetic stirrer is set in the high-salt organic wastewater to be treated in the electrolytic cell, and the rotation speed of the rotor is controlled through the rotation speed adjustment button, mixing the high-salt organic wastewater to be treated, and monitoring and adjusting the temperature of the liquid in the electrolytic cell through the temperature display. The cathode graphite plate, anode graphite plate and biochar-LDH particles are placed in the high-salt organic wastewater to be treated in the electrolytic cell. The cathode graphite plate and the anode graphite plate are placed in the electrolytic cell as electrodes.
   USE - Used as device for treating wastewater by using electrolysis combined with biochar-LDH composite material.
   ADVANTAGE - The device: combines adsorption method with electrochemical oxidation method; uses the effect of external electric field to degrade or reduce toxicity of pollutants in water while adsorbing; degrades organic pollutants into carbon dioxide, water or other small molecular substances without additional reagents, which reduces secondary pollution; aims at the insufficiency of existing methods for treating high-salt organic wastewater; and achieves the ideal treatment effect of high-salt organic wastewater under low-cost and simple operation.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for electrolyzing combined with biochar-LDH composite material to treat high-salt organic wastewater, comprising adding waste water to be treated in the electrolytic cell, simultaneously adding sodium sulfate as electrolyte, adding biochar-LDH material, adjusting DC voltage, adding magnetic rotor to the wastewater to be treated, stirring at no lower than room temperature to carry out the electrolysis reaction, during the reaction, uniformly dispersing the biochar-LDH in the electrolyte in a suspended state, and measuring the COD concentration in the water after the reaction, and discharging the wastewater supernatant after the electrolysis reaction is the treated water.

TI New polyol block copolymer used in lubricant            composition and surfactant composition, comprises            polycarbonate block and polyethercarbonate blocks.
AU LEELAND J
   KABIR R
   RILEY K
   KEMBER M
AE ECONIC TECHNOLOGIES LTD (ECON-Non-standard)
   ECONIC TECHNOLOGIES LTD (ECON-Non-standard)
   ECONIC TECHNOLOGIES LTD (ECON-Non-standard)
GA 2020A7080J
AB    NOVELTY - Polyol block copolymer comprising a                polycarbonate block and polyethercarbonate blocks,                is new.
   USE - Polyol block copolymer used as lubricant                composition and surfactant composition (all                claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The polyol block copolymer improves polyol                stability, reduces cycle times, increases process                safety, increases strength, and increases chemical                resistance.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Polyol block copolymer of formula                B'-A-Z'-Z-(Z'-A-B')n comprising a polycarbonate                block of formula (-A'-Z'-Z-(Z'-A)n-) (A) and                polyethercarbonate blocks (B), is new.
   n = t-1;
   t = the number of terminal OH group residues                on the block A;
   A = a polycarbonate chain having at least 70%                carbonate linkages;
   B' = a polyethercarbonate chain having 50-99%                ether linkages and at least 1 % carbonate linkages,                and
   Z'-Z-(Z')n = a starter residue.
   INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the                following:
   (1) a composition, which comprises polyol                block copolymer and one or more additives selected                from catalysts, blowing agents, stabilizers,                plasticisers, fillers, flame retardants, and                antioxidants;
   (2) a polyurethane, which is produced from the                reaction of a polyol block copolymer or a                composition and a (poly)isocyanate;
   (3) a isocyanate terminated polyurethane                prepolymer, which comprises a reaction product of a                block copolymer or a composition with an excess of                (poly)isocyanate;
   (4) a process for producing a polycarbonate                ether carbonate polyol block copolymer, which                involves using a first reaction in a first reactor                and a second reaction in a second reactor, where                the first reaction is the reaction of a carbonate                catalyst with carbon dioxide and epoxide, in the                presence of a starter and/or solvent to produce a                polycarbonate polyol copolymer and the second                reaction is the reaction of a DMC catalyst with the                polycarbonate polyol copolymer of the first                reaction and CO2 and epoxide to produce the polyol                block copolymer; and
   (5) a process for producing a polyol block                copolymer in a multiple reactor system, which                involves using first and second reactor where a                first reaction takes place in the first reactor and                a second reaction takes place in the second                reactor, where the first reaction is the reaction                of a carbonate catalyst with CO2 and epoxide, in                the presence of a starter and/or solvent to produce                a polycarbonate polyol copolymer and the second                reaction is the reaction of a DMC catalyst with the                polycarbonate polyol compound of the first reaction                and CO2 and epoxide to produce the polyol block                copolymer.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Process: The process further                  includes a third reaction which includes the                  reaction of the poly block copolymer of the                  second reaction with a monomer or further polymer                  in the absence of a DMC catalyst to produce a                  higher polymer.Preferred Components: The starter compound                  is selected from monofunctional starter                  substances such as alcohols, phenols, amines,                  thiols and carboxylic acid, for example, alcohols                  such as methanol, ethanol, 1 - and 2-propanol,                  1-2-butanol, linear or branched 3-20C-monoalcohol                  such as tert-butanol, 3-buten-1-ol, 3-butyn-1-ol,                  2-methyl-3-buten-2-ol, 2-methyl-3-butyn-2-ol,                  propargyl alcohol, 2-methyl-2-propanol, 1                  -tert-butoxy-2-propanol, 1-pentanol, 2-pentanol,                  3-pentanol, 1 - hexanol, 2-hexanol, 3-hexanol,                  1-heptanol, 2-heptanol, 3-heptanol, 1 -octanol,                  2-octanol, 3-octanol, 4-octanol, 1-decanol,                  1-dodecanol, phenol, 2- hydroxybiphenyl,                  3-hydroxybiphenyl, 4-hydroxybiphenyl,                  2-hydroxypyridine, 3- hydroxypyridine, and                  4-hydroxypyridine, mono-ethers or esters of                  ethylene, propylene, polyethylene, polypropylene                  glycols such as ethylene glycol mono- methyl                  ether and propylene glycol mono-methyl ether,                  phenols such as linear or branched 3-20C alkyl                  substituted phenols, for example nonyl-phenols or                  octyl phenols, monofunctional carboxylic acids                  such as formic acid, acetic acid, propionic acid                  and butyric acid, fatty acids, such as stearic                  acid, palmitic acid, oleic acid, linoleic acid,                  linolenic acid, benzoic acid and acrylic acid,                  and monofunctional thiols such as ethanethiol,                  propane-1 -thiol, propane-2-thiol,                  butane-1-thiol, 3-methylbutane-1 -thiol,                  2-butene-1 -thiol, and thiophenol, or amines such                  as butylamine, tert-butylamine, pentylamine,                  hexylamine, aniline, aziridine, pyrrolidine,                  piperidine, and morpholine, and/or selected from                  diols such as 1,2-ethanediol (ethylene glycol), 1                  -2-propanediol, 1,3-propanediol (propylene                  glycol), 1 ,2-butanediol, 1 -3-butanediol,                  1,4-butanediol, 1,5- pentanediol, 1,6-hexanediol,                  1 ,8-octanediol, 1 ,10-decanediol,                  1,12-dodecanediol, 1,4-cyclohexanediol,                  1,2-diphenol, 1 ,3-diphenol, 1,4-diphenol,                  neopentyl glycol, catechol, cyclohexenediol, 1                  ,4-cyclohexanedimethanol, dipropylene glycol,                  diethylene glycol, tripropylene glycol,                  triethylene glycol, tetraethylene glycol,                  polypropylene glycols (PPGs) or polyethylene                  glycols (PEGs) having an Mn of up to about                  1500g/mol, such as PPG 425, PPG 725, PPG 1000,                  triols such as glycerol, benzenetriol,                  1,2,4-butanetriol, 1,2,6-hexanetriol,                  tris(methylalcohol)propane,                  tris(methylalcohol)ethane,                  tris(methylalcohol)nitropropane, trimethylol                  propane, polyethylene oxide triols, polypropylene                  oxide triols and polyester triols, tetraols such                  as calix(4)arene, 2, 2-bis(methylalcohol)-1                  ,3-propanediol, erythritol, pentaerythritol or                  polyalkylene glycols (PEGs or PPGs) having 4-OH                  groups, polyols, such as sorbitol or polyalkylene                  glycols (PEGs or PPGs) having 5 or more -OH                  groups, or compounds having mixed functional                  groups including ethanolamine, diethanolamine,                  methyldiethanolamine, and phenyldiethanolamine.                  The block (A) further includes ether linkages.                  The composition further includes a                  (poly)isocyanate. The catalyst is a suitable                  urethane catalyst for the (poly)isocyanate and                  the polyol block copolymer reaction such as                  tertiary amine compounds and/or organometallic                  compounds.The monomer or further polymer is a                  (poly)isocyanate and the product of the third                  reaction is a polyurethane. The DMC catalyst is                  preactivated, optionally in reactor 2 or                  separately, optionally where the DMC is                  pre-activated with a starter compound or with the                  reaction product of the first or second reaction.                  The DMC catalyst is pre activated, optionally in                  reactor 2 or separately, optionally with the                  polycarbonate polyol copolymer is pre-activated                  with the polyol block copolymer. The epoxides are                  selected from cyclohexene oxide, styrene oxide,                  ethylene oxide, propylene oxide, butylene oxide,                  substituted cyclohexene oxides (such as limonene                  oxide, C10H16O or                  2-(3,4-epoxycyclohexyl)ethyltrimethoxysilane,                  C11H22O), alkylene oxides (such as ethylene oxide                  and substituted ethylene oxides), unsubstituted                  or substituted oxiranes such as oxirane,                  epichlorohydrin, 2-(2-methoxyethoxy)methyl                  oxirane (MEMO), 2-(2-(2-                  methoxyethoxy)ethoxy)methyl oxirane (ME2MO),                  2-(2-(2-(2- methoxyethoxy)ethoxy)ethoxy)methyl                  oxirane (ME3MO), 1 ,2-epoxybutane, glycidyl                  ethers, glycidyl esters, glycidyl carbonates,                  vinyl-cyclohexene oxide, 3-phenyl-1                  ,2-epoxypropane, 2,3-epoxybutane, isobutylene                  oxide, cyclopentene oxide, 2,3-epoxy-1                  ,2,3,4-tetrahydronaphthalene, indene oxide, and                  functionalized 3,5-dioxaepoxides. The carbonate                  catalyst is a metal catalyst includes a phenol or                  phenolate ligands. The carbonate catalyst is a                  bimetallic complex comprising phenol or phenolate                  ligands.The polycarbonate polyol copolymer or the                  polyol block copolymer is used to pre-activate                  the DMC catalyst in the second reaction, prior to                  addition of epoxide and CO2. Preferred                  Conditions: The polyol molecular weight (Mn) is                  in the range 300-20,000 dalton (Da) and the                  molecular weight (Mn) of block A is in the range                  200-4000 Da, and where the molecular weight (Mn)                  of block B is in the range 100-20,000 Da, more                  typically, the molecular weight (Mn) of block A                  is 200-5000 Da. The molecular weight (Mn) is                  measured by Gel Permeation Chromatography (GPC).                  The block A has at least 76%, more typically, at                  least 80% or most typically at least 85%                  carbonate linkages. The block A has less than 98%                  carbonate linkages, more typically, less than 97%                  carbonate linkages or most typically less than                  95% carbonate linkages. The block A has between                  75-99% carbonate linkages, more typically,                  between 77-95% carbonate linkages, most typically                  between 80-90% carbonate linkages. The block B                  has less than 40 % carbonate linkages, more                  typically, less than 35% carbonate linkages, most                  typically, less than 30% carbonate linkages. The                  block B has at least 5% carbonate linkages, more                  typically at least 10% carbonate linkages, most                  typically at least 15% carbonate linkages. The                  block B has between 1-50% carbonate linkages,                  more typically between 5-45% carbonate linkages,                  most typically, between 10-40% carbonate                  linkages.The block B has at least 60% ether                  linkages, more typically, at least 65% ether                  linkages, most typically at least 70% ether                  linkages. The epoxide is asymmetric and the                  polycarbonate has between 40-100% head to tail                  linkages, preferably more than 50% head to tail                  linkages. The 0.1 and 20% of the total epoxide in                  block A is an epoxide substrate containing more                  than one epoxide moiety, preferably a                  bis-epoxide. The block A is a generally                  alternating polycarbonate polyol residue. The                  mol/mol ratio of block A to block B is                  25:1-1:250. The 30% of the epoxide residues of                  block A are ethylene oxide or propylene oxide                  residues, typically, at least 50% of the epoxide                  residues of block A are ethylene oxide or                  propylene oxide residues, more typically, at                  least 75% of the epoxide residues of block A are                  ethylene oxide or propylene oxide residues, most                  typically, at least 90% of the epoxide residues                  of block A are ethylene oxide or propylene oxide                  residues. The 30% of the epoxide residues of                  block B are ethylene oxide or propylene oxide                  residues, typically, at least 50% of the epoxide                  residues of block B are ethylene oxide or                  propylene oxide residues, more typically, at                  least 75% of the epoxide residues of block B are                  ethylene oxide or propylene oxide residues, most                  typically, at least 90% of the epoxide residues                  of block B are ethylene oxide or propylene oxide                  residues. The epoxide in block A and block B is                  ethylene oxide at a level of at least 95% of the                  total epoxide residues in both blocks. An excess                  of (poly)isocyanate, more typically, an excess of                  polymeric isocyanate relative to polyol is                  present so that polyisocyanurate ring formation                  in the presence of the trimerisation catalyst is                  possible. The polyurethane is in the form of a                  soft foam, a flexible foam, an integral skin                  foam, a high resilience foam, a viscoelastic or                  memory foam, a semi-rigid foam, a rigid foam                  (such as a polyurethane (PUR) foam, a                  polyisocyanurate (PIR) foam and/or a spray foam),                  an elastomer (such as a cast elastomer, a                  thermoplastic elastomer (TPU) or a microcellular                  elastomer), an adhesive (such as a hot melt                  adhesive, pressure sensitive or a reactive                  adhesive), a sealant or a coating (such as a                  waterborne or solvent dispersion (PUD), a                  two-component coating, a one component coating, a                  solvent free coating). The polyurethane is formed                  via a process that involves extruding, moulding,                  injection moulding, spraying, foaming, casting                  and/or curing. The polyurethane is formed via a                  one pot or pre-polymer process. The product of                  the first reaction is a low molecular weight                  polycarbonate polyol product having a molecular                  weight (Mn) in the range 200-4000 Daltons as                  measured by gel permeation chromatography (GPC).                  The first reaction produces generally alternating                  polycarbonate polyol product. The epoxide is                  asymmetric and where the reaction produces a                  polycarbonate having between 40-100% head to tail                  linkages, preferably more than 70%, more than 80%                  or more than 90% head to tail linkages. The                  product of the first reaction is fed into the                  second reactor, as a crude reaction mixture,                  where the second reactor contains a pre-activated                  DMC catalyst. The polycarbonate polyol copolymer                  is fed into the second reactor, as a crude                  reaction mixture, where the second reactor                  contains a pre-activated DMC catalyst. The first                  reaction is carried out under CO2 pressure of                  less than 20 bar, more preferably, less than 10                  bar, most preferably, less than 8 bar. The second                  reaction is carried out under CO2 pressure of                  less than 60 bar, preferably less than 20 bar,                  more preferably less than 10 bar, most                  preferably, less than 5 bar. The carbon dioxide                  (CO2) is added continuously in the first                  reaction, preferably in the presence of a                  starter. The first reaction can be a batch,                  semi-batch, or continuous process. The second                  reaction can be a continuous process or semi                  batch process. The crude reaction mixture fed                  into the second reactor includes an amount of                  unreacted epoxide and/or CO2 and/or starter. The                  carbonate catalyst is present in the crude                  reaction mixture. The carbonate catalyst has been                  removed from the crude reaction mixture prior to                  the addition to the second reactor. The                  temperature of reaction in the first reactor is                  in the range about 0-250 degrees C, preferably                  from about 40 degrees C to about 160 degrees C,                  more preferably from about 50-120 degrees C. The                  temperature of reaction in the second reactor is                  in the range from about 50-160 degrees C,                  preferably in the range from about 70-140 degrees                  C, more preferably from about 70-110 degrees C.                  The reactors are located in series. The reactors                  are nested. The first and second reactors are                  effective to provide different reaction                  conditions, such as temperature and/or pressure,                  to each other simultaneously. The crude reaction                  mixture is stabilised by an acid prior to                  addition to the second reactor. The process                  employs a total amount of epoxide, and where                  about 1-100% of the total amount of epoxide is                  mixed in the first reaction, with any remainder                  added in the second reaction, optionally with                  about 5-90% being mixed in the first reaction,                  optionally with about 10-90%, optionally with                  about 20-90%, optionally with about 40-90%,                  optionally with about 40-80%, optionally with                  about 5-50%. The 0.1-20% of the total epoxide in                  the first reaction is an epoxide substrate                  containing more than one epoxide moiety,                  preferably a bis-epoxide. The carbonate catalyst                  is a catalyst capable of producing polycarbonate                  chains with greater than 76% carbonate linkages.                  The carbonate catalyst is selected metal salen                  catalysts, metal porphyrin catalysts, metal                  tetraaza annulene catalysts and metal                  beta-diiminate catalysts. The DMC catalyst, in                  addition to at least two metal centres and                  cyanide ligands, also includes one or more                  complexing agents, water, a metal salt and/or an                  acid, optionally in non-stoichiometric amounts.                  The DMC catalyst is prepared by treating a                  solution of a metal salt with a solution of a                  metal cyanide salt in the presence of complexing                  agent, water, and/or an acid, optionally where                  the metal salt is of the formula M'(X')P, where                  M' is selected from zinc cation (Zn (II)),                  ruthenium ion (Ru(II)), ruthenium ion (Ru(III)),                  iron ion (Fe(II)), nickel (Ni(II)), manganese                  (Mn(II)), cobalt (Co(II)), tin ion (Sn(II)), lead                  ion (Pb(II)), iron ion (Fe(III)), molybdenum ion                  (Mo(IV)), Mo(VI), aluminium ion (Al(III)),                  Vanadium ion (V), V(VI), strontium ion (Sr(II)),                  tungsten ion (W(IV)), W(VI), copper ion (Cu(II))                  and chromium ion (Cr(III)), X' is an anion                  selected from halide, oxide, hydroxide, sulfate,                  carbonate, cyanide, oxalate, thiocyanate,                  isocyanate, isothiocyanate, carboxylate and                  nitrate, p is an integer of 1 or more, and the                  charge on the anion multiplied by p satisfies the                  valency of M', the metal cyanide salt is of the                  formula (Y')qE (CN)b(A)c, where E is selected                  from Fe(II), Fe(III), Co(II), Co(III), Cr(II),                  Cr(III), Mn(II), Mn(III), Ir(III), Ni(II),                  rhodium (Rh(III)), Ru(II), V(IV), and V(V), Y' is                  a proton or an alkali metal ion or an alkaline                  earth metal ion (such as K+), A is an anion                  selected from halide, oxide, hydroxide, sulfate,                  cyanide oxalate, thiocyanate, isocyanate,                  isothiocyanate, carboxylate and nitrate, q and b                  are integers of 1 or more, c may be 0 or an                  integer of 1 or more, the sum of the charges on                  the anions Y, CN and A multiplied by q, b and c                  respectively satisfies the valency of E, the at                  least one complexing agent is selected from a                  (poly)ether, a polyether carbonate, a                  polycarbonate, a poly(tetramethylene ether diol),                  a ketone, an ester, an amide, an alcohol, a urea                  or a combination optionally where the at least                  one complexing agent is selected from propylene                  glycol, polypropylene glycol, (m)ethoxy ethylene                  glycol, dimethoxyethane, tert-butyl alcohol,                  ethylene glycol monomethyl ether, diglyme,                  triglyme, methanol, ethanol, isopropyl alcohol,                  n-butyl alcohol, isobutyl alcohol and sec-butyl                  alcohol, 3-buten-1-ol, 2-methyl-3-buten-2-ol,                  2-methyl-3-butyn-2-ol, 3-methyl-1 -pentyn-3-ol,                  or a combination, and where the acid, if present,                  has the formula HrS', where S' is an anion                  selected from halide, sulfate, phosphate, borate,                  chlorate, carbonate, cyanide, oxalate,                  thiocyanate, isocyanate, isothiocyanate,                  carboxylate and nitrate, and r is an integer                  corresponding to the charge on the counterion S'.                  The DMC catalyst has a formula of M'd(Ee(CN)f)g.                  The DMC catalyst is of formula                  M'd(Ee(CN)r)g.hE'Si.jRc.kH2O.IHrS', where S is an                  anion selected from halide, oxide, hydroxide,                  sulfate, carbonate, cyanide, oxalate,                  thiocyanate, isocyanate, isothiocyanate,                  carboxylate and nitrate, i is an integer of 1 or                  more, and the charge on the anion S multiplied by                  i satisfies the valency of E', h, j, k and I are                  each independently zero or a positive number, r                  is an integer that corresponds to the charge on                  the counterion X , and Rc is a complexing agent                  or a combination of one or more complexing                  agents. The DMC catalyst is based upon                  Zn3(Co(CN)6)2 (zinc hexacyanocobaltate). The DMC                  catalyst is zinc hexacyanocobaltate and the one                  or more ligands are selected from alcohols and                  polyols. The one or more complexing agents are                  selected from dimethoxyethane, tert-butyl                  alcohol, polyethylene glycol, polypropylene                  glycol, polyethercarbonate, poly(tetramethylene                  glycol), polycarbonate. The product of the first                  reaction is fed into the second reactor in a                  single portion or in a continuous or                  discontinuous manner, optionally where the                  product of the first reaction comprises unreacted                  epoxide and/or carbonate catalyst. The product of                  the first reaction is used to pre-activate the                  DMC catalyst in the second reaction, prior to                  addition of epoxide and CO2. The same or                  different epoxides are used in the first or                  second reactions. The epoxide used in the first                  or second reaction includes propylene oxide,                  ethylene oxide or a mixture of propylene oxide                  and ethylene oxide.

PN CN111840454-A
TI Traditional Chinese medicine oral liquid medicine useful for treating hyperkinetic syndrome of children comprises e.g. prepared rehmannia root, tuckahoe, Alisma orientale, fructus ligustri lucidi, schisandra berry, yam, and keel.
AU SU Y
   LI J
AE LIAONING ORIENTAL PHARM CO LTD (LIAO-Non-standard)
GA 2020A9424X
AB    NOVELTY - Traditional Chinese medicine oral liquid medicine comprises 8-14 pts. wt. prepared rehmannia root, 1-4 pts. wt. tuckahoe, 2-5 pts. wt. Alisma orientale, 3-6 pts. wt. fructus ligustri lucidi, 1-4 pts. wt. schisandra berry, 3-6 pts. wt. yam, 3-6 pts. wt. rhizoma acori graminei, 2-5 pts. wt. golden cypress, 2-5 pts. wt. bark of tree peony root, 5-8 pts. wt. keel, 2-5 pts. wt. Polygala, 1-4 pts. wt. rhizome anemarrhenae, and 1000-2000 pts. wt. oral water, the oral liquid pH is adjusted to 4.5-6.8 by pH regulator.
   USE - The medicine oral liquid medicine useful for treating hyperkinetic syndrome of children (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The medicine oral liquid medicine: has less impurity content and controllable quality under the condition that the pH 4.5-6.8; and improves the safety of using medicine.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for preparing traditional Chinese medicine oral liquid, comprising (i) crushing the traditional Chinese medicine raw material into particles, subjecting the crushed raw materials into normal pressure forward direct current electric field to process for 20-30 seconds, then subjecting to normal pressure reverse direct current electric field to process for 10-20 seconds to obtain processed traditional Chinese medicine raw materials, where the current density of the normal-voltage forward current and the normal voltage reverse current is 90-100 A/m2, (ii) adding the processed rhizoma acori graminei and bark of tree peony root into extraction kettle, extracting using 70-80% ethanol as cosolvent, extracting temperature of 30-40 degrees C, extracting pressure of 20-45 MPa, the extracting time is 1-2 hours, and the carbon dioxide fluid flow of 350-400 kg/hour to obtain mixture A, (iii) taking the processed other traditional Chinese medicine raw materials, adding extracted rhizoma acori graminei and bark of tree peony root, adding 8-10 times weight water, decocting for 1-2 hours, filtering, adding 6-8 times weight water to medicine residue, decocting for 1-2 hours, filtering, combining the two medicine liquid, vacuum concentrating until the density is 1.20-1.22, obtaining the mixture B, and (iv) mixing the mixture A with mixture B, adding auxiliary material, using oral water to fix volume, centrifuging and concentrating to obtain oral liquid composition.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - PHARMACEUTICALS - Preferred Composition: The traditional Chinese medicine oral liquid medicine comprises 11 pts. wt. prepared rehmannia root, 2 pts. wt. tuckahoe, 3 pts. wt. Alisma orientale, 3 pts. wt. fructus ligustri lucidi, 2 pts. wt. schisandra berry, 4 pts. wt. yam, 5 pts. wt. rhizoma acori graminei, 4 pts. wt. golden cypress, 4 pts. wt. bark of tree peony root, 7 pts. wt. keel, 4 pts. wt. Polygala, 2 pts. wt. rhizome anemarrhenae, and 1500 pts. wt. oral water, the oral liquid pH is adjusted to 5.5 by pH regulator. Preferred Components: The pH regulator is hydrochloric acid or sodium hydroxide. The auxiliary material includes sorbic acid, potassium sorbate, tea polyphenols, and/or p-hydroxytoluate. Preferred Method: In the step (i), crushing of traditional Chinese medicine raw materials under normal pressure positive direct current electric field processing for 20 seconds, then subjecting to normal pressure reverse direct current electric field to process for 10 seconds to obtain processed traditional Chinese medicine raw materials. The current density of the normal-voltage forward current and the normal voltage reverse current is 90 A/m2. In the step (iii), extracting at 35 degrees C, 25 MPa for 2 hours, and the carbon dioxide fluid flow is 350 kg/hour. The centrifuging in the step (iv) is carried out at 100-200 revolutions/minute for 20-30 minutes.

PN CN111778770-A
TI Preparing inorganic coating gradient air-conditioning fruit and vegetable fresh-keeping paper, comprises e.g. taking rolls of packaging paper as the base paper, printing the first coating on the surface of the base paper, and drying in oven.
AU ZENG J
   CHEN Q
   SI W
   CHEN T
AE CHENGDU NEW KELI CHEM ENG SCI & TECHNIC (XKLI-C)
GA 2020A33295
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing inorganic coating gradient air-conditioning fruit and vegetable fresh-keeping paper, comprises e.g. (i) uniformly dispersing the expanded perlite powder, pregelatinized starch, hydroxyethyl cellulose and water evenly at 80 degrees C, (ii) dispersing diatomaceous earth, calcium hydroxide, zeolite molecular sieve, pre-gelatinized starch and water evenly at 80 degrees C, (iii) dispersing the silica aerogel, pre-gelatinized starch and water evenly at 80 degrees C, and (iv) taking rolls of packaging paper as the base paper, first printing the first coating on the surface of the base paper using the first coating liquid roller, drying in an oven, then printing the second coating using the second coating liquid roller, drying in oven, then printing the third coating using the third coating liquid roller, and drying in oven, finally, spraying phytic acid, naturally adsorbing, and coiling to obtain final product.
   USE - The inorganic coating gradient air-conditioning fruit and vegetable fresh-keeping paper is useful for preserving fruits and vegetables.
   ADVANTAGE - The inorganic coating gradient air-conditioning fruit and vegetable fresh-keeping paper: is a gradient layer with different pore diameters; can make the storage environment of fruits and vegetables always be in a relatively constant humidity environment; maintains the balance of oxygen and carbon dioxide ratio; quickly discharges ethylene gas; slows down the cell respiration of fruits and vegetables; and achieves the purpose of long-term preservation.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for inorganic coating gradient air conditioning fruit and vegetable fresh-keeping paper, obtained by above mentioned method. The fresh-keeping paper for fruits and vegetables includes a first coating, a second coating, a third coating, and a phytic acid layer, the first coating is composed of expanded perlite powder, pregelatinized starch, and hydroxyethyl cellulose, and the thickness of the coating is 40-80 mu m, the second coating is composed of diatomaceous earth, calcium hydroxide, zeolite molecular sieve, and pregelatinized starch, and the thickness of the coating is 20-30 mu m, the third coating is composed of silica aerogel and pregelatinized starch, and the coating thickness is 5-15 mu m, and the coating thickness of the phytic acid layer is 3-10 mu m.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: In step (i), the pore size of the expanded perlite powder is 10-50 mu m, the pre-gelatinized starch is pre-gelatinized corn starch, cassava starch, potato starch, wheat starch and sweet potato starch, the hydroxyethyl cellulose is hydroxyethyl cellulose having molecular weight of 30000-100000; in step (ii), the pore size of the diatomaceous earth is 1-5 mu m, the pore diameter of the zeolite molecular sieve is 10-50 nm, and the pre-gelatinized starch is corn starch, cassava starch, potato starch, wheat starch or sweet potato starch that have been pre-gelatinized; in step (iii), the pore size of the silica aerogel is 0.01-0.5 mu m, the pregelatinized starch is corn starch, cassava starch, potato starch, wheat starch or and sweet potato starch processed by pregelatinization.
DC A97 (Miscellaneous goods not specified elsewhere - including papermaking, gramophone records, detergents, food and oil well applications.); F09 (Paper-making production of cellulose, chemical treatment of wood - including chipboard and fibre-board (D21).)

PN DE102019002720-A1
TI Stroke compression in powerful engines, has cylinder head that no longer rests on crankshaft housing, and is very torsion-resistant and encapsulated by decoupling, and upward stop through piston screws, that reduce adjustment forces.
AU ENGELS F
AE ENGELS F (ENGE-Individual)
GA 202095845H
AB    NOVELTY - The stroke compression has a cylinder head (8) with a circular or oval cylinder due to its geometry, which is provided with grooves for receiving two piston rings and two seals. The piston rings consist of a compression ring (34) and an oil control ring (33), which consist of a steel alloy. A sintered mental ring is used for the oil control ring. The sealing rings (35) consist of elastomers, PTFE and composite materials. The oval or stepped design in the upper area of the cylinder liners (16A), produces larger intake valves and thus more power from the engine with the same displacement. The cylinder head no longer rests on a crankshaft housing (21), and it is designed to be very torsion-resistant and is acoustically encapsulated by the decoupling. An upward stop is through the piston screws, that reduce the adjustment forces.
   USE - Stroke compression in powerful engines of hybrid vehicle.
   ADVANTAGE - The performance range of the reciprocating engine increases due to the variable compression ratio, combined with higher efficiency. A variable compression increases the efficiency of the engine. The higher working pressures and temperatures can be used in the combustion chamber. The compression ideally also adapts to the environmental conditions. The compression is increased to the advantage of the exhaust gas behavior in order to emit fewer pollutants. An automatic mode can independently adapt the compaction to the respective driving situation. The stroke compression can be intrigued in hybrid vehicles, since the engines now have a broader optimal map in all performance ranges. The efficiency of the piston internal combustion engine is increased, and the carbon dioxide output decreases.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) a compensation element;
   (2) an adjusting screw;
   (3) a lift wheel;
   (4) a bellows insert;
   (5) a compression spring;
   (6) a worm shaft;
   (7) a left screw rod and right screw rod;
   (8) a toothed belt pulley;
   (9) a belt tensioner;
   (10) a piston screw; and
   (11) a profile seal.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the stroke compression.

PN DE102019001378-A1
TI Drive device for motor vehicle, has exhaust gas recirculation (EGR) line that is fluidly connected to exhaust gas tract at branch point that is arranged upstream of low-pressure turbine and downstream of power or compound turbine.
AU LAMPARTER B
AE DAIMLER AG (DAIM-C)
GA 202082649U
AB    NOVELTY - The drive device (10) has an internal combustion engine (12) that is provided with an output shaft (18) through which the motor vehicle is driven by the internal combustion engine. An exhaust gas tract (20) is provided to allow the exhaust gas to flow from the internal combustion engine. The output shaft of the internal combustion engine or an electrical machine (34) is driven mechanically by the power or compound turbine. An EGR line (40) is fluidly connected to the exhaust gas tract at a branch point (A). A portion of the exhaust gas that flows through the exhaust gas tract is branched off from the exhaust gas tract at the branch point and is returned to an intake tract (22) of the internal combustion engine through which air flows by the EGR line. The power or compound turbine (32) is arranged upstream of a low-pressure turbine (26). The branch point is arranged upstream of the low-pressure turbine and downstream of the power or compound turbine.
   USE - Drive device for motor vehicle such as utility vehicle.
   ADVANTAGE - The operation of the internal combustion engine is efficient and effective, so that the fuel consumption and carbon dioxide emissions of the internal combustion engine are kept low. Since the compound turbine concept is used for engines with EGR, so that the respective engine is operated efficiently and effectively. The system efficiency of the compound turbine concept is increased significantly. The drive device solves the problem that turbo compound systems on EGR engines have a lower potential for reducing consumption due to the higher exhaust gas back pressure level. The cooling power required for cooling the EGR mass flow is reduced by releasing energy from the EGR mass flow to the compound turbine. The internal combustion engine is operated with a battery without significant loss of consumption even when the energy input is too high. The space requirement of the drive device is kept low by arranging the power turbine upstream of the low-pressure turbine.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawings show the schematic views of the drive device for motor vehicle.
   Drive device (10)
   Internal combustion engine (12)
   Output shaft (18)
   Exhaust gas tract (20)
   Intake tract (22)
   Low-pressure turbine (26)
   Power or compound turbine (32)
   Electrical machine (34)
   EGR line (40)
   Branch point (A)

PN CN111406860-A
TI Passion fruit sauce comprises water, passion fruit seed oil, persimmon vinegar, honey, edible salt, passion fruit pulp, Aloe vera, green tea extract, and sesame.
AU LIN S
   FAN Q
   XIE J
   HUANG C
   LONG T
   LIN X
AE GUANGDONG JINNIBAO TECHNOLOGY DEV CO LTD (GUAN-Non-standard)
GA 202067998X
AB    NOVELTY - Passion fruit sauce comprises 45-55 pts. wt. water, 10-15 pts. wt. passion fruit seed oil, 5-10 pts. wt. persimmon vinegar, 5-10 pts. wt. honey, 3-8 pts. wt. edible salt, 10-15 pts. wt. passion fruit pulp, 2-5 pts. wt. Aloe vera gel, 2-5 pts. wt. green tea extract, and 2-5 pts. wt. sesame.
   USE - As passion fruit sauce.
   ADVANTAGE - The passion fruit sauce has simple and easily available ingredients, adopts low-temperature and high-pressure sterilization, preserves nutrients to greatest extent, and is suitable for long-term consumption by people who detoxify and lose weight.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for preparing passion fruit sauce, which involves (a) performing pre-treatment of raw materials by (i) filtering A.vera gel to remove sugar water, (ii) rinsing sesame seeds to remove impurities and dust, and filtering, (iii) thawing passion fruit pulp, and blanching it for cleaning, (iv) separating passion fruit seeds, and pulverizing seeds, and (v) performing supercritical carbon dioxide (CO2) extraction of passion fruit seed oil by adding pulverized passion fruit seeds to extraction kettle, performing supercritical extraction process to prepare crude oil of passion fruit seed oil, adding crude oil to molecular distillation sample tank, adjusting molecular distillation process parameters for refining treatment, and collecting passion fruit seed oil, (b) performing solid phase mixing and stirring by taking 10-15 pts. wt. passion fruit pulp, 2-5 pts. wt. A.vera, 2-5 pts. wt. rinsed sesame seeds and 5-10 pts. wt. honey, adding to a mixing pot, and stirring to obtain a solid phase mixture, (c) performing liquid phase mixing and stirring by adding 45-55 pts. wt. water, 5-10 pts. wt. persimmon vinegar, 3-8 pts. wt. edible salt, and 2-5 pts. wt. green tea extract into a stirring pot, and stirring to obtain a liquid mixture, (d) filling solid phase mixture into a polyethylene terephthalate (PET) bottle, filling the liquid phase mixture into a PET bottle, and filling 10-15 pts. wt. passion fruit seed oil to obtain a sauce, (e) vacuum-sealing the sauce, and (f) subjecting vacuum-sealed sauce to ultra-high pressure sterilization i.e. high pressure processing (HPP).
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - FOOD - Preferred Product: The passion fruit sauce comprises 50 pts. wt. water, 13 pts. wt. passion fruit seed oil, 8 pts. wt. persimmon vinegar, 8 pts. wt. honey, 5 pts. wt. edible salt, 13 pts. wt. passion fruit pulp, 3.5 pts. wt. A.vera gel, 3.5 pts. wt. green tea extract, and 3.5 pts. wt. sesame, preferably white sesame. Preferred Method: The step (iii) involves blanching fruit pulp at 5-10 seconds, and washing fruit pulp at 65-85 degrees C. In step (i), shape of the A.vera gel is square particles, and length, width and height of particles are 0.5 cm. The supercritical CO2 extraction involves adjusting molecular distillation process parameters including vacuum degree of 9 Pa, distillation temperature of 100 degrees C, film scraping speed of 300 revolutions per minute, feeding speed of 15 ml.minutes-1, and condensation temperature of 50 degrees C. The HPP sterilization is carried out at a pressure of 300-500 MPa for 5-15 minutes.

AE AIXIMU LIAONING ENVIRONMENTAL TECHNOLOGY (AIXI-Non-standard)
GA 202063034N
AB    NOVELTY - Treatment of high-difficult wastewater with zero concentrated liquid and zero solid waste involves (1) subjecting the high-difficult wastewater to biochemical treatment, (2) obtaining the standard water discharge that meets the national discharge standard, subjecting the organic concentrated waste liquid produced in the lease-renovate-operate-transfer (LROT) treatment to subsequent treatment, (3) adding coagulant to the organic concentrated waste liquid and stirring evenly, so that reacting the organic material in the organic concentrated waste liquid and the coagulant to obtain a concentrated liquid, subjecting the concentrated liquid to a secondary concentration treatment for high concentration to obtain high-concentration concentrated liquid and clear liquid, and refluxing the clear liquid for LROT treatment again, (4) spray drying the high-concentration concentrated liquid and fully contacting with high-temperature hot air to obtain dried organic material and producing exhaust gas.
   USE - Treatment of with zero concentrated liquid/zero solid waste-containing high-difficult wastewater e.g. landfill leachate, coal chemical wastewater, and pharmaceutical wastewater (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables economical treatment of high-difficult wastewater with reduced energy consumption.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Treatment of high-difficult wastewater with zero concentrated liquid and zero solid waste involves (1) subjecting the high-difficult wastewater to biochemical treatment, (2) obtaining the standard water discharge that meets the national discharge standard, subjecting the organic concentrated waste liquid produced in the lease-renovate-operate-transfer (LROT) treatment to subsequent treatment, (3) adding coagulant to the organic concentrated waste liquid and stirring evenly, so that reacting the organic material in the organic concentrated waste liquid and the coagulant to obtain a concentrated liquid, subjecting the concentrated liquid to a secondary concentration treatment for high concentration to obtain high-concentration concentrated liquid and clear liquid, and refluxing the clear liquid for LROT treatment again, (4) spray drying the high-concentration concentrated liquid and fully contacting with high-temperature hot air to obtain dried organic material and producing exhaust gas, (5) discharging the exhaust gas after the spray drying treatment of the sludge, and producing the remaining sludge after subjecting the biological treatment to the sludge drying treatment to obtain the dried sludge, and (6) performing incineration of dried organic material and dried sludge for incineration treatment, because the main component is humic acid organic material, after incineration treatment, and eventually forming carbon dioxide and water. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a device.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: In the step (3), the coagulant is one or more of iron oxide, cobalt oxide, nickel oxide, copper oxide, zinc oxide, or One or more of iron salt, cobalt salt, nickel salt, copper salt, zinc, or their mixture.

TI Construction of modular green building involves installing square enclosure module and three plantings area on roof, putting planting shells, putting e.g. nitrogen fertilizer, watering plants, and placing plastic shells side by side.
AU MEI D

GA 202040457B
AB    NOVELTY - Construction of modular green building comprises installing square enclosure module and three plantings area on roof and providing square enclosure; putting plastic planting shells in the planting areas; putting soil, sand, nitrogen fertilizer, composite fertilizer, macroelement, trace element, and vegetables into the plastic planting shell in the first planting area; putting soil, sand, nitrogen fertilizer, composite fertilizer, pulverized coal ash and plant seedlings into the plastic planting shell in the second planting area; putting nutrient soil, sand, cake fertilizer, vermiculite, and flower seeds in the plastic planting shell in the third planting area; and watering plants, keeping soil moist, covering with plastic film to reduce water evaporation, removing plastic film, spraying water once a day, and placing plastic shells side by side in the square grid of the planting area.
   USE - The method is used for constructing modular green building.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is simple manufacture, effectively cools the roof of the building, reduces hot feeling of the residents on the top floor, effectively absorbs carbon dioxide to produce oxygen, and realizes cooling, greening, and beautiful appearance.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Construction of modular green building comprises:
   (A) installing square enclosure module, first planting area, second planting area, and third planting area on roof and providing square enclosure;
   (B) putting plastic planting shells in the three planting areas;
   (C) putting 80-100 pts. wt. soil, 15-20 pts. wt. sand, 5-10 pts. wt. nitrogen fertilizer, 3-5 pts. wt. composite fertilizer, 1.8161 pts. wt. macroelement, 0.00452 pt. wt. trace element, and 15 pts. wt. vegetables into the plastic planting shell in the first planting area;
   (D) putting 80-100 pts. wt. soil, 15-20 pts. wt. sand, 3-5 pts. wt. nitrogen fertilizer, 8-10 pts. wt. composite fertilizer, 10-20 pts. wt. pulverized coal ash and plant seedlings into the plastic planting shell in the second planting area;
   (E) putting 100 pts. wt. nutrient soil, 20 pts. wt. sand, 5 pts. wt. cake fertilizer, 5 pts. wt. vermiculite, and 4 pts. wt. flower seeds in the plastic planting shell in the third planting area; and
   (F) watering plants, keeping soil moist, covering with plastic film to reduce water evaporation, removing plastic film, spraying water once a day, and placing plastic shells side by side in the square grid of the planting area.

The composite fertilizer comprises nitrogen, phosphorus, and potassium composite fertilizer. The macroelement comprises 0.588 pt. wt. potassium nitrate, 0.72 pt. wt. calcium nitrate, 0.152 pt. wt. ammonium phosphate, 0.294 pt. wt. magnesium sulfate, and 0.142 pt. wt. ferric chloride. The trace element comprises 0.00284 pt. wt. potassium iodide, 0.00056 pt. wt. boric acid, 0.00056 pt. wt. zinc sulfate, and 0.00056 pt. wt. manganese sulfate. Thee nutrient soil comprises rotten leaf soil and garden soil.

An air flow diffusion portion is attached on the distal end of the central air flow channel. The air flow diffusion portion is provided with through-holes penetrating toward the outside in a direction forming a 90-degree angle with the straight flow channel, and has a discharge port in the center portion.

The ozone is generated in a gas-liquid mixture gas which is generated in the farm house and filled with a large amount of water fine particles, thus suppressing the occurrence of harmful insects. The low-temperature plasma is generated in a gas-liquid mixture gas containing a large amount of water fine particles produced by water jetting from the multi-fluid gas-liquid jet mixing nozzle in farm house, thus promoting the suppression of the occurrence of the pest and promoting the growth of the plant.

PN CN110904033-A
TI Preparing hair follicle stem cells for treating skin defects comprises e.g. cutting hair follicle roots, washing, enzymatic digesting, inoculating, transferring, purifying, coating with type IV collagen and digesting with trypsin.
AU CHEN J
   YUAN B
   LIU J
AE ZHEJIANG HEALTHFUTURE BIO-MEDICINE TECHN (ZHEJ-Non-standard)
GA 202024124V
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing hair follicle stem cells comprises e.g. cutting hair follicle roots of volunteer hair, washing, adding dispase enzyme, digesting, enzymatic digesting, inoculating in hair follicle stem cell culture medium, changing medium, transferring hair follicle stem cells to hair follicle stem cell expansion medium to continue culturing, where the hair follicle stem cell expansion medium includes DMEM basal medium, FBS, T-beta, hEGF, VEGF, and Rho-related kinases, purifying hair follicle stem cells, coating hair follicle stem cells with type IV collagen, digesting with trypsin after 1.5-2.5 hours of reaction, collecting cells by centrifugation, pipetting into single-cell suspension and inoculating in Petri dish, after period of time, aspirating non-adherent cells together with culture solution, and attaching adherent cells to complete culture medium.
   USE - The method is useful in preparing hair follicle stem cells for treating skin defects.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: can be subcultured in vitro for long time, which has high regeneration and differentiation ability, and lays foundation for hair follicle stem cells to be applied to treat skin defects and has good clinical application prospect.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing hair follicle stem cells comprises (i) cutting hair follicle roots of volunteer hair, washing, adding 0.25% dispase enzyme, digesting at 37 degrees C for 0.5-1.5 hours, (ii) enzymatic digesting, inoculating in hair follicle stem cell culture medium at 37 degrees C, 5% carbon dioxide, and changing medium every 1-2 days, where the hair follicle source culture medium includes DMEM/F12 medium, serum replacement, multivitamin, amino acid, cell growth factor, L-glutamine, penicillin double antibody solution, hydroxyethanol, trehalose, and betamethasone, (iii) transferring hair follicle stem cells to hair follicle stem cell expansion medium to continue culturing, where the hair follicle stem cell expansion medium includes DMEM basal medium, FBS, T-beta, hEGF, VEGF, and Rho-related kinases, and (iv) purifying hair follicle stem cells, coating hair follicle stem cells with type IV collagen, digesting with trypsin after 1.5-2.5 hours of reaction, collecting cells by centrifugation, pipetting into single-cell suspension and inoculating in Petri dish, after period of time, aspirating non-adherent cells together with culture solution, and attaching adherent cells to complete culture medium.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Components: In the step (ii), the hair follicle-derived culture medium includes 2-15 vol.%/vol.% preferably 8 vol.%/vol.% serum substitute, 20-90 mu g/ml preferably 50 mu g/ml multi-vitamin, 20-30 ng/ml preferably 25 mu g/ml amino acid, 1.5-9 ng/ml preferably 4 cell ng/ml growth factor, 3-7 mmol/ml preferably 5 mmol/ml L-glutamine, 12-18 mu M preferably 15 mu M penicillin double antibody solution, 18-25 mu M preferably 22 mu M hydroxyethanol, 5-10 mu g/ml preferably 7 mu g/ml chitosan, 10-15 ng/ml preferably 12 ng/ml betamethasone, and DMEM/F12 medium (balance). In the step (iii), the hair follicle stem cell expansion medium includes FBS, 0.006% T-beta, 100 ng/ml hEGF, 100 ng/ml VEGF, 20 uM Rho-related kinase, and DMEM basal medium (balance). Preferred Method: In the step (i), cutting root of hair follicle into square of 1x 1 cm, digestion time of Dispase enzyme is 1 hours. In the step (iv), the digestion time of trypsin reaction is 2 hours.

PN US2020056330-A1; US11377788-B2
TI Method of finishing specific target material            without intervening cleaning process, involves applying            sub material finish to sub target material in pressure            vessel without applying main material finish to            sacrificial cleaning material.
AU KELLY M W
   MONTERO G A
   KANCHAGAR A P
   PANCHMATIA P R
AE NIKE INC (NKEN-C)
GA 2020146135
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves introducing (1504) carbon                dioxide (CO2) within pressure vessel. An internal                temperature of pressure vessel is increased (1506)                to operating temperature. A flow rate within                pressure vessel is increased (1508) to non-zero                rate before to CO2 achieving supercritical fluid                (SCF) state. A pressure within pressure vessel is                increased (1510) to operating pressure. The first                target material is perfused (1512) with first                material finish using SCF CO2. The pressure from                operating pressure is reduced (1514) to transition                pressure while maintaining temperature above                threshold temperature and maintaining flow rate                above threshold rate. A second material finish is                applied to second target material in pressure                vessel without applying first material finish to                sacrificial cleaning material in step between                applying first material finish to first target                material and applying second material finish to                second target material in pressure vessel.
   USE - Method of finishing specific target material                without intervening cleaning process used in                commercial good e.g. apparel or footwear.
   ADVANTAGE - The material finish is integral to the                sacrificial material, which ensures the intended                amount of material finish is introduced within the                pressure vessel to ensure consistent application of                the material finish to multiple spooled materials.                The pressure and/or temperature is increased to                consume resources that reduces the efficiency of                the material finishing/dyeing process, and the                pressure and or temperature is limited to a range                that is sufficient to achieve solubility of the                material finish and sufficient for interaction with                the material finished. The maintaining of a                temperature above a threshold value until the                finishing material precipitates out of the CO2                reduces or eliminates the need for a subsequent                cleaning process.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a flowchart illustrating a                method of finishing specific target material                without intervening cleaning process.
   Step for introducing CO2 within pressure                vessel (1504)
   Step for increasing an internal temperature                of pressure vessel to operating temperature                (1506)
   Step for increasing flow rate within pressure                vessel to non-zero rate before to CO2 achieving SCF                state (1508)
   Step for increasing a pressure within                pressure vessel to operating pressure (1510)
   Step for perfusing the first target material                with first material finish (1512)
   Step for reducing the pressure from operating                pressure to transition pressure while maintaining                temperature above threshold temperature and                maintaining flow rate above threshold rate                (1514)
