PT P
PN US2020016581-A1; US10987661-B2
TI Method for converting mixed waste plastic into low molecular weight organic compound, involves feeding particles of mixed waste plastic, and solid catalyst component into reactor, heating followed by withdrawing stream and separating.
AU GAFFNEY A M
AE AMG CHEM & CATALYSIS CONSULTING LLC (AMGC-Non-standard)
   AMG CHEM & CATALYSIS CONSULTING LLC (AMGC-Non-standard)
GA 2020065260
AB    NOVELTY - A mixed waste plastic into a low molecular weight organic compound converting method involves feeding particles of the mixed waste plastic, plasma, and a solid catalyst component into a non-thermal catalytic plasma reactor, where solid catalyst component comprises a modified zeolite and alloyed metals into a fluidized bed reactor. The particles of the mixed waste plastic and the solid catalyst component are heated at a temperature effective to produce a coarse filler, inorganic components, coke, a volatile organic component, and a spent catalyst component, followed by withdrawing a first stream comprising the volatile organic component from the reactor, withdrawing a second stream comprising spent catalyst component, the coke, coarse filler and the inorganic components from the reactor, heating the second stream in a regenerator in the presence of oxygen, air, or a blend of oxygen with an inert gas at a temperature effective and separating the regenerated solid catalyst component.
   USE - Method for converting mixed waste plastic into a low molecular weight organic compound.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables converting mixed waste plastic into a low molecular weight organic compound in simple and easy manner.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A mixed waste plastic into a low molecular weight organic compound converting method involves feeding particles of the mixed waste plastic, plasma, and a solid catalyst component into a non-thermal catalytic plasma reactor, where solid catalyst component comprises a modified zeolite and alloyed metals into a fluidized bed reactor. The particles of the mixed waste plastic and the solid catalyst component are heated at a temperature effective to produce a coarse filler, inorganic components, coke, a volatile organic component, and a spent catalyst component, followed by withdrawing a first stream comprising the volatile organic component from the reactor, withdrawing a second stream comprising the spent catalyst component, the coke, the coarse filler and the inorganic components from the reactor, heating the second stream in a regenerator in the presence of oxygen, air, or a blend of oxygen with an inert gas at a temperature effective to convert the coke to a mixture of carbon monoxide, carbon dioxide and water, and to regenerate the solid catalyst component, and separating the regenerated solid catalyst component from the coarse filler and the inorganic components.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The inert gas is nitrogen, and/or steam. The volatile organic component comprises a light 2-6C olefinic product stream. The high molecular weight organic compound is selected from benzene, toluene, and/or xylene. Preferred Method: The pyrolysis is performed using a two-step temperature program comprising raising the temperature to 300-330 degrees C for two hours, and raising the temperature to 400-450 degrees C. The pyrolysis produces mixed waste plastic having less than 15 ppm halogenated hydrocarbons.
DC H09 (Fuel products not of petroleum origin - excluding coal handling, preparation or mining, but including coking, briquetting, peat processing synthesis, gas production, coal gasification. Combustion improvement additives for coal, peat and other nonhydrocarbon based fuels are included in this Section together with coal liquefaction and desulphurisation.); J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).); A35 (Other processing and general - including vulcanisation, welding of plastics and adhesive processes. PT P
PN CN110643512-A
TI Porous reticular culture meat production mold with opening of upper end useful in researching and producing porous reticulated muscle tissue comprises an outer wall, a first groove, a second groove, and a column.
AU DING S
   TANG W
   ZHOU G
   YANG J
   WU Z
   ZHU H
   LI C
   XU X
AE UNIV NANJING AGRIC (UYNA-C)
   UNIV NANJING NORMAL (UNNO-C)
GA 2020040402
AB    NOVELTY - Porous reticular culture meat production mold with opening of upper end comprises an outer wall, a first groove, a second groove, and a column. The first groove is arranged inside the outer wall and centrally connected inside the outer wall. The second groove is arranged at the bottom of the first groove and centrally connected at the bottom of the first groove. Multiple of micro-pillars are vertically arranged at the bottom of the second groove, and the micro-pillars are arranged at an offset and space to form a micro-pillar array. The micro-pillars in adjacent rows are spaced 1-3 mm apart, the micro-pillars in adjacent rows are offset parallel by 1-4 mm, and the micro-pillars in each row are spaced 0.5-3 mm apart. The mold is a rectangular parallelepiped or a cylindrical body with an open upper end.
   USE - The production mold is useful in researching and producing porous reticulated muscle tissue (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method enhances the diffusion of nutrients to the cells, facilitates the discharge of waste, controls the spatial pattern of mechanical tension, promotes differentiation and achieves the large production.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for preparing the porous mesh cultured meat, comprising (i) mixing type I collagen, phenol red-containing DMEM medium and sodium hydroxide solution, where the volume ratio of the type I collagen and phenol red-containing DMEM medium is 50:40 and adding sodium hydroxide solution to adjust the pH to 7.3-7.5 to obtain a mixed solution; (ii) mixing the muscle-derived cells with the mixed solution to obtain a mixed solution containing the cells; and (iii) adding the cell-containing mixed solution into the porous mesh culture meat production mold for culturing, where the addition amount of the cell-containing mixed solution is not greater than the height of the microcolumns 4 of the mold, culturing at 37 degrees C in 5% carbon dioxide incubator for 2 hours to form hydrogel muscle tissue, adding growth medium, culturing the hydrogel muscle tissue for 1-3 days, changing the growth medium to the differentiation medium and culturing the gel muscle tissue in differentiation medium for 5-7 days.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic representation of the porous reticular culture meat production mold.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Components: The length of the column is 1-5 mm, the width is 0.5-1.5 mm, and the height is 1-5 mm. The cross-section of the column is rectangular or oval. The height of the first groove is 3-7 mm, and the height of the second groove is 2-6 mm. The bottom width of the first groove is 2-4 mm. The porous mesh-shaped cultured meat production mold is made by 3D printing to obtain a male mold, and then the PDMS casting method is used to obtain the male mold. The muscle-derived cell is muscle stem cells, muscle progenitor cells or muscle precursor cells and the density of the muscle-derived cells is 1x 105-1x 107 cells/ml in the cell-containing mixed solution. The concentration of the type I collagen is 3.35-3.73 mg/ml, and the concentration of the sodium hydroxide solution is 1 M, and the matrigel further added to the mixed solution in a volume ratio of 8:91.5. Preferred Method: The production mold of porous network culture meat is made by PDMS casting method after the male mold is obtained by 3D printing.
DC B04 (Natural products and polymers. Including testing of body fluids (other than blood typing or cell counting), pharmaceuticals or veterinary compounds of unknown structure, testing of microorganisms for pathogenicity, testing of chemicals for mutagenicity or human toxicity and fermentative production of DNA or RNA. General compositions.); D16 (Fermentation industry - including fermentation equipment, brewing, yeast production, production of pharmaceuticals and other chemicals by fermentation, microbiology, production of vaccines and antibodies, cell and tissue culture and genetic engineering.)

PT P
PN EP3579175-A1
TI Method for sensitivity analysis of multimodal            energy system, involves determining critical value of            parameter for which objective function coefficient            vector is parallel to normal vector of active            constraint.
AU DUCKHEIM M
AE SIEMENS AG (SIEI-C)
GA 2019A2648E
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves providing (S1) a solution                of the optimization for a reference value of the                parameter. An objective function coefficient vector                associated with the optimization is provided (S2).                Multiple constraints associated with the                optimization are provided (S3). The active                constraint and associated normal vector of active                constraint are determined (S4). A critical value of                the parameter for which the objective function                coefficient vector is parallel to the normal vector                of the active constraint is determined (S5).
   USE - Method for sensitivity analysis of multimodal                energy system designed by optimization with respect                to parameter of energy system.
   ADVANTAGE - The determination of a design of an energy                system is improved by an energy system design                method. Only one optimization is required for the                determination of the critical value of the                parameter and thus for the sensitivity analysis.                Since the dependence of the design of the energy                system on the change in the value of the parameter                is determined, an improved determination of the                design of the energy system is enabled. The time                required for determining the design of the energy                system is reduced, and thus a reliable design of                the energy system is determined. The energy system                is more robustly optimized or designed. The                critical design of the energy system is                advantageously determined. The linearization of the                linear target function is carried out by a simplex                method. The carbon dioxide emissions and primary                energy use of the energy system are optimized and                the total costs of the energy system are                minimized.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the                following:
   (1) a computer system for sensitivity analysis                of energy system; and
   (2) a computer program product for sensitivity                analysis of energy system.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a flowchart illustrating a                method for sensitivity analysis of energy system.                (Drawing includes non-English language text)
   Step for providing a solution of the                optimization for a reference value of the parameter                (S1)
   Step for providing objective function                coefficient vector associated with the optimization                (S2)
   Step for providing multiple constraints                associated with the optimization (S3)
   Step for determining active constraint and                associated normal vector of active constraint                (S4)
   Step for determining critical value of the                parameter for which the objective function                coefficient vector is parallel to the normal vector                of the active constraint (S5)

MC T01-J05A2C; T01-S03

PN CN110150463-A
TI Chinese herbal medicine pig feed composition useful e.g. for strengthening spleen and stomach and enhancing immunity comprises e.g. bran, soybean cake, bone meal, lees, wheat germ, sword beans, peach, Heterosmilax japonica and kelp residue.
AU YANG K
   XIA Z
   LUO B
   YANG Y
AE HUNAN JINGCHENG ELECTRONIC TECHNOLOGY CO (HUNA-Non-standard)
GA 2019774110
AB    NOVELTY - Chinese herbal medicine pig feed composition comprises 36-58 pts. wt. bran, 16-25 pts. wt. soybean cake, 5-8 pts. wt. bone meal, 15-27 pts. wt. lees, 12-18 pts. wt. wheat germ, 3-6 pts. wt. sword beans, 8-11 pts. wt. peach, 1.6-2.5 pts. wt. Heterosmilax japonica, 0.6-1.4 pts. wt. okra, 10-16 pts. wt. kelp residue, 4-6 pts. wt. vegetable oil, 1-3 pts. wt. Taraxacum mongolicum, 0.5-1.2 pts. wt. phoenix eye fruit, 2.4-3.5 pts. wt. mulberry, 4-5 pts. wt. complex amino acids, 0.02-0.06 pts. wt. multivitamins, 0-4 pts. wt. bacteria composition, and 3-5 pts. wt. estazolam.
   USE - The feed composition is useful for strengthening spleen and stomach, improving appetite, enhancing immunity and reducing incidence of diseases.
   ADVANTAGE - The feed composition: is rich in nutrients; and improves the economic benefits of farmers.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for preparing the pig feed composition, comprising (a) crushing the kelp residue to 100-120 meshes, and carrying out enzymatic hydrolysis at 32-45 degrees C to obtain kelp hydrolysate, (b) cleaning H. japonica, okra and phoenix eye fruit to remove impurities, then subjecting to ultra-micro crushing process, respectively extracting by supercritical carbon dioxide extraction process, collecting the obtained extracts, and concentrating to dryness to obtain H. japonica, okra and phoenix eye fruit extracts, (c) crushing mulberry and T. mongolicum, then adding 5-8 times of water, and decocting at 80-95 degrees C for 3-4 times (each time decocting for 50-75 minutes) to obtain first mixture, (d) mixing estazolam, bran, soybean cake, bone meal, lees, wheat germ, sword beans and peach, crushing and passing through 100 meshes sieve to obtain second mixture, then adding vegetable oil, H. japonica extract and first mixture to the second mixture, and mixing uniformly to obtain third mixture, and (e) adding complex amino acids, multivitamins, bacteria composition, kelp hydrolysate, okra extract and phoenix eye fruit extract to the third mixture, mixing uniformly in a high speed-mixer, and granulating or puffing.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - FOOD - Preferred Components: The vegetable oil comprises soybean oil, rapeseed oil, corn oil, peanut oil and/or tea oil. The complex amino acids comprise aspartic acid, lysine, methionine, phenylalanine, tryptophan, serine and/or isoleucine. The multivitamins comprise vitamin A, vitamin B1, vitamin B2, vitamin B5, vitamin C and/or vitamin E. The bone meal has a particle diameter of 0.05-0.84 mm. The kelp residue has a length of 0.4-1.2 cm. Preferred Method: The extraction in the step (b) is carried out at 40-60 degrees C under the pressure of 20-36 MPa.
   ACTIVITY - Gastrointestinal-Gen; Immunostimulant.

PN US2019246573-A1
TI Greenhouse with integrated irrigation and environmental control system, e.g. small residential greenhouse, has pump, humidity sensor, camera, motion sensor and fans which are electrically connected to control module.
AU RUIZ F M
AE RUIZ F M (RUIZ-Individual)
GA 201969735T
AB    NOVELTY - The greenhouse has a main structure, an irrigation system, an environmental control system (4), a control module (5), and a protective sheet of netting (6). The environmental control system comprises a camera (41), a motion sensor (42), a humidity sensor (43), and fans (44). The irrigation system comprises a tubular sprinkler (21), an irrigation line (22), storage tanks (23), and a pump (24). The main structure is enclosed by protective sheet of netting. The environmental control system is mounted within main structure. The tubular sprinkler is positioned across a ceiling of main structure. The tubular sprinkler is provided in fluid communication with storage tanks via irrigation line and pump. Each of fans are connected adjacent to a lateral side of the main structure. The camera and the motion sensor are mounted adjacent to ceiling of main structure. The pump, the humidity sensor, the camera, the motion sensor and fans are electrically connected to the control module.
   USE - Greenhouse with integrated irrigation and environmental control system, such as small residential greenhouse and large agricultural greenhouse.
   ADVANTAGE - The protective sheet of netting is designed to prevent insects and air-borne contaminants from entering the main structure while providing sufficient ventilation. Each of the fans is connected adjacent to a lateral side of the main structure to ensure sufficient airflow. Each corner column connects between two adjacent crossbeams via a corner portion, so as possible to allow the user to quickly assemble or disassemble the main structure. Each fan is positioned opposite another, so as possible to generate an airflow through the main structure which removes the carbon dioxide generated by the plants, so that the plant growth and overall health can be aided. The control panel comprises a screen with an input/output (I/O) devices such as a keyboard, mouse and touchpad for interacting with the control module, so as possible to allow the user to interact with the control module from inside the main structure.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a front and top perspective exploded view of the greenhouse with integrated irrigation and environmental control system.
   Environmental control system (4)
   Control module (5)
   Netting (6)
   Tubular sprinkler (21)
   Irrigation line (22)
   Storage tank (23)
   Pump (24)
   Camera (41)
   Motion sensor (42)
   Humidity sensor (43)
   Fan (44)

PT P
PN CN109433247-A; CN109433247-B
TI Preparing Mobil Composition of Matter No. 41            molecular sieve-based load-type catalyst comprises e.g.            dissolving soluble metal salt and zirconium sulfate in            a methanol solution, heating mixture A and cooling            mixture B to room temperature.
AU LIANG Z
   ZHANG X
   HUANG Y
AE UNIV HUNAN (UYHU-C)
GA 2019250136
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing Mobil Composition of Matter No. 41                (MCM-41) molecular sieve-based load-type catalyst                comprises e.g. dissolving soluble metal salt and                zirconium sulfate in a methanol solution to obtain                a mixture A, heating mixture A at 50 degrees C for                1 hours to obtain a mixture B, cooling mixture B to                room temperature, adding molecular sieve MCM-41                powder and stirring for 5 hours to obtain a mixture                C, performing ultrasonic treatment of mixture C for                more than 0.5 hours to obtain a mixture D, drying                mixture D and calcining to obtain final product,                where catalyst is an oxide-modified sulfated                zirconium oxide solid super-strong acid; oxide is                amphoteric oxides iron(III) oxide, aluminum oxide,                chromium(III) oxide, tin(IV) oxide, titanium                dioxide, zinc oxide and germanium dioxide, or other                transition metal oxides molybdenum dioxide,                vanadium(V) oxide, vanadium(III) oxide, cobalt(II)                oxide, niobium pentoxide, manganese dioxide and                ferrous ferric oxide.
   USE - The catalyst is useful for regenerating carbon                dioxide amine-rich solution (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The catalyst: has excellent stability and                reusability; is easy to separate; and method: has                simple preparation process and low cost.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing Mobil Composition of Matter No. 41                (MCM-41) molecular sieve-based load-type catalyst                comprises (i) dissolving soluble metal salt and                zirconium sulfate in a methanol solution to obtain                a mixture A, (ii) heating mixture A at 50 degrees C                for 1 hours to obtain a mixture B, (iii) cooling                mixture B to room temperature, adding molecular                sieve MCM-41 powder and stirring for 5 hours to                obtain a mixture C, (iv) performing ultrasonic                treatment of mixture C for more than 0.5 hours to                obtain a mixture D, and (v) drying mixture D and                calcining to obtain final product, where load-type                catalyst is a catalyst loaded on a carrier MCM-41                molecular sieve; catalyst is an oxide-modified                sulfated zirconium oxide solid super-strong acid;                oxide is amphoteric oxides iron(III) oxide,                aluminum oxide, chromium(III) oxide, tin(IV) oxide,                titanium dioxide, zinc oxide and germanium dioxide,                or other transition metal oxides molybdenum                dioxide, vanadium(V) oxide, vanadium(III) oxide,                cobalt(II) oxide, niobium pentoxide, manganese                dioxide and ferrous ferric oxide.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: In step (i), mass                  ratio between metal oxide and zirconium oxide is                  1:10-1:2. In step (iii), mass ratio between                  zirconium oxide and MCM-41 molecular sieve is                  1:8-1:1. Preferred Method: In step (v),                  calcination temperature is 500-750 degrees C, and                  calcination time is not less than 3 hours. In                  step (iv), ultrasonication time is 0.5-2 hours.                  In step (ii), heating temperature is 40-80                  degrees C. The heating time is 1-4 hours. In step                  (iii), stirring time is 5-12 hours. In step (v),                  drying temperature is 100-200 degrees C, and                  drying time is not less than 8 hours.

PN DE102017115215-A1; WO2019007664-A1; EP3649140-A1; US2020199162-A1; US11267831-B2
TI New N-heterocyclic carbene rhenium complex useful as medicine to treat bacterial infection or bacterial infestation caused by gram-positive bacterium, and for identifying molecules.
AU METZLER-NOLTE N
   SIEGMUND D
   BANDOW J
   SCHAKERMANN S
   SCHAEKERMANN S
AE UNIV RUHR BOCHUM (UYRU-Non-standard)
   UNIV RUHR BOCHUM (UYRU-Non-standard)
   UNIV RUHR BOCHUM (UYRU-Non-standard)
GA 2019035700
AB    NOVELTY - N-heterocyclic carbene rhenium complex (I) is new.
   USE - (I) is useful as medicine to treat bacterial infection or bacterial infestation caused by gram-positive bacterium, and for identifying molecules (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - (I) has a high antimicrobial effect against Gram-positive bacteria.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - N-heterocyclic carbene rhenium complex of formula (I) is new.
   R1-R6 = linear or branched alkyl, linear or branched heteroalkyl, linear or branched alkenyl, linear or branched heteroalkenyl, linear or branched alkynyl, linear or branched heteroalkynyl, cycloalkyl, heterocycloalkyl, aryl, heteroaryl, linear or branched alkylaryl, linear or branched heteroalkylaryl (all are optionally substituted) or H, where each of the above groups have not greater than 20C; and
   L1-L5 = metal coordinating ligands including neutral ligands, anionic ligands or mixed ligands, preferably linear or branched alkene having not greater than 20C (optionally substituted), halo, carbonyl, 1-10C alkyl, 1-10C alkoxy, sulfides, thiocyanates, nitrates, azides, fluorides, hydroxide ion, H2O, nitrites, isothiocyanates, acetonitrile, pyridines, ammonia, triphenylphosphines, cyanides, carbon monoxide, , benzene, cyclopentadienyl, nitrosyl, oxo ligand, sulfites, tricyclohexylphosphine, trimethylphosphine, tri(o-tolyl) phosphine, cycloheptatriene or carbon dioxide, where at least three of L1, L2, L3, L4 and L5, are linked together to form a molecule including oxalates, ethylenediamine, 2,2'-bipyridine, 1,10-phenanthroline, acetylacetonate, aminopolycarboxylic acids, 1,2-bis(diphenylphosphino)ethane, 1,1-bis(diphenylphosphino)methane, diethylenetriamine, dimethylglyoximate, glycine, iminodiacetic acid, nitrilotriacetic acid, pyrazine, scorpion ligands, 2,2',6',2''-terpyridine, triazacyclononane, di(2-picolyl)amine, 2,2'-dipyridylamines, tris(2-pyridylmethyl) amine, N,N,N',N'-tetramethylethylenediamine (TMEDA), N-propyl(2-pyridyl)methanimine (NPrPMI), ethane-1,2-diylidenediamine moiety of formula (IIa), substituted C-pyridin-2-yl-methyleneamine moiety of formula (IIb), substituted (2,2')bipyridinyl moiety of formula (IIc) or substituted (1,10)phenanthroline moiety of formula (IId) ; and
   X = H or a linear or branched alkyl having not greater than 20C, where L1, L2, L3, L4 and L5, which are not linked to other metal coordinating ligands.
   INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for:
   (1) preparation of (I); and
   (2) identifying molecules that interacts with (I), comprising (a) providing (I), where R5 is a linker group, (b) immobilizing (I) on a solid support, where the solid support binds to (I) via the linker group, (c) contacting the immobilized complex with a solution comprising the molecules of interest, and (d) detaching the molecules that interact with the immobilized complex, and identifying the molecules.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preparation (Claimed): The preparation of (I), comprises contacting rhenium carbonyl compound of formula (III) with substituted 2-((triphenylphosphoranylidene)amino)aniline compound of formula (IV) to obtain N-heterocyclic carbene rhenium (I) complex of formula (V). Preferred Components: The linker group is 3-(5-((3aS,4S,6aR)-2-oxohexahydro-1H-thieno(3,4-d)imidazol-4-yl)pentanamido)prop-1-ynyl (biotin) and the solid support comprises avidin and/or streptavidin.
   Preferred Definitions:
   Either L4, L5 = (2,2')bipyridinyl, 4,4'-di-tert-butyl-(2,2')bipyridinyl, (1,10)phenanthroline, 4,4'-dimethyl-(2,2')bipyridinyl or 4,7-diphenyl-(1,10)phenanthroline; or
   L4+L5 = (2,2')bipyridinyl, 4,4'-di-tert-butyl-(2,2')bipyridinyl, (1,10)phenanthroline, 4,4'-dimethyl-(2,2')bipyridinyl or 4,7-diphenyl-(1,10)phenanthroline; and
   R1 = benzyl or 2,4,6-trimethyl-benzyl.
   ACTIVITY - Antibacterial. Test details are described but no results given.
   MECHANISM OF ACTION - None given.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

PT P
PN CN108946746-A
TI Method for separating and utilizing feldspar type aluminum-containing mineral aluminum potassium silicon element involves reacting feldspar-type aluminum-containing mineral powder with acid, activating and separating solid and liquid.
AU LI L
   WU Y
AE UNIV SHENYANG TECHNOLOGY (UYTE-C)
GA 2018A05553
AB    NOVELTY - The method for separating and utilizing feldspar type aluminum-containing mineral aluminum potassium silicon element involves grinding feldspar containing aluminum minerals, mixing feldspar-type aluminum-containing mineral powder with acid solution, activating feldspar-type aluminum-containing mineral, precipitating white carbon black, immersing in ammonium hydrogen sulfate solution, forming aluminum and potassium into liquid phase and carrying out solid-liquid separation, carrying out aluminum and potassium separation, crystallizing, subjecting ammonium sulfate solution to evaporation crystallization to obtain ammonium sulfate crystal, decomposing ammonium sulfate crystal by heating into ammonia gas and ammonium hydrogen sulfate, recycling ammonium hydrogen sulfate, preparing ammonia gas into aqueous ammonia, pressurizing and carrying out aluminum sinking.
   USE - Method for separating and utilizing feldspar type aluminum-containing mineral aluminum potassium silicon element.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables separation and utilization of feldspar type aluminum-containing mineral aluminum potassium silicon element ensuring excellent separation of potassium, aluminum and silicon, and efficient recycling of ammonium hydrogen sulfate by simple and easy process.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Method for separating and utilizing feldspar type aluminum-containing mineral aluminum potassium silicon element involves (1) grinding feldspar containing aluminum minerals to particle size of -100--400 mesh, (2) mixing feldspar-type aluminum-containing mineral powder with acid solution, carrying out solid-liquid separation, adding alkali solution to iron-removed solution to prepare iron hydroxide, separating and utilizing iron elements, (3) activating feldspar-type aluminum-containing mineral after iron removal by carrying out alkaline solution wet activation or alkali powder baking activation, after activation, carrying out solid liquid separation to obtain silicate solution and potassium-aluminum solid phase and separating silicon in feldspar, (4) passing carbon dioxide into silicate solution, filtering and washing to obtain precipitated white carbon black, (5) immersing separated potassium aluminum solid phase in ammonium hydrogen sulfate solution, forming aluminum and potassium into liquid phase and carrying out solid-liquid separation, (6) adding ammonia to leachate for aluminizing, producing aluminum hydroxide precipitate and ammonium sulfate and carrying out aluminum and potassium separation, (7) subjecting mixed solution of ammonium sulfate and potassium sulfate to crystallization separation to obtain potassium sulfate solid and ammonium sulfate solution, (8) subjecting ammonium sulfate solution to evaporation crystallization to obtain ammonium sulfate crystal, (9) decomposing ammonium sulfate crystal by heating into ammonia gas and ammonium hydrogen sulfate, recycling ammonium hydrogen sulfate, preparing ammonia gas into aqueous ammonia, pressurizing and carrying out aluminum sinking.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The acid solution is sulfuric acid or hydrochloric acid. The alkali is sodium hydroxide, potassium hydroxide, sodium carbonate or potassium carbonate. Preferred Conditions: The iron removal temperature is controlled at 20-200 degrees C for 10-120 minutes, and activating at 80-350 degrees C for 20-180 minutes. The alkali powder roasting activation is carried out using sodium hydroxide, potassium hydroxide, sodium carbonate or potassium carbonate and activating at 500-1200 degrees C for 30-150 minutes. The leaching temperature is controlled at 70-300 degrees C for 20-180 minutes. Preferred Composition: The concentration of sulfuric acid is 5-80 %mass. The concentration of hydrochloric acid is 5-36 %mass.
DC C04 (Fertilisers - including urea and phosphoric acid production. Also soil modifiers and plant growth media. Chemical aspects of compost production.)

PT P
PN DE102017207665-A1; KR2018123438-A; CN108869058-A
TI Method for operating system with combustion chamber e.g. gas engine, involves supplying specific fuel gas composition and characteristic values information including air-fuel ratio and mass-specific gas constant to control device.
AU RENK M
AE BOSCH GMBH ROBERT (BOSC-C)
GA 201887510J
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves feeding and burning both oxidizer and fuel in the form of fuel gas as a mixture in a combustion chamber (11) including the gas engine (12) through a suction region (1). The information about a specific fuel gas composition and specific characteristic values including the air-fuel ratio (AFR) and mass-specific gas constant (Rs) are supplied to a control device (14). The specific fuel gas composition and the specific characteristic values are taken into account in the determination of the operating variable.
   USE - Method for operating system with combustion chamber e.g. gas engine such as stationary gas engine for use in thermal power plant and used in mobile application such as motor vehicle.
   ADVANTAGE - The fluctuations in the fuel gas composition are taken into account when determining operating variables, so that higher accuracy of the operating variables is achieved. The need for immediate measurement of volume or mass flow is eliminated. The accuracy of the known method is improved and thus the emission of pollutants is reduced with consideration of the specific gas composition. The ratio of fuel gas to air is determined based on information about the air ratio from the lambda sensor or another source, so that the desired mixture composition is maintained even with different types of gas including methane (CH4), ethane (C2H6), propane (C3H8) and other components such as nitrogen (N2) or carbon dioxide (CO2). The operating variable of the gas is obtained in simple manner by the subsequent offsetting with the correction factor, which is corrected with respect to relation to the nominal gas composition changed special fuel gas composition.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) a control device for operating system with combustion chamber;
   (2) a computer program includes instruction for operating system with combustion chamber; and
   (3) a machine-readable storage medium storing program for operating system with combustion chamber.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawings show the schematic view of the environment in which the method is used and the schematic view illustrating the proportions of air and fuel gas at different fuel gas compositions in air-fuel gas mixture.
   Suction region (1)
   Combustion chamber (11)
   Gas engine (12)
   Control device (14)
   Fresh air portion (20)

PT P
PN EP3379469-A1
TI Method for unmanned dropping off and picking up of gas cylinder, involves providing mobile software application which gives user access to storage room and guides user to drop off and/or pick up gas cylinder without service personnel.
AU OPFERMANN A
   LAUR A
AE LINDE AG (LINM-C)
GA 2018749452
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves providing mobile software application which gives user access to storage room (1) and guides user to drop off and/or pick up gas cylinder (7) without service personnel. The storage room has door (2) for allowing user to enter the storage room from outside and the door has lock which can be unlocked from outside by mobile application and the door can be mechanically opened from inside of storage room. The lock is controlled by access control system which is integrated with mobile software application. The storage room is built as mobile container which can be easily moved.
   USE - Method for unmanned dropping off and picking up of gas cylinder, used to store different kinds of gasses under pressure, from storage room by user. Uses include flammable gasses as like propane, acetylene which are used for welding or combustion, inert gasses as like helium, argon or mixture of inert gasses which are used for preventing certain process from contacting atmosphere, as like welding, oxidant gasses as like carbon dioxide (CO2), oxygen (O2), nitrogen oxide (NO), or mixture of oxidant gasses which are used to facilitate combustion, or other gasses as like nitrogen (N2), hydrogen (H2), and air.
   ADVANTAGE - The user needs to download mobile software application and log in to it with his data as required, so only permitted user is allowed to deal with the gas cylinder which enables gas supplier to have control of their users and thus avoids any misuses. The containers can be situated near a construction site to support smooth gas handling for companies located there or in smaller towns or countryside to replace existing gas service station or in cities to complete larger gas service stations by offering superior availability or in partnership with already exiting industry sales. Improves flexibility and convenience of gas service stations. A code provides only one time access to the door and might be valid only for a limited time to ensure safe and reliable access.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic diagram of storage room.
   Storage room (1)
   Door (2)
   Steel lattice flooring (3)
   Additional big door (5)
   Gas cylinder (7)

PT P
PN CN108373957-A
TI Brewing fruit wine involves selecting raw materials of winter pear, using clean water to clean, using brine to soak, washing by clean water, peeling, removing kernel, cutting to blocks and using juice extractor to crush to pear pulp.
AU ZHANG B
   HAN S
   DENG F
   NA M
   QIU S
   XIE H
   CHEN C
   BAI X
   ZHANG S
AE UNIV GANSU AGRIC (UYGS-C)
GA 201866109K
AB    NOVELTY - Brewing fruit wine involves selecting raw materials: selecting Haas No. 1/Haas No. 2 or Haas No. 1 + Haas No. 2 winter pear, where soluble solid content is 9-11%, using clean water to clean, using 2-3% brine to soak for 3-4 minutes, washing by clean water, peeling, removing kernel, cutting to blocks, using juice extractor to crush to pear pulp, adding 60-80 mg/l sodium pyrosulfite to protect color and inhibit bacteria, adding 0.02-0.04 g/l Optizym (RTM: concentrated pectolytic enzyme) pectase in pear pulp, clarifying at 5-8 degrees C for 8-12 hours, adjusting components for first time, taking clear liquid of pear pulp, adjusting content of sugar and acid until total sugar degree in winter pear juice after adjusting is 190-240 g/l, and acid degree is 4.5-6.3 g/l, carrying out alcoholic fermentation, adding 0.15-0.30 g/l wine yeast in obtained pear juice to ferment, controlling fermentation temperature at 17-20 degrees C.
   USE - Method for brewing fruit wine.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to maintain original nutritional components of winter pear in brewed wine, where wine has clear and bright appearance, sweet taste, fragrance of roses, unique flavor and has better social and economic benefits.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Brewing fruit wine further involves adjusting components for second time when total sugar degree of winter pear juice is reduced to 150-170 g/l, adding 2.0-3.5 g/l dry rose bud, and 0.15-0.20 g/l yeast polysaccharide, stirring for 5-8 minutes, fermenting continuously for 8-10 hours, stopping fermenting, when alcohol fermentation degree of pear juice arrives at 5.5-6.5 vol.%, and total sugar content is 100-130 g/l, adjusting fermentation temperature to 0-4 degrees C, keeping for 24 hours, adding 100-120 mg/l sodium pyrosulfite, filtering, sterilizing pear wine after fermentation is stopped with filter membrane whose aperture is 0.2 microns, aging pear wine, which is filtered and sterilized in stainless steel tank filled with nitrogen or carbon dioxide, controlling ambient temperature of aging at 14-16 degrees C for 120-180 days, sterilizing and canning to obtain the desired fruit wine.
DC D16 (Fermentation industry - including fermentation equipment, brewing, yeast production, production of pharmaceuticals and other chemicals by fermentation, microbiology, production of vaccines and antibodies, cell and tissue culture and genetic engineering.)
MC D05-E
IP C12G-003/02

PT P
PN CN108277047-A; CN108277047-B
TI Nuclear-zero carbon emission system for producing fuel chemicals, comprises high temperature gas cool reactor and heat exchange system for heating raw water preheating of solid oxide electrolytic cell is set in high temperature.
AU CHEN Q
   SUN Y
   TANG Z
   GU Y
   WANG D
AE SHANGHAI ADVANCED RES INST CHINESE ACAD (CAAR-C)
   UNIV CHINESE ACAD SCI (UCAS-C)
GA 2018560904
AB    NOVELTY - Nuclear-zero carbon emission system comprises high temperature gas cool reactor (5) and heat exchange system, for heating the raw water preheating of solid oxide electrolytic cell (2). The solid oxide electrolytic cell is set in the high temperature gas-cooling reactor downstream of the heat exchange system, and electrolysis reaction generating hydrogen and oxygen in the solid oxide electrolyte tank is connected through the raw water after heated by the high temperature gas-cooling reactor, heat exchange system and gasifier. The gasifier is connected with the oxygen outlet of the solid oxide electrolytic cell, and oxygen generating gasification reaction is connected with the pulverized coal in the coal gasification furnace (3) generating gas. The gas purifying unit (4) is connected with the gasifier for converting the raw gas purified into clean gas, desulfurizing purifying unit (5), where the gas purifying unit is used for the water gas and desulfurizing purifying process.
   USE - Nuclear-zero carbon emission system for producing fuel chemicals.
   ADVANTAGE - The nuclear-zero carbon emission system has reduced direct discharge of carbon dioxide, reduces high energy consumption of production system.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for using nuclear-zero carbon emission system, which involves:
   (A) providing raw water, preheating the raw water, and then heating the preheated raw water by using the high temperature gas cooled reactor heat exchange system;
   (B) subjecting heated raw water to the solid oxide electrolysis cell to undergo an electrolytic reaction to generate hydrogen and oxygen;
   (C) subjecting the oxygen of coal gasification furnace, and gasification reaction with the pulverizing coal in the coal gasification furnace to form a crude gas, and the crude gas is purifying by the crude gas purification unit to become a net crude gas, and then desulfurization purification treatment is perform in the desulfurization purification unit; and
   (D) mixing the hydrogen gas and the net crude gas subjected to the desulfurization purification treatment into the mix purifying gas in the mixer, and inputting the mixed purifying gas into the fuel chemical synthesis tower for reaction.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of nuclear-zero carbon emission system. (Drawing includes non-English language text).
   Solid oxide electrolytic cell (2)
   Coal gasification furnace (3)
   Gas purifying unit (4)
   Desulfurizing purifying unit (5)
   High temperature gas cool reactor (5)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The method comprises high-temperature gas-cooled reactor and heat exchange system containing a circulating high-temperature gas-cooled reactor, a superheater and a steam generator, and the helium gas exiting the high-temperature gas-cooled reactor outlet passes through superheater. The superheater and the steam generator are cooled down, and then returned to the inlet of the high temperature gas cooled reactor. The temperature of the high temperature gas cooled reactor outlet is between 900-1100 degrees C, and the inlet temperature is between 180-300 degrees C. The preheated raw water passes through the steam generator and the superheater in sequence, and is heated by heat exchange with the helium gas, and the temperature of the raw water after heating is between 750-950 degrees C. The preheating of raw water comprises the raw water being the first raw water and the second raw water, where the first raw water is preheated through the first heat exchanger. The second raw water is preheated by the second heat exchanger, and is separately preheated and merged into the high temperature gas cooled heat exchange system for heating, and the raw water temperature after preheating is between 60-80 degrees C. The hydrogen and oxygen pressure at the outlet of the solid oxide electrolysis cell is between 1-1.5 MPa, the temperature is between 750-950 degrees C. The method of oxygen enters the coal gasification furnace, the second waste heat boiler recovers heat, and then enters the first heat exchanger to preheat the first raw water. The hydrogen is recovered by the first waste heat boiler before being mixed with the net crude gas, and then enters the second heat exchanger to preheat the second raw water. The preheating of first raw water and the second raw water, the temperature of the oxygen and the hydrogen is lowered to below 80 degrees C. The preheating the first raw water, the oxygen enters a distributor through a portion of the oxygen is output as an oxygen product, and another portion of the oxygen is compressed by a second compressor to entering the coal gasifier after 2-4Mpa. The hydrogen is preheated to the second raw water, it is compressed to 3-9 MPa by the first compressor, and then enters the mixer and mixed with the net crude gas which has undergone desulfurization purification treatment. The pulverized coal is obtained by pulverizing and drying the coal through a coal preparation device, and the particle size of the pulverized coal is less than 90 mu m, and the water content is less than 5%. The pulverized coal is gasified with the oxygen gas and the steam, the gasification reaction temperature is between 1400-1700 degrees C, the pressure is between 2-4 MPa, and the hydrogen gas of the crude gas produced by the reaction is generated, and ratio is between 0.3-0.6. The method after the reaction generates the crude gas, the heat is recovered by the third waste heat boiler, and then purified by the crude gas purification device to become the net crude gas, and the portion of the net crude gas. The desulfurization purification treatment is performed, and a portion of the net crude gas is pressurized to 2-4 MPa by a circulating gas through a circulation compressor, and then returned to the coal gasification furnace. The method of hydrogen gas and the net crude gas subjected to the desulfurization purification treatment are mixed in a mixer and the hydrogen to carbon ratio is adjusted, and the mixed purifying gas is pressurized to 3-9 MPa and heating to 200-400 degrees C, then enter the fuel chemical synthesis tower for reaction.
DC E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); H09 (Fuel products not of petroleum origin - excluding coal handling, preparation or mining, but including coking, briquetting, peat processing synthesis, gas production, coal gasification. Combustion improvement additives for coal, peat and other nonhydrocarbon based fuels are included in this Section together with coal liquefaction and desulphurisation.); J03 (Electrochemical processes and electrophoresis - including ozone production, brine electrolysis, water electrolysis, production of chemical compounds and non-metallic elements, but excluding batteries or other means of producing power and the treatment of metals (C25B).); X25 (Industrial Electric Equipment)

PT P
PN KR2018076042-A; KR1884459-B1
TI Rotary kiln for burning powder, has gas discharge valve that discharges gas inside firing device and material discharge device, and control unit that controls opening and closing of gas discharge valve and raw material discharge valve.
AU WOONJUNG K
   EUN S P
AE UNIV HANNAM INST IND ACAD COOP (UHAN-C)
   MS MATERIALS CO LTD (MSMA-Non-standard)
GA 201854101L
AB    NOVELTY - The rotary kiln has a storage device (110) in which a firing raw material is stored. A transfer device (120) includes one side connected to storage device to transfer firing material. A firing device (130) is formed of a rotating tube connected to other side of transfer device to receive the firing raw material. A first sensor unit (160) is formed of one of sensor selected from a pressure sensor and a gas component analysis sensor. A second sensor unit (170) is formed as a load cell in a lower direction of raw material discharging device (140). A gas discharge valve (180) discharges the gas inside the firing device and the material discharge device in the direction of the upper side of raw material discharge device. The first sensor unit and the second sensor unit both receive the measured values and use the optimal values previously input to gas injection valves. A control unit controls opening and closing of the gas discharge valve and the raw material discharge valve (141).
   USE - Rotary kiln for burning powder.
   ADVANTAGE - The firing device and the internal rotating device provided inside the firing device are rotated in the direction opposite to the rotating direction of the firing device, and the firing material is adsorbed to the firing device due to factors such as moisture and carbon dioxide generated inside the firing process, so that the quality of the powder can be prevented from deteriorating due to non-uniform firing of the fired powder which is generated by adsorption. The controller automatically controls the injection of various gases such as compressed dry air, oxygen, argon, and nitrogen used in the firing process and the internal pressure to prevent explosion by gas leaks that can occur when using hydrogen and oxygen. The rotary kiln can maintain a proper concentration and automatically control the sintering process to proceed smoothly in the case of using an inert gas such as argon or nitrogen, thus leading to a more efficient sintering process.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a rotary kiln control method.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a cross-sectional view of a rotary kiln.
   Storage device (110)
   Transfer device (120)
   Firing device (130)
   Raw material discharging device (140)
   Raw material discharge valve (141)
   First sensor unit (160)
   Second sensor unit (170)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - The inner wall of the firing device comprises 60-80 wt.% magnesium oxide, 1-6 wt.% aluminum oxide, 0.5-0.8 wt.% of calcium oxide, 15-30 wt.% chromium(III) oxide, and 3.5-4.5 wt.% of ferrous oxide.
DC Q77 (Furnaces, kilns, ovens, retorts (F27)); X25 (Industrial Electric Equipment)
MC Q77-A02; Q77-T01; Q77-T05; Q77-T20; Q77-T99; X25-C07

PT P
PN CN107986617-A
TI Preparing wear-resistant glass comprises e.g. dispersing silica in hexamethyldisilazane solution and soaking, subjecting the perlite to low-temperature plasma treatment using plasma processor and annealing.
AU CHEN X
AE ANHUI FENGYANG ASIA EURO GLASS HANDICRAF (ANHU-Non-standard)
GA 201839391T
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing wear-resistant glass comprises e.g. (i) dispersing silica in hexamethyldisilazane solution and soaking, filtering, drying, and then treating with silica, placing in supercritical equipment to obtain modified silica, (ii) grinding perlite to 300 mesh, and then soaking perlite in sodium glutamate solution, subjecting the perlite to low-temperature plasma treatment using plasma processor, (iii) adding modified silica and modified perlite, adding modified silica and calcium stearate, sealing, and (iv) introducing glass melt into molding machine and extruding, cooling and annealing.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing wear-resistant glass.
   ADVANTAGE - The glass has excellent hardness, excellent resistance to falling and wear resistance, and is not easily scratched.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing wear-resistant glass comprises (i) dispersing silica in hexamethyldisilazane solution with mass fraction of 3.5-5.5% and soaking for 40-50 minutes, filtering, drying to constant weight, and then treating with silica, placing in supercritical equipment at a pressure of 15-18 MPa, and temperature of 155-160 degrees C to obtain modified silica, where the treatment medium is carbon dioxide, and supercritical treatment time is 15-20 minutes, (ii) grinding the perlite to 300 mesh, and then soaking the perlite in 0.5-0.8 wt.% sodium glutamate solution at ratio of 150:420 g/ml at 68-72 degrees C for 1.5 hours and then filtering, washing once with clean water, placing in absolute ethanol for 40 minutes at room temperature, then filtering and drying it to constant weight, calcining perlite at 580-600 degrees C for 20 minutes, and then cooling to 40 degrees C, subjecting the perlite to low-temperature plasma treatment using plasma processor to obtain modified perlite, where processing time is 10-12 minutes and the voltage is 110 V, (iii) adding modified silica and the modified perlite in a mass ratio of 100:12-15 and adding it into the furnace, adding 5-6% modified silica and calcium oxide 0.1-0.3% of calcium stearate, sealing, heating mixture at 1620-1650 degrees C and insulating for 8-10 hours until mixture reaches a molten state to obtain glass melt, and (iv) introducing glass melt into molding machine and extruding to form glass with different specifications, cooling glass until it is hardened and then sent into annealing furnace to be annealed for 30-40 minutes.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: In step (i), silicon dioxide particle size is 250-300 mesh. In step (ii), perlite grain size is 400 mesh. In step (ii), during plasma treatment, the source of the treatment is glow discharge, processing gas is air and the vacuum in the processing chamber is 0.032 MPa. Preferred Method: The hexamethyldisilazane solution is prepared by dissolving hexamethyldisilazane in acetone.
DC L01 (Glass - includes chemical compositions, batch treatment, furnaces, flat glass forming, hollow-ware forming, postforming and glass/ceramics, but not lens designs, bottling, bottle-washing, closures for containers, glazing designs, glass cutting, chamfering edges, printing on glass, disposing of used glass or the production of pure sodium silicate. Chemical aspects of optical fibres (C03).); V05 (Valves, Discharge Tubes and CRTs)
MC L01-G02; L01-G06; L01-H09A; L01-H09C; L01-H09D; V05-F04; V05-F05C; V05-F05E3

PN CN107904397-A; CN107904397-B
TI Sintering gasification dephosphorization recovery device based dephosphorization method involves passing oxygen to cause dephosphorization product to undergo chemical reaction to generate phosphorus oxychloride.
AU WANG H
   XING H
   ZHANG W
   TIAN T
AE UNIV NORTH CHINA SCI & TECHNOLOGY (UNNS-C)
   UNIV NORTH CHINA SCI & TECHNOLOGY (UNNS-C)
GA 201830046F
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves adding a sintering raw material to a sintering furnace (5), adding carbon as fuel, turning on an igniter for intering and reacting (i) calcium chloride (CaCl2), sulfur dioxide (SO2) and oxygen (O2) around carbon particles to obtain calcium sulfate (CaSO4) and chloride (Cl2), performing dephosphorization by reacting (ii) 2 mol calcium fluoride phosphate (Ca5(PO4)3F), 9 mol chloride (Cl2), 9 mol silica (SiO2) and 15 mol carbon to obtain 6 mol phosphorus trichloride (PCl3) as a dephosphorization product, 15 mol carbon monoxide (CO), 9 mol calcium silicate (CaSiO3) and calcium fluoride (CaF2) using dephosphorization substance which is intermediate product chlorine gas generation in process (i), cooling dephosphorization product, supplying dephosphorization product to a dephosphorization product collection container (13) and passing oxygen to cause dephosphorization product (PCl3) to undergo chemical reaction to generate phosphorus oxychloride.
   USE - Sintering gasification dephosphorization recovery device based dephosphorization method.
   ADVANTAGE - The direct dephosphorization in the sintering process is realized so that the raw material cost and dephosphorization cost are reduced.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - The dephosphorization method involves adding sodium sulfate and ore containing hematite and gangue to the sintering furnace after adding the raw material, and reacting (iii) sodium sulfate (Na2SO4) with alumina (Al2O3) and 2 mol silica (SiO2) contained in the ore under the action of carbon monoxide (CO) to obtain 2 mol sodium aluminum silicate (NaAlSiO4), sulfur dioxide (SO2) and carbon dioxide (CO2), where sulfur dioxide (SO2) generated in the process (iii) is used as the reactant in the process (i). The exhaust gas generated during the sintering process of the sintering furnace is passed through a pipeline through a primary cyclone dust collector (7) and a secondary cyclone dust collector (9) under the action of an exhaust fan (14). The dust in the exhaust gas is removed by the primary cyclone dust collector and the secondary cyclone dust collector. The exhaust gas is entered into a condenser (11). The exhaust gas is cooled by condenser so that the low-boiling dephosphorization product is cooled and made into liquid and is entered into dephosphorization product collection container for enrichment and recovery.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the sintering gasification dephosphorization recovery device.
   Sintering furnace (5)
   Primary cyclone dust collector (7)
   Secondary cyclone dust collector (9)
   Condenser (11)
   Dephosphorization product collection container (13)
   Exhaust fan (14)

PN JP2018051443-A; JP6430451-B2
TI Wet deodorizing apparatus for deodorizing gas containing sulfur odor component, has circulation pump that is connected to electrolytic cell which is electrically connected with electrolytic cell control device and deodorization tower.
AU HAMAGUCHI M
AE ISSHIN KK (ISSH-Non-standard)
   ISSHIN KK (ISSH-Non-standard)
GA 201829578Q
AB    NOVELTY - The apparatus has a filler (7) which is arranged on a circulation chemical tank (8) which stores a chemical solution. A second circulation pump (11) is connected to the first circulation pump through a first piping (12). The first circulation pump is connected to the electrolytic cell through a second piping (14). The control device (20) is electrically connected to the electrolytic cell which is connected to the deodorization tower (1) through a third piping (15). The chemical solution is an aqueous solution containing sodium chloride and sodium hypochlorite.
   USE - Wet deodorizing apparatus for deodorizing gas containing sulfur odor component such as hydrogen sulfide, methyl mercaptan, methyl sulfide and methyl disulfide contained in gas emitted in human waste treatment plant and sewage work.
   ADVANTAGE - The sodium hypochlorite which reacts with the sulfur-based odorous component is supplied by electrolysis, the reaction free from precipitation of sulfur on the oxidation catalyst of the filler is promoted and the running cost is reduced by replenishing the chemical liquid. The chemical liquid is supplied to the filler and to the electrolytic cell by using one circulation pump, thus reduction in manufacturing cost and space-saving of the apparatus is realized. The flow rate of the circulating chemical solution to be supplied to the chemical solution injection portion and the flow rate supplied by the electrolytic cell are independently controlled by the two circulation pumps. The degree of freedom of flow rate control is improved and flexible correspondence can be taken according to the situation of the gas to be treated. The necessary amount of sodium hypochlorite is generated easily in accordance with the pattern even when the amount of generated gas containing the odorous component fluctuates with specific pattern in time. Thus, the electric power is saved. The hypochlorous acid is efficiently reacted with sulfur-based odor component even with gas containing high content of carbon dioxide and excessive precipitates are generated. The frequency of maintenance such as cleaning of the apparatus is reduced. The operation rate of the apparatus is improved.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a deodorizing method for gas containing sulfur type odor component.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the wet deodorizing apparatus.
   Deodorizing tower (1)
   Filler (7)
   Circulation chemical tank (8)
   Circulation pump (11)
   Pipings (12,14,15)
   Control device (20)

PN US9927148-B1
TI Generation of heat to produce e.g. steam, involves reacting metal oxide with citric acid solution to form metal ions, and then with erythorbic acid solution, precipitating resultant, reducing resultant by heating, and reacting with oxygen.
AU PHELPS C E
AE PHELPS C E (PHEL-Individual)
GA 201823994S
AB    NOVELTY - Generation of heat involves providing a metal oxide (M1), initially reacting the metal oxide (M1) with a citric acid solution (26) to generate metal ions, and then with an erythorbic acid solution (26), promoting dissolution of metal oxide using the erythorbic acid and metal ions to form a reaction solution (30) of a metal hydroxide, precipitating the metal hydroxide to produce metal hydroxide particles, separating the particles, heating the particles to reduce the metal oxide (M1) to a metal oxide (M2), and reacting the metal oxide (M2) with oxygen to produce the metal oxide (M1) and heat.
   USE - Generation of heat used to produce steam in operator (chemical looping combustion system), and to produce power.
   ADVANTAGE - The method efficiently generates heat using chemical looping combustion system. The operational costs of running the chemical looping combustion system are less than the costs of running a fossil fuel plant that produces the same amount of usable heat. The chemical looping combustion system does not produce any greenhouse gases, such as carbon dioxide. The combustion system produces more heat than is required to maintain its operation. Approximately 20% heat generated is used in operations, and the remaining 80% is heat is free for other uses. The chemical looping combustion has high efficiency.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Generation of heat involves providing a volume of a metal oxide (M1), initially reacting the metal oxide (M1) with a citric acid solution to generate metal ions, and then with an erythorbic acid solution using a two-stage reductive dissolution, promoting dissolution of metal oxide using the erythorbic acid and the metal ions to form a reaction solution that contains a metal hydroxide, precipitating the metal hydroxide from the reaction solution to produce particles of metal hydroxide, separating the particles of metal hydroxide from the reaction solution, heating the particles of metal hydroxide to reduce the metal oxide (M1) to a metal oxide (M2), and reacting the metal oxide (M2) with oxygen to produce the metal oxide (M1) and heat.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of major components of the chemical looping combustion system.
   Ferric oxide (12)
   Acid solutions (26)
   Water (28)
   Reaction solution (30)
   Vacuum membrane filter press (48)
   Decomposition and dryer chamber (52)
   Cyclone heater (60)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The method further involves using at least some of the heat to produce steam from water (28). The reaction step of metal oxide (M1) occurs in an inert atmosphere that is devoid of oxygen. The precipitating step involves changing the reaction solution from an acid pH to an alkaline pH, and cooling the reaction solution. The separating step involves passing the reaction solution through a vacuum membrane filter press (48). The heating step involves heating the particles of metal hydroxide in a decomposition and dryer chamber (52) that is heated, at least in portion, by the steam, or placing the particles of metal hydroxide in a cyclone heater (60). The reaction step of metal oxide (M2) involves injecting the metal oxide (M2) into a reaction chamber with both air and oxygen. The method further involves regenerating the reaction solution into an acid solution after separated from the metal hydroxide. The metal oxides (M1) and (M2) are iron oxides. The metal oxide is ferric oxide (12). The ferric oxide is mixed with the citric acid to generate ferric ions, and then mixed with erythorbic acid. The erythorbic acid reduces the ferric ions to ferrous ions. The ferrous ions accelerate dissolution of the ferric oxide to create a reaction solution that contains ferrous hydroxide. The ferrous hydroxide is precipitated from the solution to produce particles of ferrous hydroxide. The particles of ferrous hydroxide are reduced to ferrous oxide and water. The ferrous oxide is separated from water, and reacted with oxygen to produce the ferric oxide and heat.

TI Method for measuring concentration of at least one preselected gas and second preselected gas in environment involves accessing measurement data from optical detector when temperature of light source has reached second operating temperature.
AU JOURDAINNE L
AE ECOTEC SOLUTIONS INC (ECOT-Non-standard)
   ECOTEC SOLUTIONS INC (ECOT-Non-standard)
GA 2018160051
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves initiating activation of cooling or heating component associated with the light source (14) to adjust the temperature to predetermined second operating temperature. The measurement data from the optical detector is accessed when the temperature of the light source has reached the second operating temperature. A detection system measures second absorption of light beam at the second preselected wavelength to provide the indication of concentration of second preselected gas within the sample gas, with light source at predeternined second temperature.
   USE - Method for measuring concentration of at least one preselected gas and second preselected gas in environment. Uses include but are not limited to methane, ethane, propane, butane, oxygen, hydrogen, nitrogen, water, hydrogen fluoride, hydrogen chloride, hydrogen bromide, hydrogen sulfide, ammoniac, ammonia, carbon monoxide, carbon dioxide, nitrogen monoxide nitrogen dioxide, sulfur hexafluoride, tetrahydrothiophene, and tert-butyl mercaptan.
   ADVANTAGE - Absorption spectroscopy system is provided to automatically shift between detection of first preselected gas and at least one other preselected gas or shift between detection of first isotope and at least one other isotope of the same gas. The operating temperature of photodetector arranged to receive the light beam can be managed in correlation with that of the light source to increase the detection sensitivity of the preselected gas. Achieves control of the temperature of the light source by regulating the amount of current applied to the light source and modifying the amount of heat generated in the circuitry of the light source by resistive heating. Enables to determine that additional sampling for the first gas is required after detecting that the concentration is greater than the threshold Limit to enhance the accuracy of the detection of the first gas. Enables to notify or prompt the user to continue sampling in the area, as there may be location nearby (e.g., closer to the source of the detected gas) where the concentration is higher and may exceed the threshold.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a system for measuring concentration of at least one preselected gas and second preselected gas in environment.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing is the schematic diagram of the absorption spectroscopy analyzer.

PN CN107720748-A
TI Preparing activated carbon from sludge involves drying, crushing and grinding waste activated carbon to prepare powder and dehydrating biological sludge in sewage treatment plant to prepare semi-solid or solid mud block.
AU YU W
AE YU W (YUWW-Individual)
GA 201817193P
AB    NOVELTY - The preparation method of activated carbon from sludge involves: (1) drying, crushing and grinding the waste activated carbon to prepare powder, and dehydrating the biological sludge in sewage treatment plant to prepare a semi-solid or solid mud block; and (2) mixing the powdered waste activated carbon with semi-solid or solid mud block and a proper amount of water, mixing to form a semi-solid carbon mud mixture, putting the semi-solid carbon sludge mixture into a carbon mud block with a specific shape through a mold, and then putting the carbon sludge block into a drying chamber.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing activated carbon from sludge.
   ADVANTAGE - The activated carbon prepared by the method has excellent adsorption balance ability and fast absorption speed.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - The preparation method of activated carbon from sludge involves: (1) drying, crushing and grinding the waste activated carbon to prepare powder, and dehydrating the biological sludge in sewage treatment plant to prepare a semi-solid or solid mud block; (2) mixing the powdered waste activated carbon with semi-solid or solid mud block and a proper amount of water, uniformly mixing to form a semi-solid carbon mud mixture, putting the semi-solid carbon sludge mixture into a carbon mud block with a specific shape through a mold, and then putting the carbon sludge block into a drying chamber for drying and curing; (3) feeding the dried carbon sludge blocks into a carbonization furnace for carbonization to obtain carbonized carbon sludge blocks, cooling the high-temperature carbonized carbon sludge blocks to room temperature, packaging and storing through a plastic packaging film; (4) feeding the carbonized carbon sludge block into a carbonization activator for activation, where the activation medium is mixed gas of water vapor and carbon dioxide; (5) introducing the activated carbon sludge block into a washing kettle to carry out acid washing, then drying and feeding the dried activated carbon into an activated carbon drying machine for drying to obtain a dry activated carbon; and (6) crushing the dried-product activated carbon through a crusher, and screening the crushed materials through a material screening machine to obtain a desired crushed carbon, columnar particle carbonized and powdered carbon.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The step (1) involves adding a flocculating agent to the biological sludge in the sewage treatment plant, stirring and leaving still, filtering and dehydrating until the water content is lower than 40% to prepare a semi-solid or solid mud block, where the flocculating agent is a cationic polyacrylamide with molecular weight of 1000-1400 million dalton. The carbonizing step in step (3) is carried out using inert gas as protective gas, where the inert gas is nitrogen gas, helium gas or argon gas, and the blowing rate of the inert gas is 100-200 ml/minute. The water vapor in step (4) is obtained by microwave heating or conventional heating. Preferred Condition: The drying step in step (2) is carried out at a temperature of 105-130 degrees C for 4-32 hours. The carbonizing step in step (3) is carried out at a temperature of 500-650 degrees C for 1-5 hours. The activation temperature in step (4) is 850-950 degrees C. The drying step in step (5) is carried out at a temperature of 80-120 degrees C for 2-24 hours. Preferred Components: The acid used in step (5) is hydrochloric acid, sulfuric acid, nitric acid, phosphoric acid or hydrofluoric acid. The waste activated carbon produced in step (1) has an average particle size of 0.1-2 mm.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The flocculating agent has a mass concentration of 1-3%.

TI Low-alcohol sparkling cider comprises reducing sugar, tartaric acid, sulfur dioxide and volatile acetic acid.
AU CUI Y
   LV W
AE TIANJIN AGRIC COLLEGE (UNTN-C)
GA 201806868H
AB    NOVELTY - Low-alcohol sparkling cider comprises 0-80 g/l reducing sugar, 5-9 g/l tartaric acid, less than or equal to 50 mg/l free sulfur dioxide (SO2), less than or equal to 200 mg/l total sulfur dioxide and less than or equal to 0.8 g/l volatile acetic acid. The alcohol degree of the low-alcohol sparkling cider is 3-7 volume/volume% and the low-alcohol sparkling cider is prepared by fermenting at 20 degrees C under pressure of 0.2-0.6 MPa after introducing carbon dioxide (CO2).
   USE - As low-alcohol sparkling cider.
   ADVANTAGE - The sparkling cider has low alcohol content, is pale yellow in color, clear and transparent, produces durable bubbles generated when the wine is poured into the glass, has fresh apple flavor, and sweet and sour taste, and is suitable for young people.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for method for preparing the low-alcohol sparkling cider, which involves (a) selecting high-quality and mature apples, washing with 1-2% dilute hydrochloric acid solution to remove pesticide contamination, rinsing with water, drying and crushing, (b) juicing apple blocks, adding 6% sulfite solution, adjusting the free sulfur dioxide content in juice to 60-80 mg/l and tartaric acid content to 5-9 g/l, (c) adding 0.02-0.06 g/l pectinase to the apple juice and performing enzymolysis at 14-16 degrees C, (d) activating dry yeast with water at 38-40 degrees C, adding 0.05-0.1 g/l yeast to the apple juice, fermenting at 17-19 degrees C, closing the fermentation tank vent valve when the alcohol content reaches 1.5-6.5 volume/volume%, fermenting at 13-15 degrees C, paying attention to changes in wine pressure when the alcohol content reaches 3-7 volume/volume%, adjusting the free sulfur dioxide to 40-60 mg/l when the pressure reaches 0.2-0.6 Mpa, cooling to 0 plus minus 1 degrees C to terminate the fermentation and maintaining under static condition the mixture for 10-15 days, (e) adding 0.5-1 g/l bentonite to the fermentation broth obtained in step (d), maintaining under static condition for 10-12 days and clarifying by filtering, (f) freezing clarified wine to 0.5-1 degrees C for 5-7 days and filtering while it is cold, and (g) adjusting the free sulfur dioxide content to 35-50 mg/l, sterilizing and filtering to obtain product.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - FOOD - Preferred Components: The apple juice in step (b) has concentration of 100 %mass. Preferred Method: The clarification step in step (c) is performed for 12-24 hours.

PN JP2018003254-A; JP6755729-B2
TI Construction of waterproof antirust material on            concrete structure for e.g. bridge, involves removing            deteriorated area from concrete structure, connecting            sacrificial anode material, filling restoration            material, and forming polyurea film.
AU DEMURA K
   WATANABE M
   NAKAMURA H
   TAKAHASHI N
AE SAKURA GUM KK (SAKU-Non-standard)
GA 201803203F
AB    NOVELTY - Construction of waterproof antirust material                (11,12,13) on reinforced concrete structure (1)                involves providing a reinforced concrete structure                having a reinforced bar (2) and existing concrete                (3), removing deteriorated area (6) from the                concrete, forming a recessed portion (5) in the                existing concrete, electrically connecting a                sacrificial anode material to the reinforced bar,                filling a restoration material (12) in the recessed                portion, and forming a polyurea coating film (13)                which covers the boundary between the existing                concrete and restoration material.
   USE - Construction of waterproof antirust material                on reinforced concrete structure. Uses include but                are not limited to bridge, highway, building and                apartment.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables efficient construction of                waterproof antirust material on reinforced concrete                structure. The processed reinforced concrete                structure has excellent antirust property,                corrosion resistance, crack resistance and                long-term rigidity, and long life. The peeling of                the outer surfaces of the existing concrete and                restoration material can be prevented. Since the                existing concrete is covered by glassy topcoat and                polyurea coating film, penetration of carbon                dioxide from atmosphere to the existing concrete                and penetration of salt content can be blocked                reliably, thus neutralization of the existing                concrete can be suppressed. The corrosion of the                reinforced bar with respect to residual salt                content can be suppressed, due to the presence of                sacrificial anode material.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Construction of waterproof antirust material                on reinforced concrete structure involves providing                a reinforced concrete structure having a reinforced                bar and existing concrete, removing deteriorated                area from the existing concrete, such that at least                a portion of the reinforced bar is exposed, forming                a recessed portion in the existing concrete,                electrically connecting a sacrificial anode                material to the reinforced bar, filling a                cross-section restoration material in the recessed                portion, and forming a polyurea coating film which                covers the boundary between the outer surface (3a)                of the existing concrete and the outer surface                (12a) of the restoration material.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a sectional view of                repaired area of reinforced concrete structure                repaired by construction of waterproof antirust                material.
   Reinforced concrete structure (1)
   Reinforced bar (2)
   Existing concrete (3)
   Outer surface of existing concrete                (3a)
   Recessed portion (5)
   Deteriorated area (6)
   Waterproof antirust material                (11,12,13)
   Cross-sectional restoration material                (12)
   Outer surface of cross-sectional restoration                material (12a)
   Polyurea coating film (13)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - CERAMICS AND GLASS - Preferred Process: The construction method                  further involves forming a heat-resistant glassy                  topcoat comprising a hydrolyzable marine organism                  adhesion-preventing paint, in the outer surface                  of the polyurea coating film.
